Double-pulse tests at a voltage and current level of 400 V and 40 A validate that the proposed module achieves a significantly reduced loop parasitic inductance of 6.2 nH, compared to a commercial EasyPACK 1B six-switch module with a typical inductance of 19 nH. With the integration of Cdec, the turn-off voltage overshoot is reduced by 67.4% at maximum.

title: An Explainable, Digital-Twin-Assisted Paradigm for Self-Reconfiguring and Energy-Resilient Autonomous Transportation
authors: B. Naresh Kumar Reddy, Yellapragada Charan Krishna
abstract: The monolithic and opaque nature of current edge AI systems presents a critical bottleneck for the deployment of safe, sustainable and scalable autonomous vehicles. This research introduces Federated Eco-Morphic AI FEX-DT ENA, a paradigm that fundamentally redesigns intelligent agents as living self-reconfiguring systems. The architecture features an eco-morphic super-network that dynamically modulates its computational graph achieving an unprecedented 88.0% reduction in energy consumption during nominal conditions by by autonomously decoupling computational density from environmental entropy to maintain a 99.2% task success rate based on situational complexity. A novel federated meta-learning strategy which collectively trains a lightweight explainable controller instead of the perception model reduces communication overhead by 98.1% across fleets of 50,000+ vehicles while achieving 0.997 cross-device generalization. Inherent architectural explainability provides real-time <4 ms latency causal justifications for actions attaining a quantitative XAI score of 0.923 in safety-critical scenarios. This is augmented by an onboard personalized digital twin that enables proactive energy strategy optimization and reduces predictive maintenance costs by up to 52.3%. The FEX-DT ENA framework demonstrates a 70.3% decrease in carbon footprint through grid-aware computation and a 42% average improvement in adversarial robustness, establishing a new blueprint for trustworthy and environmentally-adaptive autonomous systems.

title: A Novel High-Accuracy Interturn Fault Diagnosis Technique for Switched Reluctance Motors Based on Inductance Profile and Superposition Principle
authors: Mahmoud Hamouda, Mohammad A. Abido
abstract: Existing fault diagnosis techniques for interturn short circuits (ITSCs) in switched reluctance motors (SRMs) still face several challenges, including limited accuracy and sensitivity, compatibility with different control strategies, and dependence on operating speed and load. This article introduces a novel fault diagnosis technique for the ITSCs in SRMs using the extracted faulty inductance data over the minimum inductance zone (MIZ) using the superposition principle. First, it provides a detailed analysis for the magnetic circuit of SRM considering ITSCs. Second, it introduces the superposition principle to accurately extract the faulty inductance component of faulty coil/pole. Third, it deduces the mathematical formulation to relate the faulty and healthy component of inductances along with their active number of turns. Fourth, in order to improve the accuracy and sensitivity, it defines the proper calculation window (CW) over the MIZ considering the switching angles and the threshold current level. Finally, the experimental results show a maximum reported error of 1.05% over the entire speed range and different fault ratios (0% to 25%) that confirms the high accuracy and high sensitivity for the proposed technique. In addition, the proposed technique features a simple structure; is completely independent of speed, load, and control algorithm; offers a powerful tool for several industrial applications including electric vehicles (EVs).

title: How Composite Metamorphic Relations Enhance Test Effectiveness of DNN Testing: An Empirical Study
authors: Huayao Wu, Peng Wang, Shengyou Hu, Xintao Niu, Changhai Nie, Tsong Yueh Chen
abstract: Metamorphic Testing (MT) is a powerful technique to alleviate the test oracle problem of DNN testing. At its core is a set of Metamorphic Relations (MRs), which indicate necessary properties that multiple test inputs and their outputs should satisfy. Recently, researchers have proposed to compose multiple individual MRs to create new Composite Metamorphic Relations (CMRs), in the hope of enhancing the cost-effectiveness of MT. However, the potential benefit of such compositions is yet to be systematically explored for DNN based systems. In this paper, we present the first empirical study that investigates the relative test effectiveness of CMRs over individual MRs in DNN testing. Our experiment is performed under five popular image recognition models and seven representative component MRs, based on which a total of 3,612 CMRs and more than 200 million pairs of source and follow-up test images are generated and exercised. The experimental results highlight the advantages of employing CMRs in DNN testing, as they generally exhibit superior failure and fault revelation effectiveness compared to any individual MR. Moreover, to further illuminate the conditions that allow CMRs to maximise their performance in DNN testing, we propose to analyse the geometric relationships of MRs in the latent embedding space of DNNs, thereby quantifying the extent to which different MRs complement each other. Accordingly, by opting for highly complementary MRs in composition, we can create CMRs that are most likely to enhance test effectiveness.

title:
First-Principle Investigation of Optical Responses in Pristine and Oxygen- Modified g- C
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    N
                    <sub>4</sub>
authors: Soumik Kumar Kundu, Prativa Saha, Soumyadeep Seth, Samit Karmakar, G. S. Taki
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: A large amount of research has been devoted to determining what materials are suitable as photocatalysts for splitting water by using solar radiation to generate hydrogen and oxygen gas as products. The recent interest in sustainable energy and clean water has led to an increase in research activity relating to photocatalysts, specifically Photocatalytic Water-Splitting Materials. Graphitic carbon nitride (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$g-\mathrm{C}_{3} ~\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex>), a low-cost and chemically stable photocatalyst, is a leading candidate for use in water-splitting reactions due to its ability to absorb a range of visible wavelengths. Due to limitations associated with not absorbing enough visible light and the quick recombination of the photo generated charge carriers, the photocatalytic performance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{g}-\mathrm{C}_{3} ~\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex> is still low. To better understand the electronic behavior of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$g-C_{3} N_{4}$</tex> as impacted by the introduction of oxygen, Density Functional Theory (DFT) will be utilized to study how the properties are affected, and therefore investigate the microscopic effects of oxygen doping of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$g-\mathrm{C}_{3} ~\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex> throughout the visible light range. The electronic properties of three types of oxygen doping have been determined for both pristine g-C <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex> and g-C <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex> with the respective type(s) of oxygen doping: substitutional, interstitial, and add-atom doping configurations. The band structure and density of states for the three different doping types were analyzed, and their electronic properties systematically compared to those of pristine <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{g}-\mathrm{C}_{3} ~\mathrm{N}_{4}$</tex>. The results from these calculations indicate that oxygen doping greatly modifies the electronic structure and energy levels and band gap of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$g-C_{3} N_{4}$</tex>. The lowest electronic band gap value for the three doping types is for the substitutional oxygen doping. The decrease in the Band Gap of substitutional doping can be explained by the strong hybridization that exists between the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O$</tex> 2p and C/N 2p orbitals of the material.

title: Analytical and Experimental Validation of Wireless Authentication through Enhanced RF Fingerprints of Chaotic Antenna Arrays
authors: Thomas Ranstrom, Omar Jebreil, Fawaz Abdul Razak, Yasin Yilmaz, Gokhan Mumcu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Chaotic antenna arrays (CAAs) have been shown to produce enhanced and robust RF fingerprints, enabling more reliable device authentication using machine learning (ML) compared to traditional methods based on subtle hardware imperfections. In this paper, we present a novel CAA-specific multipath channel model to accurately capture the CAA’s phase errors across all propagation paths providing a basis for processing schemes that remove the channel effect, enabling extraction of the RF signature of the CAA. In addition, we provide a comprehensive experimental validation of CAA-based authentication under practical wireless channel conditions and include full details covering the design, fabrication, and characterization of custom CAA nodes, along with their integration within a software-defined radio (SDR) testbed for over-the-air measurements. This manuscript shows, for the first time, that training of the ML-based authenticator on the CAA RF fingerprints can be conducted under line-of-sight (LOS) conditions and effectively generalized to diverse scenarios, including non-line-of-sight (NLOS) environments, as long as a dominant propagation path exists. High authentication accuracy is consistently achieved when this key spatial channel characteristic is preserved. Accuracy exceeds 90% in LOS and reaches up to 87% in NLOS conditions with a single dominant reflected path. This study provides the first practical validation of spatially varying CAA fingerprints, underscoring their promise for secure and robust physical layer authentication across varied wireless conditions.

title: AI-Driven Automation of Semiconductor Device Design Integrating Silvaco TCAD
authors: Arijit Mondal, Ankit Biswas, Indranil Maity
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: With continued semiconductor miniaturization, device architectures had become increasingly complex and had relied heavily on Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD), particularly Silvaco TCAD, for design and optimization. However, extensive design space exploration had remained impractical due to high computational cost and frequent simulation non-convergence. An intelligent, device-agnostic AI-agentic framework had therefore been developed to automate TCAD workflows under the paradigm of Agentic Science. The framework had integrated Bayesian Optimization with three core modules: a deep-learning surrogate model for objective approximation, a classification model for infeasible parameter detection, and a Retrieval-Augmented Generation engine for enforcing physics-based constraints. Validation had been demonstrated across three case studies. Power conversion efficiency in a perovskite solar cell had been improved to 17.73%, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{ON}} / I_{\text{OFF}}$</tex> ratio in a charge plasma-based junctionless transistor had increased by 47.4%, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 2 \%}$</tex> improvement had been achieved in a pentacene-based organic thin-film transistor. Autonomous identification and suppression of ambipolar leakage through mobility optimization had further demonstrated expert-level reasoning. Overall computational time had been reduced by up to 96%.

title: Investigating the Factors Affecting University Students' Adoption of Generative AI through PLS-SEM and Machine Learning
authors: Cheng-Feng Wu, Chei-Chang Chiou, Meng-Chen Lin, Chieh-Jung Chang, Keng Sheng Ting
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Research on behavioral intentions to adopt generative artificial intelligence (AI) has only emerged in recent years and has mainly focused on Western, African, Middle Eastern, or other non-Chinese regions, leaving the unique context of Chinese higher education relatively underexplored. This study aims to examine the facilitating, inhibiting, and moderating factors influencing Chinese university students' use of generative AI for learning purposes. The empirical results of the PLS-SEM model indicate that facilitating factors such as performance expectancy, hedonic motivation, habit, perceived trust, and personal innovativeness significantly affect Chinese university students' behavioral intention to use generative AI. Furthermore, the results of the machine learning CatBoost model and SHAP analysis reveal that the relative importance of predictors of Chinese university students' behavioral intention to use generative <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A I}$</tex> is ranked as follows: habit, personal innovativeness, perceived trust, hedonic motivation, and performance expectancy. Due to the unique context of Chinese university students, inhibiting factors such as academic guilt and privacy concerns do not exert significant influence on their behavioral intention to use generative AI. Gender moderates only the effect of price value, while no moderating effects are observed on other facilitating or inhibiting factors. This study expands the scope of technology adoption research.

title: Stock Price Prediction and Investment Recommendations using Deep Learning
authors: G N Nirupama, Dhrishya Shetty, Shabari Shedthi B, Keerthi Shetty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The stock price analysis is a big challenge owing to the inherent volatility, non-linearity, and dynamic nature of financial markets. Deep learning approach towards stock price forecasting using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), GRU+LSTM, and RNN+GRU networks to learn from historical stock data of Microsoft, Apple, and Amazon from the years 2020 to 2025. The data is preprocessed by calculating average daily prices from the open and close prices which is then followed by normalization and conversion into time step sequences for temporal modeling. The model is programmed to extract relationships over a window of 60 days to make predictions about future stock prices. GRU got good with an $\text{R}^{2}$ score of 0.987. Besides accurate prediction, the system also provides daily suggestions about trading moves like Buy, Sell, and Hold features and visual trend representation. These results show the robustness of LSTM models for multi-stock financial time-series prediction.

title: Efficient ML-Based Prediction for All-Optical AND Gate Design in 2D Photonic Crystals
authors: Gnanakumar D, Vineeth Palliyembil
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The field of optical computing is rapidly advancing toward efficient device design and optimized parameter tuning for all-optical logic gates. However, achieving an optimized design remains a major bottleneck in conventional techniques. In order to mitigate this challenge, a machine learning (ML) based design methodology is introduced. This work proposes an efficient ML-based prediction model for an all-optical AND gate design using a two-dimensional (2D) photonic crystal (PhC). The proposed PhC structure achieves a contrast ratio of 10.79 dB, indicating reliable performance for optical component design. Furthermore, the design is investigated using, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and eXtreme Gradient Boost (XGBoost) regression techniques. Among these methods, KNN regression provides the optimized rod radius with maximum output power. The proposed ML-based AND gate structure obtains a minimum Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.001987. The results indicate that the proposed approach is well suited for future optical computing technologies.

title: CAMS-UNet: Context-Aware Multi-Scale U-Net for Brain Tumor Segmentation
authors: Peerapas Chinnapatpakdeekul
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Brain tumor segmentation is challenging due to the high heterogeneity in tumor size and shape. Standard U-Net architectures, relying on fixed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> convolutional kernels, often struggle to capture features across varying scales effectively. In response to this challenge, we introduce CAMS-UNet, a novel architecture designed to integrate a dynamic multi-scale perception mechanism directly into the encoder. The proposed CAMS-Block integrates two pathways: 1) a Multi-Scale Path utilizing parallel convolutions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 1$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7 \times 7$</tex>) to capture finegrained to coarse contextual details simultaneously, and 2) an Attention Path employing a Squeeze-and-Excitation mechanism with Softmax normalization to adaptively calibrate and select the most relevant receptive fields for each pixel. We evaluated CAMSUNet on the BraTS 2020 benchmark. Experimental results demonstrate that CAMS-UNet outperforms the baseline U-Net (DSC 0.5232) with a Dice Score of 0.5245 and achieves higher Sensitivity (0.5456), indicating an improved ability to detect tumor boundaries and minimize false negatives. These findings confirm that substituting fixed kernels with context-aware multiscale aggregation significantly enhances the model's robustness in handling heterogeneous anatomical structures.

title: Hybrid Graph and Attention-Based Models for Multi-Aspect E-Commerce Recommendations: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Richa Sawant, Kishor Mane
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: it has been the consequences of the exponential growth of e-commerce platforms that cause the generation of big, heterogeneous, and multi-platform data and pose challenges to the delivery of personalized and context-sensitive recommendations. The outdated methods of recommendation such as collaborative filtering and matrix factorization are unable to reflect the complicated relational patterns among users, products and platforms. The recent developments in Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) and attention mechanisms have demonstrated great potential in terms of capturing and modeling structural relations and learning to weight features adaptively to support multi-aspect learning. In this review paper, different research methods based on deep learning, graph-based, attention-driven, and hybrid architectures in recommendation are thoroughly analyzed. The important results are that as GCNs and attention have independent positive effects on the quality of recommendations, the combined methods of handling cross-platform heterogeneity are under-researched. The paper indicates a lack of research, such as scalability, interpretability and multi-modal integration, and suggests future research requirements to create more sophisticated GCN-attention hybrid systems to create personalized and real-time e-commerce recommendations.

title: AI-Enabled Posthumous Identity Protection Using Advanced Cryptographic and Governance Mechanisms
authors: Priya Pal, Rishabh Tiwari, Varun Shukla, Abhay Soni, Shivansh Kushwaha, Atul
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital platforms has raised serious concerns during the management of of digital footprints including the ever emerging digital afterlife assets. Digital AfterLife Protection Framework (DAPF) addresses this challenge by offering a approach to the preservation of personal data and digital accounts of deceased individuals. DAPF death record integration, identity verification system access has multiple authentication factors using: block chained-based systems, asymmetric encrypted signatures, electro-sanitization of ciphertext, and digital signatures. Secure handling of posthumous identities is combined with fraud tracking to manage systems using advanced anomaly detection. Ethically, a user preference allows individuals to choose actions to be taken with their digital assets post mortem. DAPF is policy driven, transparent automated, and refined to assist and improve responsible digital legacy management to dermal service providers, decision makers, and policy makers.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning Models for Hourly Reference Evapotranspiration Prediction
authors: Pattharaporn Thongnim, Sueppong Mueanchamnong, Wansiri Khooriphan, Patcharawadee Poolsamran, Nopparat Panngam
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study evaluates the performance of three machine learning models Support Vector Machine (SVM), Artificial Neural Network (ANN) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) for predicting hourly Reference Evapotranspiration <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(E T_{o})$</tex> using real meteorological data from August 2021 to September 2025. The dataset was split into 70% for training and 30% for testing with cross-validation applied to the training data to improve model reliability. The model performance was evaluated using the coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(R^{2})$</tex> and root mean square error (RMSE). For daily <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E T_{o}$</tex> totals, SVM predicted the highest values for 1, 3, and 5 day forecasts while ANN and KNN produced more moderate and consistent values. These findings indicate that SVM tends to overestimate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E T_{o}$</tex> whereas ANN gives more stable and realistic predictions. Therefore, ANN is the most suitable model for short-term <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E T_{o}$</tex> forecasting and can be applied as a decision support tool for irrigation planning and smart water management.

title: Research on Anti-Collision Strategy for Automated Loading System Based on 3D LiDAR
authors: Xinyan Mao, Zhaolin Chen, Enbo Chen, Mengyan Cheng, Guangwei Xiao, Qiwang Yu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper addresses the collision prevention issues in the automated loading process by conducting research on anticollision strategies based on 3D LiDAR. Through analyzing the characteristics of loading operations and types of collision risks, a multi-sensor fusion perception system with 3D LiDAR as the core is designed. A layered anti-collision control strategy and safety distance model are proposed, and real-time collision detection algorithms and collision avoidance path planning methods are developed. Application results show that the designed algorithm has a response time of less than 100 msec. The system can effectively prevent various types of collisions during the loading process, significantly reduce damage to cigarette cases, and improve loading efficiency. It provides a reliable safety assurance technical solution for automated loading systems and offers an effective technical approach for achieving safe automated loading operations in complex industrial environments.

title: HRNet Neural Network-based Localization Method for Spot Center in Free-space Optical Communication
authors: Bosen Lian, Xiaoliang Li, Lei Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: In optical communication systems, when the spot energy exhibits a non-ideal Gaussian distribution due to the influence of the atmospheric channel, issues such as difficulty in accurately locating the center of the spot arise, severely compromising the stability of the optical communication system. This study employs the power spectrum inversion method to generate random phase screens and simulates three atmospheric turbulence environments using a spatial light modulator. Image data of Gaussian beam spots modulated by the random phase screens are collected. Based on this, a spot center localization algorithm utilizing grid processing and an HRNet neural network is proposed. The algorithm inputs the sequence of effective response areas of each grid into a pre-trained HRNet neural network to accurately predict the coordinates of the spot center. Experimental results demonstrate that the absolute error of the spot center coordinates predicted by this algorithm is significantly reduced compared to benchmark algorithms, amounting to only 69.0% of that of the centroid algorithm, 61.4% of that of the Gaussian fitting method, and 46.7% of that of the convolutional neural network. This algorithm effectively improves the accuracy of locating the center of the spot and significantly mitigates the impact of the atmospheric channel on the precision of spot center localization.

title: Preface and Committee Member
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)

title: Prediction of Aircraft Landing Impact Loads Using a 1D-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention Model
authors: Haifeng Wang, Ruipeng Zhou, Dejun Song, Yi Chen, Yu Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of predicting landing loads in carrier-based aircraft by proposing a deep learning architecture that integrates a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN), bidirectional long shortterm memory (BiLSTM) networks, and an attention mechanism, termed the 1D-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model. The model utilizes sensor data acquired from the aircraft full-scale drop test. The 1D-CNN module is employed to extract multi-scale local features, including shock absorber compression velocity and airframe attitude data. Bidirectional temporal dependencies are captured using BiLSTM, and an attention mechanism is incorporated to emphasize critical phases of the impact event. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves substantially higher predictive accuracy compared to conventional LSTM and CNN-LSTM models. The model accurately predicts both the time-history variations and peak values of landing loads under unseen operating conditions. This research offers an efficient, data-driven approach for predicting carrier-based aircraft landing loads and assessing landing safety.

title: Design of Pedal Sensor Simulating Signal on Brake Control Algorithm HIL Test Platform for Civil Aircraft
authors: Xing Gu, Bang Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The brake control algorithm HIL test platform simulates the actual working scene of brake system control algorithm on the aircraft through real-time running simulation model and real brake system hardware. Based on this platform, a brake pedal sensor signal simulation device was designed using physical sensors and analog boards, which can not only perform human-machine interaction but also flexibly configure the signals, greatly facilitating the HIL tests for brake control algorithm.

title: Improving COVID-19 Detection Using Transfer Learning on Chest X-Ray Images with a Modified ResNet50 Architecture
authors: Soumyasis Das, Md Ankushavali, Neha Sharma, Ritika Badhani, Ashwani Kharola
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The study paper will provide a deep learning model of the detection of the COVID-19 in X-ray chest images. The offered methodology involves a convolutional neural network based on ResNet50 improved by Global Average Pooling, Batch Normalization and full connected-layers dropout regularization. Figure 1 The data consist of three classes, namely, Covid, Normal, and Viral Pneumonia at 66 test images (26 COVID-19, 20 Normal, and 20 Viral Pneumonia). Techniques that augment the data through flipping, rotation, zoom and contrast are enhanced to enhance generalization. The model had a strong precision, recall, and F1-scores for establishing a total accuracy of $93.94 \%$. Confusion matrices were employed as well as training curves were used to evaluate performance. The results show that transfer learning can quickly and efficiently predict COVID-19 cases, and such a noticeable approach to medical imaging is scalable and repeatable, which can be used in other studies on the subject.

title: Engineering Gallium-Silk Fibroin-Gelatin Porous Scaffolds for Tissue Regenration
authors: Eman Abdulla, Aya Al-Ani, Amaal Abdulraqeb Ali, Vincent Chan, Vi Khanh Truong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: The increasing demand for effective materials in tissue regeneration has positioned bone tissue engineering (BTE) as a critical area for innovation beyond conventional bone repair strategies. BTE seeks to develop scaffolds capable of promoting osteointegration through enhanced cell adhesion, proliferation, and tissue regeneration. Natural polymers such as gelatin and silk fibroin have emerged as attractive scaffold candidates due to their inherent biocompatibility, biodegradability, low cost, and abundance. However, their limited mechanical strength necessitates reinforcement with functional nanomaterials. Among emerging candidates, gallium-based liquid metal nanoparticles (GaLM NPs) have gained attention for their favorable conductivity, antimicrobial activity, and cytocompatibility. In this study, porous scaffolds composed of a gelatin-silk fibroin matrix reinforced with GaLM NPs were fabricated using a freeze-drying process, following homogeneous nanoparticle dispersion via probe sonication. Structural characterization confirmed successful nanoparticle incorporation without disrupting the secondary structures of the native proteins. The resulting scaffolds exhibited an interconnected porous architecture with variable pore sizes, although the majority were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<50 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>-smaller than ideal for optimal bone ingrowth. Short-term swelling studies in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) over 3 days revealed minimal dimensional changes, indicating good initial aqueous stability. Moreover, the scaffolds exhibited electrical conductivity, a property increasingly recognized for its positive influence on osteogenesis. These preliminary findings suggest that GaLM NP-reinforced gelatin-silk fibroin scaffolds are promising candidates for BTE applications. However, further optimization is required. Future work should focus on tailoring pore size distribution to meet bone ingrowth thresholds <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(>100 \mu \mathrm{m})$</tex>, assessing long-term structural integrity in physiologically relevant conditions, evaluating mechanical performance under load, and conducting comprehensive in vitro assays to determine cytocompatibility, cell attachment, and proliferation behavior.

title: Quantum-Inspired Machine Learning Models for Secure Cryptographic Communication
authors: Sheela Hundekari, Anurag Shrivastava, Saif O. Husain, Rakesh Kumar, Manish Gupta, Ganesh Maurya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The escalating sophistication of cyber threats and the looming advent of fault-tolerant quantum computers necessitate a paradigm shift in cryptographic communication. Classical cryptographic protocols, which underpin modern digital security, are vulnerable to being rendered obsolete by quantum algorithms, particularly Shor’s algorithm for integer factorization. This paper investigates the emergent field of quantum-inspired machine learning (QIML) as a transformative approach to fortifying secure communication channels. By leveraging computational principles from quantum mechanics, such as superposition, entanglement, and interference, within classical machine learning frameworks, QIML models demonstrate a enhanced capability for complex pattern recognition and anomaly detection in encrypted data streams. This research systematically explores the theoretical foundations of QIML, surveys its application in cryptographic key distribution, protocol verification, and intrusion detection, and critically analyzes its potential to construct cryptographic systems that are resilient to both classical and quantumanalogous attacks. The synthesis presented herein posits that quantum-inspired classical models offer a pragmatic and powerful interim defense, bridging the gap until the widespread deployment of fully quantum cryptographic networks.

title: Lightweight CNN for Fingerprint Spoof Detection in Resource-Constrained Environments
authors: Lakshmi Boppanna, Punit Sharma, Abhinay Kumar
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Fingerprint authentication, while widely used in biometric security, remains vulnerable to spoofing attacks through artificial fingerprints. This work presents a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based fingerprint spoof detection system designed for real-time deployment on embedded hardware. The proposed model is trained on grayscale images preprocessed through resizing, normalization, and reshaping and optimized using binary cross-entropy loss with the Adam optimizer. Implemented on a Raspberry Pi integrated with an optical fingerprint sensor, the system was tested with genuine and spoof fingerprints fabricated using glue-wax mixtures. Performance evaluation using accuracy, precision, recall, F1score, and confusion matrices demonstrates a detection accuracy of 97 %, confirming the robustness of the approach. The results highlight the system's suitability for enhancing the security of biometric authentication applications, particularly in resource-constrained environments.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Online Guidance Strategy for Hypersonic Gliding Vehicles
authors: Xurui Wang, Guoxin Qu, Lianghua Sun, Minghui Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the problem of end-to-end three-dimensional trajectory performance optimization for hypersonic gliding vehicles (HGV), this paper proposes an online guidance method based on deep reinforcement learning. Using the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) algorithm, a reward function is designed to incentivize the vehicle to rapidly align toward the target. The approach directly maps flight states to control commands, including angle of attack and sideslip angle. Simulation results show that this method can achieve fast and accurate online computation; it ensures high terminal accuracy while maintaining strong generalization capabilities across different initial conditions. Compared with the proportional guidance method, this method has better flight trajectory performance.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: Insecurity of Forward Secure Equality Test for Secure Data Sharing in Healthcare Systems
authors: Jiseung Kim, Hyung Tae Lee
abstract: Recently, a forward secure identity-based encryption with equality test (FS-IBEET) scheme was proposed for secure data sharing in healthcare systems and was claimed to achieve indistinguishability against adaptive chosen-identity and chosen-ciphertext attacks (IND-ID-CCA). In this letter, we show that the proposed scheme fails to provide the claimed IND-ID-CCA security. We present a practical chosen-ciphertext attack that exploits a structural design flaw. In particular, achieving INDID-CCA security appears infeasible under the current framework, where the ciphertext publicly reveals the time slot in conjunction with the 0/1-encodings. This vulnerability is critical, as it fundamentally compromises the confidentiality guarantees essential to healthcare IoT environments, potentially leading to the corruption or leakage of patient records.

title: Non-Invasive Early Detection of Lithium-Ion Battery Heating Using CAN–BMS Temperature Forecasting
authors: Tero Niemi, Tomi Pitkäaho, Juha Röning
abstract: Temperature monitoring is critical for lithium-ion battery packs during transportation and warehousing, yet common surface-based methods (e.g., thermocouples or infrared thermography) suffer from thermal lag and may indicate internal heating too late. Modern battery management systems (BMS) already measure pack temperature using embedded sensors and broadcast the value over the Controller Area Network (CAN) bus. This paper presents a non-invasive early-warning approach that reads BMS-reported pack temperature from CAN (the arithmetic mean of three factory-installed NTC sensors between modules), decodes it using a manufacturer-provided DBC description, and forecasts temperature over 120 s, 300 s, and 600 s horizons. Three lightweight predictors are compared: a Kalman-based trend model, linear extrapolation, and a persistence baseline. Forecast accuracy is evaluated using one experimental charge–discharge cycling test, and threshold-anticipation performance (lead time relative to 40 °C and 60 °C) is evaluated using two controlled heating simulations. The experimental dataset is further used to compare CAN–BMS temperature with pack-surface thermocouples and to quantify internal-to-surface thermal lag. Results show that CAN–BMS temperature responds substantially earlier than the pack surface, and that multi-horizon forecasting can provide several minutes of additional reaction time for threshold-based warnings in the simulated heating scenarios. The findings support CAN-accessible temperature forecasting as a practical, non-invasive safety monitoring layer for lithium-ion battery transport, warehousing, and recycling operations.

title: Dynamic Hypernetwork Grouping with Diffusion-Based Sampler for Heterogeneous Federated Intrusion Detection_supp1-3674493.pdf
authors: Jinhu Lu
abstract: Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems are vulnerable to cyber-attacks due to their interconnected nature. While federated learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving approach to intrusion detection, it struggles with data heterogeneity, class imbalance, and adversarial attacks in practical IIoT settings. To address these challenges, this paper introduces HGDS, a FL-based framework that integrates a federated diffusion-based sampler for class rebalancing with a dynamic hypernetwork grouping mechanism for model personalization. The proposed framework further incorporates robust defense strategies, including gradient clipping and anomaly-based client isolation, to mitigate adversarial attacks. Comprehensive evaluations are conducted on four real world datasets. The results demonstrate the superiority of HGDS, which achieves a performance gain of up to 15.3% in F1-score over state-of-the-art benchmarks under non-IID data conditions. Furthermore, the method maintains resilience against both model poisoning and label flipping attacks.

title: Towards Substation Semantic Segmentation: A benchmark dataset and a cross-attention embedded hierarchical network
authors: Qiaoyun Wu, Yu Xia, Cheng Yi, Yuan Zhang, Jun Wang
abstract: The application of 3D semantic segmentation in substation scenarios remains challenging due to the lack of specialized datasets and the limitations of existing methods in handling complex substation point clouds. To address this, we introduce ElecsDataset, a large-scale 3D semantic segmentation benchmark, featuring meticulously annotated point clouds with intricate equipment structures. Additionally, we design ElecsNet, a novel hierarchical feature learning network embedded with cross-attention mechanisms to tackle the unique complexities of substation scenes. ElecsNet integrates local convolutions to capture fine-grained geometric details and a cross-attention interaction mechanism is introduced to dynamically fuse features, enabling adaptive information exchange between geometric and contextual representations. This design strengthens the discriminative power of features for semantically ambiguous and heavily occluded objects, which are common in substation settings. Extensive experiments on the ElecsDataset demonstrate that ElecsNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming existing methods by at least 0.3% in overall accuracy and 1.5% in mean IoU. The release of ElecsDataset and the proposed framework provide a foundational benchmark for advancing 3D scene understanding in power infrastructure, paving the way for intelligent inspection and automation in smart grid systems.

title: Thank You for Your Authorship
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: Explainable Ensemble Learning for Environmental, Social, Governance Score Prediction Through Rule Extraction and Pruning
authors: Rubab Hafeez, Tufail Muhammad, Waqas Bin Khidmat, Mustajab Hussain
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) indicators have become critical determinants in sustainable investment and regulatory decision-making. However, building models that deliver both high predictive accuracy and interpretability remains a major challenge. This paper presents an explainable ensemble learning framework that enhances the interpretability of ESG score prediction through rule extraction and pruning. The proposed approach applies a structured preprocessing, feature encoding, and Random Forest-based ensemble learning. Further, decision rules are extracted and locally pruned to eliminate redundancy while preserving predictive power. The refined rules are aggregated using a weighted voting mechanism for multi-class ESG classification. Experimental evaluation on firm-level ESG datasets demonstrates that the proposed approach achieves competitive predictive accuracy (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.90$</tex>, Precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.72$</tex>) while producing concise, humaninterpretable rules. enhancing transparency and actionable insights for sustainable finance.

title: Q-Learning-Based Mobility-Aware Offloading for Multi-Tier Vehicular Edge and Cloud Networks
authors: Afzal Badshah, Ali Daud, Umar Farooq
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: The rapid growth of Intelligent Transportation System (ITS) leads to continuous generation of vehicular data that must be processed under latency and connectivity constraints on heterogeneous computing infrastructures (e.g., Vehicular Edge Computing (VEC), Mobile Edge Computing (MEC), and Cloud Computing (CC)). Under these conditions, task offloading must account for mobility dynamics and server status to efficiently manage communication delay, cost, and computational resources. To address this challenge, this work introduces a mobilityaware offloading mechanism using Q-learning, which decides on the optimal tier. The decision process incorporates real-time indicators, including server utilization, signal quality, and Base Station (BS) handover patterns, allowing the scheduler to adapt to changing environments proactively. The proposed solution favors VEC when sufficient resources exist, shifts to MEC during VEC over-utilization, and selects CC only as a last option. A customized reward model guides the learning agent toward resource-efficient policies while penalizing unnecessary tier switching. The approach is implemented and assessed using DriveNetSim, a specially built vehicular simulator that realistically emulates mobility, signal attenuation, and BS switching. Evaluation results show that the proposed method achieves up to 43% reduction in communication delay and 38% lower processing cost, demonstrating its suitability for highly dynamic vehicular networks.

title: Oscillating Eel Robots that Leverage Collisions for Controllable Motions_supp1-3674679.mp4
authors: Steven Ceron
abstract: We introduce a platform of oscillating eel-like robots that exploits physical collisions to enable controllable collective behaviors. Each robot is actuated by a Kuramoto-style central pattern generator, and produces undulatory motion that passively synchronizes through collisions with neighboring robots and a surrounding rigid membrane. Instead of avoiding collisions, the system leverages robot-robot and robot-environment interactions to bias collective forces and torques. Using motor current as a surrogate for joint torque, we analyze how actuation parameters enable specific collective motions, and by tuning a small set of global central pattern generator parameters and selectively locking joints or robots, the collective can be programmed to exhibit clockwise and counterclockwise rotation, orbiting with adjustable curvature, and directed translation. We further demonstrate that the collective behaviors can be switched between on demand to enable trajectory following, obstacle avoidance, and object manipulation.

title: Real-Time Water Fraud Detection Using Face Recognition and Entry-Exit Monitoring
authors: Sneha Mandal, Sourish Sarkar, Subhabrata Banerjee, Malay Gangopadhyaya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: As global water availability continues to decline, fraud in water extraction and distribution poses a significant threat to resource sustainability. This paper aims to detect fraudulent activities related to water misuse by employing a novel face detection and matching based monitoring approach. The system uses a dual stage face matching technique. First, a static image is provided to register the user in the system. The image is then processed using a pair of models which include MTCNN for face detection and InceptionResNetV3 for embedding generation. In parallel, live video footage is captured and processed through RetinaFace for face detection, followed by InceptionResNetV3 for embedding.

title: A Review on Trends and Challenges in Fake News Detection Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Chanchal Sahu, Ronak Jain, Narendra Pal Singh Rathore, Praveen Bhanodia, Kamal K Sethi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Digital news platforms have altered how information is consumed, but they have also weakened traditional mechanisms of credibility assessment. In real-world settings, misleading content frequently achieves wide visibility before verification can occur, making automated detection essential. Instead of cataloging individual algorithms, this paper analyzes fake news detection approaches based on how they operate under practical constraints. Detection strategies are discussed in terms of linguistic analysis, supervised learning, neural representation models, and knowledge-aware systems, with attention to their deployment trade-offs. Recent trends involving transformer-based models, graph-oriented reasoning, and multimodal analysis are examined from an applicationfocused perspective. The study also reflects on how commonly used datasets shape experimental conclusions and reproducibility. In addition, practical aspects such as data imbalance, computational overhead, and scalability constraints are considered, as these factors strongly influence real-world adoption. The discussion emphasizes the need to align experimental design with deployment requirements observed in dynamic online environments. Ongoing challenges-including adaptive misinformation strategies, language mixing, and limited interpretability-are highlighted to emphasize gaps between research and deployment. The paper concludes by identifying directions that prioritize robustness, transparency, and real-time usability.

title: N Truths and a Lie: Consistency-based Backdoor Defense for Vertical Federated Learning
authors: Xiaoya Ma, Zijun Zhang, Kun He, Jing Chen, Ruiying Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Recently, a growing number of backdoor attacks have been implemented against vertical federated learning (VFL). To carry out such attacks, it is possible for an attacker to manipulate its local data and model, which are not accessible to the defender. This asymmetry complicates the design of defense mechanisms, resulting in a lack of effective defenses against backdoor attacks in VFL. In this paper, we propose a novel backdoor defense mechanism tailored for VFL. Specifically, we observe that the malicious embeddings provided by an attacker are often inconsistent with the embeddings provided by honest VFL participants and thus are less predictable from the latter. On the basis of this observation, we introduce a latent masked autoencoder (LMAE) to assess the semantic consistency of the embeddings provided by different VFL participants. On the basis of the LMAE, we further develop an algorithm to identify attackers and enable backdoor-resistant predictions. To evaluate the proposed defense, we conduct experiments involving four baseline defenses, seven backdoor attacks, and five datasets (CIFAR-10, CINIC-10, Yahoo Answers, BHI, and BM) of different modalities. The results show that our defense is effective at identifying attackers and achieves high prediction accuracy. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the defense is robust against various attacks in a wide range of scenarios.

title: Quantum-Ready ALU: Optimized Design Using Reversible Logic with RTL Design and FPGA Implementation
authors: Agnik Maity, Sumedha Pal, Asmita Sarkar, Bhavishek Mishra, Debjit Roy, Mihir Lal Saha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Reversible logic provides a foundation for nextgeneration computing systems, particularly in quantum and emerging nanotechnology-based architectures. Unlike conventional logic, reversible gates preserve information and support backward computation, making them crucial for designing quantum circuits. This paper introduces a reversible Arithmetic and Logic Unit (ALU) that performs eight fundamental operations-AND, OR, NOT, XOR, addition, subtraction, increment, and decrement-using Fredkin, Peres, TR, and modified Fredkin gates. A dedicated control unit manages operation selection, ensuring functional flexibility and circuit efficiency. The proposed design achieves significant improvements by minimizing ancilla inputs, quantum cost and garbage outputs, resulting in a total quantum cost of 71 and contains 16 reversible gates and 14 garbage outputs. The architecture was modeled and verified using Xilinx Vivado HLS and ISE 14.7, and implemented on the Nexys A7 (Artix-7) FPGA platform. Comparative analysis demonstrates better efficiency than existing reversible ALU designs, establishing the proposed circuit as a strong candidate for quantum computing and other advanced computing paradigms.

title: Object Detection and Recognition Framework Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: J Srikanth, B. V. Chowdary, Mandalapu Nikhitha, Vyshnayi Reddy, Jakkula Akshay
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Visually Impaired is one among the most challenging issues of the top ten disabilities in people. There are at least 2.2 billion blind or visually impaired persons in the world. To help them move around, the blind people generally depend on white canes, magnifiers, screen reading software, glasses, and guide dogs. Here, a variety of models and techniques are used to identify and recognize objects. For those people who are vision-challenged, identifying and detecting everyday objects in their environment appears to been an impossible undertaking. Our effort contributes to the development of a reliable and pleasant system that enables the blind identifies the things in their environment. The project was developed using the SSD, CNN and Inception V3 algorithms.

title: A Closed-Loop Adaptive System for Personalized Yoga Therapy Using Computer Vision
authors: Shalini Singh, Shreyash Singh, Sayan Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: YogaWise is an adaptive and personalized yoga coaching tool combining computer vision techniques (posture recognition) with rule based methods to deliver coaching that is both safety oriented and directed toward a user's goals. As part of its decision-making process YogaWise uses criteria specific to each user (e.g., user's age, body mass index (BMI), health conditions, and fitness goals). This data is used to create userspecific yoga routines that eliminate postures that are inappropriate for the user. The posture recognition was performed using a convolutional neural network (CNN) based on VGG16 and the landmark detection (MediaPipe Pose) to identify the location of the user's joints. The geometry of the angles between the identified landmarks provide a basis for determining when a deviation occurs from the expected range of motion and provide instantaneous corrective feedback. The accuracy reached 96.2% for the training set and 94.5% for the validation dataset. The development of YogaWise utilised a software architecture consisting of a Python-based back-end and a React-based front end and demonstrated consistent reliability and practical usability in real-world yoga and wellness applications

title: Phase Selection and Analysis for Multi-frequency Multi-user RIS Systems Employing Subsurfaces in Correlated Ricean and Rayleigh Environments
authors: Amy S. Inwood, Peter J. Smith, Philippa A. Martin, Graeme K. Woodward
abstract: Phase selection design for reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) is a significant research challenge, as a closed-form optimal solution for a multi-user (MU) system is believed to be intractable. While existing methods achieve strong near-optimal performance, they typically entail high computational complexity. In this work, we take a different approach and propose a practical method that achieves competitive performance while substantially reducing computational complexity. To do so, we consider a RIS divided into subsurfaces. Each subsurface is designed specifically for one user, who is served on their own frequency band. The other subsurfaces (those not designed for this user) provide additional uncontrolled scattering. We derive the exact closed-form expression for the mean signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) for the proposed subsurface design (SD) when all channels experience correlated Ricean fading. We simplify this to find the mean SNR for line-of-sight (LoS) channels and channels experiencing correlated Rayleigh fading. An iterative SD (ISD) process is proposed, where subsurfaces are designed sequentially, and the phases that are already set are used to enhance the design of the remaining subsurfaces. This is extended to a converged ISD (CISD), where the ISD process is repeated multiple times until the SNR increases by less than a specified tolerance. The ISD and CISD both provide a performance improvement over SD, which increases as the number of RIS elements increases. The SD is significantly simpler than the lowest complexity MU method we know of, and despite each user having less bandwidth, the SD outperforms the existing method in some key scenarios. The SD is more robust to strongly LoS channels and clustered users, as it does not rely on spatial multiplexing like other MU methods. Combined with the complexity reduction, this makes the SD an attractive phase selection method.

title: Solar Cell Power Plant Capacity Monitoring: A Hybrid Approach using Physics-Informed Modeling and Boosted Ensemble Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Emmanuel Ede Ogar, Watit Benjapolakul, Kenichi Kawabe, Surachai Chaitusaney
abstract: As solar photovoltaic (PV) systems become vital to sustainable energy, accurate monitoring of solar power plant capacity is essential for maximizing energy yield and maintaining operational reliability, especially under variable environmental conditions. This paper introduces a novel hybrid framework that uniquely integrates physics-informed feature engineering with boosted ensemble learning, AdaBoostM2 for classification and LSBoost for regression, to classify and predict the operational capacity of a 5 MW solar PV plant in Thailand. Leveraging meteorological data (global radiation, ambient/module temperatures) and electrical metrics (active power, generating capacity, and plant capacity) collected at hourly intervals for 365 days, the model accurately classifies plant performance into six operational modes: Optimal, Sub optimal, Severe sub-optimal, Critical, Emergency, and Offline, while predicting power output with robust precision. Unlike conventional physics-informed neural networks or data-driven ML, our approach embeds lightweight, interpretable physics constraints into tree-based ensembles, ensuring physical consistency, real-time scalability (<4 ms/sample inference), and causal interpretability via OOB feature importance. The AdaBoostM2 classifier achieves 100% test accuracy in mode classification, while the LSBoost regressor delivers a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.0276 MW and an R-squared (R²) of 0.9975, surpassing standalone ML baselines in relative error reduction and outperforming state-of-the-art PIML baselines by 95% in RMSE reduction, and 88% in MAE reduction. By embedding validated physical constraints into a data-driven approach, this framework enables early detection of critical inefficiencies, supporting operational uptime improvements and maintenance prioritization in tropical climates. This work advances beyond existing PIML models by offering a deployable, interpretable, high-precision tool for sustainable solar asset management.

title: Comparison Analysis of the Accuracy of Machine Learning Models to Predict Customer Churn in Telecom Industry
authors: Subhadra Bhattacharyya, Amit Kumar Das
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: One of the telecom sector's most enduring problems is customer attrition, which has a direct impact on long-term viability and profitability. In order to create a precise and comprehensible churn prediction model using the Telco Customer Churn dataset, this study compares three supervised machine learning algorithms: Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). To improve model generalization, the dataset is preprocessed extensively using SMOTE-based class balance, label and one-hot encoding of categorical features, imputation of missing values, and feature scaling. Key churn factors, including contract type, duration, internet service type, and monthly rates, are identified using correlation heatmaps and exploratory data analysis (EDA). Confusion matrix interpretation is used to help the evaluation of model performance utilizing Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. With an accuracy of 86.44% and an F1-score of 81.53%, XGBoost performed better than other classifiers both in terms of modelling ability and reliability among the tested models. Higher monthly charges and shorter contractual terms were further identified as significant predictors of customer churn through feature importance. Finally, a model deployment pipeline was recommended for real-time customer churn detection and prediction in telecom CRMs that uses model serialization techniques (Pickle) and API connectivity (Flask/FastAPI). The above-mentioned trials and experiments verify the efficiency of ensemble methods such as XGBoost and the potential of these methodologies to develop active customer retention strategies even in real telecom companies.

title:
Broadband Photodetector Based on SnS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /CuO/WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Multilayer Structure
authors: Samuya Tripathi, Abhinay Dubey, Ajay Kumar Dwivedi, Lucky Agarwal, Tulika Bajpai, Shweta Tripathi
abstract: Multilayer photodetector covering a wide spectral range is a critical component of effective detection system applied in optical communication, laser radar imaging, biomedical detection, atmospheric, and astronomical observation applications. In this work, a heterojunction-based photodiode is proposed, which consists of a WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> layer as a hole transport layer (HTL), CuO/MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> heterojunction as an active layer, and SnS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> as an ETL grown over flexible indium tin oxide (ITO)-coated polyethylene terephthalate (PET) substrate. A low-cost solution method is used to fabricate the proposed device structure aluminum (Al)/SnS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/CuO/WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/ITO/PET. The experimental results show that the device has a broadband detection band covering from 300 to 1250nm because of the heterojunction and the absorption of SnS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> film in the device. The ultrahigh responsivity of the device at 1250nm is observed. At an incident power of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.118~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W and a reverse bias of −1V, the measured responsivity values (A/W), external quantum efficiencies, and sensitivity of the proposed device are 60.86 (UV spectrum), 96.34 (visible spectrum), 562.21 [near-infrared (NIR) spectrum], and 91 358.61%, 57 961.93%, and 292 911.1% and 61.12205, 71.72051, and 44.79 correspondingly. The analysis also considers the stability of the proposed construction. The high responsivity and external quantum efficiency (EQE) surpassing 100% are due to the trap-assisted photomultiplication (PM) phenomenon, which is generated by a defect in the active layers of the photodetector.

title: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
authors: N/A

title: MDCA Plate: A Two Stage License Plate Recognition System with Channel Attention and Multi-Dilation Features
authors: Huachao Chen, Yi Niu, Peiying Chen
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Traffic imagery from the wild contains small plates, blur, glare, dirt, and occlusion, which makes license plate recognition difficult. We present MDCA Plate, a two stage system that detects plates with YOLOv8 and recognizes cropped plates with a PaddleOCR based recognizer enhanced by two modules. The first module is a channel attention block with batch normalization and Swish activation that strengthens channel reweighting and stabilizes optimization. The second module is a multi dilation extractor with three parallel convolutions that aggregate fine strokes and broad context followed by attention guided fusion and a one by one convolution. The pipeline is fully reproducible on CCPD2019 with automatic split generation, file name driven label conversion, deterministic cropping, OCR style label files, and scripted training. The detector converges quickly and reaches mAP at IoU 0.50 of about 0.995, so recognition is the main lever for improvement. Under a shared protocol with identical detector and crops, the combined recognizer with multi dilation and channel attention attains the highest end to end accuracy on the challenge split with 54.37, surpassing the baseline with 53.98 and the single module variants with 51.47 for channel attention and 53.09 for multi dilation, while remaining near ceiling on the base split with 99.87. Training directly on the challenge domain yields 73.87 on challenge, highlighting domain shift rather than capacity as the principal bottleneck.

title: Neural Cognitive Diagnosis Models for Intelligent Adaptive Testing: A Systematic Evidence Synthesis
authors: Yasa Khalqiah, Indriana Hidayah, Guntur Dharma Putra
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Educational assessment systems face a critical challenge in the era of smart technology: standardized tests enable efficient classification but provide limited diagnostic insight into individual learning profiles. Neural Cognitive Diagnosis Models (NCDM) have emerged as an intelligent solution, combining the scalability of computerized testing with fine-grained skill-level diagnosis through deep learning architectures. However, their integration into operational adaptive testing systems remains largely unexplored. This systematic literature review examines 27 peer-reviewed NCDM studies (Q1/Q2 journals, 2019-2025) following PRISMA 2020 guidelines to evaluate NCDM effectiveness and personalization capabilities in adaptive testing contexts. Our analysis identifies seven distinct neural architectures, with higher-order attention networks and graph-based models achieving classification accuracy above 0.90 while maintaining interpretable skill diagnostics. Evidence from operational deployments demonstrates 15% reduction in test length and 23% improvement in item exposure control without compromising measurement precision. Successful NCDM implementation requires minimum datasets of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5, 0 0 0}$</tex> student records and careful model transparency considerations.

title: DSTD-YOLOv8: Enhanced YOLOv8 with Dense Small Target Detection Model for UAV Aerial Images
authors: ZhenGui Huang, GuoYong Lin, Le Li, YiFeng Huang, ShiGan Wu, KeLin Li
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: To address the challenges of insufficient detection accuracy for small objects in drone aerial imagery due to feature loss, and the existing YOLOv8 model's difficulty balancing lightweightness and detection performance in complex scenarios, this paper proposes a dense small object detection enhancement model, DSTD-YOLOv8, based on YOLOv8n. First, a multibranch feature fusion C2fSCDownC module is designed. This significantly reduces model parameters and computational overhead while preserving shallow, high-resolution features through residual connections, preventing feature degradation of small objects. Second, a new P2- layer small object detection head is added, which is deeply fused with the Backbone output features to address the semantic deficiencies of shallow features and the blurred details of deep features. Finally, the MPDIoU loss function replaces the traditional CIoU loss, incorporating bounding box vertex distance and input image width and height factors to optimize bounding box regression accuracy for non-overlapping scenes and objects of varying scales. Experiments on the VisDrone2019 dataset show that DSTD-YOLOv8 achieves 46.71% Precision, 37.04% Recall, and 36.29% mAP50. The mAP50-95 reaches 21.46%, which is 3.00%, 3.69%, 3.41% and 2.50% higher than YOLOv8 respectively. It is also superior to mainstream models such as YOLOv5, YOLOv10, YOLOv11 and YOLOv13. This proves that the model effectively improves the accuracy of small target detection in complex scenes and fully meets the requirements of dense small target detection in drone aerial images.

title: Multi-Label Classification of Eye Disease Using Deep Learning
authors: Tanushree Mandal, Monisha Kar, Susovan Jana, Pulak Baral
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Early detection of retinal diseases is necessary to prevent vision loss but the detection faces limitations of poor generalization, inconsistency in datasets, and lack of clinical interpretability. To mitigate these challenges, the study designed a comprehensive framework to create a generalized, accurate and clinically reliable multi-label ocular disease classifier. The proposed framework involved creation of a heterogenous dataset by combining two publicly available datasets and then refining the dataset to 6 classes according to the frequency of classes to mitigate the class imbalance. 11 state-of-the-art models were evaluated where Swin-Tiny demonstrates the best performance 0.9717 accuracy, 0.9876 AUROC and F1 score of 0.8778. The best-performing single model is evaluated on the unseen APTOS 2019 dataset achieving 0.9489 F1-Score for Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) thus proving the model's generalization ability. Integration of Grad-CAM produces visuals highlighting the concerned retinal regions, supporting the clinical reliability.

title: Agro-Waste-Derived Biochar as an Efficient and Sustainable Material for Wastewater Treatment
authors: Aishwarya Varadarajula, Vaishnavi Sharma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Due to rapid growth rates of industrialization and urbanization, water resources are extensively contaminated, thus triggering the need to develop sustainable wastewater treatment materials at low costs. Biochar was produced from agro-waste orange peels using pyrolysis and tested as a prospective water treatment technology to separate heavy metals from wastewater. The surface morphological properties of the synthesized biochar were analyzed by FESEM, which clearly identified that the surface of the biochar had formed a heterogeneous structure post-pyrolysis, and the surface groups were identified by Fourier Transform Infrared Spectrometry (FTIR). Adsorption studies were performed to assess the efficiency of lead (Pb), cadmium (Cd), copper (Cu), and zinc (Zn) metal ion removal. The biochar sample obtained from 400° C showed considerable reduction in metal ion concentration, which removed 59.8% of Cu, 56.6% of Cd, 50.3% of Pb, and 63.9% of Zn metal ion from the solutions. Several factors like contact time, dosage, and pH of the solutions were found to affect the adsorption capacity. From these results, it can thus be concluded that biochar derived from orange peels is an effective, eco-friendly, and economically viable adsorbent. Through this study, the importance of agro-waste management is recognized.

title: Evaluating Data Retrieval and Quality in Pakistani High Court Systems: A Computational Approach
authors: Muhammad Qasim Pasta, Sahaab Bader Sheikh, Eman Fatima, Muhammad Ibad, Syeda Rija Hasan Abidi, Iqra Azfar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: E-governance enables improved service delivery to citizens through communication technology that is more efficient, interactive and transparent as compared to paper-based record keeping. Pakistan's judiciary has initiated a process of digitalization over the last decade and a number of online portals have been established to allow people to search for the status of their cases. The availability of these case management systems offer an opportunity to improve transparency, accessibility, and data-driven decision making. However, the quality of the data available on these online portals has not yet been investigated. For this study, we have extracted and analyzed case-level data from two high courts of Pakistan: the Sindh High Court and the Punjab High Court. We used web-scraping techniques to compile the data, and more than 11,000 entries for the Sindh High Court, and around 1,000 entries for the Lahore High Court were extracted. The data is evaluated for accessibility, consistency, completeness, and usability. Our findings suggest that data has inconsistency issues including missing values, redundant information, and limited historical coverage. We found that despite ongoing digitalization, both portals lack standardization, hindering comprehensive legal research. Such data related issues may be one of the reasons for the limited quantitative research on our judicial system. This study is an attempt to provide a structured dataset to establish a baseline assessment that can support future computational research and evidence-based policy making.

title: Research on Optimization of Composite Laminate Ply Sequence Based on Composite Ply Database
authors: Lilin Tian, Changxing Zhang, Xiaohui Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: With the increasing application of composite materials in aircraft structural design, the optimization design of composite has also garnered attention. Composite material ply optimization is a discrete variable optimization design, which often employs heuristic algorithms such as genetic algorithms for solution. However, these iterative solution methods require extensive computation time, seriously affecting the efficiency of equipment development. This paper proposes a composite ply sequence optimization method based on a ply database, inspired by the concept of engineering databases. Firstly, a ply database that meets engineering constraints is constructed. This database is based on an isogenic sequence double random genetic algorithm, which comprehensively considers various engineering constraint issues and commonly used composite material design principles in engineering. Simultaneously, a penalty function is introduced to control the number of plies under different scenarios, enhancing the application efficiency of the ply database. During ply sequence optimization, a hierarchical optimization strategy is adopted to rapidly match solutions within the database and obtain the optimal ply result. After verification through engineering examples, this method can quickly optimize the design of composite material plies and enhance the performance of the structure.

title: Identifying Website Applications Through Zero-Content URL Classification
authors: Muhammad Aleem Siddique Khan, Addul Basit, Ahmad Raza, Muhammad Zeeshan, Muzamil Khan, Ansar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Approximately 1.13 billion websites span diverse domains like music, movies, health, video games, social media, entertainment, and more, making website classification highly valuable yet challenging. This research explores using Artificial Intelligence techniques to categorize websites into appropriate classes. The DMOZ dataset is one of the most reliable data sources for research focused on URLs, containing about 1.5 million URLs manually classified into different categories, though the distribution of URLs across different categories is highly unbalanced. To overcome this limitation, we collected 500,000 URLs from DomCop's Top 10 Million Websites and classified them using Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically LLaMA 3 3B. Numerous machine learning and deep learning models were trained on both datasets, including logistic regression, support vector machine, decision trees, random forest, CatBoost, XgBoost, LSTM, Bi-LSTM, and FastText. Different data handling and pre-processing techniques were also employed, including chunking for balancing and subdomain extraction to increase URLs per category. We report the most successful pipeline using the FastText model, achieving an average accuracy of 82%.

title: A Study on Developing an Affordable 2-DoF Upper-Limb Rehabilitation Robot
authors: Hoang-Hiep Ly, Quang-Minh Dao, Thi-Thoa Mac, Xuan-Thuan Nguyen, Thi-Khanh-Huyen Nguyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: In modern society, stroke is a leading cause of longterm disability, particularly in developing countries with limited access to physical therapy resources. A common consequence of stroke is impaired motor function, especially in the upper limbs. Rehabilitation robots can help accelerate patient recovery, reduce the burden on healthcare facilities, and the workload of medical staff. This study aims to develop a cost-effective, two-degree-of-freedom (2-DOF) upper-limb rehabilitation robot to support patients' shoulder and elbow joint movements. The device is integrated into a training chair and supports six typical therapeutic exercises without direct attachment to the user's arm. The mechanical structure includes adjustable segments to accommodate diverse patient anatomies. Finite element simulations investigate mechanical integrity, with maximum deformations under 3 mm and equivalent stress values below 133 MPa-well within the safety limits of C45 steel. A PID-based velocity control system is implemented for each joint motor. Tuning via the Ziegler-Nichols method and a developed tuning app yields stable responses: for motor 1, a rise time of 0.9 s with overshoot under 18 rpm at a 48 rpm target speed; for motor 2, a settling time of 0.72 s and improved stability at speeds above 24 rpm. The prototype performs reliably across all six exercises and is estimated to cost approximately $1,000 significantly lower than comparable imported systems.
