Initial trials with healthy users indicate positive feedback on usability and comfort. This work offers a feasible rehabilitation solution for low-resource settings.

title: An Ultralow R on,sp Accumulation LDMOS with High Process and Application Compatibility
authors: Chunwei Zhang, Weihao Lu, Zhaowei Ning, Chao Liu, Yang Li, Xinyu Wang
abstract: The accumulation lateral double diffused MOS (ALDMOS) has been proven to have excellent performance. However, the existing ALDMOSs need complex processes for fabrication or specific control circuits for operation. In this letter, a ALDMOS features an additional accumulation electrode and a Super Field Plate (SuFP-ALDMOS) is proposed. The experimental results show that, by biasing the accumulation electrode at 600 V, the SuFP-ALDMOS not only improves the breakdown voltage (BV) by 11.7%, but also reduces the specific on-resistance (Ron,sp) by 60.1% through the introduction of accumulation effect. As a result, an ultralow Ron,sp of 51.38 mΩ·cm2 is achieved when BV = 708.5 V. Moreover, the SuFP-ALDMOS requires no additional process steps for fabrication and only an additional fixed bias, usually the busbar voltage, for operation. Thereby, the SuFP-ALDMOS is a promising ALDMOS with both ultralow Ron,sp and high process and application compatibility.

title: A Bootstrapping-Optimized Framework for Semantic-Prior Guided Implicit State-Space Models in Remote Sensing Image Super-Resolution
authors: Han'ge Rong, Lei Jin, Jianchao Liu, Wei Zhang, Jie Wang, Siyu Ji, Ting Tang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In feature-dense remote sensing images, super-resolution reconstruction performance relies on the ability to filter key information from complex scenes. Transformer-based methods often focus on semantically irrelevant regions due to their global self-attention mechanisms, leading to structural noise and artifacts. Although sparse attention can suppress redundant noise, its fixed threshold strategy frequently causes texture blurring by removing high-frequency details. To address the contradiction between sparsity and information retention, this paper proposes the Implicit State-Space Transformer (ISST), the first bootstrapping-optimized framework that guides sparse attention coding using semantic priors and leverages control theory inspired closed-loop state updates. Specifically, we design the Implicit State-Space Attention Block (ISSAB), which provides visual prompts for self-attention through the dynamic semantic correlations of implicit states. This preserves critical high-frequency information during sparse computation and avoids loss of detail in traditional methods. We further develop a SAM-based semantic prior prompting mechanism that generates semantic information without additional data or manual annotations. This mechanism initializes and updates Implicit Correlation Matrices (ICM), which are then used to establish pixel correlations for fine-grained texture reconstruction via closed-loop bootstrapping feedback. Experiments demonstrate that ISST outperforms other methods on multiple datasets: in the × 4 super-resolution task on the UCMerced dataset, the average PSNR is improved by 0.60 dB compared to TTST. It effectively suppresses redundant information interference and retains details in complex scenes, maintaining high quality even at low Diffusion Index (DI) values.

title: Temperature rectified refractive index sensor based on surface plasmon resonance in a tapered multimode fiber
authors: Bing Jiang, Jianhao Li, Weigeng Zhu, Kaijun Che
abstract: A surface plasmon resonance (SPR) sensor based on a tapered multimode fiber (TMMF) is proposed for the measurement of refractive index (RI) of an aqueous solution with temperature rectification. The sensor consists of an Au-coated TMMF whose sensing section is partially covered by a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) layer. As surrounding RI changes, the SPR wavelength of bare Au-coated TMMF shifts while the SPR wavelength of PDMS covered section is exclusively tuned by temperature. A sensitivity coefficient matrix is used to simultaneously determine RI and temperature by monitoring the shifts of two SPR wavelengths. By optimizing the diameter of TMMF, an RI sensitivity of 2459 nm/RIU and a temperature sensitivity of -1.75 nm/°C are achieved in the RI range from 1.333 to 1.364 and in the temperature range from 24 °C to 80 °C. The SPR sensor with high sensitivity and compact structure is expected for applications in biosensing and environmental monitoring.

title: RSMA-Enabled Covert Communications Against Multiple Spatially Random Wardens
authors: Xinyue Pei, Jihao Liu, Xuewen Luo, Xingwei Wang, Yingyang Chen, Miaowen Wen, Theodoros A. Tsiftsis
abstract: This work investigates covert communication in a rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA)-based multi-user multiple-input single-output system, where the random locations of the wardens follow a homogeneous Poisson point process. To demonstrate practical deployment scenarios, imperfect channel state information at the transmitter is considered. Closed-form expressions for the statistics of the received signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio, along with the analytical formulations for the covertness constraint, outage probability, and effective covert throughput (ECT), are derived. Subsequently, an ECT maximization problem is formulated under covertness and power allocation constraints. This optimization problem is addressed using an alternating optimization-assisted differential evolution (AO-DE). Simulation results corroborate the theoretical analysis and demonstrate the superiority of RSMA over conventional multiple access schemes except in the noise-dominated regime, as well as the effectiveness of the proposed AO-DE.

title: URLLC-Aware Resource Allocation for IRS Backscatter-Enabled Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks
authors: Yuxuan Li, Ting Xiong, Xiangyu Liu, Shibing Zhu, Changqing Li, Qi Su, Jianmei Dai
abstract: The evolution of 6 G and Internet of Things (IoT) technology is driving the proliferation of computation-intensive and latency-sensitive applications, which impose increasingly stringent requirements on IoT Devices (IDs) for powerful and timely data processing capabilities. Space-Air-Ground Integrated Network (SAGINs) have emerged as a promising candidate for providing ubiquitous computing services for processing various sensing data generated by IDs, particularly in remote areas. However, supporting these applications in SAGINs still faces two key challenges: IDs cannot afford intensive data transmission and computation due to the limited battery and computing capability, and the Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communication (URLLC) constraints are difficult to satisfy due to the network dynamics. To tackle these challenges, we propose an Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS) backscatter-enabled SAGIN architecture, where battery-efficient, radio-frequency-chain-free uplink transmissions from IDs to Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are enabled, significantly reducing the communication energy consumption of IDs. We aim to minimize the system energy consumption for balancing low-latency requirements against energy-saving demands under URLLC constraints. Considering that resource allocation decisions and URLLC constraints have different time scales, the Lyapunov optimization is employed to decompose the formulated problem into tractable subproblems, which are solved by integrating Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) with fractional programming, semi-definite programming, and the Lagrange method. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly reduces the total energy consumption by 66.67% compared to benchmark schemes, thereby enabling sustainable, high-performance computation for remote IDs.

title: Research on Key Technologies for in-Orbit Management of Spacecraft Cryogenic Propellants
authors: Bin Yu, Tianju Ma, Cheng Huang, Sendong Gu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive review of the key technologies and maturity progress in aerospace cryogenic storage tanks. It analyzes and compares various technical approach and application scenarios, ultimately determining an optimal technical approach that combines zero boil-off cryocooler mechanism cryocooler and cryogenic propellant pre-cooling liquid mixing. This determination is based on a thorough analysis of actual aerospace requirements, in-orbit service technical challenges, and the characteristics of surface tension storage tanks. The paper further explains the classification of active thermal coupling technologies and the operational mechanisms and modes of liquid mixing. It also examines the advantages, disadvantages, and application scenarios of both cryocooler-based cooling and throttle valve-based cooling methodology. Additionally, the paper highlights a typical research case study on active liquid mixing in aerospace cryogenic storage tanks conducted by the U.S. NASA. Finally, the paper analyzes and summarizes the development trends of cryogenic storage tank technologies and offers recommendations for the development of in-orbit management technologies for cryogenic propellants in China's aerospace sector.

title: UAV Path Planning Method Based on Artificial Emotions
authors: Yana Ma, Tao Jin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To enhance the autonomous decision-making and adaptive capabilities of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in complex dynamic environments, this paper proposes a new method for UAV path planning based on artificial emotions, inspired by the key role of emotional state in human intelligent decision-making process. In response to the lack of emotional dimensions in traditional algorithms, this study first uses the dynamic reasoning ability of Dynamic Fuzzy Cognitive Maps (DFCM) to infer the emotion modes of UAVs in different situations. Furthermore, the generated emotional patterns are embedded as decision factors in the underlying path planning algorithm, enabling the UAV to dynamically adjust risk preferences and behavioral strategies based on emotions. Simulation results show that compared to traditional planning methods, the routes generated by this method not only meet the basic physical and obstacle avoidance constraints of the task, but also reflect intelligent decision-making behaviors similar to humans, providing new ideas for the research of biomimetic intelligent unmanned aerial vehicle systems.

title: Unveiling Genomic Insights: Fine-Tuning LLMs for Enhanced Gene Prioritization
authors: D. Saiteja, J. Rushit Sai Vallabh, K. Poojitha, Prakash Kumar Sarangi, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Large language models have shown promise in accelerating knowledge-driven gene prioritization tasks, as demonstrated by recent studies. However, challenges such as model accuracy, reliability, and adaptability to specific biomedical contexts remain. This paper proposes and evaluates an enhanced large language model workflow for gene prioritization, leveraging fine-tuning and multi-model integration to improve performance. State-of-the-art large language models, including GPT-4 and Claude, are fine-tuned on curated biomedical datasets to enhance their ability to identify and prioritize candidate genes. Additionally, the integration of multiple large language models is explored to create an ensemble approach that reduces biases and improves the robustness of gene prioritization. Evaluation using publicly available transcriptomic datasets shows that the enhanced workflow achieves higher accuracy and reliability compared to existing methods, with the multi-model integration reaching 89.3% accuracy. The findings highlight the potential of fine-tuned and ensemble large language models in streamlining gene prioritization for biomedical research, offering a more efficient and interpretable approach for identifying candidate genes in complex diseases. This paper provides a framework for optimizing large language model workflows in genomics and sets the stage for future applications in personalized medicine and drug discovery.

title: SadNet: Deep Recurrent Emotion Dynamics Model for Real-Time Sadness Recognition Using Facial Muscle Movements
authors: A. Deepa, S. Ponmaniraj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Real-time recognition of sadness is an important issue in affective computing because it is a subtle facial expression and evolves over time. The paper introduces SadNet which is a new Deep Recurrent Emotion Dynamics Model which combines spatial, time-based, and physiologically aware features in the constant detection of sadness. SadNet is a system that jointly trains a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to extract spatial patterns of muscle deformation of key facial areas with a bidirectional Long short-term memory (Bi-LSTM) network to predict the temporal dynamics of emotional state. The attention mechanism is weighted by An Action Unit (AU) to highlight the physiologically relevant muscles related to sadness, whereas optical flow and thermal differentials images are used to detect the subtle low intensity motions. This model makes use of cross-subject generalization through transfer learning using neutral baselines, which achieves individual-level and effective emotion mapping. Using AffectNet and FER2013 datasets, SadNet got an accuracy of more than 95 per cent with a real-time inference latency of 0.125 seconds per frame. The studies on ablation verify that temporal modeling, attention weighting, and multimodal fusion are effective. The suggested framework offers a physiologically based, temporally consistent, and computationally efficient real-time sadness recognition solution allowing it to be used in human-computer interaction, mental health, and affect-aware robotics.

title: Computational Study of Integrated Phased Array Antenna and Radome Structured with Huygens Metasurface
authors: Di Gao, Yuancheng Fan, Yang Guo, Zhishen Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: In modern wireless communications and electronic countermeasures, antenna radomes protect antennas but can introduce electromagnetic coupling that degrades system performance. To quantify and control this effect, we perform antenna-radome integrated modeling and performance analysis of a microstrip patch antenna array covered by a Huygens metasurface (HMS) radome, using finite-difference time-domain (FDTD). The designed patch array exhibits high gain, low profile, and beam-reconfigurable characteristics. With the HMS radome installed, a negligible degradation in the radiation performance of the antenna is observed, indicating limited coupling-induced degradation and preserved beam quality. The simulation results demonstrate a good agreement with the commercial full-wave solver, validating the accuracy and engineering applicability of the proposed algorithm in integrated modelling. This provides a reliable tool for complex antenna-antenna radome integrated design.

title: Automated Analysis and Validation of Spatiotemporal Consistency in Team Routines of Artistic Swimming
authors: Li Li, Xin Xu, Wei Mu
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Research hotspots and trends of virtual reality technology in sports injury rehabilitation: A mixed bibliometric analysis based on CiteSpace and VOSviewer
authors: Xueqi Chen, Genghao Liang, Jiaqi Li, Yuliang Zha
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: CMCap: Cross-modal Meta-captioning for Few-shot Remote Sensing Image Captioning_supp1-3674723.pdf
authors: Jinlong Liu
abstract: Remote sensing image captioning (RSIC) aims to automatically generate natural language descriptions for remote sensing images, which holds significant application value. However, existing methods heavily rely on large-scale annotated datasets. In practical applications, due to the high annotation cost of remote sensing data and the dynamic diversity of ground object categories, only a limited number of labeled samples are available for many scenarios, leading to severe data scarcity challenges. To address this challenge, we propose the Cross-modal Meta-Captioning framework (CMCap). First, we design a sufficient task sampling strategy that constructs a large number of pseudo-tasks in each iteration to reduce gradient variance and improve data utilization. Then, we synthesize challenging support samples to enhance discriminative representation learning, and propose a task-adaptive cross-modal alignment module to decouple task adaptability from general representation learning, thereby improving the model’s discriminability and cross-task generalization under extremely few-shot conditions. Moreover, we develop a meta-conditioning decoder that deeply fuses task context with query image features to guide a frozen large language model in generating task-consistent captions. Experiments conducted on UCM-Captions, Sydney-Captions, and RSICD demonstrate that CMCap achieves competitive performance across various evaluation metrics and exhibits remarkable generalization capability under extreme data scarcity conditions.

title: Biclique Percolation Communities Computation on Temporal Bipartite Graphs
authors: Zi Chen, Haotian Huang, Long Yuan, Jianqiu Xu, Bolong Zheng, Xuemin Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Biclique percolation community (BPC) search is a fundamental problem in bipartite graph analysis and has many applications. In many real-world scenarios, bipartite graphs are temporal, and ignoring time information can lead to communities that lack temporal cohesiveness. Motivated by this, we propose efficient index-based approaches for both static and temporal BPC search. Based on the index, we can obtain the static BPCs in near-optimal time with well-bounded index space. For temporal bipartite graphs, we introduce the (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha , \beta , \theta$</tex-math></inline-formula>)-TemBPC model, which captures both structural and temporal cohesiveness. We extend the BPC-Index to efficiently enumerate and process temporal bicliques. Furthermore, several key optimizations are incorporated to accelerate the search processing. We conduct extensive experiments on 11 real bipartite graphs, and the experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the BPC models and the efficiency of our static and temporal BPC search algorithms.

title: Second-Life Batteries as Enablers of Sustainable Electric Vehicle Infrastructure: Opportunities and Future Directions
authors: A. Zabihi, P. Siano, K. Basaran, G.C. Lazaroiu
abstract: The integration of second-life batteries (SLBs) into electric vehicle (EV) facilities represents a pivotal advancement for achieving environmentally sustainable and economically viable mobility solutions. SLBs are a transformative energy-storage strategy that enhances the efficiency, resilience, and sustainability of EV infrastructure. This systematic review rigorously analyzes academic literature sourced from ScienceDirect and Web of Science to investigate key research domains, emerging trends, and international contributions pertinent to SLB applications. The study emphasizes critical performance indicators such as state of health (SoH), state of charge (SoC), and degradation rates, underscoring the necessity of continuous monitoring to uphold SLB reliability and safety. Moreover, advanced machine learning (ML) methodologies such as random forest (RF), support vector machine (SVM), and long short-term memory (LSTM)are highlighted for their robust potential in optimizing SLB characterization and operational performance. This review addresses multifaceted challenges, including battery degradation, characterization methodologies, integration complexities, testing standards, and performance evaluation frameworks, by concentrating on the latest innovations in SLB deployment. Finally, the findings underscore the imperative for sustained interdisciplinary research and cross-sectoral synergies to advance SLB integration, ultimately fostering the evolution of more sustainable and efficient EV charging networks.

title: DARS: Dynamic Reliability-Aware Routing and Scheduling for Fault-Tolerant TSN
authors: Wenbin Tian, Changqing Long, Rui Tan, Shibo He, Chaojie Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Frame Replication and Elimination for Reliability (FRER) provides fault tolerance by replicating frames and transmitting them along disjoint paths, thereby ensuring ultra-reliable and low-latency communication in TSN networks. However, existing FRER methods with static orchestration strategies, fixed reliability assumptions, and predefined redundancy levels cannot adapt to permanent, transient, or multi-concurrent failures in dynamic network service conditions. To overcome this, we propose DARS, a dynamic reliability-aware routing and scheduling method that incrementally adjusts flow paths and schedules to guarantee reliability and timeliness under evolving faults and network fluctuations. First, we introduce Dynamic Reliability Score (DRS), a new metric that quantifies device reliability and updates it according to flow arrival status, which continuously reflects the service status of traversed devices. Second, we design an incremental adaptive orchestration framework with offline and online strategies, where the former pre-configures all critical flows, while the latter continuously adjusts non-compliant flows. We further develop an FRER-based joint routing and scheduling method that leverages the state-aware DRS to generate adaptive transmission schemes. Experiments on four TSN topologies show that DARS satisfies all flow transmission demands within five iterations under single-fault cases and about 92% of flows under five-fault scenarios, outperforms baseline methods under heavy-load and multi-fault conditions, and reduces redundant transmissions by 5–12.5% through adaptive redundancy.

title: Service Composition for Satellite Computing
authors: Jiawei Lu, Pengfei Wang, Shangguang Wang
abstract: The diverse array of services provided by satellite constellations now plays a crucial role in human life, economic development, and national security. Imagine a scenario where, regardless of a user's location on Earth, commands can be transmitted to a satellite constellation. The system intelligently selects the most suitable services across the satellites, dynamically combining them to ensure rapid and seamless global fulfillment of the user's requirements. Although ideal in concept, the utilization of services is constrained by the limitations of satellite performance. This issue becomes especially critical when multiple services are requested simultaneously within a satellite constellation. An inefficient service composition strategy can fail to ensure adequate quality of service for users while also increases system load and significantly shortens the satellites' lifespan. In this work, we consider a service composition problem under a constrained space scenario, and we introduce a <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</u>ooperative-enhanced <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</u>ulti-<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>gent <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>L-based model (named CMAR) to address this issue. Specifically, CMAR formalizes the service composition problem as a Multi-agent Markov Decision Process, where two agents are designed to manage the selection for satellites and services separately to shrink the searching space. Considering collaborative difficulties caused by such a decomposition strategy, where agents can only observe partial changes in the environment, a mask-guided QMIX network is further designed to strengthen their corporation for proper joint actions. Our proposed method has been evaluated on one real-world dataset released by Tiansuan Constellation and two simulated datasets, the empirical results show that our model can improve the performance significantly compared with both traditional and RL-based baselines. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/CMARModel/CMARModel.git</uri>.

title: A Formal Framework for Reactive Heterogeneous Multirobot Task Allocation in Uncertain Semantic Environments
authors: Lin Li, Zhangli Zhou, Ziyang Chen, Hao Wang, Zhen Kan, Jiahu Qin
abstract: Existing multirobot task allocation (MRTA) research primarily assumes an environment with known geometric and semantic information. However, in most real-world scenarios, semantic information, such as the locations and classifications of landmarks, is often uncertain. This uncertainty is exacerbated by dynamic requirements, like the sudden addition or removal of tasks, making it challenging for robots to respond reactively. Moreover, tasks in MRTA typically involve multiple constraints, including temporal requirements, diverse capabilities, varying resource needs, and inter-task dependencies. To address these challenges, we propose a reactive task allocation framework for heterogeneous multirobot systems that accounts for temporal requirements and multiple task constraints described by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm {LTL^{\mathcal {R}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. Our approach assumes an environment with known geometry but unknown semantic landmarks. To efficiently solve task allocations, we encode <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm {LTL^{\mathcal {R}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> along with the system’s states into a proposed planning decision tree for exploration. Upon detecting a relevant semantic landmark, the reactive multiconstraint planning decision tree (RMC-PDT) is triggered for re-planning. Extensive experiments validate three key features of our method: 1) efficient reactive planning; 2) multiconstraint task solving; and 3) scalability.

title: Holographic Counterpart-Based Real-Time Monitoring for UAV-Assisted Disaster Management
authors: Tanveer Ahmad, Amira Elsir Tayfour Ahmed, Vasos Vassiliou, K Raghavendar, Tahani Alsubait, Loukas Dimitriou
abstract: This paper presents a holographic counterpart-based framework for real-time monitoring in UAV-assisted disaster management. In contrast to the conventional digital-twin or image-based monitoring methods, the proposed system produces an ever-reconfigured holographic model of the disaster site through the combination of multimodal UAV stream of data, predictive hazard maps, and streamlined communication channels. Having a high level of fidelity, the holographic model gives a spatial and time-dependent representation of structural damage, congestion evolution, and risk propagation so that operators may see the environment in three dimensions and can interact with the reconstructed environment in real time. A UAV coordination model enhances coverage of the situation by making UAVs fly into the areas of the greatest uncertainty whereas the decision making engine assesses the risk fields. The complete pipeline is validated through a simulation environment emulating dynamic fire spread, environmental noise, and communication latency. Results demonstrate that the holographic counterpart (HC) improves situational awareness, reduces monitoring uncertainty, and enables faster and more informed decision-making during disaster response operations.

title: Phenotype-aware Imitation Learning for Small, Oriented Object Detection in Remote Sensing
authors: Tong Niu, Qizhi Teng, Xiaohai He, Honggang Chen, Linbo Qing, Shengfeng He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Imitation learning has emerged as a promising paradigm for detecting small, arbitrarily oriented objects in remote sensing imagery. However, existing methods largely focus on global feature alignment between object instances and high-quality exemplars. This often leads to over-homogenized features across instances and distortion of intrinsic semantic structures, which in turn introduces geometric ambiguities in orientation, shape, and scale. Such issues are especially detrimental in remote sensing, where small objects exhibit significant variation and complex spatial distributions. To address these limitations, we propose a phenotype-aware imitation learning (PIL) framework that explicitly preserves geometric diversity and semantic structure for accurate object localization. PIL consists of two components: a geometric disentangling branch and a semantic structure registration branch. The geometric disentangling branch learns instance-specific deformation fields that adaptively normalize orientation, scale, and shape variations between exemplars and instances. By disentangling geometric differences, this branch allows the imitation process to focus on meaningful semantic discrepancies. The semantic structure registration branch further improves alignment by decoupling imitation into registration and imitation spaces. In the registration space, instances align their semantic structures by modeling spatial relations within the exemplar’s global context. In the imitation space, features that have been geometrically normalized and semantically aligned perform pixel-wise imitation, supervised by a mixed-grained contrastive loss (MC-loss) that balances both local and global semantic cues. To mitigate geometric detail loss in cross-scale imitation , we also introduce a contextual micro-to-macro feature pyramid network (CM2-FPN). This module levarages low-layer spatial detail to complement the contextual information of large objects in high-layer features. Extensive experiments on DOTA, DIOR-R, and SODA-A datasets show that PIL-Net consistently outperforms existing methods. The performance gains are especially significant in challenging settings dominated by small, densely distributed, and arbitrarily oriented targets.

title: A Time-Domain BEM-FEM Model for Fingertip Skin Mechanics Analysis in Mid-Air Ultrasound Haptics
authors: Fangyan Yang, Jian Chen, Lin Lin, Linan Wu
abstract: The design process of multiparameter tactile patterns (Tactons) for mid-air ultrasound haptics (MUH) faces limitations due to its reliance on an inefficient trial-and-error process and a lack of models to accurately simulate complex fingertip mechanics. To address these challenges, this article proposes a fingertip skin mechanical model for MUH that can map driving signals to skin deformation. This model integrates a time-domain (TD) boundary element method (BEM) for acoustic scattering with a finite-element method for fingertip interaction mechanics. Initially, a transducer equivalent model and the linear acoustic theory are used to predict the TD acoustic field of the MUH device. Subsequently, a TD BEM model for acoustic scattering computes dynamic acoustic radiation pressure. Finally, a multilayer anatomical fingertip model simulates responses by MUH corresponding to skin deformation. Experimental results demonstrate that 6.02%, 13.74%, and 9.027% average relative errors for sound pressure, radiation force, and skin displacement's frequency response prediction in real environments, respectively, can be reached. Moreover, the proposed method can achieve more accurate TD waveform prediction and higher computational efficiency than state-of-the-art analysis approaches. This model provides an efficient computational tool for optimizing Tactons, facilitating industrial MUH rendering system development.

title: A Hybrid AI Framework Integrating Machine Learning and Large Language Models for Intelligent Crop Recommendation
authors: Tirthanka Saha, Rajdeep Ghosh, Tirtha Dutta, Pretam Sarkar, Rounak Saha, Sainik Kumar Mahata
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Food is an essential component in living beings and for that we need agriculture and soil type detection is an essential part of agriculture. To help with that we have created a model where the model gives a hybrid decision support system accurately modeled to reduce the increasing risk of crop failure caused by unstable climatic conditions and soil nutrient differences. When the global agricultural environment drops the dependency in traditional farming, this study shows a strong computational methodology that combines mainly three particular layers to provide highly precise guidance. The starting layer in our model uses Random Forest machine learning model that focuses on soil classification based on soil type. The model then checks essential soil parameters that include Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K) levels along with the pH. The second layer brings the use of a Geospatial API needed for a 10 year longitudinal climate check in the second layer. The data of historical temperature, rainfall with local soil chemistry helps to calculate the environmental problems. The third layer is the Mistral 7B Large Language Model LLM, which is used in practical farming advice. Machine learning handles quantitative data while the LLM gives a practical context so that it offers agricultural related advice to support data results with real farming experience. The output lists the top five probable crops according to climate changes and difficulty risk scores. This dual focus on soil health and climate gives assurance on productive advice and continuity under volatile situations. Validation results indicate that the model performs almost flawlessly with benchmark data. By connecting the fixed set of probabilistic distribution of the Random Forest with the adaptive intelligence of a Large Language Model, the model provides a comprehensive decision-support tool with fewer limitations from the single-model machine learning applications.

title: SSRFense: A Multi-Layered SSRF Detection and Defense System for Kubernetes Microservices
authors: Harid Nutkumhang, Woraphol Huansuriya, Jenwit Thitikawin, Krittin Limotai, Somchart Fugkeaw
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) remains a major security risk and has been merged into the Broken Access Control category in the OWASP Top 10 (2025). Existing defenses like input validation, URL filtering, and WAFs are easily bypassed via DNS rebinding, redirect chaining, and obfuscation. These weaknesses are intensified in Kubernetes microservices, where dynamic service discovery obscures malicious serverinitiated requests. Current solutions offer only surface-level inspection and lack visibility into intra-cluster behavior and multi-hop attack propagation. This paper presents SSRFense, a multi-layered SSRF detection and defense framework for Kubernetes microservices. It integrates: (i) an unsupervised Autoencoder that models normal inter-service communication to detect anomalous internal requests; (ii) a supervised LSTM-based malicious-domain detector that analyzes outbound DNS patterns; and (iii) a Neo4j graph-based analyzer that reconstructs attack paths, identifies lateral movement, and highlights unauthorized access relationships. SSRFense further includes an automated remediation mechanism that isolates affected services and enforces dynamic Kubernetes policies for rapid containment. Together, these components provide an adaptive, context-aware defense against both internal and external SSRF threats in modern cloudnative environments. Experiments show that SSRFense achieves high anomaly detection scores (PR-AUC and ROC-AUC of 1.0) with minimal false positives, an average end-to-end latency of 3.23 s, while the detection pipeline achieves the highest throughput of 579 events/s. Furthermore, extensive evaluations show that SSRFense effectively detects common SSRF evasion techniques and multi-hop attack chains. These results demonstrate near realtime detection, scalability, and robustness for cloud-native SSRF defense.

title: Enhancement of 6T CNFET SRAM Performance: A Comparison of Traditional and Stacking Methods in 45nm Technology
authors: Drav Vilas Tatwade, D.K Raghuvanshi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper introduces in-depth evaluation of $\mathbf{6 T}$ SRAM designs based on Carbon Nanotube Field-Effect Transistors (CNFETs) at the 45 nm node with a focus on power consumption and delay optimization. The traditional and stacked 6T CNFET SRAM structures are evaluated using extensive simulations based on Cadence Virtuoso. This allows for detailed comparison of average power and delay with respect to different operation parameters, such as supply voltage (VDD). The outcomes prove that the stacked CNFET SRAM circuit attains significant power efficiency gains, significantly lowering static and dynamic power consumption. Nonetheless, it implies a price of increased delay due to greater effective resistance in the stacked structure. This work not only contributes to the understanding of important trade-offs in CNFET-based SRAM design but also provides a useful framework to use new materials in an attempt to optimize SRAM technologies for low-power applications in the future.

title: Achieving Real-Time Emotion Recognition in Speech: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach with 22ms Inference
authors: Gubbala Chanakya, Meenavolu S B Kasyapa, G. Revanth Reddy, G. Devendar, B. Siri, M. Sumana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: A key element in improving human-computer interface systems is Speech Emotion Recognition (SER). Even though emotion categorization has advanced significantly, real-time application is still difficult because of processing complexity and interference from ambient noise. An improved deep learning framework for real-time SER is presented in this paper, which combines a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) architecture with Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs). By using model pruning and quantization to overcome latency constraints, the suggested system achieves an inference time of 22 ms and maintains 86.3% accuracy on the RAVDESS dataset, which is a 14.2% improvement over conventional SVM-based methods. Using spectral augmentation approaches, experimental results show robustness to background noise, with a 12% increase in accuracy in noisy environments. The solution is set up on Raspberry Pi 4 edge devices for real-world use in call center analytics and mental health tracking. This approach establishes a standard for future studies in low-latency affective computing by bridging the gap between highaccuracy SER and real-time operational requirements.

title: RL Based Beamforming Optimization for 3D Pinching Antenna Assisted ISAC Systems
authors: Qian Gao, Ruikang Zhong, Yue Liu, Hyundong Shin, Yuanwei Liu
abstract: In this paper, a three-dimensional (3D) deployment scheme of pinching antenna array is proposed, aiming to enhances the performance of integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems. To fully realize the potential of 3D deployment, a joint antenna positioning, time allocation and transmit power optimization problem is formulated to maximize the sum communication rate with the constraints of target sensing rates and system energy. To solve the sum rate maximization problem, we propose a heterogeneous graph neural network based reinforcement learning (HGRL) algorithm. Simulation results show that the proposed 3D pinching antenna deployment improves the ISAC performance significantly, achieving around 10–20% higher communication rate compared with conventional 1D/2D layouts, while maintaining the sensing SNR near the required threshold with substantially lower power consumption. Moreover, the proposed HGRL algorithm surpasses other baselines in both performance and convergence speed due to the advanced observation construction of the environment.

title: A Comprehensive Multi-Model Framework for Lung Cancer Image Classification Integrating Advanced Deep Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Arka Goswami, Koushani Chandra, Subhajit Kar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Lung cancer is a serious global threat that demands immediate attention for early detection and accurate diagnosis. This paper presents a new multi-stage deep learning framework that uses pre-trained convolutional neural networks such as EfficientNetB0, VGG16, MobileNetV2, DenseNet121, and ResNet50. It also introduces a hybrid CNN-SVM model to improve classification success. This approach combines adaptive loss optimization, anomaly detection, and explainable AI with Grad-CAM and t-SNE. It ensures high decision accuracy and strong reliability. In our current methods, it achieves an impressive 99.55 percent test accuracy on a specialized lung radiograph dataset. This innovation combines interpretability, strength, and diagnostic capability, marking a significant advance for AI in medicine. It paves the way for smarter systems that help doctors predict lung cancer more confidently and accurately.

title: Machine Learning Optimized Trident Monopole Antenna for WLAN/WiMAX Applications
authors: Debajyoti Chatterjee, Anjan Kumar Kundu, Suchanda Chatterjee
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This paper introduces a machine learning (ML) optimization framework for the design of a high-performance, trident-shaped monopole antenna targeting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.4 / 2.5\ \text{GHz}$</tex> WLAN and 5.2 GHz WiMAX applications. The complex geometry of the trident structure, which generates dual-band operation through its central and side arms, presents a multi-parameter design challenge. Traditional design methods rely on iterative simulation and are often computationally prohibitive. To overcome this, a data-driven surrogate modeling approach is implemented. Key geometric parameters of the trident element are used as inputs to train multiple regression models, including linear regression, Support Vector Regression, and Gaussian Process Regression (GPR), to predict antenna performance metrics. The GPR model demonstrated superior accuracy and was employed to determine the optimal geometry that maximizes gain and bandwidth while minimizing return loss. The trident monopole antenna achieves a measured impedance bandwidth of 360 MHz and 340 MHz at the lower and higher bands, respectively, with stable radiation patterns and gains of 1.94 dBi (lower band) and 3.28 dBi (upper band). Furthermore, the ML optimized antenna provides higher bandwidth (760 MHz in lower band and 1 GHz in upper band) and gain (2.39 dBi in lower band and 4.11 dBi in upper band) that significantly reduces the design cycle time for complex antenna structures, ensuring performance that meets stringent modern wireless communication standards.

title: Overvoltage and Undervoltage Protection System Using Arduino
authors: Prashant Kumar, Piyush Kumar, Rohit Konsam, Khushi Bharti, Rachit Chandel
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Voltage fluctuation is very destructive to the electrical systems in the domestic, commercial and industrial sectors and may lead to serious damage of appliances, limitation of efficiency of operations and safety hazards. In order to solve this problem, this paper will design and implement Overvoltage and Undervoltage Protection System which uses Arduino and will be able to sense abnormal voltage levels and automatically disconnect load to avoid destruction. The suggested system constantly checks the supply voltage with the help of a ZMPT101B voltage sensor and calculates the data with an Arduino UNO microcontroller and a relay module to switch the load in or out of the circuit depending on the set safe voltage standards. The system includes remote monitoring based on the IoT to increase the functionality through the use of a Wi-Fi module equipped with a NodeMCU (ESP8266). Experimental tests have verified that the system is responsive to changes in voltage and reliably disconnects the load in milliseconds of fault detection and also automatically returns to normal operation once the voltage levels stabilize. Three phase protection, artificial intelligence-powered predictive fault analysis, and application to smart grid-based systems are all areas of future development of this work to make power systems more stable and automated.

title: IoT-Enabled Multi-Sensor Based Real-Time Transformer Fault Diagnosis
authors: Md Ayub Khan, Divanshu Sah, Ishaan Kothari, Ritendra Narayan Ray, Debashis Jana, Ranjita Chowdhury
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Transformers are subjected to undergo electrical, thermal, and other mechanical stresses that continuously exposing them to a greater chance of developing internal and/or external faults. In response to this issue, the work explores how to create an IoT and AI-enabled hardware system that will include Multi-Domain Sensors (MDS), which are the SW420 Vibration Sensor, ZMPT101B Voltage Sensor, and ACS712 Current Sensor. All sensors will connect to ESP32 Microcontroller (MC) and the sensor readings will be processed using the Arduino platform for Initial Validation (IV), which means to confirm proper operation of these sensors before sending data to the Cloud (via MQTT Protocol). The cloud will allow users to export the data in the form of structured files for further offline Machine Learning (ML) analysis based on the structured data file. The Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model has been used to forecast voltage, current and vibration. The experiments demonstrate that our model provides fair prediction accuracy with a predicted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.85, and the predicted RMSEs correspond to the expected values for our application.

title: An AI-Powered Voice Assistant for Health and Wellness Monitoring Using CNN-BILSTMAttention
authors: S Hemamalini, K Sathyamoorthy, P Punvesh, A Santhosh Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: AI healthcare monitoring systems are gaining interest, especially those which use ‘voice first’ technology and allow for seamless user interaction. Current health-tracking applications use and require a lot of manual user input. This not only disengages users, but can also lead to a less accurate health record. A major difficulty in health-tracking applications and user input is real-time speech recognition and the computing of latent health indicators such as stress and fatigue. This paper presents Nutri Voice Agent to address the above challenges. Nutri Voice Agent is an AI powered voice assistant that provides a personalized health and wellness monitoring system. The system merges multi-scale acoustic feature extraction and deep learning speech recognition and natural language processing to derive meaning from speech on an emotional and cognitive level. The system employs a CNN-BiLSTM architecture which is enhanced with self-attention, domain adversarial training, and a collated training corpus to improve zone the of generalization while also controlling for overfitting. This proposed system on the RAVDESS, TESS and SAVEE datasets achieved a 98.4 % accuracy and 98.6% macro-F1 score and is thus suitable to realtime, proactive healthcare applications.

title: Performance Evaluation of Lane Change Maneuvers in Self-Driving Vehicles: A Simulation-Based Approach
authors: Tanakorn Mongkondatsawat, Namchai Sokai, Panthip Pleasai, Pairoj Raothanachonkun, Nakorn Indra-Payoong
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Lane change maneuvers are among the most safety-critical actions for autonomous vehicles, as they require accurate perception, interaction prediction, and timely decision-making under dynamic traffic conditions. Unlike lane keeping or lane departure prevention, lane changes are intentional maneuvers that involve interaction with surrounding vehicles traveling in the same direction, particularly those approaching from the rear. This paper presents a simulation-based framework for evaluating Lane Change Assist (LCA) behavior using the CARLA simulator. The proposed system integrates sensor-fusion-based perception, short-term trajectory prediction, and safetyoriented decision logic to analyze overtaking maneuvers under multiple traffic scenarios. Simulation results illustrate the behavior of the proposed LCA framework in both base and failure scenarios, including cases where rear-vehicle awareness is absent. The simulation findings highlight the importance of dedicated lane change assistance mechanisms to support safe intentional lane change decision-making, particularly in scenarios where rear-side perception plays a critical role.

title: Improving Content Dissemination in Named Data Networking through Broadcast Leave-Copy-Down Caching
authors: Ridha Muldina Negara, Sofia Naning Hertiana, Naufal Hanan Lutfianto, Zaid Muhammad
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Named Data Networking (NDN) introduces in-network caching as a core mechanism that allows routers to temporarily store and reuse content, thereby reducing the dependency on content producers and improving data retrieval efficiency. In large-scale NDN deployments, routers interconnect in complex topologies, making optimal cache placement a critical factor for achieving balanced network performance. This study proposes an enhanced cache placement strategy called Broadcast Leave-Copy-Down (B-LCD), which extends the traditional Leave-Copy-Down (LCD) method. Unlike LCD, where data is cached only at a single downstream node, B-LCD enables broadcasting of content to multiple adjacent routers near the node that previously fulfilled the request. This modification accelerates content dissemination while minimizing redundancy across cache layers. Simulation experiments conducted using the Icarus simulator demonstrate that the proposed B-LCD approach improves overall caching performance, achieving higher cache hit ratios, lower latency, and reduced link load compared to existing LCD and Leave-Copy-Everywhere (LCE) strategies, thus offering a more efficient solution for content distribution in NDN environments.

title: Synthesized sgRNA Databases with Optimal GC-Content as Davenport-Schinzel Sequences
authors: Phuong-Nam Nguyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: CRISPR technology promises to revolutionize biomedical science and medicine, in which the merit of this advancement lies in its ability to edit genes with high precision and efficiency. The gRNA design of the CRISPR-associated proteins is crucial for advancing this technology, but it requires significant computational resources due to the vast search space. This work introduces CRISPR-SCORE and DS-CRISPR to address: (1) a robust scoring metric of the gRNA based on its intrinsic genetic contents and (2) a cost-efficient and effective approach to synthesize gRNA, respectively. First, we demonstrate that the proposed CRISPR-SCORE positively correlates with efficacy scores, evaluated using six publicly available databases. Second, we demonstrate that the synthesized database from DS-CRISPR is a quality in silico-designed gRNA, which can reduce costs and enhance the success of in vivo or in vitro experiments.

title: Secure Image Encryption Using 5D Hyper-Chaotic Keystream and 3D Pixel Scrambling with Adaptive Confusion-Diffusion
authors: Sana Riasat, Erum Mehmood
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: With the widespread use of various confidential images such as medical and financial images, image security has become popular. Traditional encryption algorithms such as Data Encryption Standard (DES) and Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) were proposed for texts in the beginning, thus inefficient to encrypt large images size and insecure against attacks because of low confusion and diffusion. In this paper, an improved image encryption scheme using three-dimensional (3D) pixel scrambling and a five-dimensional (5D) hyperchaotic keystream is presented for better security and efficiency. Disparities in spatial correlations are eliminated by the 3D voxel grid division of the images. The 5D hyperchaotic system is used for creating a keystream that has enlarged keyspace, and proved to be able to resist brute-force attacks. The approach presents adaptive confusion and diffusion manners according to the different configuration parameters, making it have high security as expressed by calculated criteria including Entropy, NPCR, and UACI. The test results demonstrate that this image encryption method can be qualified to protect sensitive images in real-time.

title: Sparse Deep Neural Networks for Pubic Symphysis-Fetal Head Segmentation
authors: Luu Duc Lam, Laszlo Szilagyi, Nguyen Viet Dung, Mai Tat Chuyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Pubic symphysis-fetal head (PS-FH) segmentation in ultrasound images is critical for assessing labor progression. Still, it remains challenging due to the computational complexity of deep learning models in clinical settings. While existing architectures achieve high accuracy, their resource-intensive designs hinder deployment on edge devices. This work introduces anatomically guided pruning to streamline PS-FH segmentation networks without compromising diagnostic reliability. Building on a U-Net with an EfficientNet-B0 encoder, we apply fine-grained magnitude-based pruning, selectively removing redundant weights while preserving features essential for boundary delineation. The pruned model is then fine-tuned to recover performance. Experiments on a dataset of 6,224 ultrasound frames demonstrate that our approach reduces parameters by 40% (from 6 M to 3.5 M) while maintaining a Dice score of 0.95, outperforming comparable methods like mini LinkNet (2.9 M parameters, Dice: 0.916). Sensitivityguided layer-wise sparsity allocation ensures critical early layers retain low pruning ratios, safeguarding foundational feature extraction. This work bridges the gap between algorithmic accuracy and clinical usability, enabling realtime PS-FH segmentation on resource-constrained hardware. Our findings underscore the viability of structured sparsity for medical image analysis, offering a pathway toward efficient, deployable deep learning in prenatal care.

title: Death Prediction of Heart Failure Patients Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Tran Anh Vu, Trinh Khanh Ly, Vu Le Hoang, Hoang Quang Huy, Pham Thi Viet Huong, Nguyen Trong Le Minh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Heart failure is a complex and debilitating condition that affects millions of people worldwide. Accurate prediction of heart failure progression is crucial for timely interventions and improved patient outcomes. This study compares the effectiveness of two predictive approaches: a statistical model and a domain knowledge-based model. Both models were trained using three machine learning algorithms-XGBoost, Logistic Regression, and Support Vector Machine-and evaluated using standard performance metrics. Our findings underscore the importance of incorporating both statistical data and domain-specific knowledge in heart failure diagnosis, as both models achieved comparable results. The statistical model achieved a Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) score of up to 86.33% and a cross-validation score of up to 93.78%, while the domain knowledge model attained a ROC AUC of up to 83% and a cross-validation score of up to 93%. These comparable performance outcomes suggest that both types of data contribute meaningfully to forecasting heart failure progression. Furthermore, the study demonstrates that supervised machine learning techniques can accurately predict survival outcomes in heart failure patients using only a select set of patient attributes.

title: Multi-Level Deep CNN for Glaucoma Detection and Classification
authors: Sajid Iqbal, Umair Mujtaba, Maham Shafiq, Darab Nisar Ahmed, Talha Amjad
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Glaucoma is a neurological condition that causes progressive damage to the optic nerve. The major goal of this study is to develop automatic ocular image analysis based on deep learning to aid medical diagnosis in glaucoma-related eye disorders. First, VGG16 model is implemented for glaucoma detection and classification. The second model that has been employed in this research is Residual network (ResNet50). The third model is Inception-V3, and the final model is an ensemble model. An ensemble model combines the outputs of implemented classifiers to create a composite classification. The idea of combining all the classifiers is to create a hybrid model that outperforms each individual classifier in terms of classification accuracy. It identifies the four stages of glaucoma disease: advanced, early, moderate, and normal. The ensemble model is proposed and implemented a Deep Convolutional Neural Network for glaucoma detection and classification. The model was fine tuned to improve the classifier. This proposed system had an excellent ability to classify normal and different stages of glaucoma with an accuracy of 98.42%. We found ensemble model is a suitable deep learning method to classify normal and different stages of glaucoma.

title: User Profiles and Perceptions for Predicting Technology Acceptance in Border Control Technologies from Online Social Networks
authors: Sarang Shaikh, Sule Yildirim Yayilgan, Erjon Zoto, Mohamed Abomhara
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: In 2013, European border crossing points (BCPs) introduced border control technologies (BCTs) to improve efficiency and security. Despite their potential, adoption remains limited due to traveller unawareness and insufficient support at BCPs. Traditional models such as the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) depend on structured data and fail to capture complex, non-linear relationships, reducing predictive effectiveness. Recent advances in machine learning (ML) allow the use of unstructured, large-scale data from online social networks (OSNs) and questionnaires, offering improved prediction accuracy. However, existing studies mostly examine individual attributes like age or gender, with little focus on how these interact with user perceptions. Moreover, no comprehensive dataset currently integrates user profile and perception attributes for BCT acceptance prediction. To address this gap, this study develops an automated method to generate a technology acceptance score dataset combining both attribute types. Experimental results show that this integrated approach significantly enhances prediction performance, with the TabTransformer achieving 76% accuracy. These findings highlight the importance of combining diverse attributes for technology acceptance prediction and contribute to broader research by advancing predictive methods. Beyond BCTs, the approach offers practical potential for improving adoption of other emerging technologies.

title: Energy Saving Optimization Control Method for Wind Solar Complementary Unmanned Aerial Vehicles based on Block Coordinate Descent Method
authors: Wenbin Wu, Kemeng Jiang, Weihao Zhang, Xiaobin Sun, Xiaojie Wu, Changming Zhou, Tianxu Jing
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: In order to ensure the continuous and stable operation of unmanned aerial vehicles and reduce energy consumption, a energy-saving optimization control method for wind solar complementary unmanned aerial vehicles based on block coordinate descent method is proposed. By utilizing the technology of synergistic power generation between wind and solar energy, a wind solar complementary integrated DC power supply system is constructed. The system combines operating costs, output fluctuations, and changes in the charging and discharging power of energy storage batteries to form the energy-saving optimization control objective function for wind solar complementary unmanned aerial vehicles, and corresponding constraint conditions are designed; Based on the block coordinate descent method to solve the objective function, obtain the energysaving optimization control results of wind solar complementary unmanned aerial vehicles. The test results show that after the application of this method, the maximum values of frequency variance and charge discharge balance are only 0.0077 and 0.0054, and the tracking coefficient results are all above 0.905, indicating good wind solar complementary ability; After energy-saving optimization control, the maximum loss value of the drone nest after being fully charged is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.44 \text{kW} / \mathrm{h}$</tex>, and the application effect is good.

title: Analysis of Multi-Scale Feature Aggregation in UNet and UNet++ Architectures for Robust Leukemia Segmentation
authors: Hemalatha T, Bukke Deekshith Naik, T Lakshmi, Manchala Nikhil, Vullipayala Prem, Mude Nagarjuna Naik
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Multi-scale feature aggregation is a basic component in medical image segmentation, particularly for encoder/decoder architectures that utilize cross-scale enhancement of information. UNet and UNet++ are widely used architectures that include skip connections; however, they differ significantly in their architectures, depths, and feature fusion strategies. To evaluate the performance of UNet and UNet++ for leukemia analyses, both architectures used a single dataset containing 3256 image/mask pairs of peripheral blood smears (PBS) images of Acute Lymphoblastic Leukaemia (ALL). The dataset was annotated at a magnification of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \times$</tex> so that the entire leukocyte region could be supervised segmented through the model. The models were initially trained under the same conditions, with UNet yielding higher segmentation performance than UNet++ (Dice validation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.8947 / \text{IUO}$</tex> of 0.8331 versus <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.8202 /$</tex> IUO 0.7342). It is likely that due to the simpler skip connection topology of UNet, it allowed for a better ability to propagate multiple scale features within the dataset, since the deeper pathways in UNet++ may have resulted in redundant aggregation of the features, therefore increasing the degree of over-smoothing the features, which made them all appear to be the same. This analysis shows how architectural differences affect segmentation performance, allowing the researcher/model developer to make more informed decisions when selecting models in the development of automated screening for leukaemia.

title: EEG-Based Epilepsy Detection Using Liquid State Machine
authors: Thanh V. Vu, Hanh T. M. Tran, Khoa T. D. Phan, Dung C. Truong, Tho N. Van, Au H. Huynh, Hung T. Nguyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Epilepsy remains one of the most prevalent neurological disorders that affects people worldwide. Conventional seizure detection and prediction approaches rely predominantly on analyzing electroencephalogram (EEG) signals using traditional machine learning techniques. In this study, we propose a solution that uses a Liquid State Machine (LSM) with a feedforward Spiking Neural Networks architecture to improve the accuracy and responsiveness of seizure recognition from EEG data. The experimental results indicate that the proposed architecture can reliably identify epileptic events, maintaining high levels of accuracy. EEG signals were first encoded into spike trains and evaluated using LSM with different feedforward network configurations. Optimal performance is observed in a feedforward comprising 21 neurons, achieving a detection latency of 101 milliseconds and an accuracy of 97.8%. Only a few spikes occurred outside the labeled seizure intervals, highlighting the model's potential for precise and efficient detection.

title: EfficientNetB3-Based Transfer Learning Framework for Robust Multi-Class Traffic Sign Classification
authors: Parul Nasra, Jayapal Lande, Neha Sharma, Ashwani Kharola, Ritika Badhani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Precise identification of traffic signs is a crucial necessity in the advancement of smart transportation systems and self-driving vehicle technologies. Traditional rule-based or manually crafted feature extraction methods frequently face challenges with varying lighting, scale differences, and class similarities, which restrict their effectiveness in changing real-world environments. This study introduces a deep learning method utilizing a specialized Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model designed to categorize ten different types of traffic signs, which are GuideSign, M1, M4, M5, M6, M7, P1, P10_50, P12, and W1. The model undergoes training and validation using a well-organized image dataset comprising more than 6,300 labeled samples, guaranteeing diversity and strength across different categories. Images are subjected to preprocessing that includes normalization and real-time data augmentation methods like rotation, zooming, and shearing, which improve the model’s ability to generalize. The CNN structure includes convolutional and pooling layers, then dropout and dense layers with a softmax output, optimized through the Adam optimizer and categorical cross-entropy loss. The model attains an F1-score of 95% along with robust precision and recall in most classes, showcasing its potential for practical application. These results validate that the suggested CNN model is not only lightweight and scalable but also very dependable for automated traffic sign recognition, enhancing the safety and efficiency of intelligent vehicle systems.

title: A Comparative Study: Machine Learning and Deep Learning based Approaches for Recognizing Named Entity from Bengali Corpora
authors: Diza N Sultana, Ankita Chakraborty, Anupam Mondal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The development of NER systems in Bengali faces challenges owing to complex morphology, word order flexibility, and a dearth of well-annotated data sources. This work presents an empirical assessment of three Bengali NER approaches, namely Conditional Random Field (CRF), a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network, and the multilingual transformer XLM-RoBERTa. Controlled experiments are conducted using a main Bengali NER dataset as well as adding a side dataset with an increased number of samples. The model performance is assessed through macro and micro averaged F1 scores. The CRF model exhibits consistent performance, achieving the best macro f1 as 0.8238 (micro f1 0.8735) in mainonly setting and the best macro f1 as 0.8116 (micro f1 0.8920) in combined setting in 10-fold cross validation setup. The BiLSTM model shows weaker performance due to zero prior pretrained contextual representations, achieving best macro <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{f1}$</tex> as 0.36 and micro f1 as 0.3793 in main-only setting; and the best macro f1 as 0.38 and micro f 1 as 0.4036 in combined setting. The XLMRoBERTa yields the highest overall performance, with the best f1 scores of 0.8120 in validation set and 0.8090 in test set on the main dataset. Addition of side dataset gives similar results often negatively affecting them. In our work, we find that transformers are well-suited for Bengali NER annotation, while CRF models remain dependable in low-resource conditions.

title: A Systematic Review of Generative AI Architectures for Emotion Adaptive Personalized Learning: Impact on Student Outcomes, Motivation and Engagement
authors: Thomson Kurian Jacob, Lopa Mandal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Generative AI architecture employing emotion recognition shows promise for the future of personalized learning. Research has shown that multimodal systems (combining facials, voice, biometrics, text) with deep learning, reinforcement learning and Intelligent Tutoring Systems (ITS) support positive academic achievement and engagement in K12 and Higher Education; although there may be other effects such as increased motivation in specific students. These types of adaptive systems provide real-time feedback through closed-loop mechanisms, allowing both cognitive and emotional needs of students to be met through instruction provided in real time. This review looks at how Generative AI has affected development of emotion detection within adaptive forms of digital learning and their relationship to academic progress and student emotions toward online education.

title: Prior Knowledge Assisted 3D Reconstruction GAN in Fringe Projection Profilometry
authors: Xuehui Shi, Yingchun Wu, Zihao Wang, Longfei Zhang, Li Liu, Anhong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Deep neural networks have significantly improved the measurement efficiency and feasibility of fringe projection profilometry (FPP). However, the predicted 3D contour either inherits fringe periodic fluctuations or blurs the details in the local region with existing end-to-end networks. To minimize the distance between the reconstructed result and the real object, this paper integrates the physical principles of traditional FPP and proposes a prior knowledge assisted 3D reconstruction generative adversarial network (PA-GAN). In the generator, an assisted encoder is designed to learn the prior knowledge (fringe’s coarse phase and the object’s boundary), which is helpful in subsequent feature enhancement and feature filtering. What’s more, a residual convolution block and a residual deconvolution block are designed in the main encoder-decoder branch to collect multi-feature and control the network depth. To precisely guide the network’s convergence direction, a joint loss function is developed to restrict intermediate and final outputs in the proposed network. Experimental results demonstrate the high precision and strong generalization capability of the proposed PA-GAN. The root mean square error of the predicted 3D contours is reduced to 1.6369 mm and 4.4482 mm in simulated and real data testing respectively, representing a decrease of over 30% compared to U-Net.

title: STAR-RIS-Assisted Downlink NOMA System with Index Modulation
authors: Yangling Guo, Xuan Chen, Dong Tang, Jun Li, Yu Huang, Miaowen Wen
abstract: How to achieve higher spectral efficiency (SE) remains a major challenge in wireless communication. Index modulation (IM) exploits the indices of transmit entities to convey information, providing an effective means to enhance SE. In this work, we investigate the integration of power-domain IM with a simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS)-assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) system. To this end, we propose a STAR-RIS-NOMA scheme with information-guided power selection (PS), referred to as STAR-RIS-PS-NOMA, in which the information-bearing units comprise both constellation symbols and power levels, enhancing SE without additional hardware or energy costs. Moreover, we derive upper bounds on the bit error rate (BER) for all users under both perfect and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC) scenarios. Simulation results validate our analysis, demonstrating that the STAR-RIS-PS-NOMA system has the potential to outperform conventional schemes.

title: Integrated Chassis Control Using Grip Margin for Over-Actuated Electric Vehicles With 4WIS, 4WIB, and 4WID
authors: Gyu-Yong Hwang, Dohyun Jung, Jong-Seok Oh
abstract: This paper proposes a new integrated chassis control (ICC) framework aimed at improving both lateral stability and maneuverability in electric vehicles (EVs) equipped with in-wheel motors (IWMs). As IWMs enable four-wheel independent braking (4WIB), driving (4WID), and steering (4WIS), the vehicle system becomes over-actuated, thereby requiring an efficient control allocation (CA) strategy. To address this challenge, we propose a novel algorithm, integrated grip margin-based control allocation (IGMCA). The upper-level controller integrates direct yaw-moment control and sliding mode control (SMC) to compute the control input, while the lower-level IGMCA allocates the optimal forces to individual actuators. Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulations on a Raspberry Pi 4B demonstrated that IGMCA achieved MAEs of 0.71 deg in side-slip angle and 1.67 deg/s in yaw-rate error, as well as an average execution time of 0.05 ms, which is well within the 10 ms sampling period. These results confirm both the real-time feasibility and the effective control performance of the proposed method. In particular, the fully integrated configuration (4WIS+4WIB+4WID) exhibited the highest overall performance, highlighting the potential of IGMCA as an effective ICC solution suitable for various vehicle configurations and driving scenarios.

title: Enhancing Real-World Exposure of Socially Anxious Individuals Through Game Therapy
authors: Christopher H, Surendran R, Logapriya E
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This research will use an innovative form of game therapy that would relieve social anxiety. People with social anxiety problems usually lack interpersonal relationships which do not allow them to develop as both individuals and professionals. This research project uses C++ and node-based programming on Unreal Engine to create immersive environments that simulate real-life scenarios. As compared to a control group that was not subjected to game therapy, their responses improved significantly among fifty individuals who were given game therapy. The possibility to increase the exposure to the real-life situations through the game plays indicates that the game can be successfully used to treat social anxiety in people and to spread insights on managing mental conditions.

title: A Review on Wearable ADM for Metabolic Health Assessment
authors: Aman Sharma, Rahul Kumar Chaurasiya, Amit Lalwani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper reviews recent progress in wearable sensor based Automated Dietary Monitoring (ADM) for personalized metabolic health assessment. The study adopts a hybrid taxonomy-driven approach. We examine how multimodal sensing, including mastication acoustics, inertial motion, and physiological signals, enables objective capture of dietary behavior in realworld settings. The presented work analyzes signal processing and machine learning pipelines commonly used for intake detection and classification, and discusses their implications for downstream health indicators such as glycemic load and heart rate variability. Key challenges and emerging directions, including multimodal fusion, privacy-aware learning, and explainable models, are also highlighted.

title: Leukemia Diagnosis by using EfficientNetB0: A Blood Smear Image Classification Study
authors: Nagendar Yamsani, Shanvi Chauhan, Ritika Badhani, Ashwani Kharola, Neha Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: All Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) is a hematological malignant disease that develops at a rapid pace and is mostly common with children and young adults, in both cases diagnosis is vital as it is important to treat the disease well and achieve a better survival rate. Traditional diagnosis uses peripheral blood smear (PBS) images examined manually under the microscope, and this procedure is both labor-intensive and time-consuming and has been known to suffer inter-observer variability. To overcome such constraints, the proposed study will introduce an automated deep learning system to classify the stage of multiclass leukemia based on the EfficientNetB0 architecture. PBS images are grouped into the four clinically significant classes of Benign, Pre, Pro, and Early by the system. A dataset comprising of 3,256 annotated images is used and stratified based on 70:15:15 split between training, validation and testing sets. By taking advantage of transfer learning and data augmentation, the model can be trained on the tobelearned discriminative morphological features of raw images, without manually extracting and segmenting features. The experimental findings illustrate high levels of generalization, with a testing accuracy of 95 percent on unseen data, high levels of precision, recall, and $\mathbf{F 1}$-scores across all the classes. The proposed solution is reliable, efficient, and scalable to help in screening of early leukemia to reduce diagnostic subjectivity and turnaround time. This paper demonstrates the promises of digital pathology systems based on deep learning in the assistance of clinical decision-making and the creation of intelligent and sustainable healthcare.

title: Challenges in Adopting AI Enabled Inverters in Next Generation Solar PV Systems
