authors: Jitendra Nagrare, M. K Deshmukh, Akshay Wagh, S. D. Manjare, Chandradew Sharma
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: Traditional inverters are “grid-following” inverters, and rely on the grid's signal to determine when and how to switch. Some of the challenges posed by traditional grid connected inverters are: grid instability due to voltage fluctuations causing disconnections, lack of grid support functions to regulate voltage and frequency, synchronization issues in weak grids, cybersecurity risks, high harmonic distortion, and incompatibility with renewable policies. Also, they cannot provide adaptive control and unable to alert for predictive maintenance due to limited monitoring and diagnostics. AI-enabled inverters are revolutionizing solar Photovoltaic (SPV) systems by improving power conversion, permitting grid integration, and promoting advanced energy management. Adopting AI-enabled inverters in next-generation solar PV systems can offer important advantages-such as improved efficiency, predictive maintenance, dynamic controls, and grid adaptability. AI transforms grid-connected inverters into intelligent systems that optimize conversion efficiency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>99 {\%}$</tex> in advanced models) as claimed by Huawei and enhanced performance; 30% faster fault response mentioned by Huawei technical report. By adopting predictive analytics, real-time adaptation, and decentralized coordination, AI-driven inverters play pivotal in enabling a resilient, stable powered grid.

title: Daily Life Pattern Extraction in Single Person Households
authors: Hyungsuk Lee, In-Nea Wang, Junho Jeong
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Recently, research utilizing smart home technology for anomaly detection in single-person households has been actively conducted. However, real-world sensor data flaws, such as noise and battery discharge, cause the 'Garbage In, Garbage Out (GIGO)' problem, thereby undermining model reliability. Furthermore, existing research on fixed sensors faces limitations in logically explaining complex life patterns, such as 'sleep', as it relies solely on simple activation status. To address these issues and lay the foundation for anomaly detection based on daily life patterns, this study proposes a robust extraction methodology that fuses heterogeneous sensors including respiration, heart rate and illuminance and transforms raw data into 'Interpretable Representations', referred to as daily life patterns such as sleep, outing, and activity. Specifically, we construct these patterns by determining dynamic thresholds via K-Means clustering and comprehensively considering global configuration of all sensors. Case Study results demonstrate that the proposed method resolves the GIGO problem by clearly distinguishing 'long-term absences' from 'long sleep' and accurately differentiating actual missing data marked as ' NaN ' from non-occurrences represented as ' 0 ', thereby enabling the extraction of robust daily life patterns.

title: Ahuja-Compensated Three-Stage High-Gain Folded-Cascode V-to-I Converter
authors: Tanmay Samanta, Ritam Mitra, Soham Sarkar, Soumita Mandal, Tapan Kumar Rana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Voltage-to-current (V-I) converters constitute essential functional blocks in analog and mixed-signal integrated systems, enabling reliable conversion of voltage-domain infor mation into current-mode signals for applications such as sensor readout circuits, on-chip bias generation, and power management architectures[5]. This work reports the design and detailed analysis of a high-gain, low-offset V to I converter realized in a 90nm CMOS technology. To address the intrinsic compromise among DC gain, frequency response, and closed-loop stability commonly encountered in multistage amplifiers, a three-stage architecture is adopted, incorporating a folded-cascode input stage combined with an Ahuja-based frequency compensation scheme. The selected compensation strategy guarantees stable operation while supporting very high open-loop voltage gain. Furthermore, careful bias optimization and device dimensioning are employed to suppress systematic offset contributions, while the compensation network is judiciously designed to regulate pole separation and enhance dynamic behavior. Pre layout simulation results demonstrate that the proposed converter achieves the following performance characteristics: •DC gain: 82 dB •Input-referred offset: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<200 \mu \text{V}$</tex> •Power supply rejection ratio (PSRR): -86 dB •Phase margin: 66.2° •Unity-gain bandwidth (UGB): <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>10 \text{MHz}$</tex>

title: CleanHabits: an Intelligent Waste Sorting Bin with Incentive Mechanism
authors: Abhijit Mondal, Nirmalya Ghosh, Snehanshu Bhattacharjee, Arunava Mukhopadhyay
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The paper proposes an intelligent waste management system, CleanHabits, which makes dumping of waste efficient and meaningful. The system uses a combination of AI and sensors to sort automatically the waste based on whether the material is dry, wet or metal. A weighing cell will also measure the weight of the inserted waste material and the entire data will be transmitted to a backend server via an IoT network. Every individual has a distinct identity which will enable the system to measure the amount of waste disposed by each user and reward them accordingly based on the waste type. After various performance experiments the system has shown that it is able to effectively categorize waste, transmit data to the server and also able to record individual user activity logs. It also effectively uses automation and a reward system which makes the system more interesting and interactive for the users which promotes them in good health habits and also encourage them to become more responsible towards the environment.

title: Investigation of RF Performance in n-JL-CombFET
authors: Annie Catherine J, Deekschika R, Mithika Munibabu, Arun Kumar, Rajeev Pankaj Nelapati, P S T N Srinivas
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This study examines the RF performance of n-type JL-CombFET and shows how structural changes can improve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R F}$</tex> matrics. Key RF figure of merits are calculated through the use of well-calibrated device simulations. Transconductance, trans generation factor (TGF), short-circuit current gain, cut-off frequency, maximum oscillation frequency are among the characteristics analyzed. Furthermore, these RF measurements' dependence on the Fin gap is investigated. When the gap is decreased from 22 nm to 5 nm, a noteworthy improvement of almost <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> in the TFP is seen, showing a considerable improvement in electrostatic control and carrier transport efficiency with reduced structural spacing.

title: Robust Adaptive Vortex Beam Communication via Dual-Mode Multiplexing and Deep Learning: The “Petal Pattern” Approach
authors: Ashu Raj, Aman, Keshav Bhardwaj, Shivam Kumar, Vivek Gupta, Rijuparna Chakraborty
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The twist of vortex beams, Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM), is mainly studied for wireless optical links due to its high data carrying capacity, but in real transmissions its performance is seriously degraded by the partial beam blockage and fluctuations in atmospheric density. Our dual-mode petal interferometry maintains discernible angular features even at 55% blockage, whereas conventional intensity-based identification fails when obstruction surpasses 50\%. We investigate a dual-mode multiplexing in which two orthogonal OAM modes with opposite topological charges are superposed to encode data. The resulting intensity pattern is a petal shape, with distinctive angular features. The petal-shaped intensity patterns are strongly resistant to turbulence and occlusion compared to conventional ring-shaped intensity patterns. The value of topological charge is then predicted using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that counts the number of petals and examines the down sampled angular space between them. The simulation results proposed accuracy above 95% in scenarios where the traditional method fails, providing a more reliable solution for high-capacity wireless backhaul systems.

title: Incident Response ChatOps Bot with Automated Dynamic Playbooks for Threat Mitigation
authors: Napat Decha, Apiwit Chantakhad, Pawarit Ponglimagorn, Satayu Imsaard, Somchart Fugkeaw
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Cyberattacks have become increasingly complex, exploiting vulnerabilities such as those listed in the Open Worldwide Application Security Project (OWASP) Top 10 to compromise systems and delay response times. Traditional incident response (IR) still depends heavily on manual procedures, which are prone to human error and slow mitigation. To address this challenge, this project proposes an AI-driven ChatOps (chat-based operations) Incident Response Bot that integrates automation, dynamic playbooks, and human-in-the-loop decision-making. The system operates in two main phases. In Phase 1 (Input Classification), the chatbot-built with Streamlit and LangChain-interacts with users, retrieves vulnerability data from the National Vulnerability Database (NVD) and Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE), and classifies incidents using a Gemini-powered large language model (LLM) with confidence evaluation. In Phase 2 (Playbook Generation), the bot automatically selects and merges YAML (YAML Ain't Markup Language)-based playbooks using NetworkX and applies organizational policies through the Open Policy Agent (OPA) before execution. This hybrid automation model enables rapid context-aware mitigation while preserving human oversight for high-impact actions. By integrating CVE intelligence, policy-as-code validation, and playbook orchestration, the framework establishes a scalable foundation for intelligent incident-response automation.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Anomaly Detection in Financial Transactions with SHAP-Based Explainability
authors: Rana Muhammad Amir Latif, Zakawat Liaqat, Farah H Zawaideh, Marwan Abu-Zanona, Abdulmalik Ahmad Al Jabr, Lamia Hassan Rahamatalla, Abdulrahman Alojail
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: The exponential growth of online banking has dramatically increased the potential for and complexity of fraudulent operations. Particularly with skewed and high-dimensional datasets, traditional fraud detection techniques often fail to adapt to developing trends. Our research presents a new financial transaction anomaly detection approach using a hybrid deep learning framework that combines Autoencoders, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The model employs CNNs to extract spatial features, LSTMs to capture temporal relationships, and autoencoder-based reconstruction errors to detect unobserved fraudulent behavior. Precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC were measured on a real-world credit card fraud dataset, and the results show that the hybrid model performs far better than the conventional machine learning and solo deep learning baselines. We use Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) to ensure the model can be understood, allowing for precise examination of the choices made after the fact. In addition to functioning well in highly unequal class situations, the findings show that the framework satisfies the essential transparency standards for use in regulated financial settings.

title: Gender-Stratified Ensemble Learning for Enhanced Cardiovascular Disease Risk Prediction
authors: Tahiti Ranjit Roy, Satish Kumar Dubey, Ranjit Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Gender-stratified machine learning models significantly improve cardiovascular disease (CVD) risk prediction by capturing sex-specific differences missed by traditional population-based approaches. Using a dataset of 70,000 patient records and 11 clinical features, it is trained and optimized various models. Gender-specific models outperformed the overall model, achieving higher AUC-ROC scores (0.8064 for males, 0.8070 for females vs. 0.7990 overall). The female model demonstrated enhanced sensitivity (0.7090 vs. 0.6790), and precision (0.747), resulting in a 1.22% reduction in false negatives—crucial for historically underserved women. Comprehensive evaluation across accuracy, F1-score, and calibration metrics consistently favored the gender-stratified approach. SHAP analysis revealed distinct gender-specific feature importance, with resting heart rate more predictive in women and smoking status more influential in men. These findings establish gender-stratified modeling as a transformative approach for CVD risk assessment, supporting more personalized and equitable care.

title: Smart PPE Detection System In Industrial Areas
authors: B. Aswith Kumar Reddy, N. ThejaSree, Ch. Ram Kumar, S Alex David, B Prabhu Shankar, B Sakthi Karthi Durai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Safety in the workplace of industrial environment is a crucial concern, and the manual process of observing the PPE utilization is prone to be full of errors, inefficiency, and inconsistency. The proposed paper introduces a visionbased system of PPE-detection that is smart and applies the latest and most sophisticated approaches of deep learning and computer vision to automation of the process of recognition of safety gears, i.e., the helmets, vests and gloves. The suggested system relies on the YOLOv8 object detection architecture that is founded on a substantial annotated dataset collected in the real-life industrial environments which, in turn, incorporates a CSPDarknet backbone with PANet feature pyramid and anchorfree detection head to allow achieving high accuracy and a realtime processing speed. In order to enhance the generalization, the training is done by adding data, transfer learning, rate scheduling. The model performance of a single type of PPE and the system performance are measured using standard measures of model performance, such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1score, and mAP. The overall effectiveness of deep convolutional models in solving the complicated task of object detection is demonstrated by the compared results with the classical machine learning algorithms including the Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost. In order to raise the transparency and trust on deployment, interpretability techniques relying on feature importance analysis and SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlains) are employed. The solution that is obtained out of it is scalable, low-latency, and can be easily interconnected with already-existing industrial surveillance infrastructure to enable 24/7, autonomous safety compliance monitoring, which does not require a human. This paper examines how AI-based solutions in the field of industrial safety are disruptive and lead to a proactive decision-making process based on the data. The outcome contributes to the creation of intelligent safety monitoring systems which reduce the number of accidents in the workplace, enhance the degree of regulatory adherence and provide safer and more productive working environments in the different industrial sectors. The code is available on the GitHub repository: https://github.com/RAM-ch-123/Smart-PPE-Detection-System-In-Industrial-Areas/tree/main.

title: Dual-Threshold Signal Screening and Hierarchical Graph Attention Fusion for Fatigue Crack Monitoring in Composite Laminates Using Ultrasonic Guided Waves
authors: Yiyang Zhou, Mengyue He, Fengtao Wang, Yishou Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the challenges of redundant data processing and lack of adaptive fusion in ultrasonic guided wave-based structural health monitoring, this paper proposes a novel framework integrating dual-threshold signal screening and hierarchical graph attention fusion (HGAF) for fatigue crack monitoring in open-hole composite laminates. The adaptive screening method employs dynamic thresholds, wavelet denoising, and frequency-domain verification to automatically filter out noise-dominated signals while retaining damage-related information. The HGAF mechanism performs data-driven fusion at both intra-path and inter-path levels, enhancing damage-sensitive features. Validated through comparative experiments, the proposed approach significantly improves crack prediction accuracy, achieving a coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>) of 0.9972 and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.3694 mm, demonstrating its effectiveness in multi-path guided wave data integration and damage monitoring.

title: Efficient Permutation-Based Security in Xoodyak for Resource-Constrained Devices
authors: Malik Huzaifa Arif, Israr Ullah, Muhammad Fayaz, Mahnoor Zulfiqar, Maria Imdad
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: The development of applications with limited resources, which includes the IoT, there is a greater demand to deliver data security with strict limitations in terms of computational power, memory space and power consumption. Lightweight cryptographic solutions have become the most important element of a secure communication interface to such environments, to handle those challenges. Among them, Xoodyak, a runner-up in the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Lightweight Cryptography Competition, is a strong contender in consideration of its small size, multi-purpose and use of hashing and authenticated encryption with associated data (AEAD) functionality. Xoodyak provides hashing, authenticated encryption as well as calculating message authentication code (MAC). It employs the Xoodoo 12-round permutation, which can be implemented in the width of 48 bytes, making the implementation very small. The security is stated to be 128 bits, following the NIST-recommended security level for lightweight cryptographic algorithms specifically for lightweight applications (like IoT devices, embedded systems). This security level is large enough to resist all contemporary attacks. This paper concentrates on strengthening the security of Xoodyak by redesigning its inner permutation design to enhance security. A 3d bit Rotation has been added to enhance the confusion and diffusion properties of the cipher. The objective is to achieve better resistance against cryptographic attacks while keeping the implementation lightweight and efficient for resource-constrained environments.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)

title: Multi-Scale Temporal Contrastive Learning for Accurate Weather Forecasting
authors: K. Madhavan, V. Revathy, V. Govarthanan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Forecasting weather remains challenging due to constantly changing atmospheric patterns across multiple time scales. Traditional numerical models struggle to capture rapid, short-term variations and their connection to longer trends. This paper presents a deep learning framework using multi-scale temporal contrastive learning to enhance weather forecasting accuracy. A multi-channel model processes temperature, humidity, pressure, wind speed, and precipitation data through an EfficientNet encoder capable of extracting features at various scales. The contrastive learning mechanism jointly learns representations for short-, medium-, and longterm forecasts. Experiments across multiple weather datasets show a 23% reduction in RMSE compared to baseline methods, demonstrating that multi-scale contrastive learning significantly improves predictive performance and offers a scalable solution for advanced global weather forecasting systems.

title: Terrain-Aware Multi-Uav Cooperative Search Path Planning and Task Allocation Method
authors: Minghao Cai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: As UAVs are now being used for more complex environmental tasks, such as disaster rescue and forest patrol, the collaborative path planning and task allocation under terrain constraints may become a key issue for ensuring efficiency in a multi-UAV system. We proposed a new multi-UAV collaborative search and task allocation method based on terrain perception (TA-MUCSTA) that establishes a double-layer terrain perception map neural planner to enhance the collaborative intelligence and terrain adaptability by building on the traditional coverage path planning and multi-agent task allocation model. At the global level, a weighted voxel topographic map was developed from remote sensing digital elevation model data, a task topology map was produced through visibility analysis with terrain risk weight, and mixed integer linear programming was used for the regional-level allocation of tasks. At the local level, we proposed the Dubins-ACO hybrid path planner with terrain constraints for each UAV, where I combined redundant coverage penalty factor and dynamic obstacle avoidance heuristic strategy to optimize search path. A dynamic alliance generation mechanism with terrain awareness was also designed with the improved Contract-Net protocol, for the case of communication constraints, allowing dynamic redistribution and load balancing to make tasks more manageable. Additionally, the method incorporated energy consumption modeling and a priority-aware task scheduling mechanism based on slope and wind speed, improving reliability and robustness in real-world operations. The TA-MUCSTA experimental results showed the method had the potential to reduce redundancy coverage by 23.6 % in a simulated mountainous environment.

title: Skin Temperature at Acupuncture Points in the Head and Neck Region Across Different Age Groups: A Study Using Atherm Infrared Thermometer Device
authors: Nguyen Huu Duc Minh, Nguyen Thi Anh Dao, Pham Thi Thu Hien, Nguyen Le Y, Che Quang Cong, Than Minh Tri, Nguyen Minh Hoai, Pham Huy Hung, Vo Trong Tuan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Introduction: Skin temperature at acupuncture points serves as a physiological indicator that may reflect microcirculation and thermoregulation. However, standardized reference values at specific head and neck acupoints are still limited. Objective: To determine the mean, standard deviation, maximum, and minimum skin temperatures at five acupuncture points (left/right GB20, GV16, left/right Ex-HN5) across different age groups and genders using the ATHERM infrared thermometer. Methods: A descriptive cross-sectional study involved 360 healthy participants divided into six age groups (18-29, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60s, and 70s). Skin temperatures were measured at GB20, GV16, and Ex-HN5 using the ATHERM device. Results: The youngest participants (18-29 years old) exhibited the highest average temperatures at GV16 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$36.43 \pm 0.57^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>), with temperatures decreasing with age, reaching the lowest values in participants over 70 (GV16: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35.28 \pm 0.59^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, GB20: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35.2 \pm 0.6^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>). Temperatures at Ex-HN5 displayed the highest variability among individuals, while GV16 temperatures were consistently the lowest and most stable. Additionally, female participants had slightly higher skin temperatures than males at most acupoints, although the differences were minimal and not statistically significant. Conclusion: Skin temperature at head and neck acupuncture points decreases with age and shows slight gender variations. These normative data provide valuable references for clinical and traditional medicine applications using infrared thermography.

title: Prediction of Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease Based on Anthropometric and Paraclinical Laboratory Data in the Vietnamese Population
authors: Thi-Thuy Pham, Huu-Phong Nguyen, Thi-Hong-Tham Pham, Duy-Hai Vu, Binh-Giang Tran, Nguyen-Hung Luu, Gia-Anh Pham, Vu-Khanh-An Le, Hai Vu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) is becoming a major global health concern. The prevalence of NAFLD worldwide is about 30% and is increasing. NAFLD can progress to non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH), which can lead to cirrhosis, liver failure, and hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) if not diagnosed and treated promptly. The diagnosis of NAFLD is often based on blood tests, ultrasound, and liver biopsy. However, it is an invasive procedure with a high risk level and a potential for dangerous complications. In this study, we used three machine learning techniques, such as KNN, Random Forest, and XGBoost, to identify patients with NAFLD based on anthropometric information and clinical tests. The experimental results indicate that anthropometric information and blood test indicators such as AST, ALT, GGT, AST/ALT ratio are meaningful in classifying NAFLD. In addition, classifying NAFLD based on the CAP index measured by the FibroScan ultrasound and the AST / ALT ratio gives the best prediction results. The best precision approximately equal to 100% and the recall approximately equal to 92%. The findings of this study contribute to the early assessment of the risk of NAFLD in people who have not yet performed ultrasound and liver biopsy taking advantage of anthropometric data and clinical tests.

title: Research on Security Analysis and Testing Technology of Avionics Systems Based on MIL-STD-1553B
authors: Zhuoran Chen, Hongyu Zhu, Shutong Lv
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The avionics bus system is the core system for aircraft information processing, undertaking critical tasks of data communication and control system transmission. The security of avionics buses is a key issue to be addressed in the field of network security testing technology. This paper develops a kill chain threat model specific to MIL-STD-1553B (hereinafter referred to as 1553B) avionics systems by referencing traditional security kill chain research. It constructs a simulation environment for avionics bus systems based on RPCIE1553 boards and conducts research on security-related testing technologies using the 1553B avionics bus, which is widely applied in military and industrial fields. Since security issues in avionics bus systems may affect the normal operation of the entire aviation system, research on network security test and evaluation technologies for avionics buses is particularly important.

title: Evaluation of EEG Channels Related to Cognitive Tasks: Concentration and Relaxation Using Complex Morlet CWT and Pearson Correlation
authors: Pham Phuc Ngoc, Nguyen Thi Diem Anh, Vu Hoang Quy
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: In today's modern society, various factors such as the internet and social media can disrupt brain focus, leading to increased distraction and reduced productivity. Understanding and evaluating concentration levels offers valuable insights into the brain's functional mechanisms and plays a crucial role in enhancing cognitive performance. Electroencephalography (EEG), a non-invasive technique, enables researchers to monitor temporal brain activity and wave patterns, providing a window into human cognitive processes. This study focuses on analyzing the dynamic properties of concentration-related brainwaves and identifying an optimized electrode montage for concentration measurement. We utilize PSD and the Complex Morlet Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) to qualitatively assess EEG signals in the time-frequency domain. Pearson correlation is then applied across all EEG channels under both relaxation and concentration conditions to evaluate channel-specific responses. The findings demonstrate that the Complex Morlet CWT effectively captures and visualizes state transitions between relaxation and concentration. Notably, concentration is characterized by a reduction in theta-band activity and an increase in betaband activity. Pearson correlation results reveal that frontal channels exhibit noticeable differences between the two mental states (correlation values ranging from 0.12 to 0.59, average of 0.42), while posterior channels show less variation (0.40 to 0.81, average of 0.55). Welch's t-tests confirmed these differences as statistically significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.00001)$</tex>. These results could serve as a foundation for analyzing cognitive states and selecting optimal channels for EEG-based concentration assessments.

title: A Sand Cat Swarm Optimization-Based Backstepping Controller for Trajectory Tracking of Quadrotor UAV
authors: Kang Wang, Zhongxin Li, Jie Song, Zhilin Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the issues of low tracking accuracy and slow response speed in trajectory tracking of quadrotor UAVs, this paper proposes a Backstepping Controller optimized by the Sand Cat Swarm Optimization algorithm (SCSO-BSC). First, the dynamic model of the quadrotor UAV is established. Then, a hierarchical backstepping control strategy is developed for the position and attitude loops, improving trajectory tracking accuracy and response speed while ensuring system stability and robustness. Parameters of the backstepping controller are optimized by the SCSO algorithm. Considering the stochastic nature of the SCSO algorithm, five independent simulations were performed. One representative result was selected for comparison with a manually tuned BSC controller. Results demonstrate that the proposed SCSO-BSC controller provides superior trajectory tracking performance compared to the traditional manually tuned BSC controller, verifying the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach.

title: Towards evolvable IoT device-type identification using few-shot incremental learning
authors: Bowen Ouyang, Xiaoyan Hu, Yuxin Shi, Guang Cheng, Ruidong Li, Hua Wu
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has profoundly transformed various industries. However, with the continual emergence of new IoT device types, their extensive heterogeneity and weak security have posed significant challenges for network management and security. Despite existing research excelling at classifying IoT traffic, they have yet to consider incremental updates and timely identification, which are critical for early device management and security in IoT networks. As a solution, we present EAPN, a novel and evolvable IoT device identification model that supports adapting to new IoT devices with limited traffic. EAPN extracts traffic features from only a few dozen packets and resorts to a metric-learning-based triplet network to capture accurate, discriminative behavioral representations among IoT devices. Then, inspired by Few-Shot Incremental Learning (FSIL), EAPN further considers advancing incremental model updates based on the relationship between traffic features of new and existing IoT devices. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that EAPN not only surpasses state-of-the-art adaptive IoT device-type identification methods but also achieves over 90% accuracy under almost all incremental conditions, maintaining satisfactory performance even after multiple updates.

title: An Edge-Assisted Private Set Intersection Scheme for Privacy-Preserving Vehicular Crowdsensing
authors: Xinrui Zhang, Pincan Zhao, Rongxing Lu, Suprio Ray
abstract: Vehicular crowdsensing enables Connected and Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs) to jointly contribute driving-related data to support applications such as traffic management and accident analysis. Ensuring the reliability of such data requires identifying observations that have been corroborated by multiple vehicles. However, achieving this corroboration typically necessitates comparing each vehicle’s private set of observations, which can inadvertently reveal sensitive trajectory information. To address this privacy challenge, we introduce Edge-Assisted Private Set Intersection (EA-PSI), a scheme that enables secure computation of the intersection among CAV observation sets without disclosing individual data elements. Our design begins with a protocol that leverages polynomial-based set encoding and additive secret sharing, in which each vehicle encodes its observation set as a polynomial and divides it into two shares, delegating one share to a roadside unit (RSU) while retaining the other locally. The RSU then performs intersection computation on the collected shares using randomized encoding techniques, without learning any private observations or intersection results. To support necessary polynomial operations over secret-shared data, we develop a secure multiplication mechanism based on Beaver triples, enabling the RSU and vehicles to jointly compute polynomial products without reconstructing underlying values. In addition, we design a key-distribution protocol that facilitates secure communication among vehicles through the RSU, eliminating the need for direct vehicle-to-vehicle exchange. We analyze the security of EA-PSI under the simulation-based paradigm and formally prove privacy against semi-honest adversaries. Experimental evaluation of computational and communication costs demonstrates the efficiency and practicality of the proposed EA-PSI scheme.

title: Scalable and Efficient Reinforcement Learning for Virtual Machine Rescheduling in Cloud Data Centers
authors: Xianzhong Ding, Yunkai Zhang, Binbin Chen, Donghao Ying, Tieying Zhang, Jianjun Chen, Lei Zhang, Alberto Cerpa, Wan Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Managing a vast number of virtual machines (VMs) efficiently is a critical challenge in modern large-scale data centers. The continuous creation and termination of VMs lead to resource fragmentation across physical machines (PMs), necessitating periodic VM rescheduling to optimize resource utilization. Despite its significance, VM rescheduling has received limited attention in the literature. A key challenge is that, unlike conventional combinatorial optimization problems, the efficiency of rescheduling algorithms is heavily impacted by inference time, as VM states evolve dynamically during execution. This scalability bottleneck hampers existing methods. To address this, we propose VMR <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> L, a reinforcement learning framework tailored for VM rescheduling. VMR <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> L integrates a two-stage decision-making process to accommodate complex operational constraints, a feature extraction mechanism that captures critical relational information for rescheduling, and a risk-aware evaluation strategy that enables users to balance execution speed and rescheduling accuracy. Extensive experiments using real-world data from a production-scale data center demonstrate that VMR <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> L achieves near-optimal performance while reducing inference time to a matter of seconds. To facilitate reproducibility, we provide access to our implementation and datasets.

title: An NSGA-II-based Multi-objective Optimization Method for FBG Sensor Placement
authors: Anxu Peng, Yunhong Zhu, Xiaoping Lou, Mingli Dong, Lianqing Zhu
abstract: Rational placement of fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensor is critical for aircraft structural health monitoring (SHM), as it directly influences overall monitoring performance. This paper addresses the optimization of FBG sensor placement on aircraft wing skins and proposes an NSGA-II-based multi-objective optimization method that comprehensively considers measurement accuracy, fatigue life, and the uniformity-coverage metric. Theoretical framework is established for these performance indicators, and their corresponding analytical expressions are derived. A finite element analysis (FEA) model of a 7075-T6 aluminum alloy sheet is developed to obtain simulated strain, displacement, and stress fields. An improved non-dominated sorting genetic algorithm (NSGA-II) is employed to construct the optimization framework and obtain the Pareto-optimal placement schemes. A 3D Pareto front is generated, where solution P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> (0.0143, 46.7079, 0.3736) achieves the highest measurement accuracy, P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (0.1659, 82.6056, 0.3026) yields the longest fatigue life, and P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> (0.0465, 57.3441, 0.3898) provides the best uniformity-coverage metric. To validate the proposed method, a deformation sensing experimental system was established. Ten FBG strain sensors were installed according to the respective placement schemes, with an additional temperature-compensating grating introduced to correct for thermal effects. Experimental results showed good agreement with simulation trends, demonstrating that the proposed NSGA-II-based multi-objective optimization method effectively balances multiple performance evaluation metrics, offering a practical optimization strategy for the placement of airborne FBG sensing networks with high comprehensive performance requirements.

title: Area-Significance-Driven Attack Strategy Design and Resilient Defensive Control for Multiarea Power Systems
authors: Jiancun Wu, Engang Tian, Xian-Ming Zhang, Zhiru Cao
abstract: This article investigates the security challenges associated with integrated attack-defense strategies for multiarea power systems (MAPSs). Traditional attack models often employ indiscriminate approaches that overlook the heterogeneous significance of different area subsystems. In practice, however, these areas contribute unequally to the overall stability and security of MAPSs. To address this gap, an area-significance-driven attack model is introduced, in which the importance of each area is quantitatively evaluated based on its contribution to global system stability and operational security. This targeted approach enables adversaries to maximize system disruption by selectively exploiting high-impact components. In parallel, an adaptive defensive control strategy is proposed to dynamically respond to the characteristics of detected attacks, thereby mitigating damage and enhancing system resilience. By coupling targeted attack modeling with adaptive defensive control, a unified framework is established that characterizes the interactive dynamics between adversarial threats and system responses in MAPSs. Finally, simulations on a three-area power system confirm the disruptive potential of area-significance-driven attacks and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed defense strategy in maintaining system-wide stability under adversarial conditions.

title: Interacted Planes Reveal 3D Line Mapping
authors: Zeran Ke, Bin Tan, Gui-Song Xia, Yujun Shen, Nan Xue
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: 3D line mapping from multi-view RGB images provides a compact and structured visual representation of scenes. We study the problem from a physical and topological perspective: a 3D line most naturally emerges as the edge of a finite 3D planar patch. We present LiP-Map, a line-plane joint optimization framework that explicitly models learnable line and planar primitives. This coupling enables accurate and detailed 3D line mapping while maintaining strong efficiency (typically completing a reconstruction in 3 to 5 minutes per scene). LiP-Map pioneers the integration of planar topology into 3D line mapping, not by imposing pairwise coplanarity constraints but by explicitly constructing interactions between plane and line primitives, thus offering a principled route toward structured reconstruction in man-made environments. On more than 100 scenes from ScanNetV2, ScanNet++, Hypersim, 7Scenes, and Tanks&Temple, LiP-Map improves both accuracy and completeness over state-of-the-art methods. Beyond line mapping quality, LiP-Map significantly advances line-assisted visual localization, establishing strong performance on 7Scenes. Our code is released at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/calmke/LiPMAP</uri> for reproducible research.

title: Learning Joint Dynamics for Precise Robotic Manipulation in Unknown Environments
authors: Sushant Shivankar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Precise manipulation of objects by autonomous robotic systems remains a critical challenge in mechanical engineering, particularly in dynamic and unknown environments. The complexity of such tasks arises from the difficulties in computing manipulator dynamics, modeling the surrounding environment, and managing the unknown physical properties of objects, which may be deformable or unpredictable. Inspired by human sensory-motor learning, this paper proposes a supervised learning framework to obtain joint visual representations of both manipulator and environmental dynamics. Using forward predictive models, the approach encapsulates the combined states of the manipulator and the object, enabling efficient integration with downstream tasks such as control optimization. We discuss related model-learning techniques, including Dynamic Mode Decomposition (DMD), Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics (SINDy), and Gaussian process modeling. Unlike existing methods, our framework accounts for complex interactions such as contact dynamics, offering potential advancements in robotic system design and manipulation strategies critical to mechanical engineering applications.

title: SmartGuard: Privacy-Preserving Edge-Based Video Surveillance Framework for Suspicious Activity Detection
authors: Muhammad Bilal, Muhammad Ibrahim Arif, Allah Bux Sargano
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Video surveillance has become an essential part of modern safety systems. However, most of the existing solutions depend on large models that require significant computational resources and raise privacy concerns, making them less suitable for domestic environments. To address these limitations, this work presents SmartGuard, an edge based intelligent surveillance framework designed to detect suspicious activities in real time using low cost hardware. Its ability to upgrade conventional CCTV systems into intelligent surveillance systems makes it highly practical for home use. The system operates entirely on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano and employs a two stage pipeline: YOLOv8n detects relevant objects, while a fine-tuned MobileNetV2 classifier identifies activity types. Transfer learning was applied to a modified UCF-Crime dataset, combined with a newly collected fence climbing category comprising 11,000 frames. SmartGuard triggers alerts only when at least 70% of frames within a 3second window are classified as suspicious, effectively minimizing false positives. Experimental results show that the fine-tuned MobileNetV2 achieves 73% accuracy and runs reliably at 8-15 FPS on the Jetson Nano. These findings demonstrate that SmartGuard offers a privacy preserving and cost effective edge solution for real time domestic surveillance. The project codebase-related content is available at https://github.com/INFILP/SmartGuard-CodeBase-And-Results

title: Computer Vision for Real-Time Nutrition Scanning in Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Management
authors: Ann Mariya Jacob, K R Resmi, K R Vineetha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: PCOS stands for polycystic ovary syndrome and it is widely known to be one of the leading causes of insulin resistance and hormonal imbalance among women within the reproductive age spanning from 8 to 13%. Although integrating a healthy diet is a crucial step while managing PCOS, the outdated and frustrating method of manually food logging slows the process and still lacks accuracy. Improving performance feedback is one of the key areas to ensure adherence to a healthy plan. This is the driving reason behind the development of ‘NutriScan-PCOS.’ In the PCOS focused Streamlit App, the PCOS diet MobileNet application nutrition model helps to do just that. It is trained on 4000 images of 80 diverse Indian foods for quick identification and portion size estimation with OpenCV. The driving function of the app is to track meals and overall nutrition value, scoring on a dynamic scale on 5 parameters focused on the impact on PCOS. It is designed to be user-centric, with a clear green-yellowred traffic system to ensure guidance is instantaneous and easy. NutriScan-PCOS runs on a Streamlit interface with options like photo upload, live camera, barcode scan, and daily logs-making it easy and practical for everyday use.

title: Automated Page-Turning System with AI for Printed Book
authors: Truong Minh Duc, Nguyen Dinh Dung, Nguyen Anh Tu, Dong Quang Huy, Nguyen Chi Huong, Hoang Trong Linh, Vu Toan Thang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of an intelligent device to assist reading printed books for individuals with limited hand mobility. The device uses a smart mechanical mechanism and an automatic page-turning system. It supports both eye-gesture control through image processing and voice commands, allowing users to flip pages, pause, resume, or adjust settings using simple eye gestures or speech. Moreover, the system integrates an AI language model enabling users to interact with the content by asking questions, retrieving information, or requesting text-to-speech reading. Experimental results show that the device can turn pages in approximately 2.7 seconds with 91% accuracy. These findings demonstrate the device's potential to enhance reading accessibility and independence for people with physical impairments and suggest broader applications in assistive technology.

title: Hazard Hunter: An Intelligent Rover for Risk Detection
authors: P. Ramchandar Rao, Ch.Rajendra Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The increasing risks of fire hazards, toxic gas leaks, and environmental obstacles necessitate intelligent autonomous safety systems. This study presents the Intelligent Rover, an AI-powered robotic platform with obstacle avoidance and gas and fire detection capabilities. For real-time autonomous danger mitigation, it incorporates multi- sensor fusion employing flame sensors, MQ gas sensors, and ultrasonic sensors. The rover carries out adaptive course planning and risk assessment with the help of an integrated microcontroller and AI-based decision-making. Instant notifications and remote monitoring are made possible via a wireless module. The rover performs well in hazardous and industrial settings thanks to deep learning and sensor navigation. Comparing testing to current safety robots reveal superior risk management, faster reaction times, and enhanced danger recognition, by providing real-time hazard warnings, adaptive navigation, and scalable deployment, the Intelligent Rover raises the bar for security and safety.

title: Research on a Prediction Model for Shooting Performance of Elite Air Rifle Athletes
authors: Diandong Lang, Shengyu Su, Hongbo Li
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Development and Application of a Digital Sports Movement Teaching Platform Using DTW Algorithm-Based Motion Capture
authors: Zhenxiang Tian, Yang Li
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Research on the Effectiveness of “Absolute Safety” for Civilian Critical Facilities Against Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Cluster Interference
authors: Qingliang Meng, Haishan Qu, Yunxin Li, Xinran Wang, Xinyu Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To fully ensure the safety of critical civilian facilities and effectively address the interference problem caused by unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), this paper analyzes the number of UAV capture devices required to achieve “absolute safety” to ensure such safety. First, under different single capture probabilities, the number of UAV capture devices needed to capture a single target and thus achieve “absolute safety” is determined. Then, taking different numbers of UAV clusters as examples, the number of UAV capture devices required for cluster targets to achieve “absolute safety” is analyzed through simulations. The simulation results indicate that the number of UAV capture devices needed to ensure “absolute safety” is related to both the capture probability and the number of UAV targets: the lower the single capture probability and the greater the number of UAV targets, the more UAV capture devices are required to achieve “absolute safety”. This suggests that to ensure the “absolute safety” of critical civilian facilities, it is necessary to effectively improve the single capture probability and conduct necessary preliminary planning to allocate resources appropriately.

title: Forecasting of Students with Low Academic Performance Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Suchawadee Benlengtham, Pusadee Seresangtakul, Pongsathon Janyoi, Pongsathorn Wongpraomas
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study presents a machine learning framework for predicting first-year undergraduate students at risk of low academic performance using data from 8,726 students (2013–2023) at a public university in Northeastern Thailand. A structured data preparation pipeline—comprising data cleaning, feature engineering, and feature selection—produced and selected the top 15 informative predictors. Five models (Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, and SVM) and a Stacking Ensemble were tuned using stratified 5-fold cross-validation. Logistic Regression achieved the best baseline performance (AUC = 0.8015; F1-at-risk = 0.5281). SMOTE-Tomek, evaluated experimentally, increased recall to 0.7282 but reduced precision, indicating limited suitability for operational use. The findings demonstrate that an interpretable logistic model provides effective early-risk identification to support timely intervention and enhance student retention in Thai higher education.

title: Explainable Multimodal Earthquake Event Classification Using AIME Attribution and LLM-Based Narrative Reasoning
authors: Phattaradanai Suwannahong, Piphatpong Wannapanon, Aphisara Klayburee, Takafumi Nakanishi, Ponlawat Chophuk
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Accurately distinguishing earthquake events from non-seismic environmental disturbances remains challenging, as similar patterns may arise from atmospheric radiation, soil-moisture variation, or strong wind fields. Traditional monitoring systems provide raw parameters without interpretability, limiting actionable insight. This study proposes an interpretable machine learning framework integrating seismic, geological, and environmental attributes with Approximate Inverse Model Explanations (AIME) and Large Language Model (LLM) based narrative generation. Using AIME driven feature selection and XGBoost classification, the proposed model achieved 0.9893 test accuracy, 0.9877 balanced accuracy, and 0.9885 mean cross-validation accuracy, outperforming prior earthquake classification studies by approximately 6.2% over the highest performing baseline model (92.7% Random Forest). AIME provided global and local attribution maps, while the LLM translated these attributions into human readable explanations, enhancing interpretability for both experts and non-specialists.

title: Enhancing Wearable Depression Management: Integrating Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting and Acupressure-Based Therapy
authors: Abhishek Kumar, Anshika Arya, Rounak Ghara, Souraja Chowdhury, Aniket Dey, Mayuk Paul, Rahul Das, Samit Karmakar, Soumik Kumar Kundu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This study suggests a battery-free therapeutic shoe that combines acupoint stimulation with piezoelectric energy harvesting to manage depression. By transforming mechanical stress from walking into electricity, the technology drives tailored, non-invasive reflexology vibrations designed to treat physical imbalances associated to sadness. This integrated, self- powered architecture offers a pleasant and promising approach to mood improvement that warrants future clinical testing.

title: Design of an Intelligent Tracking and Obstacle Avoidance Delivery Vehicle Based on Openharmony Platform
authors: Chunyi Hu, Weiming Zhang, Chunrong Hu, Jiayuan Li
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: As modern industrial production transitions into the era of intelligent manufacturing, a growing amount of automation equipment is replacing manual labor. Currently, most automation solutions on the market are fixed products developed by equipment manufacturers, which often lack customization options or incur high modification costs. To address this challenge, we have designed an intelligent delivery trolley specifically aimed at solving material transfer issues within factories during the manufacturing process. This solution enables manufacturers to establish fully automated production workshops without incurring prohibitive costs. The system is built around HiSilicon's Hi3861V100 chip, STM32 microcontrollers, and OpenMV cameras, and it is integrated with the OpenHarmony operating system. It is also connected to the Huawei Cloud platform. This setup allows goods to be transported from a designated starting point to various specified locations using a pre-defined map. Experimental results indicate that the trolley can efficiently plan paths and transfer goods. This makes it suitable for various scenarios requiring automated delivery, such as small- and medium-sized factories, temporary facilities, hospitals, and large pharmacies, demonstrating promising application prospects.

title: Revolutionizing Education with AI: Intelligent Tutoring, Adaptive Learning, and the Future of Smart Classrooms
authors: J. Gayathri, S B Srivathsan, S Ahamed Arsath, M. Lakshy, Rithika Gayathri, R.V Tamizh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: AI revolutionises education via intelligent tutoring systems, adaptive learning platforms and smart classrooms. These technologies personalize learning experiences through AI and adapt instructional content for individual students. Intelligent tutoring systems offer real-time feedback and targeted support that can improve student engagement and understanding. Adaptive learning platforms are capable of modifying lessons in real time based on the progress of students, allowing for improved efficiency and a more customized educational experience. AI provides smart classrooms using voice assistant, automated assessments, and predictive analytics to improve teaching strategies and resource allocation. This transformation promotes access, equity, and efficiency in education, but also raises issues for including those such as the digital divide, ethical considerations, and teacherstudent dynamics. AI is expected to have a more interactive and automated approach to the future as AI evolves with rapid growth. The Effect of Audit Technologies and Constraints on the Effectiveness of Useowa of Teaching Techniques and Learning Outcomes.

title: Preface
authors: Masayuki Morimoto, Hirohito Yamada
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: The 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE 2025) was successfully held in Nara, Japan, from October 28 to October 31, 2025. PREE 2025 served as a premier platform for researchers, scientists, and engineers to share knowledge, discuss breakthroughs, and forge partnerships in advancing power and renewable energy engineering.

title: Automated Classification of Mitotic Metaphase Images for River Fish Species via MobileNet
authors: Somkiad Khenwiset, Urachart Kokaew
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Cytogenetic analysis for fish species identification remains a vital yet labor-intensive task, often requiring expert interpretation of complex microscopic metaphase images. These images present significant challenges due to variability in chromosome size, overlapping structures, and inconsistent image quality. In this study, we introduce an end-to-end deep learning pipeline for the automated classification of fish species from metaphase chromosome spreads. To address the limitations posed by a small dataset (180 images), we employ MobileNetV2 with transfer learning from ImageNet and extensive data augmentation. A systematic hyperparameter tuning process across 24 experiments identified the optimal configuration-batch size of 64, learning rate of 0.001, and alpha of 0.5 -achieving a validation accuracy of 99.87 %. The final model demonstrated high performance on the test set with 99.75 % accuracy. Our findings validate a robust, scalable approach that reduces human bias and significantly accelerates cytogenetic-based species identification, supporting broader application of AI in microscopic image analysis.

title: Investigation and Design of a Full Adder (FA) Circuit from 3T XOR and Mux logic at 45 nm node for Low Power VLSI Circuits
authors: Diwakar Prasad Tiwari, Abhay Pratap Singh, R. K. Baghel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: In the following article, a novel high-speed arithmetic circuit named full adder by use of three transistors (3T) XOR logic and, multiplexer logic has been designed and, proposed for faster digital logic and low power consumption. The proposed circuit has been designed at $\mathbf{4 5}$ Nano-meter (nm) Technology using 8T transistor logic. The proposed circuit shows that the digital application is faster in terms of delay time $\left(\mathbf{t}_{\mathbf{d}}\right)$ average power ($\mathbf{P}_{\text {avg }}$), power delay product (PDP). A 3T XOR gate logic enhanced the switching speed of this proposed device due to fewer components present in circuit. This can be particularly useful in applications where speed is a concern, such as in high-speed data processing or communications systems.

title: An IoT-Based AQI Monitoring System with Integrated Sensor Calibration and Transformer-Based Forecasting
authors: Ahsan Tahir, Abdul Rehman Aldakhil, Sidra Sultana, Nazia Perwaiz, Ayesha Kanwal
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Air pollution is an ever-increasing threat to human beings and the environment, particularly in big urban centers. Numerous of the existing Air Quality Index (AQI) monitors fail to make predictions, sensor calibration is not reliable, and they are not updated in real time. This paper presents an IoT-based AQI monitoring and forecasting system, which is based on a Transformer deep learning model to implement timebased predictions and cheap, well calibrated sensors. This system captures weather and environmental data in real-time, performs feature engineering and normalization, using timestamps to compute and predict the AQI value of up to 48 hours. Good predictive performance is demonstrated by experimental results, and the R2 score is 91.99. The framework assists smart cities in making data-driven decisions by providing a forecasting system of air quality which is accurate and scalable.

title: Model Adaptation Method of Air Refrigeration System Based on Hierarchical Correction
authors: Liangliang Liu, Chang Wu, Xiangdong Xu, Yunhan Fu, Yong Liu, Yu Zeng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The air refrigeration system is a key part of the aircraft thermal management system, which has a vital influence on the credibility of the whole heat management system model. This paper conducts a deficiency analysis of the characteristic tables used in the Amesim simulation model of the air refrigeration system and applies a hierarchical correction methodology, inspired by correction practices in engine models, to revise the characteristic tables of the compressor and turbine. First, corrections are performed at the design points. Subsequently, using design points as a reference, a second-level hierarchical correction is implemented for the entire characteristic tables, utilizing correction factors. The results show that the revised characteristic tables with the two-level corrections significantly reduce the simulation errors of the air refrigeration system model.

title: A Dual-mode Quasi-static Cavity Wireless Power Transfer System with High Angular Robustness
authors: Cancan Rong, Yunpeng Xu, Yingzhou Guo, Haoyang Wang, Qiong Wang, Zhuoxin Luo, Weizhe Cai, Zihan Song
abstract: Compared with traditional Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technology, quasi-static cavity resonant WPT(QSCR-WPT) technology provides enhanced spatial freedom for charging within room-sized spaces. To address the limitations of central pole structures and sensitivity to receiver orientation in conventional QSCR-WPT technology, a dual-mode system comprising pole independent (PI) and diagonal magnetic field pole-independent (DMPI) modes is proposed. It can achieve full-space charging with reduced angular dependency inside the cavity. Specifically, the system can eliminate magnetic field dead zones by exciting two distinct current modes on the cavity walls. Moreover, a cavity equivalent parameter extraction method based on reflected impedance analysis and simulated annealing algorithm is presented. Through this methodology, critical circuit parameters of system can be precisely extracted, effectively eliminating the impact of random errors. Finally, experimental results demonstrate that the prediction model developed through this extraction method exhibits excellent agreement with system electrical parameters. Furthermore, the system enables wireless charging with high angular robustness, capable of delivering over 1W of power to the load with a transmission efficiency exceeding 40% throughout most regions within the cavity. Therefore, this system can provide a novel and effective solution for spatial wireless power transfer, making WiFi-like charging a practical reality.

title: A Comprehensive Comparison between Permanent Magnet Rotor and Permanent Magnet Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Rotor for Low-Speed High Torque Motors
authors: Na Huang, Zhengyu He, Guanghui Du, Keda Wang, Hongji Liu, Wanning Li, Huichong Sun, Gang Lei, Jianguo Zhu
abstract: At present, there are many studies on reducing the amount of permanent magnets by using the permanent magnet assisted synchronous reluctance (PMa-SynR) rotor structure in the field of constant speed and high speed motors. However, the research on the application of PMa-SynR rotor structure in the field of low-speed high-torque permanent magnet motor (LSHTPMM) is missing. Therefore, the main purpose of this paper is to explore the application value of PMa-SynR rotor structure in the field of low speed and high torque, especially to explore whether the use of PMa-SynR rotor structure can greatly reduce the amount of rare earth permanent magnets. Firstly, according to the design and manufacture requirements, two 55 kW, 152 rpm LSHTPMMs with interior V-shaped rotor structure and PMa-SynR rotor structure are designed respectively, and the influence of design parameters of interior V-shaped rotor and PMa-SynR rotor on electromagnetic performance is analyzed. Secondly, the multi-objective optimization design of the two motors is carried out to obtain the optimal rotor design scheme of the two motors. Thirdly, the electromagnetic characteristics of the two optimized motors, the weight and cost of the permanent magnet materials and the temperature characteristics are compared and analyzed. Finally, two prototypes with different rotor structures were fabricated for experimental verification.

title: Revisiting Face Forgery Detection: From Facial Representation to Forgery Detection_supp1-3675018.pdf
authors: Shiguang Shan
abstract: Face Forgery Detection (FFD), or Deepfake detection, aims to determine whether a digital face is real or fake. Due to different face synthesis algorithms with diverse forgery patterns, FFD models often overfit specific patterns in training datasets, resulting in poor generalization to other unseen forgeries. Existing FFD methods primarily leverage pre-trained backbones with general image representation capabilities and fine-tune them to identify facial forgery cues. However, these backbones lack domain-specific facial knowledge and insufficiently capture complex facial features, thus hindering effective implicit forgery cue identification and limiting generalization. Therefore, it is essential to revisit FFD workflow across the pre-training and fine-tuning stages, achieving an elaborate integration from facial representation to forgery detection to improve generalization. Specifically, we develop an FFD-specific pre-trained backbone with superior facial representation capabilities through self-supervised pre-training on real faces. We then propose a competitive fine-tuning framework that stimulates the backbone to identify implicit forgery cues through a competitive learning mechanism. Moreover, we devise a threshold optimization mechanism that utilizes prediction confidence to improve the inference reliability. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves excellent performance in FFD and extra face-related tasks, i.e., presentation attack detection.

title: Behavioral Analytics for Adaptive Programming Education: Insights from Real Classroom Deployment
authors: Peerasak Pianprasit, Ungsumalee Suttapakti, Aekapop Bunpeng
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Personalized support is increasingly needed in programming education, where students differ widely in prior knowledge, learning pace, and problem-solving behavior. This study presents a real classroom deployment of an adaptive learning system enhanced with behavioral analytics in an undergraduate programming course. The system recommends the next suitable task level using behavioral features such as lines of code, cyclomatic complexity, correctness, attempts, and time spent. In this course, 24 students voluntarily activated and used the adaptive feature, while 62 classmates who used the standard non-adaptive workflow served as the control group. Statistical analyses showed no significant difference in final coding exam scores, although adaptive users achieved slightly higher and more consistent outcomes. K-Means clustering revealed three behavioral profiles among active users: Fast Accurate, Consistent High, and Persistent Low, distinguished by engagement intensity and coding efficiency. These profiles were associated with meaningful differences in course grades. The results demonstrate the value of behavioral analytics for understanding learner diversity and informing more responsive adaptive programming support.

title: YOLOv8-Driven Spatio -Temporal Framework for Real-Time Detection of Risk-Prone Patient Movements in Clinical Environments
authors: Kapurapu Saketh Reddy, Puluputturi Nithin Reddy, Vankayala Sanjith, Malli Venkata Sai Vardhan, Lalithamani N
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Patient safety in clinical environments, especially among aged and mobility-compromised patients, demands ongoing observation to circumvent falls and recognize unsafe movement. Conventional monitoring systems are confounded by human fatigue, slow response, and an inability to contextualize patient behavior. This work describes a new realtime monitoring system that combines YOLOv8 object detection with movement analysis over time to provide contextual safety evaluation. Our framework leverages YOLOv8’s enhanced feature extraction capabilities, augmented with temporal analysis to distinguish between safe and hazardous movement patterns. Unlike conventional approaches, the system incorporates temporal consistency checks and movement trajectory analysis, significantly reducing false positives. A key innovation is the intelligent alert mechanism that only activates when a single patient exhibits unsafe movements, while recognizing multiple individuals as a safety condition preventing unnecessary alarms during staff-assisted scenarios. The architecture processes multiple modalities of input with one unified pipeline and has an extensive Streamlit dashboard for analytics as well as visualization in real-time. Experiment outcomes prove better performance with $\mathbf{8 9 . 2 \%}$ mAP, $\mathbf{9 3 . 4 \%}$ accuracy in movement classification, and processing in real-time at $\mathbf{4 5}$ FPS. The approach realizes $\mathbf{3 2} \boldsymbol{\%}$ false alarm reduction by integrating temporal context awareness, setting up an all-new intelligent patient monitoring paradigm that puts together the precision of computer vision with contextual behavior awareness to better clinical safety protocols.

title: MCI-Net: Attention Enhanced Convolutional Network Based on Remote Sensing Images
authors: Quanyue Cui, Jun Lu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing image object detection is currently a research hotspot, but its performance still faces fundamental challenges: existing methods have not fully exploited the unique prior knowledge of remote sensing scenes, such as the structure and distribution patterns of ground objects. At the same time, multi-scale objects and high-resolution data in images also bring computational burdens. To this end, this article selects YOLOv12-N, which achieves an excellent balance between detection performance and computational efficiency, as the baseline model and proposes a new object detector for RSI — MCI-Net (Multicore Convolutional IoU Network), which includes multiple components such as MAF (Multicore Attention Framework), CAS (Convolutional Attention Synthesis) and the Shape_IoU. MAF enhances feature representation and improves detail capture ability by introducing multi-scale and large kernel attention. CAS optimizes feature processing, reduces computational and memory requirements, and improves model efficiency. Shape_IoU loss function not only considers the overlapping area between the predicted box and the true box but also pays extra attention to the shape and scale characteristics of the bounding box itself, thereby optimizing detection performance. MCI-Net performs better than the baseline model on the SIMD, DIOR, VHR-10 and DOTA datasets, with improvements of 6.2%, 2.2%, 3.5%, 2.9% on the SIMD dataset, 1.7%, 0.6%, 1.3%, 1.3% on the DIOR dataset, and 0.3%, 0.1%, 0.1%, and 0.1% on the VHR-10 dataset, and 1.9%, 2.7%, 2.2%, 2.7% on the DOTA dateset.

title: Multifunctional 2D MXene Nanosheets for Biomedical Applications
authors: Thi Xuan Nguyen, Thi Nhat Linh Phan, Thi Thuy Truong, Thi Kim Ngan Duong, Vu Hoang Minh Doan, Jaeyeop Choi, Umapada Pal, Junghwan Oh, Sudip Mondal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: MXenes, a family of two-dimensional (2D) transition metal carbides and nitrides, have emerged as promising materials for biomedical applications owing to their exceptional electrical conductivity, large surface area, and versatile surface functional groups. In this study, MXene was prepared via selective etching of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Ti}_{3} \text{AlC}_{2}$</tex> (MAX phase) followed by exfoliation, and its physicochemical properties were systematically analyzed using UV-Vis optical absorption spectroscopy, X-ray diffraction (XRD), Raman spectroscopy, XRay photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), Scanning electron microscope (SEM), and Transmission electron microscopy (TEM). In vitro cytotoxicity assays confirmed that MXene had minimal toxicity to both normal and cancer cells at a concentration of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$150 \mu \mathrm{g} / \text{mL}$</tex>. The synthesized nanosheets displayed remarkable photothermal conversion efficiency of 24.3%, making them suitable for photothermal therapy (PTT). These properties make MXene a promising candidate for future applications in cancer treatment, bioimaging, tissue engineering and other applications (sensors, energy storage, etc.).

title: RSM-Based Optimization of Dielectric Properties in Insulating Oils for Power Transformer Applications
authors: Raymon Antony Raj, Sarathkumar D, Sampath Kumar Venkatachary, Sheik Sidthik A, Leo John Baptist Andrews, Sivadasan J, Rajeshkanna R, Annamalai Alagappan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Transformer performance [1], [2] and dependability may be improved while possible failures are reduced by making good use of and optimizing these dielectric qualities. This knowledge helps with both material selection and the use of improved maintenance procedures. RSM is a potent statistical method for modelling and analyzing issues when several factors affect a response of interest. It enables us to investigate how variables relate to one another and choose the best circumstances for getting desired results. Because it offers an organized approach to optimization and minimizes the number of required tests, this methodology works especially well in scenarios involving complicated experiments. RSM can be used to improve insulating oils’ viscosity, dielectric strength, and thermal conductivity, among other characteristics. We can improve insulating materials’ performance and longevity in electrical applications by comprehending how these aspects interact.

title: MTB-YOLO: An Enhanced YOLOv8s Model for Mycobacterium Tuberculosis Detection
authors: Yuan Xu, Erli Lyu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Tuberculosis (TB), the world's second-leading infectious killer, poses a severe threat to global public health. Early and accurate detection of Mycobacterium tuberculosis (MTB) is critical to controlling transmission and improving treatment outcomes. While sputum smear microscopy remains a widely used preliminary screening tool, its effectiveness relies heavily on the visual identification of MTB in microscopic images. This task is challenging due to the complex background, small size, and morphological variability of MTB, which often leads to low accuracy and robustness when using general-purpose object detection algorithms. To address these issues, this paper proposes MTB-YOLO, an enhanced model based on YOLOv8s. Specifically, a Lightweight Adaptive Extraction (LAE) module is introduced into the backbone to reduce parameters while strengthening multi-scale feature representation. Additionally, a shallow P2 feature layer is incorporated into the neck to improve small target sensitivity. Finally, the original detection head is replaced with a Dynamic Head (DyHead) to enhance cross-scale feature interaction. Experimental results show that MTB-YOLO achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$86.7 \% \text{mAP}$</tex> on the Tuberculosis-Phonecamera dataset, outperforming both the baseline and other mainstream detectors, confirming its strong efficacy in automating MTB detection.

title: LEO-Assisted GNSS Precise Point Positioning Based on Enhanced Factor Graphs
authors: Shuo Zhang, Lu Yin, Haitao Yu, Zhizhong Wang, Songfeng Yang, Tianlong He, Tianzhu Song
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellations provides a promising means to augment Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), particularly in environments with degraded signals. However, conventional fusion algorithms are inadequate for handling the large doppler shifts in LEO-assisted GNSS integration and lack robustness against the non-Gaussian noise commonly induced by multipath and non-line-of-sight (NLOS) conditions, often resulting in filter instability or divergence. To overcome these challenges, we propose a novel integrated positioning method based on Factor Graph Optimization (FGO) and Preference-based Dynamic Learning (PDL).
