The PDL algorithm learns state-transition models directly from GNSS/LEO observations, which are incorporated as strong prior factors into the factor graph. Additional soft constraint factors are further introduced to construct a robust dynamic model that mitigates system drift during signal outages. Simulation results show that the proposed FGO-PDL method achieves a positioning accuracy of 3.25 cm, representing a substantial improvement over the 7.34 cm accuracy obtained with a conventional FGO approach.

title: Three-level interleaved flying capacitor dc-dc converter with variable dc-link control and alternate 180° phase shift modulation
authors: Rafał Kopacz, Michał Harasimczuk, Jacek Rąbkowski
abstract: Reduction of the passives volume is critical to provide high power density converters and limit their cost. Thus, the paper proposes and investigates a three-level, two-phase interleaved flying capacitor (FLC) dc-dc converter with a variable dc-link control method and novel alternate 180° phase shift modulation for the FLC converter. The new method provides drastically minimized current ripples and allows for a significant reduction in DC-link capacitors volume. The combined approach is introduced and studied theoretically in the article. The concept is also validated by simulations and experimentally, using an MV-rated, SiC-based converter, tested up to 1.2 kV and 10 kW. Furthermore, the comparative study confirms that the suggested method exhibits vastly reduced dc-dc current ripples, including inductor, low-voltage side, and high-voltage side current ripples, while maintaining efficiency similar to that of other state-of-the-art solutions. Thus, employing the proposed method may lead to a lower capacitor volume compared with other approaches, as well as lower rms current values. Overall, the proposed system can be effectively and competitively used in modern power electronics systems. The core contribution of the paper is the proposal of a novel modulation method for the interleaved FLC dc-dc converter that, converged with a two-stage variable dc-link voltage control where the dc-dc converter is fed by an ac-dc converter.

title: SGL-CDP: Spectral Graph Learning for Cancer Driver Prioritization
authors: Sa Li, Tianle Ma
abstract: Identifying cancer driver genes (CDGs) remains a fundamental challenge in cancer genomics, as effective prioritization requires integrating heterogeneous multi-omics signals while modeling the complex topology of gene–gene interaction networks. Existing computational approaches, including many graph neural network (GNN) models, often rely on a single interaction network or employ shallow feature fusion strategies, limiting their ability to capture multiscale molecular dependencies underlying tumorigenesis. Moreover, limited interpretability restricts their translational utility in biomedical research.We propose SGL-CDP (Spectral Graph Learning for Cancer Driver Prioritization), a spectral graph learning framework that jointly models multi-omics features and network topology within a unified architecture. SGL-CDP adopts a two-stage training paradigm. In the pretraining stage, spectral representations are learned independently for each omics modality–including gene expression, DNA methylation, copy number alteration, and somatic mutation–as well as for graph topology, enabling the model to capture modality-specific molecular and structural characteristics. In the fine-tuning stage, these pretrained representations are integrated and jointly optimized using curated cancer driver annotations to improve prioritization accuracy. This design preserves biologically meaningful embeddings, mitigates overfitting, and enhances cross-modality alignment. Across multiple tumor cohorts, SGL-CDP achieves strong predictive performance and reveals biologically coherent pathway-level organization through interpretable cluster–feature associations. The results demonstrate that adaptive spectral graph learning provides an effective and interpretable framework for cancer driver gene prioritization. Data and source code are available at: https://github.com/Ericsaliway0/SGL-CDP.git.

title: A Self-Attention-Based LiDAR Point Cloud Compression Framework in Autonomous Driving Environments
authors: Mingyue Cui, Junhua Long, Mingjian Feng, Juncheng Tao, Yuyang Zhong, Yehua Ling, Daosong Hu, Kai Huang
abstract: Light detection and ranging (LiDAR) sensors are crucial for autonomous vehicles to accurately perceive the surrounding environment. However, the sparsity and irregularity of large-scale LiDAR point clouds (LPCs) bring challenges for storage and transmission. Meanwhile, existing works usually adopt insufficient context and bring intolerable computation complexity, especially for high-precision LPC reconstruction. To address these problems, we propose a novel self-attention-based framework for LPC compression and reconstruction in autonomous driving environments. Specifically, our approach employs a robust backbone for octree-based feature extraction, which can be pretrained and easily extended to various tasks, thereby reducing the need for extensive task-specific architectural modifications. The backbone constructs node sequences of octree by nonoverlapping context windows and shares the result of a multihead self-attention (MSA) operation among them. Considering the similarity in features among sibling nodes, we design a locally enhanced module for exploiting sibling features and a positional encoding generator for enhancing the translation invariance of the octree node sequence. During postprocessing, we further propose an offset prediction model to reduce coordinate distortions caused by voxelization. Experimental results indicate that compared to the benchmark geometry-based point cloud compression (GPCC), our approach achieves gains of up to 54.4% for geometry and 6.8% for intensity, while compared to the attention-based baseline, we achieve up to 99% reduction in coding time. We believe that our approach effectively mines the spatial geometric features in LPCs and has low coupling for specific tasks, which will boost the related applications from algorithm optimization to industrial products.

title: 3-D 1T-nC-1T Ferroelectric-Based KV Cache for Efficient Long-Context LLM Inference
authors: Weikai Xu, Danyun Luo, Minyue Deng, Shengjie Cao, Shuzhang Zhong, Meng Li, Qianqian Huang, Ru Huang
abstract: The storage and computation of key-value (KV) cache have become the primary bottlenecks in long-context large language models (LLMs). In this work, anovel ferroelectric (FE)-based KV cache through algorithm-architecture-device co-optimization is proposed and experimentally demonstrated. By co-optimizing with a token-wise dynamic pruning algorithm, a hybrid analog- digital computing-in-memory (CIM) architecture is designed for dynamically sparse KV cache workloads. To support the architecture in hardware, a novel disturb-free 3-D vertical 1T-nC-1T FeRAM structure is proposed, featuring orthogonally aligned word-lines (WLs) and bit-lines (BLs), along with shared multiple FE capacitor (FeCap) strings. The 3-D 1T-nC-1T FeRAM array can be utilized as a 1T-nC-based analog CIM (ACIM) with small-signal nondestructive read (NDR) for efficient similarity evaluation, and nC-1 T-based digital CIM (DCIM) with robust destructive read (DR) for exact attention computation. Moreover, the nC string-based data mapping and computing is further optimized, which can enhance computational parallelism and eliminate disturbance issue. Based on the above design, a 3-D <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times 32\times 32$</tex-math> </inline-formula> Hf<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</sub>Zr<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> FeCap array is experimentally fabricated, exhibiting excellent memory metrics and demonstrating efficient and accurate attention computation. The comprehensive system-level analysis demonstrates that the proposed design achieves comparable accuracy to full attention while delivering up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$315\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> improved throughput and energy efficiency over NVIDIA A100, providing a promising solution for efficient long-context LLM inference on edge devices.

title: AoI and Energy-Aware Resource Scheduling for Crowdsensing: A Hybrid Reinforcement Learning Framework
authors: Jingyi Xu, Xuying Zhou, Meiyan Song, Wei Wang, Dusit Niyato, Chau Yuen
abstract: Information freshness plays a pivotal role in time sensitive applications within Mobile Crowd sensing (MCS) net works and is quantitatively characterized by the Age of Information (AoI). This paper considers an MCS network where end users submit requests to an edge server (ES) for status updates monitored by a group of energy-acquiring mobile devices operating with periodic sensing. In such a system, the ES must schedule transmissions among heterogeneous mobile devices while optimizing their sensing cycles to better align with user demands. A critical challenge arises from the mobile devices' limited battery capacity, which is not directly observable by the ES. The objective is to minimize the average weighted sum of required AoI and the energy consumption of the mobile devices. To address this issue, we model the problem as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP) and leverage reinforcement learning (RL) methods to find a solution. Key challenges include sparse rewards and a mismatch between user demands and mobile device sensing frequencies. To address these issues, we propose an innovative Hybrid Offline-Online Reinforcement Learning (HOORL) algorithm, which combines the strengths of offline and online RL techniques. Finally, simulation results show that the proposed HOORL algorithm achieves superior performance compared to relevant scheduling schemes, effectively reducing system costs while maintaining stable battery levels in mobile devices.

title: Design and Performance Comparison of GaN-based Ultra-Compact Flyback and Active Clamp Flyback AC/DC Converters
authors: F. Jamshed FJAMSHED, N. Hadifar NHADIFAR, R. Mounesi, T. Mannan, S. Choi, E. Santi, A. Nasiri NASIRI
abstract: This paper presents the design and experimental evaluation of two 5W, 500kHz converters, a conventional hard switched flyback and its active-clamp (ACF) counterpart, implemented on a 10-layer PCB to increase the power density and efficiency. Both prototypes operate from a universal 85–265VAC input and regulate a 5V output across the entire 1%–100% load range. The study evaluates key performance parameters, including size, efficiency, temperature, power density and EMI performance. Experimental and simulation results confirm the superior efficiency and power density of the ACF converter, making it a more viable solution for high-performance, space constrained applications. The ACF prototype achieves 85% peak efficiency, 47 Win⁻³ power density, and maintains CISPR 32 Class B conducted-EMI levels with smaller passive EMI filter components compared to the conventional flyback; the hard switched flyback reaches 80% peak efficiency and 43 Win⁻³ under the same conditions. Thermal tests show hotspot temperatures below 60°C at full load without forced airflow. This work constitutes the first 10W investigation that concurrently reports efficiency, density, EMI, thermal behavior, and control-loop metrics for hard-switched and active-clamp topologies realized on a single embedded-magnetics PCB platform, providing a reproducible benchmark for space-constrained, high-frequency GaN converters.

title: Design and performance test of a compound torsional vibration damper with parallel variable stiffness
authors: Yi Yang, Pu Gao, Hui Liu, Changle Xiang
abstract: Torsional vibration suppression in powertrain systems under complex excitation is a key technology for heavy-duty vehicles. Torsional vibration dampers are currently the most effective torsional vibration control elements. To overcome the inherent contradiction between reducing torsional vibration and transmitting dynamic torque via a traditional torsional vibration damper and improve vibration reduction efficiency, we design a parallel variable stiffness torsional damper (PVSTD) based on the material properties of magnetorheological elastomer (MRE), absorption and isolation theory. The magnetostrictive variable stiffness characteristics of the MREs are explored, the influence law of key performance parameters is analysed, the variable stiffness vibration absorption block and vibration isolation block are matched and designed, and the PVSTD is comprehensively constructed. An PVSTD principle prototype and torsional excitation test bench are built, and the PVSTD frequency shift characteristics and vibration reduction performance are tested. The results show that the PVSTD significantly reduces the torsional vibration of powertrain systems, and the vibration reduction effectiveness of semi-active torsional vibration dampers is significantly better than that of passive dampers during the entire frequency tuning range.

title: Asynchronous UAV Trajectory Monitoring with Multi-BS Feature Fusion in Cellular ISAC
authors: Shaoqiang Yan, Mei Chen, Hongliang Luo, Ping Yang, Feifei Gao
abstract: In this paper, we propose an asynchronous unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) trajectory monitoring scheme with multi-base station (BS) feature fusion in a cellular integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) system. Different from distributed radar systems that rely on wideband radar waveforms and synchronous joint processing, the proposed scheme considers practical cellular ISAC settings such as narrowband orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) signaling and transceiver discrepancies-induced offsets. We develop a single-BS signal pre-processing method that estimates target motion parameters and effectively compensates for time offsets (TOs) and carrier frequency offsets (CFOs) caused by transceiver discrepancies. Next, we design a multi-BS feature fusion method that aligns spatial features across BSs and accurately estimates the positions and velocities of targets based on time delay and Doppler frequency features. By operating at the feature level, the fusion process circumvents the need for coherent signal-level processing as well as the extensive data-level fusion commonly required in distributed radar systems. Furthermore, we propose a cooperative trajectory tracking method that associates asynchronous trajectory observations into consistent local and global trajectories, thereby enabling reliable cross-BS trajectory fusion. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed cooperative scheme significantly enhances the accuracy of UAV trajectory monitoring compared to traditional algorithms.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: A 512-Neuron 500k-Synapse 3.58-pJ/SOP Reconfigurable Spiking Neural Network With Time-step Update Packet Aggregation and LIF/ALIF Neurons
authors: Jingyu Wang, Delong Shang
abstract: Network-on-Chip (NoC)-based Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) provide a scalable platform for neuromorphic computing, yet efficient global time-step synchronization remains a critical challenge. While event-driven time-step synchronization schemes effectively eliminate idle time, it inevitably generates a massive volume of Time-step Update Packets (TUPs), causing severe network congestion and performance degradation in large-scale meshes. To overcome this traffic bottleneck, this paper proposes a reconfigurable SNN accelerator featuring a novel TUP aggregation mechanism. Unlike traditional schemes, the proposed router intelligently merges TUPs along the feed-forward path. This mechanism significantly suppresses redundant traffic, reduces the communication load caused by TUPs. Experimental results demonstrate that this traffic suppression reduces the TUP transition time overhead by approximately 75% (from 20.51% to 6.06% of total latency). At the core level, we introduce a reconfigurable Neural Processing Element (NPE) designed to support both low-power Leaky Integrate-and-Fire (LIF) and biologically plausible Adaptive LIF (ALIF) models. By logic circuit sharing and mode-dependent clock gating, the design achieves dual-mode flexibility with negligible area overhead compared to a baseline ALIF implementation and a 26.55% area reduction compared to discrete implementations. The 8 × 8 mesh SNN accelerator, implemented in SMIC 55nm CMOS technology, achieves a competitive energy efficiency of 3.58 pJ/SOP, 97.80% classification accuracy with 1.19μJ/sample in LIF mode in image recognition and 93.53% accuracy with 8.23μJ/sample in ALIF mode in keyword spotting(KWS).

title: PriLens : An AR-Based Privacy Visualization and Control Platform Design For Transparency Enhancing in Smart Home
authors: Yonghao Long, Mingyuan Zhang, Chen Hei, Xiapu Luo, Kun Pyo Lee, Stephen Jia Wang
abstract: Data transparency is critical for fostering user trust in smart home ecosystems. However, prevailing approaches often present transparency information on a per-device basis, creating fragmented experiences that complicate interaction and obscure a holistic understanding of privacy practices. To tackle this fragmentation, we developed PriLens, a unified platform that uses Augmented Reality (AR) to integrate digital privacy information directly with the physical environment. Through a comparative study of AR, Graphical User Interfaces (GUI), and Virtual Reality (VR), we demonstrate that AR uniquely supports the formation of a coherent mental model of data flows by bridging the physical and digital worlds. Our findings reveal that users synthesize observations of physical entities with overlaid digital data to assess credibility-a process poorly supported by the segmented nature of GUI and VR. Furthermore, cross-age analysis indicates that older adults prefer contextually grounded physical controls over abstract digital dashboards, reporting higher trust in AR. Based on these results, we contribute three design guidelines for effective transparency-enhancing technologies.

title: Functional Reliability Modeling of AFSS Under Thermal Loading
authors: Xiaofeng Xue, Chaowei Huang, Guoguo Fu, Zhuorui Lin, Zheng Wei, Yunwen Feng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The reliability of active frequency selective surfaces (AFSS) under complex operational environments has become increasingly critical, with diode failure caused by solder joint fatigue cracking being a key factor leading to degradation of wave transmission performance. This paper addresses the structurefunction coupled degradation mechanism of AFSS subjected to thermal cycling loads and proposes a multi-scale reliability modeling approach. First, a thermo-mechanical coupled finite element model considering component power dissipation boundary conditions is developed to compute the plastic strain response of solder joints under typical temperature cycles. Based on fatigue life models, the temporal sequence discontinuity pattern of functional failure chains is predicted. Subsequently, electromagnetic simulations analyze the evolution of wave transmission characteristics under different failure chain states, revealing a dynamic mapping relationship among thermal cycling excitation, solder joint damage, diode failure, and electromagnetic performance degradation. Results indicate that the functional failure chains of AFSS fail sequentially at 901, 1045, and 1072 cycles. As the number of failed chains increases, wave transmission performance deteriorates significantly, evidenced by reduced passband transmission coefficients, shifted resonance points, and increased in-band fluctuations, ultimately approaching a bandstop state with complete disconnection. Notably, in the X-band, wave transmission exhibits high sensitivity to structural damage. Using the transmission rate variation ratio at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0. 1 G H z}$</tex> resonance frequency as a key degradation indicator, with a failure threshold set at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 \%}$</tex>, the task lifetime of AFSS is determined to be 901 cycles. The proposed modeling framework systematically reveals the multi-scale evolution mechanism of AFSS functional degradation, providing a theoretical basis and engineering support for reliability prediction and optimal design of active electromagnetic structures in complex environments.

title: Secure AI Model Deployment Using BlockchainBased Verification Mechanisms
authors: V. Udayavani, V.G Nishanthini, S Shalini, N Ajay Karthi, H Mohammed Aswath Sulthan, N. Shamiha Banu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: An approach that has great potential when it comes to securing, making AI models more transparent and trustworthy is to use blockchain technology to deploy AI models. Conventional ways of deploying AI models are prone to model manipulation, unauthorized access, and adversarial attacks. A blockchain is adopted to build a verification framework, where the three key properties of the blockchain, involving decentralization, immutability, and provenance-based access control, can guarantee that the models stored in the Federation are with the integrity and authenticity. The paper also describes verification methods on the blockchain and discusses how blockchain can guarantee secure deployment with focus on cryptographical methods, smart contracts and consensus algorithms. Novel solutions to solve such problems are also presented alongside potential issues such as computational overhead, scalability. The inherent characteristics of blockchain can induce increased trust and resilience, and adversarial security in the deployment of AI systems.

title: Comparative Analysis of Computational and Task-Based Methods for Measuring Attention in Short-Form Video Consumption
authors: Sourab Garg, Ruchika Gupta, Vaneet Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Short-form video (SFV) platforms TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts have quickly transformed digital media consumption and invoke concern regarding impacts on sustained attention. Current methods to measure attention state include log-based measures, task-based methods, interaction clickstream tracking, self-report surveys, and sensorbased approaches. This review highlighted 25 of the most recent studies and organized the studies according to the methods described here to analyze tradeoffs between scalability, validity, costs, and intrusiveness of the research methods. The findings describe clear tradeoffs log-based measures and clickstream and interaction measures are scalable and low-cost but only afford proxy measures, task-based methods generate valid baselines but disrupt normative usage, and sensor-based methods (e.g. EEG, eye gaze, pupillometry) provide high fidelity measures but are of high cost, require specialized expertise, and not scalable beyond lab context. Self-report surveys are still popular, reliable, and valid measures of sustained attention but are imperfect due to recall biases and low temporal resolution. In sum, the findings indicate that there currently is no one method that offers a balance of scalability and validity for SFV in the world. Each approach offers have its own distinct strengths and limitations, with trade-offs between accuracy, intrusiveness, and feasibility. This review highlights the strengths and limitations of current computational models of sustained attention and the path to scalable and data-driven measures of sustained attention in SFV contexts.

title: SAR Data Evaluation and Three-Dimensional Distribution in a Human Head Model Under Different Radio Frequency Exposure Scenario
authors: Sayan Paul, Ardhendu Kundu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Electromagnetic radiation in the environment has increased over the past few decades due to the growing use of cellular voice and data transmission across several wireless communication bands. National and international electromagnetic exposure regulatory standards have been put into place to save people from the consequent effects of radio frequency radiation. The method of measuring the rate at which living objects, including humans, animals and plants, absorb electromagnetic energy when exposed to external microwaves is known as Specific Absorption Rate (SAR). It is crucial to measure the rates at which microwave energy is absorbed in human head models because humans absorb a considerable amount of electromagnetic radiation, mostly from cell tower and Wi-Fi antennas, due to the high conductivity and permittivity of their constituent tissues. In this paper, different exposure frequencies, plane wave incidence orientations, and incident wave polarizations are taken into account when computing SAR values and spatial distributions for a homogeneous phantom head model.

title: Power - Aware VLSI Synthesis Using Static and Dynamic Clock Gating Techniques
authors: Aruna M, Archana Nair, Vaishnavi B U, Shilpa Suresh Jagadal, Yashaswini Yadav B S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Ongoing progress, in semiconductor manufacturing techniques has allowed contemporary VLSI systems to incorporate intricate digital functions into small silicon areas. Yet this swift expansion has heightened issues related to power consumption in synchronous architectures where the global clock distribution network accounts for a large portion of total dynamic power use. The clock tree frequently toggles each cycle even if submodules are not operational causing switching and significant energy loss. This study offers an analysis of power minimization by employing static and dynamic clock gating methods throughout RTL synthesis. Initially a baseline digital design without gating is created to measure the switching activity and power costs associated with ungated clock distribution. Subsequently static clock gating is applied using predefined signals extracted from finite-state machines, counters and control circuits enabling targeted inhibition of clock pulses, during predictable idle intervals. Dynamic clock gating is implemented with XOR-based activity detection and inactivity counters, allowing the clock to be turned off instantaneously when input or register changes stop, to overcome the drawbacks of static gating. This dynamic technique identifies data idle periods that static approaches cannot utilize. Experimental findings show power reductions with static gating delivering approximately a 29% enhancement and dynamic gating achieving more than a 43% decrease, in dynamic power compared to the baseline.

title: Precision Diagnosis of Apple Leaf Diseases via Enhanced ResNet-50 Architecture
authors: Preeti Badhani, Bura Vijay Kumar, Neha Sharma, Garima Jaitly, Gaurav Tuteja
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Apple leaf diseases such as apple scab, black rot, and cedar apple rust pose significant threats to global apple production, leading to substantial economic losses and compromised fruit quality. This study proposes a deep learning-based solution utilizing a pre-trained ResNet-50 model to automate the classification of apple leaf conditions into four categories: apple_scab, black_rot, cedar_apple_rust, and healthy. The methodology incorporates systematic preprocessing techniques including image resizing, normalization, and extensive data augmentation to improve generalization and reduce overfitting. The modified architecture retains the convolutional layers of ResNet-50 for feature extraction while integrating a custom dense classification head tailored for the target classes. The model was evaluated using a balanced, high-resolution dataset and achieved a classification accuracy of $98 \%$, with precision, recall, and F1-scores ranging from 0.98 to 0.99 across all categories. Training and validation curves confirmed consistent convergence without signs of underfitting or overfitting. The low error rates and high accuracy metrics demonstrate the model’s robustness in identifying visually similar disease symptoms. This research highlights the effectiveness of transfer learning in agricultural diagnostics and contributes a scalable, accurate, and efficient framework for real-time apple disease detection. The findings hold substantial potential for enhancing precision farming practices and supporting early disease management interventions.

title: Chakra-3: An Area-Efficient Lightweight Block Cipher for Resource-Constrained Iot Devices
authors: Agnik Maity, Bhavishek Mishra, Swastika Pradhan, Prabaha Mondal, Oindrila Dey, Mihir Lal Saha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The increase in the application of Internet of Things has increased the demand on cryptographic primitives that provide strong security and can be implemented on stringent hardware and resource constrained devices. This paper proposes Chakra-3, a new lightweight block cipher, which is specifically targeted at resource-constrained systems. Chakra-3 is a Feistel structure based block cipher, in which the same encryption and decryption structures are used and complex implementing the approach is minimized. The cipher is of 64-bit data block having the 256-bit secret key and runs 8 rounds of this cipher as an effective compromise between security and efficiency. The lightweight logical operations, compact substitution mechanisms, and regulated bit permutation are used in each round to increase the confusion and diffusion and reduce the hardware overhead. CMOS Hardware synthesis using GPDK 90nm technology node shows that Chakra-3 requires about 1100 gate equivalents with a peak operating frequency of about 120 MHz and a throughput of about 1 Gbps. Security analysis shows that the cipher has a large avalanche effect, with almost half the bits changing on a single bit input change, and passed NIST standard statistical tests of randomness. These findings demonstrate that Chakra-3 offers highperformance low-area cryptography implementation that can be used in secure communications in IoT and embedded systems.

title: Chakra-1: Ultra-Lightweight Hybrid Cipher Architecture for Next Generation IoT Security
authors: Mihir Lal Saha, Agnik Maity, Antarik Sinha, Sayan Chatterjee
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The prevalence of Internet of Things (IoT) in fundamental sectors such as health services, industrial automation, and smart infrastructure has intensified the demand of lightweight cryptography solutions with high security that operate with a very strict resource constraint. The current paper presents Chakra-1, a hybrid block cipher architecture which is extremely lightweight and combines the Substitution-Permutation Network (SPN) and Feistel models. A 64-bit data block is encrypted by the design with 128-bit master key by means of a bit-jumbling permutation as an alternative to traditional S-boxes to provide diffusion at the lowest hardware cost. A key scheduling algorithm without gates is proposed to avoid extra hardware expenses. The proposed cipher features a notably smaller hardware footprint of 448 Gate Equivalents (GE), extremely low power usage of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.390 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex>, and a minimal delay of just 3.374 ns when implemented using 90 nm technology. The proposed block cipher successfully completed all 15 evaluations from the NIST SP 800-22 suite using 1 billion cipher text outputs, demonstrating strong statistical randomness. Analysis of its performance reveals that it is more efficient than existing lightweight ciphers in terms of area, delay, and energy usage.

title: Beyond Hype: Will Ugandan Educators Continue Using Large Language Models for Learning Content Creation?
authors: Tibakanya Joseph, Nabaggala Maria, Tuhame Moses, Ggaliwango Marvin
abstract: Progress in generative artificial intelligence (AI) and large language models (LLMs) has enabled several educational technology breakthroughs that automate the sometimes tedious and labour-intensive processes of generating and evaluating instructional materials. Despite the hype, there is insufficient information on how task fit, perceived ethical influence, perceived utility, and reported ease of use influence teachers’ continuance intention to utilise LLMs for teaching and learning content creation. We sought to extend the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) by integrating the new constructs of Task-Technology Fit (TTF), Social Influence (SI), and Perceived Ethical Influence (PEI) with its long-established constructs of Perceived Usefulness (PU) and Perceived Ease of Use (PEU) to predict Continuance Intention (CI). A quantitative questionnaire survey was administered to Ugandan educators, yielding 231 responses. The data collection and analysis phase took two steps. In phase one, descriptive statistics were computed using IBM SPSS version 23, while the measurement model’s reliability, convergent, and discriminant validity, model fitting, and hypotheses were tested using ADANCO. The study’s findings underscore the significant influence of TTF and SI on both PU and PEU, which in turn strongly influence Ugandan educators’ continued intentions to use LLM technologies in their instructional endeavours. Nonetheless, the ethical dimension, which appears prominent in theoretical discourse, had no substantial effect. In phase two, we explored the unexpected null effect of ethical influence in a follow-up survey on awareness of LLM ethical concerns. Results from 105 participants showed a significant impact of ethical issues on educators’ trust in LLMS. The study contributes to the ongoing discourse on the responsible adoption of AI in education and underscores the need for targeted ethical literacy in AI-powered instructional practice.

title: Subgame Pruning: Efficiently Solving Two-Player Imperfect Information Games by Accelerated Public Tree Traversal in CFR
authors: Shenkai Zhang, Zekeng Zeng, Peipei Yang, Junge Zhang
abstract: Counterfactual Regret Minimization is the state-of-the-art algorithm for solving imperfect information games, yet it struggles against scalability. While existing pruning techniques primarily focus on pruning unreachable branches, they fail to account for subgames in which strategies have temporarily converged. Continuing to update strategies within these subgames leads to unnecessary computational overhead. To address this, we propose Subgame Pruning, a novel regret-based pruning paradigm that accelerates traversal of the public tree by dynamically pruning subgames where strategies remain stable across iterations. To ensure safe and efficient pruning, we introduce two key mechanisms: Pruning Constraint Checking, which verifies whether a subgame satisfies pruning criteria, and Regret Matching Compensation, which defers regret matching and average strategy updates until the pruned subgames are revisited. Our method significantly reduces computational overhead while preserving the theoretical convergence guarantees of CFR. In particular, the exploitability of the average strategy profile remains bounded by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(1/\sqrt{T})$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Experimental results across five benchmark games demonstrate substantial reductions in the number of traversed nodes, with exploitability comparable to that of standard CFR.

title: Real-time Adaptive Waveform for High PRF Alert-Confirm Strategy in Airborne Radar
authors: Ji Hwan Yoon, Ji Eun Roh, Jin Ju Won, Won Jin Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Alert-Confirm detection strategy with a high pulse repetition frequency (PRF) is an effective scanning policy for fast target detection in modern airborne radars. High PRF Confirm generally resolves range ambiguity by frequency modulated (FM) ranging through frequency modulated interrupted continuous wave (FMICW). In this article, based on the analysis of the detection capability limits due to the non-zero chirp rate in FMICW waveform, a real-time adaptive high PRF Alert-Confirm waveform design procedure that satisfies the required detection capabilities is proposed. Specifically, the proposed design procedure aims to achieve clutter-free detection at velocities higher or lower than the required minimum or maximum detectable velocities, and sufficient FM ranging accuracy for resolving range ambiguity, simultaneously. Examples of waveform parameters designed by the proposed procedure are presented alongside the analysis of the required detection capabilities and the probability of detection. The simulation of detecting randomly distributed targets was performed for both the proposed real-time adaptive waveform and the fixed-parameter waveform, and the statistical result of the maximum detection range of each target proves the superior detection capability of the real-time adaptive waveform.

title: Continuous Pulse Interval Variation Strategy and Timing Design for High-Resolution Wide-Swath Spaceborne SAR
authors: Kangxin Xiong, Zhirong Men, Pengbo Wang, Mingkai Lin, Zelin Wang, Jie Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Traditional spaceborne synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imaging, employing a constant pulse repetition frequency (PRF), is constrained by blind zone issues arising from transmit pulse blanking and nadir echo interference. These issues limit the available slant-range variation extent, making High-Resolution Wide-Swath (HRWS) imaging challenging. To address this problem, this paper proposes a continuous pulse interval (PI) variation strategy and automatic timing design based on a non-”Stop and-Go” assumption, aiming to expand the available slant-range variation extent and achieve HRWS imaging through two main aspects. Firstly, by continuously varying the PI to disturb the distribution of blind zones, the proposed method ensures that the echo at the beam center slant range is located at the center of the non-blind zone. This effectively mitigates the occupation of the reception window caused by range cell migration (RCM) in traditional constant PI systems, consequently maximizing the swath width. Secondly, it overcomes the inability of traditional constant PI systems to image across blind zones, thereby enabling continuous data acquisition over a larger slant-range extent. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a 55.44 km swath width under 0.25-m high-resolution conditions, and that the azimuth coverage length is unconstrained by blind zones. Furthermore, the introduced error can be effectively mitigated through non-uniform sampling reconstruction, thereby preventing degradation of the imaging results. Consequently, the proposed method demonstrates its capability to maintain a swath width approaching the theoretical limit under high-resolution imaging conditions.

title: A Hybrid Counterfactual Learning Approach for Electric Vehicles Integration to Power Systems Under Delayed Communication and Cyber Threats
authors: Xinghua Liu, Qianmeng Jiao, Ziming Yan, Siwei Qiao, Shiping Wen, Yu Kang, Peng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The integration of electric vehicles (EVs) into power systems via vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology offers new opportunities for bidirectional energy exchange and resource allocation. However, time delays and adversarial attacks in communication networks can undermine the coordination of EV aggregators and power systems. To address this challenge, this paper presents a hybrid counterfactual learning approach for control of EV aggregators in the multi-area power systems V2G and load frequency control (LFC) framework. The proposed hybrid approach integrates counterfactual multi-agent learning, adversarial training, and monotonic neural network (CMA-HMNN). The multi-agent counterfactual learning marginalizes the impact of individual actions on the overall reward, thereby better coordinating controllable resources and reducing variances in adversarial multi-agent training. By enforcing deviation-command monotonicity constraints within the neural network architecture, the proposed approach can satisfy Lyapunov stability conditions and improve the stability of power systems with integrated EVs. Adversarial training based on the fast gradient sign method (FGSM) is applied to enhance the robustness of the networks against perturbations. Even under concurrent time-varying communication delays and malicious threats from communication networks, the method effectively coordinates multiple generation resources and EV aggregators. Compared with four DRL-based control methods, the superiority of the proposed method is verified on the three-area power system and IEEE 39-bus power system with wide EVs integrations.

title: Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis Methods Based on Adaptive Fourier-Bessel Networks
authors: Weiwen Tian, Yanfei Liu, Yanfeng Peng, Jie Zhou, Haidong Shao, Xiaolan Hu
abstract: Deep learning has been widely applied in the field of fault diagnosis, but most research still focuses on time-domain feature analysis. Frequency-domain-based methods are relatively rare and often rely on the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). However, traditional FFT methods suffer from limitations such as low frequency resolution and inadequate representation capabilities for non-stationary signals, which restrict their diagnostic performance under complex operating conditions and high-noise environments. To address this, the Adaptive Fourier Bessel Network (AFBNet) method has been proposed. This method leverages the high-resolution advantage of the Fourier-Bessel series in signal processing. To address the non-stationary characteristics of fault features in rolling bearing vibration signals under varying operating conditions, an adaptive Bessel order determination algorithm is proposed. By quantifying the time-frequency energy concentration of the signal, the algorithm adaptively adjusts the order of the Bessel function, enhancing its diagnostic stability under multiple variable operating conditions. Building upon this foundation, to address the susceptibility of signal decomposition network methods to noise interference, a band segmentation algorithm based on a joint feature loss function is proposed. By constructing a joint feature-based loss function constraint and optimally reconstructing frequency bands containing critical fault information, this approach amplifies weak fault features, thereby enhancing the model's feature extraction capability and diagnostic accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that AFBNet exhibits good diagnostic performance on both rolling bearing experimental data and public datasets, particularly showing high robustness and accuracy in high-noise conditions.

title: Physics-Consistent Imaging Inverse Problem Solving Framework for Heterogeneous Systems via Latent Space Optimization
authors: Linjun He, Zeyu Chen, Zhicheng Yu, Zhi Li, Yiguang Liu
abstract: Modern imaging systems face diverse degradation artifacts arising from complex sensing mechanisms and noise environments. Existing methods often require extensive parame-ter tuning or retraining across different systems and degradation levels. Although recent generative-prior based approaches par-tially alleviate this issue, they typically assume that target scenes lie within the pretrained model's manifold; otherwise, optimiza-tion may yield degenerate solutions lacking target features and structural integrity. We propose a physics-guided framework for solving imaging inverse problems in heterogeneous systems. The framework reconstructs scenes progressively, from coarse struc-tures to fine details, through a three-phase procedure involving latent-vector optimization and latent-space fine-tuning, thereby relaxing the manifold constraint on target scenes. Furthermore, we design a prior module based on a local linearity assumption in the latent space, which improves reconstruction speed and quali-ty for image pairs with structural similarity through warm start initialization. Comprehensive experiments on CMOS sensors, single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) arrays, and single-pixel imaging systems (SPIS) demonstrate that the proposed frame-work consistently delivers competitive performance under ex-treme environmental conditions, ultra-low-bandwidth constraints, and out-of-distribution scenarios.

title: DBA-SR: A Denoised and Bias-Aware Framework for Social Recommendation
authors: Baokai Zu, Yi Liu, Yafang Li, Hongyuan Wang, Jianqiang Li, Ziping He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Social recommendation leverages social relations to complement user–item interactions, alleviating data sparsity in recommendation systems. However, existing approaches still suffer from two critical issues: social graphs inevitably contain noisy or redundant edges, which can hinder user representation learning, and the results of recommendations are biased toward popular items, failing to capture user preferences for long-tail items. To address these challenges, we propose a novel model named denoised and bias-aware framework for social recommendation (DBA-SR). DBA-SR incorporates two key innovations. First, a structure-aware social graph pruning module adaptively removes weak or redundant edges to construct a denoised and structurally informative social graph. Second, a tail- and popularity-aware preference modeling mechanism jointly enhances long-tail preference learning and suppresses popularity bias through dedicated regularization strategies. The overall model is trained under a unified dual graph reconstruction and variational regularization objective. Extensive experiments on three real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate that DBA-SR consistently outperforms baselines in top-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math> </inline-formula> recommendation tasks. Further ablation studies confirm the complementary effects of structure-aware pruning and preference specialization. This work provides new insights into the role of structural denoising in social graphs and demonstrates an effective solution to mitigating popularity bias.

title: MedTech: An IoT Based Pill Dispenser and Health Monitoring System
authors: Sarit Srimani, Debangshu Chatterjee, Rajnandini Banerjee, Susovan Jana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Many patients find it difficult to take their medicines at the right time and this often leads to health problems. This issue is seen mostly in older adults and people with long term diseases where missing a dose or taking it late can affect recovery. To help reduce this problem we have designed a smart pill dispenser system that supports safe and timely medicine intake. The system uses sensors to check the patient's body temperature, heart rate and skin response. These sensor readings help the system understand the patient's condition at the moment. A machine learning model studies this data and helps the system decide whether the patient is ready to take the medicine or if the dose should be postponed for safety. If the patient forgets to take a scheduled dose the system alerts them. Caregivers can check a web dashboard where they can view medicine history alerts and patient health status. The system also includes a language model that explains system decisions in simple and clear language. This makes the system easier to understand and more helpful for both patients and caregivers. The findings of this paper show that this system can lower the number of missed doses and improve overall medicine management.

title: Movable-Antennas Enabled Multi-User SWIPT in Near-Field Communications
authors: Zhipeng Wu, Yu Xu, Dingcheng Yang, Fahui Wu, Tiankui Zhang
abstract: Conventional simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems are inherently limited by the constrained spatial degrees of freedom (DoFs) associated with fixed-position antenna (FPA) arrays, thereby impeding the effective dynamic optimization of channel conditions. This paper proposes a near-field SWIPT framework based on movable antenna (MA) technology, with the goal of maximizing the total achievable rate for information decoding (ID) users while satisfying the power harvesting requirements of energy harvesting (EH) users, by jointly optimizing antenna positions and beamforming matrices. To address the intractable non-convex problem, we propose an efficient alternating optimization (AO) algorithm by invoking the semidefinite relaxation technique, the Dinkelbach method, and the successive convex approximation approach. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves significant improvement in both total achievable rate and energy harvesting power, while retaining its remarkable advantages in signal quality enhancement through MA.

title: A Low-Power and Area-Efficient Single Exact Dual Approximate Adder
authors: Sonali Agrawal, Vaijanath J
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Arithmetic circuits, such as adders, play a crucial role in digital systems, digital signal processors (DSPs) and arithmetic logic units (ALUs). As adders directly influence a system's speed, power efficiency, and area utilization, their design is critical in performance-sensitive applications. This paper presents a Single Exact Dual Approximate (SEDA) adder architecture, which dynamically switches between exact and approximate modes, using 2:1 multiplexers (MUX). Based on the application, the SEDA adder can be used as a single exact adder or a double approximate adder. In this paper, three variations of SEDA adders are designed, analysed, and compared in terms of power, delay, and number of transistors. The first and existing version of the SEDA adder used 2:1 MUX designed with the static CMOS technique. The second and third variations of the SEDA adder are proposed and designed with Transmission Gates (TG)-based 2:1 MUX and Gate Diffusion Input (GDI)-based 2:1 MUX, respectively. The Cadence Virtuoso tool with UMC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${4 5}-\text{nm}$</tex> technology is used to design and implement all three variations of SEDA adders. Performance comparison between existing and modified adders shows that the SEDA adder designed using TG-based 2:1 MUX and GDI-based 2:1 MUX consumes 44.7 and 69.5% less power and occupies 37.5% and 56.5% less area, respectively, compared to the SEDA adder with a static 2:1 MUX. The performance analysis substantiates that the SEDA adder, realized using a GDI-based 2:1 MUX, offers the most advantageous design trade-off, thereby establishing it as a compelling candidate for deployment in both exact and approximate computing paradigms.

title: Channel Estimation for Massive MIMO-OFDM With Generalized Phase Shift Pilots
authors: Siyuan Ni, Ding Shi, Rui Sun, Xiqi Gao, Xiang-Gen Xia
abstract: We investigate a non-orthogonal pilot design for massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) channel estimation with orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) modulation. Leveraging two-dimensional (2D) beam based channel model, we formulate a signal model for channel estimation with a comb-type pilot structure. Then, we propose generalized phase shift pilots (GPSPs) and reveal that both GPSPs and their discrete Fourier transform (DFT) have constant modulus, and the DFT of GPSPs also has optimal autocorrelation property and beneficial crosscorrelation property. Subsequently, we prove that the inter-user interference with GPSPs is negligible when the weight sequence length of GPSPs is large enough or when channels can be differentiated in the angle domain, validating the feasibility of GPSPs, especially when the statistical channel state information (CSI) is unavailable. Further, by leveraging the correlation properties of GPSPs, an efficient implementation for GAMP based channel estimation is provided. Simulation results indicate that GPSPs enable low-complexity channel estimation while ensuring satisfactory performance.

title: Determination of Principle Resonant Frequency and Optimum Probe Position of Microstrip Antenna with Circular, Square, and Equilateral Triangular Shaped Radiating Patch Using MATLAB
authors: Murari Shaw, Pritam Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In this paper, the principle resonant frequency and optimum probe feed location of microstrip antennas with circular, square, and equilateral triangular radiating patches are determined with the help of MATLAB codes. Analytical expressions for the dominant modes (TM11, TM10, TM10) are implemented to obtain accurate estimations of the resonant frequencies. Results indicate that patch geometry significantly influences both frequency and probe feed location of microstrip antennas. The circular, square, and triangular patches offer compact size and broadside radiation with tunability and design flexibility for specific applications such as WLAN and radar systems. Simulation results also reveal the influence of patch geometry on antenna performance.

title: Online Data-Driven Evaluation Method of Static Voltage Stability Margin for AC/DC Hybrid Power Systems Considering Uncertainty of Renewable Energy
authors: Doudou Luo, Hao Tang, Zhenghong Tu, Ying Xu, Zhongkai Yi, Zhanfei Li, Zhimin Li, Jianing Xu, Kaize Zheng
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy and HVDC technology has significantly heightened the operational complexity of large-scale power systems and amplified the risk of voltage collapse. To enable rapid and effective voltage stability assessment of such highly uncertain AC/DC hybrid systems, a data-driven online probabilistic predictive framework for the static voltage stability margin (SVSM) is proposed. A LightGBM–SHAP–KDE algorithm is developed within this framework, where SHAP is employed for feature selection and explanatory analysis, LightGBM is utilized to ensure prediction efficiency, and KDE extends point predictions to probabilistic distributions, thereby enabling accurate, fast, and interpretable probabilistic SVSM prediction. Case studies on the modified IEEE 39-bus system and the Polish 2383-bus winter-peak system demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior accuracy and computational efficiency compared with EXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), random forest (RF), and support vector machine (SVM), and also achieves better real-time performance compared with the traditional Cumulant-based interval calculation method. Additional evaluations under PMU/WAMS data latency and asynchrony, topology changes, measurement noise and missing data further confirm the robustness and scalability of the proposed framework under complex operating conditions.

title: Sparse Variational Information Bottleneck Gaussian Processes for Uncertainty Estimation_supp1-3675000.pdf
authors: Shiliang Sun
abstract: Inducing-point-based sparse variational approximation scales Gaussian process models to large datasets but tends to overestimate observation noise and underestimate posterior variance. Parametric predictive Gaussian process regressor (PPGPR) improve on point-wise uncertainty estimations, especially for heteroskedastic data, by repairing an mismatch between the training loss and the predictive metric for sparse variational Gaussian process (SVGP). In this paper, we approach uncertainty estimation with Gaussian process models from the perspective of information theory. We show both SVGP and PPGPR use the information bottleneck (IB) principle but in sub-optimal ways, which make the former fail to fully use the input-dependent latent function variance to model uncertainty, while the latter tend to underestimate observation noise and provide ill-conditioned predictive covariance. We further propose a fix for these problems through a decomposition of mutual information and designing two coupled decoders, resulting in a method named sparse variational information bottleneck Gaussian process (SVIBGP). Experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that SVIBGP has the ability to account for heteroskedastic noise as well as provide improved uncertainty estimation.

title: Deep Learning Based Hybrid Assisted Stochastic Unit Commitment With Transportable Energy Storage
authors: Jun-Shen Chia, Wen-Shan Tan, Ze Yang Ding, Wendy Kee Xuan Wong, Savini Disanka Jayasuriya, Yuan-Kang Wu
abstract: The computation load of the Transportable Energy Storage System with Stochastic Unit Commitment (TESS-SUC), when modelled as Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) problem, is very demanding, especially with complex transportation topology and large power system. This paper proposes a Deep Learning (DL) based model to reduce the optimization time of TESS-SUC while retaining close-to-optimal solutions. Hybrid Assistance (HA), a novel approach that leverages DL to support the optimization of the TESS-SUC MILP problem, is proposed. HA ensures a reduction in computation time, keeping the solution close-to-optimal and avoids the violation of constraints made by DL's prediction. Simulations on the IEEE 118-bus system with a Transportable Energy Storage (TES) showed that 94.52% of computation time reduction can be achieved while retaining the optimality of the solution with about 0.06% gap from the optimal solution, without any violation of constraints. Results further revealed that among various types of binary variables, assisting the solver with unit commitment decision variables is the most effective strategy for reducing computation time. Furthermore, correlation analysis indicated that optimizing precision and recall metrics for UC and TES binary variables, respectively, can further enhance the accuracy of DL predictions and ensure greater reduction of computation time.

title: Life Cycle Assessment Informed Real Time Carbon Intensity for Zero Energy Building Energy Storage System Operation: Cross-Signal Carbon Regret Analysis
authors: Inhyeok Lee, Shizhan Ma, Hyeonwoo Jang, Eunchan Yang, Myeong-in Choi, Sanghoon Lee, Sehyun Park
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: Building decarbonization faces the risk of overestimating or underestimating Zero Energy Building (ZEB) emissions when hourly grid carbon intensity and Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) upstream emissions are not jointly considered. This study constructs an LCA-informed real-time carbon intensity (CI) signal by weighting IEA upstream emission factors with time-varying electricity mix and compares it with the UK NESO's operational CI (renewables <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0 \text{gCO}_{2} / \text{kWh}$</tex>) under identical conditions. Both signals optimize ZEB-ESS operations through <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex>-weighted scalarization and are evaluated using crosssignal carbon regret. This case study combines residential measurements from Ireland (StoreNet, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \text{PV}+$</tex> ESS households) with Korean TOU tariffs. By weighting IEA upstream emission factors with hourly generation mix to construct LCA-informed real-time CI and comparing it with operational CI, the crosssignal carbon regret evaluation shows that schedules optimized with operational CI consistently exhibit positive regret under LCA criteria, demonstrating LCA's conservatism. Additionally, annual average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C I}$</tex> lacks operational signaling power as it remains constant over time. The case study of Irish ZEB (load/PV) with Korean TOU tariffs quantitatively demonstrates that CI metric selection significantly alters optimal strategies and expected performance.

title: Multi-Resonant Optical Fiber Gratings for Improvement in Accuracy of Strain Sensing
authors: Xiaomeng Xu, Shanshan Fu, Qimeng Lin, Tingting Gang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Photonics and Applied Technology (IPAT)
abstract: A novel demodulation strategy, which uses multiple modes within the spectral comb of tilted fiber Bragg gratings (TFBGs) allowing simultaneous interrogation of 22 resonances from a single measurement, is proposed and experimentally demonstrated for strain measurement. The stress sensitivity models of TFBGs under three conditions have been established including (1.2805×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup>)x+0.000766344 for uncoated TFBG with accuracy of 89.529%, (1.81101×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup>)x+0.00077432 for TFBG coated with an indium tin oxide (ITO) thin film with accuracy of 98.367% and (2.55981×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup>)x+0.00101 for TFBG etched with hydrofluoric acid (HF) with accuracy of 96.51%, respectively. Experimental results reveal that ITO coating modifies the cladding environment-environment interface, improving the sensitivity of specific resonances, while HF etching reduces the cladding diameter, producing spectral shifts and further increasing strain responsiveness. Comparative analysis across the three conditions confirms that the proposed demodulation method provides highly reliable and accurate 99.79% measurements. The TFBG multi-mode demodulation method enables multiple measurements per single operation, which leads to qualitative improvements in data quality, information depth, system complexity, and long-term reliability, establishing it as an essential trait of intelligent, high-performance sensing systems.

title: Polarity-Dependent Electric Field Modulation via Depletion Region Punchthrough in IGBTs Under Gate-Collector Coupled Stress
authors: Dong Zou, Yanghao Wang, Peng Liao, Xinru Chen, Changhao Wang, Jianbin Guo, Qingqing Sun, Hang Xu, David Wei Zhang
abstract: Insulated gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs) are subjected to critical combined gate-collector voltage stress under extreme <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-state blocking and <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-state saturation conditions, presenting serious reliability challenges. This study identifies a severe degradation mode under such conditions and clarifies its physical origin. Electrical measurements and TCAD simulations reveal that under negative gate voltage with positive collector bias, the IGBT exhibits a strong positive threshold voltage shift and parallel rightward drift in capacitance–voltage characteristics. Both degradation features intensify with increasing collector voltage, far exceeding the effects observed under single stress conditions. The underlying mechanism is attributed to depletion region punchthrough modulated gate oxide field: high collector voltage causes full depletion layer penetration across the drift region, reshaping and amplifying the vertical electric field in the gate oxide. This enhanced field substantially increases electron injection from the polysilicon gate through Fowler–Nordheim (F–N) tunneling, resulting in significant charge trapping and device degradation. TCAD simulations validate this mechanism by demonstrating the depletion behavior, field distribution, and trap accumulation. Crucially, IGBTs with thicker drift regions show minimal degradation in both threshold voltage and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> characteristics under identical stress, as punchthrough is prevented, providing further confirmation of the proposed mechanism. These findings uncover a critical reliability concern in IGBTs under high-voltage coupled stress and offer valuable guidance for robust power device design.

title: Forecasting South Korean Flu Outbreaks with Global Data and XGBoost
authors: Thanin Methiyothin, Natnawat Panisarasirikul, Saeyeon Cheon, Kittisak Onuean
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Accurate long-term forecasting of seasonal influenza is essential for public health preparedness. This study proposes a hybrid framework integrating cross-country lagged correlation analysis with an Extreme Gradient Boosting model to forecast influenza activity in the Republic of Korea. Weekly surveillance data from the World Health Organization FluNet system spanning January 2000 to April 2025 were analyzed to identify precursor countries whose influenza patterns exhibit strong positive correlations with those of Korea. Countries exceeding a correlation threshold of 0.6 and showing earlier epidemic peaks were retained as predictive features. Four experimental configurations were evaluated to examine how different temporal periods affect forecasting performance. Configuration 1 uses 2015 to 2018 data to forecast 2019, representing pre pandemic conditions. Configuration 2 uses 2022 to 2023 data to forecast 2024, reflecting post pandemic dynamics. Configuration 3 combines 2015 to 2019 and 2022 to 2023 data to forecast 2024 while excluding the disrupted years of 2020 to 2021. Configuration 4 uses continuous data from 2015 to 2023, including pandemic years, to forecast 2024. Among the first three configurations, Configuration 3 achieved the highest performance with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex> squared value of 0.708. Including the suppressed pandemic years increased performance further, with Configuration 4 achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex> squared value of 0.759. These findings demonstrate that integrating global surveillance signals with machine learning improves robustness and long-term forecasting accuracy for influenza activity in Korea.

title: Smart Fall Detection and Emergency Alert System for Human Safety Using OpenCV
authors: R. J. Poovaraghan, Vishnu Vardhan A, Mansoor Vali R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This writing conveys the concept and actualization of a device that would forever watch people at the exact moment to realize a drop and send help without needing any human intervention. Such a system is designed to be very useful and mainly focused on elderly people and, generally, people who are prone to risks. Our method, unlike typical ones, employs a computer vision pipeline that uses Gaussian Mixture, Based Background Subtraction and contour analytics on live video to identify changes in posture ratios that reflect a fall. The most remarkable attribute of the present system is its two, mode location scheme; initially, it obtains the user's consent to get accurate GPS coordinates, and if the user refuses, the system uses IP, based geolocation. The coordinates fetched from the device are then sent to the reverse, geocoding server to get the exact address along with the link to the interactive Maps. The notification system is very robust in that it can send the information about the incident and the exact location to the people on the list of the ones to be informed through the custom, made email client simultaneously. The prototype, which is made using a Python stack that combines OpenCV and Flask, demonstrates the feasibility of an end, to, end autonomous surveillance, accurate identification, and context, aware alert system, thus it is a great source for the enhancement of safety regulations and the reduction of the critical incident response time.
