title: Investigating Suitability of Optical Amplifier Design Using Type-I Double Quantum Well Structure considering both CB-Light-Hole and CB-Heavy-Hole Transitions
authors: Subhadeep Sen, Dibyashree Das, Pampa Debnath, Arpan Deyasi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The present work deals with theoretical investigation on complex interactions between conduction band and spin-orbit split-off valence bands for computing spontaneous emission and optical gain in a double quantum well triple barrier structure, under both transverse electric (TE) and transverse magnetic (TM) modes. The subband structure and transition matrix elements in DQWs are significantly altered by the coupling of electronic wavefunctions across adjacent wells, as reflected through the design wavelength range of 1.55 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu\mathrm{m}$</tex> and 0.87 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu\mathrm{m}$</tex> respectively. 7000 cm-1 and 10000 cm-1 peak optical gain obtained for TM and TE modes respectively, which is superior compared to published data. Varying dimensional parameters through simulation exhibits the suitability of design parameters for higher optical peak closest to the central wavelength, and that speaks in favor of the proposed study. Simulated findings will help to design optical amplifier, quantum LED and integrated photonic systems.

title: Offline VQA Chatbot for On-Device Image Understanding A Privacy-Preserving Edge-AI Approach
authors: K Nisna Haneefa, A Archana, M Hariprakash, K Muhammed Niyas, M Sreelakshmi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: We report the creation of an Off-line Visual Ques- tion Answering (VQA) Chatbot, a standalone multimodal AI system able to understand and respond to natural language questions regarding images without needing access to the internet. In contrast to traditional VQA models that use cloud-based APIs, the chatbot runs fully on local hardware, providing user privacy, faster response, and increased accessibility in bandwidth-limited or privacy-sensitive settings. The system combines a quantised vision–language transformer. (Qwen-VL 2B) with an optical character recognition (OCR) pipeline to enhance performance on text-heavy visual inputs. It also supports an adaptive cross-attention mechanism for effective multimodal alignment and a Streamlit-based conversational interface optimized for usability in educational and assistive domains. To support realistic offline deployment, the model is optimized with 4-bit weight quantisation and lightweighth decoding strategies. Testing on a custom 7,250-image benchmark demonstrates that the suggested system achieves an overall answer accuracy of 87 Index Terms—Visual Question Answering (VQA), Edge Computation, Vision–Language Transformers, Multimodal Deep Learning, Offline Artificial Intelligence, Privacy-Preserving Inference, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Optical Character Recognition (OCR), On-Device Machine Learning, Low-Latency Inference, Model Compression, Quantised Neural Networks, Explainable AI (XAI), Human-Computer Interaction, Educational Technology, Assistive Systems, Visual Reasoning, Streamlit Interface, Open-Source Deployment, Real-Time Computer Vision. To enable practical offline deployment, the model is optimized using 4-bit weight quantisation and lightweight decoding strategies. Evaluation on a custom 7,250-image benchmark shows that the proposed system achieves an overall answer accuracy of 87 percentage OCR-specific accuracy of 92.3 percentage, and a median response latency of 2.1 seconds on an RTX 3050-Ti GPU—comparable to state-of-the-art online models like MiniGPT-4, while using significantly less memory. The architecture supports optional explainability via Grad-CAM overlays, enabling visual insight into model decisions. These results validate the chatbot’s applicability for real-world use in classrooms, offline applications, and remote support systems.

title: Task Situation Analysis and Modeling of Aircraft Swarms Confronting Base Clusters Based on the Comprehensive Force Field Method
authors: Minghui Sun, Guoxin Qu, Yu Cao, Xurui Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address task situation assessment for hypersonic swarms confronting base clusters, based on improved artificial potential field, this paper proposes a comprehensive force field model. By analyzing layered response and denial capabilities of bases, a field coupling resistance and repulsion is built to transform base influences into kinetic energy attenuation. Combined with Monte Carlo simulations, the mapping between force field and penetration probability is derived, with constraint equations established. This achieves quantitative impact evaluation and situation modeling, providing a theoretical basis for task planning. Validation results demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits strong dynamic adaptability and robustness.

title: An AI Education Framework Based on LLM-Knowledge Graph for Personalized Learning
authors: Zihao Liang, Yiou Wang, Mingjie Zhao, Sen Feng, Yunfan Zhang, Yiqun Zhang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Educational applications of Large Language Models (LLMs) face two key challenges: balancing their generative adaptability with factual reliability, and ensuring content accuracy within personalized learning paths. This paper presents LKPS (Large Language Model-Knowledge Graph for Personalized Learning System), an adaptive learning system that addresses these issues via two core innovations: (1) the SafeRAG framework, which uses structured prompting, multimodal cross-verification, and post-process verification to minimize hallucinations while maintaining content reliability; and (2) a hybrid path planner that integrates collaborative filtering with real-time knowledge tracing for personalized pathway optimization. In simulated multimodal machine learning scenarios, LKPS significantly outperforms traditional methods in both personalization and learning success, offering a viable pathway toward secure and deeply personalized educational AI.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Neural Networks for Brain Tumor Classification in MRI Images
authors: Thanh-Hung Nguyen, Thi-Thoa Mac, Hoang-Hiep Ly, Duc-Dat Mai, Dang-Khoa Pham, Duy Phan, Xuan-Thuan Nguyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is now widely used in healthcare, especially for helping doctors diagnose diseases using medical images. Deep learning (DL), a subset of AI, excels at learning complex patterns from data. This study focuses on applying DL for the brain tumors classification from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans, specifically targeting three common types: glioma, meningioma, and pituitary adenoma. We examined five deep learning modelsVGG16, EfficientNetB3, DenseNet121, MobileNetV2, and a novel hybrid architecture termed Swim-EfficientNet, which integrates principles from Swin Transformer and EfficientNet-to analyze their effectiveness in identifying these brain tumors. Each model was trained on preprocessed MRI images and evaluated with metrics including accuracy, recall, and inference time. All evaluated models demonstrated strong performance, achieving classification accuracies exceeding 99%. Notably, the Swim-EfficientNet model yielded the highest performance, reaching 99.9 % for both accuracy and recall. MobileNetV2 exhibited the fastest inference speed, making it advantageous for applications where rapid prediction is crucial. This study demonstrates that hybrid architectures, such as Swim-EfficientNet, can significantly enhance the performance of AI systems for medical image classification. These advanced models hold considerable potential for assisting clinicians in the rapid and accurate detection of brain tumors. Future work could involve expanding this approach to other medical imaging tasks or integrating these models into real-time clinical decision support tools.

title: Brain-Computer Interface Based Mental Workload Classification: Utilizing EEG Signal and N-Back Test Experiment
authors: Piyush Meshram, Mitul Kumar Ahirwal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Mental workload (MWL) plays an important role in human performance, especially in high-risk settings like aviation and emergency services. Overload and underload of cognitive resources can impair decision-making, result in slow response time, and undermine task performance. Thus, real-time MWL measurement is essential for adaptive and intelligent systems. In this work, we explored the use of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals for classifying mental workload levels learned during an N-back task. A simple one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) is used to learn EEG features representing different levels of mental workload intensity. To overcome low performance issues of model because of small size of dataset, segmentation and Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) are employed, synthetic samples were generated to increase the size of dataset. The baseline model trained over original dataset have average F1-score and accuracy of 36.37% and 38.33%, respectively. But with the use of segmentation and SMOTE, significant improvement is observed in mean F1-score and accuracy, 76.36 and 76.57, respectively. These findings show the efficiency of signal segmentation combined with SMOTE in EEG-based mental workload classification. The process simulates the use of brain-computer interface (BCI) technology for MWL classification in real-world applications.

title: Development of an EfficientNetV2L and YOLO Model for Lung Anomaly Detection
authors: Rin Bui Thanh, Anh Dao Trong Ngoc, Phuc Nguyen Huu, Thao Dao Thu Le, Hoang Tran Manh, Quang Tran Minh, Phat Nguyen Huu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Lung diseases such as pneumonia, tuberculosis, and lung cancer are among the leading causes of death worldwide. Early detection and accurate diagnosis play a crucial role in improving treatment outcomes. This paper presents an artificial intelligence-based system for detecting lung anomalies from Xray images. The proposed system integrates EfficientNetV2L for classification, energy-based models (EBM) and gradient-weighted attention mapping (GAM) for refining and highlighting features, and a combination of DenseNet-121 and YOLO for lesion localization. The dataset consists of over 5,500 X-ray images from Kaggle and VinAI. Experimental results demonstrate high accuracy (93.5%) on the training dataset that can be applied to real-world applications. This system has the potential to assist doctors in making faster and more precise diagnoses, reducing workload in the healthcare sector.

title: Evaluation and Improvement of the Design of Mastectomy Bra for Breast Cancer Patients Applying Virtual Try-on Simulation
authors: Nguyen Thi Thuy Ngoc, Tran Thuy Chinh, Tiendung Vu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Mastectomy bras are essential for women with breast cancer after surgery. Mastectomy bras need to ensure a good fitting and comfort for each body. This paper presents the results of the research that continues the previous research on the shape design of breast prosthesis for mastectomy bras. In this new study, the design of the mastectomy bra and the breast prosthesis were improved to ensure better elasticity and comfort. CLO3D virtual fitting software was applied to simulate the fitting of the bra and breast prosthesis on a three-dimensional model of the female body after surgery. The one-breast body model for virtual fitting simulation was obtained from the real human body scan data. The bra and breast prosthesis meet the requirements of pressure and stretch according to the evaluation results from this software. The bra strap was improved with a larger width to reduce the pressure on the body's shoulders. The breast prosthesis is adjusted to the thickness and made air convection holes to ensure better fitting and comfort. The fitting simulation method allows for a preliminary assessment of the external shape and fitting. The results of the study showed that the virtual try-on simulation on the chest area of a one-breast body model was successful. This is the basis for developing this method in the design completion of the bras and breast prosthesis.

title: Efficient Fog-Cloud Resource Allocation Using Starfish Optimization Algorithm
authors: R Baskar, S Bharath Kumar, S Kaviyarasu, D Dhanushyaa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Efficient resource allocation in fog-cloud computing environments is critical to ensuring low latency, high throughput, and optimal utilization of computational resources. This paper proposes a novel approach using the Starfish Optimization Algorithm (SFOA) to address the challenges of dynamic task scheduling and resource distribution across fog and cloud layers. Inspired by the natural behaviors of starfish, SFOA employs exploration, preying, and regeneration strategies to balance global search and local exploitation, effectively avoiding local optima. Experimental results demonstrate that SFOA reduces average task completion time to $\mathbf{1 2 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 4 5} \mathbf{~ m s}$, achieves throughput of $40-45$ tasks/sec, improves CPU and memory utilization to 50% and 40%, and lowers energy consumption to $55-68 \mathrm{kWh}$, outperforming conventional metaheuristic algorithms. The results highlight the potential of bio-inspired optimization techniques for enhancing fog-cloud resource management.

title: Modeling and Design of a Fast Steering Mirror Actuated by Piezoelectric Actuator
authors: Ziyu Gao, Binzheng Wen, Zhijie Wen, Shixun Fan, Dapeng Fan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To meet the demands for high bandwidth and large stroke in piezoelectric-actuated fast steering mirrors (FSM), this study presents the design and validation of an FSM driven by piezoelectric actuators. Based on the pseudo-rigid-body model and Lagrange's equations of motion, a mathematical model of the amplified piezoelectric actuator (APA) and the FSM system was established. This model links system-level performance requirements to the APA design, ensuring that the mechanism aligns with overall specifications. Through finite element simulations and experimental tests, it was verified that the designed FSM achieved a two-axis angular deflection of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1.6 \text{mrad}$</tex>, with a first-order resonant frequency exceeding 2800 Hz. This work provides a theoretical foundation and a systematic design methodology for high-performance FSM systems.

title: Forecasting Thai Gold Prices Using Correlation-Based Feature Selection and Vector Error Correction Model (VECM)
authors: Soontarin Nupap, Thinzar Htet, Tint Tint Htay, Nyein Pyae Sone, Nilubon Kurubanjerdjit
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Gold plays a central role in Thailand's investment landscape as a hedge against inflation and economic uncertainty; however, accurate price forecasting remains challenging due to high volatility and complex interactions among macroeconomic variables. This study proposes an integrated forecasting framework for Thai gold prices that combines Pearson correlation-based feature selection with the Vector Error Correction Model (VECM). Using daily data spanning 2019-2023, correlation analysis is first employed to identify key determinants with strong linear associations to domestic gold prices. The selected variables include global gold prices, total gold reserves, government debt, and the THB/USD exchange rate. Subsequently, a VECM is constructed to jointly model short-run dynamics and long-run equilibrium relationships among cointegrated variables. Model performance is evaluated using a rolling one-step-ahead forecasting scheme and benchmarked against three commonly used models: the Random Walk (naïve) model, the Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) model, and the Vector Autoregression (VAR) model. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed VECM consistently outperforms all benchmark approaches, achieving the highest explanatory power (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=0.9647$</tex>) alongside the lowest forecasting errors. These findings confirm the significance of both global and domestic macroeconomic transmission mechanisms in Thailand's gold market and highlight the effectiveness of cointegration-based modelling for reliable gold price forecasting.

title:
H
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    GIN: Hierarchical Harmonic Graph Isomorphism Network for Neural Harmonic Pattern Recognition
authors: Muhammad Abdullah, Tanzila Kehkashan, Faheem Akbar, Wasim Akram
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Musical harmony is one of the key elements of composition in various traditions, with computational methods being used more to determine meaningful features in complex music. The recognition of harmonic pattern is a particular area of study that aims at recognizing common chordal progressions and tonal connections that form part of the musically coherent and expressive qualities. Although neural methods of music analysis have made great progress, the current models are unable to perform local chord-to-chord transition and global harmonic structure at the same time, and are cross-genre flexible. The proposed research seeks to come up with a new neural structure of extracting hierarchical harmonic patterns, which are consistent with the human sensation in various musical situations. We present a Hierarchical Harmonic Graph Isomorphism Network (H2GIN) which is a network that represents harmonic progressions as multi-level graph structures using data in the Million Song Dataset with Spotify and Last.fm extensions and music-theoretical constraints. The experimental analysis has shown that our method produces a high harmonic pattern recognition accuracy with the F1 score of 0.927 significantly better than state-of-the-art techniques and sufficiently computationally efficient to be applied in real-time. This development would expand computational musicology to a more precise form of harmonic analysis, offer new composition instruments, and offer better learning materials on the study of complex harmonic relations among musical cultures.

title: Research on Controlling Strategy of Autonomous VTOL and Transition Flight for Tiltrotor UAVs
authors: Shang Gao, Zhaonan Zuo, Gang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Nowadays, It is a hot topic of current era that the urban air transportation is rapidly promoted. The complex urban air traffic environment requires aircraft to be equipped with the ability to vertically takeoff and landing; and the ability to cruise efficiently. Therefore, the tiltrotor unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV), which can combine the advantages of both rotor and fixed-wing flights has gained the focus of considerable attention. It uses tilting rotors to provide lift during the vertical take-off and landing phase, and after reaching the designed altitude, it uses the tilting mechanism to gradually change the rotor orientation to complete the transition to forward flight. Eventually, when reaching the ideal speed, the lift will totally provided by fixed wings to maintain balance. However, this transition phase is particularly critical. As the reduction in rotor lift precedes the generation of sufficient aerodynamic lift from the fixed wings, and the angle of attack will be too big, contributing to a significant loss of altitude. Therefore, this article aims to explore the control strategies for achieving smooth and stable pitch transitions of UAV during rotor tilting processes. At present, according to the mature models of PID control and adaptive control, this paper proposes an integrated and multi-level control method. This new control method takes the model predictive control (MPC) as the major technology while using the neural network optimization technology to implement the overall optimization of the method. By using a mixed frame, the UAV is capable of dynamically adjusting its control parameters for take-off and landing or transition flights, retaining stability at various flight environments. The model also includes a fault detection and recovery system, which utilizes a real-time feedback loop to maintain the safety and performance of the system. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed model can provide stable signal transmission, and offer an effective transition process with high stability.

title: Flight Trajectory Prediction Based on Spatiotemporal Modeling and Multi-Scale Mamba
authors: Gaoyong Lu, Qingyue Hu, Xunchao Cong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Despite recent advances, existing methods for trajectory prediction remain limited by two key issues: insufficient modeling of spatiotemporal interactions and inefficient multi-scale feature fusion. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel prediction model named TSM, which integrates spatiotemporal modeling with a multi-scale Mamba architecture. Designed for full-flight-phase trajectory prediction, TSM offers effective technical support for enhancing airspace safety management. The model employs a two-stage architecture: first, a spatiotemporal dual-domain feature modeling stage uses a dualdomain interaction module to collaboratively encode historical trajectories, separately capturing temporal dependencies and channel-wise spatial correlations; second, a multi-scale feature modeling stage hierarchically extracts global trends and local perturbations through a multi-scale extraction module, enhances representations via a Mamba module, and dynamically integrates features using a multi-scale fusion mechanism. The main contributions of this work are as follows: (i) A dual-path spatiotemporal modeling framework that jointly captures temporal evolution and spatial multi-dimensional interactions; (ii) A hierarchical Mamba-enhanced architecture enabling efficient cross-scale feature fusion. Experimental results show that TSM reduces the Mean Deviation Error (MDE) by 17.5% compared to baseline methods. Ablation studies further confirm the critical role of multi-scale extraction module, which alone contributes a 16.6% reduction in MDE.

title:
Machine Learning-Based Classification on Gas Sensor Device Test-Data to Identify Presence of Toxic CO and CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    using SVM, RF, and KNN
authors: Indranil Maity, Tanit Ghosh, Sneha Pandey, Aheli Majumdar, Malay Gangopadhyay
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This paper describes a machine learning (ML) driven approach for the discrimination of two poisonous gases, namely Carbon Monoxide (CO) and Carbon Dioxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{CO}_{2}\right)$</tex>. Proper detection of them is essential as both are toxic and harmful environmental gases, which leads to several diseases. Supervised machine learning algorithms like Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF) and kNearest Neighbors (k -NN) classifier were used here to analyse the sensor responses from Platinum (Pt)-doped Zinc Oxide (ZnO) based nanotube (NT) sensor, and distinguish between the two adsorbed gases. The dataset was divided into the ratio of 70:30. The code was written in Google Colab, which supports python 3 environment to train, verify, and test the models. The features used for model training include adsorption energy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E}_{\text {adsorption }}$</tex>), binding distance, the highest occupied molecular orbital's energy (Еномо), the lowest unoccupied molecular orbital's energy (Elumo), chemical potential (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex>), ratio of electronic conductivity, sensitivity, and recovery time. The target variable indicated the gas type (CO or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>). Principal component analysis (PCA) helped to visualize the data by reducing its dimension. A total of 4 quality performance metrics, namely precision, along with accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score, and recall were calculated along with the confusion matrices. Overall, SVM achieved the best accuracy of 96.67 %.

title: LAW-Chat: Conversational Access to Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita Using Semantic Retrieval Techniques
authors: Aparna Raj, Manas Chandewar, Dhananjay Rupanwar, Navnath Kale
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Digital solutions for understanding criminal law have become essential with the introduction of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS). However, complex legal terminology and fragmented statutory content continue to limit accessibility for non-expert users. This paper presents LAW-Chat, a conversational legal assistant that enables users to ask natural-language questions and retrieve relevant BNS provisions with simplified explanations. Unlike keyword-based search systems, LAW-Chat uses transformer-based semantic embeddings stored in a vector database for context-aware retrieval. Experimental evaluation on real-world legal queries achieved an accuracy of 90.3%, significantly outperforming traditional keyword-based approaches (72.5%). The results demonstrate the potential of conversational AI to enhance legal literacy and public access to Indian criminal law.

title: Uniform Whole Wafer Anisotropic Etching of Structural Ta Thin Films
authors: Md Shariful Islam, Maarten P. de Boer
abstract: Tantalum (Ta) has promise as a structural material for micromechanical sensors and actuators. Anisotropic etching of alpha (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha $</tex-math> </inline-formula>) phase Ta is required for micromachining applications. Uniform thickness and etch across a full wafer are desirable features. An experimental investigation is conducted to study plasma etching rate and anisotropy in etching <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ta in relation to pressure and gas flow ratio. A comparatively low toxicity gas, carbon tetrafluoride (CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>), and argon (Ar) are used. Spectrometry by optical emission spectrometry (OES) and residual gas analysis (RGA) are employed to characterize the plasma to gain insight into the etch mechanisms. At low flow, the etch rate is slow due to an inadequate supply of the etching gas, CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>. The etch rate is also slowed at high flow due to a reduced CF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> residence time. Flow and pressure conditions to achieve a good etch rate and vertical sidewalls are identified and explained by means of a full factorial experiment coupled with emission spectra. With these conditions, uniform etching of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.5~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m thick <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ta across a 4-inch wafer is demonstrated.[2025-0220]

title: History of CRMS APL
authors: Paul McJones
publish: IEEE Annals of the History of Computing
abstract: The Center for Research in Management Science (CRMS) at the University of California, Berkeley, pioneered the use of interactive, computerized, multiperson simulation experiments for social science research. Conceived by Prof. Austin Hoggatt, the center’s Management Science Laboratory included a computer system with linked DEC PDP-5 and PDP-8 computers. An advanced APL-based version was designed and built during 1972–1976. In January 1974 it could support one experimenter developing and running an experiment connected to multiple terminals. By 1976 the system was complete, with an easy-to-use, interactive, APL subsystem and a multiuser, time-shared, operating system. The project was a technical success, making it possible to write experiments, debug them, run them, and analyze the results. It established the Management Science Laboratory as the prototype for computerized modeling in economics and behavioral science. However, the lab failed to jell as a community of experimenters and support personnel, and without continued institutional support the hardware and software quickly became obsolete.

title: High-Order Sliding Mode Control Design Subject to Unknown Nonvanishing Uncertainties
authors: Lu Liu, Hengde Lu, Shihong Ding
abstract: In this article, a high-order sliding mode control (SMC) method is proposed for a class of nonlinear systems with nonvanishing uncertainties. Unlike most methods, which treat the upper bound of disturbances and their time derivatives as known or unknown, the proposed approach does not require an upper bound for nonvanishing uncertainties. Moreover, these uncertainties and their time derivatives are usually time-invariant, such as the exogenous time-varying disturbances of unknown magnitude and the modeling uncertainties of unknown system parameters. First, a low-order virtual integral dynamics is introduced such that the system dynamics are reconstructed. Second, based on the introduced integral dynamics, an SMC law incorporating correction terms is designed using Lyapunov theory to handle the various disturbances with an unknown upper bound. Finally, the finite-time convergence of the closed-loop system is guaranteed, and the effectiveness of the method is verified by the application of the Buck converter system.

title: Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Cryptanalysis of Popular Ciphers
authors: S Akilesh, Rajeev Sekar, R Abinaya, K Palani Thanaraj, C Christopher Columbus
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Techniques of breaking encryptions are becoming increasingly advanced as encryption methods also change. Cryptanalysis normally depends on algorithmic, statistical or brute force attacks targeting ciphers. But now Large Language Models (LLMs) can be used to provide a new method of chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. They can make any effort to find solutions to cryptographic problems by logical deduction rather than brute-force search. This study is a test of the effectiveness of zero-shot CoT reasoning in decryption of both classical and modern ciphers. Three best reasoning models were tested: The O1 of OpenAI, the Gemini Thinking of Google, and DeepSeek R1. They had the job of cracking a variety of cryptographic systems, including classical ciphers such as Caesar, Vigenère and Playfair, as well as abstract block ciphers and hash codes. We noted that these LLMs are extremely effective in cracking less complicated ciphers wherein they can locate designs and leverage linguistic hints. Nevertheless, they do not perform well in comparison with modern cryptographic systems that rely on mathematical hardness.

title: Click2Buy Time: A Temporally-Aware Transformer-LSTM Hybrid for Purchase Intent Prediction in E-Commerce Sessions
authors: Meika Sugastiana Putri, Adhistya Erna Permanasari, Bimo Sunarfri Hantono
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: E-commerce platforms generate massive clickstream logs that reflect evolving user interests, yet predicting purchase intent from short, anonymous sessions remains difficult, particularly under severe class imbalance. This study examines how sequence-based representations, temporal features, and imbalance-aware training jointly shape session-level purchase prediction. Using a two-month subset of the Yoochoose 2015 dataset (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6-7 \%$</tex> purchase rate), we evaluate temporal-tabular baselines with Random Forest and XGBoost, a Hybrid LSTM-Tabular network, and a Hybrid TransformerLSTM that integrates item sequences, behavioral aggregates, and time-of-day signals. Results show that temporal-tabular features alone provide strong and stable ranking performance, while LSTM and Transformer encoders primarily increase recall but do not surpass tree-based baselines in precision-recall behaviour. The weak PR performance suggests that multi-scale sequence modeling is difficult to exploit when data are short, anonymous, and lack richer historical continuity. Future work will extend to the full six-month clickstream and investigate stronger imbalance-aware objectives to better leverage hybrid architectures.

title: URST-Net: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework Integrating U-Net, Residual Attention, and Swin Transformer for Underwater Image Enhancement
authors: Uk Vrindha, R Karthika
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Underwater Image Enhancement (UIE) aims to restore degraded images by addressing light absorption, scattering, and color attenuation in aquatic environments. Achieving accurate visibility and natural color remains challenging due to uneven illumination and suspended particles. Traditional techniques improve contrast but often cause artifacts, noise amplification, or unnatural colors in difficult conditions. This paper presents URST-Net, an integrated deep learning framework combining an attention-augmented U-Net encoder-decoder, residual modules, and a Swin Transformer to effectively capture fine details and long-range context, delivering improved underwater image restoration. The model was trained on the Underwater Fisheye Object (UFO 120) dataset of 1620 paired images with 1500 used for training and 120 for testing. On quantitative evaluation URST-Net attains a Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) of 0.96, Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) of $\mathbf{4 5. 2 6 ~ d B}$, an Underwater Image Quality Measure (UIQM) of 4.12 and an Underwater Color Image Quality Evaluation Index (UCIQE) of 0.75, outperforming UGAN with PSNR 38.00 dB, SSIM 0.93 and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) with SSIM 0.80 PSNR 30.00 dB. Visual results show URST-Net effectively restores textures, suppresses noise and preserves natural colors without artifacts. These findings pave the way for real time deployment on embedded platforms, adaptation to diverse aquatic environments and development of lightweight variants for onsite underwater imaging.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Neurodiagnostic: Safeguarding Ethical Standards in Migrant Mental Health Care
authors: Rufina Hussain, Safdar Tanweer, Sherin Zafar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly reshaping neurodiagnostic, creating new opportunities for earlier and more accurate detection of mental health conditions. Yet, migrant communities especially those living in high-pressure environments continue to face significant barriers to care and are disproportionately affected by stress, depression, and anxiety stemming from occupational demands, cultural adjustment, and social isolation. This paper introduces a dualmodality AI framework that combines clinical screening data with brain imaging to evaluate mental health in migrant populations. The approach integrates DASS-21 survey responses, demographic and cognitive data with convolutional neural networks trained on MRI scans, demonstrating the potential for scalable and interpretable diagnostic support. At the heart of the framework are essential ethical commitments: fairness in model development, transparency in decisionmaking, cultural responsiveness in design, and unwavering respect for privacy. The paper emphasizes that the success of AI in migrant mental health care depends not only on technical performance but on building systems grounded in trust, accountability, and equitable access.

title: Quantum-Inspired Generic Optimization for Multiuser Fluid-MIMO Communications and Sensing: Joint Port Selection and Beamforming
authors: Niancong Ji, Gan Zheng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: With fluid multiple-input multiple-output (Fluid-MIMO) emerging as a promising technology for next-generation systems, jointly selecting transmit or receive ports and designing multiuser beamforming becomes a key challenge. Although beamforming with fixed port selection is tractable, their strong coupling results in a high-dimensional, nonconvex combinatorial problem that limits the scalability of conventional methods. To address this, this paper proposes a generic quantum-inspired optimization framework based on quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) modeling to jointly optimize port selection and beamforming. Specifically, considering the objective function involves complex sub-problems, we formulate the overall task as a black-box binary optimization problem. To solve this, we propose a Taylor-based approximation technique to locally model the objective as a tractable QUBO form. This allows the leverage of the simulated bifurcation (SB) solver for efficient parallel search. We verify our proposed algorithm for three typical objectives: sum rate maximization, signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) balancing and sensing signal-clutter-noise ratio (SCNR) maximization. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm converges rapidly within 10 iterations. In a dense 10-user system, it outperforms the simulated annealing (SA) baseline by 10.1% in sum rate and 53.1% in minimum SINR. Furthermore, it achieves a 27.4% SCNR gain over SA under a 10 dB communication constraint.

title: A Second-Order Cone Programming Approach to Nonlinear Model Predictive Control of Optimal Active and Reactive Power Dispatch for Microgrid System
authors: Donlapat Dechawatanapaisal, Nattakit Prapanwattana, David Banjerdpongchai, Abhishek Rajan
abstract: This paper aims to enhance Energy Management System (EMS) in a microgrid by integrating Nonlinear Model Predictive Control (NMPC) with Optimal Active and Reactive Power Dispatch in accordance with Optimal Power Flow techniques. In power system architecture, we consider the setting of tap-changing transformers and reactive power compensators as additional decision variables in the microgrid. This approach enables efficient and reliable system operation by minimizing Total Power Loss in transmission lines while considering Total Operating Cost (TOC) and Total Carbon Dioxide Emissions (TCOE). We then formulate the multi-objective function by combining these three objective functions and name the new function Combined Economic, Emission, and Power Loss. The proposed work leverages NMPC to optimize power dispatch from various energy sources within the microgrid on a time-varying basis. The NMPC problem can be cast as a Second-Order Cone Program. This proposed formulation proves that reactive power dispatch can be applied successfully with real power dispatch in the NMPC for better energy management in a microgrid. It reduces power loss in transmission lines and improves overall energy efficiency. Additionally, because of lower power loss, it leads to a decrease in both TOC and TCOE compared to the previous EMS without reactive power dispatch. Moreover, the proposed approach transforms the nonconvex problem into the convex SOCP which can be solved with an efficient solver, such as the interior-point method, and global optimality can be guaranteed. To prove the theoretical result, the proposed EMS is validated through simulations on the Mae Hong Son microgrid.

title: A 40-GS/s 8-bit Time-Interleaved ADC Featuring SFDR-Enhanced Sampling and Power-Efficient Time-Domain Quantization in 28-nm CMOS
authors: Chenghao Zhang, Maliang Liu, Yuan Chang, Yihang Yang, Jinhai Xiao, Yintang Yang, Yong Chen
abstract: This article reports a 40-GS/s 8-bit time-interleaved (TI) time-domain (TD) gated-ring-oscillator analog-to-digital converter (GRO-ADC). An interleaving number of 32 is achieved with a single-channel 8-bit GRO-ADC operating at 1.25 GS/s, leading to a low front-end design complexity compared to recently published arts. The sampling front end employs a linearity-enhanced boosted switch that supports short-time and high-common-mode-level sampling, as well as a switched class-AB output buffer for low-power driving. Also, to further enhance the performance of the GRO-ADC, we devise several techniques: 1) the voltage-to-time converter utilizes differential sampling to enhance the swing and reduce the charging time while integrating the pulse-enabled cross-detector to improve power efficiency; 2) an adaptive-operation pulse generator (PG) eliminates the unnecessary pulse of the previous topology and minimizes the pulse generation power; and 3) the multi-channel interleaved architecture frees the GRO-ADC from single-channel calibration and reduces the sub-ADC design complexity. The TI GRO-ADC prototype is fabricated in a 28-nm CMOS using dual 1- and 1.8-V power supply, yielding a compact active area of 0.015 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. With a Nyquist input, it achieves a measured SNDR of 36.1 dB and an SFDR of 49 dB at the conversion rate of 40 GS/s, corresponding to the Walden figures of merit (FoMw) of 68.6 fJ/conversion step.

title: Available Spaces Indicating for Outdoor Vehicular Parking Spot by Computer Vision and AI
authors: Apiwat Sangnoree
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This research utilizes a modified AI (artificial intelligent)'s method for automatic allocation of parking lot's lanes. The mentioned method uses image processing procedures and machine learning, comprising, SVM (Supported Vector Machine) for deciding the availability of free lanes in a parking lot instead of an installed object detection's sensor in each lane. For the process of the research, it begins with inputting recorded images, consisting of parking lot's lanes, vehicles and non-vehicle objects from a static camera into a trained data's collection procedure. Secondly, a tested image from the camera is inserting in machine learning in order to compare with those trained data for available lanes' analysis. Lastly, the analysis of machine learning will be displayed as an analyzed image comprises the recorded parking lot as well as available lanes. Experimentally, implementing on three various parking lots, the percentage of accuracy is relatively high at 99.32 percent utilizing SVM as the predictor. According to the research's concept, it will be also able to be implemented for any temporal parking lots as well as free space's allocations in any outdoor areas.

title: A Hybrid Framework for Digital Forensics and Penetration Testing with Tutorial-Assisted Tool Usage
authors: R. Raja Sekar, Y. Mugila, G. Raghuram, K. Sai Kiran, M. Dhana Lakshmi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: In the fast-changing world of cybersecurity, both professionals and learners struggle to find reliable tools and learn how to use them properly. This project seeks to fill that gap by creating a web platform that features a curated collection of cybersecurity tools along with tutorial videos. Current tools work separately and require specialized technical knowledge, which leads to delays and disjointed analysis. Our project offers a single platform with an easy-to-use interface that streamlines these tools, manages cases, allows for centralized result viewing, and generates reports. This makes investigations quicker and more efficient. We are building a comprehensive Cyber Forensics and Ethical Hacking Toolkit that includes tools like FTK Imager, Volatility, Cellebrite UFED, Wireshark for digital forensics, and Nmap, Nessus, OpenVAS, Nikto, Metasploit, John the Ripper, and Hydra for ethical hacking.

title: Incorporating Edge Betweenness Centrality in Street Surfaces Reduction Algorithm
authors: Supharoek Chattanachot, Kittichai Lavangnananda, Frédéric Guinand
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: It is undeniable that environmental health and sustainability have become major concerns in urban development. Many approaches have been proposed to mitigate environmental degradation. One such approach is to repurpose part of the city streets, not only to reduce air pollution but also to create space for vegetation and recreational uses. This work focuses on the development of a street-surface reduction algorithm. Based on graph theory to model urban street networks, it incorporates edge betweenness centrality to determine the relative importance of each street. The problem is formulated as bi-objective: minimizing APSP, the total path length between all pairs of nodes, while maximizing the number of lanes that can be repurposed. These two objectives are conflicting with each other, hence, a unique optimal solution does not exist. Instead, a Pareto front is used to illustrate the trade-offs. The proposed algorithm has been applied to several real cities, and three cities are selected for illustration. The experiments demonstrate that the proposed method improves on the most recent existing approaches.

title: IoT based Smart Waste Bins for Waste Collection, Waste segregation And disposal
authors: S Siddharth, T Vickneshwari, S. Berlin Shaheema, N Sujitha, G. Gifta Jerith, A. Adlin Arul
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The rapid urbanization of regions like Chennai, Tamil Nadu, has led to significant challenges in waste management, with approximately 100 tons of waste generated daily. Alarmingly, only 30% of this waste is properly segregated, and a mere 15% is recycled, resulting in landfill overflow, environmental pollution, and inefficient waste disposal practices. The S-Bin, an innovative IoT-based Smart Waste Bin system, has been developed to address these critical issues by introducing automation and intelligence into the waste management process. A novel waste management algorithm optimizes collection routes by analyzing factors such as waste type, bin capacity, and link stability. The S-Bin features a real-time notification system that alerts municipal authorities, coupled with a smart locking mechanism to prevent unauthorized access and ensure secure disposal. The SBin promotes higher recycling rates and provides datadriven insights for strategic waste management planning. In addition, the system integrates predictive analytics to forecast waste generation trends, enabling proactive scheduling of collection vehicles. The S-Bin’s cloud-based dashboard facilitates real-time monitoring and analytics for decision-makers. Overall, this intelligent framework supports sustainable urban development by improving operational efficiency and reducing the ecological footprint of waste disposal.

title: BreathSpectrogram-X: An AI-Based Spectral Analysis Framework for Influenza and Common Cold Cough Detection
authors: Punyapat Sengdonprai, Tanicha Likhithattasilp, Aueaphum Aueawatthanaphisut
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Respiratory illnesses such as influenza and the common cold produce cough sounds with distinct acoustic and temporal characteristics, which can be captured and analyzed using signal processing and deep learning techniques. This study proposes BreathSpectrogram-X, an AI-powered framework for differentiating influenza coughs from common cold coughs through spectral analysis of recorded audio signal. The system integrates Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) and Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) for feature extraction, followed by a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifier optimized for spectral pattern recognition. Real-world cough samples are augmented and denoised using adaptive filters and spectral enhancement to simulate clinical variability. Evaluation results demonstrate classification accuracy exceeding 90 % on benchmark datasets, highlighting the discriminative potential of acoustic biomarkers in cough analysis. BreathSpectrogram-X offers a non-invasive, low-cost, and scalable diagnostic support tool, bridging the gap between telehealth accessibility and AI-driven respiratory screening. Its modular architecture allows deployment on mobile or web platforms, enabling real-time spectral visualization, data logging, and educational use in biomedical signal processing curricula.

title: Evaluation of Low-Cost Multi-GNSS PPK Positioning for Smart High-Precision Agriculture
authors: Rajkumar Mandal, Somnath Mahato, Surajit M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Smart high-precision agriculture requires reliable centimeter-level positioning to support applications such as field mapping, automated guidance, and precision input management. This study evaluates the performance of the PostProcessed Kinematic (PPK) positioning technique using lowcost, dual-frequency multi-GNSS receivers for agricultural applications. Experiments were conducted over two short baseline lengths of approximately 0.5 km and 1.5 km within the National Institute of Technology Jamshedpur campus using different GNSS constellation combinations. Raw GNSS observations were collected simultaneously at Base and Rover stations and processed offline using RTKLib software, while Precise Point Positioning (PPP) solutions were used as reference coordinates. Positioning precision was assessed using Distance Root Mean Square (2DRMS), Circular Error Probable (CEP), Spherical Error Probable (SEP), and Mean Radial Spherical Error (MRSE). The results show that centimeter- and sub-centimeter- level accuracy can be achieved with low-cost receivers in PPK mode. For the GPS+Galileo+QZSS configuration, horizontal 2DRMS values as low as 0.8-1.1 mm and MRSE values of 1.1-1.4 mm were obtained, whereas the GLONASS + BeiDou combination exhibited relatively larger errors, with 2DRMS reaching 3.4 mm and MRSE exceeding 5 mm for longer baselines. These findings demonstrate that PPK using low-cost multi-GNSS receivers is a viable and costeffective solution for smart high-precision agriculture, with constellation selection and baseline length significantly influencing positioning performance

title: Sparse Variational Student-t Processes for Heavy-Tailed Modeling
authors: Jian Xu, Delu Zeng, John Paisley
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: The Gaussian process (GP) is a powerful tool for nonparametric modeling, but its sensitivity to outliers limits its applicability to data distributions with heavy tails. Student-t processes (TPs) offer a robust alternative for heavy-tail modeling, but they lack the scalable developments of the GP to large datasets necessary for practical applications. We present sparse variational TPs (SVTPs), the first principled framework that extends the sparse inducing point method to the TP. We develop two novel inference algorithms, SVTP-UB and SVTP-MC, with theoretical guarantees and derive a natural gradient optimization that exploits a previously unused connection between the Fisher information matrix of the multivariate Student-t distribution and the beta function (the “beta link”). Experiments on UCI and Kaggle datasets demonstrate that SVTP significantly outperforms sparse GPs when the data contain outliers and heavy tails, achieving up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> faster convergence and 40% lower prediction error while maintaining computational efficiency for datasets with over 200 000 samples.

title: Twisted Bi-layer Magnetic Photonic Crystals
authors: You-Ming Liu, Shi-Kai Lin, Pei-Shi Li, Yi-Ran Hao, Biao Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Photonics and Applied Technology (IPAT)
abstract: Twisted bi-layer photonic systems enabled unprecedented control over light-matter interactions, through Moiré-induced modulation of photonic band structures. Meanwhile, magnetic photonic crystals have facilitated key applications such as Faraday rotation-based isolators and nonreciprocal devices. Nevertheless, the synergistic integration of twist engineering and magneto-optical effects in bi-layer architectures remains unexplored. This work introduces twisted bi-layer magnetic photonic crystals to unify these degrees of freedom. By continuously tuning the twist angle between two magneto-active photonic layers, the tunable giant circular dichroism is observed and can be modulated by the twist angle from 8° to 82°. At the transmission peak, the transmitted waves can be perfectly linearly polarized and rotated. At a twist angle of 45°, the rotation angle increases by approximately 7° for each 0.2 increment in α (α characterizes the strength and direction of the magnetic field). These effects arise from the interplay between resonant properties of the Moiré cell and magnetization-dependent coupling of circularly polarized states. This work introduces a new degree of freedom for dynamic, intelligent, and reconfigurable light control, establishing a versatile platform for programmable photonic devices, nonreciprocal components, and polarization-selective sensing. The findings highlight the significance of magnetic-twistronic architectures in advancing intelligent optical technologies.

title: A Survey on Knowledge Graph for Enhancing the Performance of LLM-Based Recommendation Systems
authors: Nurul Arina, Indriana Hidayah, Noor Akhmad Setiawan
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Recommender systems are increasingly used in many fields to support personalised and adaptive decision-making. Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT and Knowledge Graphs (KGs) have sparked interest in combining their strengths to improve recommendation quality, context, and explainability. Although recent reviews discuss the benefits of KGs for cloud-based LLMs, they rarely focus on their use in recommender systems. This literature review fills that gap by examining how KGs and LLMs work together to enhance recommendations. This study were guided by three research questions on application areas, integration methods, and evaluation method. Additionally, the study uses the PRISMA approach to select and analyse 20 relevant papers. The results compare different methods and performance metrics, including precision, recall, NDCG, and F1-score. This review offers a focused summary of current progress and points out important directions for future research on KG-LLM recommender systems.

title: Design and Simulation of a Power-Aware ALU with Interrupt-Driven FSM for Real-Time Health Monitoring
authors: B. Chandrababu Naik, Vineet Tripathi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper presents a low-power, 16-bit Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) integrated with an interrupt-driven finite state machine (FSM) for real-time biomedical monitoring. The system activates the ALU only when abnormal physiological inputs are detected, allowing it to stay in sleep mode during idle periods. The ALU monitors key health parameters—heartbeat, blood pressure, temperature, and respiration rate—and generates alerts when thresholds are exceeded. Designed in Verilog HDL and validated on the Xilinx Vivado platform, the system consumes only 66 mW of total power, with dynamic power limited to 2 mW. Timing analysis shows stable operation at 100 MHz with zero violations. The design offers enhanced real-time responsiveness, reduced area (0.001 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), and precise alert handling, making it suitable for wearable and implantable biomedical devices.

title: A wearable focused ultrasound device for sonogenetics in freely moving mice
authors: Zhaoning Gu, Tianqi Xu, Yaoheng Yang, Dezhuang Ye, Hong Chen
abstract: Sonogenetics, which utilizes ultrasound to modulate the activity of neurons expressing ultrasound-sensitive actuators, has emerged as a promising approach for implant-free, cell-type-specific neuromodulation. However, wearable transcranial focused ultrasound (tFUS) devices are needed to enable sonogenetic neuromodulation in freely moving mice, thereby facilitating the study of naturalistic behavioral responses with minimal physical constraints. To address this need, we present a fully validated protocol for the design, fabrication, and characterization of a wearable tFUS device. Our protocol details the selection of piezoelectric material, transducer fabrication and calibration, and installation of the device on the mouse head. We also provide a detailed protocol for using this tFUS device for performing sonogenetics in freely moving mice. The device’s efficacy in sonogenetic neuromodulation has been demonstrated in our previously published studies. The device can be used in a broad range of applications, including ultrasound neuromodulation (without genetics). All design files and accompanying software are freely available as open-source resources. This protocol is intended to lower the barrier to entry and facilitate the broader adoption of tFUS techniques in neuroscience research.

title: Application of Simulation Modeling Methods in the Calculation and Design of Lower Limb Rehabilitation Equipment for Stroke Patients
authors: Trung T. Nguyen, Hung T. Nguyen, Phuoc C. Tran, Hung D. Nguyen, Duc Q. Tran, Tam N. Bui, Huy Q.B Le
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: This paper presents a kinematic model for a 3-DOF robot used for lower limb rehabilitation for patients after a stroke. The device is designed to include the following movements: hip flexion, knee flexion, and foot flexion and extension. In this study, to support the calculation and model design, three software Solidworks, Matlab, and Simscape were applied. This method allows for the simulation of the workspace of the links and joints, thereby accurately evaluating the Robot's efficiency before manufacturing. In addition, Simscape is applied in the dynamics problem to determine the necessary moment at each joint, serving the problem of selecting the device's motor. To describe this process, the model from Solidworks in the Assembly environment will be taken as input for the model in Simscape and give results on the moments of the joints during movement. The simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of simulation modeling in design and provide important parameters for evaluating and improving the device. The study also found the influence of two sitting and reclining states on the working space of the device. The application of numerical simulations shows that the model can meet the rehabilitation exercises for patients.

title: Dynamic Utilization of an Auxiliary Population for Constrained Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Kaihui Zheng, Huiming Huang, Xiao Li, Qing Xu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Constrained multi-objective optimization problems (CMOPs) involve optimizing multiple conflicting objectives while simultaneously adhering to a set of constraints. This creates a considerable challenge for evolutionary algorithms, which must effectively balance the objectives and constraints. To tackle this issue, this paper introduces a dual-population algorithm that leverages an auxiliary population to solve CMOPs. In the early stage of the algorithm, the main population focuses on optimizing the constraints to identify the constrained Pareto front (CPF), while the auxiliary population performs unconstrained optimization to explore infeasible regions and locate the unconstrained Pareto front (UPF). In the later stages, the relationship between the CPF and UPF is established through the dominance relationship between the two populations, with the auxiliary population being dynamically utilized to help the main population locate the full CPF. The results from all three test sets demonstrate that the proposed algorithm either outperforms or performs comparably to existing state-of-the-art approaches.

title: An Autonomous Attitude Determination Algorithm Adapted to the Maneuvering for UAV
authors: Chao Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: A fully autonomous horizontal attitude estimation algorithm is proposed in this paper, the algorithm based on the airspeed and inertial navigation when maneuvering to fulfilling the long-term attitude measurement requirements of middle speed fixed-wing UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) in GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System) denied environment, an outlier detection algorithm is adopted to reduce the influence of sudden acceleration or any other disturbances, the major error sources are analyzed. The main contribution of this paper is an autonomous attitude estimation algorithm without any external information is built, which will improve the accuracy and reliability of attitude measurement of such type of UAV in GNSS denied environment greatly. As the attitude error introduced by maneuvering and accelerometer errors are sudden and much larger than that introduced by gyroscope error, a data fusion algorithm combining innovation orthogonality detection is adopted, the algorithm can identify and eliminate the outliers induced by carrier maneuvering and accelerometer measurement errors accurately. The experiment result indicated that the accuracy is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.17 \text{deg}(1 \sigma)$</tex> for roll angle and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.60 \text{deg}(1 \sigma)$</tex> for pitch angle.

title: Adaptive Control Model for Nonlinear Pilot-Induced Oscillations Caused by Multimodal Switching
authors: Xingwan Wang, Shuting Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: In multimodal flight control systems, nonlinear pilotinduced oscillation (PIO) caused by modal switching poses a serious threat to flight safety. Existing pilot models demonstrate high-frequency oscillatory limit-cycle characteristics during modal switching, with a tendency to induce type III PIO. To address this, an improved adaptive pilot model is developed to enhance the pilot's adaptation to sudden changes in vehicle dynamics through dynamic gain parameter adjustment, which can reflect the characteristics of pilot control behavior accurately. On this basis, an intelligent auxiliary controller is designed based on integrating adaptive sliding mode control to improve the highfrequency oscillations caused by modal switching. Simulation results demonstrate that the improved pilot model combined with the auxiliary controller leads to significant reductions in both oscillation amplitude and tracking error, while keeping the system outside the PIO-prone region. These improvements substantially enhance system stability and robustness. This study presents an effective solution to nonlinear oscillation suppression in multimodal flight control.
