title: Development of a Circuit to Detect Drowsiness for Drivers by Measuring the EOG Signal
authors: Le Hoang Vu, Quang Minh Tong, Thi Ha Phuong Le, Thi Thu Trang Van, Viet Hung Dao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Driver drowsiness is a significant contributor to traffic accidents worldwide, emphasizing the urgent need for reliable, real-time monitoring systems that are non-intrusive and suitable for everyday use. While current solutions include EEG-based and camera-based methods, they often face limitations related to wearability, environmental sensitivity, and user comfort. Electrooculography (EOG) has emerged as a promising alternative, yet the development of compact, low-power, and wearable EOG acquisition systems remains limited. In this study, we propose a miniaturized EOG detection circuit integrated into a pair of smart glasses, designed to capture vertical eye movement and identify signs of drowsiness by analyzing blink patterns. The hardware comprises an instrumentation amplifier, analog filters, a DC level shifter, and an ESP32 microcontroller kit for signal conditioning and processing. The system effectively distinguishes between normal blinking and prolonged eyelid closures, which are key indicators of fatigue. This work provides a practical foundation for wearable drowsiness detection systems, with potential applications in road safety, clinical diagnostics, and human-machine interaction. Future developments will focus on improving signal fidelity and integrating advanced detection algorithms for enhanced robustness in real-world conditions.

title: Characterization of the Electromechanical Impedance Performance of Piezoelectric Ceramic Sensors under Irradiation Environments
authors: Yuxuan Cui, Weixuan Zhang, Jin Yin, Yishou Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The performance degradation of piezoelectric ceramics under irradiation affects the diagnostic accuracy of piezoelectric-based SHM systems. This study investigates PZT patches, both encapsulated and unencapsulated, integrated into smart layers and bonded to CFRP specimens. Using Co-60 gamma irradiation at high and low doses, the electromechanical impedance (EMI) responses were analyzed. Results show that EMI degradation remained below 5%, indicating strong radiation resistance. These findings support the use of PZT sensors in radiation-intensive environments.

title: Emotion Recognition from Vietnamese Text Using PhoBERT and BiLSTM
authors: Van Hiep Phung, Tuan Truong Cong, Manh Tuyen Dao, Thi Thoa Mac
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: In recent years, emotion recognition from text has become increasingly popular for processing social media data from platforms such as Twitter, YouTube, and Facebook for various purposes. Previous studies show that emotion classification into 2 or 3 categories - positive and negative, or positive, negative, and neutral - consistently achieves higher accuracy than classifying emotions into a broader range (from five to seven emotions). Globally, Vietnamese is a low-resource language in natural language processing (NLP), making emotion recognition in Vietnamese text more challenging than in more commonly used languages like English. The dataset used in this research is sourced from the UIT-VSMEC corpus, consisting of 6,927 human-annotated sentences with seven common emotion labels: anger, disgust, enjoyment, fear, other, sadness, and surprise. In this study, we use the PhoBERT model combined with neural network BiLSTM to classify Vietnamese text into seven emotion labels, achieving an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 4. 5 7 \%}$</tex> during evaluation, the model achieved the highest performance with the weighted F1-score of 0.6460.

title: HDL Design and Performance Analysis of Polymorphic Advanced Encryption Standard for high-speed encryption
authors: Nikita Kumari, Balwinder Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: To achieve strong data protection across diverse applications, a polymorphic version of the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), named P-AES, has been introduced and executed. In P-AES, the main operational parameters are altered dynamically with each new encryption key, and these values are accessible only to authorized users during execution. To facilitate this polymorphic feature, the standard AES processes— Substitute bytes, Shift rows and Mix Columns—have been adjusted to depend on the key. Consequently, the internal operations are uniquely tailored for each encryption key, while the recipient can extract the transformation logic directly from the key, ensuring secure compatibility. The entire P-AES design has been articulated using Verilog HDL and implemented on an Artix-7 FPGA with Xilinx Vivado 24.1 for synthesis and simulation. In line with Kerckhoffs’s principle, P-AES depends solely on the confidentiality of the encryption key, not the algorithm itself. The design accommodates 128 distinct operational modes, complicating potential attacks. Key and plaintext avalanche scores were 0.521 and 0.504, respectively. These enhancements, validated through avalanche testing and HDL implementation, indicate that P-AES is a promising solution for high-speed and secure data encryption in contemporary systems.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Breast Ultrasound Lesion Classification
authors: Tat-Chuyen Mai, Khanh-Ly Trinh, Huyen-Trang Ong Thi, Hai-Long Nguyen, Viet-Dung Nguyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Breast cancer, a leading cause of mortality in women, underscores the need for accurate early detection. While breast ultrasound is common for lesion diagnosis, most studies overlook metadata like patient age and lesion characteristics. We propose a multimodal deep learning approach integrating metadata with ultrasound images using the BrEaST dataset (256 images, 5-fold cross-validation). Our MultimodalModel, based on MobileNetV2 pretrained on ImageNet and fine-tuned on the BUSI dataset, processes metadata (age, shape, echogenicity, posterior features, halo, calcifications) through a dense layer, concatenating them with image features for classification. The model achieved a mean validation accuracy of 86.90%, sensitivity of 84.69%, and specificity of 88.31%, outperforming the image-only model (accuracy: 76.59%) and other architectures (DenseNet121, EfficientNetB0, ResNet50). This study highlights the potential of combining imaging and metadata for enhanced AI-driven breast cancer diagnosis, aiding radiologists in precise decision-making.

title: Transparent Anaemia Prediction System with Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Aditya More, Rachna K. Somkunwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The emergence of AI-supported diagnostic systems has raised the concern regarding clearly defined and interpretable models, especially in the field of anaemia prediction where the clinical decision-making process is mostly dependent on the sensitivity of biomarkers and patient variation. In this study, the use of a Transparent Anaemia Prediction system with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) is proposed to improve diagnostic reliability and clinical trust. The model combines hematological biomarkers, clinical symptoms and electronic medical records as a prediction pipeline. The advanced preprocessing strategies are used to normalize, impute, and generate high-value feature representations. Anaemia severity is predicted with high accuracy using a hybrid machine-learning classifier based on the Random Forest, XGBoost, and deep neural architectures. Explainability tools like SHAP, LIME and Grad-CAM produce feature-attribution maps to justify all of the predictions. Homomorphic and asymmetric encryption provide security in predicting. Components of the system are a clinician-feedback loop to improve the model’s behavior. This system will facilitate ethical, traceable, and transparent AI implementation in medical diagnostics.

title: Research on a Testing System of Rocket Launchers
authors: Shuhai Wang, Qiang Fei, Jiatong Qin, Biao Jin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The new type of rocket launcher equipped in our army is a mechanical-electronic product. To improve the technical guarantee capability of rocket launchers, it is necessary to design a technical detection system for the rocket launcher. This paper studies the basic malfunction models of rocket launchers, analyzes the operating principles and composition of the detection system, and proposes the overall design scheme for the detection system. According to the malfunction phenomena of the rocket launcher, this study establishes a fuzzy malfunction diagnosis mathematical model of the electrical operation system. It includes the determination of the fuzzy relation matrix, quantitative requirements, trial operation of the malfunction diagnosis system, and evaluation of the testing system's diagnostic capability. The research aims to improve the combat effectiveness of rocket launchers.

title: Achieving 40 kA Turn-off with a 3 kA IGBT: Failure Analysis Under Extended Gate-Voltage Range in Single-Pulse Operation
authors: Junyu Luo, Xiangyu Zhang, Zihan Qu, Yuanfang Lu, Jingfei Wang, Lei Qi
abstract: This paper analyses the failure modes of IGBTs under extreme surge current stress in hybrid DC circuit breaker (HCB) applications and systematically examines their operating limits across an extended gate voltage range (from nominal levels to extreme over-drive). Chip experiments and simulations reveal an evolution of failure mechanisms: as the gate voltage increases, the dominant failure mode transitions from on-state desaturation to blocking-state thermal breakdown. Specifically, a distinct inflection point is identified around 25 V. Based on this result, the safe operating current boundary of IGBTs is redefined. Guided by the revised operational limit, the achievement of a 40.8 kA current interruption is successfully demonstrated.

title: A Reinforcement Learning Approach to Virtual Network Embedding Problems in 5G Networks
authors: Amir Javadpour, Forough Ja'fari, Tarik Taleb, Chafika Benzaïd, Pedro R. Tomas, Luis Rosa, Jorge Proença, Luis Cordeiro
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: 5G network slicing is the problem of mapping requested virtual networks on the substrate network resources. Due to resource capacity constraints, the performance of network slicing depends on the number of supported requests. This challenge is a type of Virtual Network Embedding (VNE) problem in which a weighted graph is divided into multiple smaller weighted graphs according to the user's custom requirements. These problems are NP-hard, and most existing solutions have suggested using Reinforcement Learning (RL) models to solve them. However, they do not adequately represent the weighted graph to the learning model. Therefore, their learning rate is limited. This paper proposes TRL-VNE, a Two-stage RL-based VNE solution to overcome these challenges. In the first stage of this solution, an RL model is utilized for mapping the central node of each request. Novel graph-based features (G-features) are used in this model to improve its learning rate. The second stage uses a greedy algorithm to map the other components. The simulation results show that TRL-VNE improves the requests acceptance ratio and maximum supported requests by 21% and 36%, respectively, compared to existing solutions. Moreover, we have proposed a network architecture based on TRL-VNE, and emulated it in Mininet to investigate the feasibility of the proposed solution.

title: Fault Diagnosis and Isolation in Electric Vehicles: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Sujit Kumar, Nikhil Pawar, R.K. Nema, Savita Nema
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The electric vehicle (EV) is identified as an important innovation in sustainable transport with improved energy efficiency and reduced environmental effects. However, the complex structure of EVs demands sophisticated mechanisms for effective fault detection and isolation to guarantee safe and reliable performance. This review presents an analysis of fault diagnosis and isolation techniques across major EV subsystems, including battery management systems, electric motors, power electronics, and charging infrastructure. The paper examines advanced methodologies encompassing model-based approaches, data-driven techniques, and hybrid methods. Recent advancements in AI-based and machine learning applications are discussed with emphasis on real-time implementation. Current challenges and future research directions are identified, emphasizing integrated FDI systems for preventing catastrophic failures, enabling graceful degradation, and reducing maintenance costs.

title: A Logistic Regression Analysis of the Association between Smoking, Vaping, and Respiratory Diseases
authors: Tran Anh Vu, Nguyen Ngoc Tram, Hoang Quang Huy, Pham Thi Viet Huong, Do Van Duc
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: In this study, we analyzed the association between smoking, vaping, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) using self-reported data from the 2022 and 2023 Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) surveys. Participants were categorized into six distinct groups based on smoking and vaping behavior. Using weighted univariable and multivariable logistic regression models, we found that dual users and current smokers had the highest adjusted odds of selfreported COPD, with adjusted odds ratios (aORs) of 4.35 and 3.78, respectively. Notably, current vapers who had never smoked also showed elevated odds <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(a O R=1.45)$</tex>, suggesting an independent risk associated with vaping. Age significantly modified these associations, with older adults exhibiting stronger correlations across all categories. These findings contribute to the ongoing public health discourse on the respiratory risks of e-cigarette use.

title: A TF-IDF Prescription Similarity Measurement Based on Weighting of Monarch and Minister
authors: Wuning Tong, Yali Wang, Li Jiang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: This study explores the application effect of TFIDF method based on weighting of Monarch and Minister in the calculation of prescription similarity, and verifies the influence of weight settings on prescription similarity results. Two methods, traditional TF-IDF and weighted TF-IDF (Monarch 1.5, Minister 1.2, Other 1.0), were used to calculate the similarity of 240 classic prescriptions. Comparative analysis was conducted from two dimensions: similarity distribution and recognition of highly similar prescriptions. The experimental results show that the weighted TF-IDF method can more accurately reflect the functional characteristics of the prescription while maintaining the core similarity relationship, making the similarity calculation results more in line with traditional Chinese medicine theory. Therefore, the TF-IDF method based on the weight of Monarch and Minister can effectively improve the accuracy and clinical practicality of prescription similarity calculation.

title: An Improvement on Class-Imbalanced Cardiovascular Disease Classification using Spectrogram-based 12-Lead ECG
authors: Phuong Linh Nguyen, Hoang Bach Vu, Minh Khang Trinh, Viet Dung Nguyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are the leading cause of mortality worldwide, with significant difficulties in precise diagnosis and effective treatment. The electrocardiogram (ECG) is a non-invasive diagnostic tool that plays a crucial clinical role in evaluating the severity of these conditions. Conventional ECG evaluation methods require specialized expertise and pose challenges for inexperienced cardiologists. Deep learning offers a promising solution for analyzing resting ECG signal to detect structural cardiac pathologies and predict long-term cardiovascular risk. This paper proposes a transfer learning approach for CVDs classification using two 12-lead ECG datasets (CPSC2018 and Chapman). ECG signals are upsampled and transformed into spectrograms to gain deeper insights into cardiac rhythms, which are then pretrained on CPSC2018 with optimized Focal loss to address class imbalance and enhance CVDs classification. The deep learning model is fine-tuned and tested on the Chapman dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms recent studies by up to 4 % in merged-class evaluation while achieving robust performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>92 \%$</tex> average accuracy across all classes) in handling class imbalance.

title: CTM-PolypNet: A Unified Convolution-Transformer-Mamba Model for Polyp Segmentation
authors: Minh-Ngoc Luong, Minh Le, Van-Truong Pham, Thi-Thao Tran
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Introduction: A critical challenge in computeraided diagnosis of colorectal cancer is the accurate segmentation of polyps from endoscopic images, which plays a crucial role in early detection, enabling quick interventions and improving patient outcomes. Although deep learning techniques, especially Transformers and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), have shown promising results in this field, they still face several challenges. Polyp features vary significantly in size and shape, and their boundaries are often blurred and indistinct, making it difficult to distinguish them from the background. Methodology: To address these challenges, we introduce a novel architecture integrating Convolution, Transformer, and Mamba-based components. The proposed model's backbone encoder, PVT-v2, captures a global receptive field rather than relying solely on local receptive fields like traditional CNNs. In our architecture, we designed Mamba-MLP Fusion to exploit multi-level features during the decoder stage. Also, we proposed the Reverse Attention Feature Enhancement (RAFE) block, which indicates ambiguous areas and enhances the objects' edges. As well as integrating a deep supervision procedure, we outperformed other state-of-the-art methods with notable outcomes. Results: We evaluate our approach on the Kvasir-SEG, CVCClinicDB, CVC-ColonDB, and ETIS datasets. Conclusion and Discussion: The experimental results compared with SOTA demonstrate the performance of the proposed approach for polyp segmentation. Our code will be made publicly available at: https://github.com/Ngoc-LM/CTM-PolypNet

title: A Nonvolatile Band-Reconfigurable Filter Using Memristive RF Switches
authors: Wang Chen, Zhi-Yi Zhang, Zong-Rui Xu, Liang-Feng Qiu, Lin-Sheng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: A nonvolatile band-reconfigurable filter (BRF) is proposed in this article based on memristive RF switches. The filter adopts a two-path topology consisting of four resonators loaded with switchable open stubs and coupled through transmission line and coupled-line inverters. An equivalent circuit model of the memristive RF switch, together with the parasitic of the bonding wires, is extracted and incorporated into simulations. After synthesis and optimization, four switchable states are achieved, comprising one single-band bandpass filter (SB-BPF) and three dual-band bandpass filtering (DB-BPF) responses. To verify the proposed BRF, a prototype is developed and measured. The SB-BPF response has a central frequency of 3.14 GHz and an insertion loss (IL) of 0.65 dB. For the DB-BPF responses, the central frequencies are measured at 2.61/3.76 GHz, 2.58/4.89 GHz, and 3.03/4.70 GHz, with the in-band ILs of 0.62/1.65 dB, 1.11/2.4 dB, and 0.4/1.06 dB, respectively. A good consistency between simulated and measured results confirms the validity of the proposed design. To evaluate the linearity of the proposed BRF, the 1-dB compression point and the input third-order intercept point are measured, which are around 28–35.7 dBm and 10.51–14.25 dBm, respectively.

title: Area Optimization of AES Key Expansion on FPGA-Based Cryptographic Systems
authors: Swagata Pradhan, Swastika Pradhan, Mihir Lal Saha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) is popularly used in modern cryptography systems and the hardware realization of this algorithm plays a central role in defining the overall system performance. The key-expansion unit, which is one of the constituent units of AES, is a major area-consumer of silicon as it relies on the substitution and permutation processes. This paper focuses on reducing area consumed by AES key-expanding module in the cryptographic application of field-programmable gate array (FPGA)-based systems. A modified key-expansion architecture is proposed and its performance is compared with the existing AES implementation by using the synthesis-based analysis. The synthesis of both designs is done under the same FPGA hardware. The optimized architecture resulted in a reduction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2. 3 \%}$</tex> in the number of cells, about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 6. 5 \%}$</tex> in the number of LUTs, and an 87.5 % decrease in the number of instances S-boxes. Moreover, the key-expansion block analysis in itself demonstrates that the use of slice-LUT is significantly reduced. This evidence shows that the proposed method is effective to reduce hardware area and still be compatible with the standard AES key schedule, which makes it a resource-saving implementation of cryptographic applications on a limited hardware platform of the FPGA based systems.

title: Graph-Based Multimodal DQN with Ranking Aware Learning for News Recommendation
authors: Devisree. A, A. Hency Juliet
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Personalized news recommender systems try to show users relevant articles by learning their interests from past interactions and news content. However, many existing methods find it difficult to model complex user–news relationships, combine different types of information, and generate well-ranked news lists as user interests change over time. To overcome these issues, the novelty includes combining graph-based learning, multimodal Deep Q-learning, and ranking-aware methods to better understand user–news interactions, learn user behavior over time, and directly improve the ranking of recommended news articles. The proposed approach improves news recommendation by jointly considering user–news interactions, sequential behavior, and ranked presentation to achieve better accuracy. In comparison to existing recommender models, the proposed approach outperforms by capturing intricate user–news relationships, modeling evolving user behavior, and producing higher-quality ranked recommendation lists. Experimental evaluation on benchmark datasets demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method, achieving nDCG@10 (Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain) of 0.4209, Precision@10 of 0.1350, F1-score of 0.2153, MRR (Mean Reciprocal Rank), of 0.3827, AUC (Area Under the ROC Curve) of 0.6753, and CTR (Click-Through Rate) of 0.0406, while consistently outperforming state-of-the-art baseline models across multiple ranking-base d evaluation metrics.

title: Dense Semantic Compression: A Low-Dimensional Siamese Architecture for Efficient Paraphrase Detection
authors: Sagnik Raha, Adarsh Kumar, Sainik Kumar Mahata
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The exponential growth in unstructured textual data demands the development of robust mechanisms for semantic equivalence detection. Though large language models, such as BERT, have brought about a revolution in Semantic Textual Similarity (STS), their high-dimensional output vectors introduce massive computational overheads into storage and retrieval systems. This work reports on the design and implementation of a machine learning pipeline dedicated to paraphrase detection, with a focus on creating semantically meaningful yet computationally efficient sentence embeddings. Our proposed architecture is based on a Siamese network using the pre-trained sentence-transformers/paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2 model. In particular, we propose the bottleneck adapter: a dense projection layer with Tanh activation that projects the standard 768-dimensional output onto a compact 64-dimensional latent space. After training our model against a diverse corpus comprising <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> pairs of sentences based on Cosine Similarity Loss, the model achieved an accuracy of 87.1 % and an F1-score of 88.0 %. Our empirical results show that such a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12 \times$</tex> reduction in the dimensionality leads to less than a 0.6 % drop in performance while reducing vector storage requirements by 91.6 %, hence offering a highly scalable real-time semantic search solution.

title: Domain Adaptive Named Entity Recognition for Indian Epics: Fine-Tuning Bert on the Ramayana Corpus
authors: Apratim Haldar, Srijan Roy, Soumajit Choudhury, Apurba Paul
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The standard NER software cannot identify domain-specific text; therefore, we developed our own specialized NER framework for the Ramayana using fine-tuned BERT with a CRF on top. In addition, we use specific training techniques to facilitate creating a mythology-based gazetteer and a human-in-the-loop interface to help keep all annotations at the highest possible quality and allow continual improvement. The results show that our approach significantly outperformed general-purpose models with an F1 score of 0.87 and a 99 % accuracy rate. In addition to being an excellent resource for digital humanities research, our system provides researchers with a strong resource for studying the culture-rich information contained in the Ramayana and other Hindu texts.

title: Decentralized Information Elicitation Without Verification_supp1-3674784.pdf
authors: Jianwei Huang
abstract: Information Elicitation Without Verification (IEWV) refers to eliciting high-accuracy solutions from crowd members when the ground truth is unverifiable. While prior research on IEWV has focused on central entities providing incentives to motivate effort exertion, this work explores the less-studied decentralized setting, which is increasingly relevant in machine learning, crowd decision-making, and autonomous organization applications. We model members’ strategic interactions as a two-stage game, where each member decides her incentive contribution strategy in Stage I and her effort exertion strategy in Stage II. We examine two types of incentive allocation mechanisms: Equal Allocation (EA), where each member receives an equal proportion of the total incentives, and Output Agreement (OA), where a member receives incentives if her solution matches a reference solution generated by other members. This paper first analyzes the two-member case and provides closed-form equilibrium results. For more than two members, we use a binomial approximation to simplify the combinatorial computation of the majority voting problem and characterize the symmetric Nash equilibrium under EA. For OA, we derive equilibrium results for effort exertion and propose an algorithm for the incentive contribution game due to discontinuous payoffs. Our results show that OA outperforms EA in the aggregated team solution accuracy at equilibrium. Furthermore, we reveal that higher member ability beyond a certain threshold may lead to reduced effort exertion under EA, and that smaller teams achieve better accuracy when the effort cost is high due to less free-riding behavior. Numerical and empirical simulations validate our theory.

title: Explanation-First Agentic Forecaster for Stock Market
authors: Sanket Ghosh, Sanjan Baitalik, Rajashik Datta, Romit Mukherjee, Darothi Sarkar, Ayan Chaudhuri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Our contribution tackles a classic problem of predicting stock market behavior, which originates in financial nonlinearities, volatilities, and, importantly, the need for interpretation of financial decisions. While promising, deep models cannot be applied in a trading system due to a lack of interpretability, also called a “black box.” In our paper, an explanation-centric approach to developing an agentic AI tool focused on predicting stock market behavior in terms of direction of stock prices on the next trading day is outlined. Our tool is a mixture of financial indicators, including RSI, MACD, ATR, along with an attention-enhanced LSTM architecture.The framework is based on a multi-agent architecture: an Explainer Agent translates model outputs and Shapley values into plain-English reason codes, a Risk Management Agent computes volatility-adjusted risk-reward metrics using ATR-based parameters, and a Report Generation Agent generates daily PDF summaries of actionable insights autonomously. Validation was performed using OHLCV data from the constituents of NIFTY-50 and applies walk-forward validation to simulate real-world deployment. Key findings reported include: (1) more than 60 percent directional accuracy with an F1-score of about 0.60; (2) better calibration is reflected in an approximate reduction of 0.05 in the Brier score after calibration; (3) high-confidence predictions of over 70 percent are supported by human-interpretable justifications and concrete trade parameters; and (4) ablation analyses confirm that technical features added value, as did calibration. Altogether, combining time-series deep learning with explainable AI techniques in an agent-based design, the proposed framework provides a transparent and modular approach to daily stock forecasting.

title: A Low-Cost Intelligent 3D-Printed Prosthetic Arm Controlled Exclusively by EEG Signals Using Adaptive Machine Learning
authors: Trisha Paul, Swati Chowdhuri, Joydip Roy, Sabyasachi Saha, Sougata Adhikari, Sheli Sinha Chaudhuri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based control has emerged as a promising solution for intuitive and non-invasive control of assistive robotic devices, particularly for individuals with severe limb loss or neuromuscular impairments where muscle signals are unavailable. This paper presents the design and development of a low-cost, lightweight 3D-printed prosthetic arm controlled exclusively by EEG signals. The proposed system decodes user movement intention directly from brain activity using signal processing, sensor fusion, and adaptive machine learning techniques. EEG signals are acquired through a non—invasive multi-channel headset, conditioned to suppress artifacts, and transformed into meaningful features representing motor imagery and movement intention. To improve reliability and reduce noise sensitivity inherent in EEG signals, multiple signal fusion and filtering techniques—including averaging, weighted averaging, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and Kalman filtering—are investigated. Experimental results demonstrate that Kalman filter-based fusion significantly enhances signal stability and control accuracy in real-time prosthetic operation. The prosthetic arm employs a modular 3D-printed mechanical structure with servo-driven actuation, ensuring affordability and customization. Additionally, a future bidirectional feedback framework based on deep learning is proposed to enhance user adaptation and sensory perception. This work contributes toward accessible, intelligent, and fully brain-controlled prosthetic systems for medical rehabilitation and assistive healthcare.

title: SR-VIO: Leveraging Structural Regularity for Robust Initialization and Accurate Visual-Inertial Odometry
authors: Linchuan Zhang, Huilin Yin, Wei Ye, Hao Zhang, Johannes Betz
abstract: Visual-inertial odometry (VIO) is pivotal for ego-motion estimation in autonomous mobile systems. However, Structure from Motion (SfM)-based initialization exhibits robustness degradation under low-parallax conditions, while structurally challenging scenes (e.g., textureless regions) may lead to local trajectory drift. A robust VIO is proposed to estimate the motion state of RGB-D cameras, which fully leverages structural regularity throughout the entire pipeline. First, to achieve more reliable visual-inertial initialization, especially under extreme motion, the geometric regularity of coplanar normals between different observation planes in two frames is used to stabilize the estimation of gyroscope bias. Second, for better detection of structural constraints in the environment, a loosely coupled gravitational vector-optimized method aligned with the globally unique axis in the Atlanta World is proposed. An initial state solver based on linear global translation constraints is integrated into the system to achieve rapid and accurate estimation of the gravity axis. Particularly, a frame-to-frame discriminative method using line feature endpoint reprojection identifies outlier lines in structural clusters and enhances efficiency in correlating line features with Atlanta axes. In addition, a depth covariance intersection filter in VIO initialization and optimization ensures consistent, optimal fusion results despite sensor noise and complex lighting. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method has faster and more accurate initialization state estimation results and better positioning accuracy in various complex structural environments compared to state-of-the-art simultaneous localization and mapping systems.

title: Next Generation 1024x1024 Individually Addressable Mid-Infrared LED Arrays Based on Cascaded W-Superlattice Emitters
authors: John P. Prineas, M.Z. Bellus, Alex C. Walhof, Logan M. Nichols, David A. Montealegre, Michael J. Grzesik, Rodney T. McGee, Hamzah Ahmed, Fouad Kiamilev
abstract: Mid-infrared light-emitting diodes (IRLEDs) offer high modulation bandwidth, high apparent radiance, and analog grayscale operation, making them attractive for large-area patterned emitters and high-speed illumination systems. However, their practical use in dense arrays has been limited by low wall-plug efficiency and thermal performance. Here we demonstrate a 1024×1024, 24 μm pitch mid-infrared IRLED array based on a 16-stage cascaded W-superlattice emitter, quadrupling the pixel count of prior 512×512 arrays. Improvements in superlattice design, backside roughening, and hybridization enabled >99% operability, with apparent temperatures of >1300 K for the full array, ≈1700 K in a quadrant, and >2000 K in process evaluation chips (PEC) devices, and wall-plug efficiencies up to ~1% at low injection. Advances in system control electronics and nanosecond-scale pixel response enabled >400 Hz frame rates with 11-bit radiance resolution. System performance is primarily limited by thermal droop under high drive, but synchronous operation enabled full-array output to 300 K with <1% droop, and thermal modeling predicts >2500 K apparent temperature for ~1% of pixels with enhanced cooling and active thermal management. These results establish large-format IRLED emitter arrays as a scalable platform for high-speed infrared illumination and pattern generation and show a path toward further performance gains through improved wall-plug efficiency and thermal control.

title: Flexible Distributed Buffer-Aided Link Selection for Multi-Hop Relay Networks
authors: Peng Xu, Junfeng Ren, Wei Nie, Yuanzhi He, Gaojie Chen, Yong Li
abstract: This paper investigates distributed link selection (LS) for a multi-hop buffer-aided relay network consisting of one source, one destination, and multiple relays, where each node has access only to local instantaneous channel state information (CSI) and the buffer status of its adjacent nodes. In particular, by improving the alternate-transmission strategy, we propose a novel flexible distributed LS scheme that flexibly selects odd- and even-numbered links in each time slot. Notably, acquisition of local buffer-state information is integrated into the distributed LS agreement process, so it incurs no extra signaling overhead. We also derive the average throughput and packet delay of the proposed scheme by constructing a two-layer Markov model and enumerating all feasible buffer-state transitions. In addition, a simplified expression and an asymptotic analysis are provided to give further insight into the average throughput. Theoretical analysis and simulations show that the proposed flexible scheme substantially outperforms a baseline alternate scheme and closely approaches the performance of a related centralized LS scheme.

title: Comparative Analysis of Vision Transformer Models in Power Quality Disturbance Classification
authors: Sreshtamol K G, Rahul Satheesh, Sunitha Rajan, Hassan Haes Alhelou
abstract: Power quality disturbances (PQDs) are significant irregularities in electrical power systems that can negatively impact system stability and delicate equipment. To ensure the re liability of the power system, PQDs must be classified accurately and effectively. This work investigates the application of Vision Transformer (ViT) based architectures for classifying PQDs, which are preprocessed into spectral images. The performance of the proposed model is evaluated and compared against the convolutional neural networks (CNNs). The three vision transformer variants considered in this work include the Basic ViT model, the Swin Transformer model, and the Data-Efficient Image Transformer (DeiT). Among these three variants, DeiT employs a knowledge distillation strategy, achieving accuracies of 99.33% and 99.38%, and offers qualitative interpretability through attention maps. Through comparative analysis with CNN architectures, such as EfficientNet and DenseNet, this paper highlights the potential of ViT-based models for PQD classification and proposes future extensions toward real-world validation and improved teacher-student frameworks.

title: Construction of Orthogonal Wide-Gap Frequency Hopping Sequence Sets Based on Permutation Matrix
authors: Dongpo Song, Hao Wang, Shilian Wang
abstract: This paper proposes a novel construction of orthogonal wide-gap frequency hopping sequence (WGFHS) sets based on permutation matrix. The proposed sets achieve the maximum attainable sequence capacity while maintaining orthogonality among sequences within each set. For a given frequency slot set, we first construct a wide-gap transition probability matrix, which is a permutation matrix. The orthogonality of the constructed WGFHS sets is guaranteed by the mutual orthogonality among the rows in the permutation matrix. In addition, we introduce a new concept called the wide-gap enabling matrix, which indicates the available transition options with wide-gap property for the next time slot of the current FH codes. To enhance the randomness of the sequences, we propose an effective method to perform sufficient pseudorandom row permutations on the permutation matrix under the constraints of the wide-gap enabling matrix, while ensuring that adjacent FH codes within the same sequence maintain the wide-gap property. Simulation results show that the proposed WGFHS sets exhibit excellent performance in terms of periodicity, uniformity, transition frequency slot distribution, complexity, Hamming correlation, and inter-sequence independence.

title: Free-space FIFO Device for Few-mode Multi-core fibers based on 3D-Printed Microlens Array_supp1-3674605.docx
authors: Ting Lei

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Fiber Laser Amplification and Its Associated Effects
authors: Wei Liu, Zan Cheng, Jun Li, Pengfei Ma, Zefeng Wang, Jinbao Chen
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) offer a promising approach for solving partial differential equations by leveraging modern deep learning techniques and have demonstrated success across various disciplines. In this work, we develop a PINN-based model to explore multi-physics issues in high-power continuous-wave fiber lasers. This model effectively characterizes diverse effects through a unified natural-logarithmic gain equation with tailored treatments of gain coefficients. Specifically, learning the natural logarithm gain equation improves the convergence and stability, enabling accurate simulations of laser amplification in fiber amplifiers and fiber oscillators. Tailored treatments of gain coefficients help characterize amplified spontaneous emission (spectral domain), stimulated Brillouin scattering and stimulated Raman scattering (nonlinear domain), and transverse mode evolution (spatial domain) in three distinct physical domains. Moreover, this model can robustly estimate unknown parameters and efficiently predict threshold powers. The excellent agreement between all prediction results and reference values confirms the feasibility, extensibility, and generality of the model. This work establishes a new methodology for studying laser amplification and provides a promising avenue for advancing scientific machine learning in laser physics.

title: Flight Control and Optimized Allocation for Enhanced Maneuverability of a Tilt-Rotor UAV
authors: Yunjie Yang, Jinlai Deng, Xiangyang Wang, Xiaming Yuan, Jihong Zhu
abstract: Tilt-rotor uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) combine vertical takeoff and landing capability with efficient, long-endurance forward flight. However, transition maneuvers between hover and cruise are particularly challenging due to complex aerodynamic coupling. This article proposes a control method that enables stable flight across the full range of nacelle tilt angles, treating transition not merely as a transient phase but as a robust and controllable flight regime. A velocity-based attitude compensation system combined with a control augmentation strategy is developed to suppress pitching moments caused by longitudinal asymmetry. Additionally, a linear fade-in and fade-out actuator weight optimization strategy is introduced to maximize transition control capability while considering control efficiency and coupling constraints. On this basis, a weighted pseudoinverse control allocation strategy is employed to achieve smooth and accurate actuator coordination in transition flights. Extensive flight experiments are conducted on a newly developed tilt-rotor UAV equipped with tiltable outer wings and inward-inclined tail propellers. The results demonstrate that the proposed method enables stable flight across all nacelle tilt angles and flight speeds, representing a significant advancement in improving the maneuverability of tilt-rotor UAVs.

title: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
authors: N/A

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Approaches for Pancreatic Nodule Detection and Classification in CT Imaging: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Chunliang Zhou, Pakornkiat Sawetmethikul
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Due to its unclear origins and lack of obvious early indicators, pancreatic cancer has a low early diagnostic rate. It results in a rather high death rate from pancreatic cancer. Imaging diagnosticians can diagnose pancreatic lesions more quickly, easily, and accurately by using artificial intelligence technologies to identify and diagnose malignant lesions. After analyzing the main image processing methods for pancreatic lesions from 2018 to 2025, we methodically evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of 2D & 3D deep learning models, transformer-based models, self-supervised pre-training models and hybrid feature fusion models. This study investigates fundamental methods for identifying and classifying pancreatic lesions, as well as specific surgical techniques, leading to the development of a FusionNet-based model for processing pancreatic cancer images. Multimodal pancreatic imaging data will be gathered in the future to create a better annotated database that will improve the robustness and generalization of the model.

title: Research on a Robust Detection Technique for Sequential Deepfake Manipulation
authors: Zhenrong Deng, Xu Chen, Rui Yang, Shiwei Wei
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Sequential deepfake manipulation poses a significant threat, yet existing detection methods suffer from poor robustness under real-world noise. To address this, we propose SLR, a robust framework for sequential forgery detection. First, we propose the SeqDeepFake-noise dataset, which incorporates Gaussian noise of different intensities to simulate realistic degradation. Then, we design SALK-ResNet-50, a novel backbone that enhances global context modeling and noise suppression. Experiments show that SLR achieves superior accuracy and robustness against noise compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Design and Optimization of a Unity3D-Based GOAP Computational Intelligence Decision System for Complex Multi-Agent Behavior Simulation
authors: Yujing Huang, Liuyun Wei, Ting Zhang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: This paper presents an improved Goal-Oriented Action Planning (GOAP)–based computational intelligence decision system implemented on the Unity3D platform for simulating complex multi-agent behaviors. To address limitations of Finite State Machines (FSM) and Behavior Trees (BT) under dynamic and uncertain environments, a goal-prioritized GOAP framework enhanced with heuristic search is developed. Furthermore, safety constraints and robustness mechanisms,including path fault tolerance, resource–conflict arbitration, and redundant perception,are integrated to ensure stable operation in large-scale environments. Experimental evaluations conducted with up to 200 agents demonstrate that the proposed system achieves high real-time performance, improved behavioral intelligence, and significantly enhanced safety robustness. This work provides theoretical and practical insights for designing computationally efficient and safe autonomous multi-agent systems.

title: Table of Content
authors: N/A

title: AI-Driven Real-Time Warehouse Monitoring System Utilizing PLC and SCADA
authors: M. Srinivasan, B.K. Parrthipan, K. Bashkaran, T P Saravanan, Sivakumar Annamalai, Murugesan Manivel, M. N. Muneera, Sarathkumar Duraisamy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper proposes a real-time warehouse monitoring system that integrates temperature, humidity, and gas sensors with a DIGIX-1-1-1-230 PLC via an Arduino-based OPC server. The PLC processes sensor data and automates control responses, such as activating cooling, ventilation, or alarms when environmental conditions surpass predefined safety limits. A CX Supervisor SCADA interface facilitates real-time visualization, alarm notifications, and historical data logging, ensuring a user-friendly and centralized monitoring system. By utilizing PLCs for precise automation and SCADA for comprehensive oversight, the system reduces manual intervention, enhances energy efficiency, and improves operational reliability. Designed for scalability and adaptability, this solution optimizes storage conditions, minimizes energy consumption, and aligns with Industry 4.0 standards for intelligent warehouse management.

title: Neurofusion: A Task-Aware EEG Foundation Model with Adaptive Frequency and Spatial Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Zhige Chen, Szu Chi Chiu, Yi Zhang, Jibin Wu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) decoding presents unique challenges due to data scarcity and cross-task variability. To tackle such a challenge, we propose NeuroFusion, an EEG foundation model with adaptive frequency and spatial selfsupervised learning. Our goal is to learn generalized EEG representations across tasks in the pretraining stage. Specifically, we first use a multi-branch temporal encoder to patch EEG signals into embeddings across multiple frequency bands. The embeddings are fused with learnable frequency and spatial weights and then fed into a transformer encoder for pretraining. During the pretraining stage, we design loss functions to guide frequency and spatial reconstructions, enabling dynamic representations through self-supervised learning. Extensive evaluations on eight benchmark datasets demonstrate that NeuroFusion surpasses prior state-of-the-art EEG foundation models, highlighting NeuroFusion as a robust and generalizable backbone for universal EEG decoding. The visualization of NeuroFusion in both frequency and spatial attention also demonstrates that the model learns brain patterns aligned with the corresponding cognitive tasks.

title: A Lightweight Recognition Method for Compound Jamming Based on Semantic Segmentation
authors: Pengfei Gao, Zhenghuan Wang, Yaning Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper proposes a semantic segmentation-based method for compound jamming recognition in wireless communication links, which offers a novel and effective way toward developing low-latency, high-precision intelligent anti-jamming systems. This method employs a lightweight segmentation model to analyze the time-frequency spectrogram of compound jamming composed of partial-band, comb spectrum, linear sweep, and time hopping jamming-mixed with frequency hopping signal. The proposed method aims to generate precise time-frequency masks for each jamming component. This process can execute without disrupting normal communication operations, and its output can be subsequently used to parameter estimation and effective antijamming measures. Experimental results show that, compared to three classical semantic segmentation models, the proposed model achieves comparable segmentation performance while the number of parameters (0.51 M) and computational complexity (7.35 GFLOPs) are significantly reduced.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach to Electric Vehicle Charging Management
authors: Meng Chen Tsai, Ren Shiou Liu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: As the global push for sustainability intensifies, electric vehicles (EVs) have emerged as a cleaner alternative to traditional fuel-powered cars. However, the rapid increase in EV adoption has outpaced the expansion of charging infrastructure, leading to issues such as grid overload and congestion at public charging stations. These problems are often compounded by the lack of coordinated charging behavior among EV users. Previous studies have rarely explored integrated approaches that jointly consider users' time flexibility and public charging station recommendations. To address these challenges, this study proposes a deep reinforcement learning-based system that recommends both charging locations and time slots. By centrally coordinating all EVs and charging stations within a community, the system aims to minimize users' overall charging cost and waiting time. Experimental results show that the method adapts to varying levels of resource availability, efficiently guiding EVs to avoid congestion and optimize charging performance.

title: Smart Dual Battery Switching System for Continuous Power Supply in Electric Vehicles
authors: A. Balachandran, K. Gogila Devi, S Muthaal, N Amish Kannan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are becoming a viable solution to traditional transportation, although the power outage due to a full battery charge is a significant drawback and poses a severe threat to vehicle maintenance and safety. This paper shows a Smart Dual Battery Switching System that will provide the intelligent management of the two independent battery units to ensure that power is constantly available in the electric cars. The suggested system is a real-time battery voltage and temperature monitor that constantly tracks the battery voltage and temperature with the help of the Arduino Uno based control unit. Once the voltage of the primary battery falls below a preset limit, the system automatically switches to the secondary battery, and this does not affect power delivery thus eliminating sudden shutting down of the vehicle. Temperature and fire sensors are connected to prevent overheating or any possible danger inside the battery compartment to improve the safety of operations. When the abnormal conditions are detected, the system can gradually slow the DC motor and allow safe limiting of the vehicle. An LCD module will avail visualization on-board of battery voltage, switching status and temperature value. More so, the IoT connectivity can be used to remotely monitor, with the mobile application providing realtime updates on the user and the emergency alerts. In order to simulate system operation and verify system functionality, a prototype model with a DC motor is constructed. The findings reveal better reliability, longer working time, and safety, thus the proposed system can be adopted in next-generation electric mobility solutions.

title: UAV Safe Path Planning Algorithm Based on Dual Triangulation Navigation Diagram for Polygonal Obstacle Areas
authors: Enqi Zhang, Yi Liu, Xinzhuang Chen, Xinyi Cai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Addressing the safety concerns in UAV path planning within complex environments, this paper proposes a safe path planning algorithm based on Dual Triangulation Navigation Diagram (DTND). The algorithm first performs safety buffer zone expansion on polygonal obstacle areas to ensure flight safety margins. Subsequently, it employs dual triangulation technology to construct a Voronoi diagram navigation network. Finally, it combines Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm for path searching and applies B-spline curves for path smoothing. Simulation experimental results demonstrate that both DTND and A* algorithms achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> path planning success rate, while the APF algorithm only reaches 56 %. Safety analysis reveals that the DTND algorithm achieves an average minimum safety distance of 8.11 meters, significantly superior to the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm's 5.72 meters. The research results validate the safety and reliability advantages of the DTND algorithm in path planning within complex environments, providing a reliable solution for UAV path planning in complex scenarios.

title: Automated and Optimized Design-for-Testability Implementation and Pattern Validation on Benchmark Circuits S38417 and RISC-V PicoRV32
authors: Nirbhay Bisoriya, Akkala Subba Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This work shows an efficient Design-for-Testability (DFT) implementation and validation on benchmark circuits S38417 and RISC-V PicoRV32 using Synopsys tools, including Design Compiler, DFT Compiler, and TetraMAX II. In order to achieve high fault coverage with shorter test times, the methodology focuses on scan chain insertion, test compression, and Automatic Test Pattern Generation (ATPG). Functional correctness is guaranteed during scan shift, capture, and unload operations thanks to simulation-based pattern validation. The effectiveness of the suggested DFT flow is confirmed by the results, which demonstrate fault coverage above 96.9

title: Design and Development of a Low-Cost Velostat-Based Pressure Sensor for Flatfeet Detection
authors: Trung T. Nguyen, Anh T. Dao, Hop H. Nguyen, Dung Q. Duong, Tien M. H. Nguyen, Tam N. Bui
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Identifying flatfeet is crucial for timely diagnosis and treatment intervention; however, conventional diagnostic methods such as X-rays, CT scans, ultrasound, and MRI are accurate but expensive and require specialized equipment. To provide a cost-effective alternative, this study developed a low-cost contact-sensing mat using Velostat to detect flatfeet based on static footprint analysis. The system consists of a flexible Velostat-based sensor array capable of capturing spatial contact distributions under the foot. A computational algorithm calculates the arch index, a clinically relevant indicator for flatfeet detection. Experimental evaluations demonstrated the system's accuracy, with computed indices aligning well with expected clinical patterns. These results suggest that Velostat-based mats can serve as accessible alternatives to high-cost flatfeet detection methods, with future potential for quantitative pressure measurement.

title:
Study on Synthesis of Metal-Organic Framework MIL-53(Al)-NH
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    and its Ability of Organic Dyes Removal in Aqueous Environment
authors: Dang Thi Minh Hue, Bui Phuong Linh, Dao Van Thanh, Nguyen Thi Tuyet Mai, Nguyen Kim Nga, Tran Thi Thu Huyen, Nguyen Thi Lan, Nguyen Thi Hong Phuong, Huynh Dang Chinh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: The MIL-53(Al)-NH2 material was successfully synthesized via a solvothermal method. Its structural, morphological, and compositional characteristics were systematically analyzed using advanced techniques, including X-ray diffraction (XRD), scanning electron microscopy (SEM), Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR), and UV-visible spectrophotometry. The material's adsorption performance toward Congo Red (CR) and Methylene Blue (MB) dyes was evaluated, along with the corresponding adsorption kinetics. The XRD and FT-IR analyses confirmed the successful formation of the MIL-53(Al)-NH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> framework. The adsorption equilibrium for both dyes was achieved within 15 minutes, indicating rapid adsorption kinetics. The removal efficiencies were 93.77% for CR and 98.98% for MB. Furthermore, the adsorption data for both dyes were well-fitted to the Langmuir isotherm model, with maximum adsorption capacities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$75.19 \text{mg} \cdot \mathrm{g}^{-1}$</tex> for CR and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70.92 \text{mg} \cdot \mathrm{g}^{-1}$</tex> for MB, respectively.

title: FIB: Find Interaction Behavior on Social Media for Rumor Detection
authors: Zhuo Fan, Qing Zhu, Yang Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Rumor detection is a key concern on social media, where rich user interactions pose significant challenges to rumor governance. And it also contributes greatly to rumor detection. However, existing multimodal rumor detection methods suffer from the following limitations. First, they overlook the dynamic nature of social interactions and fail to consider the evolving influence of social context during rumor propagation. Second, they rely on manually constructed social patterns to extract interaction features, which introduces subjective bias and lacks adaptability to changing social environments. Finally, they lack effective fusion strategies for integrating multiple modalities, such as text, images, and social context. To address the issues above, we propose a novel approach to find interaction behavior (FIB) on social media for rumor detection. Specifically, we first infer the latent interaction behavior to mitigate the adverse effects of snapshot limitations when leveraging social modalities. Then, instead of manually constructing, we capture interaction patterns by learning the interaction path automatically. Finally, we design a cross-modal fusion method based on a selective state space model to integrate complex relationships across multiple modalities. Extensive experiments on five large-scale datasets show that the proposed FIB model consistently outperforms all the state-of-the-art rumor detection baselines by 0.09%–4.3% on F1 score.

title: TARS: Trust-Aware Reward Shaping for Vehicle Lane Change Automation
authors: Jundi Liu, Linda Ng Boyle, Ashis G. Banerjee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: While the design of autonomous vehicle systems often centers on efficiency and reliability, user experience and trust play a critical role in adoption and long-term use. This paper presents a foundational step in that direction by introducing an Interactive Reinforcement Learning algorithm, termed Trust-Aware Reward Shaping, or TARS in short, to incorporate trust dynamics into vehicle automation design. The TARS algorithm leverages real-time trust as an additional reward signal during training of the automated trajectory generation system, with lane change as the use case. It, therefore, enables customization based on the user-preferred driving style (aggressive, neutral, or conservative) and trust dynamics (vigilant, confident, or skeptical). Consequently, the algorithm successfully generates emergent behavior that dynamically responds to trust changes, such as a more aggressive style when users show over-trust tendency, and a more neutral style when users demonstrate under-trust or skeptical behaviors. It also predicts real-world driving behaviors observed in naturalistic driving data, and outperforms non-customized, expert-coded, and game-play trust-aware RL methods, as well as baseline inverse RL methods, especially for aggressive and neutral driving styles.

title: Toward Optimal Multi-Vehicle Path Planning With Hybrid Roadmaps
authors: Marianna Turrà, Silvia Proia, Alessandro Bonetti, Lorenzo Sabattini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Effective path planning for multi-vehicle autonomous systems is essential to guarantee safe and efficient operation in industrial environments composed of both structured and unstructured settings. In the proposed framework, the overall environment is explicitly modeled as the integration of static and dynamic areas, each exhibiting distinct spatial and temporal properties. Static areas correspond to structured zones, such as corridors or production lines, and are represented by roadmaps with fixed topology. Dynamic areas, instead, represent unstructured portions of the environment and are modeled as gridmaps to enable adaptive navigation under changing occupancy conditions. Building upon this representation, a hybrid roadmap unifying static and dynamic areas is proposed, with a hierarchical control architecture for coordinated multi-vehicle navigation. The high-layer planner determines the optimal sequence of areas to traverse by solving a multi-objective optimization (MOO) problem that balances efficiency, safety, and traffic-related factors. On the other hand, the low-layer planner generates feasible paths within each area according to its structural constraints. The MOO formulation is embedded in a bi-level optimization framework, where the outer-level employs a quasi-adaptive self-tuning process for objective weights, addressing the limitations of subjective or suboptimal weight selection. Experimental validation across two realistic industrial layouts demonstrates improved mission efficiency, reduced travel-time variability, and scalability with fleet size, confirming the effectiveness of the proposed method for Industry 4.0 applications.

title: Distinct Motor Modules After Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction During Stair Descent: A One-Year Longitudinal Study_supp1-3674750.docx
authors: Yaodong Gu

title: Breaking the Observability Tax: Dynamic Resolution Anomaly Detection via Topology-Aware Active LLM Agents
authors: Rahul Kapoor, Miray Kas
abstract: The ‘‘observability tax’’—the escalating cost of telemetry in hyperscale data centers—forces a trade-off between expensive full-fidelity monitoring and low-cost sampling that misses critical ‘‘gray failures.’’ While traditional AIOps models act as passive post-hoc analyzers, the novelty of this work lies in reformulating observability as an active, hypothesis-driven process. To achieve this, we propose a Dynamic Resolution Architecture where a Topology-Aware LLM Agent dynamically controls the telemetry resolution of the underlying infrastructure. Our Topology-Aware Active LLM Agent employs ‘‘Sentinel Sampling,’’ monitoring 100% of nodes with low-cost essential metrics and only upgrading to high-resolution extended telemetry when semantically justified. Unlike rigid heuristics, the agent uses semantic reasoning to distinguish legitimate heavy workloads from pathological failures, even when root causes are hidden by layers of indirection in our physics-based simulation. Experimental results showthe Active Agent achieves an F1 score of 0.95, significantly outperforming a cost-matched heuristic baseline (F1 0.65). Using a ‘‘Skeptical Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)’’ protocol, the agent achieves 0.99 precision in distinguishing genuine failures from high-load noise. Our ‘‘Wake-on-Anomaly’’ cost model shows an 84% reduction in telemetry bandwidth and a 12.5% reduction in Total Cost of Ownership compared to standard ingestion. This work demonstrates that observability is primarily a data selection, not data volume, problem, solvable via semantic active reasoning.

title: New Tests for the Stability of 2-D Discrete Systems based on Sufficient Conditions
authors: Apostolos Kanellakis, Ayman Tawfik
abstract: This paper presents new stability tests for two-dimensional linear shift-invariant discrete systems. The tests are based on the stability criterion for 2-D discrete systems presented by Murray and Delsarte et al. and sufficient conditions for stable polynomials with complex coefficients. The new tests provide a rapid check for the stability of 2-D discrete systems. Four examples are provided to illustrate the usefulness of the proposed tests.

title: Synthetic Generation of Online Social Networks Through Homophily
authors: Alejandro Buitrago López, Javier Pastor‐Galindo, José A. Ruipérez‐Valiente
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Online social networks (OSNs) have become increasingly relevant for studying social behavior and information diffusion. Nevertheless, they are limited by restricted access to real OSN data due to privacy, legal, and platform-related constraints. In response, synthetic social networks serve as a viable approach to support controlled experimentation, but current generators reproduce only topology and overlook attribute-driven homophily and semantic realism. This work proposes a homophily based algorithm that produces synthetic microblogging social networks such as <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathbb{X}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The model creates a social graph for a given number of users, integrating semantic affinity among user attributes, stochastic variation in link formation, triadic closure to foster clustering, and long-range connections to ensure global reachability. A systematic grid search is used to calibrate five hyperparameters (affinity strength, noise, closure probability, distant link probability, and candidate pool size) for reaching five structural values observed in real social networks (density, clustering coefficient, large connected component proportion, normalized shortest path, and modularity). The framework is validated by generating synthetic OSNs at four scales (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{3}$</tex-math></inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{6}$</tex-math></inline-formula> nodes), and benchmarking them against a real-world Bluesky network comprising four million users. Comparative results show that the framework reliably reproduces the structural properties of the real network. Overall, the framework outperforms leading importance-sampling techniques and classical zero-shot generators applied to the same baseline. The generated graphs capture topological realism and yield attribute-driven communities that align with sociological expectations, providing a realistic, scalable testbed that liberates social researchers from relying on live digital platforms.

title: Texplorer: Efficient Tensor Program Optimization for GPUs via Accurate Search Space Bounding
authors: Wei Li, Bin Gao, Weng-Fai Wong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Optimizing tensor programs for deep learning inference is costly, often requiring hours of search over massive search spaces ($\gt 10^{10}$). Prior machine learning-guided approaches are stochastic and inefficient because they treat both the search and programs as black boxes, overlooking two key properties: (1) the search space is highly structured, with large regions of invalid or low-performing configurations, and (2) tensor programs exhibit analyzable patterns in memory access and pipelined execution. We present Texplorer, a deterministic tensor program optimizer that exploits these structures for robust and efficient search. Texplorer introduces a three-stage pruning algorithm to eliminate invalid, redundant, and inefficient candidates, shrinking the search space from billions to thousands. It replaces costly learned models with a lightweight analytical cost model that captures GPU pipeline and memory behavior, enabling accurate, training-free performance prediction and strong cross-operator generalization. Finally, Texplorer integrates pruning and cost modeling into a one-pass analytical evaluation pipeline that ranks all remaining candidates, enabling rapid convergence without iterative retraining or stochastic sampling. Across 121 operators and 11 DNNs on NVIDIA V100, A100, and H100 GPUs, Texplorer matches state-of-the-art performance while reducing search time by up to $300\times$. An enhanced variant, Texplorer$^+$, further improves performance by up to 3.1% with up to $72\times$ faster tuning.

title: A Review of the Past Half Century of Geostationary Satellite Thermal Observations for Global Precipitation Estimation: Developments, achievements, and future prospects
authors: Siyu Zhu, Guoqiang Tang, Soroosh Sorooshian, George J. Huffman, Qingyun Duan, Songkun Yan, Ali Behrangi, Simon Michael Papalexiou, Phu Nguyen, Kuolin Hsu, Yixin Wen, Zhong Liu, Zhi Li, Mengye Chen, Sante Laviola, Yang Hong
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: Over the past half century since 1975, the development and improvement of meteorological geostationary (GEO) satellites have played a pivotal role in observing cloud dynamics and subsequently in advancing spaceborne precipitation estimation. Infrared (IR) observation from GEO satellites offers unique advantages, such as broad spatial coverage, high temporal resolution, and long-term consistency, motivating extensive research to unlock the potential of GEO IR-based data for capturing precipitation structure and dynamics. This article reviews the major developments and milestone achievements of GEO IR-based precipitation estimation over the past five decades and summarizes the future prospects, including potential directions and remaining challenges. By examining the history of global GEO satellites and more than 100 references in this domain, we categorize the development of GEO IR-based precipitation estimation methodology and technology into three distinct stages: 1) the Exploration Phase (1975 to ca. 1995), 2) the Growth Phase (ca. 1995 to ca. 2015), and 3) the Exploitation Phase (ca. 2015 to the present). As we transition into an emerging new stage, these efforts collectively point toward multispectral retrievals, lifecycle-aware machine learning (ML), smart sensing, and advanced multisource integration as key directions shaping the future of GEO IR-based precipitation estimation. In summary, GEO IR-based precipitation estimation has made substantial contributions over the past half century and will play an increasingly important role with great potential in future precipitation science.

title: High-Temperature Superconducting Busbar for Energy-Efficient Power Distribution in Next-Generation Data Centres
authors: C. M. Mesteru, L. Hao, Z. Huang, Q. Wang, Y. Wu, T. A. Coombs
abstract: Data centres place significant stress on the energy grid. With recent advances in AI, the rising demand for computational power necessitates a rapid expansion of this infrastructure. This study proposes a next-generation 10MW data centre connected to a 100 kV HVDC grid, utilising high-temperature superconducting (HTS) tapes to distribute power with negligible resistive losses. A dual active bridge (DAB) converter system is employed to step down the HVDC input, feeding a stacked-tape superconducting busbar architecture. The analysis demonstrates that, despite higher capital and cooling costs, the superconducting design is economically superior in the long term, achieving cost parity with conventional AC data centres after approximately 5 years and DC data centres after 10 years.
