title: On Theoretical Stability Proof and Stability Margin Analysis of Enhanced Droop-Free Control Schemes for Islanded Microgrids
authors: Weipeng Liu, Upendra Prasad, Yutian Liu, Yong Dong, Haoran Zhao, Lei Wu
abstract: This article studies enhanced droop-free control strategies with sparse neighboring communication for achieving effective active power sharing of distributed energy resources (DERs) while maintaining the frequency stability of islanded microgrids. The normalized active power consensus (NAPC) based droop-free control can share the load among controllable DERs in proportion to their available capacities. However, existing literature exclusively takes the asymptotic stability of the NAPC-based droop-free control for granted, lacking a comprehensive theoretical proof that is critical for ensuring its effective design and practical implementation. This article, for the first time, provides a thorough theoretical proof of the asymptotic stability of two NAPC-based droop-free control schemes: ordinary NAPC (O-NAPC) and amplifier-equipped NAPC (A-NAPC), by testifying that all effective eigenvalues have negative real parts. The effect of various system settings on the stability margins is further analyzed with respect to the average admittance of the electrical network, the sparseness of the communication network, and the average available capacity of controllable DERs. Based on the sensitivity of eigenvalues with respect to perturbations, a vulnerability analysis is conducted to identify the weaknesses in the microgrids. Case studies demonstrate that the available capacity of controllable DERs has the most decisive influence on the stability margin of NAPC-based droop-free control, while O-NAPC/A-NAPC control scheme is more suitable for microgrids with DERs of larger/ smaller available capacities.

title: Applicability of International Delay Models to Non-Lane-Based Traffic in Dhaka City
authors: Hasan Imam, Rafat Arefeen Hanif, Ashikur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Urban traffic in Bangladesh involves complex interactions between motorized and non-motorized vehicles, resulting in a highly heterogeneous flow with poor lane discipline and irregular movement. Conventional analytical delay and signal optimization models developed for homogeneous, lane-based traffic fail to represent such conditions accurately. This study examines the applicability of three analytical delay models: the Australian, Canadian, and Highway Capacity Manual (HCM 2010) models, under Dhaka’s traffic conditions. Data were collected from the Gulshan-1 intersection using video-based observations, allowing continuous monitoring with minimal manpower. Extracted parameters were used to estimate control delay across undersaturated, saturated, and over-saturated states. Results indicate that the HCM, Australian, and Canadian models correspond well with observed delays in saturated and over-saturated conditions, while the Australian and Canadian models perform better under under-saturated flow. However, none fully capture non-lane-based traffic behavior. The study concludes that developing a contextspecific delay model is essential for accurately assessing intersection performance in Bangladesh’s mixed traffic environment.

title: Vehicle-to-Home (V2H) Applications for Residential Energy Resilience
authors: D Sarathkumar, P Sivaraman, R. Shivakumar, S Thangamani, P. Maheswari, Murugesan Manivel, N R Govinthasamy, K Karthi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The increasing penetration of electric vehicles (EVs) presents new opportunities beyond sustainable transportation, particularly in enhancing residential energy resilience through Vehicle-to-Home (V2H) applications. V2H enables bidirectional power flow, allowing EV batteries to serve as distributed energy storage systems that can support household loads during peak demand periods or grid outages. This paper investigates the role of V2H technology in improving energy reliability, cost optimization, and integration with renewable energy resources. The study reviews current V2H architectures, power electronic interfaces, communication protocols, and control strategies required for efficient implementation. Furthermore, it analyzes the potential benefits, including peak load shaving, backup power supply, and improved utilization of distributed renewable generation, alongside challenges such as battery degradation, regulatory barriers, and interoperability issues. A comparative discussion of pilot projects and case studies highlights the practical feasibility of V2H deployment in residential settings. The findings suggest that V2H has significant potential to enhance household energy resilience, provided that supportive policy frameworks, standardized communication, and advanced energy management strategies are adopted.

title: Deepfake Video Detection Framework Employing Deep Learning and Explainability Models
authors: Manivannan D, Kishore Kumar K, Durai S, Manikandan N K, Lasya Vulichi, Shaik Muskan Mohammed
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Hyper-realistic deepfake videos are becoming increasingly common in today's world, which is highly troubling for public trust, security, and information accuracy. To detect deepfake videos produced by face-swapping, this paper proposes an easy-to-understand framework. The proposed system distinguishes between original and altered video frames using a custom CNN built on a trained MesoInception-4 architecture. The framework incorporates a set of auxiliary anomaly detectors that focus on physiological cues such as blink rate and visual artefacts such as blur, colour irregularities, and haloing, in order to increase detection accuracy and guarantee interpretability. Likewise, the system employs Grad-CAM, a method for creating visual explanations that focus on the facial features that have the biggest influence on the model's choice. Thanks to Streamlit, it is carried out via a user-friendly web application that enables uploading, distributing, and providing comprehensive video reports. A key component to creating responsible AI-based media forensics tools is the framework's ability to identify deepfakes and give a clear explanation for its predictions, as demonstrated by experiments. The opacity of deep learning models is directly addressed by Explainable AI (XAI), particularly Grad-CAM. We suggest a multimodal, explainable framework that goes beyond a single classification score in order to address these issues. The synthetic video may still be unable to replicate natural human physiology, such as a regular blink rate, even if a model has trouble with a highly realistic face-swap. On the other hand, a video with strong physiological signals may have distinctive visual artefacts surrounding the blended face area. The opacity of deep learning models is directly addressed by the integration of Explainable AI (XAI), particularly Grad-CAM. We convert the internal reasoning of the model into an understandable format by creating visual heatmaps. This enables analysts to determine whether spurious correlations in the background are tricking the model or is concentrating on semantically significant areas (such as distorted edges surrounding the glasses or an unnatural texture surrounding the mouth). Building trust, making model debugging easier, and offering useful evidence all depend on this transparency.

title: Improved Gray Wolf Optimization Algorithm for Hammerstein Model Parameter Identification
authors: Shan Ouyang, Collins Amukoma, Jinghua Jiang, Zhiyuan Gao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the challenges of parameter identification in Hammerstein models, this paper proposes an Adaptive Chaotic Gray Wolf Optimization Algorithm (ACGWO). Traditional optimization algorithms often suffer from slow convergence and local optima, but the ACGWO algorithm improves performance by introducing a dynamic convergence factor and three positional updating strategies, effectively balancing global search and local development. This approach resolves the parameter coupling issue between nonlinear and linear components in the Hammerstein model. To verify the effectiveness of the proposed method, systematic numerical simulations and experimental validation schemes were designed, focusing on analyzing the algorithm's identification performance for each component parameter of the Hammerstein model. Results demonstrate that the ACGWO algorithm outperforms traditional methods in both convergence speed and recognition accuracy.

title: Korosuke: A Principled Framework for Security-Preserving Contextual Retrieval in Enterprise Knowledge Systems
authors: Prakashita Singh, Sushil Pandey, Sachin Kumar Yadav, Bj Gridhar, V. M. Manikandan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Enterprise knowledge systems confront an inherent conflict between robust contextual comprehension and rigorous security constraints. This research presents a theoretically grounded security-preserving context-aware retrieval architecture that fundamentally reimagines how Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) can operate within strictly controlled information environments. We present two principal innovations: (1) a hierarchical security-aware embedding space that mathematically preserves access control constraints during semantic search, and (2) an adaptive re-ranking algorithm that optimizes the relevance-security tradeoff through a novel information utility function. Our theoretical contributions are validated through a reference implementation deployed across three distinct enterprise environments with varying security requirements. Experimental results demonstrate statistically significant improvements in both precision-recall metrics and security compliance compared to traditional approaches. We provide formal proofs of security guarantees under specific threat models and release anonymized benchmark datasets. This research advances the fundamental understanding of how contextual reasoning can be preserved while maintaining strict information boundaries, contributing broadly applicable principles to the fields of secure information retrieval and enterprise AI systems.

title: Detecting Ambiguities in Non-Functional Requirements Using Transformer-Driven Information Extraction
authors: Fiza Talha, Touseef Tahir, Talha Nadeem
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: In software requirements engineering, ambiguous non-functional requirements can cause design inconsistencies and poor software quality. Manual ambiguity detection is time consuming and inefficient, particularly for large-scale requirements documents. Prior studies lack in handling ambiguous non-functional requirements and often analyze simple English statements instead of discussing NFRs. In this study, we emphasize detecting anaphoric, coordination, and missing-condition ambiguity in non-functional requirements. For anaphoric ambiguity detection, we applied SpanBERT due to its contextual capturing abilities. Coordination and missing-condition ambiguities were identified using a rule-based approach, i.e., spaCy's Named Entity Ruler. We conducted experiments on a significant dataset consisting of 2400 non-functional requirements from 16 different domains. Experimental results show that the suggested approach performs better than ChatGPT in terms of accuracy and consistency across the evaluated datasets. The proposed approach can assist requirement engineers and project managers in identifying ambiguities at an early stage of development. Timely detection helps reduce rework, associated costs, schedule risks, and improves overall software quality.

title: XReGViT: Explainable GAN-ViT Model for Retinal Lesion Detection and Diabetic Retinopathy Severity Classification
authors: Dakannagari Harith Reddy, Anurodh Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is one of the causes of preventable blindness and early diagnosis by retinal fundus analysis is essential to the effective treatment of diabetic retinopathy. Conventional convolutional neural network (CNN) methods are usually subject to class imbalance, poor lesion readability, and poor generalization. This paper introduces XReGViT, a framework of Explainable GAN-ViT that integrates generative augmentation, transformer-based classification, and explainable lesion localization as an automated retinal analysis framework. The model uses a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to generate highfidelity fundus images to reduce the problem of data imbalances and increase the diversity of lesions. As the main classifier, a Vision Transformer (ViT) is used that uses self-attention to learn global contextual features across five levels of DR, noDR, Mild, Moderate, Severe, and Proliferative DR on the APTOS 2019 dataset. Grad-CAM and attention-rollout visualization explainability is added, which allows localization of lesions microaneurysms, hemorrhages, and exudates of lesions at the clinical level. Through experimental analysis, it is shown that XReGViT attains a 97.18% accuracy, 96.96% precision, 97.18% recall, and $F 1$-score of $97.06 \%$, which is better than the benchmark models.

title: Blood Group Detection Using CNN from Fingerprint Images
authors: J. Lakshmi Alekhya, Bharani Choppara, Eedara Jaswanth, Nimmagadda Jaswanth
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Fingerprints serve as one of the most unique and immutable biometric identifiers, remaining unchanged throughout an individual’s lifetime. Their distinct ridge patterns and minutiae points make fingerprint recognition highly reliable for personal identification. In this study, we propose a deep learningbased approach using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model to determine an individual’s blood group from fingerprint images, specifically for emergency medical situations. Traditional fingerprint analysis relies on ridge frequency and spatial feature extraction using techniques like the Gabor filter. However, our approach leverages CNN’s ability to automatically learn and extract hierarchical features from finger print patterns, enabling accurate blood group classification. The proposed system processes fingerprint images through multiple convolutional layers to capture intricate features that correlate with blood group information. By training the CNN model on a dataset of labeled fingerprint images, we aim to provide a fast and non-invasive method for real-time blood group identification in emergencies, assisting medical responders in critical situations. This research explores the integration of biometric authentication with medical diagnostics, enhancing emergency healthcare efficiency.

title: Solar Synergy: Real-Time Monitoring of Decentralized Solar Energy Systems via IoT Cloud Application
authors: Kritika Bansal, K. Dinesh Vardhan, Vardhini Tirukkovalluri, R. Sai Monish Kumar, Chandu D S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: As solar energy has become a leading sustainable source of power, the need for real-time monitoring of solar panels performance in DC microgrids becomes increasingly important especially for remote and decentralized set-ups. To address this issue, this paper presents Solar Synergy which is an open-source, Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled monitoring system designed for tracking the real-time status of solar energy systems. The designed system incorporates components such as Arduino Mega, ESP8266 and various sensors. It collects data on voltage, current, calculated power output, temperature, humidity, and light intensity, and transmits them to cloud-based platforms such as Google Sheets and Arduino IoT Cloud with the help of WiFi and Hyper Text Transfer Protocol (HTTP) for visualization and remote access. The developed prototype provides continuous system feedback and alerts for anomalies such as sudden drops or spikes in voltage and current and zero voltage events. This work lays the groundwork for future developments, such as predictive maintenance using machine learning, edge-based decision making, and integration with hybrid energy systems for optimized performance.

title: Intelligent Digital Twins for CNC Machining Optimization Using Reinforcement Learning and IoT Data
authors: A. Hency Juliet, P. Malathi, Geetha B, S. Sathish Kumar, V. Vijayalakshmi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The objective of this study is to design an intelligent digital twin for CNC machining by leveraging IoT- based sensor data and reinforcement learning to improve process efficiency and reduce the likelihood of machine failures. Additionally, the research focuses on incorporating uncertainty- aware intelligence to support adaptive, reliable, and data-driven optimization in smart manufacturing systems. The extreme imbalance between normal and failure conditions in CNC machining data complicates effective detection of rare fault events and skews model learning. Developing a reliable real- time digital twin is challenging because of noisy IoT sensor data, uncertainty, and the requirement for safe and stable reinforcement learning optimization. To mitigate these limitations, the proposed approach integrates imbalance-aware learning strategies with Bayesian uncertainty estimation and reinforcement learning-driven adaptive optimization to achieve robust fault prediction and efficient CNC machining control. The proposed extended approach enhances the accuracy and reliability of CNC failure prediction by addressing data imbalance and incorporating uncertainty-aware intelligence, while simultaneously enabling adaptive, real-time optimization of machining parameters through reinforcement learning to improve efficiency and reduce operational risk. In conclusion, the intelligent digital twin framework significantly improves CNC machining optimization by delivering a 69.33 % gain in reinforcement learning-based performance with low predictive uncertainty, confirming its potential for reliable and adaptive industrial process optimization.

title: Autonomous Self-Healing IoT Networks: A Distributed MQTT-Based Architecture for Device-Level Fault Detection and Recovery
authors: Niel Dip Saha, Sreshtha Hira, Akash Patra, Sandipan Maji, Sambaran Sen, Piyush Rai, Samit Karmakar, Soumik Kumar Kundu, Mili Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This paper addresses device failures in distributed and embedded networks where nodes lack built-in selfmonitoring or recovery mechanisms. It presents an architecture in which each device periodically publishes its health status over a lightweight publish-subscribe channel, while one device serves as coordinator. When that coordinator fails, the remaining devices autonomously elect a new coordinator, enabling continuous monitoring and recovery without dedicated servers, external infrastructure, or redundant hardware. The system combines node-level fault detection, dynamic reconfiguration, and standard messaging to support self-healing deployments in resource-constrained environments. Appropriate simulations yielded results indicating seamless failure detection, leader reestablishment and task routing within acceptable time bounds and minimal communication and computation overload.

title: A Review on Recent Advances and Future Perspectives of Lithium-Ion Battery Technology
authors: Surbhi Kumari Shaw, Sparsh Shaw, Rajib Ghosh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The Lithium-ion batteries or LIBs have become the most popular energy storage device as they offer high energy intensive, long cycle life, and are applicable across the board, to portable electronics or electronic gadgets and electric vehicles. As the need to maintain clean and efficient energy solutions escalates across the globe, the recent development of lithium-ion battery materials and cell design has been necessary. This review paper presents in details working principles of LIBs (lithium-ion batteries) that primarily emphasizes on the main mechanisms of lithium-ion intercalation and deintercalation. It also focuses on the recent advancement of anode and cathode materials and also on the advantages as well as their limitations. The problems associated with material degradation, conductivity, safety, and performance are elaborated. The paper also describes the future considerations with a focus on next-generation electrode materials, innovations in the electrolyte and new manufacturing methods to enhance energy density, safety, and sustainability.
