title: Design of an Innovational Solar Operated Golf Cart
authors: Nader Ghareeb
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: This work proposes a novel approach of using a sustainable energy source to power an innovative design of a multifunctional golf cart in the State of Kuwait. This approach uses solar energy as a clean source. The cart, designed for versatility and eco-friendliness, integrates a brushless DC motor, charge controller, battery, solar panel and light steel frame. The proposed cart in this report is used to transport workers and farmers from their station, which is a place around the land, to the farm space in order to decrease the transportation cost and energy consumption.

title: Real-Time Pineapple Bud Counting for Yield Estimation with YOLO11 and StrongSORT
authors: Pradei Sangkhro, Bidyarani Langpoklakpam, Lithungo K Murry, R. Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Pineapple is a significant crop in Northeast India, but its cultivation remains highly labour-intensive and financially expensive. Reliable and low-cost yield estimation would help farmers with effective planning; however, manual counting is impractical. This paper proposes a real-time pipeline that integrates YOLO11s with StrongSORT to detect and count pineapple buds using drone and handheld footage. The model is trained on field-acquired data, achieving a mAP@0.5 of 88.6%, precision of $84.3 \%$, and recall of $86 \%$ on the test set. The detection-only pipeline operates at a median of $\mathbf{6 1 . 7 ~ F P S}$, while the full detect-track-count pipeline operates at 31.4 FPS, supporting near-edge deployment. Tracking and counting across three test videos yielded accuracies of $96 \%, 73.5 \%$, and $96.2 \%$. Most counting errors stemmed from repeated revisits and tracker re-initialisation rather than detector failure. The proposed system offers a practical and deployable solution for reliable, low-cost pineapple yield estimation.

title: Switchgear Technology Comparison: An In-Depth Analysis of Air-Insulated vs. GasInsulated Systems
authors: K. Vinoth, A. Surya, T. Gurunathan, L. Vamitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Understanding and analyzing the demand for safe, and proper power distribution systems has intensified the use cases between the both Air Insulated Switchgear (AIS) and Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) in the electrical networks. In this comparative analysis we have presented an analysis of AIS and GIS based on critical parameters such as design, space requirements, dielectric strength, reliability, operational safety, cost, maintenance, and environmental impact. While AIS is widely adopted due to its simplicity and lower initial cost, GIS offers superior compactness, enhanced safety, and higher reliability, particularly in urban and high-voltage applications. The study evaluates practical case scenarios where each technology demonstrates distinct advantages, highlighting the trade-offs between capital investment, lifecycle costs, and environmental considerations, including the challenges of SF gas usage in GIS. The results provide utilities and engineers with insights to select optimal switchgear solutions based on application-specific needs, balancing technical, economic, and sustainability factors.

title: Credibility Prediction of Online Reviews: Analysis of ML Classifiers and a Neural Network Model
authors: P. Vijaya Lakshmi, Chilaka Mahesh, Popuri Narapa Reddy, Battini Naga Saran Teja, Pavan Kumar Rejeti, Geeta Padole
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Customer purchase choices are strongly shaped by online reviews; however, deceptive or manipulated reviews reduce their reliability for both consumers and businesses. To address this issue, we developed a predictive framework that determines the credibility of a review based on textual content and consistency-related checks, without depending on reviewer-specific details that could raise privacy concerns. Our methodology incorporates six key features that influence credibility: review length, subjectivity, readability, extremity, internal consistency, and external consistency. Attributes extracted from Yelp reviews were analyzed through both traditional ML algorithms and a neural network model were employed for analysis. Among the tested approaches, the Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) achieved the best performance, reporting 95.19% accuracy and an AUC of 0.972, surpassing the classical models. SVM and XGBoost also delivered strong results, while Random Forest and KNN showed competitive performance. Feature importance analysis indicated that subjectivity, review length, and rating consistency exert the strongest influence on credibility prediction. These results highlight that integrating linguistic features with consistency-based indicators, alongside advanced classifiers, can provide an effective and scalable solution for detecting fake reviews. This framework enhances the reliability of online reviews, thereby assisting consumers in decision-making and improving confidence in digital platforms.

title: Development of Artificial Intelligence Tools for Predictive Maintenance in High Voltage Systems
authors: D Sarathkumar, T P Saravanan, S. Ragul, G Guna, B.K. Parrthipan, Murugesan Manivel, R. Sivakumar, Sivakumar Annamalai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The increasing complexity and operational demands of high voltage systems in modern power grids necessitate advanced maintenance strategies to ensure reliability, safety, and efficiency. Traditional maintenance approaches, often reactive or schedule-based, are proving inadequate for addressing dynamic grid challenges. This paper explores the improvement as well as application of artificial intelligence (AI) tools for predictive maintenance in high voltage systems. By leveraging machine learning algorithms, deep learning models, and innovative statistics analytics, these trappings allow the real-time observing and analysis of equipment health, facilitating early detection of faults and reducing unplanned outages. The proposed AI framework integrates sensor networks, historical maintenance data, and environmental parameters to forecast possible botches by high accurateness. Illustrative case analyses demonstrate the efficacy of AI-driven predictive maintenance in extending equipment lifespan, enhancing maintenance schedules, and reducing operational costs. Furthermore, the paper addresses contests such as statistics value, classical scalability, and integration with existing grid infrastructure. The findings underline the transformative prospective of AI in revolutionizing maintenance practices for high voltage schemes, flagging the way for smoother, further resilient power grids.

title: ACO-TLBO Based Global Demand Response Optimization for Smart Renewable Grids
authors: Harita Aneesh, P Kiran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: As the global energy landscape is rapidly shifting towards renewable sources, efficient and intelligent energy management becomes essential for maintaining the demand equilibrium. Traditional methods of demand response lack adaptability and optimization mainly while we are handling a dynamic mix of renewable sources such as solar, wind, hydro, and biofuel. This paper proposes a novelty that is hybrid intelligent system for optimizing global renewable energy allocation using an Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) and Teaching Learning-Based Optimization (TLBO) framework. The model used worldwide energy data, simulates demand using a weighted combination of key renewable sources, and dynamically adjusts allocation weights through heuristic-guided optimization. Visualization outputs include convergence patterns, correlation heatmaps, and allocation comparison plots. This approach here demonstrates a high accuracy, adaptability, and scalability, making it suitable for real-time intelligent energy management in smart renewable grids.

title: Improving the Usability and Performance of a Gaze Movement Training System for Individuals with Low Vision with a Lightweight Eye-Tracking Algorithm
authors: Daichi Kuroki, Hiroki Karashima, Akihiro Yamashita, Katsushi Matsubayashi
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Individuals with low vision, particularly those with visual field impairments such as tunnel vision or central scotomas, often face difficulties in accurately directing their gaze toward intended targets. Although recent studies have suggested the potential of eye movement training to improve visual function, standardized and effective training methods for this population remain underexplored. In this study, we developed an interactive application that enables users with low vision to train themselves to perform specific eye movements by locating and touching targets on a screen. To integrate gaze tracking, we experimentally investigate gaze estimation using a webcam to compare the performance of a method based on a CNN model with a conventional alternative using landmarks. Feedback on usability and visibility was also collected from participants with low vision to refine the interface. Here, we present the developed prototype along with the experimental results and some new insights on establishing a systematic eye movement training system.

title: Intelligent Talent Acquisition Assistant: A Multi-Agent AI Framework for Automated Recruitment Optimization
authors: Nishaad Mishra, Mayurakshi Pani, Sayan Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The modern job market is getting more competitive, which makes it important for companies to use hiring solutions that are efficient, scalable, and fair. Traditional recruitment methods are still slow, labor intensive, and often struggle to manage the sheer volume of applications. In this paper, we introduce the Intelligent Talent Acquisition Assistant, an AI driven multi agent system designed to automate and streamline the hiring process. The system uses dedicated agents for candidate sourcing, screening, engagement, and scheduling which has been built using CrewAI, LangChain, and advanced state of the art large language models such as Gemini-1.5-flash and LLama3-8B-8192. The framework supports accurate, efficient and unbiased candidate evaluation by combining contextual matching, semantic retrieval through ChromaDB, and retrieval augmented generation (RAG). The intelligent assistant brings separate parts of the recruitment workflow together into one smart connected and streamlined modular workflow with the strength of Agentic AI. The primary goal of this research is to speed up the hiring process and improve the candidate experience so as to make the decision process more transparent.

title: Multi-Task Learning Network-Based Condition Assessment of GIS Partial Discharge: Diagnosis and Severity Evaluation
authors: Y. Wang, J. Yan, J. Wang, Y. Geng, D. Srinivasan
abstract: Current methods for assessing the state of partial discharge (PD) in gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) often treat the diagnosis and severity evaluation tasks independently, which limits model performance and overlooks the role of defect types in the PD development stages. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel multi-task network for the condition assessment of PD in GIS. Firstly, a lightweight attention combined with an expert network module is introduced to explore the correlations between different tasks while retaining their unique features. Secondly, we incorporate a convolutional neural network (CNN)-Transformer module to capture both local and global features of GIS PD signals. Finally, we implement a dynamic weight averaging method for the multi-task network, which adaptively adjusts the loss weights via the variation rate of the losses of each sub-task. We conducted experiments on two GIS PD datasets with different insulating gases. Experimental results demonstrate that the multi-task network significantly improves the performance of each sub-task in GIS condition assessment, advancing the development stage evaluation of PD for various insulation defects. Furthermore, experiments on two types of insulating gases validate the generalization and versatility of the proposed method.

title: Leakage Inductance Reduction in Coupled Inductors Used in Quasi-Resonant Flyback Converters as LED Drivers
authors: Joño Renato Camara Amado, Renan Rodrigo Duarte, Giulia Kaufmann Grassi, Kalyl Sinhorini Abu Ali, Hentony Lobo, Marco Antonio Dalla Costa
abstract: This paper proposes and experimentally validates a design methodology to significantly reduce leakage inductance in coupled inductors used in quasi-resonant flyback converters for LED drivers. The methodology uses a theoretical model of the leakage inductance related to the inductor core and winding geometry, complemented by finite element simulations, and validated with experimental results from eleven inductor prototypes. Results demonstrate up to 87.6% reduction in leakage inductance, leading to lower voltage stress, reduced component temperatures, and efficiency gains of up to 5% in a 60 W LED driver prototype. The methodology is compatible with standard manufacturing processes and requires no additional circuitry, making it suitable for cost-sensitive LED lighting applications that demand compactness, thermal efficiency, and reliability.

title: Dynamic Modeling and Mutual Inductance Estimation for UAV Hovering Wireless Charging Under Continuous Disturbances
authors: Haoxiang Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Hovering wireless charging is considered a promising solution to extend the endurance of electric unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), which are otherwise constrained by limited onboard battery capacity. However, unavoidable positioning errors and environmental disturbances cause continuous variations in the relative alignment of transmitting and receiving coils, resulting in mutual inductance disturbances that severely degrade energy transfer performance. To address this issue, this paper proposes a dq dynamic model-based mutual inductance estimation method. The approach enables fast, real-time, and accurate tracking of mutual inductance variations without requiring additional sensors or wireless communication links, while also meeting the lightweight design constraints of the UAV receiver side through a series-series compensation topology deployed on the transmitter side. Both simulations and experimental validations confirm that the proposed method achieves high accuracy and low latency, with errors below 1% and response times within a few milliseconds. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach provides an effective solution to ensure stable and efficient operation of UAV hovering wireless charging systems.

title: Arthroscopic Reconstruction of the Anterior Talofibular and Calcaneofibular Ligaments for Chronic Ankle Instability: Early Outcomes from Military Hospital 175
authors: Huu Luong Do, Bao Ngoc Tu, Trung Hai Do, Thanh Tu Nguyen, Hai Dang Nguyen Trong, Khoi Luan Tran, Quoc Hung Vu, Anh Sang Nguyen, Doanh Tran Quoc
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Chronic lateral ankle instability (CLAI), often resulting from complete rupture of the anterior talofibular ligament (ATFL) and calcaneofibular ligament (CFL), remains a frequent and functionally limiting condition, particularly in young, active individuals. While open surgical techniques have long been standard, arthroscopic reconstruction has emerged as a less invasive alternative that allows for precise anatomical restoration. This study evaluates the early clinical outcomes of arthroscopic ATFL and CFL reconstruction performed at Military Hospital 175. A total of 26 patients diagnosed with CLAI underwent endoscopic ligament reconstruction between 2019 and 2024. Inclusion criteria required MRI confirmation of complete ATFL and CFL tears. The surgical technique involved harvesting the autologous semitendinosus tendon, creating bone tunnels in the fibula, talus, and calcaneus, and fixation using suture anchors. Functional outcomes were measured using the American Orthopaedic Foot & Ankle Society (AOFAS) score at 1, 3, and 6 months postoperatively. The mean patient age was 27.38 years, and the average symptom duration was 16.65 months. AOFAS scores improved significantly from a baseline of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 1. 8 8} \pm \mathbf{2. 3 7}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.42 \pm 4.41$</tex> at 6 months post-surgery (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.001$</tex>). There were no intraoperative complications. Minor postoperative events included two superficial infections and four cases of transient paresthesia, all of which resolved spontaneously. The radiographic evaluation confirmed satisfactory graft positioning and restoration of joint stability. All patients reported good to excellent satisfaction. Arthroscopic anatomical reconstruction of ATFL and CFL using autologous tendon grafts is a safe, effective, and minimally invasive option for treating CLAI. The procedure yields favorable short-term functional outcomes and high patient satisfaction, supporting its application as a modern alternative to open surgery.

title: Omnidirectional Camera-Based Fall Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: Viet Dung Nguyen, Duc Hieu Nguyen, Le Minh Duong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Falls among the elderly pose a major health risk, especially for individuals living alone. Automated fall detection systems are crucial for timely intervention. Omnidirectional cameras offer wide coverage but introduce geometric distortion, posing challenges for conventional vision models. This study evaluates fall detection performance using the BOMNI dataset, focusing on Scenario \#1, which includes various human actions. We compare different preprocessing pipelines and deep learning models, including DETR-ResNet50 and YOLOv11. Our bestperforming model, YOLOv11-M trained on preprocessed data with undistortion and background subtraction, achieved a precision of 0.978, recall of 0.957, F1-score of 0.967, and mAP@ 50 of 0.982 surpassing existing methods on the same dataset. DETRResNet50 showed strong performance in detecting the “fainted” class with high AP, while YOLOv11 demonstrated superior overall robustness. These results confirm the effectiveness of deep learning, particularly YOLO-based models, for real-time fall detection in omnidirectional video. Future work will focus on incorporating temporal dynamics and optimizing models for real-world deployment.

title: Conserved Oligonucleotides in Noncoding Regions of SARS-CoV-2 and Their Potential Roles in Viral Pathogenesis
authors: Kieu D.M. Nguyen, Long D. Dang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: This study identifies conserved motifs in the untranslated regions (UTRs) of the SARS-CoV-2 genome, exploring their roles in viral pathogenesis. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5^{\prime}$</tex> UTR motif (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5^{\prime}$</tex> CGATCTCTGTG 3'), similar to eukaryotic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5^{\prime}$</tex>, terminal oligopyrimidine (TOP) motifs, may enhance viral RNA translation through recapping. A 3'UTR motif (5' GGAAAGGC 3'), a potential miRNA seed region, could suppress host immune responses, contributing to delayed innate immunity. These findings, derived from bioinformatics analysis, highlight molecular signatures that may serve as targets for diagnostic tools and therapeutic strategies.

title: Diagnosis Method of Standing Wave Performance for Electromagnetic Functional Structures Based on Extreme Learning Machine
authors: Dongwei Mao, Xinrui Fang, Lin Wang, Ping Meng, Hao Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the challenge of manually assessing the massive standing wave performance of electrical ports in complex electromagnetic functional structures, this paper proposes a standing wave performance diagnosis method for electromagnetic functional structures based on Extreme Learning Machine (ELM). By utilizing Principal Component Analysis (PCA), discriminative frequency domain features are extracted from standing wave test data. Subsequently, a diagnostic model is constructed leveraging the classification performance of ELM. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ELM-based method outperforms both BP neural networks and Support Vector Machines (SVM), achieving an accuracy of 99.3%.

title: CBSSNet: A Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmenting Algorithm with Teacher-Student Binding via Cosine Dissimilarity
authors: Duy-Thai Nguyen, Van-Quoc-Viet Nguyen, Thi-Thao Tran, Van-Truong Pham
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Introduction: Deep learning algorithms have been increasingly used for tasks in the medical sector, where accuracy and optimal time are highly regarded. The traditional way of training a deep learning model is by going through iterations of comparing the prediction with the ground-truth and adjusting the model via gradient descent. However, for a model to achieve good results, it has to be trained on a sufficiently large dataset, which is not always available in the medical sector, especially for segmentation tasks. In such tasks, the hardest part of the dataset preparation process is producing the ground-truth masks, which is time-consuming and requires high expertise. Methodology: To contribute an algorithmic solution to this challenge, in this study, a semi-supervised segmentation model named CBSSNet is proposed with a valuable idea of using the Cosine dissimilarity to effectively determine the quality difference between the feature maps of the student's and the teacher's architectures around the encoding phase. Moreover, a new block named MSFE (Memorized-Selective Feature Extractor) is introduced to take advantage of the condensed feature at the bottleneck. Results: The model is assessed using the Data Science Bowl (DSB) and the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC18) datasets. Conclusion and Discussion: Ablation study and comparison with the state-of-the-art shows optimistic results. Further model details and the model's source code will be available at: https://github.com/thzhere/CBSSNet.git

title: URAP Under Desynchronization Attack: Analysis and a Lightweight Defense Strategy
authors: Roshan Jalil, Michelle Webb, Madiha Khalid
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) systems are widely deployed in access control, inventory tracking, and track-and-trace applications due to their low cost and ease of integration. Ultra-lightweight authentication protocols are typically used to ensure the security and privacy of the communication channel between the tag and reader pair. This paper presents the security analysis of a recent protocol, i.e, Ultra-lightweight RFID Authentication Protocol (URAP). The proposed work highlights a critical vulnerability in the architecture and working of the URAP protocol. To mitigate the threat, we propose a lightweight enhancement that introduces timestamp validation into the authentication process, improving resilience against denial-ofservice scenarios without compromising protocol efficiency.

title: Likelihood-Aware Nonlinear Kalman Filtering Via Statistical Linear Regression
authors: Sanfeng Hu, Liping Guo, Mengjiao Tang, Yao Rong, Maria Sabrina Greco, Fulvio Gini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: A variety of widely used Gaussian filters are formulated within the framework of statistical linear regression (SLR), where nonlinear measurement functions are approximated via least-squares linearization. Although effective in numerous applications, conventional SLR neglects the structure of the likelihood function, which can lead to inaccurate Gaussian approximations of the posterior distribution. To overcome this limitation, we propose a likelihood-aware SLR (LA-SLR) method that introduces a linearization criterion explicitly incorporating likelihood structural information. The resulting nonconvex, nonsmooth optimization problem is efficiently solved using a tailored alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM), with closed-form solutions derived for its subproblems. Building on LA-SLR, we develop both a posterior approximation algorithm and a nonlinear Kalman filtering algorithm, and evaluate them in several representative scenarios. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithms consistently outperform state-of-the-art Gaussian filters.

title: Interpretable Time-Aware AI Framework for Early Detection and Assessment of At-Risk Learners
authors: Khan Ghulam Murtaza, Libin Cai, Shahbaz Gul Hassan
abstract: Early detection of at-risk learners is essential for enhancing retention and delivering adaptive interventions in online education. This paper presents an interpretable, time-aware artificial intelligence (AI) framework that integrates temporal behavior modeling, explainable ensemble learning, and decision calibration to support formative assessment in massive open online courses (MOOCs). Weekly learner engagement data from an open-access MOOC dataset were reconstructed into temporal sequences to capture changes in participation, quiz performance, and video interaction. Temporal descriptors—such as engagement trend, volatility, and learning momentum—were engineered to quantify behavioral dynamics over time. Feature attribution and selection were achieved using SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), ensuring pedagogically meaningful interpretability. A soft-voting ensemble combining XGBoost and Random Forest classifiers was optimized with class-balance correction through SMOTE and threshold calibration via the receiver-operating-characteristic (ROC) curve. The proposed model achieved 87 % accuracy, 0.914 ROC AUC, and 95 % recall, outperforming comparable benchmark methods while maintaining transparency. The framework enables instructors to visualize individualized risk profiles and understand the behavioral indicators underlying each prediction, transforming dropout modeling into actionable early-warning assessment. By uniting temporal analytics with explainable AI, this study advances trustworthy, human-centered intelligence for continuous learner monitoring and personalized educational support.

title: Adaptive Multimodal Fusion Architecture for Heterogeneous Embedding Spaces in Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
authors: Timothy Dillan, Sani Muhamad Isa, Abba Suganda Girsang, Derwin Suhartono
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation systems integrate external information to mitigate hallucinations in large language models, yet existing multimodal retrieval-augmented generation implementations struggle with heterogeneous embedding spaces from diverse sources. This study introduces an adaptive multimodal fusion architecture that addresses the critical challenge of integrating embeddings from research models and commercial application programming interfaces with varying dimensionalities (ranging from 768 to 1,536 dimensions) and training paradigms. We propose a source-agnostic framework comprising lightweight adapters, cross-modal attention mechanisms, and learned confidence calibration. The system was evaluated on 20,000 multimodal samples across image, video, audio, and text modalities, demonstrating significant performance improvements. The architecture enables end-to-end training without separate pretraining steps while maintaining flexibility for pretrained encoders. Experimental results show 7.2% improvement in Recall@10 compared to single-best source approaches (82.4% versus 89.6%), with expected calibration error of 0.048, indicating well-calibrated confidence scores. The framework achieves graceful degradation with only 3.8% performance reduction when one modality becomes unavailable and maintains 81.3% of in-domain performance in zero-shot domain transfer tasks. This work advances multimodal retrieval-augmented generation systems by enabling plug-and-play integration of heterogeneous embedding sources while preserving computational efficiency and interpretability.

title: Predicting Conflict Risk for Vehicle Queues at Large-Traffic-Volume Intersections Using Unsupervised Clustering and LSTM Models
authors: Yubing Zheng, Yang Ma, Zhongxiang Feng, Weihua Zhang, Yiik Diew Wong, Said M. Easa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Vehicles at congested intersections usually move in the form of queues. The reduced inter-vehicle spacing within moving vehicle queues (VQs) creates a cascading collision risk potential, where any initial conflict may trigger chain-reaction impacts. However, traffic conflict risks were mainly assessed at the individual level in existing studies, while the analysis of VQ format was rare. To fill the gap, this paper proposes a framework for assessing and predicting conflict risks for VQs using high-resolution trajectory data. The proposed framework comprises three main steps. First, a ‘path band’ is created for each vehicle at the intersection, based on which a grid map-based method is proposed for the real-time clustering of VQs. Second, a multiagent-based approach is developed for estimating angle, rear-end, and side-swipe conflicts for each identified VQ. In this case, both straight-going and turning VQs are considered. Third, multi-dimensional feature sequences are extracted to capture VQ’s dynamic characteristics, while the developed traffic conflict analytics are used to label conflict risks. Using the feature sequences and risk labels as input and response, respectively, long short-term memory (LSTM) models are developed to predict conflict risks for VQs. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is demonstrated through tests on the open-access CitySim dataset. The test results indicate that the LSTM models using feature sequences as input can effectively predict conflict risk for VQs. It is more desirable to establish prediction models for turning and straight-going VQs separately.

title: Designing Active Assistance for Gait with Hip Abductors Weakness Using Predictive Simulation_supp1-3674842.pdf
authors: Elena Gutierrez-Farewik

title: A Novel Evaluation Metric for Object Detection in Traffic Scenarios
authors: Longjun Huang, Xueying Cai, Sheng Feng, Huxiong Li, Varshosaz Masood, Senang Ying, Binchao Zhou, Wentao Jia, Heng Liu, Lu Zhang, Yucheng Feng
abstract: Accurate decision-making in autonomous driving relies on reliable object detection, particularly for small and edge-located targets such as roadside traffic signs, vehicles, and pedestrians. Although Average Precision (AP) is widely used to evaluate object detection performance, it overlooks object position, scale, and confidence distribution, providing only an overall measure of precision and recall. As a result, AP fails to adequately reflect detector performance in real-world traffic scenarios, especially for small-scale and edge objects. In this paper, we proposed a improved metric called Position-Scale Weighted Average Precision (PSAP). Specifically, position and scale factors are incorporated into the definitions of true positives and false positives, and the conventional precision–recall (PR) curve is extended to a three-dimensional formulation. This design enables a more fine-grained assessment of detection precision with respect to object location and scale. We evaluate PSAP on the PASCAL VOC dataset and a custom-labeled traffic dataset across several state-of-the-art object detectors. The experimental results indicate that RetinaNet exhibits comparatively weaker small object detection capability, leading to the largest discrepancy between AP (0.77) and PSAP (0.63). In contrast, YOLO-based detectors demonstrate a smaller gap between AP and PSAP. Furthermore, an independent comparative analysis of YOLO variants is conducted on the custom dataset, and statistical validation is performed to demonstrate the effectiveness of PSAP in providing additional insights into small and edge object detection quality.

title: IMUDistill: Knowledge Transfer for Enhancing Low Precision IMU Performance_supp1-3674623.pdf
authors: SUMIT MISHRA
abstract: Inertial measurement unit (IMU) is a critical component for autonomous robot navigation. This paper presents a novel training framework that uses knowledge distillation to enhance low end, or low precision, IMU performance by transferring learned representations from high end IMU. In the proposed framework consisting of dual branch, teacher branch trained with high end IMU sensor data is used to train the student branch with low end IMU. For training the low end IMU branch, combined loss function that balances signal matching and knowledge transfer is used. Testing with the MAGF-ID dataset demonstrates substantial reduction of mean absolute error as much as 39-65% with accelerometer measurements and 47-70% for gyroscope readings with best improvement in the (gyroscope) X-axis, as compared to original low end IMU measurements. Qualitative analysis also reveals that proposed framework is efficient in systematic error correction and noise suppression.

title: Detection of MITM ARP Spoofing Attack: A Machine Learning-Based Framework
authors: Amna Akram, Yusra Nadeem, Muhammad Zeeshan, Muzamil Khan, Rashid Ahmad, Jabar H. Yousif
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Man-in-The-Middle (MITM) is one of the most common attacks in today's world. Its a form of cyberattack where an attacker positions himself between two entities making it show up as a normal exchange of information is taking place. The information obtained through this attack could be used for several purposes, including financial fraud, unauthorized transactions, and data leaks. To detect MITM our proposed solution includes dataset Preprocessing which includes feature selection, dealing with imbalance dataset and missing values, implementation of XGBoost machine learning model and LSTM based deep learning on Kitsune Network Attack Dataset for detection of ARP spoofing, employment of a hybrid approach which integrates the capabilities of both models, aiming to improve accuracy and reliability in ARP spoofing detection. Remarkable results are achieved: XGBoost has achieved an accuracy of 98%, while LSTM has achieved 97%. The hybrid approach maintains the best performance with an accuracy of 99% ensuring no overfitting by cross validation.

title: A Framework for Transparent Defect Detection Using Explainable AI: A Case Study in Agricultural Quality Control
authors: Pinphong Ruangraweenukit, Kritsanapon Worrasuwatthanakul, Suriyen Kongtip, Ponlawat Chophuk
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Deep learning models for quality control are effective but suffer from inherent opacity (“Black Boxes”) and imprecise spatial outputs (“Naive Action”), which limit their trustworthiness in robotic automation. We propose a novel framework to create a more accurate, transparent, and actionable system. Using YOLOv8 and Explainable AI (XAI) techniques, we extract a rich, tabular feature set from 2,000 images of apple defects. A hybrid feature selection process, utilizing SHAP, identifies the most critical features to serve as the state space for a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) Reinforcement Learning agent. We compare this agent's performance against standard object detection baselines. Our results show the RL agent is the superior classifier, achieving 97.33% accuracy, surpassing the highest baseline (YOLOv11) accuracy of 96.00%. This work presents a pipeline that successfully pairs XAI-derived features with an RL agent to create a high-performance, interpretable decision-making system.

title: Optimization of Multi-Dimensional Environmental Monitoring and Safety Assurance Scheme for Outdoor Sports Events Based on Internet of Things (IoT)
authors: Yinghui Zhang, Yunfei Zhao, Yejin Wu
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Delay Minimization for Movable Antennas-Enabled Anti-Jamming Communications With Mobile Edge Computing
authors: Yue Xiu, Yang Zhao, Kaihe Wang, Minrui Xu, Dusit Niyato, Guangyi Liu, Ning Wei
abstract: In future 6G networks, mobile edge computing (MEC) is envisioned to offer integrated computing, communication, and storage services at the network edge, enhancing both computational efficiency and communication quality. However, most existing MEC designs neglect the impact of jamming attacks, especially those from intelligent and adaptive jammers. To fill this important research gap, this paper investigates a jamming-resilient MEC framework that aims to improve communication reliability and reduce system delay under adversarial interference. Leveraging the emerging movable antenna (MA) technology, which allows dynamic adjustment of antenna positions and orientations, we propose a novel MA-assisted anti-jamming MEC architecture. Unlike existing works, our model explicitly considers the movement delay caused by MA, which is critical for practical deployment. We jointly optimize the MA positions at both the user equipment (UE) and the base station (BS), BS transmit beamforming, and task offloading ratios to minimize the total system delay. The resulting optimization problem is non-convex and highly coupled. Thus, we develop an efficient algorithm based on penalty dual decomposition (PDD) and successive convex approximation (SCA). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms traditional fixed-position antenna (FPA) baselines in terms of jamming resilience and delay minimization, offering new insights into robust MEC system design for 6G networks.

title: CADGL: Context-aware Deep Graph Learning for Predicting Drug-Drug Interactions
authors: Azmine Toushik Wasi, Taki Hasan Rafi, Raima Islam, Šerbetar Karlo, Dong-Kyu Chae
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Examining Drug-Drug Interactions (DDIs) is a pivotal element in the process of drug development. DDIs occur when one drug's properties are affected by the inclusion of other drugs. Detecting favorable DDIs has the potential to pave the way for creating and advancing innovative medications applicable in practical settings. However, existing DDI prediction models continue to face challenges related to generalization in extreme cases, robust feature extraction, and real-life application possibilities. We aim to address these challenges by leveraging the effectiveness of context-aware deep graph learning by introducing a novel framework named CADGL. Based on a customized variational graph autoencoder (VGAE), we capture critical structural and physio-chemical information using two context pre-processors for feature extraction from two different perspectives- local neighborhood and molecular context, in a heterogeneous graphical structure. Our customized VGAE consists of a graph encoder, a latent information encoder, and an MLP decoder. CADGL surpasses other state-of-the-art DDI prediction models, excelling in predicting clinically valuable novel DDIs, supported by rigorous case studies. Our code is available at: https://github.com/azminewasi/CADGL

title: Instasign:A Traffic Sign Recognition System
authors: Vijaya Kumar Gurrala, Srinivas Talasila, D Sai Jeevan Srikar, C Ramamurthy, T Sai Prithvi, Ch Sri Karthik
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Traffic rule violations are a serious issue for society, resulting in serious consequences like road accidents, deaths, physical disabilities, and mental disorders. Improving road safety by adopting sophisticated technological solutions has become the need of the hour to reduce these risks. One of the promising methods is the computer vision-based traffic sign recognition, which are very important in directing and regulating road users in a way to avoid accidents effectively. The work focuses on real-time traffic sign detection with a hardware-based approach combined with Image processing algorithms. With image processing techniques like edge detection, color analysis, and machine learning-based classification, the system recognizes and classifies different traffic signs like speed limits, warning signs, and stop signs. Further, the system is designed to operate effectively under different environmental and light conditions, i.e., rain, fog, or low lighting. Overall, the use of an automatic traffic sign detection system offers a state-of-the-art solution for reducing traffic rule violations and improving road safety.

title: Real-Time Detection and Explainable Analysis of Unsafe URLs Using Catboost and Random Forest Ensembles
authors: Uddipan Ghosh, Pratik Saha, Siddhartha Roy, Sangita Dutta, Arijeet Ghosh, Ankan Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: While web-based services have expanded at break-neck speed, attacks on the cyber space, most importantly malicious URLs, have also evolved. Traditional detection mechanisms that rely on blacklists or signatures have a tendency to miss newly created or highly obfuscated phishing URLs. To overcome these vulnerabilities, this work presents an explainable hybrid ensemble model that combines the strengths of CatBoost and Random Forest classifiers to accurately and explainably detect malicious URLs. A varied collection of manually crafted features-e.g., suspicious words, character patterns, domain pattern, and uncommon TLDs-were derived from a synthetically balanced dataset meant to simulate malicious and benign URL patterns in the real world. In pursuit of more transparency and user trust-worthiness, we blended two AI explanation approaches: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) is used for global and local interpretability and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations) is used for local high-fine-grained explanations. The model functioned very well with 96.4% accuracy, high F1-score, and AUC of 0.99, demonstrating its effectiveness. While the use of synthetic data allowed experimentation under controlled conditions and balancing classes, real-world valids on PhishTank or URLhaus-style datasets will be required to determine more generalizability. To address this limitation, future work will include evaluation on real-world malicious URL datasets collected over time to strengthen the model's real-time applicability. It is appropriate for browser plugins, firewalls, and email filters because of the unique blend of precision, interpretability, and deployment fairness that its architecture offers.

title: Txtail: A Practical Transaction Relay Incentive Scheme for Bitcoin
authors: Xiaopeng Dai, Qianhong Wu, Mingming Wang, Bo Qin, Fuyang Deng, Mingzhe Zhai, Robert H. Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Transaction propagation delay limits the block interval and is one of the main bottlenecks in improving Bitcoin throughput. However, transaction relay in Bitcoin is entirely voluntary, which results in low bandwidth and high transaction propagation delay. Improving relay motivation by introducing incentives can effectively reduce delay, but it still faces challenges such as Sybil attacks during reward allocation, leakage of network layer privacy, and high on-chain/off-chain overhead. Therefore, this paper proposes Txtail, a practical transaction relay incentive scheme for Bitcoin, based on continuously attaching relay evidence representing the relays’ identity and contribution during transaction propagation. We employ a free pricing mechanism based on the game between relays to allocate rewards fairly. We design an order-insensitive relay evidence structure based on aggregate signatures and public key mapping, which reduces off-chain data overhead while alleviating the leakage of relay paths by obfuscating the relay order. We construct a verifiable lottery mechanism based on Merkle tree commitments to reduce the data that needs to be uploaded to the chain. Both theoretical and experimental results show that Txtail reduces the per-hop off-chain overhead and the overall on-chain overhead by 96.6% and 79.8%, respectively, compared with state-of-the-art baselines, while remaining practical for deployment.

title: Federated Single-positive Multi-label Learning
authors: Meng Shen, Mang Ye, Andy J. Ma, Pong C. Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Single-positive multi-label learning (SPMLL) aims to train a multi-label classifier from data with single-positive label, to predict all applicable labels during testing. However, existing SPMLL methods are tailored for centralized datasets, which fail to be directly deployed to distributed setting like federated learning. In this paper, we start the first attempt to study federated single-positive multi-label learning (FedSPMLL), aiming to collaboratively train a SPMLL model from distributed data. To achieve this, we need to address challenges caused by label incompleteness: limited generalization ability of local model and overweighting contribution of client with local dataset suffering from severe label incompleteness. To this end, we propose a novel FedLOG method, guiding FedSPMLL with predicate LOGic-modeled label correlation. Enabling the informative knowledge extraction from limited data, we propose to model label correlation within local dataset using predicate logic. To alleviate false negative label issue, we propose to transfer confident label correlation knowledge to local model by self-distillation. To downweight the contribution of unreliable client owning dataset with severe label incompleteness, we propose a new measurement of label incompleteness to adjust client contribution for a fair aggregation. We establish a comprehensive FedSPMLL benchmark. And extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of our FedLOG method.

title: Design of a High Performance Energy -Efficient 4:2 Compressor Using Modified XOR-XNOR Logic
authors: Kanti Prajapati, Kavita Khare, Shreya Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The increasing need for high speed and low power digital systems demands the development of optimized arithmetic circuits. This paper proposes two efficient 4:2 compressor designs by using modified XOR-XNOR logics, resulting reduced logic depth, propagation delay, power and area. The Cadence Virtuoso tool employs 45 nm gpdk CMOS Technology with a 1 V power supply to implement and simulate the designs. Based on simulation results, compared to existing designs, the proposed 4:2 compressor design 1 achieves up to 79.14% reduction in power dissipation, 99.19% reduction in delay and 9% reduction in area, while design 2 achieves 84.97% reduction in power dissipation, 99.83% reduction in delay and 16.28% reduction in area. These results indicate the proposed two designs are suitable for low power, high performance, reduced area VLSI systems, particularly in modern multiplier and signal processing applications.

title: Wireless Power Transfer Technique for Electric Vehicle Battery Charging
authors: Vaibhav, Prashant Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The high pace of electric vehicles (EVs) penetration in the global market has left a considerable need in the provision of efficient, safe, and user-friendly charging infrastructure. The traditional plug-in charging systems have drawbacks of wear and tear on connectors, insecurity and decreased convenience. Contactless energy transfer, automation, and the possible integration with dynamic charging systems have become promising as a possible alternative of Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technology. The current paper will provide an extensive discussion of WPT applications in EV battery charging, that is, inductive and resonant coupling, coil topology, compensation networks, control, and performance optimization. Additionally, it addresses the problems of coil misalignment, loss of efficiency, electromagnetic interaction as well as thermal considerations. The article identifies the trends and practical solutions in recent years as well as gaps in research, and it serves as a source of information to researchers and engineers interested in developing the acceptance of wireless charging of EVs.

title: A Data-Driven Geospatial Platform for Monitoring State-Level Electricity Generation and Carbon Dioxide Emissions in India
authors: R Kiran, Nithya Anvitha Thumma, P Kiran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This article details a practical method to predict dayahead electricity generation in each Indian state along with the provision of visualisation and model explanation that can be deployed. The key novelty is a comprehensive open-source framework that combines monthly generation data from Ember with plant capacities that have been geolocated from the WRI Global Power Plant Database. The real-time presentation and interpretability are the results of a Flask-based web app that links Folium maps and SHAP-based model insights. The forecasting center is XGBoost, a regressor that takes into account lagged and rolling features, calendar effects, and structural-state capacity indicators. The model was trained and tested sequentially with an $\mathbf{8 0} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{2 0}$ split giving it an out-of-sample RMSE of $\mathbf{4, 2 5 8}$ MWh and an MAE of $\mathbf{2, 4 8 2 ~ M W h}$, it is more powerful than a persistence-based (lag-1) baseline that records an RMSE of 9,106 MWh-thus, the model allows an improvement of around $53 \%$ of the RMSE. The platform thus disseminates the information from states to plants using capacity shares to estimate plant-level hourly carbon dioxide emissions and aggregate intensity. According to the SHAP study, short-term lags, weekly rolling means, and the installed capacity appear to be the main contributors. The issues with the work are as follows: (i) daily values come from the uniform disaggregation of monthly totals with a sensitivity characterization, and (ii) capacity-weighted emission estimates are still first-order without consideration of plant-specific availability and dispatch. To sum up, the concept brings the disaggregation and capacity-weighting processes to the foreground, proves feasible and interpretable geospatial forecasts that are deployment-ready, and sketches the routes to telemetry of higher frequencies and better emissions accounting.

title: Classification of Alzheimer’s Disease
authors: M. Satya Vijaya, K. Harshitha, Ch.V. L. Satwika, V. Santhirmai, M. Prabhu Jyothi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease(AD) is aprogressive neurological condition marked by the gradual decline incognitive function and memory impairment. This paper investigates the utilization of Advanced neural network methodologies, especially Convolutional Neural Networks and Recurrent Neural Networks are few architectures to classify Alzheimer’s disease can be studied through neuroimaging techniques like MRI and PET scans. The study leverages on the ADNI research initiative, focused on studying Alzheimer’s disease through neuroimaging dataset and applies pre-processing techniques, including standardization and attribute extraction, to improve precision of classification. Findings show that deep learning models exceed conventional approaches in differentiating between Alzheimer’s disease, Mild cognitive impairment refers to a state involving noticeable but not severe declines in cognitive abilities, such as memory and thinking skills and cognitively healthy individuals. These findings emphasize the promise the utilization of advanced neural network methodologies in diagnosing the disease and tracking the progression of patients.

title: A Task Offloading Strategy for Cloud-Edge Collaborative Internet of Vehicles Based on IMS-MOEA/D
authors: Jingtao Li, Chunhua Zhu, Chong Liu, Jinlong Chen, Hai Zhu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: In the cloud-edge collaborative Internet of Vehicles (IoV) environment, different tasks exhibit heterogeneous latency requirements. Neglecting this heterogeneity can significantly affect the timeliness and reliability of task offloading. To this end, this paper models a realistic road environment for IoV using probability distribution approaches and classifies on-board tasks according to their characteristics. Corresponding bandwidth and computing resources are allocated to different task types, resulting in a multi-vehicle task offloading model with latency and energy consumption as the optimization objectives. To solve the task offloading problem, we design a multi-strategy improved multi-objective optimization algorithm based on decomposition (IMS-MOEA/D). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed IMS-MOEA/D method yields offloading strategies that reduce task completion time and energy consumption across various scenarios.

title: Real-Time Air Quality Monitoring Device Using LoRaWAN Technology
authors: Surapol Vorapatratorn, Nontawat Thongsibsong
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Air pollution, particularly exposure to fine particulate matter (PM2.5), remains a major threat to public health and the environment. Conventional monitoring stations, although accurate, are expensive to deploy and maintain, resulting in limited spatial coverage. This paper introduces MCare, an Internet of Things (IoT)-based air quality monitoring system that integrates low-cost particulate and meteorological sensors with LoRa and Wi-Fi communication for real-time data acquisition. The device measures PM1.0, PM2.5, PM10, temperature, relative humidity, and atmospheric pressure, and is connected to web and mobile applications developed on the Yakkaw platform, which provide intuitive geospatial mapping and access to historical trends. A field deployment in Chiang Rai, Thailand, demonstrated that M-Care achieved a communication range of up to 200 m with a packet delivery rate of 97 % under favorable conditions. Comparative analysis against reference-grade data from the Thai Pollution Control Department, OpenWeatherMap, and Dustboy sensors showed a lowest mean absolute error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.10 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>. Statistical evaluations confirmed no significant difference from official data, indicating that M-Care is a reliable, scalable, and costeffective solution for real-time air quality monitoring in diverse settings.

title: UAV Assisted Precision Afforestation Technique Using Innovative Seed Capsule
authors: Md Mustafa Abdullha, Atalanta Pal, Asmita Das, Archy Dey, Subhabrata Banerjee
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Still today, a vast portion of agricultural land is hard to be accessed due to difficult terrain and terrible climate. This makes ground-based seeding machines to be functionally unusable in such conditions. This grave problem can be mitigated using an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) attached with an airborne seeding device and the UAV flight speed is regulated to accurately control the seeding depth and seed space. The seeds are generally dropped from the UAV using a seed capsule which has to be designed in an aerodynamic and bio-degradable way so that the seed capsules quickly decompose and the seeds can grow at desired planting locations. The UAV seeding device has a low missing seed index and a qualified seeding index which typically meets all the requirements for afforestation and provides a new technical means to manage crop plantation and precision afforestation.

title: A Novel Lower-Device Count 9-Level Asymmetrical MLI for PV Application
authors: Anchal Raghuwanshi, Sakshee Chouhan, Amit Ojha, Nirbhay Tiwari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The rising electrical energy demand is being fulfilled by the energy obtained from natural sources like hydro power, wind energy, and Photovoltaic (PV) power. The recent trends reveal that PV systems are the most preferred among all the renewable energy sources. In this work, a 9 L asymmetrical multi-level inverter (MLI) topology with fewer-power semiconductor devices is presented. The proposed inverter utilizes nine power IGBTs with two DC power supplies. It aids in lowering switching devices, minimizing gate driver circuits, decreasing power losses, and eventually lowering the total system cost and complexity. This paper examines the operational advantages, technological considerations, and problems related to a solar PV inverter. The proposed inverter is modeled using MATLAB/Simulink across modulation indices and diverse loading conditions. The proposed topology has been successfully implemented in real real-time simulator with the Opal-RT controller.

title: Accuracy Analysis and Application Research of Beidou /GNSS PPP-AR in Chongqing Area
authors: Bin Wang, Xingwei Liu, Na Lin, Hua Cai, Shulin Zeng, Jingze Li, Nanjie Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) technology, Precis e Point Positioning (PPP) has become a major positioning method. However, the performance of PPP is significantly limited in complex geographical and meteorological conditions, especially in complex mountainous environments, where atmospheric delay has a significant impact on positioning accuracy. In this paper, Uncalibrated Phase Delay (UPD) was estimated at CORS stations in the Chongqing region and global MGEX stations and validates PPP-AR positioning performance with UPD estimates from different regions using two verification stations. The study shows that by estimating UPD in small areas, some atmospheric errors can be effectively absorbed, thereby improving positioning accuracy. This study provides important experimental data support for the application of PPP-AR technology in Chongqing Mountain City and verifies the positioning performance of multisystem and Beidou-alone system.

title: Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: The 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST 2025) was held on July 24th – 25th, 2025 in Da Nang, Vietnam. The conference is a collaborative initiative between Hanoi University of Science and Technology (HUST), the University of Connecticut (USA), the University of Da Nang (Vietnam), and AVSE Global, with strong support from our sponsors and partners. This year’s conference continues the mission of fostering collaboration and advancing research in the critical and rapidly evolving field of health science and technology.

title: AI-Powered Edge AQI Forecasting System
authors: Lakshmi Boppana, Siddhartha Palleti, Ramanjaneyulu Guntipally
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Accurate Air Quality Index (AQI) prediction is critical for public health and urban sustainability. However, traditional forecasting methods often rely on ground-truth data at every time step, which is impractical in real-world deployments. This paper presents a novel five-layer Bi-directional LSTM (BiLSTM) model that performs recursive prediction-generating future AQI values using its own previous outputs. This selfreliant design improves long-term forecasting robustness without needing continuous access to live sensor data. The model is deployed on a Raspberry Pi 5, showcasing real-time inference capability on a low-power edge device. Experimental results show high accuracy with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value as 0.955 and low mean absolute percentage error as 3.48 %, making the system suitable for decentralized, scalable environment monitoring.

title: Logo Image Dataset (LID): A Dataset of Trademark Images with Vienna Codes and Bounding Boxes for Logo Detection
authors: Thananuch Leeratanachai, Suparerk Manitpornsut
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Logos are considered crucial for brands and businesses, and the development of logo recognition processes and brand identification processes is of particular interest, with the aim of leveraging artificial intelligence to enhance the efficiency of trademark management. Although recent deep learning-based object detection methods have demonstrated promising performance, their effectiveness is often limited by the lack of datasets that provide fine-grained spatial annotations. This paper presents the Logo Image Dataset (LID), a dataset of 7,463 real-world trademark images collected from publicly available sources commonly encountered in Thailand. Each image is manually annotated with object-level labels, bounding-box coordinates, and Vienna Classification codes describing figurative elements. Evaluated using a custom YOLO architecture, the model achieved a mAP at 0.5 IoU of 0.918 and a mAP across thresholds from 0.5 to 0.95 of 0.85. The results yielded a peak F1 score of 0.84 at a confidence threshold of 0.73. Analysis of confusion matrices indicates that residual errors mainly arise from visually similar geometric motifs and long-tailed classes. These results demonstrate that LID provides effective supervision for fine-grained logo detection and offers a practical foundation for automated trademark search and analysis systems.

title:
The Influence of Annealing Temperature on the Structural and Luminescent Properties of Er:Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Films Prepared by Atomic Layer Deposition
authors: Zijian Qu, Qingshu Wu, Xuejun Kang, Mingbin Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Photonics and Applied Technology (IPAT)
abstract: Er-doped dielectrics are important for integrated photonics, for instance, C-band waveguide amplifiers for communications. This work reports growth of erbium-doped alumina thin films on silicon wafers by atomic layer deposition (ALD), and evaluates the effect of annealing on their structure and luminescence characteristics. The process results in uniform erbium doping, leading to dense films with a thickness of 400 nm and non-uniformity below 10%, verified by SEM, owing to which. SEM images of the prepared films confirm that after annealing at 900 °C, the films have the best property, largest photoluminescence intensity at 1533 nm, and significant red upconversion luminescence at 652 nm. However, at higher annealing temperatures, the film structure and the luminescence intensity obviously deteriorate. This work not only confirms the effectiveness of ALD in preparing high-performance erbium-doped gain dielectrics, shows the favourable annealing process window for preparing high-quality erbium-doped-gain media and, more importantly, provides guidance for the future development of advanced on-chip photonic devices.

title: A Baseline Absolute Measurement Method for Optical Synthetic Aperture Telescope
authors: SiQi Gao, AiMin Jiang, BaoCun Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Photonics and Applied Technology (IPAT)
abstract: In order to search for and explore terrestrial planets outside the solar system and their habitability, Chinese scientists have proposed the Multiple-spacecraft Exoplanet Aperture sYnthetic INterferometer (MEAYIN) project. Based on the project, we have established a Dual-mode Variable-array Imaging Interferometer Testbed (DVIIT) in the laboratory for key technology development. In order to solve the problem that the baselines of optical synthetic aperture telescope cannot be directly measured or has low measurement accuracy, this paper proposes a high-precision absolute measurement method for baselines based on imaging. The baselines of sub-telescopes are determined by measuring their exit pupils, and then the linear scales are calibrated. Theoretical analysis was conducted on this method and the exit pupil positions of four sub-telescopes were measured at different baselines. The measurement results indicate that the repeatability of this method is better than 6 microns @Root Mean Square (RMS). This method can improve the sampling accuracy of optical synthetic aperture telescope arrays in the spatial frequency plane (UV coverage) and subsequent measurement efficiency. It is a successful application of machine vision and detection technology in the field of optical synthetic aperture, and also provides experience for the development of this technology in virtual optical axis measurement and astronomical instrument development.

title: Vision Guided Robot Arm for Intelligent Shoe Sole Gluing Process Based on Yolov7
authors: Minh-Huy Nguyen, Thi-Thoa Mac, Duc-Nguyen Tien, Giang-Nguyen Thi Tra, Xuan-Thuan Nguyen, Hong-Hai Hoang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: This research addresses the need to provide research to quickly and improve the performance of footwear products to meet a variety of products and materials. The system includes a UR5 robot, a 3D camera and a shoe sole applicator. The solution approach offers a unified 3D and YOLOv7 image processing method to process substrates of different shapes, sizes, and materials. For training, we use a dataset consisting of 250 labeled actionable shoe images, while the validation data contains 50 images. study used initial learning rate of 0.01 and the number of integers was set to 500 and latch size was set to 4. Ablation experiments were conducted to improve YOLOv7, using the model Pre-training to save time and costs. Object detection using the Yolov7 model on the shoe dataset achieved high accuracy (97.4The research helps enhance the digitalization of the shoe sole automatic glue application system, helping to optimize glue application, significantly improving productivity, product quality and safety in the footwear production process

title: Time Domain Characteristics of Huygens Dipole Antennas Excited with Zero-Area Pulses: Near-field, midrange, and far-field explorations._supp1-3668247.pdf
authors: Richard Ziolkowski
abstract: The fields radiated by pulse-driven electric dipoles (EDs) and magnetic dipoles (MDs) and their balanced combinations, i.e., the ensuing Huygens dipole antennas (HDAs), are derived and examined in the time domain. Their characteristics are explored in the near-field, midrange, and far-field regions of these pulse-driven dipole-based sources and contrasted with their frequency domain equivalents. Engineering issues associated with their time derivative and time integral aspects are addressed along with the time domain differences between ideal MDs and practical ones realized with small current loops. Related physics issues concerning the duality of EDs and MDs in the time domain are also clarified. The excitations of HDAs with zero-area pulses (ZAPs) are emphasized. The consequent power and energy patterns emitted by these systems have intriguing consequences for applications. While an HDA’s power density pattern arises from unidirectional midrange and far-field contributions, it also contains a static component associated with its ED that is omnidirectional in the plane orthogonal to it. In contrast, the corresponding energy density patterns of ZAP-excited HDAs have no static component at any observation point and are strictly unidirectional. Consequently, it is revealed that a ZAP-excited HDA system is the most effective in delivering information (i.e., energy) not only in its far-field but also in its near-field and midrange regions.

title: Intelligent Detection of Incipient Faults in SPWM Inverters: A Hyperbolastic-Weighted Robust Sparse Coding Approach
authors: Jayanta Digar, Saibal Ghosh, Debashis Chatterjee, Amitava Chatterjee
abstract: Early detection of incipient faults in inverter-fed induction motor drives is essential for ensuring system reliability in industrial environments. Sparse representation techniques have shown effectiveness in fault diagnosis due to their ability to extract informative signal structures. Notably, robust sparse coding (RSC) introduces sample-specific weighting to suppress noise and outliers during signal reconstruction. However, RSC employs sigmoid-based weighting functions with fixed curvature, which restrict their adaptability to minor residual variations. To address this limitation, a hyperbolastic-weighted robust sparse coding (HW-RSC) model is proposed, incorporating a nonlinear and tunable hyperbolastic weighting function that dynamically adjusts sensitivity to residual errors. This adaptive weighting mechanism enhances the model’s ability to suppress outliers and separate fault-induced distortions from noise in low-SNR environments. Three-phase current signals collected under multiple fault severity levels are analyzed using a hybrid feature set comprising power spectral density (PSD), short-time Fourier transform (STFT), and statistical descriptors. Experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed HW-RSC model achieves 98.89% classification accuracy under nominal conditions and maintains over 76% accuracy at the lowest SNR considered, outperforming state-of-the-art sparse coding approaches.

title: RepkHunter: Obfuscation-Resilient Detection for Repackaged Mobile Applications
authors: Lewei Jin, Kui Zhang, Yi Gao, Wei Dong
abstract: Popular apps in the mainstream app markets are installed on millions of devices, attracting malicious actors to steal their income or distributing malware with their repackaged versions. To evade detection, their creators often obfuscate these apps and posing challenge to an effective repackaging detection. Many approaches have been proposed to address the issue. However, their resilience to obfuscation is limited and applies only to a narrow range of techniques. What is worse, a more advanced obfuscation technique have emerged, referred to as call manipulation. These techniques disrupt the original call structure by introducing new invocations or concealing existing one, significantly undermining the effectiveness of all the existing repackaging detection tools. In this paper, we present RepkHunter, a repackaging detection tool that is obfuscation-resilient against a wide range of obfuscation techniques, including the call manipulation. Specifically, we employ inter-class method invoking to construct the inter-class method invoking graph as the robust feature of an app, ensuring that it is: (i) independent of identifiers and package structures, and (ii) unaffected by instruction-level changes, such as randomized control flow and removed or inserted instructions. Then, we propose context-based call filtering. Specifically, we classify the observed call manipulation methods into two categories and propose two corresponding contexts to differentiate these manipulated calls from original invocations. Leveraging these contexts, we filter out calls introduced by obfuscators and restore the original call structure. Our experiments demonstrate that: (i) RepkHunter is resilient to all obfuscation techniques employed by five widely used obfuscators, outperforming the state-of-art tools. (ii) RepkHunter is scalable to large-scale, real-world datasets and helps to to identify more repackaged apps in the wild. We deploy RepkHunter for six months and identify 205 previously unreported repackaged applications in Google Play.

title: Self-pulsing Dynamics and Suppression in a 1940 nm Narrow-Linewidth Tm-Doped Composite Cavity
authors: Shaohua Feng, Kun Peng, Heng Xiang, Xiaoxiao Leng, Juan Yu, Li Ni, Aoxiang Lin
abstract: Developing narrow-linewidth and stable 2.0 μm high-power laser sources is essential for mid-infrared applications. Here, we report the self-pulse dynamics and pulse suppression strategies in Tm-doped fiber within a multi-longitudinal-mode composite cavity seed source. The evolution of self-pulses in Tm-doped fiber under different configurations was experimentally investigated. The results demonstrate that the composite cavity significantly suppresses self-locked mode pulses on shorter time scales, improving time-domain stability by up 75% compared to a single cavity. Furthermore, based on the optimization of the composite cavity seed laser and system integration, a narrow-linewidth laser output has been achieved in the backward pumping MOPA amplifier structure. The maximum unsaturated output power reaches 432.1 W, with a slope efficiency of 58.9%, 3 dB spectral linewidth of 0.0833 nm, and a beam quality factor M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 1.60. Notably, the limitation of TMI and SBS effects was not observed at high power output levels. The amplification structure based on a multi-longitudinal-mode composite cavity paves the way for developing compact, high-power, narrow-linewidth Tm-doped fiber lasers for practical applications.

title: Escaping the Programmatic Panopticon: A New 4p for Governing Data Capitalism &#x2014; Evidence from a Three-Round Delphi_supp1-3674893.docx
authors: MUNISE SAGLAM

title: Synchronized Alignment and Intelligent Loading of autonomous underground haul truck: Multi-Sensor Fusion and Cooperative Control with an Improved Fuzzy PID Algorithm
authors: Yonghe Xu, Xiaoquan Li, Linxin Zhang, Jiajun Feng
abstract: To address insufficient automatic alignment accuracy, poor coordination efficiency, and high risks of over or under-loading in unmanned loading–haulage operations of underground mines, we develop a synchronized automatic alignment and intelligent loading system for an autonomous underground haul truck, which achieves synchronized automatic alignment and intelligent loading through multi-sensor perception and cooperative control with an improved fuzzy PID algorithm. The hardware adopts a collaborative multi-sensor design, and the software introduces an improved fuzzy PID algorithm that stages the control into rapid loading, gain reduction during approach, fine-tuning control, and steady-state holding, effectively suppressing overshoot and oscillation. Experimental results show that, after adopting a dynamic braking-distance compensation strategy, vehicle alignment errors are significantly reduced; the along-track and lateral deviations remain within the millimeter-level range, and the angular deviation is less than 2°. In dynamic loading tests, the loading error is within ±0.02 kg, demonstrating high stability and accuracy. This study achieves high-precision alignment and precise loading control of an autonomous underground haul truck in a scaled laboratory setting and verifies the system’s application potential for future unmanned intelligent haulage operations in mines.

title: An Efficient Parameter Identification Approach for 3-D Cauer Model of Multichip IGBT Module Based on Cooperative Co-Evolution Algorithm
authors: Hengzhi Liu, He-sheng Zhang, Yicong Tang
abstract: Accurate identification of 3-D Cauer model parameters is crucial for obtaining the junction temperature distribution of multichip IGBT modules and subsequently assessing their reliability. However, existing methods suffer from low computational efficiency and limited applicability. Thus, an efficient parameter identification method for 3-D Cauer model of multichip IGBT modules is proposed. The proposed method transforms the parameter identification problem into a large scale global optimization (LSGO) problem. By constructing the fitness function based on the temperature responses obtained from both the 3-D Cauer model and one-time finite element analysis, the method achieves high computational efficiency. For the first time, a cooperative co-evolution (CC)-based LSGO algorithm, with high universality for decision variables across diverse dimensions, is employed to identify 3-D Cauer model parameters. Within the algorithm, problem decomposition considers the inherent partitioned structure of the 3-D Cauer model for multichip IGBT modules, effectively preserving internal physical correlations and enhancing both convergence speed and solution quality. Additionally, the probabilistic contribution-based computational resource allocation strategy prioritizes high-contribution subproblems for resource efficiency, while enabling low-contribution ones to run and thus avoiding local optima. Finally, the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method were validated using experimental data from a 6500V/750A multichip IGBT module.
