authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: A Unified Theoretical and Deep Learning Framework for Temporal Link Prediction in Dynamic Networks
authors: Jo Cheriyan, Lekshmi S Nair, Pao Ann Hsiung
abstract: Temporal networks extend complex networks by explicitly incorporating the time dimension into the analysis of entities and their interactions. With the rapid growth of large-scale social and information networks, modeling time-evolving relationships has become increasingly important, particularly for temporal link prediction. Unlike static networks, temporal link prediction must account for evolving node interactions, making it a substantially more challenging problem. This article focuses on temporal link prediction and presents a unified probabilistic framework for analyzing how representative deep learning approaches—including snapshot-based, temporal attention-based, and continuous-time methods—model temporal interactions. Rather than treating these methods as independent solutions, the proposed framework clarifies the underlying modeling assumptions that connect discrete- and continuous-time representations, interaction intensity estimation, time-to-event prediction, and anomaly detection. Extensive experiments on real-world temporal networks demonstrate that modeling choices consistent with the unified modeling lead to more robust and interpretable performance across different temporal link prediction subproblems, particularly for interaction intensity estimation, time-to-event prediction, and anomaly detection. The proposed framework provides a principled basis for understanding and comparing temporal network representations and learning objectives in practice.

title: List of Reviewers 2025
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Annals of the History of Computing

title: Passing on Wisdom: LLM-driven Cascaded Knowledge Distillation for Sequential Recommendation
authors: Xiang Ao, Yiran Qiao, Ningtao Wang, Xing Fu, Yang Liu, Thapana Boonchoo, Weiqiang Wang, Qing He, Xueqi Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: A critical limitation of conventional sequential recommendation models (SRMs) is their reliance on observed user-item interaction sequences within a closed-world setting, which hinders their ability to generalize to unseen or infrequent items. Recently, Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown remarkable promise in recommendation systems due to their vast world knowledge and advanced reasoning capabilities. Current research has predominantly explored two approaches: using LLMs to directly generate recommendations and distilling knowledge from LLMs to enhance conventional SRMs. However, these approaches face two major challenges: (1) high inference costs, as they require LLM responses during inference, either for generating predictions or as supplementary input; (2) inadequate distillation of the reasoning process, as existing methods focus mainly on improving embeddings or aligning outputs, without fully integrating LLMs' inherent reasoning capabilities. To address these issues, we propose LCKD-SR, an <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</u>LM-driven <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</u>ascaded <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">K</u>nowledge <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</u>istillation framework for <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>equential <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>ecommendation. In this framework, an LLM, a Teacher SRM, and a Student SRM form a hierarchical distillation structure, enabling an LLM-free inference by using only the Student model. Beyond traditional embedding and ranking distillation, our framework abstracts the LLM's sequential reasoning abilities by identifying key interactions that subsequently guide the Teacher's attention using learnable markers. The Student model, which mirrors the architecture of the Teacher, achieves seamless knowledge alignment from the Teacher across all three aspects. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed LCKD-SR, showcasing its scalability to perform multi-level knowledge transfer while enabling LLM-independent inference, thereby overcoming the inference cost and reasoning limitations of existing methods.

title: Data-Driven Predictive Control With Functional Safety Guarantees for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Carlos Conejo, Amr Alanwar, Vicenç Puig, Bernardo Morcego
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Navigation algorithms are responsible for decision-making in autonomous vehicles, generating safe trajectories based on post-processed sensor data to prevent collisions. However, failures in sensors or software modules can cause deviations from nominal behavior, potentially leading to unsafe situations. To comply with the ISO 26262 functional safety standard, road vehicles must perform a transition to a safe state when a failure is detected, until the corresponding safety goal is reached. This paper presents a novel control framework for navigation in autonomous vehicles, integrating data-driven reachability analysis and zonotopic predictive control to ensure functional safety (FuSa) under nominal conditions and potential system-level anomalies. The proposed framework explicitly accounts for interactions between vehicles, surrounding traffic participants, and infrastructure by incorporating traffic and infrastructure constraints into the control formulation. The proposed approach uses signal temporal logic specifications to formally define safe states and safety goals, guiding the zonotopic predictive control in executing safe maneuvers when they are required. The controller also enforces constraints on obstacle avoidance and infrastructure conditions, all represented as intervals or zonotopes for a unified formulation. The complete control framework is validated using data extracted from a real-world autonomous Renault Mégane (SAE Level 3), demonstrating its effectiveness in executing safe recovery maneuvers while ensuring compliance with the functional safety standard in real-world scenarios.

title: A Wideband Microwave Absorber with Wide-Angle Stability Based on Hybrid 2-D and 3-D Structures
authors: Ting-Ting Shang, Peng-Fei Gu, Meng-Meng Li, Nan Yang, Kwok Wa Leung, Da-Zhi Ding
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A microwave absorber based on hybrid 2-dimensional (2-D) and 3-D structures is proposed to achieve ultra-wide-angle absorption. Based on impedance matching conditions, the equivalent circuit model (ECM) of the absorber is established, and the surface impedance range required for effective absorption under arbitrary oblique incidence angles is derived. By comparing the simulated surface impedance values with theoretical predictions, it is confirmed that incorporating a 3-D structure enhances the capability of the absorber to achieve efficient absorption at extreme oblique incidence angles. A prototype of the absorber has been fabricated and tested for verification. The designed absorber achieves over 90% absorption for TE-polarized waves from normal incidence to 60° within 8-16GHz, and for TM-polarized waves from normal incidence to 70° across 8-17GHz. Notably, under oblique incidence, it maintains above 80% absorption efficiency over 6-18GHz, with angular stability extending to 70° for TE-polarization and 70° for TM-polarization.

title: Towards Eco-Efficient AI: Hybrid Data Strategies for BIPV Energy Prediction
authors: Youssef Jouane, Ilyass Abouelaziz, Imad Saddik, Mame Cheikh Sow
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: Accurate prediction of energy production from building-integrated photovoltaic (BIPV) systems is essential for optimizing building energy use and supporting decarbonization strategies. Synthetic data provides a valuable foundation for model development, particularly when real measurements are limited, but validation on operational systems remains critical for reliable deployment. In this study, we propose a hybrid data approach, combining a large BIMSolar-generated synthetic dataset with real production data from the Les Compagnons du Devoir et du Tour de France building in Strasbourg, France. Several machine learning models including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Decision Tree, and XGBoost were trained and evaluated. Results show that XGBoost consistently achieved the best predictive accuracy (MSE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2316.66, \mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> score of 0.98, MAE of 16.01, and MAPE of 0.18), while also being among the most energy-efficient models, with minimal training time and carbon footprint (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.06 \times 10^{-6}$</tex> gCO2eq). In contrast, deep learning approaches required significantly higher energy with lower efficiency. These findings highlight the advantages of hybrid data strategies, which leverage the diversity of synthetic datasets while grounding predictions in real-world observations. Beyond accuracy, the study also emphasizes the importance of energy-efficient AI for sustainable BIPV modeling. Limitations of the current work and future research directions are discussed, particularly regarding automated real-time data integration and the exploration of advanced ML techniques.

title: Optimization Accuracy of Sentiment Analysis for Twitter Data using LSTM Model
authors: Hemlata Pawar, Nitya Khare, Swati Khanve
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Social media sites like Twitter have developed into a wealth of information about public opinion in recent years, offering important insights into user trends, attitudes, and behaviors. Sentiment analysis, a subset of natural language processing, has emerged as a practical method for examining these expressions. However, because tweets are noisy, informal, and context-dependent, achieving high accuracy remains challenging. This work uses the LSTM model, a type of RNN that is excellent at identifying long-term dependencies in sequential data, to improve the accuracy of sentiment analysis on Twitter data. The system is trained on a pre-processed dataset of tweets containing neutral, negative, and positive sentiments. Advanced text preparation techniques like tokenization, stemming, stop-word removal, and word embeddings (Word2Vec/GloVe) are employed to improve input quality. Attention processes, dropout regularization, and hyperparameter manipulation are studied to enhance model performance. Experimental results show that in terms of accuracy and loss, the improved LSTM model outperforms both standard RNNs and traditional machine learning techniques. This paper demonstrates the potential of LSTM-based systems in real-time sentiment analysis and its applicability in fields such as marketing, politics, and public policy monitoring.

title: Sleep State-Dependent Eeg Spectral Alterations During Obstructive Sleep Apnea in Chronic Traumatic Brain Injury
authors: Tien Truong, Phat Kim Huynh, Risa Nakase-Richardson, Trung Quoc Le
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) is diagnosed in up to two-thirds of individuals with traumatic brain injury (TBI), yet how cortical responses to OSA vary between sleep states in this population remains poorly understood. We analyzed electroencephalogram (EEG) from polysomnography data of 220 chronic-TBI patients to investigate the spectral alterations of EEG signal between OSA and non-OSA episodes. Artifact-robust Welch analysis of the F3-A2 lead yielded power spectral density estimates, from which seven metrics were computed for each canonical frequency band across NREM and REM sleep states: relative power, spectral entropy, peak frequency, bandwidth, spectral edge frequency at 90% (SEF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">90</inf>), and power coefficient of variation (PowerCV). NREM OSA epochs demonstrated pronounced pathological spectral redistribution with delta power suppression (-2.8%, p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FDR</inf><0.001) and broad-spectrum higher frequency enhancement: theta (+8.2%), alpha (+8.1%), beta (+ 15.8%), and gamma (+19.2%) (all p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FDR</inf><0.05). Conversely, REM sleep showed markedly attenuated OSA-related alterations with only selective beta band elevation (+4.6%, p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FDR</inf>=0.020), indicating fundamental sleep state-dependent vulnerability patterns. Spectral complexity analysis revealed altered entropy organization and enhanced PowerCV stability during NREM versus preserved spectral architecture in REM, establishing that TBI creates sleep state-specific neurophysiological dysfunction during respiratory events. These sleep state-dependent spectral signatures reveal unique TBI-OSA pathophysiology and suggest that NREM sleep represents a period of heightened vulnerability while REM mechanisms provide partial cortical protection. This injury-specific spectral fingerprint, detectable with singlechannel EEG using non-parametric statistical analysis, may serve as a potential biomarker for screening TBI-OSA comorbidity and indicates that sleep state-targeted treatments may optimize outcomes for brain-injured populations.

title: MsMemoryGAN: A Multiscale Memory GAN for Palm-Vein Adversarial Purification
authors: Huafeng Qin, Yuming Fu, Huiyan Zhang, Mounim A. El-Yacoubi, Xinbo Gao, Qun Song, Jun Wang
abstract: Deep neural networks have recently achieved promising performance in the vein recognition task and have shown an increasing application trend. However, they are prone to adversarial attacks by adding imperceptible perturbations to the input, resulting in incorrect recognition. To address this issue, we propose a novel defense model named MsMemoryGAN, which aims to filter the perturbations from adversarial samples before recognition. First, we design a multiscale memory autoencoder (MsMemoryAE) to achieve high-quality reconstruction, where the memory module (MM) within it is capable of learning the detailed patterns of normal samples at different scales. Second, to overcome the limitations of handcrafted similarity metrics, we propose an MM with learnable similarity (LSMM), which retrieves the most relevant memory items to purify the input feature. Finally, the perceptual loss and adversarial loss are integrated with the pixel loss to further enhance the quality of the reconstructed image. During the training phase, the MsMemoryGAN learns to reconstruct the input by merely using fewer prototypical elements of the normal patterns recorded in the memory. At the testing stage, given an adversarial sample, the MsMemoryGAN retrieves its most relevant normal patterns in MMs for reconstruction. Perturbations in the adversarial sample are usually not reconstructed well, resulting in adversarial purification. We conduct extensive experiments on two public vein datasets under different adversarial attack methods to evaluate the performance of the proposed approach. The experimental results show that our approach removes a wide variety of adversarial perturbations, allowing vein classifiers to achieve the highest recognition accuracy.

title: A Multi-Temporal LULC Classification and Monitoring System
authors: Dipta Mukherjee, Biswajoy Chatterjee, Shivam Chauhan, Prithwiraj Das
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The proposed work describes a large-scale, interactive Google Earth Engine application for multi-temporal Land Use/Land Cover study. Furthermore, the study investigates the utilization of multi-sensor satellite imaging data from Landsat 5, Landsat 7, Landsat 8, and Sentinel-2 satellites to produce a consistent one-year composite from 1995 to 2025. To provide accurate results for LULC classification, sophisticated pre-processing, cloud mask layer operations, spectral index calculations (NDVI, EVI, NDBI, MNDWI, BSI, UI), and supervised machine learning classifiers such as Random Forest, SVM, and CART are utilized. The aim of the tool is to ensure that training models have a graphical interface through which accuracy checking, time series analysis, change detection, investigation of patterns, and graphics production are achieved. The tool may be applied to compute area statistics concerning class, explore temporal changes, analyze class transitions, and illustrate vegetation dynamics. Pixel-level exploration of classified raster layers, vector conversion, and production of time-lapse videos are further capabilities offered. In conclusion, the platform offered is a complete, scaleable, and easy-to-use solution designed for evaluation and sustained LULC change detection.

title: An ISAR Reconfigurable Shaping Method Utilizing Phase Modulation Metasurface
authors: Qin Pan, Hong Xu, Zhanye Chen, Haosheng Fu, Haocheng Guo, Naiwen Zhang, Heng Xu, Xiaoyang Zhou, Yinghui Quan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The coding scheme of the modulated phase is critical for ISAR jamming methods based on phase modulation meta-surface (PMM). Existing studies predominantly adopt periodic or random phase coding schemes, which yield relatively regular effects on ISAR imaging and are consequently susceptible to detection by radar systems. To this end, this paper proposes a feature-regulation method that relies on PMM to achieve ISAR reconfigurable shaping by designing modulation-phase encoding that reshapes the ISAR image of a PMM target into that of another target, thereby enabling customizable ISAR features for PMM and disrupting wideband radar recognition. Specifically, through theoretical derivation, the scattering point distribution of the shaped ISAR image is first characterized through the frequency spectrum distribution of the phase modulation sequence for PMM in the two-dimensional range–azimuth frequency domain. Subsequently, a weighted least-squares two-dimensional spectral approximation model under constant amplitude (CA) constraints is constructed to ensure that the frequency spectrum distribution of the modulation phase sequence closely matches the scattering point distribution of the shaped ISAR image, and this problem is effectively solved using a cyclic and gradient projection (GP) algorithm. Finally, simulations and experimental measurements are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: A Combined K-Nearest Neighbors and Gaussian Process Algorithm for Radio Map Construction and Sensor Deployment
authors: Pan Zhen, Kegang Pan, Bangning Zhang, Jingming Hu, Daoxing Guo
abstract: Radio maps are crucial for visualizing the distribution of electromagnetic signals, aiding in network planning and spectrum management. However, their long-term monitoring requires costly dedicated sensors. This paper proposes a novel joint optimization algorithm, KNNGP, which simultaneously constructs accurate radio maps and derives optimal sensor deployment schemes from crowdsourced data. Specifically, we model shadow fading as a Gaussian Process (GP) and leverage Variational Inference (VI) for efficient posterior approximation. A key innovation is the integration of K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) to impose a sparse prior that captures local spatial correlations, significantly reducing computational complexity. Furthermore, unlike prior works that treat mapping and de ployment separately, KNNGP co-optimizes both tasks within a unified variational framework. Simulations show that KNNGP outperforms baseline methods in reconstruction accuracy and efficiency, providing a cost-effective solution for continuous spectrum monitoring in underserved areas like urban fringes or jungles.

title: Benign Nonconvex Landscapes in Optimal and Robust Control, Part I: Global Optimality
authors: Yang Zheng, Chih-Fan Rich Pai, Yujie Tang
abstract: Direct policy search has achieved great empirical success in reinforcement learning. Many recent studies have revisited its theoretical foundation for continuous control, which reveals elegant nonconvex geometry in various benchmark problems. This paper considers two fundamental optimal and robust control problems with partial observability: Linear Quadratic Gaussian (LQG) control and $\mathcal {H}_\infty$ robust control. In the policy space, the former problem is smooth but nonconvex, while the latter one is nonsmooth and nonconvex. We highlight some interesting and surprising “discontinuity” of LQG and $\mathcal {H}_\infty$ cost functions around the boundary of their domains. Despite the lack of convexity (and possibly smoothness), we show that for a class of non-degenerate policies, all Clarke stationary points are globally optimal and there is no spurious local minimum for both LQG and $\mathcal {H}_\infty$ control. The main results are established by a new and unified framework of Extended Convex Lifting (ECL), which reconciles the gap between nonconvex policy optimization and convex reformulations. This ECL framework is of independent interest, and we discuss its details in Part II of this paper.

title: Eddyingization of chemical sensing material
authors: Luheng Wang
abstract: To endow the chemical sensor the additional capability to realize the eddy current measurement, the idea called “the eddyingization of the chemical sensing material” is proposed. The idea means that the chemical sensing material is designed into an element with coil-structured structure to make the current in the element form a vortex to endow the element with the ability to induce the eddy current effect. The eddyingization can achieve the aim that a single sensor cannot only measure the chemical quantity but also can detect the physical quantity based on the eddy current effect. The eddyingizations for the humidity sensor and the gas sensor are used as the examples to verify the effectiveness of the idea from the angles of the sensor properties, the sensing mechanisms and the measurement method. The research on the fusion between the eddy current effect and the chemical sensing effect caused by the eddyingization cannot only offer a chance to find new phenomena and new rules in exploring the chemical effect and the electromagnetic induction, but also can construct the novel sensor architecture and sensing strategy in the chemical measurement field.

title: A Lightweight Deep Learning Architecture for Accurate and Efficient Short-Term Load Forecasting
authors: Mohit Choubey, Rahul Kumar ChaurasiyaRahul, J.S. Yadav, Rahul Prasad, Aman Sharma, Aman Dubey
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Modern power systems and smart grids require accurate short-term load forecasting (STLF). This study offers TS-Mixer, a lightweight deep learning model inspired by MLP-Mixer, to capture nonlinear temporal correlations in load data with reduced computational cost. The model models relationships across time steps and contextual factors using temporal mixing and feature mixing while retaining training stability through residual connections. TS-Mixer surpasses benchmark models like LSTM, GRU, ARIMA, and SARIMA in real-world load data trials, with the lowest MAE (333.85 MW), RMSE (418.98 MW), and MAPE (5.45%). The model has smoother convergence, better generalization, and less overfitting than recurrent and statistical baselines. The simplicity, scalability, and prediction accuracy of TS-Mixer make it a realistic and high-performance option for smart grid short-term load forecasting.

title: Uncovering Cross-Omics Prognostic Signature in Prostate Cancer Using Gradient Boosting and SHAP Analysis
authors: Asmita Dey, Anushka Pandit, Arunava Mukhopadhyay
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Prostate cancer is a major contributor to cancer-related morbidity in men and continues to pose a growing clinical burden. With incidence rates increasing by nearly 3% every year since 2014, finding effective treatment alternatives is crucial. This work aims to improve detection time and prognostic assessment using multiple layers of biological data. Using the Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) Prostate Adenocarcinoma (PRAD) cohort, a comprehensive analytical pipeline is developed that integrates multi-omics and gradient boosting models. Additionally, AI explainability or SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) is encompassed to highlight relevant biomarkers for prostate cancer progression.

title: Reduction of Higher Order Harmonics in Rectangular Microstrip Antenna by Novel Cross Shaped Slots
authors: Srijita Chakraborty, Koushiki Ghosh, Srijan Bhattacharya, Shreya Kumari Sharma, Malay Gangopadhyay, Mrinmoy Chakraborty

title: A Cymbal Transducer as a Single Element Device for Enhanced Cavitation Generation
authors: Faraz Amini Boroujeni, Paul Prentice, Andrew Feeney
abstract: Cavitation driven by low-frequency ultrasound has been widely recognized as an effective method of transdermal drug delivery, but challenges remain regarding the size and efficiency of the sonicator. The cymbal transducer is a promising candidate for such applications; however, the conventional design is limited by its relatively low power output and difficulty to manufacture with consistency, limiting its success in arrays and for extended treatment times. This study introduces a modified cymbal transducer, operating at 66.2 kHz, designed to offer higher output pressure and easier fabrication process, while being compact (OD=36 mm, Thk=6 mm). The transducer was then characterised in terms of its vibration mode shape, acoustic pressure field, acoustic power, and cavitation performance captured through high-speed imaging and acoustic detection of bubble collapse shockwave emissions. Furthermore, the device was employed to permeate fluorescent nanoparticles (10 kDa) into agar-based phantom. The new design is capable of outputting up to 3.05 MPa peak-negative-pressure (PNP) at its focal region (MI=11.8), and spatial peak temporal average intensity (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SPTA</sub>) of 1.59 W/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, significantly surpassing other devices. High-speed shadowgraphic imaging reveals superior cavitation performance that has not been demonstrated in prior studies. The transducer is also capable of delivering nanoparticles at a rate of 1.19 mm/min into the phantom. As a result, the modified cymbal proved to be not only a robust sonicator with potential for sonophoresis but also viable as a compact power ultrasonics source.

title: On MDS Convertible Codes in the Merge Regime
authors: Vinayak Ramkumar, Xiangliang Kong, G. Yeswanth Sai, Myna Vajha, M. Nikhil Krishnan
abstract: In large-scale distributed storage systems, erasure coding is employed to ensure reliability against disk failures. Recent work by Kadekodi et al. demonstrates that adapting code parameters to varying disk failure rates can lead to significant storage savings without compromising reliability. Such adaptations, known as code conversions, motivate the design of convertible codes, which enable efficient transformations between codes of different parameters. In this work, we study the setting in which λ codewords of an initial [nI = kI + rI , kI] MDS code are merged into a single codeword of a final [nF = λkI + rF , kF = λkI] MDS code. We begin by presenting three constructions that achieve optimal access cost, defined as the total number of disks accessed during the conversion process. The first two constructions apply when λ ≤ rI and impose specific divisibility conditions on rI and the field size q. These schemes minimize both the per-symbol and the overall access cost. The third construction, which builds on a prior scheme by Kong, achieves minimal access cost while supporting arbitrary parameter regimes. All three constructions require field sizes that are linear in the final code length, and notably, the third construction achieves a field size that matches the lower bound implied by the MDS conjecture in almost all cases. In addition, we propose a construction that optimizes the bandwidth cost, defined as the total number of symbols transmitted during conversion. This scheme is a refinement of Maturana and Rashmi’s bandwidth-optimal construction based on the piggybacking framework, and achieves reduced sub-packetization.The code will be available at https://github.com/ZhilongNiu/SDMoMFE.

title: Evaluation of Mother Wavelets for ECG Artifacts Suppression in Diaphragmatic EMG Using Singlelevel Discrete Wavelet Transform
authors: Supanun Chitmeta, Sauwaluk Dacha, Weerayot Aramphianlert
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Diaphragmatic electromyography using surface electrodes (diEMG) is a valuable non-invasive technique for respiratory monitoring. However, diEMG signals are often contaminated by electrocardiogram (ECG) artifacts due to the anatomical proximity of the diaphragm to the heart, leading to distortion and difficulties in clinical interpretation. This study aims to identify the most suitable mother wavelets for ECG artifact removal from diaphragmatic EMG signals using a single-level discrete wavelet transform (DWT). Synthetic diaphragmatic EMG (SdiEMG) signals and real diEMG recording from four volunteers were processed using 32 mother wavelets. Signal quality was assessed using statistical parameters, including robust of kurtosis and skewness, and evaluated through a multi-criteria decisionmaking method based on TOPSIS scoring. The results show that Symlet9 (sym9) and Daubechies8 (db8) and achieved the highest TOPSIS score of 0.984 and 0.99, respectively, demonstrating superior performance in suppressing ECG components while preserving EMG characteristics. The processed diEMG signals are promising for monitoring respiratory behavior, as it shows a high correlation with actual breathing patterns.

title: Review of Machine Learning Techniques for Enhancing WDM-RoF System Capacity and Performance
authors: Manish Kumar Soni, Aditya Goel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Wavelength Division Multiplexing Radio-over-Fiber (WDM-RoF) systems represent a promising approach for meeting the growing demand for high-capacity wired and wireless services, including 5G and beyond. WDM-RoF systems have a lot of potential, but they also have many problems that restrict their capacity and performance. These problems include fiber nonlinearities, chromatic dispersion, inter-channel crosstalk, and wireless fading. Data-driven strategies, such as machine learning (ML), address these shortcomings, improve system parameters, and enhance capability. This paper examines contemporary machine learning techniques utilized in WDMRoF systems, encompassing neural networks, convolutional and recurrent architectures, and others, emphasizing their roles in alleviating nonlinearity, achieving signal equalization, forecasting performance, and optimizing resource allocation. We discuss the implementation challenges and research directions for high-capacity, real-time WDM-RoF networks.

title: TASTETALK: Multimodal NLP Framework of Contextually Aware and Emotionally Responsive Dining Recommendation
authors: Lingeshwar K. S, Mathan Kumar R, Tina Victoria A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In the modern-day hospitality industry, new digital menus and order-taking online interfaces that are based on keywords tend to place a cognitive burden on their observers and do not reflect the complex tastes of the restaurant. This paper introduces TASTETALK, a multimodal conversational recommendation system aimed at balancing unstructured user intention to explore menu items in a personalized way. It is proposed that the system combines Natural Language Processing (NLP) and modular architecture based on Django to implement intent classification based on textual and audio entries. TASTETALK samples semantic features like dietary restrictions, taste inclinations, types of cuisine and emotions to formulate context-sensitive suggestions. Compared to the old rule-based chatbots, the system has support of voice to text transcription, multilingual interaction, and contextual persistence of sequential queries. The accuracy of intent classification experimentally is 81.48, which confirms the usefulness of multimodal intent modeling to provide intelligent restaurant assistance. The findings suggest that consideration of emotional stimuli and discussion context is effective in improving the relevance of the recommendations and quality of user interaction.

title: An Adaptive Elite-Guided Co-Evolutionary Algorithm for Constrained Multiobjective Optimization
authors: Xiandong Shi, Hecheng Li
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: With the vigorous and continuous advancement of science and technology, constrained multi-objective optimization problems (CMOPs) in real-life practical scenarios have demonstrated an increasing degree of intricacy. Constraints tend to disperse the Pareto optimal solutions of CMOPs across multiple feasible regions, rendering algorithms susceptible to falling into local feasible domains and thus failing to explore the entire range of feasible regions. To resolve the aforementioned issues, this manuscript presents an adaptive elite-guided co-evolutionary algorithm. The main innovations are as follows: 1) An adaptive environmental selection strategy is designed, which adaptively selects high-quality potential solutions matching the evolutionary stage. 2) A distribution-based heuristic operator is proposed, which employs statistical information to steer the evolutionary search of the population, directs the evolutionary direction of the crossover operation via the mean vector, and regulates the search scope of mutation using the standard deviation. The proposed algorithm is compared with seven leading constrained multi-objective optimization algorithms, and experimental results confirm its significant superiority in tackling CMOPs.

title: Co-Simulation of FDI and DOS Attacks in IoT-Enabled Next-Generation Smart Grids: A Framework for Resilience
authors: Muhammad Yasir Masood, Helena Rifà-Pous, Muhammad Awais, Ayesha Khawar, Muhammad Muzaffar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Conventional, isolated security models fail to protect IoT-integrated Smart Grids from combined cyber-physical threats. Using a co-simulation testbed, this research proves that cyberattacks directly cause physical damage. In this paper, the need for holistic analysis with the help of a high-fidelity cosimulation testbed (NS-3, GridLAB-D, HELICS) is proved. We demonstrate that cyberattacks have a direct impact on causing critical physical failure; an example is a false data injection attack that leads to the severity of voltage and line loading violations. More importantly, we demonstrate a synergistic compound effect: a Denial-of-Service attack, which paralyzes situational awareness of more than 45 percent packet loss, proactively blocks the detection of the FDI-induced violations. This covering effect dramatically enhances the threat of domino failures- an amplifier of risks to which the current siloed models are incapable of capturing. Our effort offers an established avenue on which effective countermeasures to these joint threats can be built.

title: INS/GNSS/Magnetometer Integrated Navigation Algorithm Based on PSEKF
authors: Xudong Peng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This chapter focuses on the research of the INS/GNSS/Magnetometer integrated navigation system. We propose a Partial State Update Extended Kalman Filter (PSEKF) to freeze disturbed state variables during measurement updates, ensuring that other states are updated normally. A combined navigation model with 15-dimensional state variables was established and verified through simulation experiments. We can better detect whether the magnetometer is affected by interference through vector detection methods. The experiment verified the superior performance of PSEKF in a magnetic interference environment.

title: Occupancy-Based Energy Management System in Smart Meeting Rooms Using Digital Twin Technology
authors: Wathinee Kaewzaikird, Prapita Thanarak, Chakkrit Termritthikun
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study presents the design and development of a platform for monitoring energy consumption in meeting rooms through digital twin technology, facilitating the management and control of energy usage in buildings equipped with Internet of Things (IoT) systems. The research specifically targets the main meeting room of an office building, correlating electricity consumption with room usage schedules and occupancy levels, based on the building's operational days and hours. The proposed platform integrates digital twin technology with IoT and sensor systems to detect motion, count occupants, process and analyze data, and regulate lighting and air conditioning. The system visualizes statistical data through a cloud-based dashboard, providing accessible insights. Experimental results demonstrate that the platform effectively monitors energy consumption in meeting rooms equipped with temperature and lighting control, utilizing CCTV with passive infrared sensors and temperature measurement devices to track energy use according to occupancy. The recorded data is displayed via the digital twin interface and supports efficient planning for energy consumption management.

title: A Hybrid AI and Wearable-Based Clinical Decision Support System for Mid-Trimester Preeclampsia Risk Assessment
authors: D Gopika, G S Sucheta, M Dinesh Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Preeclampsia is a critical hypertensive disorder in pregnancy that requires early detection to prevent maternal and fetal complications. This work presents a hybrid clinical decision support system (CDSS) that integrates a wearable peripheral edema detection module with an AI-based risk classification algorithm for mid-trimester screening. The hardware prototype monitors ankle swelling and blood pressure, while retrospective clinical data and synthetic patient profiles inform the risk assessment. Three machine learning models, Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, and Gradient Boosting were evaluated, with the Gradient Boosting model achieving the highest accuracy. The proposed system demonstrates the feasibility of combining wearable technology and AI for timely preeclampsia risk assessment, particularly in resource-limited settings.

title: Research on 1553B Bus Data Parsing Technology Based on NFA
authors: Zhiji Zhu, Xiaoshuai Li, Haifeng Hou, Hui Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This article addresses the complexity of real-time parsing of 1553B bus data in aerospace telemetry systems by proposing a regular expression matching algorithm based on a nondeterministic finite automaton (NFA). By integrating multi-threaded parallel processing technology, an efficient method for bus data parsing is designed to tackle challenges such as complex data structures, high data volume, and stringent real-time requirements. Leveraging the flexibility of NFA and parallel computing capabilities, a regular expression matching model suitable for variable-length data and nested packet scenarios is constructed. Additionally, a data caching mechanism is introduced for parsing bus data. Experimental simulation results demonstrate that this method effectively enhances the efficiency and accuracy of bus data parsing, meeting the demands of real-time telemetry processing in aerospace missions. This study provides theoretical support and technical references for the development of generalized real-time telemetry parsing systems.

title: A Configurable Approximate CAM with Prefix Filtering and Segmented Match-Line Evaluation
authors: Nishita Biswal, Sangeeta Nakhate, D.K Raghuvanshi, Gaurav Tiwari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper presents an energy-efficient, variable approximate Content-Addressable Memory (CAM) architecture created in SystemVerilog. The domain is optimized using multi- level optimizations, which include prefix-based precharge filtering, segmented match-line evaluations with early termination, and row-level gating, to lower dynamic power consumption and allow for future scalability. Additionally, the proposed design uses a Hamming-distance based approximation approach to allow for flexible and controllable inexact search of associative memory structures for a near-memory computer, AI accelerators, and edge devices. The prefix filter only activates the rows of memory that are most likely to be matching to prevent switching in other rows, while the segmented match-lines accumulate the count of mismatching rows, and using early termination will decrease the switching events by halting the router access once the maximum count of mismatching rows is reached. In the present study, the total power consumed was 0.078 W with dynamic power of $0.010 \mathrm{~W}(13 \%)$, as well as an energy use of approximately $0.61 \mathrm{fJ} / \mathrm{bit} / \mathrm{search}$. The design was also nominal in resource usage, utilizing just 1.79% LUTs and 0.69% registers, validating the design is compact, scalable, and energy efficient for real-time associative searches.

title: CFD-Based Flow and Thermal Assessment of a Net-zero Energy Bus Stop in Kuwait
authors: Mohamad Hussein Farhat, Ahmad Sedaghat, Mohamad Salam, Mohammad Nazififard
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: This study employs Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) in SolidWorks to analyze the thermal and airflow performance of a retrofitted 20-foot shipping container bus stop in Kuwait, evaluating air distribution, temperature gradients, and humidity levels for passenger comfort optimization in extreme climates. Through particle flow tracking, iso-surface velocity mapping, and vertical mid-plane distributions of temperature and humidity, the CFD simulations reveal non-uniform airflow, thermal stratification, and localized humidity variations, identifying ventilation inefficiencies. These findings guide the development of an AI-based PMV (Predicted Mean Vote) control system for dynamic HVAC adjustments, demonstrating how CFDdriven design enhances energy-efficient retrofitting of public transport infrastructure in alignment with sustainable Kuwait Vision 2035. The integration of smart HVAC controls with CFDvalidated thermal models presents a replicable framework for climate-resilient transit shelters in hot arid regions.

title: Research on the Multi-Faceted Utilization of Tourism Data Using Generative AI
authors: Kyoko Kanzaki, Hitoshi Isahara
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study examines the analysis and utilization of tourism information using generative AI, focusing on tourism data published by public organizations. Using the English edition of tourism guidebooks as comparative data, the potential for feature extraction, information enrichment, guidance script generation, and destination selection support is investigated from four perspectives: (1) data providers, (2) local governments, (3) tour guides, and (4) tourists. The results show that the value of tourism information can be enhanced by applying generative AI to comparison, classification, guidance script generation, and evaluation. The originality of this study lies in proposing a framework for the use of generative AI as a “tourism support tool” under human judgment and verification, rather than providing AI-generated content directly to tourists.

title: Drivers’ Psycho-physiology Characteristics and Workload Identification in Prairie Highway work zones
authors: Zhen Lyu, Chen Guo, Xinyu Yang
abstract: As typical atypical driving scenarios, highway work zones heighten driving uncertainty, exacerbate perceptual and cognitive deviations, and elevate drivers’ emergency psychological workload—ultimately increasing the likelihood of operational errors and traffic crashes. These risks are further exacerbated by the unique characteristics inherent to prairie highways. To characterize the psychophysiological responses and quantify driver workload in such scenarios, multi-modal data were collected from 30 participants by a driving simulation experiment on prairie highway work zones, including eye movement metrics, electrocardiogram (ECG) indicators, and post-simulation survey data. Then, the psychophysiological characteristics analysis of these metrics was performed and five machine learning models (Extreme Gradient Boosting, XGBoost; Light Gradient Boosting Machine, LGBM; Support Vector Machine, SVM; Random Forest, RF; Deep Neural Network, DNN) were employed for workload level classification. The results reveal distinct workload-correlated patterns across selected indicators: For eye movement metrics, targeted analysis of different Areas of Interest (AOIs) revealed significant workload-related variations: fixation duration on critical AOIs lengthened, fixation counts increased to capture more environmental information, fixation shifts reflected visual attention allocation efficiency, and blink characteristics diverged significantly between low and high workload states; For ECG metrics, heart rate growth rate rose sharply in response to stimuli, while Root Mean Square of Successive Differences (RMSSD) declined markedly—these psychophysiological patterns collectively signify a pronounced elevation in driver workload as cognitive and operational demands intensify in high-pressure scenarios. Trained on these discriminative features, the five models were validated for workload identification, with XGBoost outperforming others and attaining a classification accuracy of 90.51%. ROC curve analysis further confirmed the superior performance of XGBoost, which achieved an AUC value of 0.942, followed by DNN (AUC=0.925) and LGBM (AUC=0.908), demonstrating strong model robustness and discriminative power across workload levels. These findings corroborate the efficacy of multi-modal data fusion for workload assessment in prairie highway work zones, offering actionable, data-driven insights for the development of targeted traffic safety management strategies.

title: Balancing Privacy and Precision: A Novel Lensless Approach to Driver Monitoring
authors: Jia-Lun Wu, Tzu-Yuan Huang, Yen-Chun Lin, Chung-Hao Tien
abstract: Conventional driver monitoring systems rely on lens-based RGB cameras to capture high-resolution facial images: a scenario that, while effective, raises significant privacy concerns. In this work, we proposed a privacy-preserving, hardware-level alternative: a novel lensless imaging system that replaces traditional optics with an amplitude-modulated mask. By producing inherently blurred, non-identifiable images, our system safeguards driver privacy while retaining the visual cues necessary for behavior recognition. A lightweight video recognition network (MoViNets) is employed to process lensless video sequences for real-time driver behavior classification. We construct a custom infrared (IR)-illuminated lensless dataset and evaluate the proposed system under realistic in-cabin conditions. Experimental results demonstrate an average classification accuracy of 95%, together with high sensitivity and specificity, validating the effectiveness of the proposed approach. Additional ablation studies on modulation masks and network architectures further highlight the robustness and efficiency of the system. This study is the first to apply lensless imaging to driver monitoring, paving the way for low-cost, compact, and privacy-first in-vehicle safety systems.

title: Wavelet Scattering Transform and Fourier Representation for Offline Detection of Malicious Clients in Federated Learning_supp1-3671698.pdf
authors: Alessandro Licciardi

title: Weird A.I.
authors: Benjamin Lindquist
publish: IEEE Annals of the History of Computing
abstract: This essay sketches out what I call “weird AI”: probabilistic, associative systems that behave as if they feel. It shows how midcentury architects of artificial neural networks deliberately courted the uncanny as they engineered space for intuition, emotion, and nonrational thought, even as standard histories cast computing as an Enlightenment project of calculation and control. To make sense of artificial intelligence’s past and present, historians must move beyond an information-centric framework and reckon with the affective undercurrents that have shaped the field from its start.

title: RESAR-BEV: An Explainable Progressive Residual Autoregressive Approach for Camera-Radar Fusion in BEV Segmentation
authors: Zhiwen Zeng, Yunfei Yin, Zheng Yuan, Argho Dey, Xianjian Bao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) semantic segmentation provides comprehensive environmental perception for autonomous driving, but suffers from multimodal misalignment and sensor noise. We propose RESAR-BEV, a progressive refinement framework that advances beyond single-step end-to-end approaches: 1) an inherently interpretable, coarse-to-fine refinement using a residual autoregressive learning paradigm implemented by our Drive-Transformer and Modifier-Transformer cascade, where each stage is responsible for a specific semantic scale (from road topology to lane boundaries); 2) robust BEV representation combining ground-proximity voxels with adaptive height offsets and dual-path voxel feature encoding (max+attention pooling) for efficient feature extraction; and 3) decoupled supervision with offline Ground Truth decomposition and online joint optimization, which prevent overfitting while ensuring structural coherence. Experiments on nuScenes demonstrate RESAR-BEV achieves state-of-the-art performance with 54.0% mIoU across 7 essential driving-scene categories while maintaining real-time capability at 14.6 FPS. The framework exhibits robustness in challenging scenarios of long-range perception and adverse weather conditions.

title: FedCGD: Collective Gradient Divergence Optimized Scheduling for Wireless Federated Learning
authors: Tan Chen, Jintao Yan, Yuxuan Sun, Sheng Zhou, Zhisheng Niu
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a promising paradigm for multiple devices to cooperatively train a model. When applied in wireless networks, two issues consistently affect the performance of FL, i.e., data heterogeneity of devices and limited bandwidth. Many papers have investigated device scheduling strategies considering the two issues. However, most of them recognize data heterogeneity as a property of individual devices. In this paper, we prove that the convergence speed of FL is affected by the sum of device-level and sample-level collective gradient divergence (CGD). Device-level CGD refers to the gradient divergence of the scheduled device group, instead of the sum of the individual device divergence. Sample-level CGD is statistically upper bounded by sampling variance, which is inversely proportional to the total number of samples scheduled for local update. To derive a tractable form of the device-level CGD, we further consider classification tasks and transform it into the weighted earth moving distance (WEMD) between the group distribution and the global distribution. Then we propose FedCGD algorithm to minimize the sum of sampling variance and WEMD on classification tasks by device scheduling and bandwidth allocation, within polynomial time. Simulation shows that the proposed strategy increases classification accuracy on the CIFAR-10 dataset by up to 4.2% while scheduling 41.8% fewer devices, and flexibly switches between reducing WEMD and reducing sampling variance.

title: Correlation between Surface Temperature at Neck Acupoints and Cervical Spine Range-of-Motion: A Study using Novel ROMIX System
authors: Nguyen Huu Duc Minh, Nguyen Huu Khanh, Tran Le Giang, Che Quang Cong, Le Phan The Vinh, Le Tan Kha, Ha Chi Loc, Duong Chan Viet
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Introduction: Previous research indicates that prolonged exposure to cold can lead to decreased skin temperature and restricted cervical spine range of motion (CROM) due to vasoconstriction and muscular stiffness. Objective: To investigate the correlation between CROM and skin temperature at key neck acupuncture points, namely Fengchi (GB20) and Fengfu (GV16). Methods: A cross-sectional study was conducted with 60 participants divided into coldexposed and normal groups. Their skin temperatures at GB20 and GV16 were measured using a FLIR C5 thermal camera, while CROM was evaluated in six directions (flexion, extension, left/right bending, and left/right rotation) through the ROMIX photogrammetry system after two sessions of active neck movements. Results: Significant differences in acupoint temperatures were observed between the groups, with lower temperatures in cold-exposed participants (Cohen's d between 1.27 and 3.44). Similarly, the cold-exposed group demonstrated significantly lower CROM in all directions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.05$</tex> with Cohen's d ranging from 0.76 to 2.56). Strong positive correlations (Spearman <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{r}=0.59$</tex> to 0.73) were noted between skin temperatures at GB20 and contralateral cervical rotation and bending, while the temperature at GV16 displayed a similar pattern with cervical flexion and extension. Conclusion: Cold exposure leads to reduced acupoint temperatures, which significantly correlate with decreased cervical mobility. This highlights the clinical relevance of thermal assessments at specific acupoints as objective indicators of cervical functional status.

title: MATLAB based Framework for Real-Time Human Face Detection
authors: B. Ramya Sree, M. Madhuri, Jayalakshmi Machiraju, C Ahalya, G. Susmitha, B. Poojitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This work suggests a MATLAB-based framework for real-time human face detection in order to provide a straightforward, effective, and economical method for computer vision tasks. Designing and implementing a system that can reliably identify faces in live video streams is the main goal. This will serve as a foundation for more complex applications like automated surveillance, face recognition, and interactive systems. The framework uses the Viola-Jones algorithm via the Cascade Object Detector, which uses Haar-like features for quick and accurate face localization, and makes use of MATLAB’s Image Processing Toolbox. In order to continuously capture and process video frames, a webcam interface is integrated, allowing for real-time detection with little delay. The system’s performance declines in situations of severe occlusion or extreme illumination variations, but experimental evaluation shows that it achieves high detection accuracy under various lighting conditions, face orientations, and distances. The results demonstrate how well MATLAB works as a prototyping tool because of its ease of use and robust visualization capabilities. Overall, the study lays a strong foundation for upcoming advancements in vision based intelligent systems by highlighting the significance of balancing computational efficiency and detection accuracy.

title: Universal Smart Power Hub with Remote Monitoring and Control for Home Automation
authors: S Indira, K Ravichandran, S Ananthi, J Manikandan, S Desigan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The rapid growth of home automation has increased the demand for reliable and flexible power management systems capable of controlling multiple household appliances efficiently. Conventional manual operation of electrical loads often leads to inconvenience, energy wastage, and safety risks. This paper presents a universal smart power hub for home automation that enables both remote and local control of multiple electrical devices using an ESP32-based IoT platform. The proposed system supports real-time monitoring of electrical parameters and uninterrupted operation through dual-mode control. Experimental results demonstrate a response time of $300-800 \mathrm{~ms}$, safe operation up to 1200 W, and an overall system efficiency of 93%. The system provides a scalable, cost-effective, and practical solution for centralized smart home power management.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Driven Energy Distribution for Wireless Electric Vehicle Charging Systems with Hybrid Storage
authors: Hira Iqbal, Omar Zeb, Iftikhar Ahmad
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) has emerged as a transformative technology in electric vehicle (EV) infrastructure, enabling contactless charging that enhances convenience, safety, and system resilience. However, efficiently managing energy flow within hybrid storage systems, comprising electrochemical batteries and ultracapacitors, remains a critical challenge due to their distinct electrical dynamics. Batteries provide high energy density for sustained operation but are vulnerable to rapid current fluctuations, while ultracapacitors offer exceptional power response with limited energy storage capacity. This paper describes an AI-enabled power distribution system which combines rule-based control systems with a generative pre-trained transformer (GPT3.5) model for the real-time and situational energy management of hybrid storage systems with Wireless Power Transfer (WPT). The controller intelligently steers transient power where it needs to be to an ultra capacitor while maintaining battery health by controlling the battery's charge/discharge cycles. The improvements of the AI system on the control system's energy transfer efficiency and diminished rate of electrochemical aging is verified in the simulations and real-time computational tests. The system's adaptability to variable levels of wireless power and load is also noted. The projected outline achieves efficiency optimization by 8% and 50% less battery stress compared to rule-Based control while laying the groundwork for automatic environmentally friendly wireless EV charging infrastructure.

title: Research on Adaptive Modal Multigrid Convergence Method
authors: Rongjie Qiang, Dapeng Xiong, Hongbo Wang, Taiyu Wang, Yu Zeng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive parameter screening strategy based on modal energy ratio and eigenvalue stability. The dominant modes of the flow field are extracted through dynamic mode decomposition, the sampling period corresponding to the main oscillation frequency is determined through Fourier analysis, and key modes are selected by combining energy contribution and absolute eigenvalue values to achieve adaptive selection of flow field reconstruction parameters. The three-dimensional high-Reynolds-number supersonic cylinder flow is used as a test case for validation. The results show that the method effectively suppresses flow field vortex shedding oscillations, the reconstructed flow field retains the main shock waves and flow features, the residual convergence speed is significantly improved, and it expands the engineering application potential of the modal multigrid method.

title: LLM-powered Intent-driven Configuration Generation for Multi-vendor Networks
authors: Jingyu Wang, Bo He, Jinyu Zhao, Yixin Xuan, Haifeng Sun, Qi Qi, Junzhe Liang, Zirui Zhuang, Jianxin Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Network configuration management has become increasingly complex, inefficient, and prone to errors due to frequent updates in command structures and the prevalence of multi-vendor network infrastructures. To tackle these challenges, this paper introduces a novel cognitive communication approach, formulating a new task called intent-driven multi-vendor network configuration generation. Within the broader intent-based networking lifecycle, this task specifically targets the realization and command generation stage—translating natural language operational intents into accurate and syntactically valid network commands compatible with multiple vendors, rather than addressing high-level intent interpretation or decomposition. Three primary challenges are addressed: syntactical command validity, vendor-specific syntax diversity, and outdated or inconsistent network knowledge. We propose ConfGen, a cognitive and intent-driven multi-vendor configuration generation framework that consists of two phases: vendor-agnostic syntax retrieval and syntax-constrained command generation. In the first phase, a cognitive retrieval mechanism and reranking strategy identify the most relevant syntax structures based on user intents, while vendor-specific syntax components are effectively generalized. The second phase employs a Large Language Model (LLM) guided by retrieved syntax constraints and user intents to generate precise and valid network commands. To ensure syntactical correctness and vendor compatibility, syntax-constrained decoding strategies are integrated into the LLM generation process. Extensive experimental evaluations conducted on a novel dataset containing network commands from Huawei, Cisco, Nokia, and Juniper demonstrate the superiority of ConfGen. Results confirm significant performance improvements over state-of-the-art solutions in generating accurate, multi-vendor-compatible network configurations driven by user intent.

title: Effect of Ionic Liquid in Poly(Ethyl Methacrylate)-Based Solid Electrolytes for Electric Double-Layer Capacitors
authors: H.K. Jun, C.C. Siow, Grishika Arora, Nuur Syahidah Sabran, Chiam-Wen Liew
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: Thermally stable polymer electrolyte films composed of poly(ethyl methacrylate) (PEMA) as the host polymer, sodium perchlorate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{NaClO}_{4}\right)$</tex> as the salt, and 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium thiocyanate ([BMIM][SCN]) as the ionic liquid were prepared via the solution casting method. Varying [BMIM][SCN] contents were incorporated to optimize ionic conductivity. Structural, morphological, thermal, electrical, and electrochemical characterizations were performed. Fouriertransform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy confirmed interactions between PEMA and [BMIM][SCN], while scanning electron microscopy (SEM) revealed increase of amorphicity with increasing ionic liquid content. Thermal stability improved to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 270{}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> with [BMIM][SCN] addition. The highest ionic conductivity, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.15 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{S} / \text{cm}$</tex> at ambient temperature, was achieved with 25 wt% [BMIM][SCN]. This composition was used to fabricate an electric double-layer capacitor (EDLC), delivering a specific capacitance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.69 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{F} / \mathrm{g}$</tex> as measured by cyclic voltammetry.

title: Implementation Analysis of Indoor Tracking with Millimeter-Wave Radar Sensing System
authors: Jiaqi Wang, Jinze Sun, Lijiao Ding, Changqiang Jing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Photonics and Applied Technology (IPAT)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of the Internet of Things (IoT), the importance of high-precision indoor coordinate information has become increasingly evident in both civilian and military applications. The conventional active wireless indoor positioning solutions resolve the location problem when the target lacks auxiliary equipment. This study investigates an intelligent photonic sensing paradigm that leverages millimeter-wave radar, an advanced sensing technology. In this study, the performance of a millimeter-wave radar-based location estimation technique is tested in an indoor context, the lack of localization of millimeter wave radar is compensated by Linear Regression model. Experimental results show that the proposed system effectively tracks one or two moving targets along various paths, achieving higher precision compared with traditional approaches. The proposed method can reduce the positioning error of millimeter wave radar to less than 50 cm, significantly improving accuracy in real indoor environments.

title: Minimize THD of Seven-level Three Phase Buck Converter for Electric Vehicles
authors: Rakhee Jain, Neha Rathor, Dheeraj Agrawal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Power conversion systems with excellent power quality must be small, dependable, and efficient in order to meet the growing demand for high-performance electric vehicles (EVs). Multilevel converters, especially those with seven-level topologies, provide a practical way to minimize total harmonic distortion (THD) and obtain high output voltage while putting less strain on semiconductor devices. A thorough investigation into reducing THD in a seven-level, three-phase buck converter intended for electric vehicle applications is presented in this research. The suggested converter eliminates low-order harmonics while preserving the ideal switching frequency and voltage balance by integrating a hybrid modulation strategy that combines Selective Harmonic Elimination (SHE) and Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation (SPWM) techniques. Additionally, the best switching angles that greatly enhance harmonic performance are found using an intelligent optimization approach. When MATLAB/Simulink is used to study voltage and current waveforms under different load situations, simulation results show a significant decrease in THD when compared to traditional SPWM and cascaded inverter systems. For EV traction systems, the suggested design guarantees effective power conversion, reduced switching losses, and improved DC-link voltage usage. With a promising mix between efficiency, harmonic suppression, and system compactness, this study confirms the seven-level three-phase buck converter’s promise as a workable architecture for next-generation EV chargers and motor drive applications.

title: Islanding Detection Delayed by Large Induction Machine on Inverter-Based Distributed Generation
authors: Junji Kondoh
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: An inverter-based distributed generation system with an interface unit called power conditioning subsystem (PCS) is required to prevent unintentional islanding immediately. But the run-on time increases in a local isolated grid that includes an induction machine. In this study, anti-islanding tests of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.9-\text{kW}$</tex> PCS was conducted while connecting a large (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.5-\text{kW}$</tex>) induction machine. One of the results confirmed that even when the rotational inertia moment is much lower, the run-on time is longer than that with a small (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.75-\text{kW}$</tex>) induction machine. Another important result showed that the run-on time becomes significantly long when the reactive power injected from the PCS by the frequency feedback function is absorbed by the induction machine owing to its saturation characteristic caused by a voltage rise of several percent.

title: Optimizing Basketball Shooting via an Enhanced Dwarf Mongoose Algorithm
authors: Yuanfei Wei, Shumin Li, Yifeng Huang, Qifang Luo
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: This paper proposes an Enhanced Dwarf Mongoose Optimization Algorithm (EDMOA) that incorporates an encircling foraging strategy and a survival-of-the-fittest population mechanism to improve the original Dwarf Mongoose Optimization Algorithm (DMOA). The proposed enhancement specifically addresses the slow convergence speed and a susceptibility to local optima of the original DMOA. Applied to basketball shot optimization, EDMOA identifies effective combinations of shooting parameters-including shooting distance, release velocity, release height, and launch angle-to increase scoring probability. While confirming the known optimal shooting angle of 52°, this paper also identifies viable alternative solutions at angles near 57° and 15°. These findings provide coaches and players with scientifically-grounded shooting strategies for designing more effective training programs.

title: Deep Learning Based Brain Tumor Classification Using Orange Data Analytics
authors: R Praveen, T R Saravanan, K Sibinath, M Monish, R Archith Shri Hari, R Sumanth
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This research presents a brain tumor classification workflow using the Orange image data mining tool, covering every step from image import and visualization to machine learning evaluation. The pipeline includes the steps of preparing data, extracting features from images using image embedding, and reducing the number of features with Principal Component Analysis (PCA), which helps keep the data clean and speeds up the process. Many of the algorithms used, such as random forests, SVM, Neural Networks, logistic regression, Naive Bayes, and decision trees, are systematically trained and scored, enabling comparative performance evaluation. Evaluation metrics such as ROC analysis, confusion matrix, and box plots are used to provide rigorous validation using k-fold (3, 5, and 10) cross-validation strategies. This workflow demonstrates reproducible, modular, and high-performance predictive modeling for medical imaging tasks, facilitating accurate diagnosis and advancing automatic brain tumor detection research. The results obtained show that after the completion of the program, the neural network model consistently achieved classification accuracy above 96% and AUC up to 0.997 in strict cross-validation.

title: Design and Analysis of a Circular Patch-Based Circularly Polarized Wideband Antenna for K-Band Applications
authors: Vikash Singh Rajouriya, Aditya Goel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Over the past 20 years, a wide range of planar antennas for millimetre wave applications have been reported. The majority of 4 G laminates are not suitable for 5 G transreceive operations due to high metal and dielectric losses. A small circularly polarised single input single output (SISO) antenna with asymmetries in a circular patch is created for the $\mathbf{n} 258(24.25-27.5 \mathrm{GHz}) 5 \mathrm{G} \mathrm{mmWave}$ band in the proposed study. The half-moon-shaped slot and other sectoral slots at various angles are features of the antenna, which is built on a Rogers 5880 substrate measuring $21.65 \times 18.71 \times 0.508$ meters. The axial ratio band of 23.98 to 25.59 GHz and the $2: 1$ VSWR frequency band of 22.38 to 27.50 GHz are both covered by the suggested antenna. The axial ratio bandwidth is 1.62 GHz, whereas the operating bandwidth is 5.12 GHz. Over 5.7 dBi of gain and over 95% radiation efficiency in the band are attained by the design that is being shown. The gain in band has a maximum value of $\mathbf{7. 9 4 ~ d B i}$.
