title: Temperature-Dependent Conduction and Dielectric Engineering of Dual-Barrier Pt/PtO/NbO/SiO/-GaO MOS Capacitors_supp1-3672411.docx
authors: MANIKANTHABABU N
abstract: Pt/PtO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>/Nb<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub> (63.5 nm)/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (6.6 nm)/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> metal–oxide–semiconductor (MOS) capacitors exhibit stable electrical operation across <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to establish the dominant transport mechanism and quantify thermal stability. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> measurements confirmed Poole–Frenkel (PF) emission as the prevailing mechanism, with the extracted trap barrier height ranging from 0.59 to 0.40 eV as the temperature increased, while Arrhenius analysis yielded activation energies that decreased from 0.5 to 0.42 eV as the temperature raised. The devices retain ~97% of the original accumulation capacitance obtained from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> measurements and maintain reasonable border (D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">bt</sub>), oxide (D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ot</sub>), and interface trap (D<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {it}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> densities. D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">bt</sub> is found to be <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!5\times 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and it raised to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8.1\times 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</sub> is found to be <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!5\times 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>eV<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{1}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and it raised to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.2\times 10^{{12}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>eV<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{1}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. Similarly, D<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ot</sub> is found to be <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!2.8\times 10^{{12}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C and it raised to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim\!\!6.2\times 10^{{12}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$300~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) confirmed the Nb<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{{5}+}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> oxidation state and a sharp O 1s spectra, and found oxygen-vacancy density reduction after annealed at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$400~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C in O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>. Further, high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (TEM) revealed an abrupt and conformal Nb<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub>/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> stack without any crystallization of Nb<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub> up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$400~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C.

title: NeoPlace : A Smart Career Assistant for Modern Student Placements
authors: Pramod Kumar Poladi, Sharanya Godishala, Lasya Thatikonda, Sravani Kotte, Anjaneyulu Rangula, Vinay Sriram Gudikandula, Kartheek Reddy Kamireddy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Students usually have a hard time presenting their skills in the most convincing manner in to- day's highly competitive job market. At the same time, recruiters have to deal with the tedious task of selecting the most relevant applications from a large pool. Traditional placement systems are only capable of performing simple functions such as saving resumes or job posting, but they hardly do anything to get students ready for the real recruitment process. The present work pro- poses an AI powered Student Placement App concept that would function as a comprehensive career assistant. By merging features such as ATS-ready resume building, coding and aptitude (QALR) practice, mock tests and interviews, personalized analytics dashboards, company-specific role insights, and an AI chatbot for interaction at any time, the app becomes a one-stop platform for career needs. Unlike existing systems, the newly developed app focuses on personalization, automation, and continuous feedback; thus students are not merely job seekers but job ready candidates. Through a mobilefirst approach that amalgamates these features, the app can lessen the work of placement officers, establish transparency between students and recruiters, and boost students' self-confidence during the placement process.

title: Initial Position Identification of Multiphase PMSM Based on Dual-Winding Symmetrical Pulse Voltage Excitation Using Boundary-Constrained PSO
authors: Guangdong Bi, Kaixuan Wang, Zeliang Zhang, Tao Yang
abstract: For the position sensorless system of dual threephase permanent magnet synchronous motor (DTP-PMSM), accurate initial position information is crucial for the smooth startup. When the conventional high-frequency (HF) signal injection method is applied, the detection precision will be issue, an initial position detection method based on dual-winding symmetrical pulse voltage excitation using boundary-constrained particle swarm optimization (PSO) is proposed, which is a special design tailored for DTP-PMSM. Based on the principle of saturation nonlinearity, the initial rotor position can be estimated by tracking the position corresponding to the maximum amplitude difference between two sets of induced symmetrical HF pulse currents. The optimization searching process is carried out using a boundary-constrained PSO algorithm, which has a superior detection accuracy and speed compared to the traditional fixed-step scanning approach. Through dual-axis symmetrical injection, the proposed method mitigates the influence of coupling effect, and is independent of the motor saliency and the spatial winding distribution compared with the conventional HF injection methods. This method has a good universality and adaptability. The effectiveness is validated by experiments on the developed DTP-PMSM drive platform.

title: Geometry-Guided Traversability Estimation for Compact Indoor Robots via Feature-Level Sensor Fusion
authors: Manar Lashin, Shady Y. El-Mashad, Abdullah T. Elgammal
abstract: Reliable traversability estimation in unstructured indoor environments requires handling specular flooring and dynamic lighting, factors that often degrade purely visual perception. While Vision Transformers provide high semantic accuracy, their computational latency restricts deployment on resource-constrained robotic platforms. This paper proposes a lightweight, geometry-aware system designed for compact ground robots equipped with heterogeneous sensors. The core contributions include a geometry-first pseudo-labeling strategy for self-supervised training and FiLM-UNet-Lite+, a real-time segmentation network that dynamically integrates visual and geometric cues via convolutional feature modulation. Comparative benchmarking against a fine-tuned SegFormer-B0 (Transformer) and MobileNetV2-UNet (Lightweight CNN) validates the architectural efficiency. The proposed system (≈ 0.67M parameters) achieves an inference speed of 219.1 FPS on an NVIDIA T4 GPU, exceeding the MobileNetV2-UNet baseline (160.8 FPS) and offering a 2× speedup over the Transformer baseline. Crucially, on the target edge computing platform (NVIDIA Jetson Nano), the end-to-end system seamlessly fulfills strict 10 Hz control loop requirements (10.28 FPS). Despite a 79% reduction in parameters compared to the lightweight baseline, the approach maintains competitive mean Intersection-over-Union (mIoU) and yields statistically significant improvements in Boundary F1 scores. These results confirm that integrating explicit geometric priors with efficient convolutional architectures enables high-frequency traversability estimation suitable for agile autonomous navigation.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Maintenance in Industrial IoT Systems: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Gobinath A, Rajeswari P, Andal L, Anandan M, Suresh Kumar N, Pavithra Devi R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence coupled with the Industrial Internet of Things has allowed a shift from traditional reactive maintenance approaches to more forward-thinking predictive strategies. This paper examines the application of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning algorithms in the field of predictive maintenance, using benchmark datasets such as the NASA Turbofan Engine Degradation Dataset, also known as C-MAPSS, and the Kaggle Predictive Maintenance Dataset. Different models have been studied, including Random Forest models, Support Vector Machine approaches, Long Short Term Memory networks, Autoencoder methods as well as CNN-LSTM hybrid architectures. The results from the experiments indicate that the CNN-LSTM hybrid model achieved significantly higher accuracy, meaning it reached 96.3%, followed by LSTM with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 8 \%}$</tex>, while traditional machine learning models, for instance Random Forest obtained 91.4% accuracy and Support Vector Machines 89.7%. Additionally, the CNN-LSTM model showed a reduction in unplanned downtime of about 40% and an improvement in reliability regarding fault classification of 15% compared to scheduled maintenance systems, that is to say. These findings demonstrate how applying Artificial Intelligence to predictive maintenance considerably improves efficiency in industrial systems; however, the computational demands and complexity of these models present challenges for implementing them in real-time edge computing situations within IIoT environments, to put it simply.

title: Deep Learning Framework For High Resolution Large-Scale Vegetation Optical Depth Mapping Using Airborne LiDAR and Mobile GNSS-T Data
authors: Abesh Ghosh, Mohammad Ehsanul Hoque, Md Mehedi Farhad, Ali Cafer Gurbuz, Alicia Peduzzi, Mehmet Kurum
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate, high-resolution mapping of Vegetation Optical Depth (VOD) is critical for assessing forest biomass, ecosystem health yet current satellite products lack the spatial detail needed to capture fine-scale heterogeneity. Ground-based GNSS Transmissometry (GNSS-T) offers meter-scale VOD mea surements but is limited in spatial coverage due to operational constraints. This paper presents a novel data fusion framework that combines mobile GNSS-T observations with airborne LiDAR to produce large-scale VOD maps at 5 m × 5 m resolution blocks. The method leverages a Cone-Based Point Distribution Imaging (CPDI) approach, which transforms LiDAR point clouds within GNSS-aligned conical sampling zones into 28 × 30 × 3 angu lar–height histograms, encoding vegetation structure relevant to microwave attenuation. These CPDI images are used to train a convolutional neural network (CNN) to predict block-level VOD values, which are then mapped across entire LiDAR domain over a 400 m × 400 m region. The framework was validated using data from the SMAPVEX22 campaign at Harvard Forest, MA and Millbrook, NY, achieving strong agreement between predicted and measured VOD (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.93 training, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.74 testing) and demonstrating consistent correlations with in-situ basal area (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.72) and biomass (R2 = 0.46) at the regional level. The results show that integrating GNSS-T and LiDAR enables scalable, high-resolution VOD mapping across heteroge neous forested landscapes, offering new capabilities for drought monitoring, forest management, and wildfire risk assessment.

title: A Combined Lighting–LiDAR System Based on a Laser-Excited Phosphor Source: Proof of Concept
authors: Boris Apter, Chen Tonny Dabush, Gal Varon
abstract: A proof-of-concept demonstration of a combined lighting–LiDAR system based on a commercially available laser-excited phosphor (LEP) device with a built-in phosphor and laser diode is presented. The proposed approach integrates broadband lighting and optical ranging into a single system, enabling efficient use of optical power and simplifying design. In the experimental setup, the internal blue laser diode of the LEP device operates at 440 nm to excite the integrated phosphor layer, producing white light. Simultaneously, a portion of the same laser emission is used for generating the LiDAR signal. Measurements confirm that the LEP device provides stable spectral features and sufficient modulation capability for both combined lighting and ranging applications. The LiDAR subsystem accurately detects distances up to 30 m with minimal interference from phosphor emission. These results demonstrate the viability of dual-function laser–phosphor setups and highlight their potential for integrated optical systems for general illumination, automotive sensing, and indoor positioning.

title: SAR Analyses of Typical Multilayer Orange Fruit Models for Different Combinations of Electromagnetic Wave Arrival and Polarization
authors: Adrija Chaudhury, Ardhendu Kundu, Sayan Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Specific Absorption Rate is the rate of radio frequency (RF) energy absorption in a biological body while exposed to an external electromagnetic field. It is measured in watts per kilogram. Along with humans, plants also get exposed to continuous RF electromagnetic fields from cell phone tower that transmit microwave power for radio coverage over 24 hours a day and 365 days a year. High permittivity and loss tangent parameters are observed in most plants and fruits due to high water content and ion concentrations. Therefore, it is strongly expected that the plants, fruits and vegetables can absorb a significant amount of microwave energy which might have consequent effects on their growth. This research paper investigates the SAR in typical multilayer orange models due to radio frequency (RF) exposure from source like cell phone towers (GSM 900 and 1800 MHz bands) and Wi-Fi (2400 MHz band) antennas as per the existing Indian scenario for different combinations of electromagnetic wave arrival and polarization.

title: Real-Time Threat Intelligence and Automated Prevention Using Micro Intelligent System Technology and Explainable Tree-Based Models
authors: Uddipan Ghosh, Ankan Sarkar, Siddhartha Roy, Apurba Nandi, Avik Kumar Das, Pratik Saha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Cyber-attacks are getting faster, smarter, and undetectable by traditional security measures. DDoS, password cracking, port scanning malware, and phishing attacks get evolved and tend to bypass conventional and outdated security configurations. To address this challenge, our project contributes to an adaptive cyber threat detection system with a hybrid machine learning strategy that integrates three robust models Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM. Each of these models has something valuable: Random Forest is good on big noisy data, XGBoost improves by learning from its mistakes, and LightGBM provides fast responses but accurate output. Integrating the findings of the three models led to developing a system that was more accurate and better in threat detection and less prone to false alarms. We tested the system using actual cyber attack datasets, and the result showed that our hybrid model performed better than other standalone models. To make the system even smarter, we added MIST AI to it-a helpful layer that is an adaptive rulebased threat intelligence layer. It allows the system to decide on how to act against various levels of threats by assigning actions like blocking, monitoring, or limiting the connections. Mist AI maintains the flexibility and responsiveness of the system to new emerging threats. Most importantly, the system supports near real-time decision-making under evaluated conditions. This work demonstrates how the combination of several ML models with an adaptive layer like Mist AI builds a more robust and reliable cybersecurity solution for modern digital environments.

title: Rapid Forecast of Device Transient Temperature Response at Wafer Level Through Transducer-Less Thermoreflectance Technique
authors: Yali Mao, Yunliang Ma, Haochen Zhang, Mei Wu, Shujuan Liu, Xing Hu, Yiqun Wang, Haiding Sun, Chao Yuan
abstract: GaN high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) may undergo significant transient thermal shock during high-frequency and high-power operation, compromising their performance stability and reliability. Therefore, it is crucial to study the transient temperature response of devices for effective thermal management. In this study, we explore the potential of the transducer-less transient thermoreflectance (TL-TTR) technique, which can rapidly forecast the transient temperature response of devices from the as-grown wafers without the need for device fabrication. Several GaN wafers on common substrates (Si and SiC) are measured to assess the capability of this method. For the GaN HEMTs with an epitaxial layer thickness of ~1800 nm, we quantify the critical switching frequency of 16.7 MHz, at which severe thermal accumulation effects can be avoided under 50% duty cycle operation for the GaN-on-Si devices. The finite element (FE) model verifies the TL-TTR results. This method also forecasts the critical switching frequency of the device for other GaN thicknesses. The TL-TTR technique is expected to be an effective method to directly characterize the transient temperature response of GaN HEMTs.

title: Spatial Visualization of Tourism and Daily Interest Structure for Regional Railways Using LLM
authors: Yuki Fujimoto, Jun Sakurai, Satoshi Abiko, Masanori Ikebe
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study analyzes the spatial characteristics of regional railway use in Japan by integrating large language model (LLM)-based classification of Instagram captions with geographic information. Posts mentioning 15 railway lines were categorized by an LLM (Gemma3-27B) into “tourism,” “daily,” and “other,” then linked to nearby stations and points of interest (POIs). Entropy and evenness were calculated to assess posting diversity and concentration. Results show that stable-demand lines exhibit balanced distributions of tourism and daily posts, latent-demand lines concentrate on specific tourist sites, and sparse-demand lines show dispersed but low posting activity. These differences reveal structural characteristics of regional communication and railway use. The findings demonstrate that combining LLM-based text understanding with spatial analysis can effectively visualize local interest structures and contribute to sustainable regional mobility planning.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for 2025 PM2.5 Forecasting in Thailand
authors: Kumpee Teeravech, Ratchaphon Samphutthanont, Panitada Limrueangrong
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study integrates ground-based PM2.5 measurements and satellite-derived hotspot data from 2011 to 2024 across Thailand to forecast daily PM2.5 levels for 2025 using machine-learning techniques. After reorganizing multisource datasets and constructing temporal and spatial features, including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\pm} \mathbf{3}$</tex>-day lagged <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M} 2.5$</tex> values and hotspot counts within multiple buffer distances, XGBoost models were developed at both national and regional scales. The results reveal clear seasonal patterns, with PM2.5 concentrations and hotspot activity peaking during March-April, and the strongest correlations observed for hotspots located <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{2 0 0}$</tex> km from monitoring stations. The models effectively capture seasonal and inter-annual PM2.5 variability, achieving regional RMSE values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.35-9.93 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, MAE values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.04- 4.42 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> values ranging from 0.78 to 0.94, with national-scale <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> exceeding 0.85. However, incorporating hotspot variables does not consistently improve prediction accuracy, indicating that temporal features such as particularly day-of-year and short-term PM2.5 persistence remain the dominant predictors.

title: Unveiling the Structure of Teacher Competency: An Eight-Dimensional Model for Primary Education
authors: Xiaohong Ju
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Current research on primary school teacher competency predominantly focuses on dimension identification, with limited empirical examination of the internal structure of proposed models. To address this gap, this study conducts a structural validation of a preliminary eight-dimensional competency model. Based on survey data from 527 primary school teachers in Dongguan, China, confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) demonstrates satisfactory model fit. A key finding reveals that sense of educational mission exhibits the highest second-order factor loading of =0.704, indicating its relatively central position within the competency structure, which suggests that internal belief systems may serve as important drivers of competency, alongside explicit skills. The study provides a structural perspective for teacher professional development, highlighting the potential importance of fostering a sense of mission within competency frameworks.

title: Research on a Compliance Evaluation Framework for the Informatization Construction of Group Subsidiaries Based on LLMOps
authors: ZeTao Lu, Fang Huang, Meng Wang, Nan Wu, YueLi Lu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: The disparity in informatization levels among subsidiaries presents significant challenges for large enterprise groups in evaluating whether subsidiary information systems are compliant. Manual audits are labor-intensive and often fail to deliver timely or reliable insights, thereby weakening overall group governance and increasing systemic risks. To address this issue, this paper proposes a dual-channel compliance evaluation framework based on Large Language Model Operations (LLMOps), integrating knowledge-driven questionnaire generation with locally deployed MCP (Model-Controlled Parsing) services. The system supports both active reporting and passive evidence acquisition, enabling automated and multi-dimensional compliance assessment. Simulation results show that, compared with manual methods, the framework improves efficiency in subsidiary compliance evaluation by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}-\mathbf{8}$</tex> times and enhances accuracy by about 10%. The model demonstrates practical value in intelligent compliance supervision of grassroots units and provides a scalable path toward unified governance.

title: Detecting GDP Movement and Growth Rates from Indonesian Economic News Using IndoRoBERTa with Neural Sentence Selection
authors: Eka Fitria, Afnan Afnan, Taufik Fuadi Abidin
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is widely recognized as a core indicator of national economic performance, yet official statistical releases are often subject to reporting delays. This motivates the need for timely, high-frequency textual signals that can support near real-time economic monitoring. Online economic news provides such signals, offering narrative descriptions of sectoral activities, production trends, and macroeconomic conditions. This study proposes an Indonesian transformer-based framework for extracting GDP-related information from online news by combining IndoRoBERTa classifiers with neural sentence selection. We evaluate several IndoBERT and IndoRoBERTa variants under a unified fine-tuning protocol and introduce two sentence-selection mechanisms-keyword-driven and neural embedding-based-to address the challenges posed by lengthy, information-dense articles. IndoRoBERTa with neural selection achieves strong results across three tasks: 0.8617 macro F1 and 0.8588 accuracy for 17 -sector industrial classification; 0.8878 macro F1 and 0.8971 accuracy for binary GDP movement detection; and approximately 0.6728 macro <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> and over 0.85 accuracy for four-class GDP growth-rate detection. These results outperform full-document and keyword-based baselines while reducing inference cost in non-tuned settings. Overall, the findings demonstrate that transformer models, when integrated with neural sentence-selection, offer an efficient and effective approach to extracting GDP-related signals from Indonesian economic news and can serve as a complementary source to official macroeconomic statistics.

title: Advanced Bean Leaf Lesion Classification Using EfficientNetV2-S for Agricultural Applications
authors: Garima Jaitly, Bura Vijay Kumar, Neha Sharma, Gaurav Tuteja, Preeti Badhani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Early and accurate detection of plant leaf diseases play a crucial role in ensuring sustainable agricultural productivity and minimizing crop losses. This research addresses the classification of bean leaf lesions—a common problem affecting yield and plant health—by proposing an advanced deep learning-based methodology utilizing the EfficientNetV2-S architecture. The approach integrates systematic preprocessing techniques, including normalization and augmentation, to enhance model generalization across varying visual conditions. The model was trained and validated on a structured dataset comprising three categories: healthy, angular leaf spot, and bean rust. Optimization was achieved using the AdamW optimizer along with a multi-step learning rate scheduler and early stopping to prevent overfitting. Experimental results demonstrated a classification accuracy of 99%, supported by near-perfect precision, recall, and F1-scores across all classes. The confusion matrix confirmed the model’s high reliability, with only one instance misclassified out of 133 total samples. These findings highlight the robustness and efficiency of the proposed model in classifying bean leaf lesions with minimal error. The research contributes a scalable and accurate solution to the domain of automated plant disease detection and presents significant potential for integration into real-time agricultural monitoring systems, aiding in precision farming and early intervention strategies.

title: Novel En-Route Integrity Monitoring Algorithm for Communication-Positioning Integration System
authors: Zhuoyan Liu, Lu Yin, Tianzhu Song, Wencong Xu, Zhongliang Deng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Accurate positioning and integrity assurance are fundamental requirements for future en-route aviation operations. Conventional systems such as Distance Measuring Equipment(DME) and Wide Area Multilateration provide limited accuracy and scalability, while Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), though globally available, remain vulnerable to interference and spoofing, which constrains their use in safetycritical applications. To address fragmented spectrum conditions and enable integrated communication-navigation services, this work builds on a novel signal design that embeds PRN-based ranging components within the L-band OFDM communication grid. Cross-band segmentation and aggregation are employed to achieve enhanced ranging resolution under non-contiguous spectrum. On this basis, an integrity monitoring framework termed CC-RAIM (Cross-Channel Receiver Autonomous Integrity Monitoring) is introduced. The algorithm integrates extended Kalman filtering with fault detection and exclusion (FD/FE) and protection level estimation, explicitly accounting for fragmented spectrum and non-Gaussian error characteristics. Simulation results indicate that CC-RAIM effectively mitigates impulsive and heavy-tailed errors in the presence of DME coexistence, reducing RMSE from 21.82 m to 0.77 m and improving error concentration with P50/P95 converging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.61 / 54.7 ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.31 / 1.7 ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. Furthermore, the protection level remains consistently below the alert limit throughout operation, providing a closedloop framework for integrity risk management. These findings demonstrate that CC-RAIM enables reliable high-accuracy positioning with integrity assurance for next-generation integrated communication-navigation systems in en-route scenarios.

title: Research on the Relationship Between Local Attachment and Retention Intention of Rural Primary School-Oriented Normal University Students
authors: Quanyuan Xia, Hui Wu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Local attachment is an important factor which can influence the retention willingness of government-funded normal university students. To explore the relationship between local attachment and the retention willingness of teacher-training students assigned to eastern, western, and northern Guangdong, this study conducted a questionnaire survey among governmentfunded normal students in these regions to comprehensively understand the current status and relationship between their local attachment and retention willingness. The study divided local attachment into four dimensions: local cognition, local ability, rural identity, and rural mission. Data were analyzed using SPSS 26.0 and Mplus 7.11. The results showed that: 1. The levels of local attachment and retention willingness among teacher-training students assigned to eastern, western, and northern Guangdong are generally above the medium level; 2. There is a significant positive correlation between local attachment and retention willingness, and local attachment can positively predict retention willingness. The conclusions of this study provide targeted suggestions for strengthening the cultivation of local attachment among government-funded normal students and encouraging them to devote themselves to rural education.

title: Demosaicing of Microscopic Vine Wood Images using Plug-and-Play Proximal Graphical Models
authors: Aryan Pandit, Anurodh Kumar, Amit Vishwakarma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Demosaicing plays a critical role in rebuilding highquality color images from raw sensor data, especially in microscopy applications where fine structural details are essential for accurate analysis. This paper presents a new Plug-and-Play Prior-driven proximal gradient model (PnP PGM) demosaicing framework tailored for microscopic vine wood images. The proposed approach leverages the flexibility of plug-and-play priors to incorporate powerful denoisers, ensuring superior color fidelity and structural preservation while mitigating artifacts observed in traditional interpolation-based methods. By integrating a Prior-driven proximal gradient model (PGM) optimization scheme, the method iteratively refines the reconstructed image, maintaining the spatial integrity of micro-scopic textures crucial for scientific and industrial applications. Experimental evaluations on microscopic vine wood image datasets demonstrate that our approach surpasses state-of-the-art demosaicing techniques.

title: Adaptive Sparsity-Aware Transformers: A Differentiable Constrained Optimization Framework for Sustainable AI
authors: Uday Pratap Singh, Bersha Kumari, Ebtasam Ahmad Siddiqui
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The environmental and computational costs of modern deep learning models have intensified interest in Green AI. Although pruning, quantization, and efficient architectures have improved static efficiency, they remain inflexible once deployed, allocating the same computational budget to all inputs regardless of complexity. This work proposes the Adaptive Sparsity-Aware Transformer (ASTr), an architecture that employs a differentiable sparsity controller to dynamically select attention heads and feedforward neurons on a per-input basis. Sparsity is formulated as a constrained optimization problem solved through differentiable relaxation, enabling fine-grained and adaptive inference. ASTr is evaluated on text classification, time-series forecasting, and environmental sensor prediction tasks. Experimental results show that ASTr reduces inference FLOPs by up to 45% while maintaining accuracy within 0.5% of dense baselines. In comparison with recent efficient transformer variants such as Hyena and Mamba, ASTr achieves $\mathbf{1 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 5 \%}$ additional efficiency gains without accuracy degradation. The contributions of this work are threefold: 1) a differentiable sparsity controller with budget-enforcing constraints; 2) seamless integration into transformer blocks for adaptive computation; and 3) comprehensive empirical validation demonstrating significant reductions in FLOPs and energy usage. Overall, ASTr advances sustainable AI by embedding adaptivity directly into inference-time computation, moving beyond static efficiency techniques.

title: Towards Generalizable and Robust Audio DeepFake Detection: A Comprehensive Review of Modern Techniques
authors: Tanya Kumar, Mohammed Hamza Godil, Hamza Gandhi, Surekha Janrao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This review paper consists of 10 transaction papers which integrates latest developments of Audio Deepfake Detection (ADD) and Speech Anti-Spoofing to summarize methods that improve generalization and robustness against unknown spoof attacks, low-quality speech, and silence-based attacks. It is mainly challenging to deal with the domain shift between training and unseen real data, particularly against emerging Audio Language Model (ALM)-based deepfakes. Techniques examined are Feature Decomposition Learning (FDL) to disentangle synthesizer-conditional artifacts from common content features, Dual-View Graph Neural Networks (D-GNN) to advance fine-grained detection, and FrequencyTime Domain Knowledge Distillation (FTDKD) to defend information loss in compressed speech. An important observation is that traditional models excessively depend on silence-segment features to be vulnerable to silence-trimming attacks. New frameworks utilizing Multi-Task Meta-Learning (MTML) frameworks like SASSD and ASDG demonstrate superior generalization and frequently utilize self-supervised learning (SSL) representations such as Wav2vec 2.0 (W2V2) and Whisper. Best-performing models, especially those utilizing co-training methods such as CSAM on new speech datasets such as Codecfake, demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, such as the W2V2-AASIST(CSAM) that achieves an EER of as low as $0.616 \%$ against varied test conditions.

title: Enhancing Assembly Action Recognition and Anomaly Detection via Slowfast-CSWin Fusion
authors: Chao-Lung Yang, Hsin-Chun Wu, Jian-En Wang, Yung-Jui Chen, Yi-Ju Chou
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: We propose a Human Action Recognition framework for smart manufacturing, integrating the SlowFast network with CSWin Transformer to enhance spatiotemporal representation. YOLOv7 detects human locations, from which multi-scale second-order offset motion features are computed for precise Anomaly Detection between Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) and non-SOP actions. These motion and location features are fused and classified using XGBoost, with inference stabilized by Accumulative Moving Mode filtering. Experiments on the Sims-Normal dataset achieve an F1-score above 0.88, surpassing traditional HAR and SlowFast-based models, demonstrating robustness and strong applicability in complex industrial environments.

title: Effective Medical Question Answering Chatbot Using Llama-2 Model
authors: Mummineni Chaitanya Kumar, Lopa Mandal, BMD Sai Kunal, J N Sandeep
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In this paper, a sophisticated medical question-answering chatbot has been built based on Llama-2 model. Our purpose is to provide users with a full range of support for symptom diagnosis and health advice, a personalised response, recommendations for treatment/intervention, and advice on safe use of this system. Our development and validation of our chatbot are based on The Gale Encyclopaedia of Medicine 2. The underlying technology employed in our system is the Llama-2 model, which has been demonstrated to be proficient in language. We extensively tested our chatbot and achieved an accuracy of 85% for three cases and conducted performance analyses for the various medical questions asked. The chatbot's best performance was in diagnosing symptoms; it successfully identified and provided possible conditions based on the users input, and it provided tailored responses based on individual requests, which enhanced user engagement and satisfaction.

title: Hybrid Multi-Objective Grey Wolf Optimization Technique for IoT Task Placement
authors: R Baskar, S Nishvanth, V Rahul, R M Suganeshwaran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The proliferation of IoT devices has ushered in an era of unprecedented connectivity and data generation. Nonetheless, the effective administration of tasks in IoT settings, especially within Cloud-Fog Networks, continues to pose a significant challenge. This study presents an innovative method to improve IoT task efficiency through the application of a Hybrid Multi-Objective Grey Wolf Optimizer (HMOGWO) technique. Cloud-fog computing utilizes a distributed design to position compute resources nearer to IoT devices, hence alleviating latency issues. The proposed HMOGWO integrates the Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO), which is based on the social hunting behavior of grey wolves, with a multi-objective optimization framework. This hybrid strategy seeks to resolve the competing objectives present in IoT task optimization, including latency reduction, resource utilization maximization, and energy efficiency optimization. The study encompasses an extensive literature evaluation, a precise problem definition, and a thorough methodology for the implementation and assessment of the suggested HMOGWO strategy. The paper evaluates the efficacy of the HMOGWO strategy in enhancing several performance indicators through experimentation and comparison with established optimization strategies, thereby providing useful insights into the advancing domain of IoT and fog computing. The results provide actionable insights for improving IoT task execution in Cloud-Fog Networks, facilitating increased efficiency and resource usage across various IoT applications.

title: Single-Cell RNA Sequencing Identifies Dysregulated Signaling in the Prefrontal Cortex of Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Van-An Tran, Duc-Anh Pham, Thu-Trang Mac, Trang-Nhung Nguyen, Phuong-Thao Nguyen Tran, Luong-Thang Pham, My-Linh Nguyen, Van-Khanh Do
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by cognitive decline, with the prefrontal cortex (PFC) being among the earliest regions to exhibit pathological changes. Recent advances in single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) have facilitated the high-resolution characterization of cell-type-specific transcriptomic alterations, thus deepening our understanding of AD pathogenesis. In this study, we conducted an integrative analysis of multiple scRNAseq datasets to investigate transcriptional dysregulation and disrupted signaling pathways in the PFC of individuals with AD. A systematic literature review identified three eligible studies, encompassing a total of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2,557,393$</tex> cells derived from individuals aged 55 years and older, across six Braak stages. Raw data were preprocessed through quality control, alignment, normalization, and batch correction steps. Cell types were annotated based on curated marker gene databases. Differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were examined using gene ontology (GO) and pathway enrichment analyses, while ligand-receptor interaction modeling revealed aberrant neuronal-glial communication. Pseudotime trajectory analysis identified key regulatory genes associated with disease progression. Notably, microglia exhibited distinct transcriptomic alterations, including the downregulation of genes involved in post-translational modifications and the upregulation of cytokine-responsive genes. Additional dysregulated pathways were related to protein synthesis, cholesterol metabolism, extracellular matrix organization, and collagen biosynthesis. These findings provide novel insights into the molecular underpinnings of AD and suggest potential therapeutic targets involving protein synthesis, mitochondrial dynamics, neuronal signaling, and immune system regulation.

title: Intrusion Detection Using AI and ML in Cybersecurity
authors: Md Asiful Ameen, Diptyanil Basak, Leo S. F. Lin, Baisakhi Das
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This paper provides a literature review, comparative analysis and presents an original research of the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) technologies for cybersecurity with particular attention to intrusion detection. First, the research looks at conventional ML methods to make an analysis on malicious network activity detection on the CICIDS 2017 dataset. On top of the foundations, a network based large language model phi-3-mini is fine-tuned for intrusion detection using structured and network flow data. It takes natural language inputs of network properties, and enables the ability to predict scenario various intrusion or benignity. The combination of structured queries and natural language processing also presents a new mode for interacting with security systems, particularly for expert users. We also review literature on techniques used in AI based threat detection and malware analysis as well as automated incident response. Although the results show the promise of large language models to improve detection performance and accessibility above the baseline of what already exist, the study discusses challenges such as interpretability, resource demands, and robustness against adversarial inputs. In the post-Quantum age, such a model would be much valuable for assisting human operators to reinforce traditional network perimeter defenses and other security systems, that makes the current generation (albeit somewhat minimally) more effective against increasingly sophisticated and dynamic blackhat techniques of various threats.

title: Real-Time Priority Vehicle Detection and Intelligent Green Corridor Simulation Using YOLOv8n on Raspberry Pi Edge Device
authors: Swayam Malakar, Tanisqua Guha, Amit Kumar Das
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Rapid response is a key factor in the success of emergency services. These days heavy urban traffic is the main reason why emergency vehicles cannot move quickly and thus, their response is delayed. The current method makes use of a Raspberry Pi 4 Model B along with a camera module to record the live traffic video streams. For quick local inference, a trimmed down YOLOv8n object detection model is used directly on the RaspberryPi. Once a positive detection is made, the system initiates the simulation of traffic signal control, thus dynamically extending the signal phase to give immediate right-of-way. This approach overcomes the challenges of expensive, centralized systems and demonstrates the potential of using highly optimized deep learning models at the network edge for mission-critical, low-latency applications. The experimental results show that the YOLOv8n-on-Pi implementation is able to deliver the required frame rate and accuracy for efficient real-time operation, thereby achieving a significant decrease in the simulated transit delays for emergency services.

title: Cybersecurity Strategies for Peer-to-Peer Energy Trading in Community Microgrids
authors: D Sarathkumar, R Shankar, R. Kalaiarasan, P Sivaraman, Senthil Saravanan M S, T. Loganayagi, Iyappan Murugesan, S Lavanya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The incorporation of peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading mechanisms within community microgrids represents a transformative shift towards decentralized and consumer-driven energy markets. While this paradigm enhances grid flexibility and promotes renewable energy utilization, it also introduces critical cybersecurity challenges due to the reliance on advanced communication networks and distributed ledger technologies. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of cybersecurity strategies tailored for P2P energy trading in community microgrids. We investigate potential attack vectors including data tampering, unauthorized access, and denial-of-service attacks, and examine their impact on trading integrity and grid stability. The study evaluates contemporary solutions such as blockchain-based authentication, privacy-preserving transaction protocols, and intrusion detection systems adapted to resource-constrained environments. Furthermore, a layered security framework is proposed, integrating cryptographic mechanisms, real-time monitoring, and resilient network architectures to safeguard trading operations. Simulation-based validation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed strategies in mitigating cyber risks while maintaining operational efficiency. The findings aim to guide future research and practical deployment of secure, scalable, and trustworthy P2P energy exchange platforms in communal microgrids.

title: Design of Two-Level Cooperative Interaction Timing for the Control Subsystem of Three Super Satellites
authors: Chi Zhang, Zhixiong Jiang, Yushuang Wang, Gaojian Lyu, Hong Guan, Minghui Shi, Wenyu Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper primarily addresses the high requirement for time error and delay in dynamic-imaging attitude control of three super satellites with super agility, super stability, and super precision. Firstly, the two-level GPS second pulse high-precision uniform compensation strategy is designed based on the characteristics of dynamic-imaging tasks to ensure the high precision and of time synchronization and the stability of the high-precision time. Secondly, a two-level interactive cooperative attitude compensation method is proposed based on the minimal latency principles for high-time-sensitive information. This approach achieves the optimal interaction delay between upper and lower platforms and the high-precision compensation for star tracker sensor attitude in cooperative control, which can support the super stability requirements and the super dynamic-imaging precision after rapid maneuvering of three super satellites. Finally, the simulation results indicate the validity of the approach.
