title: Text Summarization Using NLP: A Survey on Various Approaches
authors: Monikonkona Ray, Pratik Chakraborty, Monalisa Dey, Unman Chaudhuri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In this rising era of technological advancements, managing excessive amount of data has become indispensable. Text summarization is one of the key methods to perform this job. This survey paper covers the various ways of automatic text summarization in Natural Language Processing (NLP) dividing them into two categories which are extractive and abstractive summarization. It also explores hybrid methods to include the strength of both approaches and highlights their applications in various domains such as research, legal documents, generating precise summaries and easy accessibility for future advancements in this field. It provides a basic insight into what work has been done in this field and what can be future aspects of it.

title: Barrier Engineering of Silicon-Doped Boron Nitride in the Charge Trap Layer for High Performance 3-D V-nand Flash Memory
authors: Dae Hyun Kang, Jaejoong Jeong, Youngkeun Park, Dong Hun Sin, Han Mei Choi, Byung Jin Cho
abstract: This work introduces silicon-doped boron nitride (BN) as an engineered energy barrier that suppresses the dead zone effect and improves spatial charge trapping efficiency. Silicon was incorporated into BN by in situ atomic layer deposition (ALD) super-cycles, and the resulting Si-N bonds in BN generate higher density and deeper depth of electron traps. Energy band structure and Arrhenius analysis confirm the increase in electron trap depth, while device characteristics indicate enhanced trapping efficiency. Integrated into a scaled 4nm SiN charge trap layer (CTL), the silicon-doped BN barrier increases the program window without degrading erase characteristics and improves electron retention. These results indicate that an optimized Si-doped BN energy barrier enables XY-scaling without compromising memory performance.

title: An Enhanced Framework for Network Anomaly Detection and Traffic Feature Analysis
authors: Cheruku Poorna Venkata Srinivasa Rao, Gottam Hemalatha, Talari Nava Jeevan Sravan, Koduru Tanu Sai Sri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: With the enlarged utilization of computer networks, security has become one of the critical issues. A network intrusion by malicious or unauthorized consumers may cause severe interruption to networks. So, the progress of a strong and dependable Network Intrusion Detection System (IDS) is gradually significant. Intrusion detection relates to a suite of models used to recognize attacks and traffic against network infrastructures and computers. Therefore, network anomaly intrusion and traffic feature enhancement detection using deep learning is proposed to detect traffic features in wireless industrial networks. In this proposed system, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model with effective feature selection methods is used to analyze network traffic data. In this system, the border gateway and network manager are followed by feature selection using CNN. The proposed system shows efficient and accurate detection of anomalies by using complex network behavior patterns. Therefore, this system improves the overall accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.

title: Development of real-time imaging technology using an omnidirectional detector
authors: Miyuki Sasaki, Yukihisa Sanada, Tatsuo Torii
abstract: We developed and evaluated a real-time gamma-ray directional imaging framework using the Fractal Radiation Imaging Element (FRIE), an omnidirectional detector based on a Sierpinski tetrahedral structure. The system consists of sixteen scintillator crystals, each acquiring spectral data every second, and estimates the incident gamma-ray directions at 1-s intervals by analyzing variations in detector response. The estimated directional distributions are superimposed on images captured by a 360-degree camera, enabling intuitive visual confirmation. In this study, real-time imaging performance was comparatively evaluated using multiple analysis algorithms, including the conventional Maximum Likelihood Expectation Maximization (MLEM) method and similarity-based analyses employing pre-acquired reference response patterns with correlation coefficients, Euclidean distance, and Wasserstein distance. Although all methods were able to estimate the incident directions in real time, the similarity-based analyses-particularly the approach combining Euclidean and Wasserstein distances-demonstrated higher stability and reproducibility, with angular errors constrained within approximately 20 degrees. These results indicate that similarity-based analysis is effective for robust and real-time localization of radiation sources using the FRIE detector.

title: Distilling Object Detectors via Monte Carlo Dropout
authors: Junfei Yi, Hui Zhang, Jianxu Mao, Tengfei Liu, Mingjie Li, Sihao Lin, Hanyu Gu, Zhihui Li, Xiaojun Chang, Yaonan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Knowledge distillation (KD) has become a fundamental technique for model compression in object detection tasks. The data noise and training randomness may cause the knowledge of the teacher model to be unreliable, referred to as knowledge uncertainty. Existing methods neglect this uncertainty, potentially hindering the student's capacity to capture and understand latent “dark knowledge”. In this work, we introduce a novel strategy that explicitly incorporates knowledge uncertainty, named Uncertainty-Driven Knowledge Extraction and Transfer (UET). Given the unknown, high-dimensional nature of the knowledge distribution, we employ Monte Carlo dropout to effectively estimate the teacher's uncertainty. Leveraging information theory, we combine uncertainty with deterministic knowledge, enabling the student to benefit from both precision and diversity. UET is a plug-and-play method that integrates seamlessly with existing distillation techniques. We validate our approach through comprehensive experiments across various distillation strategies, detectors, and backbones. Specifically, UET achieves state-of-the-art results, with a ResNet50-based GFL detector obtaining 44.1% mAP on the COCO dataset-surpassing baseline performance by 3.9%.

title: Structural Characteristics and Formation Mechanisms of Patent Collaboration Networks in China's Prefabricated Buildings
authors: Fangliang Wang, Min Cheng, Jinsheng Yang
abstract: Inter-organizational collaborative innovation is crucial for advancing the development of prefabricated buildings (PBs). However, the structural characteristics and formation mechanisms of networks arising from such collaborative innovation remain unexplored. Using patent data on PBs in China (2010-2021), this study constructed patent collaboration networks (PCNs) and examined their structural characteristics using social network analysis and network motif analysis methods. Exponential random graph models were applied to investigate the mechanisms underlying PCN formation. The results show that (i) the PCNs of PBs exhibit small-world and scale-free characteristics without a core-periphery structure, evolving from single-core to multi-core aggregation and forming an open and diverse innovation ecosystem; (ii) State Grid Corporation of China emerges as the core organization, significantly influencing knowledge flow and resource integration; (iii) enterprise-enterprise-enterprise and enterprise-enterprise-university are dominant collaborative innovation patterns for PBs. Enterprises lead PBs' innovation while universities provide technical support; (iv) PCNs' formation is driven by triadic closure, technology proximity, geography proximity, collaboration breadth, and collaboration depth, whereas organization proximity and innovation capability show inconsistent effects. Robustness tests conducted on PCNs formed by varying time intervals also support these findings. These insights provide strategic guidance for partner selection in PBs' innovation and inform policy-making for collaborative innovation initiatives.

title: Investigation C- and L-Band Spaceborne Synthetic Aperture Radar Scattering on Oil Palm Phenology
authors: Rika Hernawati, Josaphat Tetuko Sri Sumantyo, Soni Darmawan, Ketut Wikantika, Agung Budi Harto
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Monitoring of oil palm plantations is important for supporting sustainable management and improving productivity. This study to investigates the scattering characteristics of oil palm plantations at various phenological phases using dual-polarized spaceborne synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data from C-band Sentinel-1A and L-band ALOS-2 PALSAR-2. A two-layer Random Volume over Ground (RVOG) decomposition method was implemented to separate the total backscatter into interpretable components: volume scattering (Pv), double-bounce scattering (Pd), and surface scattering (Ps). The study was conducted in the PTPN III Sei Dadap plantation in North Sumatra, Indonesia, covering plantation ages from 0 to 25 years. The methodology included SAR preprocessing, RVOG decomposition, and scattering component extraction. The results show various age-dependent scattering characteristics. Volume scattering dominates in both young and mature oil palm stands due to active canopy growth and closure, while double-bounce scattering becomes dominant in older plantations due to increased trunk-ground interactions and reduced canopy density. The C-band has more sensitivity to upper canopy dynamics, indicating a decline in Pv with increasing age, while the L-band shows improved sensitivity to vertical structures and sub-canopy interactions, shown by higher Pd values in mature and older phases. These findings highlight the beneficial functions of C-band and L-band SAR data in characterizing oil palm phenology and show the effectiveness of dual-polarization RVOG decomposition for the operational monitoring of plantation structure and age.

title: SmartVision: A Role-Based Multimodal Assistive Platform for Real-Time Object and Text Recognition with Audio Feedback
authors: Gracy Kumari, Alivia Gope, Arkodeep Chatterjee, Nilabja Jana, Anshuman Banerjee, Ankita Sen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Despite the tremendous advancements in computer vision-based assistive technologies, many current systems are still only able to perform one function, depend on cloud connectivity, or require large hardware that makes deployment impractical. In order to prepare for future wearable deployment, this study presents SmartVision, a lightweight multimodal assistive platform that combines offline text-to-speech, handwriting recognition, object detection, printed-text OCR, and text summarizing into a single pipeline. The suggested solution was put into practice as a modular web-based prototype in order to assess the fundamental AI capability. Results from experiments show consistent performance across a variety of visual inputs, such as scene-level comprehension and real-time label reading. Compatibility with edge computing platforms is made possible by SmartVision's offline and modular design, which facilitates deployment on devices with limited resources like Raspberry Pi-class hardware. The proposed approach provides a scalable and privacy-preserving pathway toward affordable assistive vision solutions for visually impaired users.

title: Resource-Aware Call Admission Control in Integrated Access and Backhaul Networks: Design and Performance Analysis
authors: Seung-Yeon Kim, Ji-Hoon Bae
abstract: Integrated access and backhaul (IAB) is a key enabler in beyond 5g (B5G) and 6G wireless systems, allowing wireless backhaul connectivity without the need for extensive fiber deployments. In a typical IAB architecture, data from user equipment (UE) is first transmitted to an IAB node via an access link, and then forwarded to the core network through a wireless backhaul link, forming a dual-hop path. This architecture supports flexible and cost-effective network densification in dense and heterogeneous environments. While existing performance studies of IAB networks have mainly focused on metrics such as latency, throughput, or link-level success probability, they have often overlooked the impact of call admission control (CAC), limiting their practical applicability under resource-constrained conditions. In this work, we incorporate a resource-aware CAC mechanism into the analysis of IAB networks and construct a tractable analytical framework based on a two-dimensional Markov chain to evaluate the resulting system dynamics. The model captures the dual-hop admission behavior of IAB-UEs by jointly considering access and backhaul resource availability, and derives closed-form success probabilities for IAB-UEs, non-IAB UEs, and IAB nodes under various interference conditions and spatial configurations. To support multi-dimensional evaluation, we introduce a normalized effective success probability (NESP) metric, which integrates signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR)-based link quality with call-level performance indicators such as blocking probability and physical resource block (PRB) utilization. The analytical and simulation results reveal how PRB allocation strategies and link distances affect system performance, offering practical design insights. For instance, prioritizing backhaul PRB allocation improves IAB-UE performance when backhaul links are long, while balanced or access-favoring allocation proves more efficient under short-link scenarios. The findings provide realistic and actionable guidelines for resource management and node placement in future B5G/6G IAB deployments.

title: XAI-Powered Smart Calorie Tracking via Image Captioning and LLMs: A Personalized AI-Based Nutrition System
authors: K.P. Swain, Soumya R. Nayak, Santosh K. Swain, Janmenjoy Nayak
abstract: Accurately monitoring diet remains a challenge, especially for home-cooked or restaurant meals that do not carry nutritional labels and are difficult to record using conventional calorie-counting apps. Most existing tools rely on manual entry or barcode scanning, which are time-consuming and often inaccurate. To address these limitations, this study introduces an AI-powered calorie tracking system that combines image captioning (BLIP model) with large language models (Groq LLM via LangChain) to automatically recognize food items, estimate their nutritional values, and generate personalized feedback. Users can simply upload a photo of a meal or enter food names, and the system provides calorie and nutrient breakdowns, daily summaries, and fitness suggestions when caloric excess is detected. Reports are automatically compiled into a downloadable PDF for long-term monitoring. The system demonstrated strong performance, achieving a precision of 92.4%, a recall of 91.1%, and an F1-score of 91.7% in food recognition, with an AUC of 0.95 for nutritional estimation. Case studies confirmed its ability to handle multi-item meals, correctly identify key nutrients, and provide contextual health advice. To ensure transparency and trust, the framework incorporates Explainable AI (XAI) techniques such as Grad-CAM, SHAP, LIME, and EDGE, which help users and healthcare professionals understand how predictions are generated. By reducing manual effort, adapting to food variability, and offering real-time, interpretable insights, this system provides a practical, reliable, and user-friendly solution for personal health management, chronic disease care, and commercial fitness applications.

title: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: A Hybrid Thermal and Machine Learning Approach for Predicting Temperature Rise in Low-Voltage Switchgear
authors: Alesandre Alves, Paulo Sérgio Sausen, Mauricio De Campos, Airam Sausen, Adalberto Jose Rossa, Jordan Passinato Sausen
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid thermoelectric and data-driven methodology to predict temperature rise in Low-Voltage Electrical Switchgear Panels (LVESP). This technical approach serves as a robust alternative to overcome the limitations of the IEC 60890 standard, particularly regarding the detailed analysis of localized heating in specific internal components, such as copper busbars and electrical connections. By integrating physical thermoelectric formulations with machine learning techniques, the method streamlines the sizing process and optimizes thermal performance. Furthermore, this approach significantly reduces the reliance on complex three-dimensional simulation software, lowering computational costs. Unlike conventional analytical methods that estimate only the average temperature increase of internal air, the proposed approach enables localized thermal predictions for copper busbars and electrical connections. A physical prototype was developed not only to replicate real-world operating conditions but also to allow systematic variation of key thermal parameters such as ventilation modes, surface painting of bars, and the configuration of busbar arrangements, enabling the creation of diverse training scenarios for the predictive model. Experimental data were collected and combined with thermoelectric formulations to compute the effective thermal exchange coefficient, which was then used to train machine learning models. The resulting model accurately predicts spatially resolved temperature rises in different panel configurations, with a mean absolute error below 2% and relative error under 3%. The complete dataset is publicly available to foster replication and further development. This methodology establishes a generalizable framework for thermal analysis in industrial panel design, enhancing safety, efficiency, and design flexibility.

title: Effective Electromagnetic Degrees of Freedom in Backscatter MIMO Systems
authors: Philipp Del Hougne
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: While the quantification of the multiplexing gain in a static linear multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) system in terms of its effective electromagnetic degrees of freedom (EEMDOFs) is well established, the counterpart for a backscatter MIMO (BS-MIMO) system is so far missing. A BS-MIMO system encodes the input information into the loads of backscatter elements. Due to mutual coupling, the mapping from load configuration to observed fields is fundamentally non-linear, which complicates the analysis of BS-EEMDOFs. We introduce a definition of BS-EEMDOFs based on the Jacobian of the observed fields with respect to the load configuration. We derive a closed-form expression from multiport network theory which demonstrates that the number of BS-EEMDOFs is fundamentally a random variable, whose distribution depends on the mutual coupling between the backscatter elements, the considered distribution of load configurations, and the coherent illumination. The modes associated with BS-EEMDOFs lie in the column space of the end-to-end channel matrix from backscatter array ports to receiver ports, but the number of BS-EEMDOFs is generally different from the number of benchmark EEMDOFs associated with the same array being coherently fed rather than tunably terminated. The dependence on the coherent illumination yields optimized coherent illumination as a control knob for the number of BS-EEMDOFs. We present numerical and experimental results for the evaluation and optimization of the number of BS-EEMDOFs in different radio environments with large reconfigurable intelligent surfaces, including an example in which the mean of the number of BS-EEMDOFs (with optimized coherent illumination) exceeds the number of conventional EEMDOFs. Our work provides an electromagnetically consistent way of quantifying and optimizing the multiplexing gain in BS-MIMO systems.

title: VLMS: Verifiable Lattice-Based Encryption With Multi-Keyword Search in Cloud Storage
authors: Na Wang, Wen Zhou, Jingjing Wang, Junsong Fu, Jianwei Liu, Bharat K. Bhargava
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the rapid development of cloud storage, many companies and individuals have stored encrypted files containing sensitive data in the cloud. However, the attacks with quantum computing technologies lead to complete exposure of private data. Therefore, many lattice-based Searchable Encryption (SE) schemes have been proposed to resist attacks from quantum computers. Yet, existing lattice-based SE schemes pay little attention to the honesty of cloud servers and multi-keyword search requirements. In this paper, we propose Verifiable Lattice-based encryption with Multi-keyword Search (VLMS) based on the lattice. Firstly, we design a keyword-matching matrix for more general multi-keyword search scenarios. This matrix realizes the rapid calculation of similarity scores, which makes multi-keyword search more flexible. Secondly, based on the keyword-matching matrix, we design an encryption algorithm based on the lattice to improve the security of the scheme. In addition, in order to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the search results, we design a verification algorithm to verify the search results at one time, which greatly improves the verification efficiency. Moreover, we give the extensible VLMS scheme to support scenarios with an increasing number of system keywords, thereby enhancing the scheme's extensibility. Finally, we prove that VLMS is secure and efficient through formal analysis and simulation experiments.

title: Adaptive Robust Planner-Attacker-Operator Model Against Multistage Uncertain Malicious Attacks for Power Systems With Volatile Wind Power
authors: Min Du, Xin Zhang, Josep M. Guerrero
abstract: Line hardening is regarded as a critical defense measure against malicious attacks in power systems. However, this defense strategy often ignores the uncertainty and multistage nature of malicious attacks. Moreover, with high wind power penetration in power system operation, the inherent volatility of wind power may compromise the performance of the defense strategy against malicious attacks. To address such issues, this article proposes an adaptive robust planner-attacker-operator (AR-PAO) model that accounts for both volatile wind power and multistage uncertain malicious attacks within a line hardening framework. A novel solution algorithm is then developed to solve the AR-PAO model for the optimal defense strategy, in which it improves computational efficiency. Comparative simulation results on two IEEE Reliability Test Systems validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: An Innovative Compact Gysel Power Divider With 1000:1 Bandwidth Ratio for HF, VHF, and UHF Receiver Applications
authors: Manoj Kumar, Gowrish Basavarajappa
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents the design and development of a compact two-way Gysel power divider for high frequency (HF), very high frequency (VHF), and ultra high frequency (UHF) receiver applications. The proposed Gysel power divider achieves the miniaturization by employing ferrite-core transformers and ensures the reliable wideband operation with 1000:1 bandwidth. To validate the proposed concept, a prototype is designed, fabricated, and measured over the frequency range from 1 to 1000 MHz. The fabricated prototype exhibits a measured return loss better than 16.3 dB and isolation exceeding 18 dB over the band. The peak insertion loss is 0.8 dB, resulting in a peak combining efficiency of 84%. The average combining efficiency is better than 74% over the HF, VHF, and UHF bands. The measured amplitude and phase imbalances (PIs) are better than 0.4 dB and 0.3°, respectively, over the entire band of operation. Furthermore, no nonlinearity is observed upto an input drive of 20 dBm.

title: A Dual-Purpose Framework for Backdoor Defense and Backdoor Amplification in Diffusion Models
authors: Vu Tuan Truong, Long Bao Le
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Diffusion models have emerged as state-of-the-art generative frameworks, excelling in producing high-quality multi-modal samples. However, recent studies have revealed their vulnerability to backdoor attacks, where backdoored models generate specific, undesirable outputs called backdoor target (e.g., harmful images) when a pre-defined trigger is embedded to their inputs. In this paper, we propose PureDiffusion, a dual-purpose framework that simultaneously serves two contrasting roles: backdoor defense and backdoor attack amplification. For defense, we introduce two novel loss functions to invert backdoor triggers embedded in diffusion models. The first leverages trigger-induced distribution shifts across multiple timesteps of the diffusion process, while the second exploits the denoising consistency effect when a backdoor is activated. Once an accurate trigger inversion is achieved, we develop a backdoor detection method that analyzes both the inverted trigger and the generated backdoor targets to identify backdoor attacks. In terms of attack amplification with the role of an attacker, we describe how our trigger inversion algorithm can be used to reinforce the original trigger embedded in the backdoored diffusion model. This significantly boosts attack performance while reducing the required backdoor training time. Experimental results demonstrate that PureDiffusion achieves near-perfect detection accuracy, outperforming existing defenses by a large margin, particularly against complex trigger patterns. Additionally, in attacking scenarios, our attack amplification approach elevates the attack success rate (ASR) of existing backdoor attacks to nearly 100% while reducing training time by up to 20×.

title: Multi-View Asymmetric Self-Distillation for Chest X-Ray Vision-Language Understanding
authors: Zheng Huang, Mingfan Qiu, Haifan Wu, Wei Xing, Meng Pang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: In chest X-ray imaging, multi-view data such as postero-anterior (PA) and lateral views offer complementary spatial context. However, current chest X-ray vision-language models (VLMs) predominantly depend on single PA views, failing to leverage the critical spatial contextualization inherent in lateral views. Concurrently, heterogeneous reporting styles in clinical texts introduce semantic ambiguity, severely compromising semantic coherence. To address these dual limitations in spatial context integration and semantic coherence, we propose a MultiView Asymmetric Self-Distillation framework. This framework integrates multi-view images and textual reports via crossview contrastive learning, bridging spatial context and semantic coherence to achieve robust cross-modal feature alignment. Furthermore, the point-to-point image-text pairing of Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training erroneously treats intra-batch semantically homogeneous samples as negative pairs, inducing semantic inconsistency. To mitigate this, we design a Crossmodal Instance Similarity Learning module that explicitly aligns cross-modal feature correlations, thereby enhancing semantic consistency. Superior performance of the proposed method is validated through extensive testing, showing leading outcomes in zero-shot classification as well as image-text retrieval.

title: Data-Driven E-Commerce Talent Training: Multi-Dimensional Feature Analysis and Machine Learning Optimization Strategy
authors: Changwei Yang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: In the context of rapid digital economic development, e-commerce has become a crucial engine driving global trade growth and industrial upgrading. However, current ecommerce education still faces issues such as outdated curriculum systems, weak practical teaching, and monolithic evaluation methods, making it difficult to effectively cultivate versatile talents that meet industry needs. We analyze multidimensional teaching data using machine learning techniques to optimize ecommerce education. This study analyzes data collected from a cohort of 1,500 students specializing in e-commerce, crossborder e-commerce, and live-streaming e-commerce at Zhejiang Dongfang Polytechnic, covering six characteristics, including professional competence, digital skills, and practical abilities. The Support Vector Machine (SVM) model, optimized via the Artificial Bee Colony (ABC) algorithm, was used to assess student performance. Experiments show that the ABC-SVM model has a higher accuracy rate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(98.13 {\%})$</tex> compared to other models, while ablation experiments reveal the critical role of practical ability, international perspective, and innovation and entrepreneurship ability in predicting student performance. The results of this study demonstrate the value of data-driven methods in optimizing e-commerce education, achieving personalized teaching, and improving teaching effectiveness, providing an innovative perspective and practical framework for course design and talent cultivation in e-commerce programs.

title: A Low-Loss and Low-Dispersion Anti-Resonant Fiber with Multi-Ring Nested Structure
authors: Qin Mei, Yihan Chen, Jingbo Yang, Min Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Photonics and Applied Technology (IPAT)
abstract: Here we design a low-loss and low-dispersion multi-ring nested antiresonant fibre, and we analyse the signal transmission performance of it by numerical simulation. To realize both low-loss and low-dispersion, we combine the gap compensation technology and the fibre antiresonant structure to design this fibre. The simulation results show that the fibre has a loss of 9.839 × 10 <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup> at 1550 nm wavelength, demonstrating superior loss performance compared to other conventional fibre structures. Meanwhile, the dispersion is maintained within the range of 1 ± 1 ps/(nm · km), effectively suppressing dispersion effects and meeting the requirements of high-speed communication systems. Numerical simulations confirm the excellent performance of the proposed fiber structure in terms of loss and dispersion control, offering new insights and solutions for fiber design in future communication systems.

title: Design and Analysis of 6T SRAM using MTCMOS and Adaptive Body Biasing (ABB) for Low-Power, High-Performance Operation at 45 nm in Cadence Virtuoso
authors: Arshika Soni, Vaibhav Sharma, Akkala Subbarao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This study explores the design and evaluation of 6T SRAM cell implemented at the 45 nm technology node using Multi-Threshold CMOS (MTCMOS) and Adaptive Body Biasing (ABB) methods. The architecture integrates highthreshold devices to curb leakage, while retaining low-threshold access transistors for speed. Simulations were conducted in Cadence Virtuoso utilizing the gpdk045 PDK. ABB dynamically modifies the body bias voltage to optimize delay and power. The results clearly show a major drop in leakage power and better speed versus standard MTCMOS SRAM. This proves the high effectiveness of the combined approach for creating powerefficient memory in advanced submicron design.

title: Improving Wind Power Dispatchability Through High-Temperature Thermal and Compressed Air Energy Storage
authors: Xuecen Zhang, Sunku Prasad Jenne, Oleh Kiselychnyk, Junying Wei, Zhengyi Li, Long Chang, Jihong Wang
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: The uncertainty in wind power generation poses a significant challenge to the power system's stability. Compressed air energy storage (CAES) is an advancing longer-duration energy storage technology suitable for aiding grid-scale integration of uncertain wind energy. This study investigates a novel High-temperature Thermal and Compressed Air Energy Storage (Hi-CAES) system, and co-locates it with wind power generation. A multi-stage distributionally robust optimisation (MDRO) method is proposed to schedule the Hi-CAES to improve wind power dispatchability. The optimisation model seeks to maximise the system revenue and provide a more stable power output for the grid. A case study is carried out on the UK regional 45-bus system. Simulation results indicate that the Hi-CAES system and MDRO method eliminate the uncertainty in power output when wind power is at rated capacity, and reduce the uncertainty by 79.8% when wind power capacity is twice the rated value.

title: From Volatility to Stability: Market Clearing Trends and Renewable Integration in Indian Real-Time Electricity Market
authors: Nandan Rajesh, K GopiKrishna, Harikrishnan Miraj Chathankandath, Sayooj Ramachandran, Anu G Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The Indian Real-Time Electricity Market (RTM) plays a pivotal role in balancing supply-demand mismatches and integrating intermittent renewable energy sources. This study presents a comprehensive empirical analysis of RTM behaviour between April 2022 and June 2025, covering Market Clearing Volume (MCV), Final Scheduled Volume (FSV), Market Clearing Price (MCP), and bidding activity across hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, seasonal, and fiscal-year timescales. Results reveal a steady growth in cleared volumes from ≈ 2800 MW in 2022 to >5200 MW in 2025, coupled with a structural decline in MCP from Indian Rupee (INR) 560 /MWh to below INR 4000/MWh, indicating improved supply adequacy and market competitiveness. Supply-side dominance emerged post mid-2023, largely driven by increased renewable penetration during solar hours. Seasonal variations are evident, with Monsoon and Summer exhibiting the highest cleared volumes, while Autumn records the lowest. These findings provide critical insights for operational scheduling, renewable integration, and market-based dispatch policies.

title: Ensemble Learning Model Based on CatBoost Algorithm for Multi-Fault Prediction in Optical Networks
authors: Rohmat Tulloh, Nana Rachmana Syambas, Eueung Mulyana, Adam Mulyono
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study proposes a CatBoost-based multifault prediction system for fiber optic cables, integrated within a web platform designed for predictive maintenance and educational purposes. The system employs advanced machine learning methods, particularly the CatBoost algorithm, to classify distinct fault types in optical cables. Both reflective faults arising from discontinuities such as breaks or connectors that cause light reflection and non-reflective faults involve signal losses such as bends or splices without light reflection are addressed. The developed model demonstrates an accuracy of 90.87%, a sensitivity of 90.87%, and an F1-score of 90.84%. In addition to delivering precise predictions, the platform functions as an interactive educational resource, featuring a catalog defining fiber optic components. Technicians may either manually input data or upload Excel files for fault prediction and receive guidance on required network maintenance. Collectively, these features position the system as a comprehensive tool for fiber optic network management.

title: Autonomous Field-Monitoring Rover for Early Detection of Cattle Health Anomalies Using Yolov8 and Thermal Imaging
authors: Samriddha Roy, Kankana Karmakar, Subhabrata Banerjee, Soumik Kumar Kundu, Malay Gangopadhyaya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Health monitoring in cattle in most cattle rearing systems is done manually by visual inspection, which is inefficient in larger or open-field settings. Accordingly, symptoms of FMD and LSD are often overlooked in their early stages due to this system, contributing to losses and increased cattle risks. The paper describes an autonomous cattle health anomaly sentinel robot developed to monitor cattle in their early stages. The robots are equipped with an RGB camera and an IR thermal imaging sensor. The YOLOv8 model is used to analyze visual symptoms such as skin lesions, posture, and injuries, with thermal imaging used to analyze surface temperature variations indicative of high temperatures (due to sickness or heat stress), as well as estrous cycles. The results showed that it achieved an mAP@0.5 of 0.88 and thermal accuracy of 89% in farm environment.

title: A Purposed Framework to Enhance Breast Cancer Imaging Using GANs: Enhancing Data Augmentation, Image Quality and Tumor Segmentation
authors: Ram Kumar Yadav, Manoj Kumar, Santosh Kumar Verma, Pratik Krishna, Rahul Moriwal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: All the recent developments in the use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for breast cancer imaging are aimed at improving diagnostic accuracy and treatment strategies. Breast cancer is among the types of cancers that affect women and cause mortality worldwide. Detection and diagnosis have become quite an issue to survival chances, but many recent applications like X-ray mammograms, ultrasound, or magnetic resonance imaging have their problems including unclear images and outlining tumors and less data to work with it. These challenges can be solved through GANs. A new framework for breast cancer imaging is presented that utilizes different GANs architectures in addressing important issues regarding breast cancer imaging. The significant steps of the presented framework include a type of GAN that is particularly beneficial for doing data augmentation is CycleGANs. It can synthesize mammograms without the need for paired images, enabling augmentation of the available data set. Small and unbalanced datasets raise many challenges that sometimes hinder image processing techniques on mammograms. The features in images with low resolutions can be observed more clearly and diagnostic features can be better visualized through improvement by Super Resolution GANs. Tumor segmentation is also defined by conditional GANs that produce very accurate and high-quality masks that outline the boundaries of tumors, allowing for effective treatment planning. This would mean identifying parameters that maximize the potential of GANs technologies, not only in breast cancer diagnosis and management but also in other areas.

title: Text Segmentation Strategies for Low-Resource Language Documents: A Study on Thai Government Texts in RAG
authors: Prawit Boonmee, Veerapat Sa-ard-aiem, Jakkapong Namwong, Pichitchai Hiranpanit, Phatcharin Butsing, Sarawoot Kongyoung, Chaianun Damrongrat
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: The advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has led to increased adoption of question-answering systems. However, context management, particularly text chunking, remains challenging for Thai documents, which represent a low-resource language without clear word boundaries, and government documents using formal language make text segmentation particularly difficult. This research investigates optimal chunking strategies for Thai government documents in RAG systems by analyzing documents from 3 government agencies with different formats: (1) documents with consistent structure, (2) documents with lengthy continuous content, and (3) documents with diverse structural formats. We conducted experiments using 4 chunking methods: (1) Fixed-Size Recursive chunking in Small, Medium, and Large sizes, and (2) Semantic chunking using the BAAI/bge-m3 model. The evaluation is performed using MRR, Recall@k, Precision@K, and NDCG@K metrics, and a benchmark dataset for Thai official documents was also created.

title: Rootkit and Bootkit Detection Using TEE–TPM Architecture with Machine Learning and Blockchain Verification
authors: Natchanan Koson, Chonnawee Udompongpipat, Teerachot Khusuwan, Somchart Fugkeaw
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Rootkits and bootkits represent one of the most dangerous classes of malware due to their ability to compromise system integrity at the lowest levels of the operating system. Traditional detection approaches such as signaturebased antivirus or heuristic scanning often fail against these threats because they hide within trusted processes, modify kernel structures, and manipulate the boot process. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a novel detection system that combines Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), Trusted Platform Modules (TPM), blockchain-based integrity verification, and Machine learning-driven anomaly detection. The system ensures tamper-resistant runtime monitoring, immutable logging of system integrity values, and adaptive detection of unknown rootkits. Experimental evaluation demonstrates the feasibility of this approach in simulated Linux environments, achieving superior detection accuracy and resilience compared to conventional methods.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Tour Guide Explanation Styles
authors: Eiko Yamamoto, Hitoshi Isahara
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study aims to clarify the characteristics and structure of explanation styles used by tour guides. We collected free-text answers from 33 professional tour guides working in the Okayama region of Japan and conducted both qualitative and quantitative analyses focusing on explanation perspectives (factual description, background explanation, narratives, visitor-oriented expression, and affective expression) and the amount of information provided. The results reveal clear differences in explanation styles among guides, and that the inclusion of background explanations and narratives significantly increases the information volume. The explanation styles also varied depending on the question type: historical and cultural questions tended to prompt longer and deeper explanations. These findings demonstrate that explanation by tour guides is not simple information transmission, but a cognitive act of value construction that gives meaning to tourist sites. Furthermore, the analytical framework developed in this study provides practical implications for guide training and evaluation. This study contributes a new approach to visualizing the structural characteristics of tour guide explanation behavior.

title: The Smart Farming: Eggplant Leaf Disease Detection using Fine-Tuned DenseNet201 Deep Learning Model
authors: Jatin Sharma, Jayapal Lande, Suhail Vij, Kartikeya Raina, Neha Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: In most developing nations, farmers rely on eggplant which is easily affected by various leaf-related diseases. Early and proper identification of these diseases will aid in provision of food, sustainable farming and lowering of costs. An accurate fine-tuning of DenseNet201 and a deep learning approach have enabled researchers to distinguish between the eggplant leaves status of healthy, insect pest disease, leaf spot disease, mosaic virus disease, small leaf disease, white mold disease and wilt disease. In total, 3552 images of the open-source Kaggle platform were chosen and separated into training, validation and testing subsets in this proportion: 70, 15 and 15 percent. The proposed system achieved an accuracy of $86 \%$ on test data and good scores in precision, recall and F1-scores on the majority of classes. The training curves and analysis of the confusion matrices indicates clearly that the model is firm and error rate is small. Plant pathology with artificial intelligence can contribute to a quicker resolution of plant diseases, responsible eating habits, and combats climate change and hunger. The study meets the goals of sustainable development by helping to improve the efficiency of farming, by promoting new ideas to find solutions, efficient utilization of resources. Farmers and agriculturalists are able to detect diseases at an earlier stage, use reduced amounts of pesticides, and get higher yields through the recommended approach.

title: Intelligent Home Energy Management with Hybrid Energy Storage and Renewable Integration Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wei-Hung Huang, Ren-Shiou Liu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: Global energy consumption is a major driver of greenhouse gas emissions, primarily due to the heavy reliance on fossil fuels in utility grids. To mitigate this impact, more and more home energy management systems (HEMS) integrate energy storage systems (ESS) with renewable energy sources (RES) to increase RES utilization and reduce dependence on the grid. This paper proposes an optimized HEMS based on deep reinforcement learning to minimize energy costs under real-time pricing schemes. The proposed approach employs a deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG) agent that manages a hybrid energy storage system by dynamically responding to pricing signals in real time. Experimental results demonstrate that the DDPG-based model not only achieves greater cost savings compared to a heuristic baseline, but also reduces ESS utilization across all units, thereby preventing excessive usage and highlighting the method's efficiency and adaptability.

title: Facile Synthesis of Reduced Graphene Oxide Using L-Ascorbic Acid for Biomedical Application
authors: Thi Nhat Linh Phan, Thi Xuan Nguyen, Thi Thuy Truong, Thi Kim Ngan Duong, Vu Hoang Minh Doan, Jaeyeop Choi, Jae Sung Ahn, Junghwan Oh, Sudip Mondal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Graphene-based nanomaterials, including graphene oxide (GO) and reduced graphene oxide (rGO), hold significant promise for biomedical applications due to their exceptional physicochemical properties. In this study, GO was synthesized via the Modified Hummers' Method and subsequently reduced using L-ascorbic acid, a biocompatible, eco-friendly alternative to conventional toxic reducing agents like hydrazine. Comprehensive characterization using UV-Vis, XRD, FT-IR, and Raman confirmed effective oxidation and reduction, highlighting the restoration of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{s p}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> carbon structure. A Prussian blue-rGO (PB-rGO) nanocomposite was then fabricated through electrostatic and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\pi}-{\pi}$</tex> interactions between rGO and Prussian blue (PB). The composite exhibited excellent aqueous dispersibility and structural stability. Its photodynamic therapeutic (PDT) potential was evaluated on MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells under red LED irradiation. Cytotoxicity and cell viability assays demonstrated significant cancer cell destruction upon light exposure, confirming the synergistic effect of PB and rGO in enhancing photochemical reactivity. The results emphasize the scalability and safety of L-ascorbic acid-based reduction and validate PB-rGO as a promising nanoplatform for cancer-specific PDT applications.

title: A Deep Neural Network and Savitzky-Golay Filter Based Model to Predict the Current-Voltage Characteristics of Feedback FETs
authors: Subir Das, Mihir Sahu, Sai Shirov Katta, Pramod Kumar Tiwari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: In this paper, we present a Deep Neural Network (DNN) to predict the current-voltage characteristics for feedback field-effect transistor (FBFET) devices. A Savitzky-Golay filter is used to remove noise from the output curve of the DNN and to make the transition in the predicted IV curve steeper. We fetch important device parameters like voltage threshold ($\mathrm{V}_{\text {th }}$) and ON-current ($\mathrm{I}_{\text {on }}$) with low deviation from actual values. We also compare the performance of DNN model with various regression models like Random Forest Regression (RFR) and Support Vector Regression (SVR) to demonstrate that our proposed model is superior to these methods. We train our model with a small data set of TCAD simulations and obtain high accuracy. Our work shows that a DNN along with a Savitzky-Golay filter can be used to understand sharp-switching device characteristics, even without a complete knowledge of the underlying physics. This can prove to be crucial for the design optimization of novel devices.

title: Enhanced Sliding Mode Control for Precise Speed Regulation in PMSG Wind Turbines: A Comparative Study with PID Control
authors: G Guna, V Anusharani, D Sarathkumar, Raymon Antony Raj, Leo John Baptist Andrews, Annamalai Alagappan, Sampath Kumar Venkatachary Grant, A Sheik Sidthik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper primarily aims to present a sliding mode control strategy for regulating the speed of a surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous generator (PMSG). The riddles of energy are whispered by the wind, yet harnessing them requires greater complexity than force. Efficiency and stability are hindered by traditional PID controllers, which struggle against the wind’s inconsistent nature like stroppy cinches. However, a ground-breaking sliding mode controller is introduced, offering a completely new approach. A path is carved with an ideal rotor speed, and a sliding surface is created to shield the turbine from erratic gusts of wind. With every gust, the error is anticipated, and appropriate corrections are made by the controller, ensuring that the turbine glides efficiently in a seamless cinch. A work of clean power is released by the sliding mode controller, optimizing every turn and allowing smooth integration into the grid, in contrast to the slow adjustments of the PID. This is not merely a theoretical advancement; simulations have been conducted to analyse every movement, ensuring the flawless execution of the system’s performance. The transformative regularity of the sliding mode controller is embraced, pushing beyond the limitations of past approaches. In this pursuit of sustainable energy, the wind is recognized as one of the greatest energy sources. Through this research, the story of energy in the future can be rewritten, where technology, guided by the wind, unlocks its limitless potential and illuminates the world with clean electricity.

title: Design of an Active-Passive Hybrid Vibration Isolator via Piezoelectric-Enhanced Quasi-Zero-Stiffness for Low Frequency Applications
authors: Keyan Huo, Ruinan Mu, Shiyue Zou, Haifeng Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This study proposes an active-passive hybrid vibration isolator using piezoelectric (PZT)-enhanced quasi-zerostiffness (QZS) units. The design features bi-cosine curved beams with PZT actuators replacing central constraints, enabling precise deflection control and leveraging inherent QZS properties for lowfrequency suppression. Theoretical modeling confirms the nonlinear dynamics and QZS behavior. A PID control converts displacement errors into PZT actuation. Simulations demonstrate that PZT intervention significantly reduces vibration transmissibility near resonance across varied conditions. Experiments validate the design: under low-frequency excitation, active control effectively suppresses displacement response and maintains it below critical levels within the resonance band, while passive QZS provides isolation elsewhere. The synergy of passive QZS and active PZT control achieves enhanced low-frequency vibration isolation, validated numerically and experimentally.

title: Flexible Analytic Wavelet Transform based analysis of Stress EEG signals
authors: Vatsal Kashyap, Varun Bajaj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This study presents an analytical framework for EEG-based stress detection utilizing Flexible Analytic Wavelet Transform (FAWT) decomposition and statistical feature evaluation. EEG signals is decomposed into distinct subbands using FAWT, which provides frequency selective representation of neural activity of brain. To capture the signal variability and complexity associated with stress state, a set of time-domain and nonlinear parameters, comprising Mean, Hjorth Activity, Hjorth Mobility, Hjorth Complexity Zero Crossing Rate and Peak-to-Peak amplitude, were extracted from each subband. The significance of the extracted features under stressed and normal conditions are assessed statistically using non-parametric Kruskal Wallis (KW) test. The KW test’s p-value $(p(\lt)0.05)$ is used to find out the statistical significance of extracted features from subbands. The results of proposed method provide the significant features that are useful for discrimination of mental stress using EEG signal.

title: SynergAI: Edge-to-Cloud Synergy for Architecture-Driven High-Performance Orchestration
authors: Foteini Stathopoulou, Aggelos Ferikoglou, Manolis Katsaragakis, Dimosthenis Masouros, Sotirios Xydis, Dimitrios Soudris
publish: ACM Transactions on Embedded Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: A Multi-Parameter Degradation Trend Perception Method for Satellite Components Based on Joint Distribution Shift and Specific Degradation Modeling
authors: Yongchao Hui, Yuehua Cheng, Bin Jiang, Na Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Accurate degradation trend perception is critical for the long-term stability of satellite components. Existing multi-parameter health assessment methods often rely on weighted fusion strategies that overlook parameter coupling and co-evolution, thereby limiting the understanding of complex degradation mechanisms. To address the challenges of coupling, nonlinearity, and distribution shifts in state evolution, this paper proposes a method that integrates joint distribution shift modeling and independent degradation component analysis. Specifically, the Sinkhorn Wasserstein distance is employed to quantify joint distribution shifts among health parameters, enabling sensitive detection of evolving statistical dependencies. In parallel, Independent Component Analysis (ICA) extracts statistically independent intrinsic degradation components, supporting both system-level trend tracking and localized anomaly detection. Experiments on real satellite telemetry data validate the method's capability to accurately characterize degradation evolution, with strong trend sensitivity and structural discernibility, supporting downstream fault diagnosis and remaining useful life prediction.

title: Bias-Aware Resume Screening: A Survey and Analytical Framework Using NLP and Machine Learning
authors: Deepalakshmi Rajendran, Sethupathi Venugopal, C. Shyamala Gowri, N.B. Libbas Khan, R. Krithiga, B. Amirthaa Nikash
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Automated resume screening systems based on Natural Language Processing (NLP) are widely used to support hiring decisions by ranking and filtering candidates. While these systems improve efficiency, they may unintentionally introduce or amplify gender bias through learned language patterns and scoring mechanisms. This paper presents a focused study on the detection and visualization of gender bias in NLP-based automated resume screening systems. A controlled resume screening pipeline is developed using a gender-labeled resume dataset, where NLP-based feature extraction and scoring are applied to simulate candidate evaluation. Visual analytics techniques, including score distribution analysis, keyword influence mapping, and ranking comparison plots, are employed to examine disparities between male and female profiles. The results reveal consistent differences in scoring behavior and ranking outcomes, even when explicit gender identifiers are excluded. The study highlights the importance of bias-aware model design and interpretable analytics and provides a reproducible framework for identifying and visualizing gender bias in automated recruitment systems.
