title: Cattle Breed Classification Using Superpixel-Driven Graph Neural Network
authors: Devansh Bhatnagar, Saurabh Natha Gharat, Manas Choudhari, Rekha Kaushik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Correctly identifying breeds of cattle in India is critical to supporting improved livestock management practices, facilitating selective breeding practices, and optimizing dairy production efficiency. The aim of this article is to describe the development of an automated framework utilized for breed identification. This was based on the application of image processing and deep learning models—the framework made use of preprocessing of the cattle images with segmenting techniques labelled as Simple Linear Iterative Clustering (SLIC) and color enhancement algorithms in order to improve the quality of the cattle images while preserving the natural visibility of the breed—as shown in the original cattle images. The features extracted using the processing techniques were then provided as input to a ResNet-based feature extractor to form deep visual representations from the images. The visual representations were provided to a Graph Neural Network (GNN) that was able to model the complex spatial relationships of segmented regions of the cattle to improve recognition accuracy. The model was trained and tested on labeled cattle breed images for a total of 30 epochs with a consistent accuracy across the epochs and fast convergence. This research has demonstrated the successful integration of SLIC, ResNet, and GNN components into an automated breed classification framework that can provide a reliable and scalable automated breed identification component of smart livestock management systems.

title: An Anti-Interference and Online Re-Detection UAV Visual Object Tracking Algorithm Based on Correlation Filtering
authors: Wei Wang, Yanjie Pu, Xuming Wang, Pengfei Zhang, Jianxin Zhao, Guohua Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems in UAV visual object tracking, such as environmental interference like occlusion, similar objects, inaccurate estimation of object spatial position information, and the accumulation of tracking errors caused by changes in object tracking state to the extent that the tracking task fails, an anti-interference and online re-detection UAV visual object tracking algorithm is proposed. An anti-interference correlation filter model is constructed, and the object is processed in multiple blocks and described using multiple features to distinguish object information and resist environmental interference, thereby enhancing tracking stability. An antiinterference object multi-blocks fusion strategy is designed to perform multi-blocks response fusion, effectively utilizing global and local detail information of the object to achieve accurate estimation of the object spatial position information. A plug-andplay online re-detection module is constructed to accurately determine and promptly correct the object tracking status, thereby reducing the accumulation of errors in the tracking process and maintaining the stability of the algorithm and the continuity of tracking. Through experimental analysis on DTB70, UAV123_10fps, and UAVDT datasets, the algorithm can effectively resist environmental interference and maintain object tracking status, with efficient, real-time, and relatively accurate tracking effects.

title: Design of a Real-Time Focus and Posture Monitoring System Using Facial Recognition and Machine Learning
authors: Saurabh Yadav, Udit Kumar Shrivastava, Saurabh Agrawal, Aditya Mishra, Rashim Rana
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: As more and more students use online examinations, it has become a major concern how to make sure students are engaged and the integrity of the academic activity is upheld. To help in this matter, we are presenting a computer vision based system that could monitor the sitting posture and the attention level of a student in real time using only a conventional web camera. In addition to analyzing upper-body posture (such as head tilt and slouching) to identify indications of pain or disengagement, the system looks at gaze direction and facial orientation to assess whether the learner is gazing at the screen. Importantly, this method allows for scale deployment because it uses commodity technology and no extra sensors. Our dualmonitoring architecture offers deeper information for identifying possible cheating and inattention, non contrast to previous techniques that simply manage gaze or posture or depend on several devices. We demonstrate how combining posture and gaze clues improves online proctoring by producing a more accurate measure of participation. By warning teachers about both inattentive behavior and bad posture, this portable webcam-based gadget can promote exam integrity and the welfare of students.

title: Influence of Acid Etching on the Surface Characteristics and Cell Attachment of Titanium Implants
authors: Pham Hong Trang, Nguyen Duc Hung, Hoang Van Vuong, Truong Quoc Phong, Nguyen Phuc Duong, Nguyen Hoang Viet, Vuong-Hung Pham
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Cell-surface interactions play an important role in osseointegration, implant lifetime, and immune response, thereby influencing the application of titanium (Ti) implants. This study focuses on acid etching with controlled concentrations and processing times to modify the microsurface roughness, enhance hydrophilic properties, and control cell attachment to Ti implants. The surface morphology of Ti was characterized using scanning electron microscopy, and surface roughness was evaluated using optical microscopy. The hydrophilic properties of Ti were examined through contact angle measurements. The results showed that the micro-surface roughness of Ti increased from approximately 1 to 10µm with high uniformity, and the hydrophilic properties of Ti were also enhanced. XRD and EDS data confirmed the purity of the Ti surface, indicating the absence of contaminants after surface modification. In vitro cell tests revealed that surfaces with high uniformity in roughness promoted better cell attachment compared to surfaces with random roughness. These results suggest that the current acid etching process is suitable for surface modification of Ti applications in biomedical implants.

title: Uncertainty Analysis Methods for Multi-dimensional Data Fusion in Flight Tests
authors: Wanyi Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The development of modern aviation technology has made flight test multi-dimensional sensor data complex, highdimensional, and uncertain, making it difficult for traditional methods to cope. This paper proposes a multi-dimensional data fusion uncertainty analysis method based on machine learning. Through intelligent preprocessing, adaptive feature selection, and integrated classification models, it can accurately identify six typical fault modes and construct a multi-category system containing normal states and five types of faults. To address data imbalance, an improved SMOTE oversampling and random undersampling combination strategy was adopted, optimizing the ratio from 10.0:1 to 2.8:1; 160 key features were selected to achieve 60% dimensionality reduction; and random forest was determined to be the optimal classification algorithm. Our experiments show that this method improves the classification accuracy from 35.3% to 72.4%, and it achieves an F1 score of 71.8% with a training time of only 2.3 seconds-traits that demonstrate its excellent real-time performance in practical scenarios. When we break down the results by category, we find the F1 scores for normal conditions and engine faults reach 87.5% and 79.3%, respectively. This method ensures high-precision identification of major fault types while maintaining acceptable detection capabilities for minor faults, and these combined strengths offer technical support for both flight safety monitoring and predictive maintenance.

title: Machine learning-based co-design of advanced flipchip packages: co-optimization of package warpage, die stress, solder joint reliability, thermal management, and signal integrity_supp1-3674462.docx
authors: Mohammad Rafiee
abstract: Advanced flip-chip packages must satisfy thermal limits, chip–package interaction constraints, solder-joint reliability, and increasingly tight signal-integrity (SI) margins. These requirements are strongly coupled. A design change that improves one metric can degrade another. Prior studies often optimize thermal or thermo-mechanical metrics in isolation, or treat SI separately from package reliability. A unified workflow for concurrent thermal–mechanical–electrical screening at the package-architecture stage is still limited. This paper presents an ML-assisted co-design workflow for advanced flip-chip packages. The framework combines thermo-mechanical FEA, a steady-state thermal model, and full-wave EM analysis. Six objectives are considered jointly: junction-to-ambient thermal resistance (ΘJA), package warpage (Wpkg), maximum principal stress in the ELK/BEOL region (σELK), viscoplastic work-based fatigue indicators for first-level bumps and second-level BGAs (ΔWbump, ΔWBGA), and a differential insertion-loss metric (ILDD21, derived from SDD21). A Latin Hypercube Sampling spans key material, geometry, and thermal-stack parameters. SI is evaluated using discrete, manufacturable substrate-core options anchored by EM simulations. Random Forest and XGBoost surrogates are trained (5-fold cross-validation) for the thermo-mechanical and thermal objectives and embedded in NSGA-II to generate Pareto-optimal trade-offs. The Net Flow Method is then used to rank Pareto candidates and identify a transparent “champion” design, which is re-simulated as a physics-based check. The workflow enables rapid and explainable exploration of coupled trade-offs. It also provides a practical screening method for early-stage flip-chip package architecture selection under mixed thermal, reliability, and SI constraints.

title: Integrating Sentiment and Emotion Awareness into Semantic Recommendations with Minimal Embedding Augmentation
authors: Samik Kundu, Sayantan Hazra, Darothi Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Many text-based recommendation systems use embeddings from pretrained language models to measure semantic similarity and determine relevance. However, semantic features alone do not capture affective factors such as sentiment and emotional tone, which are important for user engagement and relevance. This paper presents a modular framework that improves text-based recommendation by augmenting semantic embeddings with sentiment polarity and PAD emotion vectors. Semantic representations are obtained using all-MiniLM-L6-v2, and affective features come from twitter-roberta-base-sentiment and emotion-english-distilroberta-base. These components form a SentimentEmotion Block (SEB) that is concatenated with semantic embeddings to create an affect-aware representation of text. The framework evaluates different SEB configurations using a geometric mean metric that accounts for both sentiment and emotional similarity between recommended items and query texts. A second metric, EmSEB, combines semantic cosine similarity with affect-aware accuracy to quantify improvements over a semantic-only baseline. Experiments on 27,480 tweet samples show that integrating sentiment and emotion information improves affective consistency while preserving semantic relevance. This method provides a generalizable way to introduce affective dimensions at the embedding level and supports future work on sentiment and emotion aware retrieval systems.

title: Ultrasonic Motor Vibration Regulation via Adaptive Model-Free Active Disturbance Rejection Control
authors: Bingxin Xu, Zhike Xu, Long Jin, Zhan Shen, Peiwen Tan
abstract: To address the problem of asymmetric two-phase vibration in ultrasonic motors and overcome the limitations of conventional equal-amplitude excitation methods, this paper proposes a novel adaptive model-free active disturbance rejection control (AMFADRC) strategy for vibration amplitude tracking under model-free and sensorless conditions. Unlike existing approaches, the proposed method estimates the vibration amplitude in real time based on the excitation voltage and current, and adaptively adjusts the control parameters using a compensation coefficient derived from the ratio between the initial and regulated voltages. This eliminates the reliance on accurate system models or external sensors. After voltage regulation, the controller ensures both phases have equal amplitudes to maintain a stable traveling wave. The proposed strategy effectively compensates for steady-state nonlinear disturbances and adapts to variations in motor parameters, ensuring robust and high-precision real-time tracking performance. Extensive comparative simulations and prototype experiments validate the superior capability of the proposed method in suppressing periodic fluctuations and enhancing motor speed stability, demonstrating its practical applicability and robustness. Nevertheless, challenges related to limited sampling precision and sudden severe input faults remain to be addressed in future work.

title: Demand Response Integrated Multi-Area State Estimation in Edge Computing Enabled Active Distribution Networks
authors: Muneeb Mushtaq Sheikh, Sreenath J G, Praveen Tripathy, Ravindranath Adda
abstract: This paper proposes a novel demand response (DR)-aware multi-area state estimation (MASE) framework for active distribution networks (ADNs) within a three-tier edge-computing architecture. The framework transforms DR-induced variations from disturbances into sources of observability enhancement. A price-signal-driven DR forecasting framework is employed to support a dual-channel unscented Kalman filter (UKF) based state estimation approach. In this formulation, DR forecasts are incorporated as deterministic inputs during the prediction stage, while uncertainty-weighted pseudo-measurements are applied in the update stage, significantly improving estimation accuracy under dynamic load conditions. To optimize the deployment architecture, a multi-objective optimization problem (MOP) is formulated to minimize infrastructure cost while maximizing DR management efficiency and communication performance. This MOP is solved using an enhanced non-dominated sorting genetic algorithm-II (NSGA-II) that incorporates a novel DR-guided radial mutation operator. By utilizing network topology and DR coherence metrics, this operator enables superior convergence in high-dimensional search spaces. Validation on the IEEE 123-bus system, augmented with photovoltaic (PV) and DR resources, demonstrates substantial performance improvements. The proposed framework enhances SE accuracy by up to 68% during DR events, accelerates inter-cluster convergence, and minimizes false alarms through residual-based validation.

title: An Improved Flexible Bidirectional Multiport Converter Enabling Enhanced Ultracapacitor Utilization for Electrified Vehicle Powertrains
authors: Siddheswar Sen, Pratim Bhattacharyya, Santu Kumar Giri, Subrata Banerjee, Hanumath Prasad Ikkurti
abstract: To enhance the utilization of ultracapacitor (UC) energy in a battery-UC based hybrid energy storage system (HESS) for electrified vehicle (EV) powertrains, the UC needs to be discharged down to its minimum operating terminal voltage. However, this leads to higher duty cycle operation of the power converter and excessive current at source side. Such conditions hamper the steady operation of the converter and necessitates high current rated magnetic and switching components. Addressing to this challenge, an improved flexible bidirectional multiport converter is proposed, which enables deep discharging of UC without increasing the operating duty cycle or the component ratings of converter, while ensuring flexible power flow among multiple ports. The proposed converter offers the unique feature of energizing the inductor co-operatively through both UC and battery in the same switching cycle, during certain transient events of peak power delivery or regenerative braking energy recuperation. This eventually facilitates in extending the range of UC operation, thereby augmenting the dynamic performance of EV. The flexible operation of the proposed converter along with extended utilization of UC is validated at standard EV load profiles through simulation and further verified by experimentation performed on a laboratory scale hardware prototype.

title: CACM-Net++: FY-4B AGRI Day and Night Cloud Mask Algorithm Modeling_supp1-3670024.docx
authors: Na Xu
abstract: Cloud detection is critical for satellite-based atmospheric observations and subsequent applications. This study presents a deep learning-based cloud mask model, Convolutional Attention Cloud Mask Network Plus Plus (CACM-Net++), developed for both daytime and nighttime cloud detection using multispectral observations from the Advanced Geostationary Radiation Imager (AGRI) onboard the FY-4B geostationary satellite. The model incorporates physically meaningful input features and is independently trained across different satellite zenith angles to better capture radiative characteristics and enhance retrieval accuracy in complex scenes. Cloud labels derived from Cloud-Aerosol Lidar and Infrared Pathfinder Satellite Observation (CALIPSO) observations are used for both training and evaluation. CACM-Net++ achieves over 90% detection accuracy under varying illumination conditions, with particularly strong performance over land and ocean at moderate satellite zenith angles. Compared to the operational cloud mask product from the National Satellite Meteorological Center (NSMC), CACM-Net++ demonstrates significant improvements, especially in detecting clouds over nighttime land areas and at high satellite zenith angles. These gains are primarily attributed to the model’s deep learning architecture and its tailored spatiotemporal partitioning strategy. Independent validation against CALIPSO confirms the model’s robust generalization across surface types. Furthermore, annual comparisons with ERA5 reanalysis data reveal that CACM-Net++ produces more consistent cloud distributions, particularly over nighttime land regions such as Australia. Despite its strong overall performance, the model remains challenged during twilight periods due to the scarcity of reliable reference data. Future work will focus on addressing this limitation by developing specialized algorithms for transitional lighting conditions, advancing the model’s all-sky detection capabilities.

title: Anomaly Resilient Temporal QoS Prediction using Hypergraph Convoluted Transformer Network_supp1-3674650.pdf
authors: Soumi Chattopadhyay
abstract: Quality-of-Service (QoS) prediction is a critical task in the service lifecycle, enabling precise and adaptive service recommendations by anticipating performance variations over time in response to evolving network uncertainties and user preferences. However, contemporary QoS prediction methods frequently encounter data sparsity and cold-start issues, which hinder accurate QoS predictions and limit the ability to capture diverse user preferences. Additionally, these methods often assume QoS data reliability, neglecting potential credibility issues such as outliers and the presence of greysheep users and services with atypical invocation patterns. Furthermore, traditional approaches fail to leverage diverse features, including domain-specific knowledge and complex higher-order patterns, essential for accurate QoS predictions. In this paper, we introduce a real-time, trust-aware framework for temporal QoS prediction to address the aforementioned challenges, featuring an end-to- end deep architecture called the Hypergraph Convoluted Transformer Network (HCTN). HCTN combines a hypergraph structure with graph convolution over hyper-edges to effectively address high-sparsity issues by capturing complex, high-order correlations. Complementing this, the transformer network utilizes multi-head attention along with parallel 1D convolutional layers and fully connected dense blocks to capture both fine-grained and coarse-grained dynamic patterns. Additionally, our approach includes a sparsity-resilient solution for detecting greysheep users and services, incorporating their unique characteristics to improve prediction accuracy. Trained with a robust loss function resistant to outliers, HCTN demonstrated state-of-the-art performance on the large-scale WSDREAM-2 datasets for response time and throughput.

title: Analysis and Design of a Spectral-Shaper-Based RF Self-Interference Canceller for In-Band Full-Duplex Transceivers
authors: Ramasamy Palaniappan, Sankaran Aniruddhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This article presents an effective method to enhance the RF self-interference cancellation (SIC) at higher-order stages through the Spectral-Shaper circuit. The mathematical model of the Spectral-Shaper circuit, along with its simulation and experimental results, is presented. The generic architecture for scaling the canceller stages to higher orders is described. The proposed technique is validated through an experimental prototype built on a printed circuit board at 2.45 GHz, using commercially available off-the-shelf surface-mount components. The prototype comprises two-stages: in the first stage, a variable attenuator and a phase shifter are employed, achieving 22.5 dB of cancellation; the second stage employs a Spectral-Shaper circuit and provides an additional 32.9 dB of cancellation improvement. The prototype canceller was able to suppress a +27 dBm transmit signal by 73.6 dB over a 20-MHz channel bandwidth, exclusively in the RF domain. The canceller IP1dB is +30.1dBm, and the IIP3 at the transmit path and the canceller stage are +53.9 dBm and +42.3 dBm, respectively.

title: Optimal Control of Network Routing under Blameless Constraints: A Convex Approach
authors: Trazon Jimerson, Claus Danielson, Daniel Calderone, Meeko Oishi
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: In disaster scenarios, rescue plans must optimally allocate limited resources to maximize the number of feasible rescues. Full evacuation is often infeasible due to constraints such as limited fuel, time, or transportation capacity, making it crucial that allocations respect pre-established ethical priorities. These priorities are enforced through lexicographically ordered terminal sets. In this paper, we introduce the notion of blamelessness to network routing, which ensures routing plans yield terminal solutions in the highest feasible priority set. We model disaster response as a network routing problem, minimizing fuel usage while satisfying given ethical and physical constraints. First, we derive conditions on the cost function of a linear program (LP) to produce blameless but sub-optimal plans. Next, we formulate a two-stage LP that minimizes network routing cost subject to terminal priority set constraints. Finally, we show that a convex combination of blameless and optimal routing objectives always exists, allowing use of a single convex LP to produce plans that are resource-optimal subject to being blameless. Disaster rescue simulations are used to empirically study the sensitivity of blamelessness and network optimality to the convex-combination parameter. Routes must respect triage-based ethical priorities while minimizing network cost under a finite time horizon, limited route availability, and transportation capacity constraints.

title: Transformer Less Multilevel Inverter for PV System with Ground Connections
authors: Harvinder Singh, Sunny Vig, Sarabjeet Singh, Ikram, Raghav, Deepanshi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: By removing the line-frequency transformer from photovoltaic (PV) inverters, benefits such as reduced size, lower costs, and minimized energy loss can be achieved. However, this omission presents challenges for designers. The lack of isolation between the PV array and the grid creates a potential pathway for common-mode leakage currents via the stray capacitance present in solar panels.[5] Various designs of multilevel inverters have sought to address this problem, but they often replace one issue with another: needing too many switches, large capacitor banks, unstable capacitor voltages, or intricate control systems that are not easily manageable in practical applications. This study investigates an alternative approach. A multilevel inverter is designed with a common-ground configuration, which intentionally links the negative terminal of the photovoltaic (PV) system to the neutral point of the grid, thereby maintaining a stable common-mode voltage. This particular design effectively reduces the main cause of leakage current. To achieve a compact and efficient inverter design, a switchedcapacitor arrangement is implemented, enabling natural voltage enhancement and automatic balancing without the necessity for extra sensors or balancing circuits. This setup raises the input voltage to several stepped levels while requiring a minimal number of power switches, consequently minimizing losses and simplifying control requirements. Tests performed on the prototype confirm the design's effectiveness. Leakage currents are maintained at a minimal level, the switched capacitors reach their desired voltages autonomously, and the AC output demonstrates superb harmonic characteristics. The overall efficiency of conversion exceeds 90 percent under typical operating conditions. These results suggest that the suggested inverter-recognized for its straightforward grounding, fewer switches, built-in voltage enhancement, and dependable capacitor functionalityrepresents a viable option for transformer less photovoltaic systems, whether single-phase or three-phase.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Information Technology Investment on Banking Performance Using the Partial Adjustment Valuation (PAV): Model: Evidence from Indonesia State-Owned Banks
authors: Dita Aprillia Rahmani, Lukman Abdurrahman, Rd Rohmat Saedudin
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Information Technology (IT) investment is a key driver of digital transformation in banking, yet its financial value remains difficult to measure due to intangible benefits and dynamic adjustment processes. This study examines the association between IT capital investment and banking performance in Indonesian state-owned banks using the Partial Adjustment Valuation (PAV) framework. The analysis covers Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BRI), Bank Negara Indonesia (BNI), and Bank Mandiri over the period 2010-2023, using data from annual reports. Two model specifications are estimated using nonlinear regression in SPSS: a three-factor model that includes IT investment and a two-factor model that excludes IT. Banking performance is assessed using Performance Value (PV) and Performance Ratio (PR). The results indicate that models including IT investment consistently yield higher performance values across banks, highlighting the role of digital capacity in supporting long-term banking performance.

title: CNN-LSTM Hybrid for Real Time Sky Image Based Solar Irradiance Forecasting and Weather-Responsive Solar Tracking
authors: Aditya Narayan Dikshit, Rankit Saha, Moupriya Kundu, Monalisa Dey
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Short term solar irradiance is used for forecasting and the integration of systems that are photovoltaic into the modern power grids. Cloud formation movements and the density degrade the conventional Statistical performance, causing rapid fluctuations. Here this paper points out the challenges happening and presents a framework using deep learning. It uses sky images. Here Convolutional Neural Network is used for automatically extracting spacial features from the images of sky. It shows the cloud patterns and conditions of the atmosphere without featuring manually. Extensive experiences being conducted for the analysis of the configuration of the key models, including the depth on network, Rate of learning and strategic training on the performance of forecasting. Experiments represents that the model demonstrated using sky based outperforms traditional and baseline techniques that are Statistical and machine learning based, the proposed architecture gives more accuracy and less error.

title: Design and Development of a Multichannel Nano Ammeter for Measuring Electron Current in E-LINAC of RIB, VECC
authors: Mou Chatterjee, Nabhiraj P. Y.
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: A multichannel nano ammeter has been designed and developed for measuring electron beam current in e-LINAC of Radio Active Ion Beam (RIB) facility in Variable Energy Cyclotron Centre (VECC). This meter is designed to read dc as well as the pulse width modulated beam current from the electron source. It is a four-channel current measuring instrument having a range of 1nA to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex> and a resolution of 1nA over the full range. It gives an accuracy of 2% over full range with 4-line local display for measured current along with a RS232 serial interface with computer for remote data acquisition. In this instrument, a feedback ammeter configuration has been used with appropriate amplifier circuitry to read the beam current. Proper filter arrangement is implemented to eliminate noise from different sources and increase the accuracy of the measurement. Also, suitable measures have been taken to protect the circuitry from any undesirable high voltage transients. For analog data acquisition, 16bit analog to digital converter (ADC) has been used. This ADC communicates with an ARM based single board computer (SBC) over i2C and generates data for the local LCD display and PC interface. This meter has been successfully installed in beam line of electron (e)-LINAC system and being used for beam tuning.

title: Machine Condition Monitoring Using TENG-Based Vibration Sensor
authors: Rithwik Eluri, Karthik Chokkakula, Mallikharjuna Rao K, Rama Rakshith Katta, Achanta Satya Karthik
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: The relentless operation of machines in industries necessitates vigilant monitoring to avert catastrophic failures and substantial financial losses. Neglecting routine checks poses a dire risk, potentially resulting in unforeseen machine break-downs. Existing approaches primarily utilize accelerometers for vibration data collection, necessitating external power sources and presenting limitations in comprehensive fault prediction. Alternative research using Triboelectric Nanogenerators (TENGs) has emerged, but none utilize this technology for machine fault prediction. The present study proposes a new method using a TENG-based sensor system with a sphere-in-sphere structure to detect machine vibrations. The sensor utilizes mechanical vibrations to produce electrical signals, bypassing the need for external power sources and alleviating the challenges faced in prior approaches. Through stringent experimentation and analysis, our suggested method has remarkable fault prediction accuracy. By utilizing logistic regression and Support Vector Machines (SVM), our method has remarkable accuracies of up to about 97 % and 96 %, respectively. This huge accomplishment reflects the effectiveness and capability of our TENG-based sensor system in predicting machine faults accurately, providing a valuable prospect for preventive maintenance strategies and preventing costly failures.

title: MeshSegNet Performance Optimization for 3D Tooth Segmentation by Curvature Feature and Class Weighting
authors: Sutasinee Nizu, Kochakorn Lekvijitada, Nonglak Sombuntham, Chamaiporn Sukjamsri, Theekapun Charoenpong
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: In conventional orthodontic treatment planning for malocclusion, dental arch shapes are typically identified by manually locating reference points on 3D models. The conventional method is time-consuming and highly dependent on the orthodontist's experience. Automatic identification of arch shapes requires tooth segmentation as a critical preliminary step. Recently, deep learning has become a powerful tool for 3D tooth segmentation, with MeshSegNet being one of the most effective architectures. However, MeshSegNet requires optimization of several factors to achieve higher accuracy. This paper proposes a method to improve MeshSegNet performance by using input curvature features and class weighting. The proposed method involves three stages: 1) data preparation and preprocessing, 2) mesh-based feature extraction, and 3) MeshSegNet architecture and training strategy. Effectiveness was validated through five-fold crossvalidation using three metrics: Dice similarity coefficient (DSC), sensitivity (SEN), and positive predictive value (PPV). By using the proposed method, we achieved mean DSC, mean SEN, and mean PPV values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.7146,0.8174$</tex>, and 0.7776, respectively. These findings demonstrate that the proposed guidelines enhance 3D tooth segmentation performance and provide sufficient accuracy for identifying dental arch shapes in orthodontic treatment planning. The novelty of this study lies in the integration of class weighting to mitigate class imbalance and the incorporation of curvature-based features, which enhance the model's ability to learn the underlying geometric structure of dental models.

title: SWaP Optimized C-Band Solid State Power Amplifier for Troposcatter Communication
authors: Chandrashekar K, Ashwini U, Kiran S K, Vanitha Chavan L, Prajwal Kumar C
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a Size, Weight, and Power (SWaP) optimized solid-state power amplifier (SSPA) operating in the C-band, specifically tailored for troposcatter communication systems. The amplifier delivers 25 W of output power with a high gain of greater than 60 dB, ensuring long-range and reliable communication under non-line-of-sight (NLOS) conditions. The SSPA maintains excellent linearity, exhibiting an intermodulation distortion (IMD) of −25 dBc. The design leverages advanced GaN technology and an efficient thermal management scheme to achieve compactness and ruggedness required for field deployment. Performance metrics confirm the suitability of the amplifier for modern tactical and fixed troposcatter links demanding high spectral efficiency and robustness. This module is realized on a 8 layer hybrid PCB using PTFE and FR4 laminates without compromising on performance and tested in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+55^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> ambient temperature.

title: Extracting Multi-Destination Tourism Flows Using LLM-Driven Process Mining: A Case Study of Structural Shifts in Thai Domestic Travel via Social Media Group Before-, During-, and After COVID-19
authors: Sukanya Chinwicha, Worarat Krathu, Chakarida Nukoolkit, Nuttawut Atiratana
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Traditional tourism tracking methods, particularly those reliant on GPS, often face limitations regarding data intrusiveness and restricted spatial scope. Addressing this, this study introduces a novel data pipeline integrating Large Language Models with process mining to analyze unstructured social media data. The framework is validated through a case study of Thai domestic tourism, mapping trajectory shifts across pre-, during-, and post-pandemic phases. Analysis of 2,577 itineraries reveals a distinct structural evolution: from 2019's linear, mono-centric flows to 2021's rigid “safety loops” where hotels served as isolated sanctuaries. By 2023, a complex, hybrid mesh emerged, establishing hotels as permanent logistical hubs while religious tourism remained the sole consistent anchor. These findings demonstrate the pipeline's capability to uncover intricate behavioral transitions effectively. Consequently, this research contributes a scalable, nonintrusive methodology for monitoring nationwide multi-destination trajectories. To facilitate reproducibility, the complete dataset, pipeline code, and data flow graph results are published on GitHub <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1.</sup>https://github.com/mensudza/Travelyzer.

title: ChiMatch: A Matching Platform for Children's Cafeterias with Contribution-Visualization Functions for Multi-Stakeholders
authors: Soma Sugiura, Shiori Sasaki, Thatsanee Charoenporn
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This paper describes the design and development of a multidirectional needs matching platform to effectively and sustainably connect three key stakeholders: operators, users, and supporters. It aims to address the structural challenges faced by children's cafeterias, which play a vital role in tackling child poverty and solitary eating. These challenges include: (1) operators' shortages of supplies and labor, (2) supporters' motivation maintenance and information gaps, and (3) users' psychological and informational access barriers. In developed countries such as Japan, U.S. and U.K, household income disparities and child poverty remain significant social challenges. Activities of “children's cafeterias,” which provide free meals to children forced to eat alone due to poverty or family circumstances, are expanding across Japan as a vital community safety net. To solve structural challenges faced by children's cafeteria activities, we propose a matching platform to facilitate effective and sustainable collaboration among multi-stakeholders.

title: Assessing Disorders of Consciousness Using Temporal Sleep Dynamics Extracted From Whole-Night PSG
authors: Jun Xiao, Tianyou Yu, Haiyun Huang, Jiahui Pan, Fei Wang, Di Chen, Zhenghui Gu, Zhuliang Yu, Benyan Luo, Yuanqing Li
abstract: Accurate assessment of patients with disorders of consciousness (DoC) remains a major clinical challenge due to the limitations of behavior-based evaluations and task-dependent neurophysiological paradigms. Whole-night polysomnography (PSG), a passive and noninvasive monitoring tool, offers unique potential for revealing residual brain function during sleep. In this study, we propose a temporal-dynamic feature extraction and aggregation framework for PSG analysis to enable machine learning-based diagnosis and prognosis in DoC patients. Whole-night EEG/EOG signals were segmented into non-overlapping 30-second epochs, from which time-domain, spectral, and nonlinear complexity features were extracted. To obtain a unified and compact representation of variable-length feature sequences, two aggregation strategies were applied: stage- wise averaging based on sleep staging and clustering-based grouping via unsupervised learning. A two-stage feature selection pipeline further reduced dimensionality while preserving discriminative power and interpretability. Classifiers trained on the aggregated features achieved strong performance in distinguishing minimally conscious state (MCS) from vegetative state (VS), with AUC values exceeding 0.84, and demonstrated robust predictive ability for long-term recovery outcomes (AUC=0.79). These findings highlight the diagnostic and prognostic value of whole-night PSG and support the development of fully automated, task-free assessment tools for DoC.

title: Subspace Clustering via Weighted Multi-View Low-Rank Sparse Representation
authors: Yue Zhao, Shengchang Luo, Qinmu Peng, Zhifei Li, Qiao Xu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Clustering is a classic task of unsupervised learning. In subspace clustering, samples are grouped into different categories by learning the intrinsic information/feature of the input data without using label information. The cluster result of the subspace clustering is subject to the learned representation. Existing methods try to incorporate multi-view learning with lowrank sparse representation to learn the affinity matrix that can be used for clustering. However, there are two main limitations of the related methods. The first is that the affinity matrix on each view is constructed individually, thus the learned representation will contain the noise of the inputs. The second is that different views are often considered to have the same weight in the learning task, without considering different view weights. A unified subspace representation is obtained through multi-view low-rank sparse subspace clustering, which relies on learning a shared affinity matrix common to all views. In this paper, the mutual information computed from the input data and the resulting representation learned from it are leveraged to dynamically assign different view weights within the multi-view low-rank sparse subspace clustering framework. The effectiveness of the mutual information based view weighting scheme is validated by experiments on the 3 -sources and Reuters datasets.

title: Steering the Path with a Semi-Passive Robot to Break Post-Stroke Synergies_supp1-3674710.docx
authors: Chandramouli Krishnan
abstract: Objective: Abnormal coupling of elbow flexors with shoulder abductors—the “flexor synergy”—is a common post stroke motor impairment that interferes with upper extremity function. Previous studies have shown that practicing elbow extension while loading shoulder abductors can improve independent joint control. However, these approaches often involve expensive and bulky equipment, limiting their use in clinic. SepaRRo is a semi-passive rehabilitation robot that uses brakes to generate training forces, reducing its cost relative to existing systems. SepaRRo can also load the shoulder abductors during horizontal planar reaching, suggesting that it could target the flexor synergy. However, it is unclear if training with a semi-passive robot can produce out-of-flexor synergy kinematic adaptations. Methods: Chronic stroke survivors (n = 15) with upper extremity impairment participated in a randomized, crossover-design experiment where they reached for a functional target with their more-impaired limb in two conditions: SepaRRo resisting their motion to the target (Resistance), and SepaRRo generating an additional lateromedial force to load their shoulder abductors (Steering). For each condition, we measured changes in reaching kinematics with motion capture equipment. Results: Following the Steering condition, participants demonstrated significantly greater shoulder abduction than the Pre-test and the Resistance condition (p0.112). Participants reduced their el bow extension following the Resistance condition (p = 0.018). Conclusion: Steering facilitated out-of-synergy adaptations that were not present following simple resistance. Significance: Conventional training methods may facilitate post-stroke synergies and impede recovery, while SepaRRo's steering forces may lead to improvements in independent joint control in stroke survivors.

title: An Autonomous, Privacy-Preserving Emergency Response System Using on-Device AI
authors: R. Umanesan, Jeyansai Harikrishnan, Yash Lal, S. Jagadeesh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Rapid emergency response systems often rely on explicit user interaction, such as unlocking a device or pressing a panic button, which may not be feasible during high-stress or incapacitating situations. This paper presents an intelligent, passive safety system designed to operate continuously in the background with minimal user involvement. The proposed model employs on-device artificial intelligence to detect potential danger scenarios by analyzing voice characteristics, sudden motion anomalies, and other contextual cues. Upon identifying a threat, the system automatically initiates an alert without requiring user input, transmitting real-time location data and short encrypted audio samples to predefined emergency contacts. To ensure reliability and privacy, speech processing is performed locally using the Vosk framework, while Firebase is utilized for secure backend data management. Additionally, an SMS-based fallback mechanism is incorporated to maintain alert delivery in the absence of internet connectivity. The proposed approach demonstrates a robust and user-independent emergency response framework suitable for real-world safety-critical applications.

title: Magnetocardiographic Simulation and Analysis of Cardiac QT Interval Disorders
authors: Yadan Zhang, Yangyang Cui, Guiyu Bai, Min Xiang, Rui Shang, Jiaojiao Pang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: QT interval abnormalities, including prolonged and shortened QT, are linked to life-threatening arrhythmias. While electrocardiography (ECG) is commonly used for assessment, it has limitations in detecting subtle repolarization changes and spatial dispersion. Magnetocardiography (MCG) offers a potential alternative. We analyzed 735 subjects using MCG, including patients with congenital Long QT Syndrome (LQTS), Short QT Syndrome (SQTS), acquired QT prolongation, and healthy controls. QT intervals were measured by automated algorithms and manual annotation, and MCG data were compared to ECG. QTc, defined as the heart rate–corrected QT interval, was calculated for all subjects. A deep learning model was developed for classifying QT disorders. MCG QTc values were consistently longer than ECG values. LQTS patients had significantly prolonged MCG QTc (540 ± 35 ms), while SQTS patients showed shortened MCG QTc (310 ± 20 ms). MCG detected subtle QT prolongation in ECG-normal LQTS patients. The deep learning model achieved 90.8% overall accuracy in classifying QT disorders. MCG effectively measures QT interval abnormalities and identifies subtle repolarization changes missed by ECG, showing promise as a complementary diagnostic tool. MCG, combined with deep learning, provides a non-invasive, accurate approach to diagnosing QT disorders and could enhance personalized treatment strategies.

title: Construction of Conductive Hydrogels to Promote Cell Migration Through Electrical Stimulation for Rapid Wound Healing
authors: Thi Kim Ngan Duong, Thi Thuy Truong, Thi Nhat Linh Phan, Thi Xuan Nguyen, Vu Hoang Minh Doan, Nguyen Minh Hung Vu, Jaeyeop Choi, Junghwan Oh, Sudip Mondal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Flexible sensors based on ion-conductive hydrogels offer significant potential for healthcare management systems and wound monitoring. Electrical stimulation (ES) has emerged as a promising strategy in regenerative medicine to promote tissue repair, but is often constrained by the requirement for tissue-penetrating wiring and external power sources. Therefore, developing hydrogels with high electrical conductivity, combined with ES, to effectively facilitate and monitor wound healing remains a considerable challenge. In this study, a conductive hydrogel dressing was successfully developed via one-pot polymerization to enhance cell migration in combination with ES therapy. The resulting conductive hydrogel exhibited improved ionic conductivity, enabling efficient ion transport along the polymer backbone and contributing to LED illumination in the circuit. The conductive hydrogel promoted L929 fibroblast migration under ES treatment while demonstrating excellent biocompatibility and non-toxicity. This improved ionically conductive hydrogel serves as an ideal wound dressing for accelerating the healing process. By integrating conductivity with ES therapy, the hydrogel represents a promising therapeutic strategy for future applications in wound management and regenerative medicine.

title: Empowering Remote Healthcare Through Edge-AI: A Lightweight Framework for Low-Resource Environments
authors: Rana Muhammad Amir Latif, Zakawat Liaqat, Farah H Zawaideh, Marwan Abu-Zanona, Abdulmalik Ahmad Al Jabr, Muhammad Adrees
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Access to quality healthcare remains a persistent challenge in remote and underserved regions due to infrastructural limitations, intermittent internet connectivity, and a shortage of trained medical professionals. This study proposes and evaluates a lightweight edge computing framework integrated with Artificial Intelligence (AI) to address these barriers and enable real-time, offline diagnostics at the point of care. The system deploys optimized AI models on low-power devices, allowing local inference of patient vitals and symptoms without relying on cloud connectivity. Tested in a simulated rural environment using Raspberry Pi and Edge TPU platforms, the framework demonstrated significantly lower latency, reduced data transmission, and improved system reliability compared to conventional cloud-based solutions. Usability testing confirmed the framework's accessibility and effectiveness, even for non-technical users. The study highlights the potential of edge-AI systems in enhancing healthcare access, ensuring data privacy, and supporting scalable health delivery models in developing countries. Future directions include the integration of federated learning, expansion into maternal and mental health diagnostics, and mobile edge deployment for crisis response and policy integration.

title: Remaining Life Prediction of Power Modules With Digital Twin and Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Qian Xia, Chang Lu, Yuluo Hou, Chenhao Wu, Zhexin Cui, Waseem Abbas, Hiu Hung Lee, Jiguang Yue, Feng Lyu, Md Rishad Ahmed
abstract: Intelligent power module (IPM) is a critical component of a power conversion system, and accurately predicting the lifetime of the key internal component, the insulated gate bipolar transistor (IGBT), is critical to the safe operation of the system. However, numerous challenges, such as monitoring difference of the internal state, variable degradation processes, and diverse field strengths, make it difficult to predict the IGBT lifetime accurately. Therefore, this paper proposes a remaining useful life (RUL) prediction method based on multi-source data fusion under limited information conditions. A prediction model combining Position Attention Module (PAM) and Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM) is designed to accommodate the large variability of the degradation process to enhance the ability to capture the spatio-temporal dependence of degradation features. In addition, to address the difficulty of internal state monitoring and the diversity of operating field strengths, this study constructs a Digital Twin (DT) model of the intelligent power module based on Finite Element Analysis (FEA) to comprehensively analyze the internal state of the device and provide accurate data support for RUL prediction. Meanwhile, to tackle the problems of insufficient health indicators, incomplete failure information, and end-of-life (EOL) fluctuation, a multi-indicator double-layer determination mechanism is proposed to improve the accuracy of the predicted EOL. Eventually, the prediction of the RUL of intelligent power module is achieved under limited observation information. The proposed method is experimentally verified on a 10 A/1200 V IPM from STMicroelectronics with accurate prediction results, and its superiority is further proved by comparing it with other methods.

title: Secondary SMC for Microgrid Using Improved Stretchy Event-Triggered Mechanism with ACK
authors: Hao Chen, Zehao Wang, Zhenzhen Zhang, Lanfeng Hua, Kaibo Shi, Huazhang Wang, Shouming Zhong
abstract: This work addresses the secondary control issues in microgrid for frequency and voltage restoration under network threats. Firstly, a distributed control protocol is constructed using the consensus method of multi-agent system (MAS). Secondly, sliding mode control (SMC) using an improved stretchy event-triggered mechanism (ISETM) is designed to reduce data transmission. By employing Acknowledgment (ACK) mechanisms, ACK-based self-recovery (ASR) strategy is developed as an attack detection and compensation scheme to improve control performance under unsecured communication environment. Then, sufficient conditions are derived using Lyapunov stability theory to ensure a finite time convergence on the sliding mode surface. Finally, a test system consisting of 20 DGs is applied to assess the scalability of this distributed control algorithm. Experiment tests on a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) platform are conducted to verify that the designed control scheme offers advantage and efficiency on microgrid secondary control.

title: Adaptive Inference Acceleration With Fine-Grained Model Partitioning for Mobile Edge Intelligence
authors: Peng Wang, Long Huang, Wen Sun, Yi Yang, Dusit Niyato, Dapeng Oliver Wu
abstract: Edge intelligence deploys artificial intelligence models on edge nodes proximal to data sources, and delivers real-time inference support for resource-constrained devices. To realize this vision, inference offloading differs from conventional computation offloading by tailoring offloading strategies to the intrinsic characteristics of AI inference tasks. In this field, existing researchs generally lack fine-grained model partitioning capabilities and long-term resource adaptability, failing to optimize resource utilization and sustain stable performance in mobile environments. To address these issues, we propose an adaptive inference acceleration framework that dynamically partitions inference models into hierarchical subtasks and offloads these subtasks to heterogeneous edge servers. We formulate a joint optimization problem for task partitioning, offloading and resource allocation, which takes queue stability as the constraint and aims to minimize the long-term average task completion time. To realize the optimal trade-off between latency and stability without future state prediction, we adopt Lyapunov optimization to decompose the long-term stochastic optimization into slot-by-slot solvable deterministic subproblems. For these slot-by-slot subproblems, we design a Q-network Mixing (QMIX)-based multi-agent reinforcement learning method to enable collaborative strategy selection across edge servers. Experimental simulations show that, compared with baseline algorithms including the greedy, genetic and MAD2RL methods, our proposed framework achieves a substantial reduction in task completion time while preserving inference accuracy and queue stability.

title: RIS Deployment for Cooperative Relaying: A Novel Perspective in Near-Field Communications
authors: Jiachen Qian, Jue Wang, Wei Duan, Miaowen Wen, FeiFei Gao, Pin-Han Ho
abstract: The reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) has been widely studied in far-field communications (FFC), and further extended to near-field communications (NFC). With the distinct electromagnetic properties in FFC and NFC, it remains debatable whether the RIS deployment strategies established for FFC are still applicable to NFC. To bridge this gap, we examine RIS-aided cooperative relaying in NFC through two representative configurations, single-RIS and multi-RIS relaying, in which a decode-and-forward relay forwards data from the source to the user with the assistance of RIS For the single RIS deployment, we show that the achievable rates with RIS deployed near the base station (BS) or near relay are completely different. It is revealed that, with a single-antenna relay, the multi-RIS is significantly preferable over the single RIS in NFC without the requirement of massive RIS reflection elements, which is completely different from that of FFC. Furthermore, we derive closed-form expressions for the achievable rates under both single-antenna and multi-antenna relay configurations to explicitly determine the distance threshold. The presented analytical results demonstrate that locating RIS near the relay yields substantial performance gains when the transmission distance exceeds a certain threshold. This conclusion is validated by numerical simulations, which systematically illustrates the distinct impact of RIS deployment strategies in near-field versus far-field regions.

title: Automated Atrial Fibrillation Detection in Ballistocardiogram Signals with ModernTCN
authors: Dongli Cai, Hong Xian, Xihe Chen, Baoxian Yu, Han Zhang

title: Performance Analysis of a Coilable Space Mast via Computer-Aided Engineering (CAE)
authors: Yuhu Dong, Junrui Xiong, Ziqi Li, Zhouyi Ren, Kaijie Dong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: As space missions become increasingly complex, deployable space mechanisms have found extensive application in addressing the conflict between mission requirements and the limited spatial and carrying capacities of launch vehicles. Critical evaluation metrics for deployable space mechanisms encompass stowage ratio, operational stiffness, and kinematic characteristics. CAE serves as an effective methodology for addressing complex engineering problems through computational assistance. Consequently, this study first conducts theoretical calculations on stowage ratio and stiffness of coilable space masts, followed by simulation analyses using ANSYS software. Computational simulations validate the accuracy of stiffness theoretical formulations and perform modal analysis on the deployable structure.

title: Electromagnetic Docking and Separation for On-Orbit Refueling of Large-Scale Spacecraft
authors: Yiqing Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: With the ceaseless development of large-scale spacecraft, the on-orbit assembly and refueling problem is becoming more and more prominent. Establishing an on-orbit fuel depot is necessary to enhance the on-orbit maneuverability, extend the lifetime of spacecraft, and provide relay support for subsequent space missions. One main technology lies in the docking and separation between the servicing spacecraft and fuel depot, however, the traditional docking/separation mechanism based on thrusters requires continuous fuel consumption, which may affect or even pollute satellite-borne optical sensors and cause large deviations. The electromagnetic docking and separation technology of spacecraft can deal with the above disadvantages under the background of on-orbit refueling. First, the relative motion dynamics model between the servicing spacecraft and the on-orbit fuel depot during the on-orbit refueling process is developed with the comprehensive disturbance resulting from external disturbance, elliptical eccentricity, and uncertain mass, and the reference trajectory of the docking and separation process is designed by the trigonometric function. For electromagnetic docking, an impedance control strategy based on a nonlinear disturbance observer (NDO) is designed. The NDO is used to estimate and compensate for the comprehensive disturbance and model uncertainty. The global asymptotic stability of the closedloop system is analyzed by strict theoretical proof using the Lyapunov stability theory. The proposed soft docking/separation scheme has the advantages of no propellant consumption, no plume contamination, etc., which can make on-orbit refueling easy to realize.

title: Incremental Contrastive Learning With Dual Distilling for Source-Free Domain Adaptation in Industrial Process Fault Diagnosis
authors: Dan Yang, Haojie Huang, Jiaorao Wang, Minxue Kong, Xin Peng, Weimin Zhong
abstract: Source-free domain adaptation (SFDA) enables knowledge transfer without source data, addressing privacy constraints of the transfer learning. However, existing methods often depend on fixed confidence thresholds for pseudolabeling, which are poorly adaptable and lead to unstable performance. Moreover, class distribution mismatch is frequently ignored, further hindering adaptation. To tackle these challenges, incremental contrastive learning with dual distilling for SFDA is proposed and applied in industrial process fault diagnosis in this article. A threshold-free pseudolabeling strategy is first introduced to dynamically assess label reliability. Then, a stage-wise incremental contrastive learning framework progressively expands from per-class Top-K samples to the full target set, effectively mitigating class imbalance. In addition, a dual distilling mechanism at both feature and label levels is employed to alleviate model drift caused by forgetting source knowledge. Finally, extensive experiments on three-phase flow and wastewater treatment datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Closed-Loop Self-Assembly and Navigation of Magnetic Modular Millibots in Confined Environments
authors: Anuruddha Bhattacharjee, Zhengguang Wang, Arne Schmidt, Aaron T. Becker, Min Jun Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Magnetic modular millibots, capable of deterministic self-assembly and reconfiguration under wireless magnetic fields, offer a promising route toward mesoscale manipulation in structured and confined environments. This work presents a modular chain millibot composed of cubic units with free-to-spin internal magnets that align under external fields. While individual cubes cannot propel independently, their assembly into chains enables controlled locomotion through sliding and tumbling modes. We first demonstrate open-loop operation in confined workspaces, including chain formation, navigation, controlled disassembly, and wall climbing, supported by a dynamic model and parametric analysis that identify the actuation and geometric conditions required for successful climbing. Building on this foundation, we introduce a closed-loop control framework that integrates vision-based feedback. As the millibot’s movement speed increases with chain length, the order of assembly strongly affects task completion time; we therefore formulate the sequence-planning problem as a harmonic traveling salesman problem (HTSP) and solve it to compute deterministic cube-collection sequences that minimize the effective travel cost. The controller applies sliding and tumbling dynamics to realize obstacle-aware navigation and reliable self-assembly. Experiments validate autonomous assembly of four cubes with a two-cube chain into a six-cube chain in free space and collection of three cubes with wall climbing in a confined workspace, both with 100% success. The measured mean unit travel times were 0.67 s/mm in free space and 0.89 s/mm in confined environments. Collectively, these results establish a robust automation framework for reversible mesoscale self-assembly, programmable navigation, and lab-on-chip applications.

title: D2TriPO-DETR: Dual-Decoder Triple-Parallel-Output Detection Transformer_supp4-3668360.pdf
authors: gang wu
abstract: Vision-based grasping, though widely employed for industrial and household applications, still struggles with object stacking scenarios. Current methods face three major challenges: limited inter-object relationship understanding; poor grasping adaptation across different viewpoints; and error propagation. To address the above challenges, we propose D2TriPO-DETR, a dual-decoder transformer with three outputs, of which are object detection, manipulation relationship, and grasp detection. Specifically, a distributed attention perception module and a rotation attention invariance module are designed to address limited interobject relationship understanding and poor grasping adaptation across different viewpoints. These two modules are respectively integrated into the two parallel decoders to output the triple results simultaneously, partly eliminating task-level error propagation. Experimental results on the visual manipulation relationship dataset indicate that D2TriPO-DETR outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods across all metrics, e.g., +6.1% object detection recall, +6.7% manipulation relationship image accuracy, and +1.5% grasp detection accuracy. Extensive real-world experiments and quantitative results validate D2TriPO-DETR’s effectiveness.

title: Online Positioning Error Measurement and Trajectory Correction for Large-Scale Workpieces
authors: Jiawei Qiu, Wencan Lai, Zhiyong Li, Yanbin Yao, Guolei Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the problem of trajectory deviations caused by parking errors of large-scale workpieces, this paper proposes a monocular vision-based online positioning error measurement and trajectory correction method. In the proposed approach, an industrial camera guided by a linear rail and a robot is employed to measure the positions of visual targets mounted on the workpiece fixture. By integrating the positions of the robot and external axes, the measured target positions are unified into a global coordinate system. Subsequently, the placement error of the workpiece is estimated based on the three-point pose localization principle, enabling online correction of the trajectory generated by offline programming. Experimental validation on an 8 m-long large-scale workpiece demonstrates that the proposed method reduces the average trajectory error by 91.12 %, with the absolute trajectory error constrained within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 5 ~ m m}$</tex>.
