title: Augment and Virtual Reality in Healthcare, Education and Social Robotics: Case study and Applications
authors: Satyam Kumar, Aarfa Rajput, Laxman Baburao Abhnag, Asrorbek Bakhtiyorov, Neha Gupta, Meenakshi Munjal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) are revolutionary technologies that may improve the human interaction, learning process, and decision-making in various fields. Although the literature on these topics is mostly limited to conceptual models or single usage, the gap is the lack of deployment-related analysis with references to practical case studies and performance analysis. In this paper, the author offers the in-depth exploration of AR and VR in the healthcare and education and social robotics, focusing on the practical scenarios of application implementation, as well as crossdomain comparison. Three case studies are presented to consider the suitability of immersive technologies regarding the accuracy of tasks, the effectiveness of training, user engagement, and the quality of interactions. The findings show that AR-based solutions increase the accuracy of specific procedures in healthcare, VR-based learning conveys considerably better the level of engagement and intuition in learning, and AR/VRenabled platforms promote clarity and confidence in humanrobot interaction. With the help of both quantitative performance measures and qualitative user-centric simulations, the feasibility and scalability of AR and VR are confirmed in practice and confirmed to work across different deployment scenarios. The results underline that the accomplishment of the AR and VR implementation is determined by deploymentspecific matters, usability, and design of systems, but not technology. The contribution made in this piece is an application oriented, case study based orientation of understanding that provides the bubble between theoretical possibility and actual implementation of immersive technologies.

title: Election by Algorithm: Sentiment Lexicons on Twitter as Early Indicators of the 2024 U.S. Presidential Election
authors: Shreya Srivastava, Seba Susan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Social networks, particularly Twitter (now X), play a significant role in measuring public sentiment during elections. This paper examines sentiment analysis of tweets related to the 2024 U.S. Presidential Election, collected from May 1 to July 31, 2024. Tweets were cleaned and sorted by candidate (Trump or Biden) using relevant keywords. Sentiment analysis was then conducted using various lexiconbased methods to ensure a holistic assessment. We further employed SentiWordNet to assign positive and negative sentiment scores to individual words in each tweet, and computed the average scores across all words to quantify overall tweet-level sentiment. A comparative analysis of normalized sentiment scores across all lexicons revealed that tweets related to Trump were marginally more positive than those related to Biden. Such information can act as an early indicator of public sentiment and help political strategists assess candidate popularity ahead of the election.

title: Modelling Analysis of a Self-Switching Rectifier Flux Pump Using a Field–Circuit Coupling Method
authors: Jiafu Wei, Hongye Zhang, Qian Dong, Adil Shah, Ozan Keysan, Markus Mueller
abstract: High-temperature superconducting (HTS) self-switching flux pumps can inject direct current into closed-loop HTS coils without electrical contacts. Targeting practical application, this work investigated the charging capability, losses, and efficiency of the flux pump, explicitly capturing current sharing effect in the bridge tape based on a field–circuit coupled model. This pa per focuses on four key design factors: the peak value and duty ratio of input primary-side positive current, secondary (series) resistance, and the copper stabilizer thickness of the bridge tape. The results showed that increasing the positive peak value of primary current can increase charging speed and the pumped current, while the secondary resistance exhibits an optimal value. Variations in the positive duty ratio highlight a trade-off between the pumped current, secondary-side efficiency, initial ramp rate, and ripple level. Finally, changes in the copper stabilizer mainly influence the current-sharing effect of the bridge tape and the bridge voltage, with associated impact on charging performance and efficiency. The modelling framework and findings derived from these studies provide practical guidance for parameter selection and prototype design of the self-switching flux pump.

title: Analysis and Correction of Spatial Variation in Complex MEO-Airborne Bistatic SAR Configurations
authors: Yu Ma, Yong Wang
abstract: The Medium-Earth Orbit(MEO)-airborne bistatic synthetic aperture radar (SAR) system provides advantages such as high transmitter altitude, strong survivability, and high observation flexibility. However, the inherent characteristics of this system result in spatial variation in the slant range history of different scattering points within the observed area. This paper presents the qualitative assessment on the two-dimensional (2-D) spatial variation caused by the system geometric configuration firstly, and the accurate slant range history model is established followed by the proposal of a virtual scattering points fitting method to analyze the impact of 2-D spatial variation in SAR imaging procedure quantitatively. Then, the correction algorithm is proposed to compensate the spatial variation in the range dimension through the block processing and mitigate the azimuth dimension variation by the nonlinear chirp scaling(NCS) method. Finally, the experimental results with simulated and semi-real data validate the generalizability and practicality of the proposed approach.

title: Evaluating the Energy and Economic Effects of Soiling and Cleaning Dynamics in Urban Solar PV Systems
authors: Shubham Sharma, Ambrish Singh, Sunanda Sinha
abstract: Cleaning of solar photovoltaic systems is crucial for efficient operation and increased economic profits, with the frequency of cleaning significantly affecting the overall revenue of PV plants. The study presents a framework that integrates a recently developed dry deposition based soiling losses assessment model with an energy- economic criterion for enabling decision making to derive manual cleaning strategies for solar photovoltaic systems installed in urban environments. The dry deposition model links local air quality and meteorological factors to determine the dust accumulation, allowing realistic estimation of soiling behaviour under diverse urban conditions. The framework was tested to evaluate the site-specific variations in soiling losses and different cleaning strategies under varying costs across 22 situated in two Indian cities. Daily automatic cleaning was also evaluated as a realistic scenario, and its economic viability was assessed. Soiling rates ranged from 0.33–0.63%/day in Jaipur and 0.37–0.95%/day in Delhi, with higher averages in Delhi, indicating the inter and intra city variability of soiling influenced by the localized urban pollution and climate. At a lowest manual cleaning cost of ₹4/panel, optimized schedules required 15–21 cleanings in Jaipur and up to 30 in high-soiling Delhi sites. The study offers data driven insights on how localized environmental conditions affect soiling accumulation and thus, cleaning requirements in diverse urban photovoltaic deployments. The proposed framework serves as operational guidance for urban solar energy planners to enhance the “energy-economic benefits” of small- to medium-scale power plants where automated solutions may not be feasible.

title: Capacitance-Based Sensing and Disturbance Rejection Control of Dielectric Elastomer Actuators With a Portable Hardware Design
authors: Jian Yang, Fan Xu, Hesheng Wang
abstract: Dielectric Elastomer Actuators (DEAs) represent a promising technology for soft robotics and devices, owing to their compliance, rapid response, and capacitance-based self-sensing capabilities. However, realizing their full potential requires advanced control strategies to manage their complex nonlinear dynamics, a challenge yet to be fully addressed by portable hardware solutions. This article introduces a novel self-sensing-based dynamic control framework for DEAs. First, a compact hardware platform is developed to enable simultaneous capacitance sensing and charge-based voltage control for up to four independent DEAs. Next, a simplified dynamic model is proposed, effectively capturing key nonlinearities of DEAs and ensuring real-time computation on a microcontroller unit. Finally, a nonlinear Active Disturbance Rejection Controller with a Model-Assisted Extended State Observer is designed. The controller leverages the dynamic model to compensate for nonlinearities and mitigate coupling effects, achieving high-precision trajectory tracking under unknown external disturbances. Experimental results validate the controller’s effectiveness, achieving robust tracking for various trajectories on conical and planar DEAs, with disturbance rejection up to 100 mN. The proposed framework significantly enhances the portability, robustness, and autonomous performance of DEAs, paving the way for their practical application in real-world scenarios.

title: An NMPC-Based Integrated Control Strategy for Rear-Wheel-Driven Autonomous Electric Vehicles
authors: Ali Bahadır Aydemir, Muhammad Umer Khan, Amir Nobahar, Kutluk Bilge Arıkan, Bülent Ertan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: A unified approach for the Nonlinear Model Predictive Control (NMPC) is presented to control active front steering, lateral acceleration, and longitudinal velocity for a rear-wheel-driven autonomous electric vehicle. The purpose is to ensure reference tracking and system stability by utilizing multi-objective optimization functions. Through the results, the effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated to achieve the robustness and stability. The dynamic model of a rear-wheeldriven test vehicle also incorporates the Pacekja tire model, that is considered as the most accurate tire model. The performance of the proposed controller is evaluated for the constant-radius maneuver test under a dry asphalt road surface for the rear-wheel-driven EV. Through simulation results, it is observed that the NMPC is able to successfully maintain longitudinal velocity, lateral acceleration, while following the desired trajectory.

title: Next-Generation Battery Technologies for Electric Vehicles: Challenges and Opportunities
authors: D Sarathkumar, P Sivaraman, T Jayakumar, J. Karthikeyan, B.K. Parrthipan, K. Umamaheswari, Murugesan Manivel, Anandhakumar C
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The swift expansion in the use of electric vehicles has necessitated the development of advanced battery solutions to support the escalating demand for improved performance, efficiency, and sustainability. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of future battery solutions including solid-state battery systems lithium-sulfur batteries and metal-air batteries, with a focus on their potential to revolutionize EV energy storage systems. Key challenges, such as scalability, cost reduction, safety, and environmental impact, are critically analyzed alongside recent breakthroughs in material science, electrode design, and electrolyte formulations. The paper also examines the role of advanced manufacturing techniques, recycling methodologies, and emerging policies in fostering innovation within the battery industry. Finally, the study highlights opportunities for integrating these cutting-edge technologies into EV ecosystems, enabling longer ranges, faster charging, and enhanced lifecycle performance. By addressing the multifaceted challenges and leveraging the presented opportunities, this research underscores the transformative potential of next-generation battery technologies in driving the future of sustainable and efficient electric mobility.

title: Applying Generative Artificial Intelligence to Personalized Curriculum Design: A Case Study of PERLA Platform
authors: Kittipong Warasup
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: The rapid changes in technology, economy, and society today make it important for the workforce to keep learning and improving their skills. However, current education systems have limited flexibility in adapting curricula to meet emerging needs. It still relies on a single standardized curriculum that does not fit the differences between individual learners. This study presents a framework that uses Generative AI to create personalized curricula for each learner. The design is based on the main educational principles: Bloom's Taxonomy, which helps define clear learning outcomes, and constructive alignment, which connects learning outcomes with content, learning activities, and assessment methods. The system also includes checkpoints where experts can review and improve the content generated by AI to make it more accurate and reliable. A prototype platform called PERLA (Personalized Education and Resource Learning with AI) was developed to test this concept. The combination of Generative AI, educational theory, and expert review will help create personalized and trustworthy learning programs that support effective and sustainable lifelong learning.

title: Comparative Study of CNN and Transformer Models for Thyroid Cancer Classification with Explainable AI
authors: Muhammad Tayyab Zamir, Fida Ullah, Safir Ullah Khan, Meshal Nayim, Waqas Haider Bangyal, Edgardo Manuel Felipe Riverón, Alexander Gelbukh
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Thyroid cancer is an emerging global health concern that requires early and accurate diagnosis to improve patient outcomes. Conventional diagnostic techniques, such as ultrasound imaging and fine-needle aspiration biopsy (FNAB), suffer from inter-observer variability and subjectivity. Deep learning (DL) has demonstrated strong potential for automated thyroid cancer diagnosis with high accuracy and efficiency. This paper presents a comparative study of state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks (CNNs), including ResNet, DenseNet, and EfficientNet, and transformer-based architectures, including Vision Transformer (ViT), ConvNeXt, and Swin Transformer, for thyroid nodule classification. Model performance is evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, along with computational efficiency for clinical deployment. To enhance transparency and clinical trust, explainable AI techniques such as LIME and Grad-CAM are applied to visualize salient regions in ultrasound images. Experimental results indicate that transformer-based models achieve the highest accuracy of 96.3%, while EfficientNet offers a favorable trade-off between performance and model complexity. Finally, challenges and future research directions for integrating AI-based diagnostic systems into clinical practice are discussed.

title: UAV Flight Trajectory Prediction Using Neural Network Architectures Across Fixed-Wing and Multirotor Platforms
authors: Faisa Lailiyul Mutho'Affifah, Abdul Halim, Benyamin Kusumoputro
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Accurate trajectory prediction is essential for ensuring flight safety, autonomous navigation, and efficient control of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). However, existing neural network approaches often focus on a single platform, limiting their generalizability across UAVs with different dynamics. This study evaluates three neural architectures for spatiotemporal trajectory prediction using high-frequency flight data. Two UAV configurations, such as a fixed-wing (Cessna) and a multirotor (Phantom), were simulated in XPlane 11 under identical environmental conditions. Flight data of latitude, longitude, and altitude sampled at 80 Hz were preprocessed using a sliding-window segmentation method to preserve temporal dependencies. Backpropagation Neural Network (BPNN), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models were trained using standardised preprocessing and tuned hyperparameters. Results show that recurrent models outperform the feedforward BPNN in capturing temporal patterns. For the Cessna dataset, the RNN achieved balanced accuracy with a mean-squared error (MSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.5 \times 10^{-8}$</tex>, while the LSTM obtained the lowest MSE (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.7 \times 10^{-8}$</tex>). In the more dynamic Phantom dataset, the BPNN remained competitive (MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1.8 \times 10^{-7}$</tex>), whereas RNN and LSTM maintained superior altitude prediction. These results highlight the suitability of lightweight recurrent architectures for real-time UAV trajectory prediction.

title: Comprehensive Evaluation of Deep Learning-Based Oral Lesion Detection Models in Clinical Photography
authors: Nyelham Lhazay, Mansuang Wongsapai, Pherawat Wongsawad, Jitjiroj Ittichaicharoen, Surawut Pongsiriwet, Siriwan Suebnukarn, Kritsasith Warin, Chanokpim Sakdapreecha, Patiwet Wuttisarnwattana
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: In clinical dental practice, practitioners routinely capture and annotate hundreds of oral photographs daily, manually circling lesions for consultations and documentation. This time-intensive process is prone to inconsistencies and delays in patient care. This study comprehensively evaluated 17 state-of-the-art deep learning models to automate oral lesion localization, comparing one-stage (e.g., YOLO, SSD) and twostage (e.g., Faster R-CNN) detection architectures. Additionally, we investigated the impact of Test Time Augmentation (TTA) on model performance. Without TTA, YOLOv11-I demonstrated an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP}_{\mathbf{0. 5 0}}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 9. 6 6} \pm \mathbf{5. 2 7} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>, accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 2 2} \pm 2.48 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$86.39 \pm 2.39 \%$</tex>. Its average processing time on a consumer-grade CPU was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.59 \pm 0.12$</tex> seconds, indicating suitability for sub-second interactive use. With TTA, YOLOv11-1 performance improved to an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP}_{0.50}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$73.57 \pm \mathbf{4. 4 5 \%}$</tex>, accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 7. 1 7} \pm \mathbf{2. 2 7 \%}$</tex> and F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 8. 5 0} \pm 1.80 \%$</tex>, though inference time nearly doubled. Without TTA, the lightweight model, SSDLite-320 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 4 4 M}$</tex> parameters), achieved an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P}_{\mathbf{0. 5 0}}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 7. 1 6} \pm \mathbf{5. 8 2 \%}$</tex> with F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 8. 5 7} \pm \mathbf{4. 3 2 \%}$</tex>, which rose to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60.20 \pm 6.71 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$79.87 \pm 4.13 \%$</tex> respectively with TTA. These models generated precisely localized bounding boxes, enabling practitioners, especially in rural settings, to conduct preliminary screenings and generate standardized images for remote specialist review. Our findings demonstrate that automated localization can transform the dental workflow, reducing annotation time while maintaining accuracy comparable to manual expert annotation.

title: Designing a Speed Controller System for E-Bike
authors: Prashant Kumar, Muneeb Jaleel Mir, Parneet Gill, Anmol Deep Kaur
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The ESP32 microcontroller is the main controller that this project has used to create and invent an intelligent speed controller system for an electric bicycle. The primary objective of this project is to build a smart, cheap, and energy-saving control system that would improve the overall functionality, reliability, and safety of e-bikes. As environmental damage and the use of fossil fuels are becoming serious problems, electric transportation is gaining more and more popularity. Thus, to make e-bikes more efficient and practical, a smart and well-functioning speed controller is necessary. Overall, this research is a showcase of the use of power electronics and embedded systems for the development of a green, intelligent, and convenient e-bike speed control system.

title: AI-Powered Predictive Analytics for Resilient and Adaptive Supply Chain Management
authors: C. Renuga, Monica Vijayakumar, A Harinya, S Sherena Ansleen, S Nisha, S Nithisha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Contemporary global supply chains operate within an increasingly volatile, uncertain, complex, and ambiguous (VUCA) environment, characterized by disruptive events ranging from geopolitical instabilities to pandemics and climate-related incidents. Traditional supply chain management (SCM) methodologies, often reliant on historical data and reactive strategies, are proving inadequate in fostering the necessary resilience and adaptability. This paper examines the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered predictive analytics in constructing supply chains that are not only resistant to disruptions but also capable of dynamic adaptation. By leveraging advanced machine learning (ML) algorithms, deep learning models, and natural language processing (NLP) on diverse datasets, AI-driven systems can anticipate disruptions, optimize inventory and logistics in real-time, and facilitate proactive decision-making. This research synthesizes current literature, elucidates the underlying architectural frameworks, and presents a critical analysis of the capabilities of AI in enhancing predictive visibility, risk mitigation, and operational flexibility. Furthermore, the paper addresses significant implementation challenges, including data quality, model interpretability, and integration complexities, while proposing directions for future scholarly inquiry. The findings posit that the integration of AI-powered predictive analytics is a critical imperative for developing a truly resilient and adaptive supply chain paradigm capable of thriving in the face of continuous disruption.

title: Frequency Analysis of Ultrasonic Fatigue Specimens Based on Electromechanical Analogy
authors: Chao Hang, Yonghui Chen, Qun Yan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Ultrasonic fatigue testing is an important method for studying the long-life fatigue properties of materials, and the analysis of specimen resonance frequency is a prerequisite for conducting ultrasonic fatigue tests. Based on the geometric characteristics of ultrasonic fatigue specimens, the electromechanical analogy method was employed to derive the impedance expressions for both uniform cross-section cylinders and tapered transition sections. An equivalent circuit model for the ultrasonic fatigue specimen was established, presenting the frequency equation of the specimen and solving for its theoretical resonance frequency. The results show that the electromechanical analogy model has high accuracy in predicting the resonance frequency of ultrasonic fatigue specimens, with a relative frequency deviation within 2 %. Additionally, approximating the arc transition segment with a catenary is feasible. Both geometric and material parameters significantly affect the specimen frequency, with the radius of the small-diameter cylindrical segment having the greatest impact. The electromechanical analogy method provides an effective approach for frequency analysis of ultrasonic fatigue specimens, supporting the highprecision design of ultrasonic fatigue tests.

title: Simultaneous Full-Photonic Dechirping and Demodulation THz-ISAC System Based on Thin-Film Lithium Niobate Modulator
authors: Zheng Wang, Xukai Ji, Zanxun Qiu, Zili Cai, Feifei Yin, Yitang Dai, Kun Xu
abstract: The integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems up to the terahertz (THz) band play a crucial role across myriad applications in the 6G technology. Nevertheless, due to the absence of ultra-wideband electrooptic devices, previously reported THz-ISAC systems which usually implement THz linear frequency modulation (LFM) dechirping and THz signal demodulation based on THz electrical mixers are still unable to achieve full-photonic synthesis of radar sensing and communication in the THz band. We present a simultaneous full-photonic dechirping and demodulation THz-ISAC system based on the thin-film lithium niobate Mach–Zehnder modulator (TFLN-MZM). In the THz-ISAC signal generation, coherent optical carrier generation based on an optical frequency comb (OFC) is used to generate the LFM-QPSK signal, and full-photonic dechirping of W-band LFM signals is realized via an ultra-wideband TFLN-MZM in the radar dechirping receiver. Besides, to achieve full-photonic ISAC processing, a homologous coherent receiver based on the TFLN-MZM is demonstrated to demodulate the THz-ISAC signal. In the experiment, we evaluate the impact of overlapping bandwidths of LFM and QPSK signals on system performance. According to the experimental results, the demonstrated THz-ISAC system attains a range resolution of 7 mm and a data rate of 22 Gbit/s QPSK with frequency up to 110 GHz. The demonstrated simultaneously full-photonic dechirping and demodulation THz-ISAC system with mm-scale resolution is beneficial to the 6G applications in the future.

title: Stiffness Modeling and Experiments on a Low-Tension, Fully Actuated, Cable-Driven Manipulator With a Stiffness Regulator Module
authors: Yanlin Chen, Yanjiang Huang, Qinlin Tan, Xiangyu Chu, Kwok Wai Samuel Au, Xianmin Zhang
abstract: Cable-driven robots offer low inertia and high compliance, making them ideal for collaborative robotics. This article addresses the challenge of tension slack in fully actuated cable-driven manipulators by proposing solutions from both design and modeling. A novel stiffness regulator module (SRM) is proposed that integrates real-time tension monitoring and antislack control via an embedded pressure sensor. A low-tension stiffness model for the pulley-cable structure (PCS) is developed and combined with cable and SRM stiffness models to establish a system-level and joint-level stiffness models. Experimental validation on a custom cable-driven manipulator confirms model accuracy. Component tests (cable, SRM, and PCS) and joint stiffness measurements across varying angles and pretension levels yield mean absolute percentage errors (MAPEs) of 6.49% (without PCS) and 5.08% (with PCS). At high pretension, PCS influence is negligible (MAPE ≤ 6.13% ); under low tension (< 2 N) along the wrist z-axis, including PCS reduces error by up to 79% (from 2.13% to 0.44% ). These results demonstrate that in low-tension environments, the PCS stiffness model introduced in this article yields significantly more accurate predictions of the stiffness of the cable-driven system. This work provides theoretical foundations and practical implementation methods for achieving high-precision stiffness measurement and control in cable-driven robots.

title: LLM4Load-Turbo: A Prompt-Driven LLM Framework with Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Multi-Scale Workload Prediction
authors: Zeyuan Ding, Dian Ding, Jiannong Cao, Yiming Zhang, Guangtao Xue
abstract: Accurate workload prediction is essential to ensure application Quality of Service (QoS), cost efficiency, and compliance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) during cloud-based deployment. However, existing methods struggle to achieve accurate forecasts across multiple temporal scales and often fail to generalize well with limited historical data. To tackle these problems, we propose LLM4Load-Turbo, a Prompt-Driven LLM Framework with Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Multi-Scale Workload Prediction. Firstly, we design a structured prompt with dataset introduction, task description, and workload features characterization to extract multi-scale features. Secondly, we introduce a cross-modality alignment mechanism combined with label embedding to further enhance predictive accuracy and generalization, effectively mitigating the container cold-start problem. Thirdly, we propose a two-level knowledge distillation strategy, enabling LLM4Load-Turbo to maintain high accuracy while substantially reducing inference latency, memory footprint, and computational cost. Specifically, our framework achieves up to an 89.27% improvement in inference speed and a 93.30% reduction in model parameter scale compared to state-of-the-art baselines. Extensive experiments on four real cloud workload datasets validate the effectiveness of our framework. For multi-scale workload prediction, LLM4Load-Turbo improves up to 50.44%. For container cold-start scenarios, LLM4Load-Turbo improves up to 93.54%. These results demonstrate the potential of LLM4Load-Turbo to enable dynamic and efficient resource management in modern cloud systems.

title: Comparative Security Analysis through Lightweight Cipher in IoT
authors: Ashish Kumar Soni, Kumari Nidhi Lal, Swapan Maiti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Protection of sensitive medical data has been the foremost issue with the progression of the IoMT and research and development in this field. This paper is presenting a comparison study in terms of security for lightweight ciphers in IoT environments, emphasizing how well these schemes protect medical data. Among the various cryptographic techniques studied and compared, ECC, AES, and SHA are different kinds of attacks, such as replay, brute-force, and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. The goal of this research is to determine the best algorithm for protecting IoMT applications by examining key performance metrics such as CPU utilization, key generation time, and memory usage. While preserving device performance and longevity, the results of this study help construct a highly secure and efficient cryptographic framework that ensures data confidentiality, integrity, and authentication in medical IoT networks.

title: A Soft-Switched CUK Converter Design and Analysis
authors: Mirza Jawad Baig, Rishi Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This research suggests a different design for the CUK converter that includes an additional inductor and diode. This will allow all semiconductor devices to employ Zero Voltage Switching (ZVS) and Zero-Current Switching (ZCS) to do soft-switching. The recommended configuration effectively lowers switching losses and increases efficiency. A diode bridge rectifier (DBR) powered by a $220 \mathrm{~V}, 50 \mathrm{~Hz}$ AC source provides the converter with a DC voltage of 198 V. At 300 W of output power, this voltage is increased to 300 V. With a Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) of 4.08%, it complies with the IEEE-519 standard. MATLAB Simulink has been used to simulate and confirm that the ZVS and ZCS requirements have been met. The results show that the suggested converter can convert DC power effectively and with little distortion.

title: Fault Classification in Hybrid Microgrid Using Semi-Supervised CNN
authors: Nidhi Sahu, Narendra D. Londhe, Pavan Kumar Bais
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: As renewable energy sources (RESs) are increasingly used in power systems, hybrid microgrids offer a flexible solution to improve grid stability, reliability, and efficiency. However, their complex structure creates challenges in fault classification, which is vital for safe operation. Traditional supervised machine learning and deep learning need manual preprocessing and fully labeled data. This work introduces a semi-supervised convolutional neural network (SS-CNN) to classify faults in hybrid microgrids, using both labeled and unlabeled data to minimize dependence on extensive fault labels while maintaining high accuracy. The model extracts spatial–temporal features from current and voltage signals to distinguish 10 fault types under dynamic conditions. Simulation on a benchmark system shows the model achieves 99.77% accuracy, outperforming traditional methods. The results confirm SS-CNN as a robust, scalable solution for enhancing stability and reliability.

title: Machine-Learning-Assisted Wireless Power Transfer for Electric Vehicle Charging Under Coil Misalignment
authors: Maninder Kaur, Azizjon Butaev, Bhupinder Kaur, Dilshodbek Tursunov, Mandeep Kaur, Sunny Vig, Sachin Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Recent advances in wireless power transfer (WPT) have enabled contactless charging of electric vehicles (EVs), but coil misalignment and load variation still cause significant efficiency degradation and DC-link voltage fluctuations. This paper proposes a lightweight deep neural network (DNN) that predicts optimal compensation parameters in a series-series inductive WPT system to maximize powertransfer efficiency while regulating the output voltage under lateral and angular misalignment. The DNN is trained on a dataset generated from a physics-based circuit model over a wide range of misalignment and load conditions. At run time, the trained model provides real-time predictions of compensation capacitance adjustment and inverter switching frequency, which are applied through a simple supervisory controller. MATLAB/Simulink simulations show that the proposed ML-assisted controller improves system efficiency by up to 11.8% and reduces output-voltage ripple by 46.3% compared to a conventional fixed-parameter design under severe misalignment scenarios. The proposed method requires only basic sensing of coil currents and voltages and can be implemented on low-cost embedded platforms, making it suitable for practical EV WPT systems.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Friendly Jamming for Secure Multicarrier ISAC Under Channel Uncertainty
authors: Bui Minh Tuan, Van-Dinh Nguyen, Diep N. Nguyen, Nguyen Linh Trung, Nguyen Van Huynh, Dinh Thai Hoang, Marwan Krunz, Eryk Dutkiewicz
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems promise efficient spectrum utilization by jointly supporting radar sensing and wireless communication. This paper presents a deep learning-driven framework for enhancing physical-layer security in multicarrier ISAC systems under imperfect channel state information (CSI) and in the presence of unknown eaves-dropper (Eve) locations. Unlike conventional ISAC-based friendly jamming (FJ) approaches that require Eve’s CSI or precise angle-of-arrival (AoA) estimates, our method exploits radar echo feedback to guide directional jamming without explicit Eve’s information. To enhance robustness to radar sensing uncertainty, we propose a radar-aware neural network that jointly optimizes beamforming and jamming by integrating a novel nonparametric Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) estimator based on f-divergence. The jamming design satisfies the Cramér–Rao lower bound (CRLB) constraints even in the presence of noisy AoA. For efficient implementation, we introduce a quantized tensor train-based encoder that reduces the model size by more than 100 times with negligible performance loss. We also integrate a non-overlapping secure scheme into the proposed framework, in which specific sub-bands can be dedicated solely to communication. Extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed solution achieves significant improvements in secrecy rate, reduced block error rate (BLER), and strong robustness against CSI uncertainty and angular estimation errors, under-scoring the effectiveness of the proposed deep learning–driven friendly jamming framework under practical ISAC impairments.

title: Development of a Neural Network Approach to Model Magnetic Core Power Loss in High-Frequency Transformers
authors: Vittorio Bertolini, Michele Quercio, Lorenzo Sabino, Marco Stella, Antonio Faba, Francesco Riganti Fulginei, Fabio Crescimbini, Ermanno Cardelli
abstract: Accurate prediction of magnetic core losses under non-sinusoidal conditions is critical for high-frequency power converters, yet conventional sinusoidal models often fail to capture real-world behavior. This paper proposes a computationally efficient, circuit-based framework for nanocrystalline materials to bridge this gap. The model utilizes a feed-forward neural network trained exclusively on standard sinusoidal data to accurately predict complex waveforms. Implemented as a Simulink component, the framework is validated against a high-frequency transformer, showing good agreement with experimental measurements for currents and losses. Finally, a sensitivity analysis on the transformer efficiency under various input voltages and load conditions demonstrates the model's utility for system-level optimization and reliable design.

title: On-Device Visual Text Acquisition and Speech Synthesis for Assistive Applications Using Raspberry PI
authors: Shubham Acharya, Sayan Bhattacharjee, Amit Kumar Das, Sutanu Ghosh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This work presents an offline text-reading system to assist real-time visual-to-speech conversion without cloud dependency. The proposed architecture integrates Optical Character Recognition (OCR), Text-to-Speech (TTS), and voice-command control within a unified Python framework on the Raspberry Pi 4B. A structured image preprocessing pipeline using OpenCV enhances text clarity, while the LSTM-based Tesseract OCR and eSpeak TTS modules ensure efficient recognition and synthesis under constrained hardware resources. The system demonstrates robust real-time performance, maintaining reliable operation across diverse lighting and background conditions. Through optimized parallel execution and hardware–software co-design, it achieves low latency and consistent accuracy while operating entirely offline. The findings affirm the feasibility of developing compact, energy-efficient assistive technologies with text-reading capabilities without reliance on cloud services.

title: Finding Optimal Video Moment without Training: Gaussian Boundary Optimization for Weakly Supervised Video Grounding
authors: Sunoh Kim, Kimin Yun, Daeho Um
abstract: Weakly supervised temporal video grounding aims to localize query-relevant segments in untrimmed videos using only video-sentence pairs, without requiring ground-truth segment annotations that specify exact temporal boundaries. Recent approaches tackle this task by utilizing Gaussian-based temporal proposals to represent query-relevant segments. However, their inference strategies rely on heuristic mappings from Gaussian parameters to segment boundaries, resulting in suboptimal localization performance. To address this issue, we propose Gaussian Boundary Optimization (GBO), a novel inference framework that predicts segment boundaries by solving a principled optimization problem that balances proposal coverage and segment compactness. We derive a closed-form solution for this problem and rigorously analyze the optimality conditions under varying penalty regimes. Beyond its theoretical foundations, GBO offers several practical advantages: it is training-free and compatible with both single-Gaussian and mixture-based proposal architectures. Our experiments show that GBO significantly improves localization, achieving state-of-the-art results across standard benchmarks. Extensive experiments demonstrate the efficiency and generalizability of GBO across various proposal schemes. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/sunoh-kim/gbo</uri> <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/sunoh-kim/gbo</uri>.

title: Analytical Investigation of Field-Dependent Carrier Mobilities in Nanoscale GFETs Employing GO and HGO Channels
authors: Sagnik Bhattacharyya, Soumyadeep Seth, Samit Karmakar, Malay Gangopadhyay, Prativa Saha, Soumik Kumar Kundu, Ishani Ray Chaudhury, Debjit Biswas
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: This research presents the comparison of the field-dependent effective mobilities of GFETs using SLG, graphene oxide (GO), and HGO as channels at dimensions in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 1 8 ~ n m}$</tex>. Since GO and HGO utilize oxidation and hydrogenation processes for bandgap engineering, ambipolarity can be reduced and then overcome in SLG. Analyzing the transfer curve of these GFETs shows extremely high values of extracted field effect mobilities <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(10^{3}-10^{5} ~\text{cm}^{2} ~\mathrm{V}^{-1} ~\mathrm{s}^{-1}\right)$</tex> for SLG, low values of insulated regions (less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1} \text{cm}^{2} ~\mathrm{V}^{-1} ~\mathrm{s}^{-1}$</tex>) for GO, and relatively moderate values for HGO (order of 10-12 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{c m}^{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{V}^{\mathbf{- 1}} \mathbf{s}^{\mathbf{- 1}}$</tex>). Output curve measurements using MOSFET parameters also show consistent results for HGO. Additionally, electrostatic control, short-channel effect, and output resistances measured using GFETs having scaled HGO and SLG channels also support HGO as a superior variant over SLG.

title: Analysis of Combined Stresses of Partial Discharge and Operational Voltages in a Power Transformer
authors: Rahul Ray, Gaurav J. Bairagi, Jyotishman Boruah, Shabnam Ajaj, Ranjeeta Pegu, Soumyadeep Maity, Pradipta Ghosh, Kaveri Bhuyan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) activities are a major factor in insulation failure, accounting for over 60% of damage to high voltage transformers. Common causes of PD are manufacturing defects, environmental stress, and high-voltage surges. In this study, the effect of PD has been examined on minor and major insulation of a Power Transformer subjected to high voltage surges. The considered test voltages are high voltage operational surges like the Standard Lightning Impulse (SLI), Standard Switching Impulse (SSI), Oscillating Lightning Impulse (OLI), and modified SLI with tail chopped or additional overshoots. The OLI and modified SLI with superimposed oscillations or tail chopped are used to simulate the real-world like stresses on the transformer. To analyze the insulation conditions, the coherence function (CF) of the winding current is calculated at the neutral end of the transformer. CF is a statistical tool that measures the linear relationship between two signals in the frequency domain. The CF values are calculated for the simulated winding current obtained when partial discharges of varying magnitude are present versus when they are absent under different high frequency test voltages. From the results, it is found that when PD occurs across the main insulation, superimposed voltages consistently show the strongest correlation, while SLI with tailchopped voltages show minimum coherence with PD activity. The effect of varying the PD magnitude is negligible for minor insulation. This preliminary simulation study establishes a foundation for future experimental research to study the interaction of PD and test voltages on winding insulation, ultimately aiming to improve transformer performance, safety, and cost-effectiveness.

title: An Automated Testing Tool for Structural Analysis Software: Development and Validation
authors: Jun Wang, Liang Chang, Xiangyan Xu, Changxing Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The rapid development and expanding applications of structural analysis software are increasing its functional complexity and reliability requirements, significantly challenging software testing. To address inefficiency and limited test coverage, this study employs a hierarchical modular design approach to develop and implement a full-process automated testing tool. The tool integrates modules for test case design, automatic submission and solving, result extraction and storage, data comparison and analysis, and statistical report generation, supporting multi-dimensional evaluation of computational accuracy and solution efficiency. We validated the tool through regression testing of the linear static, buckling, and modal analysis functionalities in SABRE, a structural analysis system independently developed by Aircraft Strength Research Institute of China. Results demonstrate the tool's effectiveness in verifying functionality, evaluating performance, and accurately detecting artificially introduced defects. Compared to manual testing (54 hours), automation reduced testing time to 8 hours, an 85.2% improvement in efficiency. This automated tool significantly enhances regression testing efficiency and reliability, providing robust support for the iterative development and quality assurance of CAE software.

title: Composite Biomaterial for Laser-Assisted Tissue Adhesive Wound Healing
authors: Thi Thuy Truong, Thi Xuan Nguyen, Thi Nhat Linh Phan, Thi Kim Ngan Duong, Van Bang Nguyen, Vu Hoang Minh Doan, Jaeyeop Choi, Byeongil Lee, Junghwan Oh, Sudip Mondal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: This study presents a novel laser-activated adhesive for non-suture wound closure, combining chitosan (CS), bovine serum albumin (BSA), and iron oxide nanoparticles (IONPs) to achieve both mechanical sealing and photothermal therapy. The adhesive was synthesized via solvothermal production of stable IONPs, followed by composite formulation with CS and BSA. It demonstrated strong tissue adhesive, rapid-light-induced curing, and efficient photothermal activation, facilitating crosslinking and sealing. Biological assessments revealed excellent cytocompatibility with L929 fibroblasts, highlighting its potential as a multifunctional, biocompatible material for advanced surgical wound management.

title: Talent Cultivation in the Digital Economy: Evidence from China's Inbound Tourism
authors: Rong-Jun Huang, Zhi-Gang Tang, Hui Chen
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: This paper investigates how the development of the Digital Economy (DE) affects China's inbound tourism service trade by reducing trade costs (TC) and improving international service efficiency. Using balanced panel data from 22 partner countries between 2004 and 2024, the study applies a fixed-effects regression model and a mediation analysis to examine both the direct and indirect effects of DE on inbound tourism performance (IPF). The results reveal that DE significantly promotes inbound tourism, while TC serves as a partial mediator. The findings highlight that improvements in digital infrastructure, online platforms, and data connectivity enhance tourism competitiveness by reducing information and transaction frictions. This research not only enriches empirical evidence on the digital economy's trade effects but also provides valuable implications for cultivating interdisciplinary talents in higher education to support digitaleconomy transformation

title: A Designed Edge Storage System Based on SDN and UAV Assistance in Disaster Scenarios
authors: Qiuxiang Jiang, Shiquan Sun, Miao Ye, Yuan Huang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Traditional edge distributed storage systems often suffer from cumbersome network configuration and high operational overhead for measuring network state information. During peak demand periods for data storage and retrieval by terminal devices, network links can become overloaded, adversely affecting data transmission performance. Furthermore, existing distributed storage systems typically consider only the remaining storage space of nodes when selecting storage nodes, neglecting the impact of network state and node load on system storage performance. To address these issues, this paper designs and implements an edge-distributed storage system assisted by software-defined network (SDN) and drones. The system uses SDN technology to measure network state, node load, and storage node load information. Drones fly above heavily loaded network nodes to offload traffic and balance the load across different links. For the selection of heavily loaded network nodes and storage nodes, this paper proposes a node selection algorithm based on a multiattribute decision model that comprehensively considers network state and node load. The algorithm identifies heavily loaded network nodes and suitable storage nodes, and the deployment of drones helps achieve traffic offloading and load balancing. Experimental tests on a wireless Mesh network topology demonstrate that the proposed wireless edge-distributed storage system outperforms existing edge-distributed storage systems in terms of storage performance. The proposed system significantly reduces storage time and maintains good performance even under increased traffic load, demonstrating excellent load-balancing capabilities.

title: An Improved Densenet Architecture Integrated with Attention Mechanism for Potato Seedling Stage Recognition
authors: Changhui He, Xiangjuan Wu, Yongning Zhu, Wenjuan Jin, Xiaoli Wang, Tingting Zhang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of modern agricultural modernization, object recognition technology has gradually become a key means for monitoring crop growth and development stages. As one of the widely cultivated food crops globally, the accurate identification of the potato seedling emergence (PSE) stage is important for promoting the modernization of its planting and management. Traditional PSE detection methods mainly rely on manual observation, resulting in low efficiency and strong subjectivity. To address this challenge, we use images captured by 8 sets of real-scene detection systems for potato farmlands in Ningxia from 2019 to 2023 as raw data. The images were labeled to construct a dataset of PSE. Then, an improved DenseNet-based algorithm is proposed to realize the automatic recognition of the PSE stage. The proposed algorithm takes DenseNet-121 pre-trained on ImageNet as the backbone network. Then, a lightweight attention mechanism is embedded to guide the model to focus on the key regions of seedlings after extracting global features, which enhances the ability to extract critical features for the model. Subsequently, a multi-model fusion optimization strategy is employed to integrate the prediction results of multiple DenseNet models, improving the accuracy and stability of the final predictions. Performance tests of the proposed algorithm are carried out on the constructed dataset. The results show that the proposed algorithm is a significant advantage compared with other models, achieving an accuracy of over 95% in PSE detection, with precision, recall, and F1-score all exceeding 93%.

title: A Ground-Based Verification Method for the on-Orbit Tracking Function and Performance Evaluation of Space Targets
authors: Nan Wang, Qi Zhou, Senhao Jia, He Jin, Xiaonan Mao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the challenges of ground-based verification for the on-orbit tracking function and performance of space targets, this study proposes a systematic ground verification method. The method integrates three types of ground tracking tests—point targets, surface targets, and dynamic targets—to comprehensively evaluate the static accuracy and dynamic performance of space target tracking systems. For point target tracking, planets are selected as the tracking source. By simulating the positions of the eight major planets algorithmically based on the observer's latitude, planets entering the tracking field of view are chosen for verification, ensuring the system's static accuracy. Surface target tracking employs the Moon as a large-scale object to assess the system's ability to track close-range targets, which is critical for applications involving proximity operations. Dynamic target tracking utilizes drones to simulate moving objects, with controlled flight paths used to evaluate the system's responsiveness and adaptability to dynamic scenarios. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method is highly operable, with controllable errors and reproducible outcomes. It effectively validates the static accuracy and dynamic performance metrics of the tested system, such as tracking stability and response speed. By leveraging natural celestial bodies (planets, Moon) and controllable artificial targets (drones), this approach provides a versatile framework for ground-based validation. The methodology not only reduces reliance on expensive orbital testing but also offers a scalable solution for evaluating the functional and performance capabilities of on-orbit space target tracking systems. This work serves as a practical reference for advancing the development and reliability of space situational awareness technologies.

title: Accurate Prediction of Aerodynamic Parameters of Intelligent Flight Test Based on Deep Learning
authors: Rongqiang Bai, Li Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Aerodynamic parameter prediction of intelligent flight test plays a key role in aircraft performance evaluation. To solve the problems of poor adaptability and insufficient nonlinear modeling ability of traditional prediction methods in complex flight states, an intelligent prediction method based on particle swarm optimization algorithm and Transformer fusion is proposed in this paper. By integrating <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 9 6}$</tex> million sets of heterogeneous multi-source data of a certain type of aircraft flight test system, a time series data set covering flight state parameters, environmental parameters and aerodynamic parameters is constructed. PSO algorithm is used to optimize the hidden layer nodes and learning rate of Transformer model globally. The experimental results show that the prediction accuracy of PSO-Transformer model is significantly improved compared with ARIMA, SVR and standard Transformer. The root mean square error of PSO-Transformer model is reduced to 0.0091, and the coefficient of determination is 0.941. Furthermore, a supplementary experiment on crosswind landing scenarios demonstrates that expanding training data diversity significantly enhances the model's robustness and generalization, with the RMSE of key lateral parameters reduced by over 18 %. The intelligent prediction method provides a high precision aerodynamic characteristic prediction scheme for intelligent flight test.

title: Privacy-Preserving Rare Disease Diagnosis Via Integrated AI and Fully Homomorphic Encryption
authors: Vu-Thu-Nguyet Pham, N. Nga Nguyen, Valeria Gottardo, Quy Vo-Reinhard
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Rare disease diagnosis faces two major challenges: prolonged diagnostic odysseys for patients and stringent privacy requirements for sensitive genomic and phenotypic data. This paper presents a privacy-preserving framework that allows accurate prediction of rare diseases while maintaining full confidentiality of patient data through Full Homomorphic Encryption (FHE). Our solution achieves 99.8 percent top-1 diagnostic accuracy in 400+ rare diseases, matching plain text methods despite the computational overhead inherent in FHE. Although encryption increases prediction latency by 1,363× to 2,388× compared to unencrypted methods, the system remains clinically feasible, generating predictions for 446 diseases in under two hours on standard hardware. By securing genomic variant analysis and phenotypic symptom matching, this approach enables cross-institutional collaboration without exposing sensitive patient data, addressing a critical bottleneck in rare disease research. The framework's integration with standardized ontologies (e.g., Human Phenotype Ontology) and real-world genomic pipelines ensures compatibility with existing clinical infrastructure. These advancements represent a significant step toward global, secure rare disease databases, empowering clinicians to leverage collective knowledge while complying with GDPR, HIPAA, and other privacy regulations.

title: Real-Time Nonlinear Model Predictive Control of Large Multirotor Aircraft
authors: Jan Zwiener, Johannes Stephan, Christoph Seiferth
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This brief introduces a nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC) scheme for multirotor electric Vertical Take-off and Landing (eVTOL) aircrafts with many actuators. We present a novel modeling approach using real-time iteration (RTI), which directly incorporates constraints on numerous actuators, effectively handling common control allocation issues within the model predictive control (MPC) formulation. This approach in particular accounts for the slower actuation system dynamics inherent to large-scale multirotors while satisfying real-time embedded computational constraints. Hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) tests on a Jetson TX2 confirm up to ~70% faster solve times at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N=32$</tex-math> </inline-formula> while maintaining the flight stability.

title: Transformer-Based 3D Hand Pose Estimation via Bidirectional Multiscale Fusion and Learnable Anchor Guidance
authors: Tingting Liu, Ji Gan, Weiqiang Wang, Feng Gao, Jiaxu Leng, Haosheng Chen, Xinbo Gao
abstract: Accurate 3D hand pose estimation faces inherent challenges, including self-occlusions, joint similarities, and high degrees of freedom. Although most existing CNN-based or Transformer-based methods leverage global contexts, they often fail to capture fine-grained local details and robustly handle occluded joints. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Transformer framework for depth-based 3D hand pose estimation, which incorporates two key designs: the Bidirectional Multiscale Fusion and Learnable Anchor Guidance. Firstly, we propose bidirectional multiscale fusion that sequentially propa-gates features from different encoder levels in both top-down and bottom-up directions, followed by a final aggregation of all scale features. Such an intricate feature interaction eventually enables effective joint modeling of fine-grained local details (e.g., fingertip positions) and high-level semantic context information (e.g., palm orientation). Secondly, we introduce the learnable anchor query as prior guidance to dynamically guide the decoder to localize ambiguous joints better. The learnable anchors are derived from joint-specific attention maps under 3D ground-truth supervision and then are concatenated with static grid anchors to form hybrid anchors, which effectively enable more precise 3D hand pose estimation, especially for occlusions. To further demonstrate the practicality of our framework for IoT-oriented deployment, we conduct edge-device experiments to validate its deployment feasibility. Experiments on benchmark datasets (including NYU, ICVL, MSRA, and DexYCB) demonstrate the superiority of our method over previous state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Flexible Reconfiguration Strategy for Novel Advanced Traction Power Supply Equipment Under Fault Conditions
authors: Ziyi Chen, Xin Wang, Qi Guo, Chunming Tu, Fan Xiao, Junhao Li
abstract: The novel advanced traction power supply equipment (NATPSE), incorporating a two-phase input to single-phase (2AC-AC) conversion unit, provides an effective solution to eliminate neutral sections and improve power quality in electric railways. However, the high integration of active components in this 2AC-AC conversion unit is regarded as the cause of higher fault probability, resulting in insufficient power supply, negative-sequence currents, and even system instability. To address these limitations, a flexible reconfiguration strategy for NATPSE is proposed. Through coordinated control of the controllable switches and the healthy port of the 2AC-AC conversion unit, islanded operation and power supply path reconfiguration of the faulted traction station are achieved. As a result, a 100%-rated power supply is maintained, and 100% suppression of negative-sequence currents is realized. Moreover, power supply is maintained within 0.2 s after port fault occurrence, without adversely affecting the traction load. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed strategy is verified by simulation and experiments.

title: Data-Driven Temperature Modelling of Machine Tools by Neural Networks: A Benchmark
authors: Cecília Coelho, Michael Hohmann, Darío Fernández, Lars Penter, Steffen Ihlenfeldt, Oliver Niggemann
abstract: Thermal errors in machine tools significantly impact machining precision and productivity. Traditional thermal error correction/compensation methods rely on measured temperature-deformation fields or on transfer functions. Most existing data-driven compensation strategies employ neural networks (NNs) to directly predict thermal errors or specific compensation values. While effective, these approaches are tightly bound to particular error types, spatial locations, or machine configurations, limiting their generality and adaptability. In this work, we introduce a novel paradigm in which NNs are trained to predict high-fidelity temperature and heat flux fields within the machine tool. The proposed framework enables subsequent computation and correction of a wide range of error types using modular, swappable downstream components. TheNNis trained using data obtained with the finite element method under varying initial conditions and incorporates a correlation-based selection strategy that identifies the most informative measurement points, minimising hardware requirements during inference. We further benchmark state-of-the-art time-series NN architectures, namely Recurrent NN, Gated Recurrent Unit, Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM), Bidirectional LSTM, Transformer, and Temporal Convolutional Network, by training both specialised models, tailored for specific initial conditions, and general models, capable of extrapolating to unseen scenarios. The results show accurate and cost-effective predictions of temperature and heat flux fields, which lay the groundwork for flexible and generic thermal error correction through a shift from direct error prediction to spatio-temporal thermal field modelling.
