title: CAAFNet: An Edge-Optimized Context-Aware Mask-Based Face Authentication Framework for IoMT Environments
authors: R Praveen, S Balaji
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Face authentication in the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) is challenging with facial occlusions, which hide important features and reduce recognition accuracy. Existing solutions typically employ fixed facial representations, relying either on full-face features that are sensitive to occlusion or on periocular regions with limited discriminative capacity. Existing approaches are not well suited for real-world clinical scenarios where facial visibility varies dynamically. The paper introduces CAAFNet, a lightweight and context-aware face authentication approach that adapts feature extraction based on occlusions. The framework incorporates region-aware identity embedding networks to selectively utilize full-face features for unmasked inputs and periocular features when facial occlusion is detected. To address uncertainty in mask detection, a confidence-aware fusion strategy is employed to combine region-specific embeddings, ensuring stable authentication performance without additional computational overhead.The proposed framework is assessed using the RMFD dataset, attaining a verification accuracy of 98.7%, a False Acceptance Rate of(FAR) 0.8%, and a False Rejection Rate(FRR) of 0.9 %. The system operates in real time at approximately 60 frames per second on CPU-only hardware, while maintaining a compact model size of 14.5 MB. The results demonstrate that CAAFNet achieves an effective balance between accuracy, efficiency and practical deployability for secure facial authentication in IoMT networks.

title: Architectural Assessment of a CIM-OR hard-block for FPGAs
authors: Bharat Singh Choudhary, Arvind Rajawat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: In conventional Field Programable Gate Array (FPGA) fabrics, the separation of processing and memory results in significant performance and area losses for broad, bitparallel workloads. Although architectural exploration is prevalent, but the effectiveness of fine-grained, memory-centric primitives is still not well explored. This study presents a stateful Compute-In-Memory OR (CIM-OR) primitive, incorporated as a hard block within a typical FPGA architecture. We show that this fine-grained specialization leads to big improvements in architecture. Our CIM-enhanced design gets a performance boost of up to $158 \%(2.5 \mathrm{x})$ and a cut in logic area and Configurable Logic Blocks (CLBs) utilization by 70%, and reduced delay up to 61% compared to a normal LUT-based implementation when we do a scalability analysis on an N-bit ALU test. These results confirm that our strategy is a strong way to make FPGAs that are denser and have higher throughput, which is what we need for bitwise processing.

title: A Japanese Pattern Design Support System Using Image Words and Color Scheme Algorithm
authors: Shiori Sasaki, Moe Endo
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This paper presents a design and implementation method for a Japanese pattern design support system using image words and a color scheme technique algorithm. Since Japanese patterns are not registered as trademarks, anyone can design their own favorite Japanese patterns for various applications. However, it is difficult to design culturally informed and aesthetically refined patterns for ordinal users because each Japanese pattern has its own meaning and color arrangement requires knowledge of color science. Therefore, we propose a system that automatically generates Japanese patterns by selecting adjectives and nouns, which reflect the meaning of the corresponding patterns and knowledge of color arrangement. In addition, this system is designed to promote the use of Japanese patterns both Japanese and international users. With the increasing involvement of international creators in producing Japanese-themed content across cultural and artistic domains, there is a growing need for systems that enable entertainment content generation based on expert knowledge of Japanese cultural assets.

title: A Holistic Evaluation of the Safeline WAF: A Study on Performance Scalability and Defense Efficacy
authors: Tuul Triyason, Ukrit Ruckcharti, Thanh Duy Vo, Montri Supattatham
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: A Web Application Firewall (WAF) is a critical component in securing modern Web Applications. However, its deployment often impacts system performance and latency, making a comprehensive evaluation of both performance and protection essential. This paper presents a holistic assessment of Safeline, a modern container-based WAF solution, through two primary investigations: performance scalability and defense efficacy against selected OWASP Top 10 (2021) categories. The experiments were conducted in a virtualized environment. Performance scalability analysis used load testing tools to measure throughput sustained under varying allocations of vCPU and RAM resources. The evaluation of defense efficacy used penetration testing tools to evaluate the protection capabilities of WAF against different attack categories, tested under both default and customized rule configurations. The results indicate that WAF performance scales significantly with the number of vCPUs, while RAM size has only a minor effect. The reverse proxy of Safeline was identified as the main bottleneck in normal traffic. Furthermore, a diminishing returns effect and a performance anomaly were observed in the 4-vCPU configuration. From a security perspective, the default rule set provided partial protection; full coverage of the tested selected OWASP Top 10 (2021) categories required additional custom rule configurations. This study highlights the trade-off between performance and security, emphasizing the importance of proper resource allocation, alongside tailored security rule adjustments. The findings provide practical guidance for system administrators and security practitioners in planning and deploying WAF solutions for optimal performance and protection.

title: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Efficient and Accurate Brain Tumor Detection
authors: Swagatam Adak, Abhigyan Kulavi, Sanchari Chatterjee, Baisakhi Das
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The paper aims to give a comparative analysis of deep learning models for the classification of brain tumors, using a hybrid dataset of brain MRI images, based on the performance evaluation of four models: NeuroTumorNet, ImageNetXception, TumorDetectCNN, and TumorClassifier. It is crucial to identify the best model for tumor classification while giving attention to the efficiency in training time, which becomes critical in practical deployment. Models were compared based on test accuracy, test loss, and training time. NeuroTumorNet turned out to be the winner with a test accuracy score of 99.69 % and a low test loss score of 0.0031, which puts it as the best accuracy model. On the other hand, ImageNetXception exploited transfer learning to finish training in just 10 epochs unlike NeuroTumorNet which needed 50 epochs and offered a highly efficient model with a test accuracy of 99.24 %, the quickest among the models. This innovation in using pre-trained networks demonstrated a considerable reduction in training time without any major sacrifice in accuracy. Finally, it balances the accuracy and training efficiency by providing valuable insights into the real-world applications involving brain tumor detection.

title: Time-Hopping Covert Communication With Random Slots and Dispersed Energy
authors: Kun Lu, Zhe Song, Shuai Wang, Tingting Li, Gaofeng Pan, Heng Liu, George Karagiannidis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The growth of communication technologies has led to a corresponding increase in the need for information security. The use of covert communication techniques has the potential to significantly reduce the likelihood of the transmitted signal being intercepted by unintended receivers, thereby significantly increasing the security of the information. In this paper, we propose an energy-dispersed random time-hopping (ED-RTH) covert communication scheme. Building on existing time uncertainty schemes, this scheme further enhances time uncertainty through random time-hopping, while dispersing signal energy to achieve stronger covertness. We present a model for analyzing the covertness of the ED-RTH scheme. Given the complexity of the likelihood ratio distribution, the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence is used to analyze the lower bound of Willie’s detection error probability. Through constructing behavioral-level simulations for validation, we investigated the impact of the number of dispersed time slots K on both Willie’s detection error probability and the system’s covert throughput. Finally we compared the proposed ED-RTH scheme with existing time uncertainty scheme, and the results demonstrate that the ED-RTH scheme achieves significantly enhanced covertness.

title: A Revocable CP-ABSE Scheme with Efficient Multi-Keyword Boolean Search for Outsourced EHRs
authors: Maneesha Perera, Somchart Fugkeaw
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Searchable Encryption (SE) enables keyword-based retrieval over encrypted data while preserving confidentiality, yet integrating efficient access control and revocation in dynamic multi-user settings remains challenging. Existing Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption (CPABSE) schemes suffer from high computational costs, complex re-encryption during revocation, and poor scalability for expressive Boolean queries. To address these limitations, this paper presents an Efficient and Revocable CP-ABSE framework for Electronic Health Records (EHRs) that supports multi-keyword Boolean search, fine-grained access control, and lightweight attribute revocation. The proposed system integrates puncturable encryption for instantaneous revocation without re-encrypting all ciphertexts, and employs proxy re-encryption (PRE) at fog nodes to securely update affected ciphertexts. A hybrid multi-indexing strategy combining inverted, bitmap, and Merkle-based indexes enables scalable complex query execution, while a blockchain layer anchors integrity proofs, revocation records, and audit trails. Experimental evaluation using synthetic EHR datasets on AWS demonstrates that our scheme achieves up to 47% faster search performance and 62% lower revocation latency compared to recent baselines. The results confirm that the proposed design provides a secure, verifiable, and high-performance foundation for privacy-preserving healthcare data retrieval in cloud-fog environments.

title: Fitness Monitoring and Personalized Wellness Guidance Using Electronic Health Records
authors: Subodh Bist, Lopa Mandal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In our current fast-paced society, it is very difficult to fit ourselves into a good lifestyle, and people often find it difficult to make decisions about good health. In order to determine who may be more susceptible to heart disease, we developed and tested an artificial intelligence (AI) system that analyzes blood pressure, body weight, cholesterol, and glucose levels using data from real-world medical records. Using contemporary explainable AI (SHAP) techniques, the system not only forecasts risk but also explains why. It functions similarly to a smart assistant, assisting medical professionals and individuals in making individualized wellness decisions and understanding their heart health in simple terms. Systems providing health and wellness recommendations are tailored to meet the specific needs of each user based upon their personal style, health status and fitness level. Health & wellness Systems use data analytics to analyze health and fitness requirements based on data collected from various individuals. Logistic Regression, Random Forest Classifier and Gradient Boosting Classifier is used to classify the data. Gradient Boosting Classifier is the best algorithm out of the models reviewed and achieved 99% accuracy in a 75:25 training-to-testing ratio. We also address health issues by prescribing adequate food and exercise suggestions per patient's requirement. According to the findings of this study, Gradient Boosting Classifier emerges as the best possible model for this research.

title: Decentralized E-Voting with Biometric Authentication: A Framework for Secure and Transparent Elections
authors: Suman Kumar, Deepan Halder, Ankesh Kumar, Nirban Kumar Saha, Madhumita Pal, Sanjoy Mondal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Electronic voting systems must preserve voter privacy while ensuring that the process remains secure, transparent, and accessible. This paper presents a comprehensive blockchain-based electronic voting system that integrates biometric verification with Proof of Authority (PoA) consensus. The system combines elliptic curve cryptography with digital tokens called V-Coins to guarantee that no voter can cast more than one ballot. The security of the system is evaluated against eleven attack types, including replay attacks, man-in-the-middle attacks, double voting, and Sybil attacks, demonstrating cryptographic strength comparable to real-world systems. A functioning prototype utilizing R307 fingerprint sensors, a custom Python-based blockchain, and Oracle Cloud validator nodes achieved an 85% fingerprint verification success rate. The end-to-end voting process takes approximately 10-20 seconds. Voters can safely cast ballots, view results in real time, and audit the process without revealing their identity, using encrypted transactions and anonymous vote identifiers. Compared with traditional paper voting and electronic voting machines, the proposed system offers significantly higher transparency, security, and speed. This study proposes a practically implementable architecture designed for scalability to national-level elections, while addressing major deficiencies in current e-voting systems.

title: A Multi-Source Data Visual Recognition and Grading Model for Student Pickleball Skill Levels in University Physical Education
authors: Aiming Zeng, Weijie Zhong
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: Accurately assessing students' motor skill levels is crucial for implementing differentiated instruction and precision teaching in university physical education (PE) courses. Addressing the significant variation in student skill levels within pickleball PE classes, this paper proposes a visual representation learning-based model for predicting student performance. This research integrates multi-source teaching data to construct eight feature dimensions, including gender, general sports foundation, pickleball training duration, serve success rate, technical stability, match point differential, physical fitness test index, and inclass performance evaluation. The core innovation of the model lies in its transformation of traditional one-dimensional feature data into image-like two-dimensional heatmaps, which are subsequently processed using an improved YOLOv8 classification model. To enhance the network's feature extraction capability for this specific data pattern, a Lightweight Adaptive Extraction (LAE) module is introduced into the backbone network. Experimental results demonstrate that our model achieves a top accuracy of 96.3% on a real-world teaching dataset, outperforming traditional methods. This solution proves to be an efficient and precise tool for grading student pickleball skill levels, providing reliable decision support for PE teachers in implementing differentiated instruction.

title: RxGuardian: An Uncertainty-Aware Dose-Sanity and Geriatric Deprescribing Planner Using Standards-Driven Unit Algebra, Renal/Hepatic Adjustment, and Constraint-Based Reasoning
authors: Korboon Ariyapol, Aueaphum Aueawatthanaphisut
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: A standards-first and auditable pipeline for medication order verification and geriatric deprescribing is presented. The system unifies (i) unit/scale normalization using UCUM with daily-dose consolidation, (ii) organ-aware adjustment via CKD-EPI 2021 for renal function and hepatically guided multipliers, and (iii) transparent constraints from Beers 2023 and STOPP/START v3. A gated TabTransformer (RxGuardianDetect) scores order-level risk, followed by post-hoc temperature scaling for probability calibration and selective risk control to set deployable thresholds. Candidate alerts are prioritized using a learning-to-rank module (ClinAlert-LTR), and deprescribing plans are produced by a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1}$</tex> integer linear program (PolyDepILP) under guideline clauses. In model-centric evaluation on a held-out split without patient overlap, higher AUROC and average precision were observed against logistic and rulesonly baselines; reliability improved after calibration; and alert burden-precision trade-offs were tunable by the target risk level. Ranking quality measured by NDCG@K indicated earlier surfacing of high-utility alerts. These results support the integration of standards-aligned features, calibrated detection, and constraint-based planning as a transparent foundation for prospective clinical evaluation.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Image Captioning with Cultural Bias Mitigation
authors: Chetany Kalaneya, Gaurav Ganguly, Poonam Poonia, Kunal Sharma, Rohit Kumawat
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Traditional image captioning systems often generate linguistically accurate but culturally and gender-biased captions, perpetuating stereotypes that undermine ethical AI deployment. This work proposes a novel multimodal deep learning architecture that seamlessly integrates Vision Transformer (ViT) for robust visual feature extraction, a GPT-like Transformer for sequential text generation, and a dynamic bias mitigation layer to enforce fairness during inference. Bias detection leverages VADER sentiment analysis for emotional neutrality and CLIPbased semantic alignment for cultural coherence, enabling realtime probability adjustments to suppress stereotypical tokens. Trained and evaluated on the Flickr8k dataset-a benchmark comprising 8,000 diverse images and 40,000 human-annotated captions-the model achieves state-of-the-art results: BLEU score of 0.67, METEOR of 0.52, ROUGE-L of 0.63, a Fairness Index of 0.08 (representing a 72% reduction over baselines), and CLIP alignment of 91.7%. Comparative analysis against established models, including Show and Tell, NIC, Transformer Caption, and CLIP+GPT, demonstrates consistent superiority in both linguistic accuracy and bias mitigation. Ablation studies confirm the efficacy of the fairness-aware loss function, while qualitative examples illustrate the system's ability to produce inclusive outputs. This framework advances ethical multimodal AI by balancing descriptive fidelity with cultural inclusivity, paving the way for bias-resilient applications in accessibility, content moderation, and social media analysis.

title: Crop Disease Prediction and Regulation Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Ayan Patra, Arnab Pal, Ankur Sil, Soham Kanti Bishnu, Samit Karmakar, Malay Ganguly
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In response to the challenges presented by unpredictable weather changes and pest invasions, this initiative aims to create a software program designed to assist farmers in identifying plant diseases and detecting pests. The main goal is to provide agricultural producers with a tool that allows for swift detection, which in turn enables informed decisions regarding crop management. Utilizing machine learning methods and sophisticated image analysis techniques, the proposed software is expected to deliver precise identification of pests and diseases. This technological solution is promising in its potential to significantly reduce crop losses and lower dependence on synthetic chemicals in agriculture. Convolutional neural networks are commonly used in this field for image classification tasks due to their remarkable ability to autonomously extract relevant visual features and understand spatial patterns. The suggested software tool is set to be an invaluable asset for growers around the world, providing an easy way to enhance their skills in addressing increasing threats to agricultural health. With this advancement, the agricultural sector can move toward a more environmentally friendly and resilient approach to farming, ensuring long-term sustainability and production in the face of challenging environmental conditions.

title: Solar-Powered IoT-Based Air Quality Regulator for Indoor Air Purification
authors: Aahana Chowdhury, Soumyadeep Bit, Abhishek Majee, Aritra Biswas, Mili Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The citizens of the entire globe are extremely concerned about the presence of fine particulate matter, elevated AQI, poisonous gases, germs, and allergens. Many more people are losing their lives in the world due to lung cancer, COPD, and allergies. A solar-powered IoT-based air quality regulator has been designed and implemented to assist in keeping this under control. It aids in reducing indoor air contamination and healthy lifestyle. It purifies the air automatically and on a real-time basis. It has inbuilt air quality sensors such as MQ135, MQ2, MQ7, and DHT22, which provide it with real-time information about the environment. A microcontroller known as the Node MCU32s processes the data offered by the sensors. The system uses a three-step filtration method, which consists of a HEPA filter, a UV-C band filter, and an activated carbon layer to filter viruses/bacteria, particulate matter (up to 0.2 to 0.3 micron), volatile organic compounds (VOCs), harmful gases, etc. The ThingSpeak platform, which is a cloud-based IoT platform, offers system analysis and comparison in real-time. It gives a graphical report on the environmental parameters of the air coming in and the air leaving the device. A cost-effective, scalable, and eco-friendly solution has been developed. It enhances the indoor air quality.

title: Decompose, Route, Retrieve: A Modular NLP Pipeline Implementing Multi-Hop Architecture for Answering Complex Questions Over Heterogeneous Sources
authors: Debanjan Shome, Ambarish Sengupta, Sainik Kr. Mahata
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: We present a modular, multi-hop question answering (QA) pipeline that implements a “Decompose, Route, Retrieve” architecture to synthesize information from heterogeneous knowledge sources. Unlike monolithic QA systems, our five-stage pipeline orchestrates a sequence of specialized transformer models to dynamically query structured (Wikidata), encyclopedic (Wikipedia), and real-time (NewsAPI) data. This paper validates the core architecture's reasoning and filtering capabilities on the widely-used HotpotQA benchmark. The system first decomposes a complex query into simpler, self-contained questions using a text-to-text generation model (google/flan-t5-large), augmented by a programmatic reference resolution layer to resolve anaphora in sub-queries. Each sub-query is then routed by a zero-shot classification model (facebook/bart-large-mnli) to the optimal data source. Retrieval from structured sources is driven by a Vector-Space Semantic Mapper, which embeds Knowledge Graph properties to bridge natural language and SPARQL schemas without task-specific training. Retrieved evidence is semantically assessed using a sentence-transformer model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) to compute cosine similarity, filtering out any evidence with a score below a 0.5 threshold. Finally, the validated evidence snippets are synthesized into a coherent answer by an instruction-tuned model (google/flan-t5-large). This modular architecture provides an extensible framework for complex QA, demonstrating a robust method for managing information flow across disparate data types.

title: Analytical IGBT Modeling for Rapid TCAD Switching Simulations of Power Diodes
authors: Calvin Stephen, Greca Jean-Charles, Sophie Ngo, Luong Viêt Phung, Christophe Raynaud, Dominique Planson
abstract: TCAD simulations of the switching of power diodes are essential in the development process. Power diodes are optimized structurally to be compliant with the specifications required by the targeted applications. For silicon power diodes, carrier lifetime engineering is performed to modify the trade-off between the forward voltage drop (Vf) and the reverse recovery charges (Qrr). To precisely simulate the switching characteristics of the diode, the test circuit needs to be accurately modeled in the simulator. The industry standard for switching test circuits is the Double Pulse Test bench (DPT). It is essential to accurately model the power transistor in the test bench which is often an IGBT or a MOSFET. A finite element analysis (FEA) model of the transistor is ideal but is not necessarily available and increases simulation time drastically. This paper demonstrates that a simple analytical approach to modeling the characteristics of an IGBT is sufficient to perform mixed mode switching simulations of a lifetime engineered silicon power diodes and reduces simulation time.

title: TransformerXL-GAN-Based Secure Forecasting Architecture for Mitigating Algal Bloom-Induced Sensor Spoofing in Marine IoT
authors: Jing Yang, Muhammad Umair Ali, Muhammad Attique Khan, Jamel Baili, Zaffar Ahmed Shaikh, Nazik Alturki, Por Lip Yee, Vijay Govindarajan, Seung-Won Lee
abstract: The growing reliance on Internet of Things (IoT) networks in marine environments has highlighted concerns about sensor spoofing attacks, particularly under the disruptive influence of harmful algal blooms (HABs). These blooms introduce considerable biological noise and sensor drift, creating a challenging environment where distinguishing between genuine environmental anomalies and deliberate adversarial attacks becomes difficult. This study tackles the pressing issue of spoofing detection in marine IoT systems affected by bloom-induced interference, aiming to offer a solution that is both accurate and interpretable. The proposed TransformerXL-GAN with explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) framework combines TransformerXL’s long-term temporal modeling with GAN’s adversarial robustness while incorporating SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP)-based interpretability for clearer decision-making insights. Additional strategies, such as event-aware data augmentation, temporal anomaly filtering, and hybrid Cascaded Hyperparameter–Multi-Stage Adaptation (CH-MSA) optimization, further bolster learning stability and detection precision. Validation is conducted using the MARSS-2025 dataset, a comprehensive collection of marine sensor readings from the Norwegian Institute for Water Research. Experimental outcomes reveal that the TransformerXL-GAN+XAI framework surpasses both traditional and cutting-edge deep learning methods, achieving an impressive 98.8% accuracy and an AUC of 0.995. An accompanying ablation study underscores the collective benefit of each architectural component. These findings demonstrate the framework’s potential to deliver reliable, transparent, and biologically adaptive spoofing detection in complex marine IoT environments.

title: Benchmarking Semantic Segmentation Models via Appearance and Geometry Attribute Editing_supp1-3674640.pdf
authors: Kongming Liang
abstract: Semantic segmentation takes pivotal roles in various applications such as autonomous driving and medical image analysis. When deploying segmentation models in practice, it is critical to test their behaviors in varied and complex scenes in advance. In this paper, we construct an automatic data generation pipeline Gen4Seg to stress-test semantic segmentation models by generating various challenging samples with different attribute changes. Beyond previous evaluation paradigms focusing solely on global weather and style transfer, we investigate variations in both appearance and geometry attributes at the object and image level. These include object color, material, size, position, as well as image-level variations such as weather and style. To achieve this, we propose to edit visual attributes of existing real images with precise control of structural information, empowered by diffusion models. In this way, the existing segmentation labels can be reused for the edited images, which greatly reduces the labor costs of constructing datasets. Using our pipeline, we construct two new benchmarks, Pascal-EA and COCO-EA. We benchmark a broad variety of semantic segmentation models, spanning from conventional close-set models to recent open-vocabulary large models.We have several key findings: 1) advanced open-vocabulary models do not exhibit greater robustness compared to closed-set methods under geometric variations; 2) traditional data augmentation techniques, such as CutOut and CutMix, are limited in enhancing robustness against appearance variations; 3) our generation pipeline can also be employed as a data augmentation tool and improve both in-distribution and out-of-distribution performances. Our work suggests the potential of generative models as effective tools for automatically analyzing segmentation models, and we hope our findings will assist practitioners and researchers in developing more robust and reliable segmentation models. Codes and datasets are available at https://github.com/PRIS-CV/Pascal-EA.

title: Large AI Models-Driven Immersive Communication
authors: Yang Ma, Wenchi Cheng, Jingqing Wang, Wei Zhang, Hailin Zhang
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) mobile networks are poised to bridge the physical and virtual worlds, transforming how people work, entertain, and interact. However, delivering high-quality immersive media over 6G entails stringent demands on transmission rate, latency, and reliability, posing significant technical challenges. Recent advancements in large AI models (LAMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in multimodal understanding, large-scale knowledge modeling, and powerful content generation. These capabilities enable LAMs to process and integrate modalities such as text, images, audio, and video, and to generate semantically rich content from compact representations, creating new opportunities for immersive communication. Leveraging these capabilities, we propose a LAMs-driven immersive communication (LAMs-IC) framework that extends the principles of semantic communication for immersive media, ensuring efficient communication with minimal channel overhead. LAMs-IC adopts a hierarchical architecture consisting of cloud, edge, and terminal layers, which collaboratively support semantic abstraction, compression, and multimodal generation. Within this architecture, terminal data is transmitted to the edge layer, where cloud-pretrained LAMs utilize their semantic understanding and generative capabilities to compress information and generate task-specific multimodal content. Consequently, the system facilitates efficient data transmission and high-quality reconstruction, even under bandwidth-constrained conditions, thereby supporting immersive communication. Experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of the LAMs-IC framework in terms of reconstruction quality and symbol efficiency. Finally, we outline key challenges that must be addressed to realize the full potential of LAMs-IC in future 6G immersive media applications.

title: Efficient and Secure Dynamic Auditing and Deduplication in Multi-Cloud Storage
authors: Xiaoxu Zhang, Rongxi Wang, Keyan Chen, Guanxiong Ha, Chunfu Jia
abstract: Existing schemes that combine provable data possession (PDP) and proof of ownership (PoW) for data integrity and efficient deduplication face several practical shortcomings. First, integrity tag generation relies on data block indices or user keys, causing redundant tags for duplicate data and increasing storage overhead. Second, dynamic operations like data insertion and deletion require linear scanning, resulting in a high computational complexity of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$O(N)$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Furthermore, in multi-cloud backup environments, existing mechanisms struggle to ensure consistency between data ciphertexts and their tags. If an audit fails, locating damaged replicas requires repetitive verification by the third-party auditor (TPA), leading to high inefficiency. To overcome these issues, this paper proposes a multi-cloud data auditing mechanism (MDAM), which is based on an authenticated data structure called variable merkle hash tree (VMHT). MDAM utilizes message-derived RSA tags and secret-sharing-based proxy re-signing to achieve efficient deduplication and secure user-tag association. By integrating updatable block-level message-locked encryption (UMLE) technology with a variable branching structure, it supports dynamic updates with an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$O(\log N)$</tex-math></inline-formula> complexity, saving storage and improving update efficiency. Additionally, MDAM employs a dynamic Bloom filter for efficient fault localization. Experimental results demonstrate that MDAM surpasses existing schemes in computational overhead, update efficiency, and fault localization performance.

title: An Overview on Safety-Critical Control Under Disturbances: A Control Barrier Function Approach
authors: Jun Yang, Xinming Wang, Xiang Yu, Shihua Li, Lei Guo
abstract: Safety-critical control systems, such as autonomous vehicles and robotics, must operate reliably in complex, dynamic environments where ensuring both stability and strict adherence to safety constraints is essential. Here, safety refers to maintaining state forward invariance within a desired safe set. The control barrier function (CBF)-based safety-critical control (CBF-SC) has emerged as a promising framework, enabling control systems to maintain states within specified forward invariant sets. However, real-world applications often encounter undesirable disturbances and uncertainties, complicating the implementation of CBF and potentially compromising safety guarantees. This article provides a comprehensive survey of recent advances in CBF-based safety-critical control, with a particular focus on handling disturbances and uncertainties. The article begins with an exploration of the foundational principles of the control Lyapunov function and CBF, then reviews and compares strategies to improve robustness in CBF-SC systems, drawing on robust control, adaptive control, and disturbance/uncertainty estimation and attenuation. The comparison between CBF-SC and other related methods is also explored. Advanced mechatronic applications are also reviewed, showcasing how CBFs are implemented in autonomous vehicles, robotics, industrial automation systems, and energy platforms. Finally, future research directions are provided in the area to trigger further discussion and investigation.

title: Impact of Quasi-Two-Level Converters on Common-Mode Currents in Inverter-Fed Drive Systems
authors: Anand Krishnamurthy Iyer, Yang Wu, Henk Polinder, Thiago Batista Soeiro
abstract: In motor drives, the quasi-two-level (Q2L) operation of a multilevel converter introduces a controllable inter-cell delay between level transitions, enabling shaping of the generated output-voltage spectrum and common-mode voltage (CMV). This article quantifies the impact of Q2L on common-mode currents and presents a simplified harmonic approximation that maps any two-level (2L) SPWM spectrum to its Q2L counterpart via a closed-form, delay-dependent scaling factor that also captures finite <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathbf{d} \boldsymbol{v}}/{\mathbf{d} \boldsymbol{t}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The model supports rapid benchmarking over key design variables, namely the number of switching cells <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{N}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, inter-cell delay <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{t}_{\boldsymbol{d}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and rise/fall time <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{t}_{\boldsymbol{rf}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and is accurate in practice for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{t}_{\boldsymbol{d}} \boldsymbol{f}_{\boldsymbol{c}} \boldsymbol{\lesssim 0.1}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{f}_{\boldsymbol{c}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> is the PWM carrier frequency. Experimental validation on a flying-capacitor multilevel inverter operated in Q2L mode, assessed using a DC-side conducted emissions measurement test setup based on the CISPR 25 standard, demonstrates tunable attenuation bands in the low-MHz range and measured reductions of up to 12 dB around 1 MHz when increasing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{N}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and appropriately tuning <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{t}_{\boldsymbol{d}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. These results provide simple design rules for targeting frequency bands of concern and indicate that Q2L can materially lower common-mode emissions, and thus required filter size in weight- and volume-constrained applications such as in electric transportation.

title: Channel-Aware Adaptive Two-Stage Authentication in RIS-Assisted Communication Systems
authors: Xinzhuo Yin, Pinchang Zhang, Fu Xiao
abstract: This letter presents a robust, channel-aware adaptive two-stage physical-layer authentication framework for Reconfigurable intelligent surface-assisted communication systems. This framework jointly exploits multi-domain propagation features alongside intrinsic hardware features to ensure high discriminative capability. A novel channel-aware switching mechanism is employed to dynamically select between an efficient Stage-I detector and a robust Stage-II detector based on real-time channel quality, optimally balancing security and overhead. The final decision is obtained through an inter-stage weighted fusion strategy. Closed-form expressions for false alarm and detection probabilities are derived under a rigorous statistical framework. Simulation results demonstrate the superior detection probability and robustness of our scheme compared with existing works.

title: Wearable Technology-Enhanced Assessment of the Effect of Interpersonal Trust on Adolescents’ Physical Activity Behavior: The Mediating Role of Family Capital
authors: Baixia Li, Zhiling Zhang, Yonghong Chen, Bo Zhang, Maoshui Chen, Xiangyi Zeng, Qunfeng Li
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Anisotropic Diffusion Filtering For Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Sneha Kumari, Devendra Kumar Bajpai, P.K Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Timely and correct diagnosis of brain tumours is crucial to proper planning of treatment and better patient outcomes. This article displays a conventional image processing pipeline on fully automated tumour detection and localisation on MRI images without involving deep learning. Anisotropic diffusion filtering, which is done using imdiffusefilt in MATLAB, is used to start the process to remove noise but preserve meaningful features. A Region of Interest (ROI) is chosen manually in order to target potential tumour areas. Segmentation is then performed using adaptive thresholding and morphological operations are used to eliminate noise and small artefacts. Solidity and area are the two components used to identify the most important tumour area. An erosion and filling are used to extract tumour contours by the use of a bounding box. The filtered grayscale MRI is overlaid with the tumour border so that it can be clearly visualised. Lastly, a result table is created with tumour area and bounding box size and detection status. This interpretable, lightweight and transparent method proves especially helpful under resource-constrained environments where deep learning is computationally expensive.

title: Comparative study on the Techniques and Tactics of Fan Zhendong and Wang Chuqin in the Men's Singles Semifinals of the 15th National Games Table Tennis – Based on the Four-Segment Index Evaluation Method for Table Tennis

title: Research on Air-to-Ground Connected Cargo Loading System Technologies for Future Wide-Body Aircraft Based on Systems Engineering
authors: Xiaoliang Tang, Yuxin Pang, Hongyuan Song, Yiqi Yuan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: With the continuous growth in global air cargo demand and the ongoing advancement of air transport and intelligent technologies, future wide-body aircraft cargo loading systems face evolving requirements for higher efficiency, intelligence, and collaboration. Traditional onboard cargo loading systems exhibit significant limitations due to their architectural and functional constraints, struggling to adapt to increasingly complex operating environments and rapidly changing customer and market demands. This paper applies systems engineering methodologies to future wide-body aircraft. Through the identification of stakeholders and their interests, the establishment of a lifecycle-oriented systems engineering framework, and research on system architecture and key subsystems, it constructs the technical architecture for an air-to-ground connected cargo loading system. The research findings demonstrate that systems engineering methods can effectively support the top-level design and optimization of future air-to-ground connected cargo loading systems for wide-body aircraft, enhancing system integration, agility, and scalability. This study provides a theoretical foundation and engineering reference for the design and implementation of next-generation intelligent air cargo platforms.

title: Intelligent Hybrid Feature Optimization and Boosting Ensemble Model for Software Defect Prediction
authors: Umair Mujtaba, Nafeesa Ambreen, Jawad Usman Arshad, Nauman Khan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Software bugs have remained a prominent problem in contemporary software development environments, resulting in financial losses, project delays, and system unreliability. Conventional bug prediction models have proven less effective when dealing with higher-dimensional problem spaces and less relevant attribute information. To counter these effects, this paper proposes a hybrid machine learning model incorporating PCA, Mutual Information, RFE, and an Ensemble Boosting classifier. In this hybrid model, PCA is used to capture the principal directions of variance, which can successfully handle dimensional issues. Mutual Information and RFE are used in combination to discover and select more informative and discriminating features. Ensemble Boosting is used as a classifier to successfully learn non-linear relationships among these informative features. The hybrid model proposed in this paper performed defect accuracy tests on a variety of NASA datasets, with an accuracy of 97.

title: Research on the Flexible Measurement Method of the Center of Gravity of Ultra-Long Carrier Rockets Based on Four-Vehicle Support
authors: Xiaolin Zhang, Yizhao Tan, Meitong Lin, Meibao Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: For super-long launch vehicles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>50$</tex> meters), traditional methods of measuring the center of mass have issues such as significant body deflection and insufficient accuracy. This paper proposes an innovative four-vehicle joint support flexible measurement method for the center of mass. This method reduces the deformation of the vehicle body through multi-point support, combines a laser tracker to achieve system joint leveling and spatial positioning, then uses group unloaded measurement followed by loaded measurement of mass, and finally calculates the center of mass using the principle of static moment balance. The center of mass coordinates are then transformed to the required product coordinate system through multi-coordinate system conversion technology. Experimental verification shows that this method significantly improves measurement accuracy. The relative standard uncertainty of mass measurement is 0.078%, and the relative standard uncertainties of the center of mass in the X, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Y$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Z$</tex> directions are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.015 \%, 0.087 \%$</tex>, and 0.079%, respectively.

title: Advance battery management system for electric vehicle a comprehensive review
authors: A Adarsh, R Sowmya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: All Electric Vehicles (EV) and hybrids rely on the Battery Management System (BMS) to function. The BMS is there to make sure the battery works reliably and safely. The BMS has features that ensure the battery is safe and reliable, such as the ability to monitor and evaluate its status, manage its charge, and balance its cells. A battery’s behavior changes depending on the operating and ambient factors as it is an electrochemical product. The execution of these functions is made more difficult by the inherent unpredictability in battery performance. Issues with existing BMSs are discussed in this article. An essential function of a BMS is to assess the battery’s condition, which includes its charge, health, and life. The current approaches for state assessment of batteries are reviewed, and the future problems for BMSs are outlined along with potential answers. In response to these issues, this study suggests a BMS framework that is both improved and driven by deep learning. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) algorithms, and RNNs allow the system to learn intricate spatial and temporal patterns from data collected from the batteries in real-time. The BMS, which is based on Hybrid CNN-LSTM, makes better predictions, allows for preventive maintenance, while optimizes energy use, which means the battery life is longer and the car is operated safely. The model outperforms conventional approaches with regard to of efficiency, dependability, and flexibility, as shown by simulation results with real-world datasets. The groundbreaking impact of AI on electric transportation systems of the future is shown in this research.

title: A Federated Learning Framework for Proactive Model Poisoning Defense Using a Trusted Client Module
authors: Mohammad Shees Abdullah
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative learning on decentralized data but is critically vulnerable to model poisoning. Current server-side protections are often inefficient and incompatible with privacy technologies. To address this, we propose a Trusted Client Module (TCM), a software-rooted security layer on every client device. Unlike server-side defenses that cannot inspect local data, our TCM performs preliminary self-validation locally to ensure updates are helpful, preventing attacks at their source. We integrate this with a ledger-based token system for incentives, Differential Privacy (DP) to prevent inference, and Selective Homomorphic Encryption (SHE) for confidentiality. Evaluation on a public arrhythmia dataset shows the TCM defense maintains high accuracy under sophisticated poisoning attacks where standard FL collapses. Our unified architecture offers strong security, privacy, and auditability with explicit, manageable trade-off.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)

title: Privacy Preserving Decentralized Learning with Positive-Incentive Noise_supp1-3672569.pdf
authors: Shaofu Yang
abstract: Ensuring the privacy of local datasets has emerged as an important concern in decentralized learning. However, the inherent privacy-utility tradeoff remains a fundamental challenge for privacy preserving decentralized algorithms. To address this issue, we introduce Positive-Incentive Noise Generator (PING), a novel mechanism designed to eliminate negative impact of privacy noise on convergence while defending against powerful colluding inference attacks. PING leverages network topologies and lightweight encryption-decryption operations to generate correlated noise. Building upon PING, we propose PP-DPIN, a privacy preserving stochastic algorithm tailored for decentralized learning. By integrating differential privacy and differential information entropy, we provide a comprehensive privacy quantification for PP-DPIN, with at least half nodes achieving arbitrarily strong privacy guarantees. Furthermore, convergence rate of PP-DPIN is established under stochastic convex and nonconvex settings, which characterizes the impact of privacy noise and demonstrates the linear speedup relative to the network size. Experiments on computer vision tasks validate PP-DPIN's superior performance and robustness against attacks compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: An Event-Triggered Communication-Based Resilient Consensus Control Scheme for DC Microgrids Under False Data Injection Attacks
authors: Keting Wan, Yongpan Chen, Jinghan Zhao, Miao Yu
abstract: Distributed consensus control, with its advantages of higher efficiency and better accuracy, has gradually replaced centralized control and become a common method to regulate voltage and share current in DC microgrids. However, the false data injection attacks targeting communication channels bring great security risks to DC microgrids, and existing resilient control methods are still plagued by their poor applicability and high implementation costs. To this end, this article proposes an event-triggered communication-based consensus control scheme, which consists of a decentralized fault-tolerant controller using input–output feedback linearization and a distributed attack observer based on the dual-layer cyber network with the event-triggered communication strategy. Lyapunov analysis method proves that the proposed scheme can effectively resist the unbounded false data injection attacks and guarantee the resilient control of bus voltage regulation and current sharing for DC microgrids. Finally, a DC microgrid model is built in the hardware-in-the-loop testbed to validate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed scheme.

title: A Slot-loaded Monopole Antenna for UWB Applications
authors: S Vishnuprian, A Sumanth Sriram, S Dhusyanth, Jonish Jack Kenned, K S Revin, Tanmaya Kumar Das
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The article describes a slot-loaded monopole antenna intended for ultra-wideband applications. The suggested design is linearly polarized and comprises of a square-shaped radiating element inscribed with slots made by repeated subtraction of square from hexagon-shapes. The monopole element uses microstrip line feeding with a defected ground structure. The simulated results of the ultra-wideband antenna indicate -10 dB bandwidth of $3.6-9.66 \mathrm{GHz}$. The realized gain of the suggested work remains >1.5 dBi in the operational bandwidth. The stable radiation characteristics with omnidirectional pattern provide the usable feature of the design in UWB applications.

title: DC-DC 48V Input Step-down Converter with Low Choke Inductance and Voltage Stress of Transistors Comprising Naturally Balanced Voltage Divider
authors: Robert Stala, Szymon Folmer
abstract: This paper presents the concept and research results of a low voltage non-isolated switch-mode step-down DC-DC converter in a new topology favorable for high-frequency operation. In the proposed converter, the voltage across the components and the inductance of the choke are reduced. Voltage reduction is achieved by the implementation of an appropriate topology, in which modulation is carried out from the input voltage pre-divided into three capacitors. For such a system, the results characterizing its modulation, voltage and current stress, and efficiency are presented. The proposed converter provides continuous voltage regulation, and a low-complexity PWM generator is required for its proper operation. The paper also presents a method of natural voltage balancing in the proposed converter with three-capacitor input divider. This method does not require voltage measurements or any special control strategy and may be more reliable in high-frequency systems than active methods based on modulation correction. The article presents an experimental validation of the feasibility, proper operation and parameters of the proposed converter. The reported results include operation at 48-V-to-12-V and 48-V-to-5-V conversion ratios, demonstrated on a GaN-based converter operating at a 1.2-MHz switching frequency. Such a converter can be used in growing technologies such as 48 V mild hybrid vehicle systems and data centers.

title: Maximizing Charging Utility for Height-adjustable Directional Pendant Wireless Chargers
authors: Sixu Wu, Yun Yang, Haipeng Dai, Linfeng Liu, Lijie Xu, Jia Xu
abstract: None

title: A Novel Hybrid Quantum-Classical Path Optimization for Methane Detection Using Remote Quantum Intensity Prediction Models
authors: Anjana Rajendra Prasad, Nathaniel Ketema, Eric Yocam, Varghese Vaidyan, Gurcan Comert, David Werth, Robert Buckley, Milinda Rambel Stone, Baby Vennela Kothakonda
abstract: Mobile methane leak monitoring and detection systems may be constrained by the time required to reach the leak location and the energy needed throughout the process. In this paper, a physics-aware hybrid quantum–classical architecture combined with classical methods with a quantum design is developed. The proposed approach leverages environmental factors to calculate leak locations and areas of higher concentration. The output is provided to a self-guided drone, which detects possible leak sources within its battery and within environmental constraints. In simulated tests, the proposed approach found leaks faster and used less energy per confirmed leak than traditional/classical methods, while meeting the same flight-time and power constraints.

title: Towards Programmable Infrastructure for Organic & Flexible 6G Networks
authors: Fabian Eichhorn, Roland Bless, Paul Seehofer, Michael Gundall, Daniel Lindenschmitt, Bastian Bloessl, David Volz, Navid Keshtiarast, Hendrik Borchert, Markus Petri, Elena-Ramona Modroiu, Joel John, Hossein Mohammadalizadeh, Theo Radig, Leonard Paeleke, Heiner Ackermann, Daniel Hauer, Marius-Iulian Corici, Marina Petrova, Thomas Magedanz, Hans D. Schotten
abstract: The next generation of wireless mobile networks will be driven by software and infrastructure virtualization. At the same time, the underlying hardware will become more diverse. Edge, intermediate and central network nodes will allow distributed deployments of never seen before scale. Non-terrestrial networks, nomadic networks and networks in networks promise more dynamicity, changing their topologies over time in predictable and unpredictable ways. To handle the complexity and dynamicity, a highly flexible and adaptable network is required. The key enablers of this complex digital communication system will be programmable infrastructures. From programmable access and flexible backhauls to zero-touch, adaptable and resilient control planes, we conducted research into advanced programmability at different layers of the network, within the Open6GHub project. In this article, we present innovative solutions for advanced programmable network infrastructure and show why they are essential for our vision of the programmable infrastructure of future wireless mobile networks.

title: TPRA-CNN-Transformer: A Hybrid Architecture for Music Genre Classification
authors: Weijian Li, Jianpeng Xu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: This paper addresses the fundamental challenge of balancing local feature extraction with global dependency modeling in music genre classification. We propose an innovative solution featuring two key contributions: a TriPath Rectangular Attention (TPRA) mechanism that enhances the capture of discriminative features, and a hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture that synergistically integrates local perception with global modeling capabilities. Experimental results on the GTZAN dataset demonstrate that our approach achieves a classification accuracy of 96.86%, significantly outperforming existing mainstream methods. Ablation studies validate the contribution of each component, with the collaborative effect between the TPRA module and Transformer encoder yielding substantial performance improvements. This research provides new technical insights for music information retrieval tasks, offering valuable references particularly in feature representation learning and model architecture design.

title: An LSTM – Attention-Enhanced DDPG Approach to UAV Autonomous Obstacle Avoidance and Target Tracking Under Partial Target Observation
authors: Jiatong Zhang, Zhou Fang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Autonomous obstacle avoidance and target tracking are key capabilities for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle(UAV) operating in unknown environments. Although deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has been widely applied to this problem, most studies assume UAV can fully obtain target information, which conflicts with practical scenarios where communication delays and signal interference lead to target observation loss. To address this gap, we first model the UAV obstacle avoidance and target tracking task and introduce a stochastic target-observation loss mechanism. We then propose LADDPG (LSTM-attention-enhanced Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient), which processes historical observation sequences with a LSTM network and temporal attention to obtain a robust state representation, while employing decayed Ornstein - Uhlenbeck (OU) noise to dynamically balance exploration and exploitation. The experimental results show that LADDPG significantly outperforms original DDPG and TD3 in many different scenarios, achieving higher success rates and lower collision and timeout rates.

title:
Development of ECSO Current Table for Photovoltaic System and Simple Calculation Method of Energy Saving and CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Reduction
authors: Daigo Yonetsu, Noriharu Ujiie, Katsushi Ohsawa, Kazuhiko Masuo
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: This paper proposes a current table for realizing Environmental and Economical Conductor Size Optimization (ECSO) design for photovoltaic systems. A simple method for calculating the energy saving and CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> reduction based on the power conditioning system capacity is also proposed. The method is demonstrated using an ECSO design. The proposed table and method are applied to three case studies.

title: 3D Digital Modeling of Power Transformers Based on Morphology-Aware Registration Network
authors: Qiubing Shen, Yige Zhao, Chen Yang, Yuhao Zhang, Hui Chen
abstract: In the context of new power systems, there is a growing demand for digital and intelligent modeling of power equipment. Recent advances in 3D point cloud registration (PCR) have significantly facilitated the development of digital twin models for power equipment. Nevertheless, automated 3D modeling of power transformers (PT) still faces substantial challenges, primarily due to the lack of high-quality 3D datasets, the absence of dedicated preprocessing methods, and the limited adaptability of existing feature extractors to complex PT morphologies. To address these challenges, this paper proposes Morpheus, a morphology-aware deep registration network for 3D PT digital modeling. We first construct TR3D, a dataset comprising 420 transformer models across approximately 50 categories. Statistical analysis shows that TR3D contains significantly more edges than general 3D datasets. Building on this observation, we design a salient point sampling (SPS) to preserve fine-grained geometric details, and propose a multi-scale feature extractor (MSFE) to enhance feature representation. Finally, a graph matching method is employed to solve the Euclidean transform. Experimental results on PT data demonstrate that compared with the baseline method, Morpheus's comprehensive registration accuracy is improved by 61.13%, and it has the potential to be used for high-precision digital twin construction and automated modeling in the power sector. Project page: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/pupupuOUO/TR3D</uri>.

title: CLLC Converter Balanced Design to Reduce Common Mode Conducted EMI
authors: Colin P. Ball, Keon M. Briscoe, Alexander L. Julian, Giovanna Oriti
abstract: The CLLC resonant converter using wide bandgap devices has been gaining popularity as a solid state transformer (SST) and is widely used in microgrid and transportation electrification applications. However, its high switching frequency causes electromagnetic interference (EMI), requiring large EMI filters and reducing its overall power density. This paper presents a novel CLLC topology featuring a balanced resonant tank circuit to reduce the common mode (CM) voltage, thus mitigating the overall converter conducted EMI. Physics-based CM modeling and analysis in the frequency and time domains provide important insights into the converter CM behavior with respect to the benchmark, unbalanced CLLC. The differential mode performance of the novel converter is comparable to that of the traditional topology. The experimental measurements on a SiC laboratory converter setup demonstrate that the new CLLC SST achieves 10-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$20\ dB$</tex-math></inline-formula> of CM EMI attenuation with respect to the state-of-the-art CLLC SST, where the resonant tank circuit is unbalanced.

title: Efficient Intrusion Detection for Intra-Vehicle CAN Network with Light-weight Deep Learning based Model
authors: Ankit Kumar Singh, Mazhar Ali, Moharana Suchismita, Subeen Choi, Bong Jun Choi
abstract: Modern vehicles have communication protocols that are vulnerable to cyberattacks. A lack of authentication and encryption makes Controller Area Network (CAN) bus messages susceptible to injection attacks, spoofing, and time-based security threats. Existing intrusion detection systems (IDS) based on deep learning models cannot extract fine-grained features from CAN message frames. These models also have a limited ability to detect stealthy attacks and require significant computational resources. Additionally, large transformer-based models struggle to balance detection accuracy with the resource constraints of in-vehicle electronic control units (ECUs), hindering their practical deployment. Therefore, we propose a lightweight, deep-learning-based approach that can effectively detect anomalies with high accuracy. Our approach combines feature engineering, payload splitting, and label matching with a lightweight CNN-LSTM model that accurately flags intrusions while maintaining real-time efficiency. Feature engineering and payload splitting maximize knowledge extraction from CAN messages. Label matching analyzes flags for each set of attributes using a supervised CNN-LSTM-based detector to capture anomalies missed by time-window-based labeling approaches. The proposed approach is evaluated on two distinct datasets: HCRL car-hacking and ORNL ROAD, in both multiclass and binary attack-detection settings. We compare our proposed architecture against multiple baseline approaches, including rule-based checks and deep learning-based models. Experimental results demonstrate that the CNN–LSTM model achieves near-perfect detection performance, attaining up to 100% accuracy, F1-score, and ROC-AUC on the Car-Hacking dataset, while consistently outperforming CNN, LSTM, ANN, hybrid, and rule-based baselines. On the ROAD dataset, the proposed model achieves F1-scores of 0.9976 for masquerade attacks and 0.9578 for fabrication attacks, with robust performance across combined attack scenarios. Notably, the CNN–LSTM architecture requires significantly fewer parameters (as low as 8.4%) compared to transformer-based models, while delivering comparable or superior detection accuracy. These results highlight the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed IDS for real-time, resource-constrained in-vehicle deployment.

title: Multi-Camera Surround View Monitoring System for Oversized Semi-Trailer Trucks: Camera Placement Optimization and Dynamic Image Concatenation
authors: Feng Wang, Zhenjie Zheng, Kai Wang, Wei Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The growth of Modular Integrated Construction (MiC) has intensified the transportation of oversized goods in urban areas, posing challenges for semi-trailer trucks due to blind spots and heavy traffic. Multi-camera Surround View Monitoring (SVM) systems have the potential to provide 360° visibility to eliminate blind spots and enhance transportation safety. However, existing SVM systems are primarily designed for passenger vehicles and face limitations when applied to semi-trailer trucks due to camera placement constraints and dynamic tractor-trailer rotations. This paper develops a systematic methodology for designing and implementing a novel SVM system tailored for oversized semi-trailer trucks. It features three innovative components: a Camera Placement Optimization (CPO) method to determine the optimal camera placement, an Articulation Angle Estimation (AAE) method to determine articulation angle utilizing spatial and temporal information, and a Dynamic Image Concatenation (DIC) method to generate Bird’s Eye View (BEV) images. These methods were thoroughly evaluated in a simulation environment, with results showing that: 1) the CPO method is applicable to various semi-trailer trucks (e.g., drop-deck, flatbed, dry van) and can improve BEV image sharpness by 10% compared to conventional installation plans; 2) the AAE method enhances articulation angle estimation with over 50% greater accuracy and reduces fluctuations compared to existing spatial feature-matching approaches; and 3) the DIC method can generate panoramic BEV images that effectively depict the truck’s surroundings and enable distance estimation to potential risks in challenging transportation scenarios.

title: Prior-ESM: Enhancing Multi-camera 3D Object Detection with Embedded Semantic Priors and Instance-level Data Augmentation
authors: Hangwen Zhang, Yonghui Huang, Zhi Liu, Chuan Hu, Guofa Li, Xi Zhang
abstract: End-to-end 3D object detection methods based on multi-camera setups have recently gained attention for their simple yet effective architectures. However, these approaches suffer from two major limitations. First, they often struggle to capture spatial relationships efficiently. Second, their performance depends heavily on large amounts of well annotated and distributed data. To address these challenges, we propose Prior-ESM, which integrates Embedded Semantic Maps (ESM) as priors into the detection queries and employs deformable attention to guide these queries toward features surrounding reference points. By embedding implicit, spatially aware priors into the decoder queries, our method enhances the model’s ability to infer geometric relationships. Moreover, an instance-level data augmentation technique based on the Segment Anything Model2 (SAM2) is introduced. Unlike conventional 2D image augmentations ormultimodal 3D procedures, our method uses single-modality images from multi-view inputs to enhance datasets within the 3D spatial domain. By performing data augmentation offline, we mitigate class imbalance without adding computational overhead during training. Prior-ESM demonstrates the state-of-the-art performance on the nuScenes dataset, achieving 48.9% mAP over non-temporal methods and 56.2% mAP over temporal methods. Furthermore, it attains a superior performance of 23.7% mAP on the challenging Argoverse 2 sensor dataset.

title: Diffusion-Augmented Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolution for Clinical Gait and Motor Function Assessment
authors: Eran Beeri Bamani, Joao Buzzatto, Hermano Igo Krebs
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Accurate assessment of gross motor function in children with cerebral palsy (CP) is essential for clinical decision-making, yet current practice is limited by data scarcity, severe class imbalance, and patient heterogeneity. Recent skeleton-based deep learning approaches, such as spatio-temporal graph convolutional networks (STGCN), enable automatic GMFCS prediction from monocular video but are limited in generalizability and fairness. In this work, we propose a unified generative–diagnostic pipeline that integrates a Conditional Skeleton Diffusion Model (CSDM) with a Biomechanically-Aware Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Network (BA-STGCN). The CSDM generates clinically plausible 2D skeleton gait sequences conditioned on Gross Motor Function Classification System (GMFCS) level, Gait Deviation Index (GDI), and anthropometric covariates, guided by an anatomically structured covariance model to preserve biomechanical fidelity and clinical distributions. These synthetic sequences, combined with real patient data, are used to train the BA-STGCN, which incorporates a symmetry-based loss and a multi-task head for joint GMFCS classification and continuous GDI regression. Extensive evaluation on a pediatric clinical gait dataset demonstrates that our approach achieves 85.7% GMFCS classification accuracy with balanced precision and recall, reduces mean absolute error in GDI prediction to 4.6, and markedly improves recognition of severe phenotypes. These findings highlight that conditional skeleton diffusion, coupled with biomechanically informed graph learning, provides a scalable, interpretable, and privacy-preserving pathway for automated clinical gait assessment in CP.

title: Semantic Gaussians: Open-Vocabulary Scene Understanding with 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Jun Guo, Xiaojian Ma, Yue Fan, Huaping Liu, Qing Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding presents a significant challenge in computer vision, with wide-ranging applications in embodied agents and augmented reality systems. Existing methods adopt neural rendering methods as 3D representations and jointly optimize color and semantic features to achieve rendering and scene understanding simultaneously. In this paper, we introduce Semantic Gaussians, a novel open-vocabulary scene understanding approach based on 3D Gaussian Splatting. Our key idea is to distill knowledge from 2D pretrained models to 3D Gaussians. Unlike existing methods, we design a versatile projection approach that maps various 2D semantic features from pre-trained image encoders into a novel semantic component of 3D Gaussians, which is based on spatial relationships and needs no additional training. We further build a 3D semantic network that directly predicts the semantic component from raw 3D Gaussians for fast inference. The quantitative results on ScanNet segmentation and LERF object localization demonstrate the superior performance of our method. Additionally, we explore several applications of Semantic Gaussians, including object part segmentation, instance segmentation, scene editing, and spatiotemporal segmentation, with better qualitative results over 2D and 3D baselines, highlighting its versatility and effectiveness in supporting diverse downstream tasks.
