title: Hybrid Quantum – A Framework for High Frequency Stock Trading Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Anuj Mishra, Aviral Garg, Aditya Ghosh, Abhishek Kumar Singh, Tripti Pandey
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This Paper shows information about the High Speed Stock Trading, In todays era the normal computers are struggling to identify and find the complex market patterns and strategies. So in order to solve this issue we built a new system that is a combination of quantum computing and classical computing in which Reinforcement Learning (a way for computers to learn with trial and error) is used with Quantum Machine Learning to make smarter trading decisions and strategies. Specially, we have used a quantum model (called a Variational Quantum classifier) as a part of Deep Q Network agent to help the system learning better trading strategies. Our goal is to use the superior representational power of quantum feature spaces for financial decision making. The hybrid agent was trained in a high-fidelity simulation environment that includes realistic market frictions and a risk-adjusted reward function. Backtesting results on unseen data indicate that the hybrid agent consistently performs better than its traditional counterpart. It achieves a total return of $21.2 \%$, which is higher than the $\mathbf{1 8 . 5 \%}$ return from the classical model. Additionally, risk-adjusted performance has clearly improved. The hybrid agent’s Sortino Ratio is $32.6 \%$ higher and its Sharpe Ratio is $26.4 \%$ higher. Its maximum drawdown is also lower. These findings demonstrate that quantum models are able to detect minute market relationships that conventional approaches might miss. As a result, trading tactics become more reliable and successful. This research marks a substantial breakthrough in the application of quantum technologies to challenging computational finance problems.

title: Smart Farming Model for Poultry in Indonesia
authors: Kenneth Hizkia Suwarto, Jeff Nathan Setiawan, Gede Taruna Eka Wijaya, Rudy Tjahyadi
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Indonesia, as an agricultural country, faces challenges such as unpredictable climate, limited technological adoption, and animal welfare concerns that hinder efficiency. As poultry farming evolves rapidly and the global demand rises, Indonesia's improvement in poultry farms is considered slow. This study analyzes how Smart Farms that utilize Internet of Things (IoT) technologies can enhance productivity, animal welfare, and sustainability using a Design Science Research Methodology (DSRM). Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA) was used to identify critical factors for a successful harvest, which include temperature, humidity, air quality, feeding, and body weight monitoring. Through semi-structured interviews with the farm owners, an Information Systems (IS) model was developed using the People, Process, and Technology framework. The result presents a proposed model or artifact to facilitate the adoption of smart poultry farming in Indonesia

title: Multi-Modal Forensic Retrieval Framework Combining Semantic Clip Embeddings with Biometric Attribute Analysis for Suspect Identification
authors: Adrija Ghosh, Roushan Paul, Sainik Kumar Mahata
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Today's criminal identification systems encounter major obstacles while matching textual descriptions to visual databases since they use manual processes that show poor scalability and human-dependent decision making. The research introduces an automatic multi-modal framework which combines CLIP for text-image semantic linking with FaceNet for biometric feature extraction and EfficientNet for visual attribute detection. The pipeline system receives natural language request input, then extracts meaningful weighted attributes (semantics at 70% and attributes at 20% with face information at 10%) before finding top-k matches through a combination scoring method. The system achieves an 94% hit rate appearing in the top 10 results against the database of suspects. The paper presents technical specifications about face detection using MTCNN alongside confidence threshold values (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 0.9$</tex>) and attribute matching through Jaccard approach which are examined together with operational ethics related to real-world application.

title: Game-Theoretic Analysis of Information Sharing Evolution and Stability in Digital Supply Chains
authors: Ying Zhao
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: To investigate information sharing behavior between manufacturing enterprises and logistics service providers in digital supply chains, this paper constructs an evolutionary game model and analyzes its dynamic evolution paths and stability. Findings reveal that the long-term stable strategy of the system involves both parties choosing either to share or not to share information. When the benefits of sharing outweigh costs and risks, the system's evolutionary direction is determined by saddle points, forming two strategy attraction domains. Sensitivity analysis indicates that moderate enhancing information sharing levels, strengthening information absorption and utilization capabilities, increasing cooperative benefits, and intensifying incentives effectively promote information sharing, while high costs and risks remain primary barriers. Numerical simulations further validate the influence of these factors. This study reveals the evolutionary mechanism of information sharing, providing theoretical and practical guidance for corporate collaborative decision-making.

title: Reduction of Common Mode Voltage Transitions in Two-Level Voltage Source Inverters Using Predictive Control and H10 Inverter Topology
authors: Amit Kumar Singh, Arpan Hota, Sonti Venu, Sachin Jain
abstract: This paper presents a Predictive Control-based H10 inverter (PCH10) strategy designed to reduce common-mode voltage (CMV) transitions. The proposed solution mitigates the CMV transition particularly those caused by switching to zero-voltage vector (ZVV). The proposed solution also considers the effect of the DJC while retaining the CMV magnitude to the previous active state. Thus, the proposed predictive control method strategically avoids transitions during the zero state and the system experiences CMV transitions only during changes between active states. Reduction in the magnitude and number of CMV transitions further helps in the reduction of the common mode current (CMC). Reduced CMC directly reduces EMI, safeguards motor drive performance, and enhances overall system reliability. Details of the PCH10 strategy with the required analysis are presented in the manuscript. The effectiveness of the given approach is validated through detailed required simulations and experimental results.

title: Adaptive Niching-Based Gradient-Accelerated Differential Evolution for High-Dimensional Nonconvex Optimization_supp1-3671634.pdf
authors: Xi-Jun Liang
abstract: Nonconvex optimization presents significant challenges in many fields, particularly in training deep neural networks (DNNs), where poor local minima can degrade generalization—especially with limited data. High-dimensional nonconvex optimization presents two central challenges: 1) effectively balancing global exploration with rapid local exploitation and 2) establishing convergence guarantees, particularly with sparse individuals under nonsmooth regularizations. To address these limitations, we propose adaptive niching-based gradient-accelerated DE (AdaptiveGDE), an AdaptiveGDE differential evolution (DE) algorithm. It introduces a novel two-step mutation operator that decouples differential mutation and gradient descent, allowing independent control of exploration and exploitation. An adaptive niching strategy dynamically adjusts the number of subpopulations based on population similarity and iteration progress, enabling diverse early exploration and refined late-stage convergence. Under relaxed smoothness assumptions and approximate <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell _{1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> regularization, we provide convergence guarantees in expectation to a near-optimal solution within <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(1/\epsilon ^{4})$</tex-math> </inline-formula> iterations. Extensive experiments show that AdaptiveGDE achieves robust global exploration on complex multimodal functions, strong local exploitation on convex problems, and significantly improves test accuracy and loss in DNN training, especially under limited data scenarios.

title: Increasing the Quality of User-Centered Work Quantitative Ratings for Evaluating User Satisfaction with the Gamified Learning App
authors: Uppuluri Raju, R Surendran, Sumathy Krishnan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The proposed work will contribute to a better quality user-centered assessment of gamified learning apps because it provides a powerful quantitative rating scale to understand the level of user satisfaction. It is centered on a process of establishing important metrics regarding usability, engagement, and learning efficiency through the iterative feedback mechanism. This study provides a comparative structure to determine the effectiveness of this system over a web application and an iOS application. A sample of N=11 was used to repeat tests on both platforms by iterative tests to determine the statistical power of the test at 80 per cent, using G*Power. Rating Accuracy: the criterion of the degree of user feedback and the following platform improvements showed an extremely significant difference between the two platforms <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(t=-8.803, \mathrm{p}<0.000)$</tex>. The web application also had a much larger mean rating accuracy (92.09) than the iOS (83.64). This leads to the idea that both platforms can be utilized with the help of the quantitative rating system; however, the architecture of the web platform can allow more effective feedback implementation. The study provides a systematic, quantifiable framework for continuously improving the user-centric design of gamified learning.

title: Design and Investigation of Intelligent Reflecting & Refracting Surface (IRRS) for Future 6G Communication
authors: Wrishav Das, Sujan Siddiquee, Prakash Singh, Sutapa Ray, Souradip Ghosh, Pritom Adhikary
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In this study, we propose the design and performance analysis of an electronically reconfigurable Intelligent Reflecting and Refracting Surface (IRRS) for next-generation 6G wireless communication systems. Our objective is to achieve simultaneous control of reflected and transmitted electromagnetic waves within a single, compact structure operating in the FR-3 frequency band (7-24 GHz). We design a self-complementary periodic unit cell based on Babinet's principle to achieve polarization-dependent reflection and transmission using a single-layer structure. We integrate PIN diodes into the unit cell to enable electronic switching and realize 1-bit phase control (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0^{\circ} / 180^{\circ}$</tex>) for both reflected and transmitted waves. By changing the diode states, the proposed IRRS supports dualpolarized operation at some frequencies and single-polarized reflect-refract operation at others. Full-wave simulations validate the polarization-selective characteristics and phase reconfiguration across the FR-3 band. The compact, passive, and single-layer design makes the proposed IRRS suitable for adaptive beam control and interference mitigation in future 6G wireless environments.

title: Contactless in-line fan quality inspection by a mmWave FMCW radar: insights on the use of displacement measurements
authors: Gianluca Ciattaglia, Savino Pontino, Emily Carbonari, Gianluca Catalini, Susanna Spinsante
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In-line quality inspection processes are of crucial importance in verifying and maintaining a production site performance, both in terms of production conformity and timely delivery. During the fabrication process, defects and imperfections can occur, and the affected manufactured parts must be identified and discarded, if necessary. The method used to perform this operation must be both reliable and fast, so as not to add excessive delays in the production process. To improve the inspection speed, contactless methods are preferred by companies, as they do not involve the application and removal of probes on the device under test. In such application context, this paper explores the performance of a measurement method based on the use of a mmWave radar sensor for contactless quality inspection. The device under test is an industrial fan, and displacement measurements are used to derive its rotation speed and main vibration harmonics. The results are validated by comparison with reference rotation speed and vibration measurements. Experiments show that using the radar-based method, the measured displacement is representative of the vibrations occurring in the central part of the fan, and provides a relative percent error in the evaluation of the rotation speed of 3%, compared to the 1.6% obtained using piezoelectric accelerometers. The influence of the radar’s transmitting antenna radiation pattern on the displacement measurement is also experimentally demonstrated.

title: Comparative Analysis Review of Deep Learning Techniques Using Data Augmentation for Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Priyanka Sharma, Sakshi Ahuja
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Brain tumor classification is a critical challenge in neurological examination due to the complexity of tumor types. The classification of tumors using Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) plays a vital role in planning patient treatment. MRI, especially T1-Contrast Enhanced (T1-CE) scans, provides detailed views of brain tissues, making it highly valuable for detecting abnormalities. The review shows the use of deep learning models to classify three types of brain tumors such as glioma, meningioma, and pituitary using MRI data. It presents recent literature and methodologies integrating transfer learning, ensemble learning, hybrid architectures, and data augmentation, particularly with data augmentation to increase classification accuracy. Deep learning techniques delivers strong performance, making it a practical option for real-world medical imaging tasks. Image Fusion and CNN Classifier achieve the highest accuracy as compared to the other deep learning techniques proving hybrid models can provide better result.

title: Hybrid Neuro-Symbolic Planning Frameworks: A Systematic Review (2023-2025)
authors: Ebai Geslen Muwa, Vaneet Kumar, Ruchika Gupta
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The development of large language models has motivated researchers to combine symbolic planners with LLMs to make them reliable, rational, and reasonably efficient in planning. LLMs can comprehend and produce natural language, yet their poor logical reasoning prevents them from being trusted in independent planning. On the other hand, symbolic planners ensure the logical validity of actions but are not able to read unstructured or ambiguous human specifications. Hybrid neuro-symbolic models like Teriyaki, Formal-LLM and Metagent-P fill these gaps with the language capability of LLMs and logical reasoning of symbolic planners, to generate more predictable and context-specific plans. after a detailed analysis of the studies reviewed, we noticed that standalone LLM planners have higher execution errors with less accuracy compared to symbolic frameworks that are more reliable, precise and safe in all planning activities. Nevertheless, the reviewed evidence also indicates that existing hybrid models have efficiency, scalability and deployment cost challenges, which this review compares with existing frameworks and points out how far hybrid neuro-symbolic planning architectures have gone. It also highlights gaps that future researchers must address in order to develop credible and extendable AI planning systems

title: High-Accuracy Water Quality Detection Using IoT-Integrated Ensemble Machine Learning
authors: Ritam Mitra, Tanmay Samanta, Soumik Kumar Kundu, Samit Karmakar, Malay Gangopadhyay
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Availability of clean and fresh drinking water sources is an increasingly insurmountable challenge with the accelerated urbanization, industrial wastes and pollution of natural sources. Traditional Lab analysis techniques are highly credible yet less cost and time efficient and also not practical in distant and repeated testing. To address these shortcomings, this paper explains and illustrates an effective and compact solution to testing the water quality, which is the integration of the IoT sensors with an ML prediction algorithm. It uses a specially developed probe of pH, turbidity and TDS parameters of data that continually monitors the data on site and wirelessly encrypts the data and subsequently sends it to a cloud server. It operates an ML algorithm that is learned on a big pre-screened database and it either classifies water as drink or undrinkable depending on sensor values. Ten models were tested experimentally under supervision, and it was established that the ensemble techniques, to be more precise the Random Forest Classification algorithm, were highly accurate and recovered 99.95% of the recall rate when it came to detecting unsafe water. The dashboard that can be immediately displayed on any device. Based on the outcome It is possible to observe that the IoT-ML architecture is a low-cost efficient system that can be used to analyze the water quality and, therefore, can be applied in the home environment, agriculture, and rural settings.

title: A Pilot Study on the Efficacy of Zingiber Officinale and Houttuynia Cordata in Balancing Yang Deficiency and Damp-Heat Constitutions Among Medical University Students
authors: Nguyen Huu Duc Minh, Tran Nguyen Minh Nhat, Tran Tuan Hai, Nguyen Huu Khanh, Nguyen Thi Nhu Quynh, Duong Thi Ngoc Lan, Vuong Chi Thanh, Nguyen Ngoc Nhu Thao, Che Quang Cong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Introduction: Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) emphasizes the principle of “treating heat with cold and cold with heat” aligned with the philosophy of “medicine and food sharing the same origin”. Individuals with Damp-Heat constitution often require cooling herbs, such as fish mint (Houttuynia cordata), whereas those with Yang Deficiency benefit from warming ones like ginger (Zingiber officinale). Despite widespread usage, scientific validation of their efficacy remains limited. Objective: To evaluate the efficacy of ginger and fish mint in relieving symptoms specific to Damp-Heat and Yang Deficiency constitutions among medical university students. Methods: A non-controlled, non-blinded pilot study was conducted from February to April 2025 involving 63 students from Ho Chi Minh City University of Medicine and Pharmacy. Participants with Damp-Heat or Yang Deficiency constitutions were identified through a constitution web questionnaire according to TCM. Participants consumed either <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 ~ g}$</tex> /day of fresh fish mint for Damp-Heat or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 ~ g}$</tex> /day of fresh ginger for Yang Deficiency over 14 days. Symptom severity was evaluated using a Numerical Rating Scale at baseline, day 7, and day 14, and adverse effects were monitored. Results: Significant symptom reductions were observed in both groups. Participants with Yang Deficiency reported notable improvements in aversion to cold, cold extremities, and sensitivity to cold. Similarly, participants with Damp-Heat constitution exhibited significant alleviation of mouth stickiness, bitter taste, fatigue, and related symptoms. These improvements were statistically significant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) on both day 7 and day 14 of intervention, along good safety profiles with no serious adverse events. Conclusion: Ginger and fish mint effectively alleviated constitution-specific symptoms among medical university students, which can support their potential role in dietary interventions aligned with TCM principles.

title: Digital Twin Empowers Intangible Cultural Heritage Martial Arts Education——A Study on Digital Preservation and Pedagogical Practice Based on the Oral History of Yangxi Hongquan
authors: Yang Yang, Chuwen Wang, Huaidong Luo, Quanze Xiao, Manqi Zheng, Zi Huang
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Early Prediction of Motor Intervention Efficacy for Preschool Sensory Integration Dysfunction: A Baseline-Driven Machine Learning Model
authors: Ziqiao Xu, Zhiyu Wang, Zhaojie Li
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Development and Design of the Multimodal Digital Standardized Patient
authors: Hongbin Li, Yang Feng, Wuning Tong
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: In traditional medical education, reliance on standardized patients for clinical history-taking and skill training presents limitations such as high costs, poor repeatability, and low standardization. To address these challenges, this paper designs and develops a digital standardized patient system based on multimodal interaction. The system integrates technologies such as natural language processing, speech recognition and synthesis, and knowledge graphs to construct a highly realistic virtual patient. It encompasses the following core functions: 1) conducting natural and coherent doctor-patient dialogues through speech and text, simulating real consultation processes; 2) dynamically responding to medical students' questions and physical examinations based on predefined case scripts, while presenting the dynamic progression of the condition; 3) evaluating students' performance in history-taking and providing personalized learning pathways for consultation practice. Practice has shown that this system can offer medical students a repeatable, standardized, and risk-free clinical practice environment, effectively honing their history-taking skills, clinical reasoning, and humanistic care abilities. It holds broad application prospects in the fields of medical education and clinical skill assessment.

title: Aircraft Sensor Fault Diagnosis Based on HAD-HNN
authors: Ruoheng Chen, Yaoqi Wang, Jun Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Sensor Fault Detection, Identification, and Accomm odation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{SFDI}+\mathrm{A})$</tex> is a critical challenge in the design of complex dynamic systems. This paper proposes a novel approach based on a Hierarchical Dynamically Adaptive Heterogeneous Neural Net work (HDA-HNN) for flight control systems lacking physical redu ndancy. The robustness and performance of the proposed scheme are evaluated through simulation tests involving various types of soft faults (e.g., abrupt bias, ramp deviation) under different mod el-mismatch conditions, including system modeling errors and noi se parameter discrepancies. The results demonstrate that the HD A-HNN approach, enabled by dynamic online learning, effectively adapts to model uncertainties and non-ideal noise environments, significantly reducing detection delay and false alarm rates. This study offers a practical reference for fault-tolerant sensor design i n flight control systems and highlights the distinctive advantages of HDA-HNN in handling complex, nonlinear, and dynamic scena rios.

title: An Adaptive and Intelligent Trust Driven Off-loading Framework to Counter Computational Forgery for Reliable Edge Computing
authors: J. Umamageswaran, Md. Khadar Fhareed, Paul P Allen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Mobile Edge Computation (MEC) is a way to offload computation-heavy tasks from the mobiles to the nearby edge servers, thus reducing latency by as much as 50 percent while saving around 40 percent of the mobile device’s energy. Yet, from real-world deployments, it has been reported that some edge nodes behave maliciously; they misreport resource availability to lure tasks (computational forgery) or simply go offline intermittently when they sense detection, causing up to 65 percent in task failure rate. These activities not only compromise QoS and overburden honest nodes but also create further reliability and security gaps in the already existing MEC infrastructures. Addressing the aforesaid issues, we develop an adaptive intelligent computing offloading framework, with trust-awareness for the edge servers, where servers are dynamically evaluated for their trustworthiness through behavioural profiling and lightweight machine learning techniques. Our model considers direct trust metrics like task success rate, response time stability, and resource usage consistency, alongside indirect collaborative trust scoring. It also applies anomaly detection in real-time by way of temporal clustering and employs reinforcement learning to downgrade nodes exhibiting deceptive behaviour or instability. This framework is modular and scalable, able to work across dynamic and heterogeneous environments such as smart cities, vehicular networks, and healthcare IoT. By focusing attention on high-trust considerations and constantly learning from the behavioural feedback, it ensures a secure, energy-aware, and reliable task offloading procedure. Resilient and trustworthy MEC ecosystems established by way of this approach place a stake into computational forgery and on-off attack patterns with the depletion of system performance and integrity.

title: Exploration and Design of a BYOD-Based CBT Examination System: A Case Study for University Admission
authors: Wanwisa Netsawang, Vajirasak Vanijja, Montri Supattatham
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study aims to implement Bring Your Own Device Computer-Based Testing (BYOD CBT) for the Thai University Central Admission System, such as Thailand's TCAS, to address challenges in scalability, infrastructure, and security. The research employs the Design Science Research (DSR) methodology to develop and evaluate a Unified Reference Model for a secure and scalable BYOD CBT system. The proposed model uniquely integrates a Persistent Queue (PQ) mechanism to ensure seamless data continuity and resilience against network instability. Key security features are incorporated, including policy enforcement, OS-layer isolation via USB-bootable systems, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), and privacy protection conforming to international standards. Furthermore, this study conducted a rigorous quantitative validation to confirm the system's scalability and performance under highconcurrency conditions typical of national-scale assessments. Expert evaluation confirmed the conceptual validity, while the quantitative results demonstrated the system's potential to achieve security and efficiency equivalent to traditional CBT, providing a robust framework for large-scale digital examination deployment.

title: Medical Image Classification Using Spiking Neural Networks and an Unbalanced R-STDP Learning Rule
authors: Ebtasam Ahmad Siddiqui, Uday Pratap Singh, Bersha Kumari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Spike neural networks (SNNs) have excellent visual recognition, biological plausibility, and power efficiency. These individuals excel as medical image classifiers, especially when training data is scarce. The extremely class unbalanced issue hinders medical picture categorization. Deep learning networks frequently favor the majority and misidentify the minority due to this problem. Although there are various methods to solve this challenge, few algorithm-level techniques work for SNNs. We propose R-STDP, an unbalanced reward-modulation spike-timing-dependent plasticity learning technique for support vector machines (SNNs), to address medical picture class imbalance. We may increase the minority class’s reward in the R-STDP learning algorithm by adding an unbalanced reward coefficient. This reward coefficient may help you design class-dependent rewards using training dataset data statistics. Our technique solves the medical image class imbalance problem better than data- and algorithm-level solutions. Experimental results on three benchmark datasets with uneven splits showed this. This is seen by our technique’s performance over baseline SNNs. Our technique also classifies the unbalanced ISIC-2018 medical dataset well. SNN classification on unbalanced medical picture datasets is greatly improved by the suggested strategy. By modifying the incentive coefficient, our technique may be very illness class sensitive. This disease sample identification approach is important for medical diagnosis.

title: Design of a Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN)-Enabled Network Interface Card for Flight Control Computer Systems
authors: Yunfan Yang, Chao Zhang, Jiadong Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) clock synchronization mechanism for flight control systems, which replaces the standard primary/backup clock switching scheme with a more robust quadruple-redundant time synchronization architecture to improve fault tolerance. An FPGA-based NIC is implemented to emulate the deployment within the flight control computer, enabling TSN-compliant clock synchronization and time-aware traffic shaping for downstream nodes while preserving the existing multi-redundant Flight Control Computer (FCC) architecture. Experimental and simulation results demonstrate that safety-critical avionics messages can be transmitted alongside other traffic types on shared physical infrastructure without compromising QoS requirements. This approach significantly reduces the deployment of network devices and cabling, thereby achieving weight reduction and cost savings.

title: Large Language Models as a Cyber Range
authors: Muhammad Mudassar Yamin, Basel Katt, Saira Yamin, Mohib Ullah, Ali Shariq Imran
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: The Cyber ranges function as platforms designed to educate individuals on managing technical systems through systematic procedures within a Cyber security domain. They offer areas to experiment with and also to validate various cyber security concepts without exposure to actual risks. Furthermore, establishing these cyber ranges is often a time-consuming endeavor that requires a blend of diverse skills. These environments can support everything from Capturing the Flag (CTF) exercises, to extensive cyber security simulations encompassing an organizations strategic, tactical and operational levels. Considering the difficulties in designing and operating cyber ranges, the researchers have proposed a novel approach that uses recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs). By simulating cyber range activities using LLMs, the researchers aim to significantly reduce the challenges associated with organizing these exercises. In this work, the researchers have introduced this method, which represents First of its kind effort. It can be seen as a valuable tool for carrying out advanced cyber security drills by treating LLMs as cyber ranges.

title: Back Cover
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)

title: Optimization of Aircraft Skin Laser Scanning with an Improved SPBO-ACO Algorithm
authors: Wanyue Hou, Bo Li, Zhenwei Wang, Bomeng Deng, Yu Pu, Xiaolong Zhu, Hao Yang, Runrun Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To enhance path planning efficiency and scanning quality for aircraft skin inspection, this paper proposes a laser scanning method combining an improved Student Psychological Behavioral Optimization (SPBO) algorithm with Ant Colony Algorithm (ACO). Based on Catia numerical modeling, the skin surface is divided into sub-areas and the path planning task is formulated as a Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP). A dual-optimization framework integrates SPBO's enhanced search diversity through perturbation and learning mechanisms with ACO's global guidance capability. Experimental comparisons on MATLAB demonstrate that the SPBO-ACO algorithm outperforms traditional SPBO, ACO, and GA in path length, number of turns, and repetition rate, showing superior convergence and global optimization performance. The method significantly improves scanning path quality and operational efficiency with strong engineering applicability.

title: Decoupling Control of Electro-Optical Platform based on Inverse System Decoupling Compensation-Linear Active Disturbance Rejection
authors: Yangbo Wang, Lei Zhu, Jianghua Wen, Hao Sun, Hu Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the adverse effects of coupling interactions among the azimuth, pitch, and roll axes on control accuracy in a three-axis three-gimbal electro-optical platform, this paper proposes a decoupling control method based on inverse system compensation combined with linear active disturbance rejection control (LADRC). First, the state-space equations are established according to the system dynamics model of the three-axis three-gimbal electro-optical platform. The inverse system decoupling compensation and LADRC controller are then separately designed. Subsequently, the compensation value is integrated with the observations from the extended state observer (ESO) to formulate an improved LADRC controller incorporating inverse system decoupling compensation. Comparative simulations are conducted between this enhanced controller and a conventional LADRC controller without compensation. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed decoupling control method, which combines inverse system compensation with LADRC, effectively reduces inter-axis coupling and significantly improves control performance.

title: Construction of a Lateral Flow Model for the Simultaneous Rapid Detection of Anti-Dengue IgG and IgM Antibodies
authors: Nguyen Minh Tuan, Vu Quynh Trang, Do Hoang Minh Tue, Thieu Phuong Linh, Tran Diep Anh, Bui Gia Anh, Truong Quoc Phong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Dengue virus (DENV) is a virus typically transmitted by the Aedes Aegypti mosquito that poses a significant public health threat, particularly in tropical and subtropical areas of the world. Since the beginning of 2025, 3.6 million dengue cases and over 1,900 dengue-related deaths have been reported from 94 countries/territories in the WHO Region of Europe (EURO), the Regions of the Americas (PAHO), South-East Asia and West Pacific (SEARO and WPRO, respectively), in the Eastern Mediterranean WHO Region (EMRO), and in Africa. Early detection of dengue fever is essential for preventing the rapid and uncontrollable spread of the disease. In this study, we developed a model of lateral flow immunochromatographic assay (LFIA) test strip for the dual rapid detection of antidengue antibodies. Recombinant E protein expressed in E. coli and purified, was used for the development of the test strip. Protein A was used as a detection agent and conjugated with gold nanoparticles to construct the test strip. Optimal conditions for preparation of protein A conjugate and immobilized protein E were successfully investigated. The assay demonstrated excellent diagnostic performance with high sensitivity and specificity across the evaluated samples.

title: A Deployable Tape-Spring Robotic Arm with a Cable-Actuated Mid-Arm Joint
authors: Rui Chen, Yi Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper proposes a deployable tape-spring robotic arm with a cable-actuated joint located in the mid-arm, designed for on-orbit satellite assembly, precise sample collection, equipment maintenance or complex repairs. The core advantages of the robotic arm include its compact folded size, lightweight structure, and large extension range, which make it easy to integrate into an on-orbit satellite platform. The specific research work includes: completing the 3D structural design of the robotic arm; describing the motion mode of the robotic arm; constructing the kinematic model of the tape-spring robotic arm; and analyzing its workspace characteristics. The research in this paper provides new insights for the design of novel space robotic arms.

title: Guidance and Control for 3D Flight Trajectory-Tracking
authors: Yawen Cao, Yifeng Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of the application field of civil aircraft, the flight tasks to be performed are becoming more and more complex. In order to meet the requirements of aircraft maneuvering trajectory tracking performance, this paper proposes a guidance control algorithm that is easy to implement in engineering. The outer loop designs the guidance law based on the detection step size. The tracking trajectory is linearized to obtain the optimal detection step size. Considering curvature change of the maneuvering trajectory, variable detection step size is further designed on the basis of current guidance algorithm to improve the trajectory tracking efficiency. The inner loop adopts longitudinal and lateral motion decoupling and height velocity coupling method to design the control law. Taking a certain type of aircraft as an example, the typical space maneuvering trajectory is tracked, and the simulation model is built on desktop platform. The simulation results show that the aircraft has high tracking accuracy for the space maneuvering trajectory.

title: A Robust Double Integral SMC Approach for Fault Transient and Frequency Excursion Mitigation in DFIG-based Wind Energy Conversion Systems
authors: Mohammad Zohurul Islam, Md Nafiz Musarrat, Afef Fekih, Senior Member
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: The direct connectivity of doubly fed induction generator (DFIG)-based wind energy conversion systems to the power grid makes them highly vulnerable to grid faults and network disturbances. This paper proposes a double integral sliding mode controller (DISMC) for a DFIG-based wind energy conversion system operating under unbalanced grid voltage conditions. The proposed control framework is derived using a double integral sliding manifold, specifically designed to mitigate adverse dynamics caused by grid faults and frequency deviations. The stability of the system dynamics and convergence in finite time are demonstrated through Lyapunov theory. The controller's effectiveness is assessed under various faulty scenarios, including a single line-to-ground fault, a three-phase symmetrical fault, and frequency excursion. Simulation results validate the proposed method's robustness, efficiency, and rapid transient response.

title: Cryptanalytic Vulnerabilities in PRNS-Based Symmetric Encryption
authors: Ratnakaram Venkata Sai Sidhartha, Rahul Patel, Shazia Tazeen, R. Padmavathy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper presents a cryptanalytic study of a recently proposed symmetric encryption scheme based on the Polynomial Residue Number System (PRNS) [1]. Our scheme employs arbitrarily selected polynomials and pairwise coprime residues as keys. Three critical vulnerabilities are identified: (1) direct recovery of the secret polynomial through factorization attacks, (2) complete parameter extraction via polynomial factorization, and (3) coefficient recovery using lattice reduction techniques. The analysis shows that the scheme’s security claims do not hold under realistic conditions, with all three attack vectors achieving full success rates in experiments across various finite fields.

title: Harmonic-Constrained Envelope Spectrum: A Robust and Efficient Demodulation Technique for Rotating Machinery
authors: Ruizhe Zhang, Oingcui Wang, Shuanping Du
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Rotating machinery is widely used in various industrial applications. The extraction of modulation features from rotating machinery plays a crucial role in both condition monitoring and target identification. Existing demodulation methods based on cyclostationary analysis struggle to achieve a satisfactory balance among demodulation performance, robustness, and computational complexity in complex interference environments. This paper proposes a Harmonic-Constrained Envelope Spectrum (HCES) method. It first proposes the cyclic sparsity measure to quantify modulation intensity, then constructs the salient weighting function to select the frequency band with the highest modulation intensity for demodulation. The effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method are verified using both simulated signals and actual rotating machinery data.

title: Accelerating Transistor Simulations with Self-Supervised Graph Attention Networks
authors: Tarek Mohamed, Hussam Amrouch
abstract: Technology CAD (TCAD) tools are pivotal for transistor modeling, enabling physics-based simulations with high accuracy essential for developing next-generation technology nodes. However, their high computational cost and low throughput severely constrain large-scale design-space exploration. Accelerating TCAD simulations while preserving physical accuracy has therefore become a central research objective. A promising approach involves the use of graph-based representations of TCAD meshes fed into graph neural networks (GNNs), which offer rapid and accurate alternatives. However, traditional GNNs, such as Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) and Graph Attention Networks (GATs) often falter with noisy transistor-mesh graphs, where embeddings become cluttered and complex. In thiswork, we propose a self-supervisedGNNframework for TCAD device modeling, specifically employing the Self-Supervised GAT (SuperGAT).We demonstrate that self-supervised GNN models, in contrast to GCNs and GATs, introduces a self-supervised edge-prediction task to distinguish informative from redundant topological connections. Our evaluation compares various GNN aggregation schemes demonstrates that SuperGAT successfully reproduces key transistor transfer characteristics drain current–gate voltage (Id–Vg) curves, subthreshold swing (SS) and threshold voltage (Vth). The dataset was generated using 14nm FDSOI transistors. Our SuperGAT model achieves a minimum R2 score of 0.992 across all characteristics and an inference time of only 0.007 134 sec per transistor, resulting in a speed-up exceeding 100 000 x compared to TCAD simulation, which underscore the capability to substantially accelerate TCAD transistor simulations in advanced technology development.

title: Going beyond IQ: An integrative model of human potentials for a successful career
authors: Rumjhum Bandyopadhyay, Seema R. Singh
abstract: Human intelligence is no longer a single concept confined to IQ. The concept of human intelligence has evolved in the last few decades, leading to the exploration of various kinds of human potentials that are effective in dealing with the emerging challenges of the 21st century. The three potentials—namely, emotional intelligence (EI), spiritual intelligence (SI), and digital intelligence (DI)—have recently drawn particular attention from researchers. Instead of studying them as discrete entities, present authors explore the interconnection between these intelligences. This perspective will help develop a new insight into their interaction and in how they can facilitate success in professional settings.

title: Vision-Based Nonfixed Contact Manipulation of Deformable Objects With Force Constraints
authors: Yiming Liu, Lijun Han, Hesheng Wang
abstract: In deformable object manipulation, the utilization of nonfixed contact holds promise for dynamically adjusting contact positions in response to ongoing deformations, thereby mitigating contact forces and enhancing operational efficiency. Within this discourse, we introduce an active contact adjustment strategy tailored to nonfixed contact manipulation of deformable objects, particularly under force constraints and uncertain environmental conditions. To evaluate the impact of contact adjustment, the deformation Jacobi matrix (DJM) across different contact points is modeled by considering tangent and radial distances, and the unknown parameters in DJM are estimated online. According to the DJM, a novel evaluation metric, combining task objectives with constraints, is devised to assess the feasibility of current contacts and guide contact adjustment. By integrating shape servoing and contact adjustment, a vision-based controller is proposed, which enables the manipulator to achieve the desired object shape under force constraints. The effectiveness of our controller has been verified in practical experiments.

title: Power Prediction and Optimization for FPGA Designs Using Explainable Machine Learning
authors: Kankana Karmakar, Samriddha Roy, Swastika Sau, Soumik Kumar Kundu, Samit Karmakar, Subhabrata Banerjee, Sutapa Ray Adhikari
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In the context of the increasing need for energy-efficient and resource-constrained computing systems, power consumption has become a major design constraint for the design of modern FPGA-based architectures. The conventional approach to power estimation for FPGA-based architectures relies on the analysis of the design at the post-synthesis level, which often requires a considerable amount of design iterations. This often results in increased design time and computation. To overcome this limitation of the conventional approach, this work presents a novel AI-based predictive approach for the power estimation of FPGA-based architectures using a set of machine learning regression models. The design parameters used for the proposed approach include a set of synthesis-inspired design parameters such as logic utilization, registers, fanout, clock frequency, and switching activity. The proposed approach uses a set of machine learning regression models such as Linear Regression, Decision Tree Regression, Random Forest Regression, and Gradient Boosting Regression for power estimation. The performance of the proposed approach is evaluated using a set of metrics such as cross-validation, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score, and Root Mean Square Error. From the experimental results, it is observed that Linear Regression has the highest accuracy compared to other models, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score of 0.992 and RMSE of 0.43 mW, which signifies the strong relationship between the design parameters and power consumption. Ensemble learning models are found to have high accuracy, where Gradient Boosting Regression achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score of 0.985 and RMSE of 0.60 mW, and Random Forest Regression achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score of 0.955 and RMSE of 1.03 mW, and Decision Tree Regression achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score of 0.913 and RMSE of 1.44 mW. Moreover, feature importance analysis provides useful insights into power-critical design parameters, which can be used for interpretable and AI-driven optimization. The proposed approach allows for accurate power prediction at the early design stage without relying on exhaustive synthesis-based power analysis, which simplifies the design complexity and reduces the development cycle for power-aware intelligent FPGA-based systems.
