title: Structure-Aware Image Inpainting Using GANs with Low-Rank Regularization
authors: Adarsh Sikreewal, Armaan Pandey, Shivanshi Mishra, Sagar Shivprasad Rajoriya, Jyoti Prakash Panda, Pravin Kumar Samanta
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: Image inpainting aims to reconstruct missing or corrupted regions of an image in a visually coherent manner.. Traditional inpainting methods often struggle to preserve both global structure and fine details. In this paper, we propose an advanced image inpainting approach that combines Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) with a Low-Rank Prior to achieve structure-aware and realistic image restoration. The GAN framework consists of a U-Net-based generator that predicts missing regions and a PatchGAN discriminator that enforces local texture realism. To further enhance global consistency, a low-rank regularization term is incorporated into the loss function, encouraging smoothness and structural stability in reconstructed regions. Experiments on the CelebA-HQ dataset demonstrate that the proposed model effectively balances texture sharpness and structural integrity, outperforming standard GAN-based inpainting methods in terms of both perceptual quality and quantitative metrics such as PSNR and SSIM. Image inpainting is a fundamental problem in computer vision that involves reconstructing missing or corrupted regions of an image in a visually consistent manner. Traditional interpolation or patch-based approaches often fail to produce realistic results, especially when dealing with large missing areas or complex textures. To address these challenges, this paper presents an Image Inpainting framework using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs).

title: Multi-Agent Architecture Design for Urban Short-Distance Travel Planning
authors: Junyi Tu, Nattapong Tongtep, Thatsanee Charoenporn
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: The global tourism landscape is shifting toward more personalized, immersive, and experience-driven travel. Standardized travel packages are no longer sufficient-particularly for short urban trips lasting one to three days. Planning such trips is inherently complex, involving multiple objectives and constraints. Current online platforms still face major challenges in meeting diverse user needs, integrating heterogeneous data sources, and respecting practical limitations such as attraction operating hours or user budgets. To address these gaps, this study proposes a prototype Multi-Agent System (MAS) framework designed for personalized urban short-trip planning. The approach leverages the principle of division of labor and dynamic collaboration among specialized agents to seamlessly combine user preferences, tourism resources, and realworld constraints. The framework consists of three key agents: the User Agent, which models user preferences and limitations; the Resource Agent, which manages both static and dynamic information about points of interest (POIs); and the Planning & Coordination Agent, which constructs feasible itineraries. Inter-agent communication is coordinated through a simplified Contract Net Protocol (CNP). Experimental validation shows that the Planning Agent effectively generates itineraries that comply with time windows, budgets, and other constraints. The system also records the decision-making process, offering a degree of transparency that helps enhance user trust. Overall, this research lays a practical foundation for the next generation of intelligent tourism planning systems capable of delivering adaptive and trustworthy travel experiences.

title: Decentralized Control Strategies for EV-Enabled Microgrids
authors: D Sarathkumar, Sathesh Kumar Thirumalaisamy, M. Sugumaran, K. Mohanraj, Nageswari D, I Raja Rajeswari, G. Dinesh, S. Prince Samuel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The integration of electric vehicles (EVs) into microgrids offers significant potential to enhance grid resilience, optimize energy usage, and support renewable energy integration. However, managing these resources in a centralized manner poses challenges related to scalability, complexity, and cybersecurity vulnerabilities. This paper investigates decentralized control strategies tailored to EV-enabled microgrids, focusing on optimizing energy flow, enhancing system reliability, and minimizing operational costs. We explore various decentralized approaches, including agent-based models, peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading, and local control algorithms that allow EVs to dynamically interact with microgrid resources, such as Decentralized energy resources (DERs) and energy storage systems (ESS). Through simulations and real-world case studies, we demonstrate how decentralized control improves load balancing, voltage stability, and frequency regulation in EV-integrated microgrids while enabling flexible energy management in response to fluctuating renewable generation and plea. Additionally, the proposed methods address key interoperability and communication challenges, providing a framework for scalable, secure, and efficient operation. The outcomes of this article is best part of decentralized control in progressing smarter, resilient microgrid architectures, particularly as EV penetration continues to grow, paving the way for enhanced grid sustainability and stability.

title: Operating Principle and AC Copper Loss of a High-Speed Flux Reversal Machine with Air-Gap Magnetic Field Modulation
authors: Wenfei Yu, Jiawei Zhou, Yanshen Ma, Wei Hua
abstract: Since both the permanent magnets (PMs) and armature windings are located on the stator side, the rotor features a simple salient pole structure. The flux reversal PM (FRPM) machine offers advantages such as high reliability and suitability for high-speed operation. However, during high speed conditions, the machine faces challenges such as high fundamental frequency, significant electromagnetic losses, and reduced efficiency. To reduce electromagnetic losses and improve machine efficiency, this paper proposes a 6-slot 4-pole (6s/4p) axial winding complementary FRPM structure for molecular pump drive applications. This structure effectively eliminates even-order harmonics in the flux linkage and back electromotive force (EMF), while also helping to reduce cogging torque and torque ripple. Firstly, the proposed machine topology and key parameters are introduced. Secondly, based on the general air-gap field modulation theory (GAFMT), the operating mechanism of the machine is explained from three aspects: the PM magnetic field, armature reaction magnetic field, and electromagnetic torque. Furthermore, a winding magnetomotive force (MMF) correction coefficient is proposed that accounts for the impact of AC copper loss, providing a more accurate representation of the influence of high-frequency AC copper loss on the MMF. Following this, based on the field-circuit coupled method, the influencing factors and extent of high-frequency AC copper loss under high-frequency harmonic excitation are investigated. The suppression effects of AC copper loss are analyzed in terms of harmonic current, temperature, wire gauge, winding configuration and conductor positions. Finally, a prototype machine is manufactured, and experimental validation confirms the accuracy of the proposed analysis and modeling methods.

title: TR-Gaussians: High-fidelity Real-time Rendering of Planar Transmission and Reflection with 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Yong Liu, Keyang Ye, Tianjia Shao, Kun Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We propose Transmission-Reflection Gaussians (TRGaussians), a novel 3D-Gaussian-based representation for highfidelity rendering of planar transmission and reflection, which are ubiquitous in indoor scenes. Our method combines 3D Gaussians with learnable reflection planes that explicitly model the glass planes with view-dependent reflectance strengths. Real scenes and transmission components are modeled by 3D Gaussians and the reflection components are modeled by the mirrored Gaussians with respect to the reflection plane. The transmission and reflection components are blended according to a Fresnelbased, view-dependent weighting scheme, allowing for faithful synthesis of complex appearance effects under varying viewpoints. To effectively optimize TR-Gaussians, we develop a multistage optimization framework incorporating color and geometry constraints and an opacity perturbation mechanism. Experiments on different datasets demonstrate that TR-Gaussians achieve real-time, high-fidelity novel view synthesis in scenes with planar transmission and reflection, and outperform state-of-the-art approaches both quantitatively and qualitatively.

title: Internal-Dynamics-Originated Stability Region Leveraging for Targeted MMC System Stabilization Control Design Strategy
authors: Hongyi Chen, Heya Yang, Fan Yang, Jing Sheng, Haoze Luo, Wuhua Li, Xiangning He
abstract: The modular multilevel converter (MMC) has emerged as a promising solution for large-scale renewable energy integration. However, its inherent internal dynamics pose challenges in ensuring and assessing system stability. This article focuses on addressing the internal-dynamics-driven (IDD) instability in MMC systems. Unlike existing studies that rely on the output impedance compressing the entire dynamics into an all-in-one transfer function with internal dynamics hidden, this article visualizes the MMC internal dynamics as a targeted equivalent impedance, which directly links internal control parameters to system stability and thus facilitates targeted control design. The analysis reveals that the capacitive characteristics shaped by internal dynamics cause instability when coupled with inductive grids. Moreover, circulating current control can broaden this capacitive frequency band and exacerbate the instability risk without targeted design. Based on this insight, an internal-dynamics-originated (IDO) stability region is derived as practical control design guidance. A targeted stabilization control design framework is then established, enabling system stability without precise prior knowledge of the system impedance. Finally, multicondition experiments validate the effectiveness of the framework, providing a generalizable guideline for stability enhancement in MMC-based renewable energy systems and other MMC-interfaced applications.

title: Pervasive Multi-Level Cooperative ISAC in 6G Networks: Architecture, Key Technologies and Experiments
authors: Xiaoyu Sun, Ruoyun Liu, Qingji Jiang, Jiamin Li, Dongming Wang, Junhui Zhao, Xiaohu You
abstract: Multi-static integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) serves as a key enabler for realizing the dynamic massive Internet of Things (mIoT) scenarios in 6G. However, existing supporting architectures for multi-static ISAC exhibit significant discrepancies in terms of network frameworks and implemented communication and sensing (C&S) functionalities. Furthermore, none of these architectures can fully meet the stringent requirements of 6G dynamic mIoT scenarios, which include massive terminal access, highly reliable uplink and downlink communications, high-precision sensing, and full-coverage dynamic management. In this article, we systematically investigate the foundational architecture required for the practical deployment of multi-static ISAC and propose the pervasive multi-level (PML) cooperative ISAC system architecture to address the aforementioned requirements. Subsequently, a comparative analysis between the proposed architecture and existing architectures is conducted to elucidate its advantages in terms of pervasiveness, scalability, near real-time responsiveness, multi-dimensional resource schedulability, and stable performance guarantee. We further outline the core research thrusts of the PML cooperative ISAC system architecture and identify promising opportunities for future studies. Finally, we validate its superior performance through numerical simulations and prototype testbed experiments.

title: Performance-Aligned LLMs for Generating Fast HPC Code
authors: Daniel Nichols, Pranav Polasam, Harshitha Menon, Aniruddha Marathe, Todd Gamblin, Abhinav Bhatele
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Optimizing scientific software is a difficult task because codebases are often large and complex, and performance can depend upon several factors including the algorithm, its implementation, and hardware among others. Causes of poor performance can originate from disparate sources and be difficult to diagnose. Recent years have seen a multitude of work that use large language models (LLMs) to assist in software development tasks. However, these tools are trained to model the distribution of code as text, and are not specifically designed to understand performance aspects of code. In this work, we introduce a reinforcement learning based methodology to align the outputs of code LLMs with performance. This allows us to build upon the current code modeling capabilities of LLMs and extend them to generate better performing code. We demonstrate that our fine tuned model improves the expected speedup of generated code over base models for a set of benchmark tasks from 0.9 to 1.6 for serial code and 1.9 to 4.5 for OpenMP parallel code.

title: An Integrated Boost Multilevel Converter with Asymmetrical Digitized Modulation Scheme for an Inductive Power Transfer Application
authors: Bharat Vardani, Duleepa J. Thrimawithana, Grant A. Covic
abstract: The misalignment between the primary and secondary coils of an inductive power transfer (IPT) system used to charge electric vehicles (EVs) reduces the charging efficiency as well as the charging power. This issue is compounded by the fluctuations in battery voltage, posing challenges in designing the power converters for an IPT-based EV charger that efficiently handles varying load conditions. Typically, this challenge is addressed by using expensive SiC MOSFETs with pre-regulation and secondary-side control or employing complex control and compensation strategies. Recently, an integrated boost multilevel converter (IBMC) topology utilizing low-voltage Si MOSFETs has been proposed as a solution. However, its requirement for 12 sub-modules (SMs) can increase system costs. Furthermore, to overcome the limited number of useful states, the power factor correction (PFC) circuit had to support the IBMC by varying the DC-link voltage. This article improves upon the IBMC topology by introducing a new configuration that integrates both Si and SiC MOS-FETs to reduce the switch count, thereby reducing the system costs and losses. Additionally, this work proposed the asymmetrical digitized modulation scheme employed in proposed IBMC configuration to increase the number of useful states while minimizing the number of SMs, enabling efficient operation over a wide load range.

title: A Novel Visual Servoing Strategy of Continuum Robots for Target Tracking with Multi-scale Distances
authors: Haijun Peng, Pengfei Ma, Hao Yang, Chaozhong Yang, Fei Li, Jie Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: The visual servoing strategy of a continuum robot has wide-ranging applications and significant engineering value. However, limited by the accuracy of its motion control, research on visual servoing of continuum robots is currently limited to close-range conditions (distance < 1m). Therefore, a novel visual servoing strategy for continuum robots is proposed in this paper that can track external targets in real-time and across distance from centimeters to tens of meters. Firstly, a continuous mapping relationship was proposed by integrating the visual coordinate transformation process with the mechanical model of the continuum robot, establishing a mapping relationship between pixel error and drive values. Secondly, a closed-loop control system for trajectory tracking of moving targets is designed by combining the continuous mapping relationship. Finally, trajectory tracking experiments are conducted on a moving target (i.e., human-shaped target) at different distance scales. Compared with the eye-in-hand strategy at different distances (0.2m, 2m and 3m), the experimental results amply demonstrate the tracking accuracy and stability of the proposed visual servoing strategy. Furthermore, under the experimental condition of long distance (20.5m), the proposed visual servoing strategy can still achieve accurate tracking of the moving target, with an average tracking error of 27.97 pixels (about 2.31cm).

title: Design, modeling and control for a stiffness compensation compliant mechanism based reluctance-actuated nanopositioner
authors: Datong Pan, Yunlang Xu, Xiaofeng Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a nanopositioning stage actuated by a hybrid reluctance actuator (HRA) integrated with a novel stiffness compensation compliant mechanism (SCCM) employing initially curved beams (ICBs). The ICB-based SCCM is analytically designed using Castigliano’s theorem to generate a tailored nonlinear positive stiffness that cancels the inherent negative stiffness of the HRA over its full stroke, mitigating stability challenges and reducing control effort. System dynamics incorporating both magnetic and elastic potentials are optimized via the Dung Beetle Optimizer (DBO). The nonlinear actuator stiffness is reduced by 70% through mechanical compensation, as confirmed by force measurement experiments. To achieve nanometric tracking precision, an enhanced Koopman-based trajectory modification method (EKTMM) is developed, improving disturbance estimation and compensation of residual nonlinearities. The proposed system achieves a root mean square tracking error of 17.8 nm under a fourth-order polynomial reference trajectory. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves a millimeter stroke with high positioning accuracy.

title: Optimized Lightweight Midori Cipher Architecture Featuring Side-Channel Resilient S-Boxes for Secure Edge Computing
authors: Ruby Mishra, Manish Okade, Kamalakanta Mahapatra
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents novel hardware architectures for Midori, a lightweight symmetric block cipher optimized for low-energy edge computing applications. To enhance the efficiency of the Midori-64 variant, the architecture has been redesigned with a 16-bit datapath. The proposed design adopts a hybrid approach, combining parallel and serial data processing to meet the demands of encryption. Specialized selector units are integrated into the architecture to streamline this process, resulting in improved performance while maintaining a compact footprint. In addition to architectural enhancements, a masking countermeasure has been incorporated into the S-boxes of Midori to strengthen resistance against side-channel power analysis attacks. The masked S-boxes were initially integrated into the cipher’s substitution-permutation network (SPN), forming a secure variant of the core transformation layer. This modified SPN was subsequently embedded into the proposed Midori-64 architecture, and the associated performance and area overheads were systematically evaluated. The proposed masked S-boxes have an area reduction of 55.7%, power reduction of 62.38% and energy reduction of 78.2% compared to other masked designs. These significant improvements come with a 50% reduction in area overheads and a 7% reduction in delay overheads when compared to the current state-of-the-art masked designs with respect to the unmasked designs. We also evaluated the modified, overall masked and unmasked encryption architecture for Midori-64 on Nexys4 DDR FPGA and for standard cell libraries. Our findings indicate that the proposed masked designs offer improved performance with significantly reduced overheads compared to their unmasked counterparts. The FPGA-based implementations have proven to be better for the low area and high throughput requirements for a constrained device in IoT applications. Moreover, the resiliency of the proposed masked S-boxes to the differential power analysis attack has been explored and demonstrated in detail in this paper.

title: NUFFT-Extended Keystone Transform for Efficient Long-Time Coherent Integration in Hypersonic Target Detection
authors: Hao Shi, Guisheng Liao, Yufeng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: For pulse-Doppler radars employing large time-bandwidth product waveforms, coherent integration and detection of weak hypersonic targets are challenged by echo scaling effect and linear range cell migration, which manifests as Doppler cell migration in the two-dimensional frequency domain. Additional compensation is required when targets undergo constant acceleration. To address these issues, this paper proposes an efficient long-time coherent integration algorithm for hypersonic targets with constant velocity, termed NUFFT-EKT. The method, inspired by the Improved Radon Inverse Fourier Transform (IRIFT), exploits the periodicity of raw two-dimensional frequency-domain echoes, while employing the NonUniform Fast Fourier Transform (NUFFT) to implement the Doppler-domain extended Keystone Transform (EKT). A velocity-segmented update strategy is further introduced for the velocity-dependent pulse compression filter construction, significantly reducing update frequency and memory requirements. For accelerating targets, the approach is extended to Dechirp-NUFFT-EKT by applying acceleration search and compensation prior to integration. The proposed algorithm achieves near-optimal performance in terms of output SNR, detection, and parameter estimation, while avoiding the parasitic harmonics along the range dimension inherent in IRIFT, with comparable computational complexity. In scenarios with large Doppler ambiguity numbers, NUFFT-EKT even achieves slightly lower complexity than IRIFT. Numerical simulations confirm the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed method, demonstrating substantial gains over conventional coherent integration techniques for hypersonic targets.

title: Machine Learning-Based Classification of EEG Signals for Differentiating Between Stress and Relaxation States
authors: Vu Duy Hai, Tran Bao Tram, Pham Thi Hong Tham, Nguyen Ha Anh, Hoang Quang Huy, Tran Anh Vu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: This study evaluates the performance of machine learning models in classifying stress and relaxation states from electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. Timely stress detection is necessary, and EEG stands out as an objective and effective tool. The main objective is to review the method of stress detection from EEG based on frequency band power ratio and analyze the data processing/evaluation strategy to improve the generalization ability. The EEGMAT dataset from 36 subjects is used for this research. The study compares two data partitioning strategies: global blending (Case 1) and subjectspecific partitioning (Case 2). Features based on power spectral density and EEG frequency band power ratio are extracted. Two feature selection methods, Mutual Information (MI) and ANOVA F-test, are applied, along with popular machine learning models such as KNN, SVM, Random Forest (RF), XGBoost, and Voting Ensemble. The results of Case 1 achieved high accuracy (up to 96.32% for KNN/KNN + SVM with MI), but did not accurately reflect the generalization ability due to the risk of overfitting. In contrast, Case 2 (subject-specific separation) gave lower performance (up to 87.6% for RF with MI) but demonstrated better generalization ability to new subjects. In particular, MI provided significantly more effective and stable results than ANOVA F-test due to its ability to detect non-linear relationships. The study emphasizes the importance of designing appropriate data processing and evaluation strategies, opening up the potential for developing stress recognition systems for new subjects.

title: Classification of Fetal Health Status Based on Application of Deep Learning and Support Vector Machine from Cardiotocogram Data
authors: Dinh Do Van, Bui Bao Anh, Huy Dang Tran, Thuy Hang Dang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: This paper proposes and evaluates machine learningbased models for the classification of fetal health status using cardiotocogram (CTG) signals. The dataset is categorized into three classes: Normal, Suspect, and Pathological. Two hybrid models, AlexNet-SVM and ResNet50-SVM, are developed and comparatively analyzed. Experimental results indicate that the AlexNet-SVM model achieves a classification accuracy of 97.18%, while the ResNet50-SVM model attains 96.98%. The superior performance of the AlexNet-SVM model demonstrates its potential as a clinical decision-support tool, contributing to improved diagnostic accuracy, reduced risks, and more comprehensive maternal-fetal healthcare.

title: Identifying Risk Regimes in Thai Equities Using Rolling-Correlation Features and Unsupervised Learning
authors: Saowaluk Watanapa, Bunthit Watanapa
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study proposes a model for analyzing Thai equities based on their sensitivities to global and local macroeconomic indicators. By using rolling correlations between stock returns—sampled across sectors according to FTSE Russell's Industry Classification Benchmark (ICB)—and selected global and local market indicators, we develop time-varying exposure profiles for each stock. Applying <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K$</tex> -means clustering to these profiles yields three behaviorally coherent groups. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) then reduces these exposure features into a two-dimensional regime space, with quadrants representing risk-on and risk-off conditions. Mapping the clustered stocks onto this regime space demonstrates how stock behaviors relate to prevailing macroeconomic conditions and aids transparent, data-driven investment decisions. Empirical results suggest that the proposed framework provides a clear and interpretable foundation for risk-aware investors, especially retirees seeking clarity in portfolio building.

title: Secure Phishing Analysis and Robust Training Algorithm (SPARTA) for Phishing Website Detection
authors: Diksha Damahe, Ajay Kumar Phulre
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The common defenses nowadays such as blocklists and hard-to-learn heuristics no longer seem to be resistant enough to keep users safe on phishing websites as the attacks grow increasingly advanced. We present SPARTA (Secure Phishing Analysis and Robust Training Algorithm) in this paper as a strong security framework where machine learning is utilized to characterize the threats and break in their paths before striking. Our hybrid feature extraction map explores far within the URL layout of a website, the HTML/JavaScript implemented within it, and the state of its own SSL/TLS certificate to establish a comprehensive computerized print. It is this rich feature set against which we then apply an ensemble of classifiers such as Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest (RF), and a Neural Network which we combine together via hardschema vote scheme in order to make a highly reliable and true-to-the-point verdict. Comparing it to benchmark datasets available on PhishTank and the UCI Machine Learning Repository, SPARTA has achievable detection rates of 99.15%. SPARTA registers as an impressive game-changer in the practical application of browsers, email gateways, and other security tools, capable of keeping pace with the constantly evolving techniques used by cybercriminals.

title: UAV-Framework for Environmental Monitoring and Urban Land Use Detection
authors: Sneha Paul, Swetashree Mal, Ratna Chakrabarty
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In this study, a UAV-powered system of environmental monitoring and city land-use mapping is defined, combining multi-sensorial data capture, paradigms of edge detection in computational, and deep learning-based geospatial intelligence. The temperature and atmospheric gas sensors, LiDAR and high-resolution optical-equipped UAV manages to scan the environment autonomously in order to examine metamorphosis of the terrain, vegetation loss, and human-made encroachment. Innovated edge detection algorithms effectively divest structural periphery, hydrologic structures, and transitions between landforms and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and encrypted segmentation structures enhance classification accuracy. Moreover, the system shows excellence in the detection of prognostic anomalies, which develops an anticipatory warning against deforestation paths, pollution gradients, and weather variations. Stringent comparative evaluations against manual reconnaissance highlight the fidelity of the framework and it proves the suitability of this framework to macroscopic environmental diagnostics. The paper confirms the UAVs as inevitable tools of sustainable urban analytics and ecological strengthening, and the future development of self-reliant AI frameworks and variable air surveys will increase their strategic effectiveness.

title: Advances in Emotion-Aware AI Agents for Mental & Emotional Health
authors: Chitraksh Suri, Anshul Munjal, Khushi Kapoor, Shreya Jaiswal, Tripti Pandey
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper presents an emotion-aware AI agent designed to provide empathetic mental health support with cultural, situational, and contextual awareness. The proposed system integrates multimodal emotion recognition by combining text, speech, and facial expression analysis with a culturally adaptive natural language processing framework. By leveraging real-time user inputs and ambient contextual cues, the agent dynamically adapts its therapeutic dialogue and guidance strategies. Key features include a journaling module for identifying emotional patterns and a dual-interaction mechanism that enables user feedback through text or audio. The agent delivers brief, personalized interventions such as cognitive behavioral exercises and mindfulness-based techniques tailored to individual needs. By promoting emotional self-awareness, resilience, and sustained mental well-being, the proposed approach aims to support inclusive and context-sensitive mental health assistance.

title: Smart Energy Management Framework for Optimal Integration of Renewable Energy in Modern Power Systems
authors: Vivek Kumar, Anoop Arya, Abhay Pratap
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)

title: Adaptive Hybrid Agents for CRM: Combining Rule Engines, LLMs, and Feedback Loops for Reliable Automation
authors: Nikita Kothari, Vinay Venkatesh, Varun Joshi, Lav Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: As enterprise systems evolve from deterministic automation toward adaptive agentic intelligence, the challenge of balancing creativity, compliance, and reliability has become central to Customer Relationship Management (CRM) automation. This research presents the Adaptive Hybrid Agent (AHA) — a unified architecture that combines symbolic rule reasoning, large language model (LLM) inference, and continuous feedback-driven adaptation. The AHA architecture features a meta-controller that dynamically orchestrates between symbolic, neural, and hybrid reasoning paths according to context complexity, confidence thresholds, and feedback gradients derived from user interaction and system metrics. By employing multi-modal feedback including rule validation outcomes, engagement signals, and hallucination scores, the framework achieves self-calibrating behavior that improves both accuracy and adaptability under domain drift. Empirical evaluation across CRM workflows such as campaign generation, text-to-SQL analytic reasoning, and policy-aware message composition demonstrates significant improvements in factual grounding (+57%), task success rate (+14%), and engagement uplift (+25%) relative to static or single-modality baselines. These findings suggest that adaptive hybrid architectures enable reliable, auditable, and continually self-improving CRM agents, capable of aligning generative reasoning with enterprise governance.

title: Modular-Based Design of Passenger Address System for Civil Aircraft
authors: Xu Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To effectively suppress complex ambient noise in civil aircraft cabins and meet relevant airworthiness regulatory requirements, this paper proposes a modular passenger address system for civil aircraft. The system employs a modular zoning architecture enabling zonal broadcast control across cabin regions. By constructing an ambient noise matrix of the cabin environment and utilizing adaptive filtering techniques to dynamically adjust filter parameters, it achieves background noise suppression. Simulation testing demonstrates that adaptive filtering outperforms FIR filtering in suppressing both noise and acoustic feedback (howling).

title: Research on Distributed Parallel Association Technology for Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data
authors: Xiaoke Lei, Jiyan Dai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the multi-target tracking (MTT) challenges in complex electromagnetic environments of modern battlefields, this paper focuses on an MTT fusion system, and proposes an efficient and engineering-friendly distributed parallel association fusion architecture. Specifically, a multi-level association mechanism is designed for multi-source heterogeneous sensors, integrating coarse threshold judgment, dynamic multi-factor correlation, an optimized auction algorithm-based association allocation, and time sliding window detection. Simulation results in multi-aircraft tracking scenarios demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a 99.99% correct association probability, enabling continuous, stable, and accurate tracking of multiple targets. It providing an efficient and reliable solution for multi-target tracking in complex battlefield environments.

title: EfficientNetB0-Based Deep Learning Framework for High-Accuracy Satellite Image Classification
authors: Chandra Kishore, Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Neha Sharma, Saurabh Fulara, Gaurav Tuteja
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Satellite image classification plays a pivotal role in remote sensing applications such as environmental monitoring, land-use analysis, and disaster response. This research addresses the challenge of achieving high-accuracy classification with computational efficiency by utilizing the EfficientNetB0 deep learning architecture. The methodology involves preprocessing satellite images through normalization, resizing, and augmentation, followed by transfer learning using a pre-trained EfficientNetB0 model fine-tuned for multi-class classification. The model was trained on a well-balanced dataset comprising four distinct classes: cloudy, desert, green area, and water. Evaluation metrics, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix, were used to assess performance. The model achieved an overall classification accuracy of 99.56%, with individual class metrics indicating high reliability-cloudy and desert categories achieved perfect scores of $\mathbf{1. 0 0}$, while green area and water achieved F1-scores of 0.98. Graphical analysis of training history demonstrated rapid convergence and minimal overfitting, supported by stable accuracy and loss curves. This study contributes to the field by demonstrating that EfficientNetB0 can serve as an effective and lightweight solution for satellite image classification. The results indicate strong potential for deployment in real-time or resource-constrained environments, with further applications in scalable remote sensing platforms and automated geospatial analysis systems.

title: EcoSyn: Remote-Sensing-Guided Eco-State Control for Integrated Irrigation and Forestry Management
authors: Sajid Hussain, Xi Benye, Muhammad Altaf, Hagumubuzima Fulgence, Wenhan Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: EcoSyn is a unified control-and-learning framework for coupled irrigation–forestry management under non-stationary climate drivers. The architecture comprises: (i) a Hierarchical Adaptive EcoState Machine (HAESM) that encodes hybrid dynamics, fusing discrete event triggers with continuous sensor fields to govern zone-level mode transitions; (ii) an Eco-Impact Learning Module (EILM) that optimizes a composite Eco-Impact Index <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$ \rm{EII}=\omega _{1}\,\mathrm{W}_{\rm{eff}}/\mathrm{W}_{\rm{req}}+\omega _{2}\,\mathrm{C}_{\rm{abs}}/\mathrm{C}_{\max }$</tex-math></inline-formula>, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{W}_{\rm{eff}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> integrates effective water use and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{C}_{\rm{abs}}$</tex-math></inline-formula> is a carbon-uptake functional of flux and vegetation indices; (iii) a Distributed Knowledge Node Network (DKNN) for federated, privacy-preserving parameter sharing across heterogeneous agro-ecological zones with edge execution; and (iv) a Climate-Resilient Feedback Loop (CRFL) that introduces forward-weighted reinforcement using event-conditioned gains for anomaly and extremes handling. Online optimization ties HAESM transition policies to EILM's loss-regularized objective, while DKNN aggregation stabilizes local controllers against covariate shift. The full stack ingests multi-source IoT measurements and operates in a calibrated simulation with FAOSTAT-derived priors and grid-structured geospatial features. In six stress scenarios spanning demand surges, variable precipitation, soil degradation, and high evapotranspiration, EcoSyn attains 94.2% resource-allocation accuracy, 80.2% drought-response efficiency, and 89.7% anomaly resilience.

title: Blockchain Cooperative Spectrum Management for Wi-Fi and LTE-Unlicensed Coexistence Networks
authors: Ziqing Yu, Zheng Chang, Tommi Mikkonen, Valerio Frascolla, Shahid Mumtaz
abstract: Efficiently running services in the worldwide scattered spectrum bands is increasingly difficult as those bands get ever more congested. Unlicensed spectrum offers a promising solution but effective coexistence among access technologies is required to mitigate interference and ensure the always more stringent Quality of Service required by new services. Besides, the increasing number and complexity of security threats further challenge system reliability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a mechanism to enhance security and reliability , and a blockchain-based framework for spectrum sensing and sharing in Wi-Fi and Long Term Evolution-Unlicensed (LTE-U) coexistence networks. Performance under different channel fading models is analyzed, and obtained results show the effectiveness of the proposed scheme, improved sensing accuracy, and resistance to malicious behavior in both low- and high-density scenarios.

title: A Framework for Lossy Compression of Heavy-Tailed Sources
authors: Karim Ezzeddine, Jihad Fahs, Ibrahim Abou-Faycal
abstract: We study the rate-distortion problem for both scalar and vector memoryless heavy-tailed α-stable sources (0 < α < 2). Using a recently defined notion of “strength” as a power measure, we derive the rate-distortion function for α-stable sources subject to a constraint on the strength of the error and show it to be logarithmic in the strength-to-distortion ratio. We show how our framework paves the way for finding optimal quantizers for α- stable sources and other general heavy-tailed ones. In addition, we study high-rate scalar quantizers and show that uniform ones are asymptotically optimal under the error-strength distortion measure. We compare uniform Gaussian and Cauchy quantizers and show that more representation points for the Cauchy source are required to guarantee the same quantization quality. Our findings generalize the well-known results of rate-distortion and quantization of Gaussian sources (α = 2) under a quadratic distortion measure.

title: Zero-Knowledge Federated Learning: A New Trustworthy and Privacy-Preserving Distributed Learning Paradigm
authors: Taotao Wang, Yuxin Jin, Qing Yang, Yihan Xia, Long Shi, Shengli Zhang
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a promising paradigm in distributed machine learning, enabling collaborative model training while preserving data privacy. FL still contends with significant challenges- most notably regarding security and trust. Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) offer a potential solution by establishing trust and enhancing system integrity throughout the FL process. Although several studies have explored ZKP-based FL (ZK-FL), a systematic framework and comprehensive analysis are still lacking. This article makes two contributions. First, we propose a structured ZK-FL framework that categorizes and analyzes the technical roles of ZKPs across various FL stages and tasks. Second, we introduce a novel algorithm, Verifiable Client Selection FL (Veri-CS-FL), which employs ZKPs to refine the client selection process. In Veri-CS-FL, participating clients generate verifiable proofs for the performance metrics of their local models and submit these concise proofs to the server for efficient verification. The server then selects clients with high-quality local models for uploading, subsequently aggregating the contributions from these selected clients. By integrating ZKPs, Veri-CS-FL not only ensures the accuracy of performance metrics but also fortifies trust among participants while enhancing the overall efficiency and security of FL systems.

title: The Influence of Music Modes on Cybersickness and Sense of Presence in Virtual Reality
authors: Apirati Kottharin, Waranrach Viriyavit, Sittisak Saechueng, Eri Sato-Shimokawara, Yosuke Fukuchi, Nobuyuki Nishiuchi, Suwanna Rasmequan, Athita Onuean, Peerasak Pianprasit
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR) has been increasingly utilized in education, healthcare, and entertainment. However, many users experience cybersickness, manifested as dizziness, nausea, and fatigue, particularly in highly dynamic scenarios such as VR roller coasters, which hinders the user experience. Previous studies indicated that music can influence emotional states. Music has also been shown to reduce stress, anxiety, and discomfort in VR environments. This study aims to investigate the effects of major-minor mode music on cybersickness and sense of presence during VR experiences. Seventy-two participants aged 18 years and above were randomly assigned to no-music, major-mode, or minor-mode conditions in a controlled VR roller coaster simulation using the HTC Vive Pro. The Simulator Sickness Questionnaire (SSQ) and ITCSense of Presence Inventory (ITC-SOPI) are used to measure outcomes, and data are analyzed using one-way ANOVA (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<$</tex> 0.05) with post-hoc Tukey tests. Results indicated no significant differences in cybersickness severity within the specific context of a high-intensity VR roller coaster simulation, either among the three distinct groups or between the music and no-music conditions. However, the Major mode condition exhibited the steepest rise in symptom magnitude. However, minor-mode music significantly increased spatial presence dimension compared with no music. When the no-music and with-music groups were compared, significant differences were also found in spatial presence and ecological validity/naturalness dimensions indicating greater immersion and perceived realism of the virtual environment. These findings indicate that music, while not reducing cybersickness, can enhance user immersion through emotional modulation, providing insights for affective design in immersive VR environments.

title: IWSAR: Intelligent Website Security and Anomaly Response
authors: Vandana Shakya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Nowadays, websites on the internet face threats inclusion of SQL injection and DDoS attacks. Some of the traditional mechanisms and software that we use on our daily life are not properly able to find and detect threats. In this research paper, the IWSAR has been presented, which is a hybrid security mechanism comprised of an AI model used to detect threats. The developed security framework has a function to detect viruses and threats using AI and ML and a dashboard for monitoring and also have automated mechanisms for the response. Consisting of a React based frontend for dashboard and a Node.js backend. The result come out of experiments demonstrate that Random Forest(RF), Logistic Regression, Decision Tree(DT), and SVM have 97.10 %, 94.50 %, 93.20 %, 98.85 % accuracy respectively as well as low false positives, which makes it scalable and sufficient for anomaly detection and for blocking the security threats.

title: The Potential of Biogas from Dairy Cow Waste: Combined with Return-Sludge in Methane Optimization
authors: Wuri Margaretha Arnita, Pertiwiningrum Ambar, Wicaksono Pamungkas Aji
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: The increasing demand for renewable energy and the need to reduce greenhouse gas emissions have encouraged the exploration of biogas technology as a sustainable solution. This study investigated the biogas production performance of dairy cow manure combined with rumen fluid and return-biogas sludge (the sludge produced from the previous biogas fermentation cycle). Four treatment groups were established with varying proportions of manure and return sludge <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0 \%, 5 \%, 10 \%$</tex>, and 15%). Each treatment was fermented anaerobically for 40 days using a 20 -litre batch biodigester. The study evaluated changes in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, carbon-to-nitrogen <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{C} / \mathbf{N})$</tex> ratios, and methane <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{CH}_{4}\right)$</tex> concentration during the digestion process. Results showed that treatments with return-sludge (P2 and P3) achieved higher <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C H}_{4}$</tex> concentrations in the early fermentation phase (the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}^{\text{th }}$</tex> day), attributed to the higher availability of methanogenic microorganisms from the added sludge and rumen fluid. However, the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{4}$</tex> concentration on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0}^{\text{th }}$</tex> day was recorded in the control treatment (P0), suggesting a delayed substrate degradation process in the absence of sludge, which allowed more organic material to remain available for methanogenesis at the final stage. The highest biogas calorific value of 1227.58 kJ was observed in treatment <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P 0}$</tex> on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0}^{\text{th }}$</tex> day, demonstrating its potential for small-scale thermal applications such as water heating. The findings suggest that the return-sludge addition can accelerate the start-up process but did not increase the final methane concentration or energy yield under test conditions. Therefore, the use of return sludge should be aligned with production goals, whether prioritizing early gas yield acceleration or maximizing cumulative methane volume.

title: YOLOv11-RC: Road Crack Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv11n
authors: Siting Liang, Zhengui Huang, Chunliu Lu, Chengxi Tao, Jianpin Li, Caiguo Meng
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional pavement distress detection methods, such as low efficiency and insufficient accuracy, as well as the constraints of existing deep learning models in balancing multi-target adaptation, small target detection, multi-scale feature fusion, and computational efficiency, this paper proposes a pavement crack and pothole detection algorithm, YOLOv11-RC, based on an improved version of YOLOv11n. This model first introduces the SPPCSPC module into the neck network, enhancing the difference between cracks and the background through multi-scale pooling and cross-stage feature fusion, thereby improving multi-scale target detection capabilities. Secondly, a C3k2_DWR module is designed in the backbone network, combining regional residualization and semantic residualization to achieve efficient multi-scale feature extraction, reducing computational cost while compensating for the shortcomings of shallow and deep layer features. Experimental validation based on the RDD2022 dataset shows that YOLOv11-RC outperforms the YOLOv11n model in terms of accuracy, recall, mAP50, and mAP50-95, which are 64.82%, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50.96 \%, 55.71 {\%}$</tex>, and 28.15%, respectively. The experiment demonstrates that the YOLOv11-RC model can accurately detect cracks and potholes at different scales, effectively reducing missed detections and false detections, and providing reliable technical support for road crack detection.

title: Multi-parameter Collaborative Determination of Particle Material in Vacuum Optoelectronic Composite Trap
authors: Yifan Wang, Dun Qian, Mingquan Zeng, Wenbo Li, Peng Chen, Huizhu Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Photonics and Applied Technology (IPAT)
abstract: During long-term operation, electrical equipment accumulates nanoscale contaminants of unknown composition. These particles directly affect local electric field distribution, inducing failures such as surface flashover and partial discharge. Existing offline detection methods suffer from poor timeliness and an inability to perform in-situ measurements. This study proposes a composite trapping technique integrating optical tweezers in vacuum with a controllable electric field, enabling real-time online multiparameter analysis of randomly introduced particles. This provides a novel solution for condition monitoring of electrical equipment. When new particles are randomly trapped by the optical tweezer, rapid measurements yield parameters related to mass, particle count, radius, and refractive index. The particles are classified based on these parameters, achieving rapid characterization of random samples combined with experimental factors. Experiments dynamically trapped nanospheres of varying diameters and materials. Measurement results demonstrate that this method can accurately identify unknown particle types. The refractive index uncertainty is within 5%, the density uncertainty is within 9%, and the material resolution of 3 particles can be achieved. As a new detection method, it provides new technical innovation for intelligent photonic sensing.

title: A Unified PV–BESS Capacity Estimation Framework for Rural Microgrids: From Equatorial Roots to Global Regions
authors: Hironobu Matsuo, Yash Pandey
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: The reliable sizing of photovoltaic (PV) and battery energy storage systems (BESS) remains a central challenge for rural microgrid development, particularly in data-scarce environments where advanced optimization tools are inaccessible. Previous work introduced a simplified, simulation-light framework for PV-BESS capacity estimation based on daily demand and Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI), validated for near-equatorial regions. This paper extends the framework to twelve representative cities across diverse Köppen-Geiger climatic zones, spanning tropical, arid, temperate, and continental contexts. Using Typical Global Horizontal Irradiance Year (TGY) datasets, both cosine-based normalization <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 / \cos \theta$</tex> and half-cosine adjustment <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 /(1+\cos \theta)$</tex> are applied to mitigate latitude-induced bias, while commercial load archetypes are used to test sensitivity to variability. As a result, normalization according to latitude with half cosign adjustment significantly improved the accuracy of PV capacity estimation and allowed the new formula to be extended to a latitude range of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}}$</tex>. Similarly, the BESS capacity can be more accurately estimated using the new formula, which employs cosine normalization. The study establishes a generalized, zone-inclusive extension of the equatorial framework, offering a practical pre-feasibility tool for microgrid planners and decisionmakers in rural electrification initiatives in a wide range of areas.

title: Worker Health Tracker for Construction Sites: Heart Rate and Heat Exhaustion Prediction Leveraging AI Using Smart Bands
authors: Kamalesh Chinnadurai, Praveen Prasad, Alex David S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: The construction industry is one of the most challenging industries where employees face many difficulties such as changing weather, dust and heat. Due to this they frequently face health issues, illness, injuries or in the worst case fatalities. Traditional safety measures are not good enough to monitor these in real time and provide predictions or alerts beforehand. The WHT represents a solution implemented through a worker health tracker system (WHT) in construction sites. The system is centered on a continuous monitoring process of the most vital health indicators—primarily heart rate and body temperature—with the aim of locating the first symptoms of heat exhaustion and stopping it at the stage that is still mild. As a result, the system, which is powered by Artificial Intelligence (AI) and consists of wearable smart bands which are connected through the Internet of Things (IoT), intends to enhance worker safety, health, and thus, overall productivity. The WHT is a potential source of joy for both the workers and the management as it provides personal health feedback and facilitates the preemptive action of sending automatic alerts for early intervention.

title: Tuberculosis Detection from Chest X-Ray Images Using CNNs and Ant Colony Optimization
authors: P K Devika, Praveen Kumar Tyagi, Ajay Sharma, Mohit Kumar Saw, Shreya Mallick, Harsh Garg, Devika Nair Jayakumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Tuberculosis (TB) remains a leading global health challenge, especially in resource-constrained regions due to limited access to expert radiology. To address this diagnostic gap, this study introduces a deep learning-based framework for automated TB detection using chest X-rays. The proposed model makes use of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to find and sort features that are important for the detection of TB wherein Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) is used as a metaheuristic method to improve hyperparameters. This approach enables the system to overcome local minima, a common limitation in traditional gradient-based algorithms by simulating the foraging behavior of ants, the ACO efficiently explores the search space for optimal learning rate, batch size, and network architecture. The pipeline achieves an accuracy of around 98% through extensive preprocessing steps-including adaptive histogram equalization, normalization, and data augmentation, CNN training, and careful evaluation to improve model generalization and address class imbalance on publicly available TB chest X-ray datasets from Kaggle, significantly outperforming traditional optimization methods. Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) are used to ensure transparency and build trust by letting the doctors view the most important areas that affect TB predictions, which boosts their confidence in the diagnosis. This research shows that scalable, explainable AI-powered TB screening is possible.

title: Graph-Based Multi-Task Learning Constrained Multi-Objective Evolutionary Algorithm
authors: Lei Fan, Junhua Liu, Qinghong Jin, Wei Zhang
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: To address the issues of insufficient solution diversity and weak adaptability in complex constrained optimization problems, this paper proposes an innovative multitask optimization algorithm, GMTL-CMOEA. The algorithm deeply integrates graph neural networks with a multi-task learning framework. By constructing a GNN, the algorithm can dynamically learn and automatically update key information such as population state configurations; subs-equently, using the MTL framework, it achieves parallel processing and knowledge transfer for both constrained and unconstrained tasks. Ultimately, this method can efficiently generate a solution set that is diverse, highly feasible, and broadly applicable. Experimental results show that GMTL-CMOEA demonstrates excellent efficiency and flexibility across multiple complex problem scenarios, providing more valuable decision-making solutions.

title: Retrieval-Verified Trust Envelopes: Hallucination-Safe Brain Tumor Classification with Frozen Large Language Models
authors: Phat Kim Huynh, Phi Huynh, Duy Nguyen, Jacques Kpodonu, Jose Azucena, Joao Paulo Jacomini Prioli, Hasti Namazi, Anthony Apatika, Minh Huu Nhat Le
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Deep learning pipelines for brain tumor magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) have achieved high accuracy, yet most remain opaque “black boxes” that can hallucinate unsupported findings, undermining clinical trust. We develop a retrievalverified trust envelope that converts frozen large-language models (LLMs)-ChatGPT o3 and Gemini 2.5 Pro-into interpretable classifiers of glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and notumor scans. An image-conditioned prompt first produces classprobability estimates. Next, three chained prompts then supply a 50 -token radiologist-style rationale, a 20 -token contrastive cue that clarifies the decision boundary, and a single PubMed identifier that corroborates the cited imaging feature. Experiments used the 7023-image Brain Tumor MRI dataset, with 120 images randomly sampled (30 per class) for evaluation. ChatGPT o3 achieved 85.0% accuracy with macro precision, recall, and F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> of 86.0%, 85.2%, and 84.9%, respectively, whereas Gemini reached 71.1% accuracy with corresponding macro scores of 81.6%, 79.7%, and 80.6%. Micro-average area under the curve (AUC) was 0.93 for 03 and 0.90 for Gemini. Reliability diagrams yielded expected calibration errors of 0.036 and 0.141, confirming superior calibration for o3. Applying a dual uncertainty threshold (maximum class confidence below 0.80 or instance-level calibration error above 0.20) deferred 31.7% of o3 outputs and 41.3% of Gemini outputs to human review, capturing all residual errors. The prompt-driven envelope thus delivers hallucinationsafe, evidence-linked explanations while leaving the underlying LLM weights untouched, advancing transparent decision support for neuro-oncologic MRI.

title: Benchmarking Data-Driven Imputation Methods for IMU Sensors: A Comprehensive Evaluation Across Scenarios and Parameters
authors: Qixiao Zhou, Xuyin Zhu, Yihang Chen, Zhe Wang, Hongcheng Qiu, Fanghao Zhou, Tiefeng Li
abstract: Raw Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) data is fundamental to navigation and monitoring applications but is highly susceptible to data loss, severely compromising system reliability. While numerous imputation algorithms exist, their performance specifically on high-frequency, multi-axis IMU signals remains underexplored. This paper presents a systematic empirical study evaluating 12 representative data-driven imputation methods, across six diverse public datasets. Unlike generic evaluations, we introduce a benchmarking evaluation framework tailored to IMU dynamics. We quantitatively assess the impact of key sensor characteristics: variable sampling frequencies (1Hz–100Hz), heterogeneous physical channels, and complex temporal loss patterns. Key empirical findings include: (1) MissForest (MF) and Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations (MICE) demonstrate superior accuracy and stability in resource-constrained scenarios; (2) Human activity periodicity mitigates the impact of continuous row data loss; (3) While Deep Learning models show potential at high frequencies (>60 Hz), they struggle to generalize under limited-data conditions without extensive tuning; (4) Magnetometer data proves significantly harder to recover than kinematic features due to weak inter-axis correlations. These findings provide evidence-based guidelines for algorithm selection, offering a roadmap for deploying robust IMU systems in failure-prone environments.

title: Reconstructing Semantic Mobility Timelines with Semi-Live Incremental Processing: Balancing Responsiveness and Fidelity in Mobile Telemetry Systems
authors: Ioannis Tsouros, Tsirimpa Athena
abstract: Mobile telemetry enables fine-grained observation of human mobility, yet transforming raw sensor data into semantic mobility timelines remains architecturally polarized between high-latency batch processing and low-latency but context-limited real-time inference. This paper evaluates Pythia, a mobility reconstruction pipeline designed to bridge this gap through a semi-live incremental processing architecture that executes semantic inference intermittently using adaptive, event-driven triggers and limited temporal context. Using high-resolution GNSS and inertial traces collected via a mobile travel-logging application, we conduct a controlled comparison between monolithic end-of-day batch reconstruction and semi-live execution across 215 real-world user-day instances. Results show that semi-live processing closely approximates batch-level reconstruction quality, achieving transport segment recall consistently above 90%, mean Intersection-over-Union (IoU) values between 0.89 and 0.94, and fragmentation rates below 5%, while reducing semantic latency from end-of-day delays to 15–30 minutes. We further demonstrate that reconstruction quality is governed primarily by trigger design and data sufficiency, rather than execution frequency alone, and identify an accuracy-aware trigger range (150–250 high-quality points) that balances semantic stability with user-facing responsiveness. These findings provide practical design guidance for energy-efficient, context-aware mobility systems that require timely semantic feedback without sacrificing reconstruction fidelity.

title: Short-Circuit Fault Ride-Through Method for Dual-Active-Bridge Converters Considering DC-Voltage Inertia Support
authors: Heyang Sun, Chuang Liu, Dehao Kong
abstract: The DC distribution network has the characteristics of “low inertia” and “low damping,” among which pole to pole short circuit fault is the most serious type of fault in the DC distribution system. When a short-circuit fault occurs, the port voltage of the dual active bridge (DAB) converter drops sharply. However, there is a risk that the DC circuit breaker (DCCB) may not be able to operate in a timely manner, which can lead to overcurrent locking inside the DAB converter and trigger emergency power outages in the distribution substation area. Therefore, this paper proposes a fault ride-through (FRT) method with voltage inertia support capability. By incorporating a DC voltage inertia support (DC-VIS) circuit at the output port of the DAB converter, the rate of port voltage decline during short-circuit fault conditions is effectively slowed through designing support capacitance. This provides additional time for system identification and DCCB operation while eliminating the risk of overcurrent inside the DAB converter. Under normal conditions with the support capacitor fully charged, the proposed structure does not affect the dynamic response capability of the DAB converter. Furthermore, based on the DC-VIS circuit, an adaptive dynamic limiting strategy is designed to maximize the transmission power of the DAB converter while ensuring coordinated control among multiple modules. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through simulations and experiments.

title: Robust Filtering and Learning in State-Space Models: Skewness and Heavy Tails Via Asymmetric Laplace Distribution_supp1-3672131.pdf
authors: Shengjie Xiu
abstract: State-space models are pivotal for dynamic system analysis but often struggle with outlier data that deviates from Gaussian distributions, frequently exhibiting skewness and heavy tails. This paper introduces a robust extension utilizing the asymmetric Laplace distribution, specifically tailored to capture these complex characteristics. We propose an efficient variational Bayes algorithm and a novel parameter estimation strategy, significantly enhancing the efficiency of the filtering, smoothing, and parameter estimation processes. Our comprehensive experiments demonstrate that our methods provide consistently robust performance across various noise settings without the need for manual hyperparameter adjustments. In stark contrast, existing models generally rely on specific noise conditions and necessitate extensive manual tuning. Moreover, our approach uses far fewer computational resources, thereby validating the model’s effectiveness and underscoring its potential for practical applications in fields such as robust control and financial modeling.

title: Multi-Antenna Beamforming for Integrated Sensing and Covert Communication in AF Relaying Networks
authors: Shihao Wang, Jiaqi Zhen
abstract: This paper investigates multi-antenna beamforming for integrated sensing and covert communication (ISCC) in consumer-electronics-assisted relaying networks. Specifically, we consider an amplify-and-forward (AF) relay network where a dual-functional multi-antenna transmitter Alice and one multi-antenna AF relay jointly serve a consumer electronics receiver Bob, in the presence of one warden Willie, while simultaneously conducting environmental sensing. Recognizing the hardware constraints and dense deployment characteristics of consumer electronics, we exploit the high spatial degrees of freedom offered by multi-antenna arrays. The proposed system steers beams to deal with the interference at the warden while maintaining high-gain links for legitimate users, thereby turning the dense multipath environment into an advantage for covertness. The objective is to maximize Bob’s signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) under stringent covertness and power constraints while maintaining a minimum sensing quality. To this end, we jointly optimize Alice’s communication/sensing beamforming and the relay amplification factor through a fractional programming framework. The covertness constraints are reformulated into tractable power-domain inequalities by employing total variation distance, KL divergence, and S-procedure-based convex relaxations, where three eavesdropper channel state information (ECSI) of instantaneous, bounded-error, and statistical scenarios are considered. These scenarios are carefully selected to cover the full spectrum of warden capabilities, from active detection to passive surveillance. The resulting non-convex problem is solved via semidefinite relaxation combined with Dinkelbach’s method and alternating optimization, complemented by relay antenna selection to enhance consumer-device link reliability. Simulation results are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. In particular, for a 30 dBm transmit power, the proposed scheme with instantaneous ECSI achieves approximately 14 dB SINR at the consumer receiver, compared to 5.5 dB and 3.5 dB under bounded-error and statistical ECSI, respectively, demonstrating the proposed scheme’s robustness and effectiveness in covert, consumer-electronics-integrated wireless networks.

title: A One-Step-Adjusting MPPT With 0.5-Cycle FOCV Sampling and DCB Monitoring for Self-Powered PEHs
authors: Yu Du, Xufeng Liao, Haiqin Wu, Xincai Liu, Xiudeng Wang, Yukai Zhang, Zhangming Zhu, Lianxi Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents a rapid-response and self-powered piezoelectric energy harvesting (PEH) system. To address the trade-off between tracking time and accuracy in traditional maximum power point tracking (MPPT) methods, a one-step-adjusting (OSA) MPPT technique is proposed. Based on the relationship between the open-circuit voltage and optimal impedance, the proposed MPPT can directly adjust the equivalent input resistance of the DC-DC converter by controlling the power switch conduction time and complete tracking within one MPPT cycle. Moreover, a 0.5-cycle Fractional Open-Circuit Voltage (FOCV) sampling algorithm with the duty-cycle-based (DCB) monitoring method is proposed to reduce losses caused by tracking latency and open-circuit sampling duration. The proposed PEH system has been implemented in a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.18\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m standard CMOS process, with a core area of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.47\times 0.64$</tex-math> </inline-formula> mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed PEH system achieves a peak-tracking efficiency of 99.99% and a peak End-to-end efficiency of 87.9% over one MPPT cycle.

title: Communication-Efficient Stochastic Distributed Learning
authors: Xiaoxing Ren, Nicola Bastianello, Karl H. Johansson, Thomas Parisini
abstract: We address distributed learning problems, both nonconvex and convex, over undirected networks. In particular, we design a novel algorithm based on the distributed Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) to tackle the challenges of high communication costs, and large datasets. Our design deals with these challenges i) by enabling the agents to perform multiple local training steps between each round of communications; and ii) by allowing the agents to employ stochastic gradients while carrying out local computations. We show that the proposed algorithm converges to a neighborhood of a stationary point, for nonconvex problems, and of an optimal point, for convex problems.We also propose a variant of the algorithm to incorporate variance reduction thus achieving exact convergence. We show that the resulting algorithm indeed converges to a stationary (or optimal) point, and moreover that local training accelerates convergence. We thoroughly compare the proposed algorithms with the state of the art, both theoretically and through numerical results.

title: Throughput-Aware Two-Stage User Grouping and Scheduling Based on Genetic Algorithm for NOMA in Industrial Wireless Networks
authors: Tabinda Ashraf, Sean McSweeny, Álvaro A. M. de Medeiros, Victor Cionca
abstract: Ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) has become an integral part of modern industrial wireless networks (IWNs) with rapidly growing number of interconnected sensors. Owing to its notable benefits, nonorthogonal multiple access (NOMA) has become a great choice for IWNs. To enhance resource utilization, user grouping in NOMA systems has been investigated in recent years, focusing on power allocation and sum-rate optimization. This article goes beyond state-of-the-art and proposes a novel user grouping and scheduling algorithm for a hybrid NOMA–OFDMA system tailored to URLLC in IWNs by considering the effective throughput of individual users as a function of bit error rate. First, we formulate an optimization problem to maximize the sum rate while ensuring that the channel gains of the grouped users are sufficiently different and users with similar throughput requirements are grouped together. We propose novel usage of one-stage and two-stage genetic algorithm to find the optimal solution to the user grouping problem in uplink IWN. Scheduling users to physical resource blocks is solved as a bin packing problem. The proposed algorithm is rigorously evaluated based on effective throughput and user fairness. Our findings conclusively demonstrate that the framework not only satisfies the individual throughput demands of each user but also significantly reduces latency compared to the existing order-based grouping and OFDMA. In addition, a notable outcome of the proposed approach is a marked improvement in user fairness, ensuring a more balanced and equitable distribution of network resources among users, which is a critical aspect of enhancing overall network performance.
