title: Hybrid Force-Velocity Model Predictive Control Framework for Coordinated Manipulation of Humanoid Dual-Arm Robots
authors: Bing Han, Wang Jin, Xiaolong Yu, Haiyun Zhang, Xiaofei Li, Jianwei Niu, Guodong Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This article presents a model predictive control (MPC) method for humanoid dual-arm robots (DARs) to realize hybrid force-velocity control in the task space. The proposed method, named hybrid force-velocity MPC (HFV-MPC), enables simultaneous tracking of velocity and external force in the task space, as well as internal force regulation. First, a synchronous decoupling model of internal force, external force, and velocity is developed to address the limitations of conventional methods that decouple internal and external forces solely in the end-effector space and fail to meet the independent regulation requirements of external force and velocity in the task space. Furthermore, a generalized velocity model that integrates task-space velocity, external force, and internal force was established, and a HFV-MPC framework is constructed to enhance system robustness and achieve precise dual-arm force–velocity tracking. Subsequently, a comprehensive MPC cost function is designed, incorporating a dual-arm motion coordination coefficient to suppress undesired redundant joint motions and improve whole-body motion stability. In addition, a feedforward-linearized incremental system is derived, where the incremental model and force prediction jointly construct a generalized state-space model for MPC, to balance modeling accuracy and computational efficiency. Finally, the proposed method is validated in terms of effectiveness and robustness through both simulation and physical robotic experiments.

title: Real-Time Supply Chain Visibility via Integrated IoT and AI
authors: Wael A. Farag
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: The increasing complexity and volatility of global supply chains necessitate a shift from traditional, reactive management to proactive, data-driven strategies. This paper examines how the convergence of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing supply chain management by delivering unprecedented real-time visibility. We explore the current status of core enabling technologies, from foundational IoT sensors, tracking solutions, and edge computing architectures to the integration of AI capabilities such as machine learning for prediction, anomaly detection, and digital twins for simulation. Drawing on pioneering implementations across pharmaceutical, automotive, retail, and manufacturing sectors with companies like Walmart, Pfizer, and Siemens, we distill critical lessons learned. Our findings indicate that while technical challenges persist, organizational factors-such as cross-functional governance and partner ecosystem management-often emerge as the most critical determinants of implementation success. Finally, the paper concludes by discussing a horizon of emerging trends poised to shape the future of logistics, including explainable AI (XAI), blockchain integration for trust, federated learning, 5G connectivity, and the rise of fully autonomous supply chain systems.

title: Fault Diagnosis for Aero-Engine Rolling Bearings Fusing an Improved Sparrow Search Algorithm with Deep Learning
authors: Hanqi Jiang, Yundong Sha, Wenhao Zhang, Chengjing Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: To address the challenge of diagnosing aero-engine rolling bearing faults under complex conditions by high noise and variable loads, this paper proposes a fault diagnosis method that integrates an Improved Sparrow Search Algorithm (ISSA) with deep learning. Minimum Envelope Entropy is adopted as the fitness function. The Improved Sparrow Search Algorithm (OSSA) is employed to optimize the layer number and penalty factor of Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD), obtaining the optimal influencing parameters and the IMF index value corresponding to the minimum fitness. Subsequently, the optimized VMD algorithm is utilized to decompose the fault signal, yielding the optimal IMF for each sample, from which nine time-domain indicators across ten states are calculated and selected. The dataset is appropriately divided into training and testing sets. A combined model of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network is then applied to train the rolling bearing fault dataset. The model performs classification and discrimination on the test set, outputting the diagnostic accuracy. The proposed method is verified by using the typical bearing fault data and the aero-engine intershaft bearing test data. The results show that the method still has excellent diagnostic performance under high noise and variable load complex conditions. The diagnostic accuracy of the two test data is 97.3 % and 98.75 %, respectively. This approach not only enhances antinoise interference capability but also improves the adaptability and robustness of the diagnostic system, providing an effective solution for fault early warning and health management of aeroengine rolling bearings.

title: Designing A Three-Layer Pedagogical Architecture for Integrating Digital Twin and Machine Learning in Engineering Education
authors: Meisam Gordan, David Ayala Cabrera
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Digital Twin is a relatively new technology in education and there is a limited understanding of how it can effectively be used in teaching engineering. Data-driven modelling is now central to civil and infrastructure engineering, yet most early-stage programming modules still stop at generic exercises. Focusing on pedagogical design rationale and theoretical validation, this paper presents a methodological framework for integrating a Digital TwinMachine Learning (DT-ML) block into a second-year module, i.e., Computer Applications in Civil Engineering at University College Dublin. The redesign has two main contributions, i.e., a three-layer DT-ML pedagogical architecture is proposed, and design principles are derived. These principles connect constructive alignment and experiential, inquiry-based learning to the teaching of DT and ML content. In other words, the redesign aims to strengthen the relevance, coherence and pedagogical depth of the mentioned module as well as examining how DT concepts and introductory ML can be meaningfully incorporated into the module for civil engineering students.

title: Hybrid Evolutionary Algorithm for Coordinated Power Flow Control in Microgrids
authors: Anand Kumar, Srikant Prasad, Shravan Kumar Yadav, Aditya Kumar Pankaj, Alok Priyadarshi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Efficient power flow (PF) management plays a vital role in microgrids (MGs) minimizing operational costs, maximize renewable energy (RE) utilization, and ensure stable system performance. This study presents a hybrid optimization technique that integrates the Pelican Optimization Algorithm (POA) and the Wildebeest Herd Optimization Algorithm (WHO), forming a combined model known as the Pelican-WHO (PWHO), for effective PF management in microgrids with hybrid renewable energy sources (HRES). The proposed method controls voltage source inverter (VSI) signals while accounting for changes in active power (AP) and reactive power (RP). By making a multi-objective function, it efficiently sort out power exchange imbalances among energy sources and loads. The developed strategy improves the tuning of power controller parameters, ensures a uniform energy supply, reduces reliance on the main grid, and supports seamless switching between grid-connected and islanded operating modes. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results validate the efficiency of the suggested methodology. Strong economic and environmental benefits are demonstrated by the PWHO approach, which reduces operating expenses by $41.31 \%$ and air pollutants by $21 \%$. It also exhibits better optimization performance compared to POA, PSO, AQO, and SOA algorithms. Moreover, PWHO attains a maximum efficiency of $96.1 \%$, substantially enhancing power flow (PF) management in microgrids (MGs) and beating traditional optimization techniques.

title: WISE: A Variance-Adaptive, Segment-Aware Throughput Mixer for Client-Side HTTP Adaptive Streaming
authors: Shuaibu Yau, Suphakit Awiphan, Jakramate Bootkrajang
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Accurate throughput estimation and control are critical for HAS over volatile mobile links. We present WISE, a client-side ABR that adapts to measured variability. WISE introduces the Weighted Harmonic-Exponential Mean (WHEM), which blends an EWMA with a weighted harmonic mean using a coefficient of variation gate: It leans toward a fast EWMA when links are calm and toward conservative WHM as burstiness grows. A variance-scaled buffer cushion further sizes the guard band to real-time dispersion and segment size, while a closedform QoE score balances bitrate, smoothness, and stall risk. All computations are constant-time and do not require offline training. On 5G traces, WISE improves mean quality by up to 46.1% and reduces switching magnitude by up to 53.3% versus state-of-the-art baselines, with competitive stall rates, yielding robust gains under both stable and highly volatile conditions.

title: QoS-Driven Trust-Aware Fog Node Selection Using Fuzzy-Based Framework
authors: Bhupendra Panchal, Preeti Saxena
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Fog computing, an extension of cloud computing, brings cloud services closer to edge devices by utilizing distributed fog nodes for processing. In this dynamic environment, selecting reliable fog nodes is critical because trust is inherently variable and influenced by continuously changing Quality of Service (QoS) requirements. Traditional approaches struggle to manage the uncertainty, ambiguity, and continuous information needed for accurate trust assessment. This study proposes a Fuzzy-Based Fog Node Selection framework for evaluating fog node trustworthiness. The framework employs a fuzzy inference system with Gaussian membership functions to compute a trust score for each node and classifies nodes as either trustworthy or untrustworthy. The proposed fuzzy method is evaluated and compared with existing fog node selection techniques, and experimental results demonstrate its effectiveness in accurately computing fog node trustworthiness.

title: Design of a Dual-band Monopole Antenna for RFID Applications
authors: M Vinodh Kumar, Tanmaya Kumar Das, Biswajit Dwivedy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The article illustrates the design of a linearly polarized dual-band monopole structure. The design features a square-shaped radiating element, and a microstrip line feeds it, with a defected ground element. The outcomes in the simulation environment depict- $\mathbf{1 0 ~ d B}$ bandwidths of $\mathbf{2 . 1 9 - 4 . 7 7 ~ G H z}$ and $5.27-6.18 \mathrm{GHz}$ covering 2.4 and 5.8 GHz ISM bands. The simulated results indicate peak gain values of 3.52 dBi at 2.4 $\mathbf{G H z}$ and $\mathbf{4 . 8 3 ~ d B i}$ at $\mathbf{5 . 8 ~ G H z}$ with omnidirectional pattern over the operating bands. The stable gain and read range of 5.94 cm, 2.86 cm at the $2.4,5.8 \mathrm{GHz}$ bands respectively make the design useful for RFID reader applications.

title: Optimization of Aircrafts Taxiing Path Based on Improved A* Algorithm
authors: Longhai Li, Chenqi Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low efficiency and frequent conflicts in aircraft taxiing path planning for large airport surface operations, this study proposes an improved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm. The approach reconstructs the evaluation function by introducing direction factor, distance factor, and turn cost function to more accurately reflect actual taxiing constraints. A conflict resolution strategy combining priority-based waiting design with path replanning is developed. Simulation analysis using Tianjin Binhai International Airport as a case study demonstrates that compared with the traditional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithm, the improved version achieves 12.8 % reduction in total taxiing time and 25.4 % decrease in number of turns compared to the traditional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{*}$</tex> algorithms respectively. Comparative studies with Dijkstra and Ant Colony Algorithms further verify the significant advantages of the improved A* algorithm in taxiing efficiency optimization, turn minimization, and conflict resolution capabilities. The results highlight the algorithm's enhanced performance in addressing practical constraints of surface movement operations at major airfields.

title: A Patch-Based Transformer Framework for High-Accuracy and Efficient Short-Term Load Forecasting
authors: Mohit Choubey, Rahul Kumar Chaurasiya, J.S. Yadav, Rahul Prasad, Aman Sharma, Aman Dubey
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Reliable short-term load forecasting (STLF) is essential for modern power system stability and efficiency. The lightweight transformer-based deep learning model Patch-TST improves forecasting accuracy while reducing computational complexity. The model captures nonlinear load fluctuations by dividing input time series into sequential patches and using self-attention to learn local and global temporal interdependence. Patch-TST surpasses LSTM, GRU, ARIMA, and SARIMA on real-world load datasets with the lowest MAE ($\mathbf{5 2 1. 8 4}$ MW), RMSE ($\mathbf{5 5 6. 0 6 ~ M W}$), and MAPE ($\mathbf{8. 5 1 \%}$). The suggested model converges faster, has reduced prediction variance, and is more resilient to load fluctuations. Patch-TST is a practical and effective short-term load forecasting solution for intelligent energy management and smart grid systems due to its simplicity, scalability, and predictive power.

title: Development of IMU in Post-Stroke Rehabilitation Assessment
authors: Trung T. Nguyen, Thanh N. Nguyen, Quang D. V. Chu, Chien Q. Phan, Doanh D. Dao, Tuan M. Dao, Truong Q. Hoang, Truong D. Nguyen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Stroke is not only one of the leading causes of fatality and disability but also poses significant health issues due to the risk of recurrence. Rehabilitation plays a critical role in improving motor function and quality of life for people suffering from stroke, and gait assessment is a major component in evaluating post-stroke recovery, as lower-limb damages often lead to reduced mobility. However, traditional motion capture systems are far more expensive and require specialized laboratory setups. This study proposes a costeffective, portable system to assist gait recovery in stroke patients. The proposed system includes Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) sensors to capture lower-limb motion. The collected data is then analyzed for assessing motor recovery in stroke patients. Preexisting patient data is also incorporated along with the measured figures to enhance the accuracy of the system. Experimental validation demonstrated a high level of synchronization with the data that can be retrieved by the VICON motion capture system, a gold standard in motion analysis, showing strong equivalence in joint angle measurement and gait parameters. These findings indicate that the proposed setup can reliably estimate recovery progress in stroke patients with a much lower cost and higher portability. The study confirms that a combination of IMU can serve as a practical tool for evaluating lower-limb functional recovery in post-stroke patients. The system's affordability, ease of use, and portability make it suitable for both clinical and home-based rehabilitation. Future work may focus on integrating machine learning for automated recovery prediction and expanding the system to assess upper-limb function.

title: Low-Power 4-Bit Array Multiplier with Hybrid PTLCMOS Logic
authors: Richa Gupta, Bhavana Prakash Shrivastava, Laxmi Pradhan Kumre
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper presents a 4-bit array multiplier designed primarily using Pass Transistor Logic (PTL) to achieve significant reductions in power consumption and delay. The architecture takes advantage of PTL’s advantage of minimizing transistor count, resulting in a compact and energy-efficient design. Key logic components such as the XOR gate are implemented using PTL to reduce transistor overhead, while AND gates are realized using conventional CMOS logic to effectively address the voltage degradation issues experienced in PTL circuits. This hybrid approach ensures full voltage swing and robust signal integrity throughout the multiplier. Implemented on a $\mathbf{4 5}-\mathbf{n m}$ technology node and verified through Cadence Xcelium simulations, the proposed design demonstrates considerable improvements in power efficiency and area savings compared to conventional full CMOS multipliers, making it highly suitable for low-power, high- density VLSI applications.

title: Application of Optical Flow Sensors for Smooth Indoor UAV Flight
authors: Shibiao Wei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: This paper focuses on the application of optical flow sensors for smooth indoor UAV flight. By analyzing their operating mechanism and integrating them with the UAV kinematic model to establish a mapping relationship between optical flow information and pose parameters, an application model is constructed, consisting of three core modules: data acquisition, positioning and velocity measurement, and flight control. Simulation experiments were conducted on the ROS Gazebo platform under varying light intensities (50–1200 lux), ground texture complexity, and scene conditions. Results show that in a basic scene with 300 lux illumination and four-level texture, the root mean square error (RMSE) for X- and Y-axis positioning is minimal, at 0.06m and 0.05m, respectively. Compared to IMU positioning alone, the model improves positioning accuracy by 60%− 75%. The mean velocity measurement deviation is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 0.035 \mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$</tex>, and the hovering displacement fluctuation range is ± 0.08m. These results demonstrate the model's effectiveness in improving the reliability of smooth indoor UAV flight and provide support for practical applications.

title: Satellite Image Classification Using Xception: A Deep Learning Approach for High-Accuracy Land Cover Mapping
authors: Gaurav Tuteja, Bura Vijay Kumar, Neha Sharma, Garima Jaitly, Preeti Badhani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The increasing volume and complexity of satellite imagery necessitate the development of efficient and accurate classification techniques to support environmental monitoring, urban planning, and disaster management. Addressing the challenge of accurate land cover classification from high-resolution satellite images, this research proposes a deep learning framework utilizing the Xception architecture. The methodology involves systematic data preprocessing, including image resizing, data augmentation, and normalization, followed by feature extraction using depth wise separable convolutions. The pretrained Xception model is adapted with a customized classification head comprising a Global Average Pooling layer, dense layers, dropout regularization, and a final softmax activation for multi-class classification. Experimental evaluations conducted on a diverse satellite image dataset demonstrate the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed approach. The model achieved an overall classification accuracy of 98.76%, with particularly high precision, recall, and F1-scores across the “cloudy,” “desert,” “green area,” and “water” classes. Confusion matrix analysis highlighted minimal misclassification, primarily between spectrally similar classes, while the training and validation curves confirmed stable learning and generalization. The proposed framework significantly contributes to advancing satellite image classification by providing a scalable, efficient, and highly accurate solution. This research offers valuable insights into leveraging lightweight deep learning architectures for remote sensing applications and lays the foundation for future enhancements through dataset diversification, incorporation of spectral bands, and real-time deployment optimizations.

title: A Resilient Peer-to-Peer Validation Framework for Reliable Digital Coupon Utilization
authors: Kushika Agarwal, Rishabh Tiwari, Varun Shukla, Priya Pal, Abhay Soni, Mehul Kumar Das
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Considering the evolution of the technology itself, users of coupon codes for online transactions now receive them directly. Unfortunately, many expire unused overlooked, irrelevant, or otherwise resulting in lost promotional value for the provider and lost value for the coupon users. This research focuses on the Attempted Exchange System proposing a form of copyright expunged anonymity of digital coupons. Users mesh net redeem off and mesh trust lose in submission of identified counterfeit coupons. It is built on a MVP of the MERN stack, and is designed for large scale real time intercommunications with flexible security, proprietary multi-trust, and is flexible mesh scalable. These include assigned verifying, secured uploaded coupon images, and reputation interfaces for moderated contributions and net redeem value. Efficient coupon utilization is assured by the feedback users receive on redeem option feedback and redeem choice utilization.

title: Deep-DoPIo: Deep Learning for Shear Elasticity Prediction in Double-Profile Intersection Ultrasound
authors: Sabiq Muhtadi, Caterina M. Gallippi
abstract: We herein propose Deep-DoPIo, a deep learning–based framework that predicts shear elastic modulus from paired narrow- and wide-track displacement profiles generated by a single acoustic radiation force (ARF) push. Unlike the traditional Double Profile Intersection (DoPIo) ultrasound technique, which relates the intersection time (tint) between these profiles to shear modulus through a linear empirical model, Deep-DoPIo leverages neural networks to learn this mapping directly from the displacement data. Four architectures were evaluated: a feed-forward neural network (FFNN), a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (CNN), an encoder–transformer (E-Tr), and a one-dimensional vision transformer (ViT). Models were trained on simulated displacement data and fine-tuned on calibrated phantom measurements for domain adaptation. Across simulated materials, traditional DoPIo yielded increasing mean absolute errors (MAE) with increasing material stiffness, with MAE exceeding 13 kPa for 28 kPa material. Conversely, all examined DNN models maintained MAEs below 0.5 kPa regardless of material stiffness. In calibrated phantoms, FFNN and CNN produced similar error trends to traditional DoPIo (MAE ≈ 4-8 kPa in a 22.20 kPa medium), indicating limited benefit over the empirical approach. By contrast, transformer-based models (E-Tr and ViT) consistently yielded low errors (<0.2 kPa) across all media of varying stiffness. In a custom liver phantom with a gelatin–graphite inclusion, traditional DoPIo substantially underestimated elasticity relative to shear wave elasticity imaging (SWEI), with errors approaching 50%. In contrast, transformer-based elasticity predictions closely matched those of SWEI, differing by less than 4% in the inclusion and less than 9% in the background. These results demonstrate that transformer-based architectures can generalize from simulation to both calibrated and excised tissue phantoms, providing accurate shear modulus estimates under conditions where traditional DoPIo struggles. Future work will evaluate performance in vivo and systematically compare Deep-DoPIo against SWEI under clinically relevant conditions.

title: A Non-Isolated High Step-Down DC-DC Converter with Zero Voltage Switching
authors: Tsorng-Juu Liang, Doan Thoi Pham, Kim Kien Nghiep Huynh, Kai-Hui Chen, Thai Anh Au Tran
abstract: In this paper, a novel non-isolated high step-down DC-DC converter is proposed to supply power for microcontrollers, CPUs, and integrated circuits applications. The proposed high step-down topology comprises a capacitor, two series-connected coupled inductors with opposite polarity, two main switches operating complementarily, and two synchronous rectifier switches. The main switches can be turned on with zero voltage switching, and their voltage stress is clamped to the input voltage. Furthermore, the energy stored in the leakage inductance can be recycled back to the input. To verify the feasibility of the proposed converter, a 66 W experimental prototype using planar transformers is implemented with the input voltage of 48 V, the output voltage of 3.3 V, and the switching frequency of 100 kHz. Synchronous rectifier switches are employed to reduce conduction losses and enhance the overall efficiency of the converter. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed converter achieves a peak efficiency of 97.2% at 40% load and a full load efficiency of 93.8%.

title: Interpretable Machine Learning for Protein Science: Structure, Function, and Interactions
authors: Luiz Felipe Vecchietti, Minji Lee, Begench Hangeldiyev, Bryan Nathanael Wijaya, Hyunkyu Jung, Hahnbeom Park, Tae-Kyun Kim, Meeyoung Cha, Ho Min Kim

title: Compact Super Wideband Octagonal Shaped Patch Antenna for Wireless Applications
authors: Pallak Prusty, Budhadeb Maity
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: In this article, a compact super-wideband octagonal-shaped patch (OSP) antenna is presented. The proposed antenna consists of an octagonal radiating patch integrated with an optimized L-shaped slit and a modified ground plane, which together enhance the impedance bandwidth (IBW) and improve impedance matching. A simulated IBW from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.161-26.849 \text{GHz}(170.2 \%)$</tex> is achieved. Furthermore, the antenna attains a peak gain of 9.14 dBi at 25.41 GHz and exhibits nearly stable radiation characteristics across the entire operating band. Owing to its compact size, simple configuration, and wideband performance, the proposed design is a promising candidate for modern wireless applications.

title: A Hybrid CRNN and NLP-Based PostCorrection Framework for Optical Character Recognition
authors: Aadit Mali, Ninad Sathe, Ebrahim Bootwala, Atharva Khodkumbhe, Hanmant Magar, Hanmant Magar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) remains an essential technology in digitization, data automation, and document analysis, yet it is prone to errors when dealing with degraded, handwritten, or noisy text inputs. To overcome these shortcomings, this work introduces a hybrid OCR framework that combines a Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network (CRNN) and a lightweight post-correction subsystem for smoothing linguistic and structural discrepancies in recognized text. With CRNN architecture adopting the convolutional layers for spatial extraction and recurrent layers for sequence modeling, it optimizes recognition for variablelength text data. For vocabulary modeling, 20 transcripts’ dataset was compiled to cover 162 distinct tokens for supporting contextual correction. Performance of the proposed model was assessed using Word Error Rate (WER) and Character Error Rate (CER) metrics. Experimental results indicate significant accuracy gains following post-correction, with WER for CRNN improved from 0.1560 to 0.1453 and $C E R$ from 0.0425 to 0.0394. Comparative analysis conducted with TrOCR produced comparable gains, affirming the validity of the correction approach. The post-correction subsytem successfully handled the numerous lexical and grammatical irregularities that are typical of OCR output, especially with regard to replacements, contractions, and partial word truncations, according to token-by-token analysis of perplexity. In-depth tracking of learning curves, visual inspection of error distributions, and a word frequency analysis validated the created method’s validity. Longterm deep learning methods for contextual correction and sequencing improve OCR’s accuracy without generating significant processing cost by upholding a stable paradigm. The study’s findings demonstrate that lightweight linguistic corrections for deep learning-based OCR systems can significantly increase the robustness of these systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Legal Frameworks: Conceptual Foundations and Implications
authors: Animesh Kairi, Tapas Bhadra, Priyanka Das, Abhinov Kumar, Abhishek Das, Parichay Sarkar, Soumily Chatterjee, Samit Karmakar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Electronics, Materials Engineering &amp; Nano-Technology (IEMENTech)
abstract: To be absolutely honest, it has not been that simple on my part to grasp the impact that Artificial Intelligence is having on the world of law, as it seems everything is evolving at a pace, way too swift, to keep up with. My understanding of law has recently changed because I believe that most of it relies on human judgment and hours reading cases but now the AI actually participates in it in a manner that I did not believe. It is not only to accelerate the process or to perform repetitive tedious work. It as a matter of fact appears to be transforming the way lawyers think and the manner in which they could possibly make decisions, which appears to be overwhelming in my view. I was forced to read about what AI translates to specific to the field of law since it was something I did not fully understand initially. It is not like regular computers; machine learning or natural language processing systems can learn data rather than simply act on some set rules. Systems like the TAXMAN which were used many years ago were extremely rudimentary in comparison to the current systems such as scanning large stacks of legal documents, case prediction or assisting in legal research. People criticize that this is a great improvement being done, but I am yet to be convinced. I also have issues that persistently annoy me. As an illustration, imagine a misjudgment of an AI tool due to the biased data? Or what when no one can tell how the system was determined to its ending owing to the presence of those black box models? Whom do you accuse at that, the lawyer or the machine? These questions seem to have no definite answers to them and that is scary in terms of justice. Admittedly, I think there is no way that AI can substitute real lawyers or judges. Emotions and fairness are something that cannot be perceived by technology, yet at least by real people. I believe that probably the most appropriate solution is to use AI, not to rule people but rather to assist them. Perhaps in this way out of balance we can make the legal system more efficient and yet retain the human element.

title: SecureIIoT: Hybrid Machine Learning Model Based False Data Injection Attacks Detection
authors: Kumar Saurabh, Ashwani Kumar Sharma, Uphar Singh, Alok Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Increasingly, IIoT; a major aspect of Industry 4.0 has quickly been integrated into critical systems such as manufacturing, power generation, and smart transportation, which has greatly improved performance and automation, resulting in features such as predictive maintenance and smart grid control. This hyper-connectivity comes with the price tag of a sweeping enlargement of the attack surface for complex cyber-physical systems, meeting chronic cybersecurity threats. Traditional strategies for defense, such as encryption or secure authentication, do not work against subtle and stealthy-threats like False Data Injection (FDI) attacks. FDI attacks constitute a significant attack surface by intelligently altering sensor data so as to emulatereal system response to test commands, thus evading common forms of detection and introducing cascading results such as loss of economic value, loss of operation, and threat to human life. In order to solve this issue, this work presents a new hybrid machine learning model through the use of comprehensive and realistic Edge-IIoTset dataset selected for the detailed cover of up-to-date IIoT a wide array of attack scenarios. After some exploratory data analysis and advanced data preprocessing, we trained a model that combines the predictive ability of both XGBoost and a MLP. The MLP has ability to auto-learn higher order non-linear features from the data which can be directly used by the XGBoost classifier to achieve speed and accuracy in detecting the threats. The work emphasizes the need for smart data-driven intrusion detection systems to protect future smart industrial infrastructure.

title: Virtual Reality for Healthcare Education: Applications and Research Challenges
authors: Pavuluri Snehitha, Namala Dhanush, Padma Srivalli, Manikanta Tumpilli, Shaiju Panchikkil, V. M. Manikandan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) is transforming healthcare education by providing experiential, risk-free environments for medical students to develop clinical competencies. The purpose of this study is to examine the current status of VR technology in health care training, using a systematic, critical approach to identify research regarding training/education in surgery and anatomy, as well as identify gaps related to the implementation of VR technology into medical education that stem from a lack of standardized outcome measures and limited longitudinal validation studies. Furthermore, based on the findings of this review, various strategies for developing theory-driven strategies for VR technology will be proposed, and institutional barriers, such as cost, faculty preparedness, and infrastructure, will be explored.

title: HiDeNET: Hybrid Deep Neural Architecture for Multilevel Sentiment Classification of Cryptocurrency Comments
authors: Nitya D Dhagat, Nikita P Desai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are extremely volatile, with opinions on platforms such as Twitter and Reddit having a significant influence on the market trends. However, these sentiments are hard to analyze due to the noisy and complex nature of user comments. Current models often fail to capture the fine-grained semantics needed for proper classification. To solve this, we introduce HiDeNET, a hybrid deep learning model integrating global contextual information from DeBERTa-v3 and local patterns from a 1D CNN for hierarchical sentiment analysis. With a Reddit-Twitter dataset trained on a three-level hierarchical sentiment framework, HiDeNET performed better than five baseline models, with 17% improved accuracy compared to the best (SVM), due to its hierarchical design. It allows a more nuanced, multi-level understanding of sentiment than do flat classifiers. Yet performance drops slightly at the most granular level of sentiment, revealing potential for further tuning.

title: A Detailed Experiment Based Review on the Segmentation Models Used in Medical Images
authors: Gaurav Tiwari, Sangeeta Nakhate, Nimmala Hemalatha, Addepalli Sreeja, Nethi Lehya Sri, Abhinandan Jain
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Deep learning has dramatically advanced medical image segmentation, enabling precise delineation of anatomical structures and pathological regions that are critical for diagnosis and treatment planning [1]. Among the numerous architectures, convolution neural network (CNN) based models and hybrid CNN transformer networks have proven especially effective. In this review paper, we concentrate on five prominent segmentation models ranging form the original U-Net, its successor UNet++, the hybrid TransUNet and lastly the two backbone-based CNN models FCNResNet50 and DeepLabV3ResNet50. We first outline the evolution of CNN-based and transformer-based segmentation methods, highlighting the strengths of local feature extraction in CNNs versus global context modeling in transformers [2]. We then present detailed discussions of each model’s design, performance, and limitations. Comparative analysis considers segmentation is done by replicating the models and then running them in parallel to see how was there performance. The experimented is conducted on the HPC Cluster in parallel so that there is no chance of bias based optimization or stabilization of a process by the OS or the firmware on prolonged run of the same program. Clinical applications of these models are discussed, including tumor and organ segmentation, with emphasis on their practical benefits. Finally, we address current challenges, such as limited annotated data and variability of imaging modalities and suggest future research directions including transfer learning, zero-shot segmentation, and multi-modal integration [3]. This comprehensive review synthesizes insights from recent literature [4] [5] and clarifies how each model contributes to medical image analysis.

title: FraudGraph-Diff: Graph-Enhanced and Diffusion-Aided Fraud Detection for QR/PromptPay in Thailand
authors: Fuardi Minsakorn, Aueaphum Aueawatthanaphisut
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: A graph-augmented, diffusion-aided framework for real-time fraud screening on Thailand's QR/PromptPay transactions is presented. Transactional attributes are first standardized with time-aware statistics, while missing fields are imputed via a conditional denoising diffusion model that provides feature-level uncertainty. Relational context among accounts, devices, phone numbers, and merchants is captured with a heterogeneous graph neural network; node embeddings are pooled over transaction endpoints and fused with imputed tabular signals and uncertainty summaries. A cost-sensitive classifier is calibrated (temperature + isotonic) and tuned under a Neyman-Pearson constraint to enforce a fixed false-positive rate. In offline, imbalanced evaluation reflecting production traffic, the method attains AUROC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.985$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AUPRC}=0.842$</tex>, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TPR}=0.73$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FPR}=0.1 {\%}$</tex> and a normalized partial AUC over FPR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 0.1 {\%}$</tex> of 0.93. Calibration quality is evidenced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E C E}=0.018$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N L L}=0.215$</tex>, and weekly alert volumes remain stable once the operating threshold is fixed. Ablations indicate that graph signals and diffusion-based imputation contribute complementary gains in the low-FPR regime. Limitations and deployment considerations (label latency, traffic scaling, temporal drift) are discussed, and avenues for continual, privacy-preserving learning and prospective A/B validation are outlined.

title: An Efficient Multi-AGV Dispatching System for “Order-to-Person” Warehousing: A Simulation-Based Study
authors: Yu-zeyuan Song, Yuhua Huang, Wenbin Pan, Jianxiong Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Aerospace Engineering (ICMTAE)
abstract: The rapid growth of aviation warehouse management demands a transition from manual to intelligent logistics, yet the “order-to-person” model creates significant challenges in coordinating multiple Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs), particularly in path planning, resolving scheduling conflicts, and allocating tasks efficiently. This paper proposes a comprehensive multi-AGV dispatching system to address these problems. The system is built on a dynamic centralized architecture and features three key innovations: a Floyd prestorage path algorithm with a HashTable to accelerate shortest-path calculations; an exclusive zone control algorithm to prevent AGV collisions and deadlocks; and an improved Hungarian algorithm that uses a composite cost function to optimize task allocation by considering AGV path length, utilization, and power level. A dispatching and management system was developed for validation. Simulation experiments demonstrated significant improvements in path planning efficiency, a reduction in scheduling conflicts, and a decrease in the total time needed to complete order tasks, confirming that the integrated system provides an effective and reliable solution for optimizing multi-AGV operations

title: Wavelet Packet Entropy Analysis for Detecting Humidity-Induced Interference in Smart Gas Sensors
authors: Pan Xiong, Xiaowen Yuan, Zhixing Lu, Yinglin Wang, Xue Jun Li
abstract: Humidity significantly affects the accuracy and stability of smart gas sensors by altering response characteristics, introducing signal drift, and masking target gas signals, which poses challenges for reliable gas detection under variable environmental conditions. Consequently, humidity interference remains a key bottleneck for dependable gas sensing in resource-constrained embedded and wearable Internet of Things (IoT) systems. Although this study employs simulated synthetic datasets, all simulation parameters were carefully benchmarked against empirical sensor characteristics reported in existing literature, ensuring high fidelity to real-world behavior. This paper proposes a physics-informed computational framework based on Wavelet Packet Modal Entropy (WPME) to detect humidity-induced signal degradation without hardware augmentation and establishes a quadratic entropy-humidity model (WPME = 0.059A² − 0.119A + 0.094) derived from simulated NH₃ signals under 30%~86% RH. This model identifies a critical transition at 62.0% RH, which marks the shift from monolayer adsorption to capillary condensation. Below this threshold, entropy decreases by about 0.05 bits per 10% RH, while above it, the rate accelerates to 0.12 bits per 10% RH. The method achieves 98.26% detection accuracy (with only 1.47% degradation under extreme humidity) with a 0.16 ms response time, which demonstrates the viability of WPME as a fast, hardware-free solution for real-time humidity compensation in smart gas sensors.

title: Spatiotemporal Analysis of Parallelized Computing At the Extreme Edge_supp1-3673215.pdf
authors: Yasser Nabil
abstract: Low-latency computational-task execution can be achieved by leveraging device-to-device offloading and parallel processing over nearby extreme edge devices (EEDs), a paradigm known as extreme edge computing (EEC). However, EEC performance is challenged by device spatial randomness with intermittent wireless connectivity, limited device computing power, time-varying availability, and device failures. This paper introduces a novel spatiotemporal analytical framework for EEC by integrating stochastic geometry with an absorbing continuous-time Markov chain (ACTMC) to capture the interplay between communication and computation. Modeling a large-scale millimeter-wave network, we derive tractable expressions for the average task response delay and the task completion probability under both random and location-aware EED selection. Numerical results quantify the impact of location-awareness and unveil the existence of an optimal task segmentation that minimizes delay, which depends on network parameters and EED capabilities. We also demonstrate that device failures and EED scarcity exacerbate delay, which can be mitigated through a collaborative load-balancing approach between EEC and Multi-Access Edge Computing (MEC) schemes. Simulations and sensitivity analyses validate the proposed framework and offer design insights for optimizing system performance.

title: Adaptive Zone Barrier Lyapunov for USV Motion Constraint With Uncertain Control Gain
authors: Xiaoling Liang, Xuanlin Chen, Dan Bao, Shuzhi Sam Ge
abstract: Uncertainty in vessel control direction poses significant challenges in maritime operations, particularly in dynamic positioning, towing, and offshore wind farm maintenance. Traditional control methods struggle to handle uncertainties in system states for maritime operations, unmodeled dynamics, and multibody interactions. This study introduces an adaptive zone barrier Lyapunov control approach to address these challenges by ensuring vessel stability within a predefined operational zone while allowing adaptive parameter adjustments to compensate for uncertainties. The zone barrier Lyapunov function is employed to enforce safe operational constraints, preventing excessive deviations, while an adaptive control law dynamically estimates and adjusts system parameters. The proposed approach is validated through simulations of unmanned surface vehicle station-keeping and trajectory tracking. Results demonstrate that adaptive zone barrier Lyapunov control maintains vessel stability and maneuverability even when control direction is uncertain, outperforming conventional Lyapunov-based methods in handling constraint and robust effects. This study highlights the effectiveness of integrating zone barrier Lyapunov control with adaptive mechanisms for vessel motion control, offering a robust framework for enhancing maritime safety and operational efficiency in offshore environments.

title: Tempering the Bayes Filter towards Improved Model-Based Estimation
authors: M. J. T. C. van Zutphen, D. Herceg, G. Delimpaltadakis, D. Antunes
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: Model-based filtering is often carried out while subject to an imperfect model, as learning partially-observable stochastic systems remains a challenge. Recent work on Bayesian inference found that tempering the likelihood or full posterior of an imperfect model can improve predictive accuracy, as measured by expected negative log likelihood. In this paper, we develop the discrete-time tempered Bayes filter, improving performance of the belief-distribution estimate through both of the aforementioned, and one newly introduced, tempering modalities. The result admits a recursive implementation with a computational complexity no higher than that of the original Bayes filter. Our analysis reveals that — besides the well-known fact that likelihood tempering affects the balance between prior and likelihood — full-posterior tempering tunes the belief-distribution entropy level. We further find that a region of the tempering space can be understood as interpolating between the Bayes- and MAP filters, recovering these as special cases. Analytical results further establish conditions under which a tempered Bayes filter improves predictive performance. Specializing the results to the linear Gaussian case, we obtain the tempered Kalman filter. In this context, we interpret how the parameters affect the Kalman state estimate and covariance. Empirical results confirm that our method consistently improves predictive accuracy over the Bayes filter baseline.

title: Towards Intelligent Microfluidics: An LLM-Based Droplets Controlling Framework_supp1-3674635.mp4
authors: Jianguang Zhou
abstract: Digital microfluidics (DMF) is a versatile platform that enables parallel droplet operations and on-site programmable control, where droplet routing is a critical challenge for achieving automation. To enable automatic path generation from source to target electrodes and process-level feedback correction, this study proposes an adaptive intelligent control framework based on large language models (LLMs). The framework integrates language-guided topological map reconstruction, LLM-based path planning, and a dual closed-loop feedback mechanism to achieve high-precision droplet manipulation. To alleviate the spatial hallucination issues of LLMs in spatial reasoning such as discontinuous path planning and out-of-bound errors, a linguistic topological map reconstruction method is designed, improving path generation accuracy by 23.4% in complex obstacle environments compared to conventional spatial representations. In addition, a dual closed-loop feedback strategy is proposed, consisting of model self-correction feedback and camera-based image feedback, which enables real-time correction of routing deviations and enhances operational stability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves 100% task success across various droplet distribution scenarios, significantly advancing the intelligence of microfluidic operations and providing a promising foundation for fully autonomous DMF systems.

title: Knowledge-Informed Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction at Signalized Intersections for Infrastructure-to-Everything
authors: Huilin Yin, Yangwenhui Xu, Jiaxiang Li, Hao Zhang, Gerhard Rigoll
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Multi-agent trajectory prediction at signalized intersections is pivotal for the safety of autonomous driving and the efficiency of intelligent transportation systems. However, conventional vehicle-centric approaches are limited by restricted perception ranges and occlusion. Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) cooperation is widely regarded as an effective approach to alleviating these limitations. Furthermore, existing cooperative systems often suffer from complex coupling and selection bias, hindering universal and real-time service. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a novel Infrastructure-to-Everything (I2X) collaborative prediction scheme. This scheme decouples infrastructure capabilities from vehicle requests by independently forecasting and broadcasting trajectories for all detected vehicles. Building on this scheme, we propose I2XTraj, a dedicated infrastructure-based model that leverages three core mechanisms. First, a continuous signal-informed mechanism to adaptively encode real-time traffic light information. Second, a maneuver strategy awareness mechanism that integrates intersection geometric constraints to estimate maneuver distributions. Third, a spatial-temporal-mode attention network to refine multi-agent interactions. Extensive evaluations on two real-world datasets, V2X-Seq and SinD, demonstrate the superiority of our approach. In both single-infrastructure and collaborative scenarios, I2XTraj outperforms state-of-the-art methods by over 30% and 15%, respectively, confirming its strong generalizability and robustness in complex intersection environments.

title: SEGMN: A Lightweight Model for Bearing Fault Diagnosis on IoT Edge Device
authors: Yulin Li, Feng Luan, Zhichao Liu, Shuhao Liu, Xinlan Li, Xiumin Zhou
abstract: Traditional deep learning models have made progress in bearing fault diagnosis but are limited by large parameter sizes, high computational complexity, and poor adaptability to industrial edge hardware, restricting their practical application. To address these issues and promote the engineering transformation of lightweight algorithms, this study proposes SEGMN, a robust and lightweight fault diagnosis model improved from MobileNet V2, specifically for edge-side bearing fault diagnosis on the K230 platform. First, short-time Fourier transform (STFT) converts one-dimensional vibration signals into grayscale time-frequency images, preserving key fault features while adapting to the K230’s resource constraints. The model then undergoes three-stage optimization: channel dimension adaptation, inverted residual module simplification, and integration of the Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention mechanism, balancing lightweight deployment and diagnostic performance. Experiments on the CWRU dataset show that SEGMN achieves 92.7% accuracy under -6 dB noise (GFLOPs: 0.103, Params: 0.816 M), while it reaches 93.0% accuracy on a private dataset under 2db noise. For K230 deployment, a customized uint8 quantization scheme is adopted to reduce memory usage and enable low-latency inference. In the deployment simulation test, the model maintains 100% accuracy with only 1.05 MB of memory and an inference time of 1.85 ms, and an online test achieves 98.6% accuracy, providing a cost-effective and portable solution for industrial edge bearing fault diagnosis.

title: Hand Gesture-Based Biometric Verification and Identification Using Embedded-STQNet Deep Neural Architecture in Security-Oriented Systems
authors: Asif Ullah, Zhendong Song, Waqar Riaz, Xiaozhi Qi, MD Moinul Hossain
abstract: This study presents Embedded-STQNet, a dual-path deep neural architecture for surface electromyography (sEMG)-based biometric authentication. The network integrates a local-spectral CNN and a modified ResNet-18 encoder, with concatenated features passed through a shared 128-dimensional ℓ₂-normalized projection head. A three-stage metric learning strategy—Siamese contrastive pre-training, triplet-margin tuning, and quadruplet loss optimization—progressively enhances intra-class compactness and inter-class separation. We evaluate the model on a 28-channel sEMG spectrogram dataset collected across multiple trials from 43 subjects, performing 16 gestures in a multi-session setting. The Siamese configuration achieves a verification accuracy of 98.48% and the lowest Equal Error Rate (EER) of 0.0155, outperforming Triplet (0.0776) and Quadruplet (0.051) variants. In Rank-K identification, Siamese achieves 99.55% (Rank-1) accuracy in single-code and 99.52% in multi-code scenarios, while the Quadruplet model achieves 99.54% and 95.90%, and the Triplet model achieves 97.70% and 92.13%, respectively. Additional evaluations of DET, ROC, FAR/FRR, and clustering metrics confirm advanced discriminability, with a silhouette score of 0.88 and an intra/inter-class variance ratio of 0.0345. The model generates compact 128-D embeddings without relying on gesture classification, enabling generalizable and real-time suitability for identity recognition. Compared to prior EMG-based methods, Embedded STQNet offers improved accuracy, multi-day generalization, real-world viability for wearables, and real-time suitability for biometric systems.

title: A Hybrid Framework for Human-Wildlife Conflict Mitigation Using Infrared-Triggered YOLO and IoT Alerting
authors: P Shyam Kumar, S Sreekanth, B Sabarinath, P Kiran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Human wildlife conflict has become a pressing issue in regions where human activities increasingly overlap with natural habitats. Incidents such as crop destruction, property damage, and human casualties caused by wild animal intrusion are rising in frequency, creating both economic and social challenges. Traditional mitigation approaches, including physical patrolling, fencing, or manual surveillance, often fail due to their limited scalability, high labor demands, and lack of real time responsiveness. To address these limitations, this paper presents an integrated AI and IoT framework that combines event-driven sensor triggers with real-time, deep learning-based object detection for wild animal monitoring and alerting that combines sensor technology with computer vision for efficient monitoring and rapid response. The system employs infrared (IR) sensors to detect the presence of movement, which then activates a lightweight image recognition model (YOLOv11s) to capture and classify the animal. Once the animal is identified, both the captured image and the species label are transmitted to a NodeMCU microcontroller, which subsequently forwards the information as an SMS alert through the Twilio cloud messaging service to local residents and forest authorities. This ensures timely notification and enables proactive human intervention before conflicts escalate. The architecture emphasizes real time data acquisition, low power operation, and wireless communication, making it suitable for rural and remote environments where infrastructural support is minimal. The proposed system demonstrates how integrating IR sensing, deep learning-based image classification, and IoT enabled communication can create a reliable, scalable, and cost-effective solution to mitigate human wildlife conflict.

title: Maintaining Test Cases Under Requirement Evolution in Automotive Systems
authors: Surasak Phetmanee
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: Software has long been an essential element in cars. Impact analysis is to determine factors and entities which are either directly or indirectly affected by changes. This paper focuses on impact analysis between the requirement based classification tree and test cases. Since requirements are usually described with informal language and transformed into the requirement based classification tree, testers also convert this requirement based classification tree into test scenarios. Furthermore, the classification tree is used for decision making in a self driving vehicle. In developing software for automotive systems, effects caused by changes to existing test cases not applicable elsewhere. The traditional solution to solve this problem was to generate all test cases to verify the changed system. Therefore, the traditional solution consumes time and resources. This paper presents an implementation of test cases impact analysis based on changes in an automotive system. This approach compares an automotive system version, diagnose changed entities, and generate new test cases. Results contain the solution of impact analysis for an automotive system. This technique addresses the traditional problem of test case regeneration in automotive software by selectively updating only test cases affected by requirement changes, thereby reducing unnecessary regeneration effort and testing overhead. Test cases are successfully updated and can be used to verify changes in the automotive software.
