title: Developing an AI-Enhanced Individualized Prediction Tool for Psychopathological Symptoms in Vietnam: A Study Protocol
authors: Hung Nguyen, Minh Khau, Huyen Nguyen, Hieu Pham
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming mental healthcare by enabling early detection, monitoring, and personalized intervention. Yet, most AI tools rely on categorical diagnostic systems (DSM-5, ICD-11), limiting their ability to address comorbidity and the complex, interconnected nature of mental health symptoms. To overcome these limitations, this study introduces an AI-powered tool that integrates dimensional (HiTOP) and network-based models to create individualized risk profiles for internalizing disorders such as depression, anxiety, and bipolar disorder. The study unfolds in three phases: (1) model development and validation of assessment tools; (2) data collection, training, and refinement of the model; and (3) application in clinical and psychological practice settings in Vietnam. Central to this approach is the Risk-aware Taxonomy-enhanced Symptom Encoder (RiTaSE), which uses symptom data and severities within a Transformer-based model trained on HiTOP-aligned datasets. This project is among the first to employ AI for personalized psychopathological profiling in Vietnam and similar settings. The expected outcomes include a diagnostic-support tool for clinicians, deeper cross-cultural insights into symptom comorbidity, and improved mental health monitoring. Future work will expand to all HiTOP dimensions and incorporate longitudinal, multimodal data for predicting clinical transitions.

title: Transformer-Based Multi-Modal Temporal Embeddings for Explainable Metabolic Phenotyping in Type 1 Diabetes
authors: Pir Bakhsh Khokhar, Carmine Gravino, Fabio Palomba, Sule Yildirim Yayilgan, Sarang Shaikh
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Advancements in Computational Sciences (ICACS)
abstract: Type 1 diabetes (T1D) is a highly metabolically heterogeneous disease that cannot be adequately characterized by conventional biomarkers such as glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c). This study proposes an explainable deep learning framework that integrates continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data with laboratory profiles to learn multimodal temporal embeddings of individual metabolic status. Temporal dependencies across modalities are modeled using a transformer encoder, while latent metabolic phenotypes are identified via Gaussian mixture modeling. Model interpretability is achieved through transformer attention visualization and SHAP-based feature attribution. Five latent metabolic phenotypes, ranging from metabolic stability to elevated cardiometabolic risk, were identified among 577 individuals with T1D. These phenotypes exhibit distinct biochemical profiles, including differences in glycemic control, lipid metabolism, renal markers, and thyrotropin (TSH) levels. Attention analysis highlights glucose variability as a dominant temporal factor, while SHAP analysis identifies HbA1c, triglycerides, cholesterol, creatinine, and TSH as key contributors to phenotype differentiation. Phenotype membership shows statistically significant, albeit modest, associations with hypertension, myocardial infarction, and heart failure. Overall, this explainable multimodal temporal embedding framework reveals physiologically coherent metabolic subgroups in T1D and supports risk stratification beyond single biomarkers.

title: Integrating Machine Learning into Consumer Behaviour Analysis for Insect-Based Protein Supplements
authors: Nilubon Kurubanjerdjit, Hnin Oo Wai, Nyein Nyein Kyaw, Nyein Pyae Sone, Soontarin Nupap
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: This study explores the application of machine learning techniques to support data-driven marketing for insect-based protein supplements in Thailand, using Brand <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{X}$</tex> as the focal case. Survey data were analysed using three methodological approaches: classification, clustering, and association rule mining. To estimate consumers' likelihood of trying the product, three classification algorithms—Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, and Random Forest—were implemented, with Random Forest achieving the highest performance (76.11 % accuracy). K-means clustering identified two distinct consumer profiles: (1) premium- and variety-oriented consumers who emphasise product quality, certifications, and flavour diversity, and (2) price- and safety-conscious consumers who prefer familiar flavours and lower price ranges. FP-Growth and association rule mining further uncovered behavioural patterns, revealing that price concern is the most frequent and influential factor influencing purchasing decisions. The analysis showed that consumers motivated by weight control and those preferring vanilla flavour are often associated with the mid-tier price range <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(801-1,200 \text{THB})$</tex>. Overall, the findings demonstrate that machine learning offers valuable insights for segmentation, product positioning, and pricing strategies within the emerging insect-based protein market, and can support more targeted and evidence-driven marketing decision-making.

title: Evaluating Emissions and Sustainability of Electric Vehicles through the GREET Model
authors: Raymon Antony Raj, D Sarathkumar, G Guna, Leo John Baptist Andrews, Sampath Kumar Venkatachary, V Anusharani, J Sivadasan, A Sheik Sidthik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The application of Electric Motor (EM) technology has transformed the world in recent years, especially within the transportation industry. This shift has been recognized as a sustainable approach to reducing pollution and cutting down the reliance on fossil fuels for transportation. Approximately 80% of the fuel used for transportation worldwide is considered polluting fuel. By implementing EM in vehicles such as two-wheelers (2W), three-wheelers (3W), four-wheelers $(4 \mathrm{~W})$, and buses, we effectively convert them into Electric Vehicles (EV). This EV technology primarily includes two main variants: the Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) and the Plug-In Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV). However, this EV technology does not automatically qualify for Clean Alternative Fuel Vehicle (CAFV) status due to battery-related concerns, as emphasized by the International Energy Agency (IEA). While electric vehicles, including hybrids, do not produce direct emissions, they do generate indirect evaporative emissions (EE) and tailpipe emissions (TE). On a broader scale, cradle-to-grave emissions (CTGE) significantly contribute to the overall emissions associated with vehicles, highlighting the need for a comprehensive review to achieve future sustainability goals.

title: Analysis and Optimization of Friction Characteristics in Speed Skating Based on Computer Simulation Technology
authors: Zhao Wang, Mingyuan Zhao, Yakai Zhang, Zhou Deng, Haijuan Han
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Machine Learning–Driven Framework for Stratified Physical Education Based on Multidimensional Fitness and Movement Assessment: An Empirical Study in Adolescents
authors: Feiyu Zhang, Xuehan Li
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Sports Technology and Performance Analysis
abstract: None

title: Personalized Recommendation Based on Deep Learning and Knowledge Graphs: Integration Analysis and Framework Construction
authors: Lei Liu, Siriporn Angsopa, Kitti Juikumjorn
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Knowledge and Smart Technology (KST)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of online education, “knowledge overload” and “knowledge disorientation” have become critical issues, while mainstream recommendation algorithms suffer from challenges like cold start, data sparsity, and inadequate interpretability. This study systematically synthesizes 128 relevant literatures (2019-2024), to explore personalized learning resource recommendation. It first identifies limitations of traditional recommendation systems, then analyzes the application of knowledge graphs (KG) in course and learning path recommendation, as well as the role of deep learning (DL) models (CNN, RNN, GNN) in learner behavior modeling. A reinforcement learning-based integrated framework of KG and DL is proposed to address insufficient user profiling and poor interpretability. Finally, current challenges and future trends are discussed, providing valuable insights for advancing the field.

title: Research and Fabrication of an Electrochemical Immunosensor Based on Gold-Sputtered Glass for Protein Detection
authors: Chi Tran Nhu, Loc Do Quang, Tung Thanh Bui, Chun-Ping Jen, Trinh Chu Duc
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: Protein plays a vital role in the human body and serves as a key biomarker in cancer diagnostics. Due to their high sensitivity and low detection limits, electrochemical immunosensors have gained widespread application in protein detection. In this study, an electrochemical immunosensor was designed and fabricated on a gold-sputtered glass substrate for the detection of target proteins. The proposed sensor was designed with a three-electrode configuration, including working, counter, and reference electrodes, fabricated using the photolithography technique. The working electrode, where target binding occurs, was circular with a radius of 1.2 mm. The reference electrode was formed by selectively electrolyzing the gold surface in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AgNO}_{3}$</tex> and KCl solutions to produce Ag/AgCl layers, while other areas were covered with a thin layer of photoresist. After fabrication, the electrodes were immersed in a standard redox solution to perform cyclic voltammetry (CV). The results demonstrated consistent CV responses across the independently fabricated electrodes, indicating good reproducibility and uniformity of the electrode fabrication process. Besides, the working electrode was modified to immobilize antibodies on its surface and create the electrochemical immunosensor. Experiments using bovine serum albumin-fluorescein isothiocyanate conjugate (BSAFITC) demonstrated effective protein retention and specific antibody binding. These interactions were validated by fluorescence imaging and CV measurements. The appearance of green fluorescence on the electrode surface demonstrated the success of the functionalization process, the protein had specifically bound to the antibody and was retained. Besides, a pronounced reduction in the CV oxidation peak confirmed the sensor's detection performance. These findings highlight the sensor's potential for future development into commercial immunosensors and point-of-care diagnostic applications.

title: Meta Heuristic Driven Adaptive Parameter Optimization for Spectral Clustering
authors: Hui Du, Lixing Chang, Yitong Yang, Bo Chen, Fangyu Guo
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: The effectiveness of spectral clustering critically depends on manually setting its parameters, and traditional parameter tuning methods are inefficient. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an adaptive parameter spectral clustering based on a meta-heuristic algorithm(APSM). The core of APSM is a hybrid search strategy that balances local search and global exploration. First, it combines the NewtonRaphson search rule (NRSR), records the locations of the optimal solutions found in history and uses them to obtain the search direction. Second, the differential evolution (DE) is introduced to enhance population diversity via random vectors, thereby preventing premature convergence to local optima. Finally, the dynamic balance mechanism automatically adjusts the search step-size, enabling the algorithm to first conduct extensive exploration of the parameter space and then finetune the results to obtain the required optimal parameters for clustering. To verify the effectiveness of the APSM, experiments were conducted on both artificial datasets and UCI datasets. The results indicate that the three evaluation metrics of all datasets have been significantly improved.

title: Analysis of the Suitability of End-Effectors for Agricultural Harvesting Robots
authors: Le Li, Xiaochen Liu, Guoyong Lin, Yifeng Huang, Zhengui Huang, Rui Li
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: As the component directly contacting the harvested object, the end-effector is a critical factor influencing both the success rate and damage rate of fruit picking. This paper aims to systematically analyse the applicability of different end-effector types within highly unstructured fruit and vegetable harvesting environments. Drawing upon principles of bionic design and mechanical engineering, this study first systematically categorises end effectors and dissects the operational mechanisms and characteristics of their typical structures. Subsequently, by integrating literature data and case studies, an analytical framework linking mechanical design to harvesting performance (success rate, damage rate) is established. This framework quantitatively and qualitatively explores the influence mechanisms of key factors-such as drive methods, structural design, and perception-based control-on harvesting outcomes. Finally, it proposes future development trends centred on ‘intelligent flexible bodies’, specifically outlining implementation pathways and technical challenges in areas such as flexibility, versatility, adaptive control, and self-healing capabilities. This research provides structured theoretical references and practical guidance for the design and optimisation of future highperformance harvesting end-effectors.

title: FusionNeXt: A ConvNeXtV2 and ResNet Framework Guided by a Hybrid Loss Function for Combining Anatomical and Functional Medical Imagery
authors: Pathan Mujahid, Gunnam Suryanarayana, Bandarupalli Gopichand, Thori Kranthikiran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: Medical images can give doctors valuable information about how a patient is doing when they look at different types of images together, like MRI scans which show structural details of the brain and PET scans which show functions of the brain. In this paper we introduce a new deep learning framework called FusionNeXt, which will combine MRI and PET scans into one new image (the fused image) that has more useful information than either image would alone. FusionNeXt uses ConvNeXtV2 layers for building large-scale structures and ResNet blocks for refining small-scale elements in the images. MRI and PET input data are separated out into two elements (base and detail) and then combined using a hybrid technique that combines both mean and maximum feature information. The decoder reconstructs the combined image and a hybrid loss function is used to preserve structural integrity and visual quality of the combined image. In tests using benchmark datasets, FusionNeXt has produced images with clearer, more distinct edges, better contrast, and more details than the original images. Therefore, we believe that FusionNeXt has strong potential to be a tool for better diagnoses and clinical decision making.

title: A Spatial-Temporal Graph Approach for Reliable and Green Communications in Lossy Industrial WSNs
authors: Mengmeng Xu, Xingsi Xue, Wenjun Gao, Haipeng Yu, Hengzhou Xu, Hai Zhu
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of data collection from sensor nodes to a control node within a predefined mission cycle. To capture the time-varying connectivity resulting from duty-cycling and dynamic link conditions, we model the network evolution as a spatial-temporal graph. Within this framework, the topology control problem is then formulated as the construction of a set of spatial-temporal paths, which effectively balance network energy consumption and transmission reliability. A heuristic algorithm is proposed to efficiently find a near-optimal topology graph explicitly trading off the green communication objective against the transmission reliability. Simulation results validate the capability to support energy-efficient and reliable data collection in lossy industrial environments.

title: A Novel MLCS Algorithm Based on Hierarchical Parallelization
authors: Zhenhao Kong, Shiwei Wei, Wenming Huang, Yuyao Huang, Zhenrong Deng, Rui Yang, Yannan Xiao
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: The multiple longest common subsequence (MLCS) problem is an NP-hard problem. Traditional algorithms often suffer from state space explosion, computational redundancy, and inefficient memory usage when handling large-scale sequences, which significantly constrains their scalability and performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel MLCS algorithm based on hierarchical parallelization (HP-MLCS), which employs an innovative parallel computing architecture to fully parallelize the construction of the directed cyclic graph (DAG) and the longest path search. Experiments demonstrate that this algorithm achieves a 3-10-fold improvement in time efficiency over existing state-of-the-art algorithms under largescale sequence conditions.

title: Smart 3D Printing Filament Dehydrator & Storage System with Automated Temperature and Humidity Control
authors: Sudhanwa Rajurkar, Abhyuday Rai, Pranav Joshi, Krishna Ramanbainwad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: The seepage of moisture in hygroscopic materials like nylon and PETG creates a lot of problems in additive manufacturing processes like 3d printing. To solve this issue filament dehydrators are used, but the conventional filament dehydrators do not have the capacity to store filaments for a very long time after it has been dried. In the conventional methods we use a dehydrator to dry the filament and as soon as the filament has been dehydrated it is kept in an airtight container to keep it away from further seepage of moisture. Our solution eliminates this problem by using a two mode system a) drying mode b) storing mode. Once the dehydrator dehydrates the filament it transforms the same container into an airtight container thus making it a dry box to store the filament. This method consumes less power as it does not need to dehydrate the filament every time the printer needs to be used.

title: Smooth Switching Control Strategy for ACC Based on LPV Models
authors: Weilin Wu, Daqiao Wang, Kunsheng Wu, Yaojian Lei
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: In order to address the complex operating conditions and uncertainties encountered by vehicle adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems, a design methodology for a smooth-switching linear parameter-varying (LPV) output feedback controller is proposed. Firstly, employing relative velocity as the time-varying parameter, an LPV model for the vehicle adaptive tracking system is constructed and a hybrid sensitivity function is introduced. Subsequently, by selecting a weighting function, the hybrid sensitivity optimisation problem is transformed into an equivalent standard control problem. Considering the gradual nature of the time-varying parameters, a switching law without minimum average dwell time constraints is employed, thereby reducing the conservatism of the design. By partitioning the time-varying parameter into a finite number of subspaces with overlapping regions, nonoverlapping regions activate their respective controllers independently. Finally, by partitioning the time-varying parameter into finite subspaces with overlapping regions, a performance-weighted smooth switching controller for the ACC system is designed. Non-overlapping regions activate their corresponding controllers independently, while overlapping regions fuse adjacent subspaces' controllers through interpolation.

title: Comprehensive Data-Driven Framework for Statistical Transient Stability Analysis in Variable Inertia Power Systems Enabled by High-Throughput Simulation
authors: Keito Nishida, Ryuto Shigenobu, Akiko Takahashi, Masakazu Ito
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Power and Renewable Energy Engineering (PREE)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy reduces electrical power system inertia, increasing vulnerability to disturbances. Conventional transient stability analysis suffers from event selectivity limitations. Hence, predetermined “worst case” scenarios overlook the risk associated with critical vulnerability patterns. This paper demonstrates that high-throughput simulation can systematically explore the entire contingency pattern to identify essential clusters of vulnerability. The automated framework reduces simulation time by 85 % while enabling statistical analysis across different inertia levels (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 ~\mathrm{s}, 8 ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>, 16s) and disturbance magnitudes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$250-1000 \text{MW}$</tex>) using the IEEJ EAST 10-machine system. The RoCoF and Nadir results reveal distinct spatial vulnerability clustering, with buses 18, 19, 20, 36, 37, and 47 consistently exhibiting the highest impacts regardless of operating conditions. These topology-dependent vulnerability patterns require comprehensive rather than selective analysis for practical power system security assessment.

title: Computational Optimization of Gold Nanostar Geometry for High-Sensitivity SERS Biosensing
authors: Nguyen Thanh Tung, Hoang ChuDuc, Nguyen Thanh Son
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Health Science and Technology (ICHST)
abstract: The detection of cancer biomarkers at ultra-low concentrations (picomolar to femtomolar) in clinical samples remains a critical challenge, often precluding early diagnosis and effective treatment monitoring. This study presents a comprehensive computational framework for the rational design and optimization of a Surface-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy (SERS) biosensor to address this need. Employing finite-element method (FEM) simulations, we systematically optimized plasmonic gold (Au) nanostructures, identifying a unique nanostar geometry (60 nm core, 10 nm tip radius) that generates intense and reproducible electromagnetic “hot-spots” under 785 nm excitation. These optimized structures are predicted to yield a robust, surface-averaged SERS enhancement factor (EF) consistently exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sup>. Based on this theoretical EF, the projected detection limit could reach the picomolar <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10^{-12} \mathrm{M})$</tex> range. This work establishes a predictive in silico blueprint to guide future experimental fabrication and validation, representing a sensitivity improvement of at least two orders of magnitude compared to many experimentally realized, non-aggregated SERS substrates [32], [33]. Furthermore, in silico modeling of surface functionalization with specific aptamers demonstrates high selectivity, capable of differentiating the target biomarker's spectral signature from a complex background. Ultimately, this work provides a predictive in silico blueprint that can significantly accelerate the experimental development of next-generation molecular diagnostic tools, establishing a clear pathway toward creating clinically viable SERS sensors for non-invasive cancer detection at its earliest stages.

title: 6G Security Architecture: The Prospects of AI-Based THz Radio
authors: Tingguang Gan, Wansha Tang, Shigan Wu, Jincheng Xie
publish: 2025 21st International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Security (CIS)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of sixth-generation (6G) mobile communication technology, terahertz (THz) communication has emerged as a key future direction for wireless communications, thanks to its high bandwidth, high data transmission rates, and low latency. Concurrently, the development of artificial intelligence (AI) offers new opportunities to enhance the intelligence, automation, and security of communication systems. This paper explores the characteristics of the THz band and its potential in the field of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 G}$</tex> communication security. Based on this, this paper proposes a security architecture for 6G networks by integrating AI. Subsequently, it discusses the theoretical foundations of security technologies in the future 6G and the role of combining AI with THz communication in security, while also outlining future research directions.

title: Cover

title: Elasticity-Driven Hybrid Demand Response with Rooftop PV: Enhancing End-User Profitability and Peak Reduction in Residential Smart Grids
authors: Rahul Goswami, Ranjit Roy, Kamal Krishna Mandal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)
abstract: This paper proposes an elasticity-based hybrid demand response (DR) framework that integrates dynamic pricing and monetary incentives to improve residential profitability in prosumer-rich grids. The model incorporates hourly rooftop PV generation from location-specific irradiance data and represents demand reduction through price and incentive elasticity matrices capturing self- and cross-hour effects. Simulations involving 100 residential users, organized into 10 heterogeneous sets over a 24hour horizon, demonstrate that the hybrid scheme consistently outperforms incentive-only and price-only models. On average, it achieves peak demand reductions of $\mathbf{1 9 - 2 2 \%}$ compared to less than 10% for single-method schemes, while delivering end-user profits $1.5-2$ times higher. These results highlight the hybrid model’s ability to balance financial incentives, pricing signals, and PV revenues, offering a practical and consumer-centric strategy for future smart grid demand management.

title: Optimal Control of Entity-Based Systems via Koopman Representations with Product Density Observables
authors: Madeline Blischke, João P. Hespanha
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: The Koopman operator, which describes a dynamical system via a linear representation that can be approximately learned from data, allows application of linear control techniques to nonlinear systems. This work considers a nonlinear optimal control problem with final state constraints, which we alternatively represent by a linear problem on a lifted state space described by the Koopman operator. We show that the optimal cost-to-go to this linear problem has a piecewise linear form with respect to the lifted state. A robust formulation, in which we minimize the worst-case cost with respect to bounded errors in the initial state and Koopman dynamics, retains a piecewise affine structure. Due to the combinatorial nature of the problem, the number of possible input sequences grows exponentially in the horizon, and so we provide a heuristic pruning algorithm that reduces the search space to a much smaller subset. Our control approach is generally applicable, but its effectiveness depends on a domain-specific choice of lifting functions, termed observables. For a class of systems with dynamics described by some number of distinct “entities”, we define observables that describe “densities” of these entities, as well as products of densities that capture a richer class of interactions between entities.

title: Monocular Vision-Based Target Enclosing Control for USVs With FOV Constraint and Uncertain Dynamics_supp1-3669742.mp4
authors: Fanghao Huang
abstract: Target enclosing control of uncrewed surface vehicles (USVs) is essential for maritime monitoring and security. For noncooperative targets in practical scenarios, relying on global information becomes ineffective, thus making vision-based perception a promising solution. However, achieving reliable vision-based enclosing faces significant challenges including field of view (FOV) constraint and various uncertainties. To address these challenges, this article presents a monocular vision-based target enclosing framework for USVs. The proposed framework first incorporates a tailored YOLOv5-based detector coupled with a bearing-only state estimator, which can achieve reliable target state estimation from monocular vision. Then, to maintain continuous visual contact, a control barrier function based safety-critical filter is developed to constrain the USV’s FOV during target enclosing. Furthermore, a dual-layer extended state observer (ESO) based control structure is implemented to handle various uncertainties, where kinematic-level ESOs compensate for unknown USV lateral velocity and target motion, while dynamic-level ESOs address lumped dynamic uncertainties. This approach ensures robust and accurate target enclosing with continuous visual tracking. Both simulations and experiment are implemented to verify the effectiveness of the proposed framework.

title: Price-Based Demand Response Participation in Implicit Balancing Services: A Value-Oriented Inverse Optimization Framework
authors: Behzad Vatandoust, Kenneth Bruninx, Jean-François Toubeau, François Vallée
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Markets, Policy and Regulation
abstract: Indirect Demand Response (IDR) programs that incentivize consumer participation through unidirectional price-based mechanisms offer a promising way to mobilize small-scale flexibility. The main challenge in such IDR programs lies in modeling the uncertain price-response relationship of demand response resources (DRRs), which complicates DR pricing. Inverse Optimization (IO) provides an effective method for capturing historical price-response patterns with interpretability and seamless integration into retailers' decision-making frameworks. While traditional IO prioritizes forecast accuracy, recent research advocates for value-oriented forecasting, which prioritizes decision quality to mitigate the practical impacts of forecast errors. Despite their advantages, fully integrated value-oriented approaches can become computationally intensive for IO. To address this, we propose a novel value-oriented IO (VOIO) framework that facilitates a data-driven, value-oriented identification of DRRs' aggregate price-response parameters by shifting IO hyperparameter evaluation metrics to decision-making regret minimization. The proposed method was applied in a case study examining a Balance Responsible Party's (BRP) participation in the Belgian single-price imbalance market. The results demonstrated that the VOIO approach was able to reduce overall financial losses from forecast errors compared to a forecast-oriented IO benchmark, achieving overall profit gains in the validation and test sets through more conservative pricing.
